	      The Lean-facing kernel/range proof bodies are:

	      ```lean
	      def BHW.skewMatrixSubmodule
	          (D : Type) [Fintype D] [DecidableEq D]
	          (K : Type) [Field K] [CharZero K] :
	          Submodule K (Matrix D D K) :=
	        { carrier := {A | A.transpose = -A}
	          zero_mem' := by simp
	          add_mem' := by
	            intro A B hA hB
	            ext i j
	            simpa [Matrix.transpose_add, hA, hB]
	          smul_mem' := by
	            intro c A hA
	            ext i j
	            simpa [Matrix.transpose_smul, hA] }

	      theorem BHW.skewMatrixSubmodule_finrank
	          (D : Type) [Fintype D] [DecidableEq D]
	          (K : Type) [Field K] [CharZero K] :
	          Module.finrank K (BHW.skewMatrixSubmodule D K) =
	            Fintype.card D * (Fintype.card D - 1) / 2 := by
	        -- Coordinates are the strict upper-triangular entries; diagonal
	        -- entries vanish because `A i i = -A i i` and `2` is nonzero.
	        exact BHW.skewMatrix_strictUpperLinearEquiv_finrank D K

	      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrix_withDet_factorization_finrank
	          (D : Nat) :
	          Module.finrank Ōäé
	              (BHW.sourceFullFrameSymmetricCoordSubmodule (D - 1)) =
	            D * (D + 1) / 2 + 1 := by
	        -- Product of the symmetric `D ├Ś D` matrix-coordinate submodule
	        -- and the free determinant coordinate.  This should be implemented
	        -- by reusing `sourceSymmetricMatrix_factorization_finrank` for the
	        -- first component and `Module.finrank_self Ōäé Ōäé = 1` for the second.
	        exact BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrix_factorization_finrank_with_scalar D

	      theorem BHW.eta_isUnit
	          (d : Nat) :
	          IsUnit
	            (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) :
	              Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé) :=
	        IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one _ (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_sq (d := d))

	      theorem BHW.matrix_inv_mul_three_of_isUnit
	          {D : Type} [Fintype D] [DecidableEq D]
	          {A B C : Matrix D D Ōäé}
	          (hA : IsUnit A.det) (hB : IsUnit B.det) (hC : IsUnit C.det) :
	          (A * B * C)Ōü╗┬╣ = CŌü╗┬╣ * BŌü╗┬╣ * AŌü╗┬╣ := by
	        exact BHW.matrix_inv_mul_of_isUnit_det_three hA hB hC

	      noncomputable def BHW.specialLorentzLieAlgebra_skewLinearEquiv
	          (d : Nat) :
	          BHW.specialComplexLorentzLieAlgebra d ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
	            BHW.skewMatrixSubmodule (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé :=
	        { toFun := fun A =>
	            Ō¤©ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) *
	              (A : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé), by
	              -- If `AßĄĆ╬Ę + ╬ĘA = 0`, then `(╬ĘA)ßĄĆ = -╬ĘA`.
	              ext i j
	              simpa [Matrix.transpose_mul,
	                ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_transpose, Matrix.mul_assoc]
	                using A.property.1Ō¤®
	          invFun := fun B =>
	            Ō¤©ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) *
	              (B : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé), by
	              constructor
	              ┬Ę -- If `BßĄĆ = -B`, then
	                -- `(╬ĘB)ßĄĆ╬Ę + ╬Ę(╬ĘB) = 0`.
	                ext i j
	                simpa [Matrix.transpose_mul,
	                  ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_transpose,
	                  ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_sq, Matrix.mul_assoc]
	                  using B.property
	              ┬Ę -- Trace of an ╬Ę-skew-adjoint matrix is zero in char zero.
	                exact
	                  BHW.trace_eta_mul_skew_eq_zero
	                    (d := d) (B : Matrix _ _ Ōäé) B.propertyŌ¤®
	          left_inv := by
	            intro A
	            apply Subtype.ext
	            simpa [Matrix.mul_assoc, ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_sq]
	          right_inv := by
	            intro B
	            apply Subtype.ext
	            simpa [Matrix.mul_assoc, ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_sq]
	          map_add' := by
	            intro A B
	            apply Subtype.ext
	            ext i j
	            simp [Matrix.mul_add]
	          map_smul' := by
	            intro c A
	            apply Subtype.ext
	            ext i j
	            simp [smul_eq_mul] }

	      noncomputable def BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedTangentSpace_linearEquiv_kernel
	          (d : Nat)
	          {M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
	          (hM0 : IsUnit M0.det) :
	          BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedTangentSpace d
	              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGram d M0) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
	            LinearMap.ker
	              ((BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation_deriv d
	                (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord d M0)).restrict
	                (BHW.sourceFullFrameSymmetricCoordSubmodule d)) :=
	        { toFun := fun Y =>
	            let hY :=
	              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedTangentSpace_mem_iff_symmetric_deriv_zero
	                (d := d) hM0 Y).1 Y.property
	            Ō¤©Ō¤©Y, hY.1Ō¤®, hY.2Ō¤®
	          invFun := fun Y => Ō¤©Y.1, by
	            exact
	              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedTangentSpace_mem_iff_symmetric_deriv_zero
	                (d := d) hM0 Y.1).2
	                Ō¤©Y.1.property, Y.2Ō¤®Ō¤®
	          left_inv := by intro Y; rfl
	          right_inv := by intro Y; rfl
	          map_add' := by intro Y Z; rfl
	          map_smul' := by intro c Y; rfl }

	      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_range_subset_tangent
	          (d : Nat)
	          {M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
	          (hM0 : IsUnit M0.det) :
	          LinearMap.range
	              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferentialCLM d M0).toLinearMap Ōēż
	            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedTangentSpace d
	              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGram d M0) := by
	        rintro Y Ō¤©X, rflŌ¤®
	        refine
	          (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedTangentSpace_mem_iff_symmetric_deriv_zero
	            (d := d) hM0
	            (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential d M0 X)).2 ?_
	        constructor
	        ┬Ę -- Symmetry of `X * ╬Ę * M0ßĄĆ + M0 * ╬Ę * XßĄĆ`.
	          intro a b
	          simp [BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential,
	            ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_transpose, Matrix.transpose_mul,
	            Matrix.transpose_add, Matrix.mul_assoc, add_comm]
	        ┬Ę -- Differentiate the exact identity
	          -- `det(M * ╬Ę * MßĄĆ) = minkowskiMetricDet d * M.det^2`.
	          -- Equivalently, use the trace identity
	          -- `trace((M0╬ĘM0ßĄĆ)Ōü╗┬╣ *
	          --   (X╬ĘM0ßĄĆ + M0╬ĘXßĄĆ)) = 2 * trace(M0Ōü╗┬╣ * X)`.
	          have htrace :=
	            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_trace_identity
	              (d := d) hM0 X
	          simpa [BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation_deriv,
	            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential,
	            BHW.sourceFullFrameGram_det_eq d M0, htrace,
	            pow_two, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]

	      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_trace_identity
	          (d : Nat)
	          {M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
	          (hM0 : IsUnit M0.det)
	          (X : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé) :
	          Matrix.trace
	              ((BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d M0)Ōü╗┬╣ *
	                ((X * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) * M0.transpose) +
	                  (M0 * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) *
	                    X.transpose))) =
	            (2 : Ōäé) * Matrix.trace (M0Ōü╗┬╣ * X) := by
	        have hGramInv :
	            (BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d M0)Ōü╗┬╣ =
	              (M0.transpose)Ōü╗┬╣ *
	                (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d))Ōü╗┬╣ *
	                M0Ōü╗┬╣ := by
	          -- `sourceFullFrameGram d M0 = M0 * ╬Ę * M0ßĄĆ` and all three
	          -- factors are units.  `╬ĘŌü╗┬╣ = ╬Ę` by `╬ĘŌäé_sq`.
	          exact
	            BHW.matrix_inv_mul_three_of_isUnit
	              (A := M0) (B := ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d))
	              (C := M0.transpose)
	              hM0 (BHW.eta_isUnit d) hM0.transpose
	        calc
	          Matrix.trace
	              ((BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d M0)Ōü╗┬╣ *
	                (X * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) * M0.transpose +
	                  M0 * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) *
	                    X.transpose))
	              =
	            Matrix.trace (M0Ōü╗┬╣ * X) +
	              Matrix.trace ((M0Ōü╗┬╣ * X).transpose) := by
	                simp [BHW.sourceFullFrameGram, hGramInv,
	                  ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_sq,
	                  ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_transpose,
	                  Matrix.trace_add, Matrix.trace_mul_cycle,
	                  Matrix.mul_assoc]
	          _ = (2 : Ōäé) * Matrix.trace (M0Ōü╗┬╣ * X) := by
	            simp [Matrix.trace_transpose, two_mul]

	      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_gram_zero_iff
	          (d : Nat)
	          {M0 X B : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
	          (hM0 : IsUnit M0.det)
	          (hX_eq : X = M0 * B) :
	          (fun a b =>
	            (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential d M0 X).1 a b) = 0 Ōåö
	            B * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) +
	              ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) * B.transpose = 0 := by
	        constructor
	        ┬Ę intro h
	          have hconj :
	              M0Ōü╗┬╣ *
	                ((fun a b =>
	                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential d M0 X).1 a b) :
	                    Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé) *
	                (M0.transpose)Ōü╗┬╣ =
	                B * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) +
	                  ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) * B.transpose := by
	            simp [BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential, hX_eq,
	              Matrix.mul_assoc, ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé_transpose]
	          simpa [h] using hconj
	        ┬Ę intro h
	          ext a b
	          have hconj :=
	            congrArg
	              (fun C =>
	                (M0 * C * M0.transpose) a b) h
	          simpa [BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential, hX_eq,
	            Matrix.mul_assoc] using hconj

	      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_det_zero_iff
	          (d : Nat)
	          {M0 X B : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
	          (hM0 : IsUnit M0.det)
	          (hX_eq : X = M0 * B) :
	          (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential d M0 X).2 = 0 Ōåö
	            Matrix.trace B = 0 := by
	        have hdet_ne : M0.det ŌēĀ 0 := hM0.ne_zero
	        constructor
	        ┬Ę intro h
	          have :
	              M0.det * Matrix.trace B = 0 := by
	            simpa [BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential, hX_eq,
	              Matrix.mul_assoc] using h
	          exact mul_eq_zero.mp this |>.resolve_left hdet_ne
	        ┬Ę intro h
	          simp [BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential, hX_eq, h,
	            Matrix.mul_assoc]

	      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_kernel_eq_orbitTangent
	          ... := by
	        ext X
	        constructor
	        ┬Ę intro hX
	          let B : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé := M0Ōü╗┬╣ * X
	          have hX_eq : X = M0 * B := by
	            simpa [B, Matrix.mul_assoc] using
	              BHW.matrix_eq_mul_inv_mul_of_isUnit_det M0 X hM0
	          have hGram :
	              B * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) +
	                  ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) * B.transpose = 0 := by
	            exact
	              BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_gram_zero_iff
	                (d := d) hM0 hX hX_eq
	          have hTrace : Matrix.trace B = 0 := by
	            exact
	              BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_det_zero_iff
	                (d := d) hM0 hX hX_eq
	          let A : BHW.specialComplexLorentzLieAlgebra d :=
	            Ō¤©B.transpose, by
	              constructor
	              ┬Ę simpa [Matrix.transpose_transpose] using
	                  congrArg Matrix.transpose hGram
	              ┬Ę simpa [Matrix.trace_transpose] using hTraceŌ¤®
	          refine Ō¤©A, ?_Ō¤®
	          simpa [BHW.infinitesimalRightSpecialLorentzAction, A, hX_eq]
	        ┬Ę rintro Ō¤©A, rflŌ¤®
	          ext a b <;>
	          simp [BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential,
	            BHW.infinitesimalRightSpecialLorentzAction,
	            BHW.specialComplexLorentzLieAlgebra,
	            Matrix.mul_assoc, A.property.1, A.property.2]

	      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_constructedGram
	          (d : Nat)
	          {M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
	          (hM0 : IsUnit M0.det)
	          {Y : BHW.SourceFullFrameOrientedCoord d}
	          (hYsym : ŌłĆ a b : Fin (d + 1), Y.1 a b = Y.1 b a) :
	          let G : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé := Matrix.of Y.1
	          let B : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé :=
	            (2 : Ōäé)Ōü╗┬╣ ŌĆó (M0Ōü╗┬╣ * G * (M0.transpose)Ōü╗┬╣ *
	              ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d))
	          Matrix.of
	              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential d M0 (M0 * B)).1 =
	            G := by
	        -- The two Gram summands are both half of
	        -- `M0 * (M0Ōü╗┬╣ * G * (M0.transpose)Ōü╗┬╣) * M0.transpose`.
	        -- Use `╬Ę┬▓ = 1`, `GßĄĆ = G`, `transpose_nonsing_inv`, and the
	        -- left/right inverse identities for `M0` and `M0.transpose`.
	        exact
	          BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_constructedGram
	            (d := d) hM0 hYsym

	      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_constructedDet
	          (d : Nat)
	          {M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
	          (hM0 : IsUnit M0.det)
	          {Y : BHW.SourceFullFrameOrientedCoord d}
	          (hY : Y Ōłł BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedTangentSpace d
	            (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGram d M0)) :
	          let G : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé := Matrix.of Y.1
	          let B : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé :=
	            (2 : Ōäé)Ōü╗┬╣ ŌĆó (M0Ōü╗┬╣ * G * (M0.transpose)Ōü╗┬╣ *
	              ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d))
	          Matrix.of
	              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential d M0 (M0 * B)).1 = G ŌåÆ
	          (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential d M0 (M0 * B)).2 =
	            Y.2 := by
	        -- Apply the trace identity to `X = M0 * B`, rewrite
	        -- `M0Ōü╗┬╣ * (M0 * B) = B`, rewrite the tangent equation by
	        -- `sourceFullFrameGram_det_eq`, and cancel
	        -- `(2 : Ōäé)`, `minkowskiMetricDet d`, and `M0.det`.
	        exact
	          BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_constructedDet
	            (d := d) hM0 hY

	      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_range_eq_tangent
	          ... := by
	        apply le_antisymm
	        ┬Ę exact
	            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_range_subset_tangent
	              (d := d) hM0
	        ┬Ę intro Y hY
	          let G : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé := Matrix.of Y.1
	          let B : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé :=
	            (2 : Ōäé)Ōü╗┬╣ ŌĆó (M0Ōü╗┬╣ * G * (M0.transpose)Ōü╗┬╣ *
	              ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d))
	          refine Ō¤©M0 * B, ?_Ō¤®
	          apply Prod.ext
	          ┬Ę ext a b
	            exact congrArg (fun M => M a b)
	              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_constructedGram
	                (d := d) hM0 (Y := Y) hY.1)
	          ┬Ę exact
	              BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_constructedDet
	                (d := d) hM0 (Y := Y) hY
	                (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_constructedGram
	                  (d := d) hM0 (Y := Y) hY.1)
	      ```

	      This is the only allowed source for the `frame` field of
	      `SourceOrientedFullFrameGaugeChartData`; the proof may not choose a
	      branch of a determinant square root or a Gram-Schmidt process without
	      proving holomorphicity and determinant-sheet compatibility.

      Lean checkpoint, 2026-05-03: the first production slice of this
      full-frame gauge packet is checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedFullFrame.lean`.  The checked layer covers
      the full-frame Gram/oriented-coordinate model, continuity and
      differentiability of `sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord`, the
      determinant-nonzero open sheet, the symmetric coordinate submodule, the
      oriented equation/hypersurface definitions, the special Lorentz Lie
      algebra and orbit tangent, the linearized oriented-equation map
      (`sourceFullFrameOrientedEquationDerivLinear`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedEquationDerivCLM`, determinant-direction
      nonvanishing, and symmetric-coordinate surjectivity), the linearized
      oriented differential
      (`sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferentialLinear` and
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferentialCLM`), the linearized tangent
      submodule, `SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData`, and the differentiability
      of the translated slice map.  The same file now also checks
      `minkowskiMetricDet_mul_self`, `isUnit_minkowskiMetricDet`,
      `minkowskiMetricDet_ne_zero`,
      `sourceFullFrameGram_eq_mul_eta_transpose`,
      `sourceFullFrameGram_det_eq`,
      `sourceFullFrameGram_det_isUnit_of_frame_det_isUnit`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord_mem_hypersurface`, and
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedGramVarietyCoord_subset_hypersurface`, so the
      Cauchy-Binet/image-containment step is no longer a live blocker.  The
      tangent target is also checked as the symmetric-coordinate kernel of
      the linearized equation.  Lean checkpoint, 2026-05-03: the immediate
      derivative implementation is now checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedFullFrameDerivative.lean`, so the
      regular-zero-locus part is no longer blocked by the full Jacobi trace
      formula.  The companion module proves
      `contDiff_sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation`,
      `differentiable_sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation_hasFDerivAt`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation_hasDerivAt_detLine`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation_fderiv_detDirection`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation_fderiv_detDirection_ne_zero`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation_fderiv_surjective_of_det_ne_zero`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation_fderiv_surjectiveOnSymmetric_of_det_ne_zero`,
      `RegularZeroLocusAt`, `RegularZeroLocusInSubmoduleAt`,
      and the noncomputable regularity data
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedHypersurface_regularAt` and
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedHypersurface_regularInSymmetricAt`.

      The proof avoids a premature global determinant Jacobi formula.  First
      prove smoothness of
      `H Ōå” (Matrix.of H.1).det - minkowskiMetricDet d * H.2 ^ 2` by expanding
      `Matrix.det` as a finite sum of products of coordinate projections and
      using `contDiff_snd` for the square.  Then compute the
      one-variable line

      ```lean
      fun t : Ōäé =>
        BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation d
          (H0.toCoord + t ŌĆó BHW.sourceFullFrameDetDirection d)
      ```

      as
      `Matrix.det (Matrix.of H0.gram) -
        minkowskiMetricDet d * (H0.det + t) ^ 2`.  `HasDerivAt.const_sub`
      and `HasDerivAt.pow` give derivative
      `-((2 : Ōäé) * minkowskiMetricDet d * H0.det)` at `0`.  Composing the
      actual `HasFDerivAt` supplied by differentiability with
      `t Ōå” H0.toCoord + t ŌĆó sourceFullFrameDetDirection d` identifies the
      actual Frechet derivative on that direction.  If `H0.det ŌēĀ 0`, this
      value is nonzero, so the actual Frechet derivative is surjective; the
      restricted symmetric-coordinate surjectivity uses the checked
      `sourceFullFrameSymmetricDetDirection`.

      Lean checkpoint, 2026-05-03: the algebraic orbit-kernel half is also
      checked in `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedFullFrameOrbit.lean` as
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_kernel_eq_orbitTangent`.  The proof
      is pure matrix algebra: a kernel vector is written as
      `X = M0 * (M0Ōü╗┬╣ * X)`, the zero Gram-variation equation is conjugated by
      `M0Ōü╗┬╣` and `(M0ßĄĆ)Ōü╗┬╣` to get `B ╬Ę + ╬Ę BßĄĆ = 0`, the determinant-coordinate
      equation gives `trace B = 0`, and `BßĄĆ` is exactly an element of
      `specialComplexLorentzLieAlgebra d`.  The reverse inclusion expands
      `M0 * AßĄĆ` and uses `A.property.1` plus `A.property.2`.

      The same file also checks the trace identity

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_trace_identity
          (d : ŌäĢ)
          {M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
          (hM0 : IsUnit M0.det)
          (X : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé) :
          Matrix.trace
              ((Matrix.of (BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d M0))Ōü╗┬╣ *
                Matrix.of
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential d M0 X).1) =
            (2 : Ōäé) * Matrix.trace (M0Ōü╗┬╣ * X)
      ```

      and uses it to prove
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_range_subset_tangent`.  The same
      file now proves
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_constructedGram`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_constructedDet`, and the
      constructive range equality
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedDifferential_range_eq_tangent`.  It also checks
      the next gauge-slice layer:
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSlice_exists` is a `noncomputable def` choosing a
      complement by `Submodule.exists_isCompl`, not a theorem eliminating a
      proposition into data; `sourceFullFrameSlice_differential_linearEquiv`
      exposes the stored slice equivalence; and
      `sourceFullFrameSlice_restricted_range_eq_tangent` proves the restricted
      differential has exactly the oriented tangent-space range.
      `contDiff_sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMap` is checked too, by composing the
      affine slice translation with
      `contDiff_sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord`.
      Lean checkpoint, 2026-05-03: the full Frechet/Jacobi packet is now
      checked in `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedFullFrameJacobi.lean`.  The
      determinant chain is
      `matrix_det_hasFDerivAt_expansion`,
      `matrix_det_one_add_hasDerivAt_trace`,
      `matrix_det_one_hasFDerivAt_trace`,
      `matrix_det_hasFDerivAt_trace`, and
      `sourceFullFrameDet_hasFDerivAt`; the Gram chain is
      `sourceFullFrameTransposeCLM`,
      `sourceFullFrameGramDifferentialCLM`,
      `sourceFullFrameTranspose_hasFDerivAt`,
      `sourceFullFrameGramMatrix_hasFDerivAt`, and
      `sourceFullFrameGram_hasFDerivAt`.  They assemble as
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedGram_hasFDerivAt`.
      The same file also proves
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedEquation_hasFDerivAt_trace` and
      `mem_sourceFullFrameOrientedTangentSpace_iff_symmetric_and_fderiv_eq_zero`,
      so the local-image theorem may use the actual `fderiv` of the oriented
      hypersurface equation, not a parallel hand-written linearization.

      The same checked Jacobi file now also contains the actual gauge-slice
      derivative bridge: `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMap_zero`,
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMap_mem_varietyCoord`,
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMap_mem_hypersurface`,
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMap_hasFDerivAt`,
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMap_hasStrictFDerivAt`,
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMap_fderiv_eq`,
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMap_fderiv_range_eq_tangent`, and
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMap_fderiv_kernel_eq_bot`.  Therefore the
      local-image theorem no longer has a derivative blocker.

      Lean checkpoint, 2026-05-03: the symmetric hypersurface IFT layer is
      checked in `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedFullFrameLocalImage.lean`.
      The checked declarations are
      `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquation`,
      `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquationDerivCLM`,
      `sourceFullFrameSymmetricBase`,
      `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquation_hasFDerivAt`,
      `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquation_hasStrictFDerivAt`,
      `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquationDerivCLM_range_eq_top_of_det_ne_zero`,
      and `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquation_implicitChart`.  The remaining
      full-frame local-image target is now the comparison theorem between
      this implicit hypersurface chart and the checked gauge-slice map:
      first produce `sourceFullFrameSlice_localImage_eq_hypersurface`, then
      restrict it to `sourceFullFrameSlice_localImage_eq_variety`.

      The same full-frame gauge packet has two different downstream uses, and
      the proof docs must keep them separate.  `sourceOrientedMaxRankChart_at`
      is a chart on the oriented source variety near an arbitrary complex
      max-rank point; it has no `ExtendedTube` hypothesis.  The max-rank
      part of the checked extended-tube local-realization producer is stronger: its
      base point is `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant z0` with
      `z0 Ōłł ExtendedTube`, and the reconstructed vector section must be
      shrunk to remain inside `ExtendedTube`.  Both use the same selected
      full-frame coordinates:

      ```lean
      -- Checked in `SourceOrientedFullFrameChart.lean`; the complement index
      -- now has a `Fintype` instance so the free mixed-row coordinate carries
      -- Mathlib's product normed-vector-space instances.
      def BHW.sourceComplementIndex
          {n : Nat}
          {D : Nat}
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n) : Type :=
        {k : Fin n // k Ōłē Set.range ╬╣}

      instance BHW.sourceComplementIndex_fintype
          {n D : Nat}
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          Fintype (BHW.sourceComplementIndex ╬╣)

      -- Existing checked selected full-frame oriented coordinate.
      def BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoordOfSource
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :
          BHW.SourceFullFrameOrientedCoord d :=
        (fun a b => G.gram (╬╣ a) (╬╣ b), G.det ╬╣)

      def BHW.sourceSelectedMixedRows
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :
          BHW.sourceComplementIndex ╬╣ -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun k a => G.gram k.1 (╬╣ a)

      abbrev BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0) :=
        slice.slice ├Ś
          (BHW.sourceComplementIndex ╬╣ -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)

      noncomputable def BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n :=
        BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
          (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ M0 slice y)

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_mem_variety
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant d n ╬╣ M0 slice y Ōłł
            BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoordOfSource_reconstructInvariant_eq
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoordOfSource d n ╬╣
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y) =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord d
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame d n ╬╣ M0 slice y)

      theorem BHW.sourceSelectedFrameGramMatrix_reconstructInvariant_eq
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice) :
          BHW.sourceSelectedFrameGramMatrix d n ╬╣
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y) =
            Matrix.of
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d
                (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame d n ╬╣ M0 slice y))

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_selectedDet
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice) :
          (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant
              d n ╬╣ M0 slice y).det ╬╣ =
            (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame d n ╬╣ M0 slice y).det

      theorem BHW.sourceSelectedFrameGramMatrix_reconstructInvariant_det_isUnit
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice)
          (hdet :
            (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame
              d n ╬╣ M0 slice y).det ŌēĀ 0) :
          IsUnit
            (BHW.sourceSelectedFrameGramMatrix d n ╬╣
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y)).det

      theorem BHW.sourceSelectedMixedRows_reconstructInvariant_eq
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice)
          (hA_unit :
            IsUnit
              (Matrix.of
                (BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame
                    d n ╬╣ M0 slice y))).det) :
          BHW.sourceSelectedMixedRows d n ╬╣
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y) =
            y.2

      theorem BHW.sourceSelectedMixedRows_reconstructInvariant_eq_of_frame_det_ne_zero
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice)
          (hdet :
            (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame
              d n ╬╣ M0 slice y).det ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.sourceSelectedMixedRows d n ╬╣
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y) =
            y.2

      structure BHW.SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) where
        M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé
        M0_det_unit : IsUnit M0.det
        M0_oriented :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord d M0 =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoordOfSource d n ╬╣ G0
        slice :
          BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0
        kernelChart :
          OpenPartialHomeomorph slice.slice
            (BHW.sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquationDerivCLM d
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord d M0)).ker
        kernelChart_eq :
          (kernelChart : slice.slice ->
            (BHW.sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquationDerivCLM d
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord d M0)).ker) =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceImplicitKernelMap d M0_det_unit slice
        ╬® : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
        ╬®_relOpen :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n ╬®
        ╬®amb : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
        ╬®amb_open : IsOpen ╬®amb
        ╬®_eq_ambient :
          ╬® = ╬®amb Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n
        center_mem : G0 Ōłł ╬®
        center_mem_amb : G0 Ōłł ╬®amb
        ╬®_maxRank :
          ╬® ŌŖå {G | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G}
        chart :
          BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n ->
            BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice
        chart_continuousOn_amb :
          ContinuousOn chart ╬®amb
        chart_open : IsOpen (chart '' ╬®)
        chart_biholomorphic :
          BHW.LocalBiholomorphOnSourceOrientedVariety d n ╬® chart
        inv_formula :
          ŌłĆ y Ōłł chart '' ╬®,
            chart_biholomorphic.inv y =
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y

      -- The constructor theorem
      -- `BHW.sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at` is displayed after
      -- the reconstruction formula below, because its proof depends on the
      -- selected-frame/mixed-row chart inverse and on the ambient-open domain
      -- split.  It is not a forward declaration in production Lean.
      ```

	      The chart map is concrete.  Its first component is the slice parameter
	      returned by `kernelChart.symm` applied to the kernel coordinate of the
	      selected full-frame oriented coordinate under
	      `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquation_implicitChart`; its second component is
	      the mixed row matrix `sourceSelectedMixedRows d n ╬╣ G`.  The inverse
	      first reconstructs the selected full frame from the slice parameter,
	      then reconstructs every unselected vector by the
      coefficient row
      `mixed(k, ┬Ę) * (selectedGram)Ōü╗┬╣`.  The nonselected-nonselected Gram
      entries and every full-frame determinant are then forced by the
      oriented algebraic relations and block determinant expansion.  The
      proof of `chart_biholomorphic.left_inv_on` is therefore the selected
      frame/mixed-row reconstruction theorem plus the oriented relations; the
      proof of `right_inv_on` is the local-image inverse theorem and the
      mixed-row definition.  `chart_open` is the product
      `kernelChart.source ├Ś╦ó Set.univ` after the determinant-nonzero shrink:
      the chart coordinate is the slice point `kernelChart.symm K`, so the
      first model coordinate lives on the source side of the
      `OpenPartialHomeomorph`.

      The reconstruction formula must be an actual definition, because both the
      max-rank chart inverse and the extended-tube shrink depend on its
      holomorphicity.  The selected source labels are read through `╬╣`; every
      other source label is represented as an element of
      `sourceComplementIndex ╬╣`.  If the selected frame is `M` and
      `A := sourceFullFrameGram d M`, then the unselected vector with mixed row
      `m(k,a)` is
      `Ōłæ b, (Ōłæ a, m(k,a) * AŌü╗┬╣ a b) ŌĆó M b`.  This is the only allowed formula:
      an arbitrary vector realizing the mixed row would not give a holomorphic
      chart inverse.

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.sourceSelectedIndexOfMem
          {n D : Nat}
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n)
          {j : Fin n}
          (hj : j Ōłł Set.range ╬╣) : Fin D :=
        Classical.choose hj

      theorem BHW.sourceSelectedIndexOfMem_spec
          {n D : Nat}
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n)
          {j : Fin n}
          (hj : j Ōłł Set.range ╬╣) :
          ╬╣ (BHW.sourceSelectedIndexOfMem ╬╣ hj) = j :=
        Classical.choose_spec hj

      noncomputable def BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice) :
          Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé :=
        M0 + (y.1 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)

      noncomputable def BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun j ╬╝ =>
          if hj : j Ōłł Set.range ╬╣ then
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame
              d n ╬╣ M0 slice y
              (BHW.sourceSelectedIndexOfMem ╬╣ hj) ╬╝
          else
            let k : BHW.sourceComplementIndex ╬╣ := Ō¤©j, hjŌ¤®
            let M :=
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y
            let A : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé :=
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d M
            Ōłæ b : Fin (d + 1),
              (Ōłæ a : Fin (d + 1), y.2 k a * AŌü╗┬╣ a b) * M b ╬╝

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector_selected
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ╬╣
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y) =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame
              d n ╬╣ M0 slice y := by
        ext a ╬╝
        have hmem : ╬╣ a Ōłł Set.range ╬╣ := Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®
        have hidx :
            BHW.sourceSelectedIndexOfMem ╬╣ hmem = a :=
          ╬╣.injective
            (by
              rw [BHW.sourceSelectedIndexOfMem_spec ╬╣ hmem])
        simp [BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector, hmem, hidx]

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector_mixedGram
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice)
          (hA_unit :
            IsUnit
              (Matrix.of
                (BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame
                    d n ╬╣ M0 slice y))).det)
          (k : BHW.sourceComplementIndex ╬╣)
          (a : Fin (d + 1)) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y k.1)
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y (╬╣ a)) =
            y.2 k a

      ```

      The proof of `sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector_mixedGram` is the
      matrix identity `(m * AŌü╗┬╣) * A = m`, using `hA_unit` to rewrite `AŌü╗┬╣ * A`
      and the selected-frame theorem above.  Lean-ready transcript:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector_mixedGram
          ... := by
        let M :=
          BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame d n ╬╣ M0 slice y
        let z :=
          BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ M0 slice y
        let A : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé :=
          Matrix.of (BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d M)
        let coeff : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun b => Ōłæ c : Fin (d + 1), y.2 k c * AŌü╗┬╣ c b
        have hk_not : k.1 Ōłē Set.range ╬╣ := k.2
        have hz_k :
            z k.1 = fun ╬╝ => Ōłæ b : Fin (d + 1), coeff b * M b ╬╝ := by
          ext ╬╝
          simp [z, BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector,
            hk_not, coeff, M, A]
        have hz_sel : z (╬╣ a) = M a := by
          have hsel :=
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector_selected
              d n ╬╣ M0 slice y
          exact congrFun (congrFun hsel a) -- extensional form
        have hinner :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => Ōłæ b : Fin (d + 1), coeff b * M b ╬╝) (M a) =
              Ōłæ b : Fin (d + 1), coeff b * A b a := by
          -- expand `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner` and `sourceFullFrameGram`;
          -- move finite sums with `Finset.mul_sum`, `Finset.sum_mul`, and
          -- `Finset.sum_comm`.
          simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner, BHW.sourceFullFrameGram,
            A, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, Finset.sum_comm,
            mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
        have hrow :
            Ōłæ b : Fin (d + 1), coeff b * A b a = y.2 k a := by
          let row : Matrix (Fin 1) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé :=
            fun _ b => y.2 k b
          have hcancel :
              row * AŌü╗┬╣ * A = row :=
            Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul_cancel_right (A := A) row hA_unit
          have happ := congrFun (congrFun hcancel (0 : Fin 1)) a
          simpa [Matrix.mul_apply, coeff, row, Finset.mul_sum,
            Finset.sum_mul, Finset.sum_comm, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm,
            mul_comm] using happ
        rw [hz_k, hz_sel, hinner, hrow]
      ```

      The theorem statement therefore uses
      `hA_unit : IsUnit (Matrix.of (sourceFullFrameGram d M)).det`, because
      `sourceFullFrameGram d M` is a function-valued coordinate and matrix
      inversion is on `Matrix.of` of that coordinate.  The invariant recovery
      is now split into checked algebra:
      `sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_eq_of_frame_eq_mixedRows_eq`
      proves full recovery once the local-image half supplies selected-frame
      and mixed-row equality, while
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedCoordOfSource_reconstructInvariant_eq`,
      `sourceSelectedFrameGramMatrix_reconstructInvariant_eq`,
      `sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_selectedDet`, and
      `sourceSelectedMixedRows_reconstructInvariant_eq_of_frame_det_ne_zero`
      provide the right-inverse readoff facts.  The local-image constructor is
      therefore responsible only for producing the selected slice point and the
      determinant-nonzero shrink; `SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData`
      must not store `vector_det` as an unexplained field.

      Lean implementation proceeds through a generic coefficient-row packet
      before the `G`-specific Schur theorem is invoked.  Define
      `sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructCoeff` from the same reconstruction data:
      it is the identity row for selected labels and the mixed row
      `y.2 k * AŌü╗┬╣` for unselected labels, with
      `A = Matrix.of (sourceFullFrameGram d reconstructedFrame)`.  The proof
      obligation is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector_eq_sum_coeff_frame
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y :
            slice.slice ├Ś
              (BHW.sourceComplementIndex ╬╣ -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (j : Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ M0 slice y j =
            Ōłæ b : Fin (d + 1),
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructCoeff
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y j b ŌĆó
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame d n ╬╣ M0 slice y b

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructMatrix_eq_coeff_mul
          ... (╬║ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ╬║
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ M0 slice y) =
            (fun a b =>
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructCoeff
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y (╬║ a) b) *
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame d n ╬╣ M0 slice y

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructDet_eq_coeff_det_mul
          ... (╬║ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬║
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ M0 slice y) =
            (Matrix.det
              (fun a b =>
                BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructCoeff
                  d n ╬╣ M0 slice y (╬║ a) b)) *
            (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame d n ╬╣ M0 slice y).det
      ```

      The proofs are by the selected/unselected case split in the definition of
      `sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector`, followed by `Matrix.mul_apply`
      and `Matrix.det_mul`.  They do not use the oriented source variety yet;
      they only expose the algebraic spine to which the oriented determinant
      relation will attach.  The companion Gram-expansion theorem is similarly
      pure reconstruction algebra:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector_gram_eq_coeff_gram
          ... (i j : Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ M0 slice y i)
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ M0 slice y j) =
            Ōłæ a : Fin (d + 1), Ōłæ b : Fin (d + 1),
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructCoeff
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y i a *
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructCoeff
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y j b *
              Matrix.of
                (BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame
                    d n ╬╣ M0 slice y)) a b
      ```

      This follows from
      `sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector_eq_sum_coeff_frame`,
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_left`, and
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_right`.  The Schur theorem later
      identifies this coefficient Gram expression with the input `G.gram`.

      The determinant/Schur algebra used in that last sentence must be exposed
      before implementation.  Fix a selected full frame `╬╣`, write
      `A := G.gram (╬╣ ┬Ę) (╬╣ ┬Ę)`, and for any source label `j` define the
      coefficient row of `j` relative to the selected frame by the identity row
      when `j = ╬╣ a`, and by `G.gram j (╬╣ ┬Ę) * AŌü╗┬╣` otherwise.  The reconstructed
      tuple is exactly the selected frame multiplied by this coefficient matrix.
      The following surfaces are the precise recovery obligations:

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé

      def BHW.sourceCoeffGramFromSelected
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
          (i j : Fin n) : Ōäé :=
        Ōłæ a : Fin (d + 1),
          Ōłæ b : Fin (d + 1),
            BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff d n ╬╣ G i a *
              G.gram (╬╣ a) (╬╣ b) *
              BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff d n ╬╣ G j b

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_schurGram_eq_of_selectedDet_ne_zero
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (h╬╣ : G.det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0) :
          ŌłĆ i j,
            G.gram i j =
              BHW.sourceCoeffGramFromSelected d n ╬╣ G i j

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_det_eq_coeff_det_selected
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ ╬║ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (h╬╣ : G.det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0) :
          (Matrix.of
              (fun a b =>
                BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff d n ╬╣ G (╬║ a) b)).det *
            G.det ╬╣ =
          G.det ╬║

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector_eq_of_frame_eq_mixedRows_eq
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hframe :
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame d n ╬╣ M0 slice y =
              BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ╬╣ z)
          (hmixed :
            y.2 =
              BHW.sourceSelectedMixedRows d n ╬╣
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z))
          (hdet :
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z).det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ M0 slice y = z

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_eq_of_frame_eq_mixedRows_eq
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0)
          (y : BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ M0 slice)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hframe :
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructFrame d n ╬╣ M0 slice y =
              BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ╬╣ z)
          (hmixed :
            y.2 =
              BHW.sourceSelectedMixedRows d n ╬╣
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z))
          (hdet :
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z).det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice y) =
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z
      ```

      Proof transcript.  `sourceOrientedVariety_schurGram_eq_of_selectedDet_ne_zero`
      is the rank-`D` Schur complement theorem in coordinates.  Since
      `G.det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0`, the Cauchy-Binet oriented relation gives
      `det A = minkowskiMetricDet d * (G.det ╬╣)^2`, hence `A` is invertible.
      For a realized tuple `z` with invariant `G`, the selected frame spans the
      full `D = d + 1` ambient source span, so every source vector has coefficient
      row `G.gram j (╬╣ ┬Ę) * AŌü╗┬╣`; expanding the bilinear form gives
      `sourceCoeffGramFromSelected`.  In Lean this should be proved either by
      the checked rectangular Schur-complement theorem at full rank or directly
      from `row * AŌü╗┬╣ * A = row`; it may not appeal to a generic "same
      coordinates" slogan.

      For determinants, define the coefficient matrix
      `C╬║ a b := sourceSelectedFrameCoeff d n ╬╣ G (╬║ a) b`.  The reconstructed
      selected-frame matrix for `╬║` is `C╬║ * M╬╣`, hence its determinant is
      `det C╬║ * det M╬╣`.  The oriented relation for the mixed Gram minor
      `det(G[╬║,╬╣]) = minkowskiMetricDet d * G.det ╬║ * G.det ╬╣`, together with
      `G[╬║,╬╣] = C╬║ * A`, gives
      `det C╬║ * det A = minkowskiMetricDet d * G.det ╬║ * G.det ╬╣`.  Substitute
      `det A = minkowskiMetricDet d * (G.det ╬╣)^2` and cancel the nonzero
      constants and `G.det ╬╣` to obtain
      `det C╬║ * G.det ╬╣ = G.det ╬║`.  This is the sign-sensitive step: the
      determinant coordinate `G.det ╬╣`, not a square root of `det A`, fixes the
      special-orthogonal sheet.

      Lean-facing proof skeleton for the Schur and determinant subtheorems:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff_vector_expansion_of_invariant
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (h╬╣ :
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z).det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0) :
          ŌłĆ j,
            z j =
              Ōłæ a : Fin (d + 1),
                BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff d n ╬╣
                  (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z) j a ŌĆó
                BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ╬╣ z a := by
        intro j
        by_cases hj : j Ōłł Set.range ╬╣
        ┬Ę rcases hj with Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®
          simp [BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff,
            BHW.sourceSelectedIndexOfMem_spec]
        ┬Ę have hA_unit :
              IsUnit (BHW.sourceSelectedFrameGramMatrix d n ╬╣
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z)).det := by
            exact
              BHW.sourceSelectedFrameGramMatrix_det_isUnit_of_variety_det_ne_zero
                d n ╬╣ Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤® h╬╣
          -- pair the difference with every selected frame row, use
          -- `row * AŌü╗┬╣ * A = row`, then use the checked spanning theorem
          -- `sourceFullFrameMatrix_rows_span_top_of_det_ne_zero`.
          ...

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_schurGram_eq_of_selectedDet_ne_zero
          ... := by
        rcases hGvar with Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®
        have hvec :=
          BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff_vector_expansion_of_invariant
            d n ╬╣ z h╬╣
        intro i j
        rw [hvec i, hvec j]
        simp [BHW.sourceCoeffGramFromSelected,
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner,
          Finset.sum_mul, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_comm,
          mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]

      theorem BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff_matrix_eq_frame_of_invariant
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ ╬║ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (h╬╣ :
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z).det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ╬║ z =
            (fun a b =>
              BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff d n ╬╣
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z) (╬║ a) b) *
            BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ╬╣ z := by
        ext a ╬╝
        simpa [Matrix.mul_apply, BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix] using
          congrFun
            (BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff_vector_expansion_of_invariant
              d n ╬╣ z h╬╣ (╬║ a)) ╬╝

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_det_eq_coeff_det_selected
          ... := by
        rcases hGvar with Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®
        have h╬╣z :
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z ŌēĀ 0 := by
          simpa [BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant] using h╬╣
        have hmat :=
          BHW.sourceSelectedFrameCoeff_matrix_eq_frame_of_invariant
            d n ╬╣ ╬║ z h╬╣
        have hdet := congrArg Matrix.det hmat
        -- `hdet` is
        -- `sourceFullFrameDet ╬║ z =
        --   det coeff * sourceFullFrameDet ╬╣ z`.
        simpa [BHW.sourceFullFrameDet, Matrix.det_mul,
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant, mul_comm, mul_left_comm,
          mul_assoc] using hdet.symm
      ```

      The support theorem
      `sourceVector_expand_in_selectedFrame_by_gramInverse` is ordinary linear
      algebra for a nondegenerate selected frame: the coefficient row
      `Ō¤¬z_j, z_╬╣┬ĘŌ¤½ * AŌü╗┬╣` is the unique row whose linear combination of selected
      frame vectors pairs correctly with every selected frame vector; since the
      selected vectors form a basis, equality of all those pairings gives vector
      equality.  It can be proved by transporting the selected frame matrix to
      a `LinearEquiv (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)` and using
      `Matrix.mul_inv_of_isUnit_det`/`Matrix.inv_mul_of_isUnit_det`.  This
      support is finite-dimensional source algebra and belongs with the
      full-frame chart file, not with OS, EOW, or PET.

      The constructor of `SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData` is then a
      concrete local-product chart.  The selected full-frame coordinate is
      controlled by the full-frame local-image theorem; the mixed rows are free
      coordinates.  The target must be shrunk to the determinant-nonzero sheet
      before defining the ambient domain, because the reconstruction formula
      uses the inverse selected Gram block and because max-rank follows from
      `G.det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0`.

      ```lean
      def BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoord_detNonzero
          (d : Nat) : Set (BHW.SourceFullFrameOrientedCoord d) :=
        {H | H.2 ŌēĀ 0}

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoord_detNonzero_open
          (d : Nat) :
          IsOpen (BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoord_detNonzero d)

	      -- There is no generic local-image wrapper layer in the current Lean
	      -- route.  Shrinking is performed directly on the checked
	      -- `OpenPartialHomeomorph` source/target sets of
	      -- `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceImplicitKernelOpenPartialHomeomorph`, after
	      -- transporting hypersurface points through
	      -- `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquation_implicitChart_snd`.

      abbrev BHW.FullFrameSliceMap
          (d : Nat)
          (M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé)
          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0) :
          slice.slice -> BHW.SourceFullFrameOrientedCoord d :=
        fun X =>
          BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord d
            (M0 + (X : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé))

	      abbrev BHW.FullFrameKernelChart
	          (d : Nat)
	          {M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
	          (hM0 : IsUnit M0.det)
	          (slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0) :=
	        BHW.sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceImplicitKernelOpenPartialHomeomorph
	          d hM0 slice

	      -- The ambient max-rank domain is the intersection of three checked
	      -- conditions: the selected determinant is nonzero, the selected
	      -- full-frame coordinate is in the symmetric implicit-chart source, and
	      -- its kernel coordinate lies in `FullFrameKernelChart.target`.  In Lean
	      -- this definition must carry the proof that the selected coordinate is
	      -- symmetric from the current source-variety membership; it is not a
	      -- total predicate on arbitrary ambient coordinates.

	      -- `sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_ambientDomain_open` is proved from
	      -- openness of the selected determinant sheet, the implicit chart source,
	      -- and `kernelChart.open_target`, pulled back along the continuous
	      -- selected-coordinate map and the continuous implicit-chart coordinate.
	      -- `sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_map` sends `G` to
	      -- `(kernelChart.symm selectedKernelCoord, sourceSelectedMixedRows d n ╬╣ G)`.
	      -- Its differentiability on the ambient domain is the product of
	      -- differentiability of `kernelChart.symm` on `kernelChart.target`,
	      -- differentiability of the selected implicit-coordinate map, and
	      -- linearity of the mixed-row projection.

	      -- The remaining max-rank chart helper statements should be written in
	      -- terms of the checked `FullFrameKernelChart`.  Their product image is
	      -- `kernelChart.source ├Ś╦ó Set.univ`: the selected hypersurface
	      -- coordinate is first transported to a kernel coordinate by
	      -- `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquation_implicitChart_snd`, then mapped
	      -- back to a slice coordinate by `kernelChart.symm`.  The local
	      -- biholomorph inverse uses the slice coordinate directly and the
	      -- mixed-row reconstruction formula below for all unselected rows.
	      -- The max-rank-domain theorem assumes the selected determinant-nonzero
	      -- shrink explicitly; it does not refer to a `W` field.
	      ```

	      Proof obligations for these chart helpers are finite-dimensional and
	      local.  The target shrink intersects `kernelChart.target` with the
	      transported determinant-nonzero condition and replaces
	      `kernelChart.source` by the corresponding preimage under the
	      implicit-kernel map.  `sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_image_eq_product` has
	      two directions.  For `G Ōłł ╬®`, the selected coordinate's kernel coordinate
	      lies in `kernelChart.target`, so `kernelChart.symm` is in
	      `kernelChart.source` and the mixed component is unrestricted.
	      Conversely, given `(X,m) Ōłł kernelChart.source ├Ś univ`, reconstruct a
	      source tuple from the selected frame `M0 + X` and mixed rows `m`;
	      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceImplicitKernelMap_left_inv_on_chartSource`
	      gives the selected coordinate, and the reconstruction theorem gives an
	      oriented source-variety point whose chart is exactly `(X,m)`.  This
	      converse is where
	      `sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_eq_of_frame_eq_mixedRows_eq`
	      is consumed.
	      `chart_open` is then `kernelChart.open_source.prod isOpen_univ` after
	      the explicit shrink.

      The local biholomorph proof is the same product calculation.  `left_inv_on`
      says that reconstructing from `chart G` returns `G`; this is the
      source-invariant reconstruction theorem.  `right_inv_on` says that the
	      selected part is fixed by the kernel-chart right inverse, while the mixed rows are fixed
	      by the definition of the reconstructed unselected vectors.  The inverse
	      is differentiable on the open product `kernelChart.source ├Ś univ`
	      because the selected frame map is polynomial in `X`, matrix inverse is holomorphic on the
      determinant-nonzero sheet, and the mixed-row reconstruction is a finite
      sum of products.  No choice of a vector realization occurs after the chart
      is built.

      With these helpers the full-frame chart constructor is an assembly
      theorem, not a black box:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0_var : G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (h╬╣ : G0.det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData d n ╬╣ G0 := by
        classical
        rcases hG0_var with Ō¤©z0, rflŌ¤®
        let M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé :=
          BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ╬╣ z0
        have hM0_det :
            IsUnit M0.det := by
          exact isUnit_iff_ne_zero.mpr h╬╣
        have hM0_coord :
            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord d M0 =
              BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoordOfSource d n ╬╣
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0) := by
          ext a b <;>
          simp [M0, BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoordOfSource,
            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord,
            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGram,
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGram,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram, BHW.sourceFullFrameDet]
        let slice : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0 :=
          BHW.sourceFullFrameGaugeSlice_exists d hM0_det
	        let L0 : BHW.FullFrameKernelChart d hM0_det slice :=
	          BHW.sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceImplicitKernelOpenPartialHomeomorph
	            d hM0_det slice
        have hy0_det :
            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord d M0 Ōłł
              BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoord_detNonzero d := by
          simpa [BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedCoord_detNonzero,
            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGramCoord,
            BHW.sourceFullFrameOrientedGram, M0] using h╬╣
	        -- The next implementation step is to shrink `L0.source` and
	        -- `L0.target` directly so the transported selected-frame coordinate
	        -- stays in the determinant-nonzero sheet.  The shrink is an
	        -- `OpenPartialHomeomorph` source/target restriction, not a generic
	        -- local-image wrapper call.
	        let L := L0
	        let ╬®amb :=
	          BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_ambientDomain
	            d n ╬╣ M0 slice L
        let ╬® := ╬®amb Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n
        let chart :=
          BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_map d n ╬╣ M0 slice L
        refine
          { M0 := M0
            M0_det_unit := hM0_det
            M0_oriented := hM0_coord
            slice := slice
	            kernelChart := L
            kernelChart_eq := by
              exact
                BHW.sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceImplicitKernelOpenPartialHomeomorph_coe
                  d hM0_det slice
            ╬® := ╬®
            ╬®_relOpen := by
              exact
                BHW.isRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety_of_ambientOpen
                  (d := d) (n := n)
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_ambientDomain_open
                    d n ╬╣ M0 slice L)
            ╬®amb := ╬®amb
            ╬®amb_open :=
              BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_ambientDomain_open
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice L
            ╬®_eq_ambient := rfl
            center_mem := by
              refine Ō¤©?_, Ō¤©z0, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®
              simpa [╬®amb, BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_ambientDomain,
                hM0_coord.symm] using L.y0_mem
            center_mem_amb := by
              simpa [╬®amb, BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_ambientDomain,
                hM0_coord.symm] using L.y0_mem
            ╬®_maxRank :=
              BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_domain_maxRank
                d n hn ╬╣ M0 slice L
                (by
                  intro H hH
                  exact hH.2)
            ╬®_selectedDetNonzero :=
              BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_domain_selectedDetNonzero
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice L
            chart := chart
            chart_eq_candidate := by
              funext G
              rfl
            ╬®_selectedSymmetric_mem_implicitSource :=
              BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_selectedSymmetric_mem_implicitSource
                d n ╬╣ M0 hM0_det slice L
            ╬®_selectedKernel_mem_target :=
              BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_selectedKernel_mem_target
                d n ╬╣ M0 hM0_det slice L
            ╬®_gaugeSlice_mem_implicitSource :=
              BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_gaugeSlice_mem_implicitSource
                d n ╬╣ M0 hM0_det slice L
            chart_continuousOn_amb :=
              BHW.SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.chartCandidate_continuousOn_╬®amb
                shrink
	            chart_open := by
	              -- Product openness of the explicitly shrunken
	              -- `kernelChart.source ├Ś╦ó univ`.
	              exact BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_image_open
	                d n ╬╣ M0 slice L
            chart_biholomorphic :=
              BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_localBiholomorph
                d n ╬╣ M0 slice L
            inv_formula := by
              intro y hy
              rfl }
      ```

      The nontrivial finite-dimensional input underneath this constructor is
      now the model-open theorem consumed by
      `SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.toFullFrameMaxRankChartData_of_modelOpen`.
	      It starts from the ambient shrink, chooses an open model neighborhood
	      `V` of the center, proves `V ŌŖå kernelChart.source ├Ś╦ó Set.univ`, proves
	      the reconstructed selected determinant is nonzero on `V`, proves
	      reconstruction maps `V` into the old relative domain, and proves the
	      explicit reconstruction map is differentiable and continuous on `V`.
	      The Lean-ready choice is
	      `V = {y | y.1 Ōłł kernelChart.source}
	        Ōł® ((sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant d n ╬╣ M0 S) Ōü╗┬╣' T.╬®amb
	            Ōł® sourceFullFrameGaugeModelDetNonzero d n ╬╣ M0 S)`.
	      Its openness follows from `kernelChart.open_source.preimage
	      continuous_fst`, `sourceFullFrameGaugeModelDetNonzero_open`, and
	      `continuousOn_iff'` applied to
	      `continuousOn_sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_on_modelDetNonzero`.
	      The center belongs to `V` because the selected kernel coordinate of
	      `G0` is zero, `kernelChart.symm 0 = 0`, the reconstructed selected frame
	      is `M0`, and
	      `T.reconstructInvariant_chartCandidate_eq_of_mem_relDomain
	      (T.center_mem_relDomain hG0)` puts the reconstructed invariant back at
	      `G0 Ōłł T.╬®amb`.
	      The checked method
	      `SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData.reconstructInvariant_chart_eq_of_mem_╬®_from_fields`
      supplies the stored-chart left-inverse invariant equality, while
      `SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.chartCandidate_reconstructInvariant_eq_of_chartSource_frameDet`
      supplies the model-side right inverse.
      The packet fields `╬®_selectedDetNonzero`,
      `╬®_selectedSymmetric_mem_implicitSource`,
      `╬®_selectedKernel_mem_target`,
      `╬®_gaugeSlice_mem_implicitSource`, and `chart_eq_candidate` record the
      shrink and formula facts explicitly, so downstream code no longer asks
      the local-image theorem to rediscover determinant nonzero or the
      implicit-chart source memberships.  The ambient chart formula uses
      `sourceFullFrameSelectedSymmetricCoordAmbient` and
      `sourceFullFrameSelectedKernelCoordAmbient`; on the source variety these
      are checked equal to the proof-carrying selected symmetric and kernel
      coordinates.

      This package gives the arbitrary complex max-rank chart used in the
      identity principle:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_sourceOrientedFullFrameDet_ne_zero_of_maxRank
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0_var : G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hG0_max : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0) :
          Ōłā ╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n, G0.det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0 := by
        rcases hG0_var with Ō¤©z0, rflŌ¤®
        have hfull :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) = d + 1 := by
          simpa [BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt, min_eq_left hn] using hG0_max
        rcases
          BHW.exists_sourceFullFrameDet_ne_zero_of_sourceGramRank_eq_spacetime
            d n z0 hfull with
          Ō¤©╬╣, h╬╣Ō¤®
        exact Ō¤©╬╣, h╬╣Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData.to_maxRankChart
          {d n : Nat}
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          {╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n}
          (C :
            BHW.SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData d n ╬╣ G0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData d n
            (M := BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChartModel d n ╬╣ C.M0 C.slice)
            G0 :=
        { ╬® := C.╬®
          ╬®_relOpen := C.╬®_relOpen
          center_mem := C.center_mem
          ╬®_maxRank := C.╬®_maxRank
          chart := C.chart
          chart_open := C.chart_open
          chart_biholomorphic := C.chart_biholomorphic }

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedMaxRankChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0_var : G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hG0_max : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData d n G0 := by
        rcases
          BHW.exists_sourceOrientedFullFrameDet_ne_zero_of_maxRank
            (d := d) hd n hn hG0_var hG0_max with
          Ō¤©╬╣, h╬╣Ō¤®
        exact
          (BHW.sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at
            (d := d) hd n hn hG0_var ╬╣ h╬╣).to_maxRankChart
      ```

      The max-rank extended-tube local section is the same full-frame source
      chart with two additional ingredients: an ambient holomorphicity theorem
      for the explicit section formula, and an open tube shrink.  Starting from
      `z0 Ōłł ExtendedTube d n`, choose `╬╣` with
      `sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z0 ŌēĀ 0` and construct the source-based chart
      `sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at_source ╬╣ z0 h╬╣`.  This
      source-based constructor is important: its checked center theorem says
      the reconstructed vector at `G0 := sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
      z0` is exactly `z0`, so the tube shrink uses `hz0` directly and does not
      need an arbitrary ambient source-matrix transport.

      The remaining full-frame analytic theorem is not tube stability.  It is
      differentiability of the implicit-kernel inverse after composing with the
      ambient selected-kernel coordinate.  Production Lean already has the
      strict derivative at the base and the local homeomorphism
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceImplicitKernelOpenPartialHomeomorph`; the
      local-section proof still needs the open-neighborhood regularity theorem
      below before the scalar descent can consume this chart.  The reusable
      inverse-chart differentiability wrapper
      `SCV.openPartialHomeomorph_symm_differentiableOn_of_hasFDerivAt` is now
      checked; the remaining work is to feed it the full-frame-specific
      derivative-invertibility and smoothness facts on the selected ambient
      shrink.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.chartCandidate_differentiableOn_╬®amb
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n}
          {M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
          {hM0 : IsUnit M0.det}
          {S : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0}
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (T :
            BHW.SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink d n ╬╣ hM0 S G0) :
          DifferentiableOn Ōäé
            (BHW.SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.chartCandidate
              (d := d) (n := n) (╬╣ := ╬╣) hM0 S)
            T.╬®amb

      theorem BHW.SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.reconstructVector_chartCandidate_differentiableOn_╬®amb
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n}
          {M0 : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé}
          {hM0 : IsUnit M0.det}
          {S : BHW.SourceFullFrameGaugeSliceData d M0}
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (T :
            BHW.SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink d n ╬╣ hM0 S G0) :
          DifferentiableOn Ōäé
            (fun G =>
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ M0 S
                (BHW.SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.chartCandidate
                  (d := d) (n := n) (╬╣ := ╬╣) hM0 S G))
            T.╬®amb

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at_source_reconstructVector_differentiableOn_ambient
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n}
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h╬╣ : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z0 ŌēĀ 0) :
          let C :=
            BHW.sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at_source ╬╣ z0 h╬╣
          DifferentiableOn Ōäé
            (fun G =>
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector d n ╬╣ C.M0 C.slice
                (C.chart G))
            C.╬®amb

      theorem BHW.SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.tubeShrink_at_source
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n}
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (h╬╣ : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z0 ŌēĀ 0) :
          let C :=
            BHW.sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at_source ╬╣ z0 h╬╣
          Ōłā ╬®tube : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n),
            IsOpen ╬®tube Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 Ōłł ╬®tube Ōł¦
            ╬®tube ŌŖå C.╬®amb Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ G Ōłł ╬®tube,
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector
                  d n ╬╣ C.M0 C.slice (C.chart G) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)

      theorem BHW.SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData.shrink_to_extendedTube
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n}
          (h╬╣ : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z0 ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedLocalHolomorphicSectionData d n
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0) := by
        let C :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at_source ╬╣ z0 h╬╣
        rcases
          BHW.SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.tubeShrink_at_source
            (d := d) n hz0 h╬╣ with
          Ō¤©╬®tube, h╬®tube_open, hcenter, h╬®tube_sub, hsection_memŌ¤®
        let toVec :
            BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n ->
              Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun G =>
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructVector
              d n ╬╣ C.M0 C.slice (C.chart G)
        refine
          { ╬® := ╬®tube
            ╬®_open := h╬®tube_open
            center_mem := hcenter
            toVec := toVec
            toVec_mem := by
              intro G hG
              exact hsection_mem G hG
            toVec_right_inv := by
              intro G hG
              have hG_C╬® : G Ōłł C.╬® := by
                rw [C.╬®_eq_ambient]
                exact Ō¤©h╬®tube_sub hG.1, hG.2Ō¤®
              have hG_var :
                  G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
                rw [C.╬®_eq_ambient] at hG_C╬®
                exact hG_C╬®.2
              rcases
                BHW.sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_localImage_kernel_mixed_eq
                  d n ╬╣ C G hG_C╬® with
                Ō¤©hsel_source, hchart_source, hkernel, hmixed, hdetŌ¤®
              simpa [toVec] using
                BHW.sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_eq_of_kernel_eq_mixedRows_eq
                  d n ╬╣ C.M0_det_unit C.slice hG_var hdet (C.chart G)
                  hsel_source hchart_source hkernel hmixed
            toVec_holomorphic := by
              exact
                (BHW.sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at_source_reconstructVector_differentiableOn_ambient
                  (d := d) n h╬╣).mono h╬®tube_sub }

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedMaxRank_localSection_fullFrame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (h╬╣ :
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z0 ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedLocalHolomorphicSectionData d n
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0) := by
        have hG0_var :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 Ōłł
              BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n :=
          Ō¤©z0, rflŌ¤®
        have h╬╣G :
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0).det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0 := h╬╣
        exact
          BHW.SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData.shrink_to_extendedTube
            (d := d) hd n hn hz0 h╬╣
      ```

      The ambient-domain split in this transcript is mandatory.  `C.╬®` is a
      relatively open chart domain in the oriented variety; it cannot be the
      open set used by `DifferentiableOn` unless the variety has ambient
      interior.  `C.╬®amb` is the ambient open neighborhood on which the
      coordinate formula and reconstructed vector are holomorphic, with
      `C.╬® = C.╬®amb Ōł® sourceOrientedGramVariety d n`.  The theorem
      `tubeShrink_at_source` is just continuity of the reconstructed vector on
      the source-based ambient chart plus openness of `ExtendedTube`: the
      checked center theorem
      `sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at_source_reconstructVector_center`
      rewrites the reconstructed center to the original `z0`, so `hz0` gives
      center membership in the tube; then shrink the ambient neighborhood so
      every reconstructed vector remains in `ExtendedTube`.  This avoids the
      invalid shortcut "relative open chart domain is open" and keeps the
      identity-principle chart separate from the scalar-descent local section.

      Current checked max-rank inverse boundary, 2026-05-04: the future local
      image theorem should be named and proved in the kernel/mixed-row form
      shown above, not in the older selected-frame-equality form.  Production
      Lean now proves that a source selected coordinate and a gauge-slice
      coordinate in the symmetric implicit-chart source are equal as
      full-frame oriented coordinates once their kernel coordinates agree:
      `sourceFullFrameSelectedImplicitChart_eq_zero_kernelCoord`,
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceMapSymmetric_implicitChart_eq_zero_kernelMap`,
      `sourceFullFrameOrientedCoordOfSource_eq_gaugeSliceMap_of_kernel_eq`,
      and
