      `sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_selectedCoord_eq_of_kernel_eq`.
      It also proves that selected coordinate plus mixed rows determine all
      oriented Gram data on the determinant-nonzero sheet:
      `sourceSelectedFrameCoeff_eq_of_selectedCoord_eq_mixedRows_eq`,
      `sourceCoeffGramFromSelected_eq_of_selectedCoord_eq_mixedRows_eq`,
      `sourceOrientedGramData_eq_of_selectedCoord_eq_mixedRows_eq`, and
      `sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant_eq_of_kernel_eq_mixedRows_eq`.
      Thus `sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_localImage_kernel_mixed_eq` must only
      supply the source-membership/shrink facts, the kernel-coordinate identity
      induced by the local inverse of
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceImplicitKernelMap`, the definitional mixed-row
      choice for `C.chart G`, and the determinant-nonzero shrink.  The Schur
      algebra and determinant recovery are no longer blockers for this step.

      Current checked shrink boundary, 2026-05-04: the ambient-open shrink
      itself is now a production Lean object.  The symmetric implicit chart
      source is exposed as a neighborhood of `sourceFullFrameSymmetricBase`
      by `sourceFullFrameSymmetricBase_mem_implicitChart_source` and
      `sourceFullFrameSymmetricEquation_implicitChart_source_mem_nhds_base`.
      The selected ambient coordinate is checked to hit this base at `G0`
      when `M0` represents the selected coordinate of `G0`, and its kernel
      coordinate is checked to be `0` there:
      `sourceFullFrameSelectedSymmetricCoordAmbient_eq_base_of_oriented_eq`,
      `sourceFullFrameSelectedSymmetricCoordAmbient_eventually_mem_implicitSource`,
      `sourceFullFrameSelectedKernelCoordAmbient_eq_zero_at_base`, and
      `continuousAt_sourceFullFrameSelectedKernelCoordAmbient_at_base`.
      The kernel-chart target and lifted-gauge implicit-source shrink facts
      are checked as
      `sourceFullFrameSelectedKernelCoordAmbient_eventually_mem_kernelTarget`,
      `sourceFullFrameGaugeSliceImplicitKernel_symm_implicitSource_mem_nhds_zero`,
      and
      `sourceFullFrameSelectedKernelCoordAmbient_eventually_gaugeSlice_mem_implicitSource`.
      Finally,
      `SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink` and
      `sourceFullFrameMaxRankChart_ambientShrink` package an actual open
      ambient neighborhood satisfying all three source/target membership
      fields plus the selected determinant-nonzero field.  The shrink now also
      carries the relative domain API
      `SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.relDomain`,
      `relDomain_relOpen`, and `center_mem_relDomain`, together with the exact
      chart formula
      `SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.chartCandidate`,
      its two projection readoffs, and the checked left inverse
      `reconstructInvariant_chartCandidate_eq_of_mem_relDomain`.  The same
      file now proves the determinant-to-rank bridge
      `sourceOrientedMaxRankAt_of_selectedDet_ne_zero`, the relative-domain
      max-rank field `maxRank_of_mem_relDomain`, chart continuity on both the
      ambient and relative domains, and the model-side right inverse
      `chartCandidate_reconstructInvariant_eq_of_chartSource_frameDet`.
      The checked assembly lemmas are
      `SourceFullFrameMaxRankChartAmbientShrink.localBiholomorph`,
      `exists_restrict_modelOpen_image_eq`,
      `toFullFrameMaxRankChartData`, and
      `toFullFrameMaxRankChartData_of_modelOpen`.  Therefore the remaining
	      constructor proof should consume this shrink directly and use the
	      canonical model-coordinate set
	      `V = {y | y.1 Ōłł kernelChart.source}
	        Ōł® ((sourceFullFrameGauge_reconstructInvariant d n ╬╣ M0 S) Ōü╗┬╣' T.╬®amb
	            Ōł® sourceFullFrameGaugeModelDetNonzero d n ╬╣ M0 S)`.
	      This set is open by the kernel-chart source openness, the checked
	      determinant-nonzero model openness, and reconstruction continuity on the
	      determinant-nonzero locus.  Its three membership projections give the
	      source, determinant, and old-domain hypotheses of
	      `toFullFrameMaxRankChartData_of_modelOpen`.  Reconstruction
	      differentiability is the finite-dimensional rational-map theorem on the
	      same determinant-nonzero locus: matrix inverse is `detŌü╗┬╣ ŌĆó adjugate`,
	      and all remaining coordinates are finite sums/products.  The checked
	      model-open assembly then gives `chart_open`, the local biholomorph
	      packet, max-rank, and the full
	      `SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData` fields.

      Thus the previously named `sourceOrientedMaxRankChart_at` is not a
      black box: it is the source-variety version of the full-frame gauge
      chart, while `sourceOrientedMaxRank_localSection_fullFrame` is the same
      construction after shrinking the reconstructed vector map into the
      extended tube.  This separation is load-bearing for the proof order:
      the identity principle can use max-rank charts without an extended-tube
      basepoint, while scalar descent needs the stronger tube-valued section.

      The rank-deficient half of the extended-tube local-realization producer
      is different from the max-rank local section.  It is not a local
      holomorphic section theorem; it is a local image-realization theorem plus
      continuity/local-boundedness support for the quotient scalar value:

      ```lean
      structure BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientRealizationData
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) where
        ╬® : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
        ╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®
        center_mem :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 Ōłł ╬®
        realize :
          ŌłĆ G, G Ōłł ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ->
            {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé //
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z = G}

      def BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChartProducer
          (d n : Nat)
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartProducer d n

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_relOpen_connected
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n
            (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n) Ōł¦
          IsConnected (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
      ```

      The rank-deficient proof consumes the ordinary Hall-Wightman Lemma-3
      adapted-normal-form machinery, but it does not stop at ordinary Gram
      realization.  Its concrete obligations are:

      * choose the adapted representative with the same ordinary Gram as
        `z0`, using the ordinary Lemma-3 selected-span and residual
        isotropic-frame data;
      * add residual variables in the common isotropic frame, so the map from
        residual coefficients to the full oriented invariant has the correct
        image germ inside `sourceOrientedGramVariety`;
      * prove that every nearby oriented-variety point in `╬®` is represented
        by one of those residual-variable tuples, not by an arbitrary lift of
        the ordinary Gram data;
      * shrink the residual coefficient polydisc using the Hall-Wightman
        coefficient-freedom theorem so all these tuples remain in
        `ExtendedTube d n`;
      * expose max-rank parameter density in the same original-coordinate
        image, so the later quotient-descent proof can use this family for
        exceptional-rank control.

      The previous `realize` field is not strong enough by itself to prove
      continuity or local boundedness of the quotient scalar value, because it
      contains no controlled choice of lifts.  The proof must first build the
      following residual-coordinate chart and then derive
      `SourceOrientedRankDeficientRealizationData` from it:

      ```lean
      structure BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) where
        m : Nat
        ╬® : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
        ╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®
        center_mem_ET : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        K : Set (Fin m -> Ōäé)
        K_compact : IsCompact K
        P : Set (Fin m -> Ōäé)
        P_open : IsOpen P
        P_subset_K : P ŌŖå K
        c0 : Fin m -> Ōäé
        c0_mem : c0 Ōłł P
        toVec : (Fin m -> Ōäé) -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        toVec_continuousOn : ContinuousOn toVec K
        toVec_mem :
          ŌłĆ c Ōłł K, toVec c Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        toVec_c0_invariant :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toVec c0) =
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0
        toInv_eq :
          ŌłĆ c Ōłł P,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toVec c) Ōłł ╬® Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toVec c) Ōłł
              BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n
        image_surj :
          ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ŌŖå
            (fun c =>
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toVec c)) '' P
        maxRank_dense_parameters :
          ŌłĆ c Ōłł P,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toVec c) Ōłł
              closure
                ((fun c' =>
                  BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toVec c')) ''
                  {c' | c' Ōłł P Ōł¦
                    BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
                      (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                        (toVec c'))})
      ```

      `K` is a compact residual control polydisc after all Hall-Wightman
      shrinkings; `P` is the open Schur parameter domain with `P ŌŖå K` on
      which the chart surjects onto the nearby oriented variety.  The
      invariant membership field is intentionally only over `P`.  If it were
      required over all of `K`, then `P ŌŖå K`, `image_surj`, and continuity
      would force `╬® Ōł® sourceOrientedGramVariety d n` to be the image of a
      compact set, making the intended nontrivial open Schur patch compact.
      The map `toVec` is the actual residual-frame vector formula, not a
      choice from existence.  `image_surj` is the oriented replacement for
      ordinary Lemma-3 relative openness: it says nearby oriented invariant
      data are represented by this residual family.
      `maxRank_dense_parameters` is needed later to identify the normal
      Riemann extension with the branch-defined value at exceptional points.
      The center condition is intentionally invariant-level:
      `toVec c0` need not equal the original source tuple `z0`.  The adapted
      representative may be a same-Gram rank representative obtained by
      deleting isotropic residual coefficients.  What is required, and what
      the branch law can consume, is
      `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toVec c0) =
       sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0`, together with
      `center_mem_ET : z0 Ōłł ExtendedTube d n`.

      Lean implementation checkpoint.  The sliced Schur local-image packet
      `SourceOrientedRankDeficientMaxRankLocalImageData` is not, by itself, a
      tube-realization theorem.  Its image is transported back from canonical
      normal form through a `SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv`, and the
      concrete transport currently comes from an invertible source-label
      matrix.  Such source-label linear changes preserve the oriented source
      variety, but they do not preserve `ExtendedTube d n`.  Therefore no
      production proof may derive
      `SourceOrientedExtendedTubeLocalRealizationData` from the rank-deficient
      local-image packet alone.

      The precise checked conversion surface is instead:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData.to_realizationData
          [NeZero d]
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (C : BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientRealizationData d n z0 := by
        refine
          { ╬® := C.╬®
            ╬®_open := C.╬®_open
            center_mem := C.center_mem
            realize := ?_ }
        ┬Ę intro G hG
          let c : Fin C.m -> Ōäé := Classical.choose (C.image_surj hG)
          have hc_spec :
              c Ōłł C.P Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (C.toVec c) = G :=
            Classical.choose_spec (C.image_surj hG)
          refine Ō¤©C.toVec c, ?_Ō¤®
          exact Ō¤©C.toVec_mem c (C.P_subset_K hc_spec.1), hc_spec.2Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData.to_localRealization
          [NeZero d]
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (C : BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedExtendedTubeLocalRealizationData d n z0 :=
        C.to_realizationData.to_localRealization
      ```

      The hard producer is consequently not a topology wrapper; it is the
      checked theorem constructing this residual chart:

      ```lean
      def BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChartProducer
          (d n : Nat)
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartProducer d n
      ```

      In production Lean the long target is named by:

      ```lean
      def BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartProducer
          (d n : Nat) : Type :=
        ŌłĆ {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
          z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
            ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0) ->
              BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0
      ```

      The checked residual chart accessors are
      `SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData.center_mem`, derived from
      `toInv_eq` at `c0 Ōłł P`, and
      `SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData.toVec_c0_mem`, derived
      from `P_subset_K` and `toVec_mem`.

      Its proof provides the actual `toVec` formula after all
      Hall-Wightman residual-frame shrinkings, proves `toVec_mem` directly in
      `ExtendedTube`, and prove `image_surj` for `╬® Ōł® sourceOrientedGramVariety`.
      The already checked Schur local-image stack can supply the algebraic
      image and max-rank-density bookkeeping, but only after it is paired with
      this extended-tube-valued vector family.

      The construction of this chart is not a new black box.  It must pass
      through a Schur-complement residual oriented realization theorem.  In
      Hall-Wightman normal form, let
      `D := d + 1` and
      `r := sourceGramMatrixRank n (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0)`, with
      `r < D`.  After the adapted normal-form transport, the first `r`
      source vectors are the standard nondegenerate head frame and the
      remaining base vectors are zero.  For a nearby oriented invariant `G`,
      shrink so the head block
      `A_G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé` is invertible, write the mixed block
      as `B_G`, and define
      `L_G := B_G * A_GŌü╗┬╣` and the residual Schur complement
      `S_G := C_G - B_G * A_GŌü╗┬╣ * B_GßĄĆ`.  The determinant coordinates induce
      residual determinant coordinates only after the local head gauge has
      chosen an actual factor `H_G` with
      `H_G * sourceHeadMetric d r hrD * H_GßĄĆ = A_G`; the selected
      head-tail full-frame determinants are divided by `H_G.det`, not by a
      quantity recoverable from `A_G` alone:

      ```lean
      structure BHW.SourceTailOrientedData
          (D m : Nat) where
        gram : Fin m -> Fin m -> Ōäé
        det : (Fin D Ōå¬ Fin m) -> Ōäé

      def BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant
          (D m : Nat)
          (q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m :=
        { gram := fun u v => Ōłæ ╬╝ : Fin D, q u ╬╝ * q v ╬╝
          det := fun ╬╣ =>
            Matrix.det (fun a ╬╝ => q (╬╣ a) ╬╝) }

      def BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety
          (D m : Nat) : Set (BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m) :=
        Set.range (BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m)

      def BHW.sourceTailPermuteOrientedData
          (D m : Nat)
          (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin m))
          (T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m) :
          BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m :=
        { gram := fun u v => T.gram (Žā u) (Žā v)
          det := fun ╬╣ => T.det (╬╣.trans Žā.toEmbedding) }

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant_perm
          (D m : Nat)
          (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin m))
          (q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m (fun u => q (Žā u)) =
            BHW.sourceTailPermuteOrientedData D m Žā
              (BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q)

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety_perm_iff
          (D m : Nat)
          (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin m))
          (T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m) :
          BHW.sourceTailPermuteOrientedData D m Žā T Ōłł
              BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m Ōåö
            T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m

      def BHW.sourceTailOrientedAlgebraicRelations
          (D m : Nat)
          (T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m) : Prop :=
        (ŌłĆ u v, T.gram u v = T.gram v u) Ōł¦
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank m T.gram <= D Ōł¦
        (ŌłĆ (╬╣ ╬║ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin m),
          Matrix.det (fun a b => T.gram (╬╣ a) (╬║ b)) =
            T.det ╬╣ * T.det ╬║) Ōł¦
        (ŌłĆ (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin m) (Žü : Equiv.Perm (Fin D)),
          T.det ((Žü.toEmbedding).trans ╬╣) =
            (Žü.sign : Ōäé) * T.det ╬╣)

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety_eq_algebraic
          (D m : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m =
            {T | BHW.sourceTailOrientedAlgebraicRelations D m T}

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m,
              T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m ->
              (ŌłĆ u v, ŌĆ¢T.gram u vŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              (ŌłĆ ╬╣, ŌĆ¢T.det ╬╣ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q = T
      ```

      The tail theorem is the same invariant-theory content as the oriented
      algebraic model, but specialized to a small neighborhood of the zero
      tail.  Its proof is finite dimensional and constructive; it must not
      take an arbitrary realizing tuple and scale it, because scaling changes
      the prescribed Gram and determinant coordinates.  The checked route now
      uses the global quantitative Autonne-Takagi factorization instead of a
      separate intermediate-rank Schur recursion.  For rank `D`, factor the
      whole small Gram matrix and repair the two possible determinant sheets by
      one coordinate reflection.  For rank `< D`, every full `D ├Ś D`
      determinant coordinate is forced to vanish, so the same small Gram
      factor already realizes the complete oriented datum.  If the tail Gram
      is zero, this specializes to the older zero-tuple case.

      The implementation exposes the following cases; otherwise the small-tail
      theorem would still be too opaque for downstream use:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_zeroGram
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          (T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m)
          (hTvar : T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m)
          (hgram_zero : T.gram = 0) :
          T.det = 0 Ōł¦
          BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m
            (fun _ _ => 0) = T

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety_selectedGram_det
          (D m : Nat)
          {T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m}
          (hTvar : T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m)
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin m) :
          Matrix.det (fun a b : Fin D => T.gram (╬╣ a) (╬╣ b)) =
            T.det ╬╣ * T.det ╬╣

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety_mixedGram_det
          (D m : Nat)
          {T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m}
          (hTvar : T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m)
          (╬╣ ╬║ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin m) :
          Matrix.det (fun a b : Fin D => T.gram (╬╣ a) (╬║ b)) =
            T.det ╬╣ * T.det ╬║

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant_reflection
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          (q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé) :
          let i0 : Fin D := Ō¤©0, hDŌ¤®
          let sign : Fin D -> Ōäé := fun ╬╝ => if ╬╝ = i0 then -1 else 1
          BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m
              (fun u ╬╝ => sign ╬╝ * q u ╬╝) =
            { gram := (BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q).gram
              det := fun ╬╣ =>
                - (BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q).det ╬╣ }

      theorem BHW.sourceTail_reflection_norm
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          (q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé) :
          let i0 : Fin D := Ō¤©0, hDŌ¤®
          let sign : Fin D -> Ōäé := fun ╬╝ => if ╬╝ = i0 then -1 else 1
          ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢sign ╬╝ * q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant_eq_of_gram_eq_selectedDet
          (D m : Nat)
          {T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m}
          (hTvar : T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m)
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin m)
          (q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé)
          (hgram :
            (BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q).gram = T.gram)
          (hdet╬╣ :
            (BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q).det ╬╣ = T.det ╬╣)
          (hdet_ne : T.det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q = T

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant_or_reflection_eq_of_gram_eq
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          {T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m}
          (hTvar : T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m)
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin m)
          (hdet_ne : T.det ╬╣ ŌēĀ 0)
          (q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé)
          (hgram :
            (BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q).gram = T.gram) :
          Ōłā q' : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé,
            (ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q' u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢) Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q' = T

      theorem BHW.sourceTailFullFrame_factorWithDet
          (D : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          (A : Matrix (Fin D) (Fin D) Ōäé)
          (hA_symm :
            (fun i j : Fin D => A i j) Ōłł
              BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace D)
          (╬┤ : Ōäé)
          (h╬┤ : A.det = ╬┤ * ╬┤) :
          Ōłā M : Matrix (Fin D) (Fin D) Ōäé,
            M * M.transpose = A Ōł¦
            M.det = ╬┤

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_fullRankStep
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          (hDm : D <= m)
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin m)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m,
              T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m ->
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank m T.gram = D ->
              IsUnit
                (Matrix.det
                  (fun a b => T.gram (╬╣ a) (╬╣ b))) ->
              (ŌłĆ u v, ŌĆ¢T.gram u vŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              (ŌłĆ ╬╣, ŌĆ¢T.det ╬╣ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q = T

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety_rank_le
          (D m : Nat)
          {T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m}
          (hTvar : T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m) :
          (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin m => T.gram i j).rank <= D

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety_det_eq_zero_of_rank_lt
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          {T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m}
          (hTvar : T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m)
          (hrank_lt : BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank m T.gram < D) :
          T.det = 0

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_rankLt_bound
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m,
              T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m ->
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank m T.gram < D ->
              (ŌłĆ u v, ŌĆ¢T.gram u vŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              (ŌłĆ ╬╣, ŌĆ¢T.det ╬╣ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q = T
      ```

      The checked theorem `sourceTailFullFrame_factorWithDet` is the
      full-rank orientation core: an ordinary symmetric factorization gives
      some `M0` with `M0 * M0ßĄĆ = A`; the relation `A.det = ╬┤^2` implies
      `M0.det = ╬┤` or `M0.det = -╬┤`; if needed, right-multiply by the fixed
      reflection `diag(-1,1,...,1)` to flip the determinant without changing
      the Gram.  The active top-rank small-tail proof does not require a
      separate quantitative selected-block factor theorem.  Instead,
      `SourceOrientedTailSmallRealization.lean` factors the whole tail Gram
      matrix by the checked `sourceComplexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE`, then
      uses the selected block only to choose between the two determinant
      sheets.

      The top-rank residual case `sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_fullRankStep`
      is a direct same-Gram factorization branch, and it should not solve the
      remaining rows by explicit inverse coefficients.  The cleaner route is:
      factor the whole small rank-`D` symmetric Gram matrix `T.gram` by
      `sourceComplexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE`, giving a small
      `q0 : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé` with the same Gram.  For the selected
      invertible block `╬╣`, both `det(q0 Ōłś ╬╣)` and `T.det ╬╣` square to
      `det(T.gram (╬╣ ┬Ę) (╬╣ ┬Ę))`, so they differ by a sign.  If the sign is
      wrong, apply the checked coordinate reflection
      `sourceTailOrientedInvariant_reflection`; it preserves Gram coordinates
      and all coordinate norms while multiplying every full-frame determinant
      by `-1`.  After the selected determinant matches, every other full
      determinant coordinate follows from the checked mixed-minor identity:
      the same mixed Gram determinant equals
      `det(q Ōłś ╬╣) * det(q Ōłś ╬║)` for the factor and
      `T.det ╬╣ * T.det ╬║` for `T`, and the selected determinant is nonzero.
      This sign-propagation argument is now checked as
      `sourceTailOrientedInvariant_or_reflection_eq_of_gram_eq`; the
      quantitative small same-Gram factorization is now checked as
      `sourceComplexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE`.  Consequently
      `sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_fullRankStep` and
      `sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_fullRank_bound` are production Lean
      in `SourceOrientedTailSmallRealization.lean`.

      The lower-rank branch handles all `rank < D`, including the old
      intermediate range `0 < rank < D`.  The checked theorem
      `sourceTailOrientedVariety_rank_le` obtains the rank bound from the
      range description of `sourceTailOrientedVariety` and
      `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_eq_rank_le`.  Then
      `sourceTailOrientedVariety_det_eq_zero_of_rank_lt` proves every full
      determinant coordinate is zero: rank `< D` gives all `D ├Ś D` Gram minors
      zero by `sourceMatrix_minors_eq_zero_of_rank_le`, and the selected-Gram
      square identity forces `T.det ╬╣ = 0`.  Applying the same argument to the
      small same-Gram factor shows its determinant coordinates are also zero,
      so Gram equality is already equality of oriented data.

      The selected-block and source-permutation helpers remain useful support
      for Schur residual charts, but they are no longer called by the Euclidean
      tail small-realization theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceTail_exists_principalMinor_of_rank
          (D m r : Nat)
          (T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m)
          (hTvar : T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m)
          (hr : BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank m T.gram = r)
          Ōłā ╬╣ : Fin r Ōå¬ Fin m,
            IsUnit
              (Matrix.det (fun a b => T.gram (╬╣ a) (╬╣ b)))

      theorem BHW.sourceTail_permute_to_head
          (m r : Nat)
          (hrm : r <= m)
          (╬╣ : Fin r Ōå¬ Fin m) :
          Ōłā Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin m),
            ŌłĆ a : Fin r,
              Žā (BHW.finSourceHead hrm a) = ╬╣ a

      theorem BHW.sourceTailSmallRealization_transport_perm
          (D m : Nat)
          (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin m))
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ}
          {T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m}
          {qŽā : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé}
          (hsmall : ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢qŽā u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą)
          (hrealizes :
            BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m qŽā =
              BHW.sourceTailPermuteOrientedData D m Žā T) :
          Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé,
            (ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q = T

      ```

      Source-label permutation transports determinant coordinates by
      ordered-embedding composition `╬╣.trans Žā.toEmbedding`; there is no extra
      row sign because the ordered embedding itself is transported.  If two
      embeddings with the same image are compared later, the determinant
      alternation relation supplies the sign.

      The top-level small-tail theorem is now a two-way rank split:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_zeroRank_bound
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m,
              T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m ->
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank m T.gram = 0 ->
              (ŌłĆ u v, ŌĆ¢T.gram u vŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              (ŌłĆ ╬╣, ŌĆ¢T.det ╬╣ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q = T

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_fullRank_bound
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m,
              T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m ->
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank m T.gram = D ->
              (ŌłĆ u v, ŌĆ¢T.gram u vŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              (ŌłĆ ╬╣, ŌĆ¢T.det ╬╣ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q = T

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_rankLt_bound
          (D m : Nat)
          (hD : 0 < D)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ T : BHW.SourceTailOrientedData D m,
              T Ōłł BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety D m ->
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank m T.gram < D ->
              (ŌłĆ u v, ŌĆ¢T.gram u vŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              (ŌłĆ ╬╣, ŌĆ¢T.det ╬╣ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant D m q = T

      theorem BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization ... := by
        classical
        obtain Ō¤©╬ĘFull, h╬ĘFull_pos, hfullŌ¤® :=
          BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_fullRank_bound D m hD h╬Ą
        obtain Ō¤©╬ĘLow, h╬ĘLow_pos, hlowŌ¤® :=
          BHW.sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization_rankLt_bound D m hD h╬Ą
        let ╬Ę := min ╬ĘFull ╬ĘLow
        refine Ō¤©╬Ę, lt_min h╬ĘFull_pos h╬ĘLow_pos, ?_Ō¤®
        intro T hTvar hTgramSmall hTdetSmall
        let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank m T.gram
        by_cases hr : r = D
        ┬Ę exact
            hfull T hTvar hr
              (fun u v => lt_of_lt_of_le (hTgramSmall u v) (min_le_left _ _))
              (fun ╬╣ => lt_of_lt_of_le (hTdetSmall ╬╣) (min_le_left _ _))
        ┬Ę have hrle : r <= D := by
            simpa [r] using BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety_rank_le D m hTvar
          have hrlt : r < D := lt_of_le_of_ne hrle hr
          exact
            hlow T hTvar hrlt
              (fun u v => lt_of_lt_of_le (hTgramSmall u v) (min_le_right _ _))
              (fun ╬╣ => lt_of_lt_of_le (hTdetSmall ╬╣) (min_le_right _ _))
      ```

      The public determinant-smallness hypotheses are retained because the
      later local Schur radius choice uses the same input shape, but the
      checked one-way realization proof only needs Gram smallness and the
      algebraic variety relations.  No case uses an arbitrary preexisting
      realization of `T`, because the estimates would be lost.

      The local-image proof below should consume the one-way shifted-tail
      realization for Schur image-surjectivity.  It does not need a
      single-radius compatible-box theorem at this layer: the forward inclusion
      of a small normal-parameter box into the ambient Schur neighborhood is a
      separate continuity/polynomial-shrink estimate for the normal parameter
      map.  This avoids imposing an artificial same-`epsilon` condition on the
      quadratic tail invariant map.

      The Schur extraction theorem packages the algebra that the oriented
      determinant fields satisfy after the head block is inverted:

      Signature convention for the residual tail.  The tail coordinates in
      `sourceTailEmbed d r hrD` inherit the shifted full metric
      `╬╝ Ōå” MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d (finSourceTail (Nat.le_of_lt hrD) ╬╝)`.
      This is not the Euclidean dot product used by
      `sourceTailOrientedInvariant`, and for `0 < r` it is the positive tail
      block after the time direction has been selected into the head.
      Therefore the Schur residual data below must use shifted-tail oriented
      data.  The checked `sourceShiftedTailSmallRealization` theorem is the
      one-way Euclidean realization transported through the finite diagonal
      normalization equivalence: choose coordinate scalars whose squares are
      the shifted signs, transport Gram data unchanged, multiply every full
      determinant coordinate by the product of those scalars, call the
      Euclidean theorem, and scale the realizing tuple back.

      ```lean
      def BHW.sourceTailMetric
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          Matrix (Fin (d + 1 - r)) (Fin (d + 1 - r)) Ōäé :=
        Matrix.diagonal fun ╬╝ =>
          (MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d
            (BHW.finSourceTail (Nat.le_of_lt hrD) ╬╝) : Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.sourceTailMetric_det_isUnit
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          IsUnit (BHW.sourceTailMetric d r hrD).det

      def BHW.sourceShiftedTailGram
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (m : Nat)
          (q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé

      structure BHW.SourceShiftedTailOrientedData
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (m : Nat) where
        gram : Fin m -> Fin m -> Ōäé
        det : (Fin (d + 1 - r) Ōå¬ Fin m) -> Ōäé

      def BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (m : Nat)
          (q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.SourceShiftedTailOrientedData d r hrD m :=
        { gram := BHW.sourceShiftedTailGram d r hrD m q
          det := fun ╬╣ =>
            Matrix.det (fun a ╬╝ => q (╬╣ a) ╬╝) }

      structure BHW.SourceShiftedTailMetricNormalization
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1) where
        scale : Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé
        scale_ne_zero : ŌłĆ ╬╝, scale ╬╝ ŌēĀ 0
        scale_sq :
          ŌłĆ ╬╝,
            scale ╬╝ * scale ╬╝ =
              (MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d
                (BHW.finSourceTail (Nat.le_of_lt hrD) ╬╝) : Ōäé)
        detScale : Ōäé
        detScale_eq : detScale = ŌłÅ ╬╝, scale ╬╝
        detScale_ne_zero : detScale ŌēĀ 0

      def BHW.sourceShiftedTailMetricNormalization
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          BHW.SourceShiftedTailMetricNormalization d r hrD :=
        { scale := BHW.sourceTailMetricScale d r hrD
          scale_ne_zero :=
            BHW.sourceTailMetricScale_ne_zero d r hrD
          scale_sq :=
            BHW.sourceTailMetricScale_mul_self d r hrD
          detScale := BHW.sourceTailMetricDetScale d r hrD
          detScale_eq := rfl
          detScale_ne_zero :=
            BHW.sourceTailMetricDetScale_ne_zero d r hrD }

      theorem BHW.sourceTailMetricScale_norm
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (╬╝ : Fin (d + 1 - r)) :
          ŌĆ¢BHW.sourceTailMetricScale d r hrD ╬╝ŌĆ¢ = 1

      theorem BHW.sourceTailMetricDetScale_norm
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          ŌĆ¢BHW.sourceTailMetricDetScale d r hrDŌĆ¢ = 1

      def BHW.sourceShiftedTailDataToEuclidean
          (d r m : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (N :
            BHW.SourceShiftedTailMetricNormalization d r hrD)
          (T : BHW.SourceShiftedTailOrientedData d r hrD m) :
          BHW.SourceTailOrientedData (d + 1 - r) m :=
        { gram := T.gram
          det := fun ╬╣ => N.detScale * T.det ╬╣ }

      theorem BHW.sourceShiftedTailInvariant_toEuclidean
          (d r m : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (N :
            BHW.SourceShiftedTailMetricNormalization d r hrD)
          (q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant (d + 1 - r) m
              (fun u ╬╝ => N.scale ╬╝ * q u ╬╝) =
            BHW.sourceShiftedTailDataToEuclidean d r m hrD N
              (BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant d r hrD m q)

      theorem BHW.sourceShiftedTailVariety_toEuclidean_iff
          (d r m : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (N :
            BHW.SourceShiftedTailMetricNormalization d r hrD)
          (T : BHW.SourceShiftedTailOrientedData d r hrD m) :
          BHW.sourceShiftedTailDataToEuclidean d r m hrD N T Ōłł
              BHW.sourceTailOrientedVariety (d + 1 - r) m Ōåö
            T Ōłł BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedVariety d r hrD m

      theorem BHW.sourceShiftedTailDataToEuclidean_injective
          (d r m : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (N :
            BHW.SourceShiftedTailMetricNormalization d r hrD) :
          Function.Injective
            (BHW.sourceShiftedTailDataToEuclidean d r m hrD N)

      theorem BHW.sourceShiftedTailInvariant_eq_of_toEuclidean_eq
          (d r m : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (N :
            BHW.SourceShiftedTailMetricNormalization d r hrD)
          (q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé)
          (T : BHW.SourceShiftedTailOrientedData d r hrD m)
          (hE :
            BHW.sourceTailOrientedInvariant (d + 1 - r) m
                (fun u ╬╝ => N.scale ╬╝ * q u ╬╝) =
              BHW.sourceShiftedTailDataToEuclidean d r m hrD N T) :
          BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant d r hrD m q = T

      theorem BHW.sourceShiftedTailSmallRealization
          (d r m : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ T : BHW.SourceShiftedTailOrientedData d r hrD m,
              T Ōłł BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedVariety d r hrD m ->
              (ŌłĆ u v, ŌĆ¢T.gram u vŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              (ŌłĆ ╬╣, ŌĆ¢T.det ╬╣ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ u ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q u ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant d r hrD m q = T
      ```

      The proof is the checked diagonal-normalization argument: convert shifted
      data to Euclidean data, use `sourceTailOrientedSmallRealization`, then
      scale the Euclidean realizing tuple back by `N.scaleŌü╗┬╣`.  The norm of
      every `N.scale ╬╝` and of `N.detScale` is `1`, so the smallness constants
      are unchanged.

      ```lean
      structure BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) where
        A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé
        A_eq :
          A = BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G
        A_unit : IsUnit A.det
        headFactor : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé
        headFactor_gram :
          headFactor * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD *
              headFactor.transpose =
            A
        headFactor_det_unit : IsUnit headFactor.det
        L : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé
        L_eq :
          L = BHW.sourceSchurMixedCoeff n r hrn G A
        tail : BHW.SourceShiftedTailOrientedData d r hrD (n - r)
        tail_gram_eq :
          tail.gram =
            BHW.sourceSchurComplement n r hrn G A
        tail_det_eq :
          tail.det =
            BHW.sourceSchurResidualDeterminants
              d n r hrD hrn G headFactor
        tail_mem :
          tail Ōłł
            BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedVariety d r hrD (n - r)

      abbrev BHW.SourceSymmetricMatrixCoord (r : Nat) :=
        {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé // A.transpose = A}

      def BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n) :
          BHW.SourceSymmetricMatrixCoord r

      structure BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1) where
        U : Set (BHW.SourceSymmetricMatrixCoord r)
        U_open : IsOpen U
        center_mem : BHW.sourceHeadMetricSymmCoord d r hrD Ōłł U
        factor : BHW.SourceSymmetricMatrixCoord r ->
          Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé
        factor_continuousOn : ContinuousOn factor U
        factor_center :
          factor (BHW.sourceHeadMetricSymmCoord d r hrD) = 1
        factor_gram :
          ŌłĆ A Ōłł U,
            factor A * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD *
                (factor A).transpose =
              (A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
        factor_det_unit :
          ŌłĆ A Ōłł U, IsUnit (factor A).det

      def BHW.sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
          (headFactor : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé) :
          BHW.SourceShiftedTailOrientedData d r hrD (n - r)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData_mem_variety
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł
              Head.U) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData d n r hrD hrn G
              (Head.factor
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
                  d n r hrD hrn hGvar)) Ōłł
              BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedVariety d r hrD (n - r)

      structure BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD) where
        p : BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter
          d n r hrD hrn
        invariant_eq :
          G =
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
                d n r hrD hrn p)
        head_eq :
          Head.factor
              (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
                d n r hrD hrn hGvar) =
            p.head

      theorem BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData.residualTail_mem_variety
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          {hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n}
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
              d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł Head.U)
          (D :
            BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData
              d n r hrD hrn hGvar Head) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData d n r hrD hrn G
              (Head.factor
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
                  d n r hrD hrn hGvar)) Ōłł
            BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedVariety d r hrD (n - r)

      def BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData.schurResidualData
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
              d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł Head.U)
          (D :
            BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData
              d n r hrD hrn hGvar Head) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G

      def BHW.sourceOriented_headGaugeNormalParameterData_of_lorentz_normalized
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz : G = BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z)
          {p : BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter
            d n r hrD hrn}
          (hhead :
            Head.factor
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
                  d n r hrD hrn hGvar) =
              p.head)
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (h╬ø :
            BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z =
              BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
                d n r hrD hrn p) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData
            d n r hrD hrn hGvar Head

      def BHW.sourceOriented_schurResidualData_of_tail_mem
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł
              Head.U)
          (htail_mem :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData d n r hrD hrn G
                (Head.factor
                  (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
                    d n r hrD hrn hGvar)) Ōłł
              BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedVariety d r hrD (n - r)) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_reconstruct_from_schurResidual
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          {q : Fin (n - r) -> Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé}
          (hq :
            BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant d r hrD (n - r) q =
              R.tail) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
            (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn
              { head := R.headFactor
                mixed := R.L
                tail := q }) = G

      theorem BHW.exists_sourceOriented_reconstruct_from_schurResidual
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          {q : Fin (n - r) -> Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé}
          (hq :
            BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant d r hrD (n - r) q =
              R.tail) :
          Ōłā z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z = G
      ```

      The coordinate data layer in this display is checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedSchurResidual.lean`: the head, mixed, tail,
      Schur-complement, residual determinant, shifted-tail-variety definitions
      and `BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData` compile with these theorem
      surfaces.  The head-gauge interface and algebraic residual-data
      constructor are checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedHeadGauge.lean`: the symmetric head block
      is available as
      `BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm` only after the source-variety
      hypothesis `hGvar`, `BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData` records the
      local factor `H` with `H * sourceHeadMetric * HßĄĆ = A`, and
      `BHW.sourceOriented_schurResidualData_of_tail_mem` proves that the
      shifted-tail-membership theorem is the only algebraic input needed to
      build `BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData`.  That membership theorem is
      now checked in `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormal.lean` as
      `BHW.sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData_mem_variety`, and the top-level
      residual packet producer is checked as
      `BHW.sourceOriented_schurResidualData_of_headGauge`.  The remaining
      non-algebraic input for local charts is the analytic head-gauge producer,
      not a separate arbitrary `hA_unit` assumption.  The reconstruction
      endpoint is now checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedSchurReconstruct.lean`:
      it specializes the checked hard-range Schur propagation theorem after
      the shifted residual tail has been realized by `q`.  The checked data
      structure deliberately stores only the residual fields and does not hide
      the head-gauge/tail-membership producer as a field.

      The theorem `sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData_mem_variety` is proved
      through the normal-parameter data surface above, not by an isolated
      existential tail argument.  The checked implementation uses:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_headGaugeNormalParameterData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł
              Head.U) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData
            d n r hrD hrn hGvar Head

      def BHW.sourceOriented_headGaugeNormalParameterData_of_lorentz_head_tail
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz : G = BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z)
          {p : BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter
            d n r hrD hrn}
          (hhead :
            Head.factor
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
                  d n r hrD hrn hGvar) =
              p.head)
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (h╬øhead :
            ŌłĆ a : Fin r,
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a) =
                BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
                  d n r hrD hrn p (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a))
          (h╬øtail :
            ŌłĆ u : Fin (n - r),
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u) =
                BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
                  d n r hrD hrn p (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData
            d n r hrD hrn hGvar Head

      def BHW.complexMinkowski_detOneWittExtension_to_headFactorFrame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (H : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (hHdet : IsUnit H.det)
          (x y : Fin r -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hx : LinearIndependent Ōäé x)
          (hy :
            y =
              fun a =>
                BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn
                  { head := H, mixed := 0, tail := 0 } a)
          (hxGram :
            ŌłĆ a b,
              BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d (x a) (x b) =
                (H * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD * H.transpose) a b) :
          { ╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d //
            ŌłĆ a,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (x a) = y a }

      def BHW.sourceOriented_headGaugeWittData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł
              Head.U)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz : G = BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeWittData
            d n r hrD hrn Head hGvar hHead hz

      def BHW.sourceOriented_headGaugeNormalParameterData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł
              Head.U)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz : G = BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData
            d n r hrD hrn hGvar Head :=
        (BHW.sourceOriented_headGaugeWittData
          hd hrD hrn Head hGvar hHead hz).normalParameterData
          d n r hrD hrn Head hGvar hHead hz

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData_mem_variety
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł
              Head.U) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData d n r hrD hrn G
              (Head.factor
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
                  d n r hrD hrn hGvar)) Ōłł
            BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedVariety d r hrD (n - r)

      def BHW.sourceOriented_schurResidualData_of_headGauge
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł
              Head.U) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_headGauge_tailCoordinates_after_witt
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (Head : BHW.SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData d r hrD)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hHead :
            BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm d n r hrD hrn hGvar Ōłł
              Head.U)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz : G = BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z)
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (h╬øhead :
            ŌłĆ a,
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a) =
                BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn
                  (BHW.sourceOrientedHeadGaugeHeadParameter
                    d n r hrD hrn hGvar Head) a) :
          Ōłā q : Fin (n - r) -> Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé,
            let H :=
              Head.factor
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm
                  d n r hrD hrn hGvar)
            let L :=
              BHW.sourceSchurMixedCoeff n r hrn G
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G)
            BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z =
              BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn
                { head := H
                  mixed := L
                  tail := q }
      ```

      Checked SOComplex support for the finite Witt constructor:

      ```lean
      theorem SOComplex.inv_mulVec_zero_eq_sum_col
          {n : Nat} [NeZero n]
          (A : SOComplex n) (w : Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          ((AŌü╗┬╣).val *ßĄź w) (0 : Fin n) =
            Ōłæ k : Fin n, A.val k (0 : Fin n) * w k

      theorem SOComplex.inv_mulVec_zero_eq_zero_of_signed_col_orth
          {n : Nat} [NeZero n]
          (A : SOComplex n) {Žā : Ōäé} (hŽā : Žā ŌēĀ 0)
          {v w : Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hA : ŌłĆ k : Fin n, Žā * A.val k (0 : Fin n) = v k)
          (horth : Ōłæ k : Fin n, v k * w k = 0) :
          ((AŌü╗┬╣).val *ßĄź w) (0 : Fin n) = 0

      theorem SOComplex.tail_sq_eq_of_inv_mulVec_signed_col_orth
          {m : Nat}
          (A : SOComplex (m + 2)) {Žā Žä : Ōäé} (hŽā : Žā ŌēĀ 0)
          {v w : Fin (m + 2) -> Ōäé}
          (hA : ŌłĆ k : Fin (m + 2), Žā * A.val k 0 = v k)
          (hvw : Ōłæ k : Fin (m + 2), v k * w k = 0)
          (hww : Ōłæ k : Fin (m + 2), w k ^ 2 = Žä ^ 2) :
          Ōłæ i : Fin (m + 1), ((AŌü╗┬╣).val *ßĄź w) i.succ ^ 2 = Žä ^ 2

      theorem SOComplex.tail_dot_eq_of_inv_mulVec_signed_col_orth
          {m : Nat}
          (A : SOComplex (m + 2)) {Žā : Ōäé} (hŽā : Žā ŌēĀ 0)
          {v w u : Fin (m + 2) -> Ōäé} {c : Ōäé}
          (hA : ŌłĆ k : Fin (m + 2), Žā * A.val k 0 = v k)
          (hvw : Ōłæ k : Fin (m + 2), v k * w k = 0)
          (hvu : Ōłæ k : Fin (m + 2), v k * u k = 0)
          (hwu : Ōłæ k : Fin (m + 2), w k * u k = c) :
          Ōłæ i : Fin (m + 1),
            ((AŌü╗┬╣).val *ßĄź w) i.succ * ((AŌü╗┬╣).val *ßĄź u) i.succ = c

      theorem SOComplex.mul_embed_val_col_zero
          {m : Nat}
          (A : SOComplex (m + 2)) (B : SOComplex (m + 1))
          (k : Fin (m + 2)) :
          (A * SOComplex.embed B).val k 0 = A.val k 0

      theorem SOComplex.mul_embed_val_col_succ
          {m : Nat}
          (A : SOComplex (m + 2)) (B : SOComplex (m + 1))
          (k : Fin (m + 2)) (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
          (A * SOComplex.embed B).val k j.succ =
            Ōłæ l : Fin (m + 1), A.val k l.succ * B.val l j
      ```

      The signed-prefix-frame induction should now be implemented literally:
      normalize the first vector by
      `SOComplex.exists_so_with_firstCol_of_sq`; use
      `inv_mulVec_zero_eq_zero_of_signed_col_orth` to show the remaining
      transformed vectors live in the tail; use the two `tail_*` lemmas to
      transfer the remaining Gram table; recursively normalize the tail; and
      assemble the final determinant-one matrix as `AŌéĆ * SOComplex.embed B`.
      The `mul_embed` column formulas are the checked final rewrite, and
      `SOComplex.mulVec_inv_mulVec` is the checked identity turning the
      embedded tail vector back into the original vector after left
      multiplication by `AŌéĆ`.

      Proof transcript for this producer:

      1. Choose `z` with
         `hGvar = Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®`.  Let
         `Acoord := sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm ... hGvar` and
         `H := Head.factor Acoord`.  The head-gauge equation gives
         `H * sourceHeadMetric d r hrD * HßĄĆ = A`, where `A` is the actual
         selected head Gram block of `z`; `Head.factor_det_unit` gives
         `IsUnit H.det`.
      2. Define the gauge head frame
         `y a := sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn
           {head := H, mixed := 0, tail := 0} a`.  The checked normal-head
         formula gives `<y a,y b> = A a b`, matching the actual head frame
         `x a := z (finSourceHead hrn a)`.
      3. Use the checked
         `sourceOriented_headGauge_actualHeadGram_eq_normalHeadGram` to record
         equality of the actual and gauge head Gram matrices.  Use
         `sourceHeadRows_linearIndependent_of_headGauge` for linear
         independence of `x`, and
         `sourceOriented_headGauge_normalHead_linearIndependent` for linear
         independence of `y`.  The head Gram is invertible by
         `sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock_det_isUnit_of_headGauge`, so both spans
         are nondegenerate and the equality of restricted bilinear forms
         defines an isometry `span x Ōēā span y`.
      4. Apply the typed finite-dimensional constructor
         `complexMinkowski_detOneWittExtension_to_headFactorFrame`, or
         equivalently the specialized producer
         `sourceOriented_headGaugeWittData`, to obtain
         `W : SourceOrientedHeadGaugeWittData ...`.  This data contains
         `W.╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d` and the head normalization
         `complexLorentzAction W.╬ø z (finSourceHead hrn a) = y a` for all
         `a`.  The theorem must return this Type-valued data, not merely
         `Ōłā ╬ø, ...`, because the checked tail-coordinate descent constructs a
         Type-valued normal-parameter witness from the chosen `╬ø`.
      5. Put `w := complexLorentzAction ╬ø z`.  Define
         `L := sourceSchurMixedCoeff n r hrn G A`.  For each tail label,
         subtract the head projection
         `Ōłæ a, L u a ŌĆó y a`.  The Schur identity
         `L * A = sourceOrientedSchurMixedBlock` and the equality of the
         transformed head frame prove the residual vector is orthogonal to
         every `y a`.  Because the `y a` span the first `r` canonical
         coordinates, this residual vector has zero head coordinates and is
         therefore `sourceTailEmbed d r hrD (q u)` for a unique tail coordinate
         vector `q u`.  This is the content of
         `sourceOriented_headGauge_tailCoordinates_after_witt`; it is pure
         Schur/head-coordinate algebra after the Witt head normalization.
      6. Define
         `p := { head := H, mixed := L, tail := q }`.  The previous two
         steps prove pointwise
         `w = sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p`.  Proper
         complex Lorentz invariance of `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant`
         gives
         `G = sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
           (sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p)`, and
         the head equality is `rfl`.
      7. The checked consumer
         `SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData.residualTail_mem_variety`
         now gives `sourceOrientedSchurResidualTailData_mem_variety`, and
         `SourceOrientedHeadGaugeNormalParameterData.schurResidualData` gives
         the full `SourceOrientedSchurResidualData`.

      This finite-dimensional Witt theorem is now checked, Type-valued, and
      independent of OS, Wightman functions, EOW, PET, and locality.  It is
      not a new axiom and does not introduce a production `sorry`.

      Checked subproof transcript for
      `complexMinkowski_detOneWittExtension_to_headFactorFrame`:

      1. Prove `SOComplex.exists_so_with_signedPrefixCols` by induction on
         `r`.  Its input is a signed dot-orthogonal prefix frame with diagonal
         squares `Žā a ^ 2`; its output realizes those vectors as signed prefix
         columns of an `SOComplex` element.  The one-column step uses
         `SOComplex.exists_so_with_firstCol_of_sq`, itself a checked wrapper
         around `SOComplex.exists_so_with_firstCol`.
      3. In the induction step, first use
         `SOComplex.exists_so_with_firstCol` to get `A0` with first column
         equal to the first frame vector.  For every remaining frame vector
         `v a`, prove `(A0Ōü╗┬╣.val *ßĄź v a) 0 = 0` from orthogonality to the
         first column.  Drop the zero coordinate to obtain a dot-orthonormal
         `(r-1)`-frame in `Fin (N-1)`.  Apply the induction hypothesis there
         to obtain `B : SOComplex (N-1)`, embed it as
         `SOComplex.embed B`, and set `A := A0 * SOComplex.embed B`.  The
         checked stabilizer proof in `SOConnected.lean` follows this same
         row/column mechanism.  The required public consequences are now
         checked as `SOComplex.embed_val_zero_zero`,
         `SOComplex.embed_val_zero_succ`, `SOComplex.embed_val_succ_zero`,
         `SOComplex.embed_val_succ_succ`, `SOComplex.embed_mulVec_zero`,
         `SOComplex.embed_mulVec_succ`, and
         `SOComplex.of_first_col_e0`, so production Lean should call those
         instead of duplicating the block algebra.
      4. The local theorem does not need a general symmetric-congruence
         theorem: the head gauge already supplies the factor `H` with
         common Gram `H * sourceHeadMetric d r hrD * HßĄĆ`.  Define normalized
         source vectors
         `x' a := Ōłæ b, HŌü╗┬╣ a b ŌĆó x b`.  By `Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul` from
         `hHdet`, the Gram of `x'` is exactly `sourceHeadMetric d r hrD`, and
         the target frame becomes the canonical head frame.
      5. Apply the checked canonical-head-frame producer, which casts the
         `(d - r) + r` prefix theorem to ambient dimension `d`, uses the public
         `ComplexLorentzGroup.fromSOComplex_val_apply` Wick-conjugation formula,
         and returns a Type-valued Lorentz witness.
      6. Recompose through `H`: since `x = H ŌĆó x'` and the gauge normal head
         frame is `H` applied to the canonical head frame, linearity of
         `complexLorentzVectorAction` gives
         `complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (x a) = y a`.  The source-oriented
         producer `sourceOriented_headGaugeWittData` obtains its input frame
         packet from the checked `sourceOriented_headGaugeFrameSameGramData`
         and packages the resulting `╬ø` as `SourceOrientedHeadGaugeWittData`.

      Subproof transcript for
      `sourceOriented_headGauge_tailCoordinates_after_witt`:

      1. Put `w := complexLorentzAction ╬ø z`,
         `A := sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G`,
         `H := Head.factor (sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlockSymm ... hGvar)`,
         `L := sourceSchurMixedCoeff n r hrn G A`, and
         `y a := sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector ... head := H ... a`.
      2. The head labels are already normalized by `h╬øhead`.  The oriented
         invariant is proper-Lorentz invariant, so the ordinary Gram of `w`
         is `G.gram`; in particular
         `<w (finSourceTail u), y a> = sourceOrientedSchurMixedBlock ... G u a`
         and `<y b, y a> = A b a`.
      3. Use
         `sourceSchurMixedCoeff_mul_headBlock` with
         `sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock_det_isUnit_of_headGauge` to get
         `L * A = sourceOrientedSchurMixedBlock ... G`.
      4. Define the residual vector
         `res u := w (finSourceTail hrn u) -
           fun ╬╝ => sum a, L u a *
             sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector ... H a ╬╝`.
         Expanding the bilinear form and using the previous step gives
         `<res u, y a> = 0` for every `a`.
      5. Since `H` is invertible, the `y a` span exactly the canonical head
         coordinate subspace.  Orthogonality to all `y a` is the matrix
         equation `H * sourceHeadMetric d r hrD * headCoords(res u) = 0`.
         Both factors are invertible, so all head coordinates of `res u`
         vanish.
      6. Define
         `q u beta := res u (finSourceTail (Nat.le_of_lt hrD) beta)`.
         The zero-head-coordinate result proves
         `res u = sourceTailEmbed d r hrD (q u)` by splitting each spacetime
         coordinate into `finSourceHead` or `finSourceTail`.
      7. With `p := { head := H, mixed := L, tail := q }`, the head equality
         is `h╬øhead` and the tail equality is the residual decomposition.
         Apply
         `sourceOriented_headGaugeNormalParameterData_of_lorentz_head_tail`.

      The last line of the producer proof is now checked by
      `BHW.sourceOriented_headGaugeNormalParameterData`: the Witt/head-frame
      argument supplies `╬ø` through `sourceOriented_headGaugeWittData`, and the
      existing tail-coordinate consumer plus
      `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_complexLorentzAction` assemble the
      normal-parameter data.
      The blockwise variant
      `sourceOriented_headGaugeNormalParameterData_of_lorentz_head_tail` is
      also checked for future refactors that prove head and tail equalities
      separately and assemble the pointwise equality by `finSourceHead_tail_cases`.

      The residual polydisc is not an arbitrary compact parameter set.  It is
      the finite product of the normal-form head factor, mixed coefficient,
      and tail residual-vector coordinates:

      ```lean
      structure BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n) where
        head : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé
        mixed : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé
        tail : Fin (n - r) -> Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé

      def BHW.sourceOrientedNormalCenterParameter
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn :=
        { head := 1
          mixed := 0
          tail := 0 }

      def BHW.sourceTailEmbed
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (q : Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé

      def BHW.sourceHeadMetric
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé :=
        Matrix.diagonal fun a =>
          (MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d
            (BHW.finSourceHead (Nat.le_of_lt hrD) a) : Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.sourceHeadMetric_transpose
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          (BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD).transpose =
            BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD

      theorem BHW.sourceHeadMetric_det_isUnit
          (d r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          IsUnit (BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD).det

      def BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner
          (d : Nat)
          (x y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) : Ōäé :=
        Ōłæ ╬╝ : Fin (d + 1),
          (MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d ╬╝ : Ōäé) * x ╬╝ * y ╬╝

      theorem BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_hwLemma3CanonicalSource_head
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (a b : Fin r) :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
              (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r)
              (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
              (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b) =
            BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD a b

      def BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector
          [NeZero d]
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (a : Fin r) :
          Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun ╬╝ =>
          Ōłæ b : Fin r,
            p.head a b *
              BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r
                (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b) ╬╝

      def BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
          [NeZero d]
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun i =>
          if hhead : Ōłā a : Fin r, i = BHW.finSourceHead hrn a then
              let a := Classical.choose hhead
              BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn p a
          else
            let htail : Ōłā u : Fin (n - r),
                i = BHW.finSourceTail hrn u :=
              (BHW.finSourceHead_tail_cases hrn i).resolve_left hhead
            let u := Classical.choose htail
            fun ╬╝ =>
              (Ōłæ a : Fin r,
                p.mixed u a *
                  BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector
                    d n r hrD hrn p a ╬╝) +
              BHW.sourceTailEmbed d r hrD (p.tail u) ╬╝

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_head
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (a : Fin r) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p
              (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a) =
            BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn p a

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_tail
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (u : Fin (n - r)) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p
              (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u) =
            fun ╬╝ =>
              (Ōłæ a : Fin r,
                p.mixed u a *
                  BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector
                    d n r hrD hrn p a ╬╝) +
              BHW.sourceTailEmbed d r hrD (p.tail u) ╬╝

      theorem BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner_sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (a b : Fin r) :
          BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn p a)
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn p b) =
            (p.head * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD *
              p.head.transpose) a b

      theorem BHW.sourceNormalHeadGram_transpose
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn) :
          (p.head * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD *
              p.head.transpose).transpose =
            p.head * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD * p.head.transpose

      theorem BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner_headVector_sourceTailEmbed
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (a : Fin r)
          (q : Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn p a)
              (BHW.sourceTailEmbed d r hrD q) = 0

      theorem BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner_sourceTailEmbed_headVector
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (a : Fin r)
          (q : Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceTailEmbed d r hrD q)
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn p a) = 0

      theorem BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner_head_tailParameterVector
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (a : Fin r)
          (u : Fin (n - r)) :
          BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn p a)
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p
                (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)) =
            ((p.head * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD * p.head.transpose) *
              p.mixed.transpose) a u

      theorem BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner_tailParameterVector_head
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (u : Fin (n - r))
          (a : Fin r) :
          BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p
                (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u))
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector d n r hrD hrn p a) =
            (p.mixed *
              (p.head * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD *
                p.head.transpose)) u a

      theorem BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner_mixedHeadPart_sourceTailEmbed
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (u : Fin (n - r))
          (q : Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ =>
                Ōłæ a : Fin r,
                  p.mixed u a *
                    BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector
                      d n r hrD hrn p a ╬╝)
              (BHW.sourceTailEmbed d r hrD q) = 0

      theorem BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner_sourceTailEmbed_mixedHeadPart
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (q : Fin (d + 1 - r) -> Ōäé)
          (v : Fin (n - r)) :
          BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceTailEmbed d r hrD q)
              (fun ╬╝ =>
                Ōłæ b : Fin r,
                  p.mixed v b *
                    BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector
                      d n r hrD hrn p b ╬╝) = 0

      theorem BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner_mixedHeadPart_mixedHeadPart
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (u v : Fin (n - r)) :
          BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ =>
                Ōłæ a : Fin r,
                  p.mixed u a *
                    BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector
                      d n r hrD hrn p a ╬╝)
              (fun ╬╝ =>
                Ōłæ b : Fin r,
                  p.mixed v b *
                    BHW.sourceOrientedNormalHeadVector
                      d n r hrD hrn p b ╬╝) =
            (p.mixed *
              (p.head * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD *
                p.head.transpose) *
              p.mixed.transpose) u v

      theorem BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner_tailParameterVector_tail
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (u v : Fin (n - r)) :
          BHW.sourceVectorMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p
                (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u))
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p
                (BHW.finSourceTail hrn v)) =
            (p.mixed *
                (p.head * BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD *
                  p.head.transpose) *
      ```
