      `sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur_headTail_eq_source_det`.  Downstream
      code must call this theorem rather than redoing multilinearity or
      dividing by a determinant inferred only from the Gram block.

      The actual residual-vector setup for the non-selected exterior proof is
      now checked.  Define
      `sourceActualSchurResidualVector d n r hrn z R.L u` as
      `z_tail u - Ōłæ a, R.L u a ŌĆó z_head a`.  The checked decomposition theorem
      rewrites each actual tail vector as its head projection plus this
      residual, and the checked orthogonality theorems
      `sourceActualSchurResidualVector_inner_head` and
      `sourceActualSchurResidualVector_head_inner` prove that the residual
      lies in the orthogonal complement of the selected head span.  The checked
      theorem `sourceActualSchurResidualVector_inner_residual` then identifies
      the Gram matrix of the actual residual vectors with the stored
      Schur-complement coordinate `R.tail.gram`.  Its Lean proof avoids a
      separate symmetric-matrix quadratic-form lemma: decompose only the left
      tail vector to replace the first residual by the actual tail vector, then
      expand the right residual and use `R.L * R.A = mixed` to identify the
      single mixed projection term with `(R.L * R.A * R.LßĄĆ) u v`.

      The remaining non-selected determinant proof is now exactly the oriented
      complement-volume propagation theorem.  Its Lean-ready decomposition is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceActualSchurResidual_selectedFrameDet
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (L : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) Ōå¬ Fin (n - r)) :
          Matrix.det
              (fun k ╬╝ =>
                if h : k.val < r then
                  z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn Ō¤©k.val, hŌ¤®) ╬╝
                else
                  BHW.sourceActualSchurResidualVector d n r hrn z L
                    (lam Ō¤©k.val - r, by omegaŌ¤®) ╬╝) =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail d n r hrD hrn lam) z

      theorem BHW.sourceActualSchurResidual_fullFrameExpansion
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (L : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z =
            -- the Cauchy-Binet/Laplace expansion obtained by replacing each
            -- selected tail vector by `headProjection + actualResidual`.
            -- Terms are indexed by the rows whose contribution is taken from
            -- the selected head span; repeated head rows vanish by
            -- alternation, and the surviving terms pair a head coefficient
            -- minor with an actual residual complement-volume minor.
            BHW.sourceActualSchurResidualFullFrameExpansion
              d n r hrD hrn z L ╬╣

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedSchur_complementVolumePropagation
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z))
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
              R.headFactor R.L R.tail ╬╣ =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hH : H Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hA :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G).det)
          (hgram : H.gram = G.gram)
          (hheadTail :
            ŌłĆ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) Ōå¬ Fin (n - r),
              H.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam) =
                G.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam)) :
          H.det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq_of_exists_nonzero
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hH : H Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hgram : H.gram = G.gram)
          (hheadTail :
            ŌłĆ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) Ōå¬ Fin (n - r),
              H.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam) =
                G.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam))
          (hNZ :
            Ōłā lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) Ōå¬ Fin (n - r),
              G.det
                (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                  d n r hrD hrn lam) ŌēĀ 0) :
          H.det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_zero_of_not_maxRank
          (d n : Nat)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hlow : ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G) :
          G.det = 0

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_of_not_maxRank
          (d n : Nat)
          {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hH : H Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hgram : H.gram = G.gram)
          (hlowG : ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G) :
          H.det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq_of_allZero_notMaxRank
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (hallZero_notMax :
            ŌłĆ {K : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n},
              K Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ->
              IsUnit
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn K).det ->
              (ŌłĆ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) Ōå¬ Fin (n - r),
                K.det
                    (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                      d n r hrD hrn lam) = 0) ->
              ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n K)
          {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hH : H Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hA :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G).det)
          (hgram : H.gram = G.gram)
          (hheadTail :
            ŌłĆ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) Ōå¬ Fin (n - r),
              H.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam) =
                G.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam)) :
          H.det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_notMaxRank_of_headTailDet_eq_zero
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hA :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G).det)
          (hzero :
            ŌłĆ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) Ōå¬ Fin (n - r),
              G.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam) = 0) :
          ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedSchur_fullFrameDet_reconstruct_of_headTailPropagation
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (hprop :
            ŌłĆ {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n},
              G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ->
              H Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ->
              IsUnit
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G).det ->
              H.gram = G.gram ->
              (ŌłĆ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) Ōå¬ Fin (n - r),
                H.det
                    (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                      d n r hrD hrn lam) =
                  G.det
                    (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                      d n r hrD hrn lam)) ->
              H.det = G.det)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
              R.headFactor R.L R.tail ╬╣ =
            G.det ╬╣
      ```

      The first theorem is now checked in Lean as
      `sourceActualSchurResidual_selectedFrameDet`, with the explicit support
      definitions `sourceActualSchurSelectedOriginalMatrix`,
      `sourceActualSchurSelectedResidualMatrix`, and
      `sourceSchurHeadTailRowOperation`: subtracting the `L`-linear combination
      of the selected head rows from the selected tail rows is multiplication
      by a block lower-triangular row-operation matrix whose determinant is
      `1`.  The checked theorem
      `sourceActualSchurResidual_selectedFrameDet_eq_headFactor_mul_tail_det`
      combines that row operation with `R.tail_det_eq`, proving that each
      selected actual residual head-tail determinant is exactly
      `R.headFactor.det * R.tail.det lam`.  This theorem is only the selected
      head-tail calibration case; it must not be generalized by row operations
      to an arbitrary selected frame, because an arbitrary frame need not
      contain the head rows being subtracted.  The second theorem is the finite
      multilinear expansion for arbitrary frames: substitute
      `tail = headProjection + actualResidual`, expand by determinant
      multilinearity, and kill repeated-head terms by alternation.  The third
      theorem is the only geometric comparison: the actual residual tuple and
      the stored shifted-tail tuple have the same residual Gram by
      `sourceActualSchurResidualVector_inner_residual`; their selected
      complement-volume coordinates are calibrated by
      `sourceActualSchurResidual_selectedFrameDet_eq_headFactor_mul_tail_det`;
      the shifted-tail oriented variety equations then propagate those
      complement-volume coordinates to every residual minor appearing in the
      finite expansion.  The same hard content is now isolated as
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq`: two
      oriented source-variety points with the same ordinary Gram coordinates,
      an invertible selected head Gram block, and matching all selected
      head-tail full-frame determinants have identical determinant-coordinate
      functions.  The checked theorem
      `sourceOrientedSchur_fullFrameDet_reconstruct_of_headTailPropagation`
      proves that this single propagation theorem mechanically implies the
      Schur reconstruction equality for every ordered full frame: choose a
      shifted-tail realization from `R.tail_mem`, build the normal parameter,
      use the checked Gram realization theorem and the checked selected
      head-tail determinant theorem, then apply the propagation theorem and
      rewrite by `sourceFullFrameDet_normalParameter_eq_schurFormula`.  A Gemini
      theorem-shape check on 2026-05-05 agreed that this is the missing
      oriented-volume uniqueness/propagation input and explicitly warned that
      both Gram-only and naive arbitrary-row operation arguments are false.
      The determinant-nonzero branch of this propagation theorem is now
      checked as
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq_of_exists_nonzero`
      in `SourceOrientedSchurPropagation.lean`: if some selected `head Ōł¬ lam`
      determinant is nonzero, use that full frame as the selected chart,
      recover equality of selected full-frame coordinates from `hgram` and
      `hheadTail`, recover equality of mixed rows from `hgram`, and call the
      checked full-frame identity theorem
      `sourceOrientedGramData_eq_of_selectedCoord_eq_mixedRows_eq`.  The
      non-max-rank bridge is also checked in the same file:
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_zero_of_not_maxRank` proves that
      outside `SourceOrientedMaxRankAt` all full-frame determinant coordinates
      vanish, and
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq_of_allZero_notMaxRank`
      assembles the full propagation theorem from the all-zero branch.  The
      all-zero branch itself is now checked as
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_notMaxRank_of_headTailDet_eq_zero` in the
      hard range `d + 1 <= n`: its contrapositive first proves
      `sourceHeadRows_linearIndependent_of_schurHeadBlock_isUnit`, then uses
      `exists_headTail_fullFrameDet_ne_zero_of_headRows_linearIndependent_span_top`
      to extend the nondegenerate head rows to a full source frame by tail rows
      after quotienting by the head span.  Therefore the hard-range theorem
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq` is checked.
      The Schur reconstruction wrappers
      `sourceOrientedSchur_fullFrameDet_reconstruct`,
      `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur_det`, and
      `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur` are also checked.
      This is a finite basis-extension theorem; it is not a full-frame chart
      argument and not a Gram-only comparison.

      From this chart the local analytic estimates become genuine theorem
      surfaces:

      ```lean
      def BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChart
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hlow :
            ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_locallyBounded_of_residualChart
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_ext_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hlow :
            ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)) :
          Ōłā ╬® : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n),
            IsOpen ╬® Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 Ōłł ╬® Ōł¦
            Ōłā C : ŌäØ, 0 <= C Ōł¦
              ŌłĆ G,
                G Ōłł ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n ->
                ŌĆ¢BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F GŌĆ¢ <= C

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_of_residualChart
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_ext_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hlow :
            ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)) :
          ContinuousWithinAt
            (BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F)
            (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)
      ```

      The boundedness proof is compactness: `extendF F Ōłś toVec` is continuous
      on compact `K`, hence bounded; `image_surj` and `hBranch` rewrite every
      quotient value in the nearby oriented domain to a value on that compact
      family.  The continuity proof is the same first-countable compact
      cluster argument made explicit below: for a sequence `G_j -> G0` in the
      within-domain filter, choose parameters `c_j Ōłł K` with invariant `G_j`;
      compactness gives convergent subsequences in `K`, continuity of the
      invariant gives invariant `G0`, and `hBranch` identifies all possible
      cluster values with `extendF F z0`.  The dense max-rank parameter field
      is then used to identify the normal-space extension obtained from the
      regular stratum with this branch-defined value at the singular point.

      Historical extended-tube residual-chart sketch.  This block is not the
      active no-tube connected-basis producer, and its ambient
      `SourceOrientedInvariantTransportEquiv` may only be used for genuine
      full-coordinate homeomorphisms.  Source-matrix normal-form changes must
      use the variety-relative packet
      `SourceOrientedRankDeficientAlgebraicNormalFormData` together with
      `N.originalNormalVarietyPoint`, as recorded in the active ledger below.
      The following order remains useful only for the later compact
      extended-tube residual chart where tube-stability fields are present.

      ```lean
      structure BHW.SourceOrientedInvariantTransportEquiv
          (d n : Nat) where
        toHomeomorph :
          BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n ŌēāŌé£
            BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n
        mem_variety_iff :
          ŌłĆ G,
            toHomeomorph G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n Ōåö
              G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n
        maxRank_iff :
          ŌłĆ G,
            BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n (toHomeomorph G) Ōåö
              BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G
      -- Checked derived API:
      -- `toFun`, `invFun`, `left_inv`, `right_inv`, `continuous_toFun`,
      -- `continuous_invFun`, `isOpen_invFun_image`,
      -- `invFun_mem_variety_iff`, `invFun_maxRank_iff`, and `refl`.

      structure BHW.HWLemma3AdaptedNormalForm
          [NeZero d]
          (d n r : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) where
        rank_eq :
          r =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        span_finrank_eq :
          Module.finrank Ōäé
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) = r

      structure BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) where
        r : Nat
        hrD : r < d + 1
        hrn : r <= n
        adaptedBase : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        adaptedBase_mem : adaptedBase Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        adaptedBase_same_oriented :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n adaptedBase =
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0
        normalBase : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        normalBase_eq :
          normalBase = BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r
        varietyTransport :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n
        toOriginal :
          (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ->
            Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        toOriginal_continuous : Continuous toOriginal
        toOriginal_normalBase_eq_adaptedBase :
          toOriginal normalBase = adaptedBase
        toOriginal_normalBase_invariant :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toOriginal normalBase) =
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0
        toOriginal_oriented :
          ŌłĆ z,
            BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (toOriginal z) =
            varietyTransport.invFun
              (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z)

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_notMaxRank_sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_fullFrame
          (n : Nat)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hlow :
            ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z)) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1 := by
        -- Checked in `SourceOrientedRankBridge.lean`.  First prove strict
        -- inequality below `min (d+1) n`, using the rank upper bound for
        -- actual source configurations and failure of the equality defining
        -- `SourceOrientedMaxRankAt`; then compose with
        -- `min (d+1) n <= d+1`.
        exact
          BHW.sourceOriented_notMaxRank_sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_fullFrame
            d n z hlow

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_eq_zero_of_sourceRank_lt
          (d n : Nat)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z = 0 := by
        by_contra hdet
        let M := BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ╬╣ z
        have hGramDet :
            (Matrix.of (BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d M)).det ŌēĀ 0 := by
          rw [BHW.sourceFullFrameGram_det_eq]
          exact mul_ne_zero (BHW.minkowskiMetricDet_ne_zero d)
            (pow_ne_zero 2 hdet)
        have hminor :
            Matrix.det
                (fun a b : Fin (d + 1) =>
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z (╬╣ a) (╬╣ b)) ŌēĀ 0 := by
          simpa [M, BHW.sourceFullFrameGram_sourceFullFrameMatrix]
            using hGramDet
        have hrank_ge :
            d + 1 Ōēż
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) := by
          have h :=
            BHW.matrix_rank_ge_of_nondegenerate_square_submatrix
              (A := Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n =>
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z i j)
              (I := ╬╣) (J := ╬╣) hminor
          simpa [BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank] using h
        exact (not_le_of_gt hrank) hrank_ge

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_eq_of_sameGram_rank_lt
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w := by
        apply BHW.SourceOrientedGramData.ext
        ┬Ę simpa [BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant,
            BHW.SourceOrientedGramData.gram] using hgram
        ┬Ę funext ╬╣
          have hzdet :
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z = 0 :=
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_eq_zero_of_sourceRank_lt
              d n hrank ╬╣
          have hrank_w :
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) < d + 1 := by
            simpa [hgram] using hrank
          have hwdet :
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ w = 0 :=
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_eq_zero_of_sourceRank_lt
              d n hrank_w ╬╣
          simp [BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant,
            BHW.SourceOrientedGramData.det, hzdet, hwdet]

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource_head_unit
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n) :
          IsUnit
            (Matrix.det
              (fun a b =>
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r)
                  (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
                  (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b))) := by
        -- The canonical head Gram block is the signature diagonal
        -- `sourceHeadMetric d r hrD`; every diagonal entry is ┬▒1, hence the
        -- determinant is a unit.
        exact
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource_head_unit
            d n r hrD hrn

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource_tail_zero
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n) :
          BHW.sourceTailResidualCoordinates d n r hrD hrn
              (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r) = 0 := by
        -- The canonical source has zero vectors on the tail labels.
        ext u ╬╝
        simp [BHW.sourceTailResidualCoordinates,
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource]

      noncomputable def BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n :=
        Ō¤©BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z, Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.exists_sourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv_of_normalFormTransport
          [NeZero d]
          (r : Nat)
          {base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r base) :
          Ōłā Q : BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n,
            (ŌłĆ z,
              BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (T.toNormalVec z) =
                Q.toFun (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z)) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z,
              BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (T.toNormalVec.symm z) =
                Q.invFun (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z)) := by
        exact
          Ō¤©T.varietyTransport, T.variety_commute,
            T.variety_symm_commuteŌ¤®

      noncomputable def
        BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.ofHWNormalFormTransport
          [NeZero d]
          (r : Nat)
          {base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r base) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n :=
        Classical.choose
          (BHW.exists_sourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv_of_normalFormTransport
            (d := d) (n := n) r T)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyPoint_of_normalFormTransport_symm
          [NeZero d]
          (r : Nat)
          {base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r base)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (T.toNormalVec.symm z) =
            (BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.ofHWNormalFormTransport
              (d := d) (n := n) r T).invFun
              (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z) := by
        exact
          (Classical.choose_spec
            (BHW.exists_sourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv_of_normalFormTransport
              (d := d) (n := n) r T)).2 z

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyPoint_toOriginal_of_normalFormTransport
          [NeZero d]
          (r : Nat)
          {base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r base)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (T.toNormalVec.symm z) =
            (BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.ofHWNormalFormTransport
              (d := d) (n := n) r T).invFun
              (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z) := by
        -- This is the source-variety transport statement, not an ambient
        -- coordinate-homeomorphism statement on arbitrary determinant data.
        exact
          BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyPoint_of_normalFormTransport_symm
            (d := d) (n := n) r T z

      theorem
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.exists_ofExtendedTube
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hlow :
            ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)) :
          Ōłā N : BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0,
            N.adaptedBase Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        -- Checked in `SourceOrientedRankDeficientTubeResidual.lean`.
        -- The proof calls
        -- `BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative`, converts
        -- same scalar Gram to same oriented invariant by
        -- `BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_eq_of_sameGram_rank_lt`,
        -- then uses
        -- `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.exists_ofAdaptedBase`.
        exact
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.exists_ofExtendedTube
            (d := d) hd hz0 hlow

      structure BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData
          (d n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0) where
        m : Nat
        c0 : Fin m -> Ōäé
        K : Set (Fin m -> Ōäé)
        P : Set (Fin m -> Ōäé)
        K_compact : IsCompact K
        P_open : IsOpen P
        P_subset_K : P ŌŖå K
        c0_mem : c0 Ōłł P
        residualVector :
          (Fin m -> Ōäé) -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        residualVector_continuousOn :
          ContinuousOn residualVector K
        residualVector_c0 :
          residualVector c0 = N.normalBase
        toOriginal_residualVector_mem_ET :
          ŌłĆ c Ōłł K,
            N.toOriginal (residualVector c) Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        ╬® : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
        ╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®
        ╬®_center :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 Ōłł ╬®
        originalInvariant_mem :
          ŌłĆ c Ōłł P,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (N.toOriginal (residualVector c)) Ōłł
              ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n
        image_surj :
          ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ŌŖå
            (fun c =>
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (N.toOriginal (residualVector c))) '' P
        maxRank_dense_original :
          ŌłĆ c Ōłł P,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (N.toOriginal (residualVector c)) Ōłł
              closure
                ((fun c' =>
                  BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                    (N.toOriginal (residualVector c'))) ''
                  {c' | c' Ōłł P Ōł¦
                    BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
                      (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                        (N.toOriginal (residualVector c')))})

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydiscData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData d n N := by
        exact
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_tubeResidualPolydisc
            (d := d) hd N

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_tubeStability
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0)
          (D :
            BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData d n N) :
          ŌłĆ c, c Ōłł D.K ->
            N.toOriginal (D.residualVector c) Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
        D.toOriginal_residualVector_mem_ET

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_imageSurj
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0)
          (D :
            BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData d n N) :
          Ōłā ╬® : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n),
            IsOpen ╬® Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 Ōłł ╬® Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ c, c Ōłł D.P ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (N.toOriginal (D.residualVector c)) Ōłł
                ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n) Ōł¦
            ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              (fun c =>
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                  (N.toOriginal (D.residualVector c))) '' D.P :=
        Ō¤©D.╬®, D.╬®_open, D.╬®_center, D.originalInvariant_mem, D.image_surjŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_maxRankDense
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0)
          (D :
            BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData d n N) :
          ŌłĆ c, c Ōłł D.P ->
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (N.toOriginal (D.residualVector c)) Ōłł
              closure
                ((fun c' =>
                  BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                    (N.toOriginal (D.residualVector c'))) ''
                  {c' | c' Ōłł D.P Ōł¦
                    BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
                      (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                        (N.toOriginal (D.residualVector c')))}) :=
        D.maxRank_dense_original
      ```

      `SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.exists_ofExtendedTube`
      consumes the checked ordinary adapted Hall-Wightman Lemma-3
      representative `adaptedBase` in the extended tube, then records the
      canonical normal-form source
      `normalBase = hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r` separately.  This
      separation is load-bearing.  The canonical normal source is the point at
      which the head block is the signature diagonal
      `sourceHeadMetric d r hrD` and the tail is zero; it is not asserted to
      lie in `ExtendedTube`.  The inverse map `toOriginal`
      transports normal-form source tuples back to the adapted extended-tube
      coordinates.  The source normal-form transport may be used on actual
      source tuples, but the proof may not require it to be an ambient
      homeomorphism of all oriented Gram/determinant coordinates.
      `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_tubeResidualPolydisc` then chooses a
      compact residual control polydisc `K` and an open Schur parameter set
      `P ŌŖå K` in normal coordinates after all tube shrinkings.  Its field
      `toOriginal_residualVector_mem_ET`, not any global invariance of the
      normal-form transport, is the theorem that every chosen residual tuple
      in `K` lands back in `ExtendedTube`.  Its fields `╬®`,
      `originalInvariant_mem`, `image_surj`, and
      `maxRank_dense_original` are already in the original oriented
      coordinates, with `originalInvariant_mem` asserted on `P`, the same
      open set used by `image_surj`.  In particular, `╬®_open` must be an ambient open set in
      `SourceOrientedGramData`, chosen after the variety-relative normal-form
      image has been pulled back and represented by an ambient open
      neighborhood.  The residual-chart assembly must not manufacture this
      ambient open set by applying an unavailable ambient source-matrix
      coordinate homeomorphism.  Image surjectivity is the Schur theorem: for
      a nearby oriented-variety point, compute its head block, mixed
      coefficients, and residual oriented tail; realize the shifted residual
      tail by
      `sourceShiftedTailSmallRealization`;
      reconstruct the full normal-form source tuple; and transport back.
      Max-rank density perturbs only the residual Schur complement and
      determinant sheet inside `P`, then reconstructs by the same formula.
      The checked connectedness theorem to use after this rewrite is
      `BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchur_varietyTransported_orientedMaxRank_preimage_of_eq`.
      The normal Schur parameter is first bundled as a
      `BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n` point, then the source-change factor is
      applied by `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.invFun`; its
      `invFun_maxRank_iff` absorbs the inverse transport in the max-rank
      predicate.  The older ambient
      `BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchur_transported_orientedMaxRank_parameterSet`
      is reserved for genuine full-coordinate oriented-invariant
      homeomorphisms, not for determinant-coordinate source changes.

      The proof body of the chart theorem is consequently fixed.  The Lean
      interface part of this correction is now checked in
      `OSReconstruction/ComplexLieGroups/Connectedness/BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedRankDeficientTubeResidual.lean`:
      `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData` records the actual
      source-level `toOriginal` return map and its variety-relative
      compatibility, `BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData` records the
      compact residual family with original-coordinate `╬®`,
      `originalInvariant_mem`, `image_surj`, and `maxRank_dense_original`, and
      `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.ofSourceMatrixLorentz`
      checks the concrete return-map construction from an invertible source
      matrix and Lorentz normalizing equation:
      `toOriginal z = sourceTupleLinearChange d n MŌü╗┬╣
      (complexLorentzAction ╬øŌü╗┬╣ z)`,
      `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.exists_ofAdaptedBase`
      packages the existing Schur normal-form matrix construction and
      Lorentz/Witt normalization once an adapted ET base is supplied, and
      `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.exists_ofExtendedTube`
      combines the checked adapted ET representative with the rank-deficient
      same-oriented-invariant conversion to produce the source-level normal
      form data from an original extended-tube tuple,
      `BHW.sourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartProducer_of_tubeResidualPolydiscProducer`
      mechanically converts such a producer into the already checked
      `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartProducer`.  This is only
      the checked interface/reducer plus normal-form producer; the hard theorem
      remains the construction of the tube residual-polydisc fields.

      Notice that
      it consumes the original-coordinate polydisc fields directly; it does
      not apply `SourceOrientedInvariantTransportEquiv.invFun` to a normal
      open set.

      ```lean
      noncomputable def
          BHW.sourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartProducer_of_tubeResidualPolydiscProducer
          (H : BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientTubeResidualPolydiscProducer d n) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartProducer d n := by
        intro z0 hz0 hlow
        exact (H hz0 hlow).2.to_residualChart hz0
      ```

      The transport lemmas used in this script are finite linear algebra:
      source permutations and determinant-`1` Lorentz changes are homeomorphisms
      of the oriented invariant coordinate space, preserve
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety`, and preserve the max-rank predicate.  They
      do **not** assert that an arbitrary normal-form source tuple remains in
      `ExtendedTube` after inverse source-coordinate transport.  Extended-tube
      membership is supplied only by the shrunk residual-polydisc field
      `toOriginal_residualVector_mem_ET` and its theorem wrapper
      `sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_tubeStability`.  This separation is
      essential: Hall-Wightman's source-coordinate changes are finite
      linear-algebra devices, while tube stability is the analytic
      coefficient-freedom theorem.

      Finally, the compactness arguments used downstream should appear as
      small SCV/topology lemmas rather than as prose inside the descent proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_compactBound
          {d n : Nat}
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (R :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0)
          (hF_ext_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n)) :
          Ōłā C : ŌäØ, 0 <= C Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ c Ōłł R.K, ŌĆ¢BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c)ŌĆ¢ <= C

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter
          {d n : Nat}
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (R :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          {c : Fin R.m -> Ōäé}
          (hc : c Ōłł R.P)
          (hcG :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (R.toVec c) = G) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F G =
            BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_clusterValue
          {d n : Nat}
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (R :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0)
          {c : Fin R.m -> Ōäé}
          (hc : c Ōłł R.K)
          (hc0 :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (R.toVec c) =
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0) :
          BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c) = BHW.extendF F z0
      ```

      Lean checkpoint: `SourceOrientedScalarRepresentative.lean` checks
      `sourceOrientedResidualChart_compactBound`,
      `sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter`,
      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_locallyBounded_of_residualChart`, and
      `sourceOrientedResidualChart_clusterValue`.  The same file also checks
      the compact-parameter continuity helper
      `SCV.continuousWithinAt_of_compact_parameter_surjection` and its
      rank-deficient consumer
      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_of_residualChart`.
      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_locallyBounded_of_residualChart` is
      `compactBound` plus `image_surj` and
      `sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter`.
      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_of_residualChart` is the
      compact-parameter separation theorem applied to `R.K`, followed by
      `sourceOrientedResidualChart_clusterValue`: for every open neighborhood
      `V` of the center value, the bad set `K Ōł® value Ōü╗┬╣' VßČ£` has compact
      closed parameter image away from the center invariant, so its complement
      gives the required target neighborhood.  No sequential/subnet abstraction
      layer is needed.
      No choice of parameters may be made outside `R.K`; otherwise the
      compactness proof and the branch-law identification break.

      Production-order correction for these compactness helpers: the
      definitions `sourceOrientedQuotientValue` and
      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_wellDefined`, displayed below with the
      descent theorem, must be placed before
      `sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter` in Lean.  The
      residual-chart theorem statements are grouped here because they belong
      mathematically to the rank-deficient chart, but their proof scripts use
      the quotient-value well-definedness lemma.

      The compact-bound proof is ordinary Mathlib compactness.  The only
      source-specific input is that `R.toVec` maps the whole closed parameter
      polydisc into the extended tube:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_compactBound ... := by
        classical
        have hcomp :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun c : Fin R.m -> Ōäé => BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c))
              R.K := by
          exact hF_ext_cont.comp R.toVec_continuousOn R.toVec_mem
        have hnorm :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun c : Fin R.m -> Ōäé =>
                ŌĆ¢BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c)ŌĆ¢)
              R.K := hcomp.norm
        obtain Ō¤©B, hBŌ¤® :=
          R.K_compact.exists_bound_of_continuousOn
            (f := fun c : Fin R.m -> Ōäé =>
              ŌĆ¢BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c)ŌĆ¢)
            hnorm
        refine Ō¤©max 0 B, le_max_left _ _, ?_Ō¤®
        intro c hc
        exact le_trans (hB c hc) (le_max_right _ _)
      ```

      The quotient-to-parameter rewrite is also mechanical after
      well-definedness.  The membership proof for `R.toVec c` uses
      `R.P_subset_K`; no separate hypothesis that `G` lies in the
      extended-tube oriented image is needed, because the equality
      `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (R.toVec c) = G` supplies the
      required image witness:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter ... := by
        have hcK : c Ōłł R.K := R.P_subset_K hc
        exact
          BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_wellDefined
            (d := d) F hBranch
            (R.toVec_mem c hcK) hcG
      ```

      Local boundedness near the rank-deficient point is then not another
      analytic theorem.  Use the residual chart itself as the neighborhood,
      bound the compact parameter family, and rewrite every local quotient
      value through `image_surj`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_locallyBounded_of_residualChart
          ... := by
        classical
        let R :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChart
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hlow
        obtain Ō¤©C, hC_nonneg, hCŌ¤® :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_compactBound
            (d := d) F R hF_ext_cont
        refine Ō¤©R.╬®, R.╬®_open, R.center_mem, C, hC_nonneg, ?_Ō¤®
        intro G hG
        rcases R.image_surj Ō¤©hG.1, ?_Ō¤® with Ō¤©c, hcP, hcGŌ¤®
        ┬Ę have hcK : c Ōłł R.K := R.P_subset_K hcP
          have hq :
              BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F G =
                BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c) :=
            BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter
              (d := d) F hBranch R hcP hcG
          simpa [hq] using hC c hcK
        ┬Ę exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_subset_variety
              (d := d) (n := n) hG.2
      ```

      The auxiliary subset theorem in the last line is definitional:
      `sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n` is the image of
      `BHW.ExtendedTube d n` under
      `BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n`, while
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety d n` is the full range of that same map.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_subset_variety
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n ŌŖå
            BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
        intro G hG
        rcases hG with Ō¤©z, _hzET, rflŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®
      ```

      Continuity at a rank-deficient point requires one reusable topology
      lemma so the Lean proof does not hide compactness inside the
      Hall-Wightman descent.  The checked helper is a compact-separation
      theorem: a quotient function locally represented by a compact parameter
      family is continuous at the base point when all compact parameters over
      the base fibre have the base value.

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.continuousWithinAt_of_compact_parameter_surjection
          {X Y : Type*}
          [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace Y]
          [T2Space X] [T2Space Y]
          {K P : Set X} {╬® U : Set Y}
          {y0 : Y}
          {param : X -> Y}
          {value : X -> Ōäé}
          {phi : Y -> Ōäé}
          (hK_compact : IsCompact K)
          (hP_sub : P ŌŖå K)
          (hparam_cont : ContinuousOn param K)
          (hvalue_cont : ContinuousOn value K)
          (hy0U : y0 Ōłł U)
          (h╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®)
          (hy0╬® : y0 Ōłł ╬®)
          (hsurj :
            ŌłĆ y, y Ōłł ╬® Ōł® U -> Ōłā c, c Ōłł P Ōł¦ param c = y)
          (hphi_param :
            ŌłĆ y, y Ōłł ╬® Ōł® U ->
              ŌłĆ c Ōłł P, param c = y -> phi y = value c)
          (hcluster_base :
            ŌłĆ c Ōłł K, param c = y0 -> value c = phi y0) :
          ContinuousWithinAt phi U y0
      ```

      Proof transcript for this helper.  Given an open neighborhood `V` of
      `phi y0`, set `Bad := K Ōł® value Ōü╗┬╣' VßČ£`.  The field
      `hvalue_cont` and compactness of `K` make `Bad` compact; `hparam_cont`
      makes `param '' Bad` compact and hence closed in the Hausdorff target.
      The cluster-base hypothesis proves `y0 Ōłē param '' Bad`: any bad
      parameter over `y0` would have `value c = phi y0 Ōłł V`, contradicting
      badness.  Therefore `(param '' Bad)ßČ£ Ōł® ╬®` is an open target
      neighborhood of `y0`.  For `y` in this neighborhood and in `U`,
      `hsurj` chooses `c Ōłł P` with `param c = y`; if `value c Ōłē V`, then
      `c Ōłł Bad` and hence `y Ōłł param '' Bad`, contradiction.  Thus
      `value c Ōłł V`, and `hphi_param` transfers this to `phi y Ōłł V`.

      With that helper, the rank-deficient continuity proof has no hidden
      analytic step:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_of_residualChart
          ... := by
        classical
        let R :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChart
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hlow
        have hvalue_cont :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun c : Fin R.m -> Ōäé => BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c))
              R.K := by
          exact hF_ext_cont.comp R.toVec_continuousOn R.toVec_mem
        have hparam_cont :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun c : Fin R.m -> Ōäé =>
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (R.toVec c))
              R.K := by
          exact
            (BHW.continuous_sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant
              (d := d) (n := n)).continuousOn.comp
              R.toVec_continuousOn
              (by intro c hc; exact Set.mem_univ _)
        have hy0U :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 Ōłł
            BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n := by
          exact Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤®
        refine
          SCV.continuousWithinAt_of_compact_parameter_surjection
            (K := R.K) (P := R.P)
            (╬® := R.╬®)
            (U := BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
            (y0 := BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)
            (param := fun c =>
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (R.toVec c))
            (value := fun c => BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c))
            (phi := BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F)
            R.K_compact R.P_subset_K hparam_cont hvalue_cont hy0U
            R.╬®_open R.center_mem ?_ ?_ ?_
        ┬Ę intro G hG
          exact R.image_surj Ō¤©hG.1,
            BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_subset_variety
              (d := d) (n := n) hG.2Ō¤®
        ┬Ę intro G hG c hcP hcG
          exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter
              (d := d) F hBranch R hcP hcG
        ┬Ę intro c hcK hc0
          have hcenter :
              BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F
                  (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0) =
                BHW.extendF F z0 :=
            BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_wellDefined
              (d := d) F hBranch hz0 rfl
          exact
            (BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_clusterValue
              (d := d) F hBranch R hcK hc0).trans hcenter.symm
      ```

      Finally, the cluster-value lemma uses the branch law only once.  The
      residual chart stores the original endpoint membership
      `R.center_mem_ET`; it does not try to derive `z0 Ōłł ExtendedTube d n`
      from equality of source tuples at the compact-parameter center.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_clusterValue ... := by
        have hz_param : R.toVec c Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          R.toVec_mem c hc
        exact hBranch hz_param R.center_mem_ET hc0
      ```

      At the point where this paragraph was first written, this
      rank-deficient residual package was the main unnamed mathematical gap in
      the oriented source layer.  The later residual-normal-form, Schur-tail,
      compact-polydisc, and compact-parameter continuity packets below now
      decompose it into named theorem surfaces with implementation-level proof
      transcripts.  The remaining gate is Lean implementation of those named
      finite-dimensional/SCV support lemmas; it is not permission to replace
      the oriented residual chart by ordinary
      `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen`, an arbitrary extended-tube lift,
      or a wrapper around the public scalar representative.

      Holomorphic descent through the oriented invariant must then be proved
      by the same regular-stratum/removable-singularity architecture as the
      ordinary Hall-Wightman descent.  Local holomorphic sections are required
      on the maximal-rank oriented stratum; at exceptional rank, the proof
      must use normality and a Riemann extension theorem, not a fictitious
      local section through the singular quotient map.  The representative is
      not an arbitrary function on the ambient coordinate space; it is the
      quotient value of `extendF F` on the oriented image:

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue
          {d : Nat}
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) : Ōäé :=
        if hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n then
          BHW.extendF F hG.choose
        else
          0

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_wellDefined
          {d n : Nat}
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz : z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hGz : BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z = G) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue (d := d) n F G =
            BHW.extendF F z := by
        classical
        have hGmem :
            G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n :=
          Ō¤©z, hz, hGzŌ¤®
        unfold BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue
        rw [dif_pos hGmem]
        let zpick : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé := Classical.choose hGmem
        have hzpick : zpick Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hGmem).1
        have hpickG :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n zpick = G :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hGmem).2
        exact hBranch hzpick hz (hpickG.trans hGz.symm)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_holomorphicOn_maxRank
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
          (hReg : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0) :
          Ōłā ╬® ╬©,
            IsOpen ╬® Ōł¦ G0 Ōłł ╬® Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© ╬® Ōł¦
            ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn (BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F) ╬©
              (╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n) := by
        obtain S :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTube_holomorphicLocalSection
            (d := d) hd n hG0 hReg
        refine Ō¤©S.╬®,
          fun G => BHW.extendF F (S.toVec G),
          S.╬®_open, S.center_mem, ?_, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
        ┬Ę exact
            (BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn
              (d := d) n F hF_holo hF_cinv).comp
              S.toVec_holomorphic S.toVec_mem
        ┬Ę intro G hG
          exact Ō¤©S.toVec G, S.toVec_mem G hG.1,
            S.toVec_right_inv G hGŌ¤®
        ┬Ę intro G hG
          exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_wellDefined
              (d := d) F hBranch
              (S.toVec_mem G hG.1)
              (S.toVec_right_inv G hG)

      theorem SCV.continuousWithinAt_of_local_eqOn_relNeighborhood
          {E : Type*} [TopologicalSpace E]
          {U V ╬® : Set E} {x : E} {phi ╬© : E -> Ōäé}
          (hU_sub : U ŌŖå V)
          (hxU : x Ōłł U)
          (h╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®)
          (hx╬® : x Ōłł ╬®)
          (h╬©_cont : ContinuousOn ╬© ╬®)
          (hEq : Set.EqOn phi ╬© (╬® Ōł® V)) :
          ContinuousWithinAt phi U x := by
        have h╬©_at : ContinuousAt ╬© x :=
          h╬©_cont.continuousAt (h╬®_open.mem_nhds hx╬®)
        have h╬©_tendsto :
            Tendsto ╬© (­ØōØ[U] x) (­ØōØ (╬© x)) :=
          h╬©_at.tendsto.mono_left nhdsWithin_le_nhds
        have h╬®_ev : ŌłĆßČĀ y in ­ØōØ[U] x, y Ōłł ╬® := by
          exact Filter.Eventually.filter_mono nhdsWithin_le_nhds
            (h╬®_open.mem_nhds hx╬®)
        have hV_ev : ŌłĆßČĀ y in ­ØōØ[U] x, y Ōłł V :=
          eventually_mem_nhdsWithin.mono fun y hy => hU_sub hy
        have hEq_ev : ŌłĆßČĀ y in ­ØōØ[U] x, ╬© y = phi y :=
          (h╬®_ev.and hV_ev).mono fun y hy =>
            (hEq Ō¤©hy.1, hy.2Ō¤®).symm
        have hxEq : ╬© x = phi x :=
          (hEq Ō¤©hx╬®, hU_sub hxUŌ¤®).symm
        simpa [hxEq] using h╬©_tendsto.congr' hEq_ev

      theorem SCV.locallyBoundedAt_of_local_eqOn_relNeighborhood
          {E : Type*} [TopologicalSpace E]
          {U V ╬® : Set E} {x : E} {phi ╬© : E -> Ōäé}
          (hU_sub : U ŌŖå V)
          (h╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®)
          (hx╬® : x Ōłł ╬®)
          (h╬©_cont : ContinuousOn ╬© ╬®)
          (hEq : Set.EqOn phi ╬© (╬® Ōł® V)) :
          Ōłā ╬®0 : Set E,
            IsOpen ╬®0 Ōł¦ x Ōłł ╬®0 Ōł¦
            Ōłā C : ŌäØ, 0 <= C Ōł¦
              ŌłĆ y, y Ōłł ╬®0 Ōł® U -> ŌĆ¢phi yŌĆ¢ <= C := by
        have h╬©_at : ContinuousAt ╬© x :=
          h╬©_cont.continuousAt (h╬®_open.mem_nhds hx╬®)
        have hpre :
            ╬© Ōü╗┬╣' Metric.ball (╬© x) 1 Ōłł ­ØōØ x :=
          h╬©_at (Metric.ball_mem_nhds (╬© x) zero_lt_one)
        rcases mem_nhds_iff.mp hpre with
          Ō¤©S, hS_sub, hS_open, hxSŌ¤®
        let ╬®0 : Set E := S Ōł® ╬®
        refine
          Ō¤©╬®0, hS_open.inter h╬®_open, Ō¤©hxS, hx╬®Ō¤®,
            ŌĆ¢╬© xŌĆ¢ + 1, by positivity, ?_Ō¤®
        intro y hy
        have hyS : y Ōłł S := hy.1.1
        have hy╬® : y Ōłł ╬® := hy.1.2
        have hyU : y Ōłł U := hy.2
        have hball : ╬© y Ōłł Metric.ball (╬© x) 1 := hS_sub hyS
        have hdist : dist (╬© y) (╬© x) < 1 := by
          simpa [Metric.mem_ball] using hball
        have hnorm : ŌĆ¢╬© yŌĆ¢ <= ŌĆ¢╬© xŌĆ¢ + 1 := by
          calc
            ŌĆ¢╬© yŌĆ¢ <= ŌĆ¢╬© xŌĆ¢ + dist (╬© y) (╬© x) := by
              simpa [dist_eq_norm, norm_sub_rev, add_comm] using
                norm_add_le (╬© x) (╬© y - ╬© x)
            _ <= ŌĆ¢╬© xŌĆ¢ + 1 := by linarith
        have hphi : phi y = ╬© y := hEq Ō¤©hy╬®, hU_sub hyUŌ¤®
        simpa [hphi] using hnorm

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_locallyBounded_of_maxRankLocal
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_ext_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          (hMaxLocal :
            ŌłĆ {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n},
              G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n ->
                BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0 ->
                  Ōłā ╬® ╬©,
                    IsOpen ╬® Ōł¦ G0 Ōłł ╬® Ōł¦
                    DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© ╬® Ōł¦
                    ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ŌŖå
                      BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n Ōł¦
                    Set.EqOn (BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F) ╬©
                      (╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)) :
          ContinuousOn (BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F)
            (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n) Ōł¦
          BHW.LocallyBoundedOn (BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F)
            (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n) := by
        have hU_sub :
            BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_subset_variety
            (d := d) (n := n)
        constructor
        ┬Ę intro G0 hG0
          rcases hG0 with Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤®
          by_cases hmax :
            BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)
	          ┬Ę rcases hMaxLocal Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤® hmax with
	              Ō¤©╬®, ╬©, h╬®_open, hG0╬®, h╬©, h╬®_sub, hEqŌ¤®
            exact
              SCV.continuousWithinAt_of_local_eqOn_relNeighborhood
                (U := BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
                (V := BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
                (╬® := ╬®)
                hU_sub Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤® h╬®_open hG0╬®
                h╬©.continuousOn hEq
	          ┬Ę exact
	              BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_of_residualChart
	                (d := d) hd n F hF_ext_cont hBranch hz0 hmax
        ┬Ę intro G0 hG0
          rcases hG0 with Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤®
          by_cases hmax :
            BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)
	          ┬Ę rcases hMaxLocal Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤® hmax with
	              Ō¤©╬®, ╬©, h╬®_open, hG0╬®, h╬©, h╬®_sub, hEqŌ¤®
            exact
              SCV.locallyBoundedAt_of_local_eqOn_relNeighborhood
                (U := BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
                (V := BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
                (╬® := ╬®)
                hU_sub h╬®_open hG0╬® h╬©.continuousOn hEq
	          ┬Ę exact
	              BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_locallyBounded_of_residualChart
	                (d := d) hd n F hF_ext_cont hBranch hz0 hmax
	      ```

	      Lean checkpoint: the conditional assembly theorem
	      `BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_locallyBounded_of_maxRankLocal`
	      is checked in `SourceOrientedScalarRepresentative.lean`.  The
	      unconditional theorem with hypotheses `hF_holo` and `hF_cinv` remains
	      blocked precisely by the max-rank local representative producer
	      `BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_holomorphicOn_maxRank`.

      The two `SCV.*_local_eqOn_relNeighborhood` helpers are ordinary
      topological glue.  For continuity, `h╬®_open` and `hx╬®` make membership
      in `╬®` eventual in the within-filter `­ØōØ[U] x`; `hU_sub` then makes
      membership in `╬® Ōł® V` eventual, so `Filter.Tendsto.congr'` transfers
      `h╬©_cont.continuousWithinAt hx╬®` through `hEq`.  For local boundedness,
      continuity of `╬©` on the open neighborhood `╬®` gives a smaller open
      `╬®0 ŌŖå ╬®` on which `ŌĆ¢╬© yŌĆ¢ Ōēż ŌĆ¢╬© xŌĆ¢ + 1`; set
      `C := ŌĆ¢╬© xŌĆ¢ + 1` and transfer the bound to `phi` on `╬®0 Ōł® U` using
      `hU_sub` and `hEq`.  These helpers contain no Hall-Wightman or OS
      content, but they are needed so the max-rank local representative really
      produces the `ContinuousOn` and `LocallyBoundedOn` hypotheses consumed by
      the normal-space Riemann theorem.

      The proof of
      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_locallyBounded` must split at
      each point `G0 = sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0`.  If `G0` is
      max-rank, use the holomorphic local section and
      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_holomorphicOn_maxRank`, whose local
      ambient representative is continuous and bounded on a smaller
      coordinate ball.  If `G0` is exceptional rank, use
      `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChart` and the two residual-chart
      theorems above.  There is no allowed proof by arbitrary choice of a
      lift from `sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain`: such a choice gives no
      continuity or boundedness.

      ```lean

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedExceptionalRank_eq_lowerRank
          (d n : Nat) :
          {G | BHW.SourceOrientedExceptionalRank d n G} =
            BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n Ōł®
              {G | BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G.gram < min (d + 1) n}

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedExceptionalRank_eq_minorsVanishing
          (d n : Nat) :
          {G | BHW.SourceOrientedExceptionalRank d n G} =
            BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n Ōł®
              {G | ŌłĆ I J : Fin (min (d + 1) n) -> Fin n,
                BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n (min (d + 1) n)
                  I J G.gram = 0}

      theorem BHW.isClosed_sourceOrientedMaximalMinorsVanishing
          (d n : Nat) :
          IsClosed
            {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n |
              ŌłĆ I J : Fin (min (d + 1) n) -> Fin n,
                BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n (min (d + 1) n)
                  I J G.gram = 0}

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
            (BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
            {G | BHW.SourceOrientedExceptionalRank d n G}

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_normal
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)

      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety.to_isRelOpenIn
          {U : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hU : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n U) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenIn
            (BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n) U

      theorem BHW.not_exceptional_rank_iff_maxRank
          {d n : ŌäĢ}
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n) :
          G Ōłē {H | BHW.SourceOrientedExceptionalRank d n H} Ōåö
            BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedMaxRank_dense_in_domain_inter_maxRank
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n ŌŖå
            closure
              (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n Ōł®
                {G | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G})

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedMaxRank_dense_in_domain
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n ŌŖå
            closure
              (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n \
                {G | BHW.SourceOrientedExceptionalRank d n G})

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_normal_riemannExtension
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {Phi : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n -> Ōäé}
          (hcont :
            ContinuousOn Phi (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n))
          (hbdd :
            BHW.LocallyBoundedOn Phi
              (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n))
          (hregular :
            ŌłĆ G0,
              G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n \
                {G | BHW.SourceOrientedExceptionalRank d n G} ->
              Ōłā ╬® ╬©,
                IsOpen ╬® Ōł¦ G0 Ōłł ╬® Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© ╬® Ōł¦
                ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ŌŖå
                  BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn Phi ╬©
                  (╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi
            (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)

      def BHW.sourceOrientedAlgebraicRelations
          (d n : Nat)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) : Prop :=
        G.gram Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1) Ōł¦
        BHW.sourceOrientedDetAlternating d n G Ōł¦
        BHW.sourceOrientedCauchyBinetRelations d n G

      theorem BHW.sourceMatrixMinor_sourceMinkowskiGram_fullFrame
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ ╬║ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n (d + 1) ╬╣ ╬║
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
            BHW.minkowskiMetricDet d *
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z *
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬║ z

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_reindex_selectedFrame
          (d n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)
          (Žü : Equiv.Perm (Fin (d + 1)))
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ((Žü.toEmbedding).trans ╬╣) z =
            (Žü.sign : Ōäé) * BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z

      def BHW.sourceOrientedAlgebraicVariety
          (d n : Nat) : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :=
        {G | BHW.sourceOrientedAlgebraicRelations d n G}

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_mem_algebraicRelations
          (d n : Nat) (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedAlgebraicRelations d n
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_subset_algebraicVariety
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ŌŖå
            BHW.sourceOrientedAlgebraicVariety d n

      theorem BHW.isClosed_sourceOrientedAlgebraicVariety
          (d n : Nat) :
          IsClosed (BHW.sourceOrientedAlgebraicVariety d n)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_eq_algebraic
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n =
            BHW.sourceOrientedAlgebraicVariety d n

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedAlgebraicVariety_normal
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceOrientedAlgebraicVariety d n)
      ```

      The algebraic relation
      `det (fun a b => G.gram (╬╣ a) (╬║ b)) =
      minkowskiMetricDet d * G.det ╬╣ * G.det ╬║` is the Cauchy-Binet identity
      for `M╬╣ * ╬Ę * M╬║ßĄĆ`.  It is the finite-dimensional equation saying the
      determinant fields really are oriented full-frame determinants, not
      independent extra coordinates.  The reindexing relation records
      determinant alternation under reordering of the selected full frame.
      The forward Cauchy-Binet equation on actual source tuples is checked in
      `SourceOrientedCauchyBinet.lean`: rewrite the selected source Gram minor as
      `det (M╬╣ * ╬Ę * M╬║ßĄĆ)`, then use `Matrix.det_mul`,
      `Matrix.det_transpose`, and the definition of `minkowskiMetricDet`.
      The determinant alternation equation is likewise checked by
      `Matrix.det_permute` on the row-permuted selected full-frame matrix.
      `SourceOrientedAlgebraicRelations.lean` now checks the exact forward
      algebraic support: actual oriented source invariants satisfy
      `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1)`, alternation, and Cauchy-Binet;
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_subset_algebraicVariety` follows by range
      elimination; and `isClosed_sourceOrientedAlgebraicVariety` is the finite
      closed-intersection proof using the checked continuity of source minors
      and determinant-coordinate projections.
      The theorem `sourceOrientedGramVariety_eq_algebraic` is the
      source-coordinate form of the first fundamental theorem for the special
      complex orthogonal/Lorentz group on tuples.  The normality theorem must
      be proved for this algebraic model, or imported as a standard
      finite-dimensional invariant-theory result with exactly these
      relations; it may not be replaced by the ordinary pure-Gram normality
      theorem.

      The allowed normality proof route is the reductive invariant-ring
      route.  It must be stated at the coordinate-ring level before Lean
      invokes `sourceOrientedAlgebraicVariety_normal`:

      ```lean
      def BHW.sourceTupleCoordinateRing
          (d n : Nat) :=
        MvPolynomial (Fin n ├Ś Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé

      def BHW.sourceOrientedInvariantCoordinateRing
          (d n : Nat) :=
        MvPolynomial
          ((Fin n ├Ś Fin n) ŌŖĢ (Fin (d + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n)) Ōäé

      def BHW.specialComplexLorentzAlgebraicGroup
          (d : Nat) : BHW.LinearAlgebraicGroup Ōäé
            (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
        BHW.linearAlgebraicGroupOfMatrixEquations
          (fun A =>
            A.transpose * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) * A =
              ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) Ōł¦
            A.det = 1)

      def BHW.sourceTuplePolynomialAction
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.AlgebraicGroupAction
            (BHW.specialComplexLorentzAlgebraicGroup d)
            (BHW.sourceTupleCoordinateRing d n)

      def BHW.sourceOrientedInvariantSubalgebra
          (d n : Nat) :
          Subalgebra Ōäé (BHW.sourceTupleCoordinateRing d n) :=
        BHW.invariantSubalgebra
          (BHW.sourceTuplePolynomialAction d n)

      def BHW.standardTupleCoordinateRing
          (D n : Nat) :=
        MvPolynomial (Fin n ├Ś Fin D) Ōäé

      def BHW.specialOrthogonalAlgebraicGroup
          (D : Nat) : BHW.LinearAlgebraicGroup Ōäé (Fin D -> Ōäé) :=
        BHW.linearAlgebraicGroupOfMatrixEquations
          (fun A => A.transpose * A = 1 Ōł¦ A.det = 1)

      def BHW.standardSOTuplePolynomialAction
          (D n : Nat) :
          BHW.AlgebraicGroupAction
            (BHW.specialOrthogonalAlgebraicGroup D)
            (BHW.standardTupleCoordinateRing D n)

      def BHW.standardSOInvariantSubalgebra
          (D n : Nat) :
          Subalgebra Ōäé (BHW.standardTupleCoordinateRing D n) :=
        BHW.invariantSubalgebra
          (BHW.standardSOTuplePolynomialAction D n)

      def BHW.standardPairingCoordinatePolynomial
          (D n : Nat)
          (ij : Fin n ├Ś Fin n) :
          BHW.standardTupleCoordinateRing D n :=
        Ōłæ ╬╝ : Fin D,
          MvPolynomial.X (ij.1, ╬╝) * MvPolynomial.X (ij.2, ╬╝)

      def BHW.standardVolumeCoordinatePolynomial
          (D n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          BHW.standardTupleCoordinateRing D n :=
        Matrix.det
          (fun a ╬╝ : Fin D => MvPolynomial.X (╬╣ a, ╬╝))

      abbrev BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateRing
          (D n : Nat) :=
        MvPolynomial
          ((Fin n ├Ś Fin n) ŌŖĢ (Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n)) Ōäé

      def BHW.standardSOAlgebraicRelationGenerators
          (D n : Nat) :
          Set (BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateRing D n) :=
        (Set.range
          (fun ij : Fin n ├Ś Fin n =>
            MvPolynomial.X (Sum.inl ij) -
              MvPolynomial.X (Sum.inl (ij.2, ij.1)))) Ōł¬
        (Set.range
          (fun ╬╣ : Fin (D + 1) Ōå¬ Fin n =>
            Matrix.det
              (fun a b =>
                MvPolynomial.X
                  (Sum.inl (╬╣ a, ╬╣ b))))) Ōł¬
        (Set.range
          (fun p : (Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n) ├Ś Equiv.Perm (Fin D) =>
            MvPolynomial.X
                (Sum.inr ((p.2.toEmbedding).trans p.1)) -
              MvPolynomial.C (p.2.sign : Ōäé) *
                (MvPolynomial.X (Sum.inr p.1) :
                  BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateRing D n))) Ōł¬
        (Set.range
          (fun p : (Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n) ├Ś (Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n) =>
            Matrix.det
                (fun a b =>
                  MvPolynomial.X
                    (Sum.inl (p.1 a, p.2 b))) -
              MvPolynomial.X (Sum.inr p.1) *
                MvPolynomial.X (Sum.inr p.2)))

      def BHW.standardSOAlgebraicRelationIdeal
          (D n : Nat) :
          Ideal (BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateRing D n) :=
        Ideal.span (BHW.standardSOAlgebraicRelationGenerators D n)

      def BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateMap
          (D n : Nat) :
          BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateRing D n ŌåÆŌéÉ[Ōäé]
            BHW.standardSOInvariantSubalgebra D n

      def BHW.standardPairingInvariantElement
          (D n : Nat)
          (ij : Fin n ├Ś Fin n) :
          BHW.standardSOInvariantSubalgebra D n :=
        Ō¤©BHW.standardPairingCoordinatePolynomial D n ij,
          BHW.standardPairingCoordinatePolynomial_invariant D n ijŌ¤®

      def BHW.standardVolumeInvariantElement
          (D n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          BHW.standardSOInvariantSubalgebra D n :=
        Ō¤©BHW.standardVolumeCoordinatePolynomial D n ╬╣,
          BHW.standardVolumeCoordinatePolynomial_invariant D n ╬╣Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateMap_apply_pairing
          (D n : Nat)
          (ij : Fin n ├Ś Fin n) :
          BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateMap D n
              (MvPolynomial.X (Sum.inl ij)) =
            BHW.standardPairingInvariantElement D n ij := by
        rfl

      theorem BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateMap_apply_volume
          (D n : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin D Ōå¬ Fin n) :
          BHW.standardSOInvariantCoordinateMap D n
              (MvPolynomial.X (Sum.inr ╬╣)) =
            BHW.standardVolumeInvariantElement D n ╬╣ := by
        rfl

      theorem BHW.algHom_range_le_adjoin_images_mvPolynomial_X
          {Žā A : Type*}
          [CommSemiring A] [Algebra Ōäé A]
          (f : MvPolynomial Žā Ōäé ŌåÆŌéÉ[Ōäé] A) :
          f.range Ōēż
            Algebra.adjoin Ōäé (Set.range (fun i : Žā => f (MvPolynomial.X i))) := by
        intro y hy
        rcases hy with Ō¤©p, rflŌ¤®
        induction p using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
        | C a =>
            simpa using
              Algebra.algebraMap_mem
                (Algebra.adjoin Ōäé
                  (Set.range (fun i : Žā => f (MvPolynomial.X i)))) a
        | add p q hp hq =>
            simpa using
              (Algebra.adjoin Ōäé
                (Set.range
                  (fun i : Žā => f (MvPolynomial.X i)))).add_mem hp hq
        | mul_X p i hp =>
            have hX :
                f (MvPolynomial.X i) Ōłł
                  Algebra.adjoin Ōäé
                    (Set.range
                      (fun i : Žā => f (MvPolynomial.X i))) :=
              Algebra.subset_adjoin Ō¤©i, rflŌ¤®
            simpa [map_mul] using
      ```
