`BHW.BHWOrientedTerminalChainComparisonData.continuedValue_eq` | Checked in `OSReconstruction/ComplexLieGroups/Connectedness/BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedContinuationProducers.lean`. | Producer-level assembly only.  The first theorem turns a normal-form patch producer plus uniform oriented descent into branch-free transfer neighborhoods at every point of `U`; the next two expose the compact transfer-cover chain fold and its path-connected selected-chain family.  The provenance-retaining analogues now select raw and certified transfer traces from the same compact-cover data, and the raw glued-branch theorem composes a path-connected transfer cover, raw terminal-point trace comparison, and initial chart into the glued holomorphic branch on `U`.  The certified glued-branch theorem is the strict-route consumer: it takes already selected certified traces, certified terminal-point comparison, and the initial chart, then forgets the certificates only at the checked atlas storage boundary.  The path-connected certified theorem first selects those certified traces from a uniqueness-certified transfer cover and then applies the certified glued theorem; the source-normal-form producer theorem specializes this to normal-form patches plus uniform descent, leaving the one-step uniqueness theorem for the produced controls and certified terminal comparison as the two hard analytic inputs.  The monodromy-facing certified variants replace the local terminal-chart comparison input by same-endpoint comparison of the two terminal-retargeted certified chains; the retargeted-comparison bridge then rewrites `retargetTerminal`/`snoc` to recover the atlas local-chart comparison mechanically.  The chain-atlas data object packages a fixed base point, one selected terminal chain through each point of `U`, terminal overlap equality, and base agreement, then assembles the checked source-patch atlas and glued branch.  The selected trace-atlas constructor is the minimal raw consumer; the certified constructor is the strict-route atlas surface because it asks only for comparisons between certified selected traces observed at actual overlap points, plus comparison with the certified zero-step base trace.  The same-endpoint comparison constructor remains a checked high-level consumer when arbitrary chain comparison is available; on the strict source-backed route the final producer instead uses selected certified transfer-trace comparison and then forgets trace provenance before storing terminal charts in the atlas.  The base-chain variant derives atlas base agreement by retargeting a normalized base chain to each `z Ōłł ╬®0 Ōł® U` and comparing it with the selected chain ending at `z`; the initial-chart variant builds that normalized base chain as the zero-step chain from an initial chart covering `╬®0 Ōł® U` with branch `B0` there.  The terminal-comparison data object packages the output terminal comparison patch, the local-chart constructor turns terminal local-chart comparison into chain comparison, and its consumer proves equality of continued values by evaluating the stored branch equality at the endpoint.  The hard analytic inputs remain the normal-form patch producer, uniform oriented descent theorem producing uniqueness-certified transfer controls, selected certified transfer-trace comparison via finite one-step uniqueness and closed-path source monodromy, and an initial chart covering `╬®0 Ōł® U` with branch `B0`; no new `sorry`, axiom, or public theorem-2 wrapper is introduced. |

It remains useful where an ambient oriented invariant coordinate homeomorphism is actually available, but the source-matrix normal-form route no longer uses it: determinant coordinates make the ambient source-change shortcut false off the source variety.  Source-change consumers must use the variety-relative row immediately below. |
      | `BHW.SourceOrientedVariety`, `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyUnderlyingSet`, `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyUnderlyingSet_subset_variety`, `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyUnderlyingSet_relOpen_of_isOpen`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.toFun`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.invFun`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.left_inv`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.right_inv`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.continuous_toFun`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.continuous_invFun`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.invFun_maxRank_iff`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.refl`, `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.isOpen_invFun_image`, `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyTransport_invFun_image_subset_image`, `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyTransport_invFun_image_eq`, `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyTransport_invFun_image_underlying_relOpen`, `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyTransport_closure_maxRankDense` | Checked in `OSReconstruction/ComplexLieGroups/Connectedness/BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedTransport.lean`. | Variety-relative transport interface for source changes whose determinant-coordinate formula is meaningful on actual alternating oriented data but not on arbitrary ambient ordered-frame coordinates.  This is now the chosen normal-form source-change interface; source matrices are supplied by `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietySourceMatrixTransportEquivOfMatrix` in `SourceNormalFormTransport.lean`.  The subtype set API transports open subtype images, converts them back to the repo's ambient relative-open predicate, transports exact parameter images, and transports max-rank density without ever extending the determinant-coordinate action to a false ambient homeomorphism. |

`OSReconstruction/ComplexLieGroups/Connectedness/BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedBHWFiniteOverlap.lean`. | Terminal finite-overlap interface for the remaining BHW/Jost monodromy theorem.  The max-rank assembly lemmas reduce connectedness of `D Ōł® MaxRank` to connectedness of `D`, hard-range max-rank density, and a local connected max-rank basis; the max-rank-center local case is checked from the full-frame chart shrinker, and the exceptional-rank local-image case is now supplied by the checked head-slice IFT producer.  The seed extractor shrinks any nonempty relatively open oriented patch to a nonempty preconnected relatively open max-rank seed.  The terminal propagation data records a final connected max-rank domain, terminal equality seed, and closing-patch containment; its consumers now package directly to closed-loop seed, oriented monodromy, and source monodromy.  The smaller accumulated-germ package `BHWJostOrientedClosingPatchTerminalSeedData` records just a terminal max-rank equality seed inside the closing oriented patch, with `of_sourceRealized_branch_eq` handling the final source-representative-to-`Psi` rewrite, `of_sourcePatch_eqOn` packaging equality on an actual source patch whose image realizes the terminal seed, `of_closingPatch_exists_sourceBranch_eq` extracting the max-rank seed from one source-realized branch equality over each closing invariant, and `of_closingPatch_sourceBranch_eq` handling the stronger all-representatives equality; the public endpoint `bhw_jost_closedChain_sourceMonodromy_of_closingPatch_exists_sourceBranch_eq` closes source monodromy directly from the stronger whole-closing-patch existential equality input.  The minimal patch-by-patch route still needs only the terminal-seed package, not whole-closing-patch equality.  The closed-loop domain-data consumers remain valid for the stronger large-start-domain route; the zero-transition edge case is completely mechanical from the stored nonempty preconnected closing oriented patch.  The hard theorem is now the source-backed producer for the specific strict OS I ┬¦4.5 BHW/Jost loops: either prove the explicit large-start-domain finite-overlap package, or on the preferred patch-by-patch route prove the closing-patch terminal-seed package.  An arbitrary closed-loop statement with only `L` and `hn` is not a Lean-ready theorem surface.  No new `sorry` or axiom is introduced. |

The rank upper bound for actual source configurations is proved from the restricted-Minkowski-rank formula and finite-dimensional span bounds, so the equality-style oriented max-rank predicate is equivalent to the scalar `min (d+1) n Ōēż rank` predicate.  Failure of oriented max-rank for an actual source configuration now gives strict rank deficiency below both `min (d+1) n` and `d+1`, which is the checked input for choosing the normal-form rank.  In the hard range `d + 1 Ōēż n`, the max-rank predicate is also exposed directly as the ordinary full-frame equation `sourceGramMatrixRank n G.gram = d + 1`, both for arbitrary oriented Gram data and for actual source invariants.  This is support for the upcoming exceptional Schur/residual parameter connectedness proof, not a new analytic input. |

Therefore all oriented determinant coordinates vanish in the strict low-rank branch, and same ordinary Gram implies equality of the full oriented source invariant. |

neighborhood basis, strict derivative, raw local IFT chart, restricted small-window chart, determinant-unit lemma, left inverse, and final sliced-head data producer are checked in `OSReconstruction/ComplexLieGroups/Connectedness/BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedHeadSliceGaugeIFT.lean`. | The canonical extracted-image proof correctly proves openness by finite-coordinate continuity and proves both Schur-extraction inclusions, but the final full-head-window wrapper is conditional on the legacy `SourceRankDeficientHeadGaugeData` surface.  That surface is over-strong for `r >= 2`: `H Ōå” H ╬Ę HßĄĆ` has a local complex orthogonal stabilizer, so no full open matrix neighborhood can satisfy the stored left-inverse law.  The active route therefore replaces the full head polydisc by an open connected neighborhood in the slice `SourceHeadGaugeSlice`, with the constructible data surface `SourceRankDeficientHeadSliceGaugeData`.  The sliced parameter space and sliced canonical-image packet are checked: the slice head coordinate window is path-connected by straight segments in the affine slice, the residual-tail exact-rank slice is reduced to the checked tail connectedness theorem, forward image membership uses the sliced left inverse, and reverse image membership keeps the preimage in the sliced parameter space.  The checked strengthened local-image wrapper now consumes a supplied `SourceRankDeficientHeadSliceGaugeData` directly, the concrete producer is checked as `BHW.sourceRankDeficientHeadSliceGaugeData`, and the arbitrary exceptional-rank producer `BHW.sourceOrientedRankDeficientMaxRankLocalImageData_of_headSliceIFT` now chooses normal-form data and supplies the IFT slice gauge automatically.  The hard-range connectedness consumer now calls that producer directly, and the closed-loop finite-overlap constructors can take nonempty preconnected overlap/closing domains instead of max-rank-connected domains.  The next implementation step is the genuine Hall-Wightman/Jost geometric producer of those nonempty preconnected finite-overlap domains and the ordered containments. |

      The oriented route also changes the Figure-2-4 path obligation.  The
      currently checked source patch exports a pure-Gram path field.  That is
      insufficient for an oriented representative.  The oriented route must
      strengthen the path provenance to:

      ```lean
      def BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (x : NPointDomain d n) : Prop :=
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
        let y :=
          BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
        Ōłā (╬ō ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
          Continuous ╬ō Ōł¦
          Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
          ╬ō (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōł¦
          ╬ō (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Ōł¦
          (ŌłĆ t, ╬ō t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
          (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
          (ŌłĆ t,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (╬ö t) =
              BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žä
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (╬ō t)))

      def BHW.OS45Figure24RotatedPathFormulaField
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (x : NPointDomain d n) : Prop :=
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        Ōłā ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
          Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
          (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
          (ŌłĆ t,
            ╬ö t =
              BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                  (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                    (d := d) (n := n) x t)))
      ```

      The proof may not merely lift the existing pure-Gram path by choice.
      It must prove the determinant fields along the selected Figure-2-4
      realization.  The intended proof source is the actual checked geometry:
      the exported `╬ö` in
      `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists` is definitional
      as

      ```lean
      ╬ö t =
        BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
          (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä (╬ō t))
      ```

      and the proof already obtains
      `╬øinv : ComplexLorentzGroup d` from
      `BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse` with

      ```lean
      BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬øinv (╬ö t) =
        BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä (╬ō t)
      ```

      This local API audit closes the determinant-control subquestion for the
      Figure-2-4 rotation itself: `╬øinv` is a `ComplexLorentzGroup`, whose
      structure field is determinant `1`; the underlying construction is the
      checked `(3,4,5)` two-plane rotation
      `spatialRotCLG12` with `proper := spatialRotMatrix12_det`.  Therefore
      the oriented path proof is a finite algebra replay of the pure-Gram
      proof once the oriented-invariant lemmas above exist:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField_of_checked_figure24
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (x : NPointDomain d n)
          (hx_ordered :
            x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector
              (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hRotated :
            BHW.OS45Figure24RotatedPathFormulaField
              (d := d) hd n i hi x) :
          BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField (d := d) n i hi x := by
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        let ╬ō : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x
        rcases hRotated with Ō¤©╬ö, h╬ö_cont, h╬ö_ET, h╬ö_defŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse
            (d := d) (n := n) hd with
          Ō¤©╬øinv, h╬øinvŌ¤®
        refine Ō¤©╬ō, ╬ö, ?_, h╬ö_cont, ?_, ?_, ?_, h╬ö_ET, ?_Ō¤®
        ┬Ę exact BHW.continuous_os45Figure24IdentityPath
            (d := d) (n := n) x
        ┬Ę simpa [╬ō] using
            BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero (d := d) (n := n) x
        ┬Ę simpa [╬ō] using
            BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_one (d := d) (n := n) x
        ┬Ę intro t
          exact BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube
            (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_mem_forwardTube
              (d := d) (n := n) hx_ordered t)
        ┬Ę intro t
          have hInv :
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬øinv (╬ö t) =
                BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä (╬ō t) := by
            rw [h╬ö_def t]
            simpa [╬ō, Žä] using
              h╬øinv (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä (╬ō t))
          calc
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (╬ö t)
                =
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬øinv (╬ö t)) := by
                  exact
                    (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_complexLorentzAction
                      (d := d) (n := n) ╬øinv (╬ö t)).symm
            _ =
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä (╬ō t)) := by
                  rw [hInv]
            _ =
              BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žä
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (╬ō t)) := by
                  exact
                    BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_permAct
                      (d := d) (n := n) Žä
                      (╬ō t)
      ```

      The checked public source patch currently exports only the pure-Gram
      closure field `hV_figPath_closure`.  That field is intentionally lossy
      for the oriented route: from an existential `╬ö` satisfying only a Gram
      equality one cannot recover determinant-coordinate equality without
      choosing a new lift.  Therefore the oriented proof must add the following
      strengthened checked-geometry theorem by replaying the existing
      `swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists` proof and exporting
      the definitional rotated path formula before it is compressed to
      pure-Gram equality.  The first Lean slice is neighborhood-level,
      because the current production file has no canonical source-patch
      structure yet and because this theorem exposes exactly the path object
      that the existing pure-Gram theorem hides:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_rotated_exists
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          Ōłā (Upath : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (xfig : NPointDomain d n),
            IsOpen Upath Ōł¦ xfig Ōłł Upath Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ k : Fin n, xfig k 0 = 0) Ōł¦
            xfig Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed xfig Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed (fun k => xfig (Žä k)) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Upath,
              BHW.OS45Figure24RotatedPathFormulaField
                (d := d) hd n i hi x) := by
        classical
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        -- Copy `swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists`
        -- through the compact-open construction of `H`.
        rcases BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlaneRotationSupport
            (d := d) (n := n) hd i hi with
          Ō¤©xfig, xrot, ╬øfig, hxfig_time0, hxfig_J, hxfig_ET,
            hrot_formula, hxrot_FJ, h╬øfigŌ¤®
        have hxrot_ET : BHW.realEmbed xrot Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := ...
        have hxfig_swapET :
            BHW.realEmbed (fun k => xfig (Žä k)) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n := ...
        let H : NPointDomain d n ├Ś unitInterval ->
            Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun p =>
            BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
              (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                  (d := d) (n := n) p.1 p.2))
        have hH_cont : Continuous H :=
          by simpa [H] using
            BHW.continuous_figure24RotatedIdentityPath
              (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä
        have hH_base : ŌłĆ t : unitInterval,
            H (xfig, t) Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := ...
        rcases BHW.exists_open_nhds_forall_mem_of_compact_parameter
            (X := NPointDomain d n) (Y := unitInterval)
            (Z := Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (f := H) hH_cont BHW.isOpen_extendedTube hH_base with
          Ō¤©Upath, hUpath_open, hxfig_Upath, hUpath_pathŌ¤®
        refine
          Ō¤©Upath, xfig, hUpath_open, hxfig_Upath,
            hxfig_time0, hxfig_J, hxfig_ET, hxfig_swapET, ?_Ō¤®
        intro x hxUpath
        refine Ō¤©fun t => H (x, t), ?_, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
        ┬Ę exact hH_cont.comp (continuous_const.prodMk continuous_id)
        ┬Ę exact hUpath_path x hxUpath
        ┬Ę intro t
          rfl

      theorem BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithRotatedPathStability
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          Ōłā (Ufig Upath : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (x0 : NPointDomain d n),
            IsOpen Ufig Ōł¦ IsOpen Upath Ōł¦
            x0 Ōłł Ufig Ōł¦ x0 Ōłł Upath Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ k : Fin n, x0 k 0 = 0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                  (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain
                  (d := d) (n := n) 1) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                  (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain
                  (d := d) (n := n) Žä) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Upath,
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector
                (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) ->
              BHW.OS45Figure24RotatedPathFormulaField
                (d := d) hd n i hi x Ōł¦
              BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField
                (d := d) n i hi x)
      ```

      Its proof copies the construction of
      `swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithPathStability`, but starts
      from
      `swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_rotated_exists`.  The final
      field does not construct a new path: for `x Ōłł Upath`, take the stored
      `hRotated : OS45Figure24RotatedPathFormulaField ... x`; the first
      conjunct is `hRotated`, and the second is
      `OS45Figure24OrientedPathField_of_checked_figure24 hd n i hi x
      hx_ordered hRotated`.  The proof of all `Ufig` fields is exactly the
      checked proof of
      `swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithPathStability` up to
      replacing the final pure-Gram path consumer by this oriented-field
      consumer.  This pins the exact Lean API: there is no use of an arbitrary
      chosen lift from a Gram equality.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch_with_orientedPath
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          Ōłā (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n)) (xseed : NPointDomain d n),
            -- Same source-patch fields as
            -- `os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch`, with the
            -- final closure path field strengthened from pure Gram equality
            -- to the full oriented Figure-2-4 path field.
            IsOpen Ufig Ōł¦
            IsOpen V Ōł¦ IsConnected V Ōł¦ V.Nonempty Ōł¦ xseed Ōłł V Ōł¦
            IsCompact (closure V) Ōł¦
            closure V ŌŖå Ufig Ōł¦
            ... Ōł¦
            (let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
             (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
               BHW.realEmbed
                 (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                   (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                 BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Ōł¦
             (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
               BHW.realEmbed
                 (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                   (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                 BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä) Ōł¦
             (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
               x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector
                 (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Ōł¦
             (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
               (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                 EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector
                   (d := d) (n := n) Žä) Ōł¦
             (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
               BHW.realEmbed
                 (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                   (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                 BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Ōł¦
             (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
               BHW.realEmbed
                 (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                   (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                 BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä) Ōł¦
             (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
               BHW.OS45Figure24RotatedPathFormulaField
                 (d := d) hd n i hi x) Ōł¦
             (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
               BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField (d := d) n i hi x)) := by
        -- Copy `os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch`, but call
        -- `swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithRotatedPathStability`
        -- and store `hclosureV_rotated` / `hclosureV_oriented` from its final
        -- field.  All ordinary OS45 source-patch fields are unchanged.
        ...

      structure BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedCanonicalSourcePatchData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) where
        toCanonical :
          BHW.OS45Figure24CanonicalSourcePatchData (d := d) hd n i hi
        rotatedPath_closure :
          ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł closure toCanonical.V ->
            BHW.OS45Figure24RotatedPathFormulaField
              (d := d) hd n i hi x
        orientedPath_closure :
          ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł closure toCanonical.V ->
            BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField (d := d) n i hi x

      theorem BHW.exists_os45_adjacent_identity_canonicalSourcePatch_with_orientedPath
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          Ōłā P :
            BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedCanonicalSourcePatchData
              (d := d) hd n i hi, True

      noncomputable def BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_canonicalSourcePatch_with_orientedPath
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedCanonicalSourcePatchData
            (d := d) hd n i hi :=
        Classical.choose
          (BHW.exists_os45_adjacent_identity_canonicalSourcePatch_with_orientedPath
            (d := d) (n := n) hd i hi)
      ```

      Production order: `OS45Figure24CanonicalSourcePatchData` is the
      deterministic-lift packet defined in the adjacent-source section below.
      The production Lean port proves the oriented existential packet in
      `Prop` and exposes both the oriented packet and its pure canonical
      projection by `noncomputable def`, since data cannot be obtained from
      upstream existential propositions by direct theorem elimination into
      `Type`.  This is now checked in
      `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45Figure24.lean`.  It is not a new OS input and
      not a new route; it preserves the actual Figure-2-4 rotated path
      before the pure-Gram projection forgets determinant signs, and it keeps
      the compact real-patch contact separate from the quarter-turn complex
      endpoint.

      The production-oriented environment theorem should not introduce a new
      path.  It should expose the same `╬ō` and `╬ö` already used in
      `swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists` and then replace the
      final pure-Gram calculation by the oriented calculation above.  This
      non-canonical oriented path/source-patch slice is now checked in Lean.
      The remaining Figure-2-4 selector work is the canonical deterministic
      adjacent-lift packet and the corrected two-point common-edge contact
      producer; downstream scalar consumers still need migration from pure
      Gram data to the oriented data structure.

      Because this route is now the active theorem-2 source route, every
      downstream consumer that currently
      mentions `hRep.Phi (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)`,
      `sourcePermuteComplexGram`, `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain`, or
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.branch_eq` must be migrated to the
      oriented analog before any theorem-2 Lean proof starts.  In particular,
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`,
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq`, and
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq` need
      oriented-domain statements before Lean.  Keeping their pure-Gram surfaces while
      proving only `extendedTube_same_sourceOrientedInvariant_extendF_eq`
      would be a wrapper error.

      Oriented downstream migration ledger.  The following finite
      source-variety infrastructure replaces every pure-Gram double-domain and
      identity-principle consumer.  The base `sourceOrientedGramVariety` and
      `IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety` definitions were introduced in
      the active source API above.

      ```lean
      def BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain
          (d n : Nat) (Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :=
        {G |
          G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n Ōł¦
          BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žä G Ōłł
            BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n}

      theorem BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram_mem_variety_iff
          (d n : Nat) (Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :
          BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žä G Ōłł
              BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n Ōåö
            G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
        constructor
        ┬Ę rintro Ō¤©z, hzŌ¤®
          refine Ō¤©BHW.permAct (d := d) ŽäŌü╗┬╣ z, ?_Ō¤®
          calc
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) ŽäŌü╗┬╣ z)
                =
              BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n ŽäŌü╗┬╣
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z) := by
                exact
                  BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_permAct
                    d n ŽäŌü╗┬╣ z
            _ =
              BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n ŽäŌü╗┬╣
                (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žä G) := by
                rw [hz]
            _ = G := by
                apply BHW.SourceOrientedGramData.ext
                ┬Ę simpa [BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram] using
                    BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_inv_mul n Žä G.gram
                ┬Ę funext ╬╣
                  -- Determinant coordinates compose with the embeddings
                  -- `ŽäŌü╗┬╣.toEmbedding` and `Žä.toEmbedding`, hence return to
                  -- the original ordered full-frame embedding.
                  simp [BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram]
        ┬Ę rintro Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®
          refine Ō¤©BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä z, ?_Ō¤®
          exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_permAct
              (d := d) (n := n) Žä z

      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety.preimage_sourcePermuteOrientedGram
          {U : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hU : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n U)
          (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n
            {G | BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žā G Ōłł U} := by
        rcases hU with Ō¤©U0, hU0_open, rflŌ¤®
        refine Ō¤©{G | BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žā G Ōłł U0},
          hU0_open.preimage
            (BHW.continuous_sourcePermuteOrientedGram
              (d := d) (n := n) Žā), ?_Ō¤®
        ext G
        constructor
        ┬Ę intro h
          exact Ō¤©h.1,
            (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram_mem_variety_iff
              (d := d) (n := n) Žā G).1 h.2Ō¤®
        ┬Ę intro h
          exact Ō¤©h.1,
            (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram_mem_variety_iff
              (d := d) (n := n) Žā G).2 h.2Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain_relOpen_of_sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain
          {d n : Nat}
          (hU :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n))
          (Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n
            (BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n Žä) := by
        have hpre :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n
              {G | BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žä G Ōłł
                BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n} :=
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety.preimage_sourcePermuteOrientedGram
            d n hU Žä
        simpa [BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain] using
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety.inter d n hU hpre

      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety.iUnion
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬╣ : Type*}
          {U : ╬╣ -> Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hU :
            ŌłĆ i,
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n (U i)) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n (Ōŗā i, U i) := by
        choose U0 hU0_open hU0_eq using hU
        refine Ō¤©Ōŗā i, U0 i, isOpen_iUnion hU0_open, ?_Ō¤®
        ext G
        simp [hU0_eq]

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {D : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hD_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n D)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0D : G0 Ōłł D) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n
            (connectedComponentIn D G0) := by
        classical
        rcases hD_rel with Ō¤©D0, hD0_open, hD_eqŌ¤®
        let C : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :=
          connectedComponentIn D G0
        have hpatch :
            ŌłĆ Y : {G // G Ōłł C},
              Ōłā V : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n),
                Y.1 Ōłł V Ōł¦
                BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n V Ōł¦
                IsConnected V Ōł¦
                V ŌŖå D Ōł¦
                V ŌŖå C := by
          intro Y
          have hYD : Y.1 Ōłł D :=
            connectedComponentIn_subset D G0 Y.2
          have hYD0 : Y.1 Ōłł D0 := by
            rw [hD_eq] at hYD
            exact hYD.1
          have hYvar : Y.1 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
            rw [hD_eq] at hYD
            exact hYD.2
          obtain Ō¤©V, hYV, hV_rel, hV_conn, hV_sub_D0varŌ¤® :=
            BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis
              (d := d) hd n hYvar hD0_open hYD0
          have hV_sub_D : V ŌŖå D := by
            intro G hGV
            rw [hD_eq]
            exact hV_sub_D0var hGV
          have hV_sub_component_Y :
              V ŌŖå connectedComponentIn D Y.1 :=
            hV_conn.isPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn
              hYV hV_sub_D
          have hcomponent_eq :
              connectedComponentIn D Y.1 = C := by
            have hEq :=
              connectedComponentIn_eq (F := D) (x := G0)
                (y := Y.1) Y.2
            simpa [C] using hEq.symm
          refine
            Ō¤©V, hYV, hV_rel, hV_conn, hV_sub_D, ?_Ō¤®
          intro G hGV
          simpa [hcomponent_eq] using hV_sub_component_Y hGV
        choose V hV_mem hV_rel hV_conn hV_sub_D hV_sub_C using hpatch
        have hC_eq :
            C = Ōŗā Y : {G // G Ōłł C}, V Y := by
          ext G
          constructor
          ┬Ę intro hGC
            exact Ō¤©Ō¤©G, hGCŌ¤®, hV_mem Ō¤©G, hGCŌ¤®Ō¤®
          ┬Ę intro hGU
            rcases hGU with Ō¤©Y, hGYŌ¤®
            exact hV_sub_C Y hGY
        rw [show connectedComponentIn D G0 = C from rfl, hC_eq]
        exact
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety.iUnion
            d n hV_rel
      ```

      The local connected-basis theorem is genuine source geometry, not a
      wrapper around the ordinary Gram basis.  Its proof must use the same
      oriented local-realization packets required by
      `sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_relOpen_connected`, but without the
      extended-tube restriction: max-rank points use the full-frame
      determinant slice and rank-deficient points use the oriented Schur
      residual chart.  Once that basis theorem is available, the component
      theorem is the same connected-component proof as the checked pure-Gram
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen`: write
      `D = D0 Ōł® sourceOrientedGramVariety d n`; for each
      `Y : connectedComponentIn D G0`, apply the local connected basis inside
      `D0`, use
      `IsPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn` to put the local connected
      patch inside the component, identify components by
      `connectedComponentIn_eq`, and express the component as an indexed
      union over `{G // G Ōłł connectedComponentIn D G0}` of those relatively
      open patches.  This assembly is now checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedConnected.lean` as
      `BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen_of_local_connectedRelOpen_basis`,
      with the path-tube version checked as
      `BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_connectedRelOpenTube_around_compactPath_of_local_connectedRelOpen_basis`.
      The union is relatively open by
      `BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety.iUnion`.

      The local connected-basis theorem itself is implemented through a
      variety-local patch packet.  This packet is deliberately weaker than
      `SourceOrientedLocalHolomorphicSectionData`: it does not require the
      selected vectors to remain in `ExtendedTube`, because the connected
      component theorem is a statement about the whole oriented source
      variety.  Extended-tube shrinkings remain only in
      the checked extended-tube local-realization producer.

      The stratum dispatcher is now checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedLocalBasis.lean`.  The checked theorem
      `BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis_of_chart_and_localImage_producers`
      takes exactly two producer inputs: a finite-coordinate
      `SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData` at each max-rank point, and a
      `SourceOrientedRankDeficientVarietyLocalImageData` packet at each
      exceptional-rank point.  It also exports the checked component and
      path-tube adapters
      `BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen_of_chart_and_localImage_producers`
      and
      `BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_connectedRelOpenTube_around_compactPath_of_chart_and_localImage_producers`.

      ```lean
      structure BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyLocalConnectedPatchData
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          (G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) where
        V : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
        center_mem : G0 Ōłł V
        V_relOpen :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n V
        V_connected : IsConnected V

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_localConnectedPatch_maxRank
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hmax : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā P :
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyLocalConnectedPatchData d n G0,
            P.V ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_localConnectedPatch_rankDeficient
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hlow : ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā P :
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyLocalConnectedPatchData d n G0,
            P.V ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 : G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0) (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā V : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n),
            G0 Ōłł V Ōł¦
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n V Ōł¦
            IsConnected V Ōł¦
            V ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
        classical
        by_cases hmax : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0
        ┬Ę rcases
            BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_localConnectedPatch_maxRank
              (d := d) hd n hG0 hmax hN0_open hG0N0 with
            Ō¤©P, hPsubŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©P.V, P.center_mem, P.V_relOpen, P.V_connected, hPsubŌ¤®
        ┬Ę rcases
            BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_localConnectedPatch_rankDeficient
              (d := d) hd n hG0 hmax hN0_open hG0N0 with
            Ō¤©P, hPsubŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©P.V, P.center_mem, P.V_relOpen, P.V_connected, hPsubŌ¤®
      ```

      The two patch producers are now pinned at the same granularity as the
      component theorem.  The max-rank patch is a generic connected-ball
      restriction of an oriented local chart.  It uses exactly the
      full-frame/small-arity chart theorem already listed for the oriented
      quotient proof, but with the `toVec_mem : toVec G Ōłł ExtendedTube`
      field dropped.  The chart restriction may not assume that a relatively
      open chart domain is ambient-open; the ball is chosen in chart
      coordinates and pulled back by the local inverse.

      The checked max-rank chart support lives in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedLocalChart.lean`.  Besides the connected
      ball and pullback lemmas displayed below, it now includes
      `BHW.finiteDimensionalCoordinateEquiv`,
      `BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData.to_finrankCoordinateChart`, and
      `BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData.local_identity_near_point_finiteDimensional`,
      so a future finite-dimensional chart producer can feed the finite
      coordinate identity theorem without redoing coordinate transport.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_connected_ball_in_localInverse_domain
          {M E : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup M] [NormedSpace Ōäé M]
          [TopologicalSpace E]
          {Dom : Set M}
          {inv : M -> E}
          {y0 : M}
          {N0 : Set E}
          (hDom_open : IsOpen Dom)
          (hy0Dom : y0 Ōłł Dom)
          (hinv_cont : ContinuousOn inv Dom)
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hy0N0 : inv y0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā ╬Ą : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ą Ōł¦
            Metric.ball y0 ╬Ą ŌŖå Dom Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ y Ōłł Metric.ball y0 ╬Ą, inv y Ōłł N0) Ōł¦
            IsConnected (Metric.ball y0 ╬Ą) := by
        classical
        rcases Metric.isOpen_iff.mp hDom_open y0 hy0Dom with
          Ō¤©╬ĄD, h╬ĄD, hballDŌ¤®
        rcases
          (continuousOn_iff.mp hinv_cont)
            y0 hy0Dom N0 hN0_open hy0N0 with
          Ō¤©O, hO_open, hy0O, hO_subŌ¤®
        rcases Metric.isOpen_iff.mp hO_open y0 hy0O with
          Ō¤©╬ĄO, h╬ĄO, hballOŌ¤®
        let ╬Ą := min ╬ĄD ╬ĄO
        have h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą := lt_min h╬ĄD h╬ĄO
        refine Ō¤©╬Ą, h╬Ą, ?_, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
        ┬Ę intro y hy
          have hyD : y Ōłł Metric.ball y0 ╬ĄD := by
            exact Metric.mem_ball.mpr
              (lt_of_lt_of_le (Metric.mem_ball.mp hy) (min_le_left _ _))
          exact hballD hyD
        ┬Ę intro y hy
          have hyO : y Ōłł Metric.ball y0 ╬ĄO := by
            exact Metric.mem_ball.mpr
              (lt_of_lt_of_le (Metric.mem_ball.mp hy) (min_le_right _ _))
          have hyD : y Ōłł Metric.ball y0 ╬ĄD := by
            exact Metric.mem_ball.mpr
              (lt_of_lt_of_le (Metric.mem_ball.mp hy) (min_le_left _ _))
          exact hO_sub Ō¤©hballO hyO, hballD hyDŌ¤®
        ┬Ę exact (convex_ball y0 ╬Ą).isConnected
            Ō¤©y0, Metric.mem_ball_self h╬ĄŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData.exists_connected_chartBall
          {M : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup M] [NormedSpace Ōäé M]
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (C : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData d n (M := M) G0)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā ╬Ą : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ą Ōł¦
            Metric.ball (C.chart G0) ╬Ą ŌŖå C.chart '' C.╬® Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ y Ōłł Metric.ball (C.chart G0) ╬Ą,
              C.chart_biholomorphic.inv y Ōłł N0) Ōł¦
            IsConnected (Metric.ball (C.chart G0) ╬Ą) := by
        -- `C.chart G0 Ōłł C.chart '' C.╬®` by `C.center_mem` and
        -- `C.chart_open`.  Continuity-on of the local inverse at
        -- `C.chart G0`, together with `hN0_open` and
        -- `C.chart_biholomorphic.left_inv_on G0 C.center_mem`, gives a
        -- chart neighborhood whose inverse lies in `N0`.  Intersect it with
        -- a smaller metric ball inside `C.chart '' C.╬®`.
        -- Open balls in a complex normed vector space are convex, hence
        -- connected.
        exact
          BHW.exists_connected_ball_in_localInverse_domain
            C.chart_open
            Ō¤©G0, C.center_mem, rflŌ¤®
            C.chart_biholomorphic.inv_continuousOn
            hN0_open
            (by
              simpa [C.chart_biholomorphic.left_inv_on G0 C.center_mem]
                using hG0N0)

      theorem BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData.inv_image_openBall_relOpen
          {M : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup M] [NormedSpace Ōäé M]
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (C : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData d n (M := M) G0)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ}
          (hball : Metric.ball (C.chart G0) ╬Ą ŌŖå C.chart '' C.╬®) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n
            (C.chart_biholomorphic.inv ''
              Metric.ball (C.chart G0) ╬Ą) := by
        -- On `C.╬®`, the inverse image of the ball is
        -- `{G Ōłł C.╬® | C.chart G Ōłł Metric.ball (C.chart G0) ╬Ą}` by the
        -- chart inverse laws.  This is relatively open because `C.╬®` is
        -- relatively open in the oriented variety, the ball is open in the
        -- model, and the chart map is continuous on the ambient chart
        -- neighborhood supplied by the max-rank local-image theorem.
        exact
          BHW.LocalBiholomorphOnSourceOrientedVariety.inv_image_open_relOpen
            (d := d) (n := n)
            C.╬®_relOpen C.chart_biholomorphic
            (Metric.isOpen_ball) hball

      theorem BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData.connectedPatch_inside_open
          {M : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup M] [NormedSpace Ōäé M]
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (C : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData d n (M := M) G0)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā P :
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyLocalConnectedPatchData d n G0,
            P.V ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
        classical
        rcases C.exists_connected_chartBall hN0_open hG0N0 with
          Ō¤©╬Ą, h╬Ą, hball_dom, hball_N0, hball_connŌ¤®
        let B : Set M := Metric.ball (C.chart G0) ╬Ą
        let V : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :=
          C.chart_biholomorphic.inv '' B
        have hcenter : G0 Ōłł V := by
          refine Ō¤©C.chart G0, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
          ┬Ę exact Metric.mem_ball_self h╬Ą
          ┬Ę exact C.chart_biholomorphic.left_inv_on G0 C.center_mem
        have hrel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n V :=
          C.inv_image_openBall_relOpen hball_dom
        have hconn : IsConnected V :=
          by
            have hcont : ContinuousOn C.chart_biholomorphic.inv B :=
              C.chart_biholomorphic.inv_continuousOn.mono hball_dom
            simpa [V, B] using
              hball_conn.image C.chart_biholomorphic.inv hcont
        have hsub :
            V ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
          rintro G Ō¤©y, hyB, rflŌ¤®
          constructor
          ┬Ę exact hball_N0 y hyB
          ┬Ę exact
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety.subset
                C.╬®_relOpen
                (C.chart_biholomorphic.inv_mem_on y (hball_dom hyB))
        exact
          Ō¤©{ V := V
             center_mem := hcenter
             V_relOpen := hrel
             V_connected := hconn }, hsubŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedMaxRankChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hmax : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankChartData d n G0 := by
        -- If `n < d+1`, use the ordinary source-Gram max-rank chart and the
        -- empty determinant-coordinate extensionality theorem.  If
        -- `d+1 <= n`, choose a nonzero full-frame determinant coordinate and
        -- use `SourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData`, whose selected
        -- frame is supplied by the special-orthogonal gauge slice.
        by_cases hn : n < d + 1
        ┬Ę exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedMaxRankChart_smallArity
              (d := d) hd n hn hG0 hmax
        ┬Ę have hn_le : d + 1 <= n := Nat.le_of_not_lt hn
          rcases
            BHW.exists_nonzero_orientedFullFrameDet_of_maxRank
              (d := d) hd n hn_le hG0 hmax with
            Ō¤©╬╣, h╬╣Ō¤®
          exact
            (BHW.sourceOrientedFullFrameMaxRankChartData_at
              (d := d) hd n hn_le hG0 ╬╣ h╬╣).to_maxRankChart

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_localConnectedPatch_maxRank
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hmax : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā P :
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyLocalConnectedPatchData d n G0,
            P.V ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
        exact
          (BHW.sourceOrientedMaxRankChart_at
            (d := d) hd n hG0 hmax).connectedPatch_inside_open
            hN0_open hG0N0
      ```

      The rank-deficient patch is a separate no-tube algebraic local-image
      theorem.  This is load-bearing: the connected-basis theorem is about an
      arbitrary point of `sourceOrientedGramVariety d n`, not necessarily a
      point in the extended-tube image.  Therefore it may not reuse
      `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChart`, whose compact polydisc
      and tube-stability fields depend on an `ExtendedTube` base point.
      Instead the proof uses the same Schur residual coordinates before the
      compact/tube shrink and produces a local image of the algebraic oriented
      variety itself.

      ```lean
      structure BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientAlgebraicNormalFormData
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          (G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) where
        r : Nat
        hrD : r < d + 1
        hrn : r <= n
        varietyTransport :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n
        canonical_to_original :
          (varietyTransport.invFun
            (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSourceOrientedVariety d n r)).1 = G0

      -- Algebraic-adapted representative gate.  The normal-form transport
      -- may not be applied to an arbitrary source realization of `G0`: a
      -- realization can contain radical tail vectors, so its source span can
      -- have dimension strictly larger than the scalar Gram rank.  The
      -- no-tube local-image proof first replaces the ordinary Gram coordinate
      -- by a same-Gram representative whose source span has the correct
      -- dimension.  This is purely finite-dimensional algebra, unlike the
      -- extended-tube adapted representative used in Lemma 3.
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_exists_adaptedSourceRepresentative
          (d n : Nat)
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :
          Ōłā z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z = G Ōł¦
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) =
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G := by
        classical
        let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G
        have hGsym : G Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n :=
          BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_subset_sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace
            d n hG
        have hr_exact :
            G Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum n r :=
          by
            refine Ō¤©hGsym, ?_Ō¤®
            have hM : (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => G i j) = G := by
              ext i j
              rfl
            simp [r, BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank, hM]
        have hrD : r <= d + 1 := by
          -- From `hG` and
          -- `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le`, or equivalently from
          -- `sourceComplexGramVariety_subset_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety`.
          exact BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_le_spacetime_of_mem_sourceComplexGramVariety
            (d := d) (n := n) (G := G) hG
        rcases BHW.exists_fullRank_sourceComplexDotGram_of_rankExact
            (m := n) (r := r) hr_exact with
          Ō¤©A, _hA_full, hA_gramŌ¤®
        let Apad : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          BHW.sourceComplexPad d n r hrD A
        let z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun i => (BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d).symm (Apad i)
        have hzGram : BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z = G := by
          rw [BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_eq_dotGram_after_equiv]
          have hrow :
              (fun i =>
                BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d
                  ((BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d).symm
                    (Apad i))) = Apad := by
            ext i ╬╝
            exact congrFun
              ((BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d).apply_symm_apply
                (Apad i)) ╬╝
          rw [hrow]
          rw [show BHW.sourceComplexDotGram (d + 1) n Apad =
              BHW.sourceComplexDotGram r n A by
            simpa [Apad] using
              BHW.sourceComplexDotGram_padded_eq d n r hrD A]
          exact hA_gram
        have hspan_le :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) <= r := by
          -- The coefficient-evaluation range is contained in the `r`-dimensional
          -- padded coordinate subspace because every `Apad i` has zero tail;
          -- transport through `complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv.symm`.
          simpa [z, Apad] using
            BHW.finrank_sourceCoefficientEval_range_le_of_paddedDot
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) hrD A
        have hrank_eq :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) = r := by
          simpa [hzGram, r]
        have hrank_le_span :
            r <= Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) := by
          -- `sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range` and
          -- `restrictedMinkowskiRank <= finrank`.
          simpa [hrank_eq] using
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_le_finrank_sourceCoefficientEval_range
              (d := d) (n := n) z
        exact Ō¤©z, hzGram, by
          simpa [r] using le_antisymm hspan_le hrank_le_spanŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_lowRank_exists_adaptedRepresentative
          (d n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 : G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hlow : ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0) :
          Ōłā z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 = G0 Ōł¦
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z0)) =
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) := by
        classical
        rcases hG0 with Ō¤©zRaw, hzRawŌ¤®
        subst hzRaw
        have hraw_low :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zRaw) < d + 1 :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_notMaxRank_sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_fullFrame
            d n zRaw hlow
        have hGvar :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zRaw Ōłł
              BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
          exact Ō¤©zRaw, rflŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_exists_adaptedSourceRepresentative
            (d := d) (n := n) hGvar with
          Ō¤©z0, hz0Gram, hz0spanŌ¤®
        have hsame :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n zRaw =
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 := by
          exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_eq_of_sameGram_rank_lt
              d n (z := zRaw) (w := z0) hz0Gram.symm hraw_low
        exact Ō¤©z0, hsame.symm, by simpa [hz0Gram] using hz0spanŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_algebraicNormalFormData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hlow : ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientAlgebraicNormalFormData d n G0 := by
        classical
        rcases
          BHW.sourceOriented_lowRank_exists_normalFormVarietyTransport_from_canonical
            d n hG0 hlow with
          Ō¤©r, hrD, hrn, T, hTŌ¤®
        refine
          { r := r
            hrD := hrD
            hrn := hrn
            varietyTransport := T
            canonical_to_original := hT }

      -- The checked topology-facing packet in
      -- `SourceOrientedRankDeficientLocalImage.lean` has the abstract
      -- parameter-space shape below.  This older packet is still the right
      -- input for the connected local-basis dispatcher.  It is not enough for
      -- finite-overlap monodromy, because that later propagation needs
      -- connectedness of the local image after intersecting with the max-rank
      -- stratum.
      structure BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientVarietyLocalImageData
          {P : Type*} [TopologicalSpace P]
          (d n : Nat)
          (G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
          (N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)) where
        parameterBox : Set P
        parameterBox_open : IsOpen parameterBox
        parameterBox_connected : IsConnected parameterBox
        p0 : P
        p0_mem : p0 Ōłł parameterBox
        image : P -> BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n
        image_continuousOn : ContinuousOn image parameterBox
        center_eq : image p0 = G0
        image_relOpen :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n
            (image '' parameterBox)
        image_sub :
          image '' parameterBox ŌŖå
            N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n

      -- Checked subtype adapter for the corrected source-change route.  A
      -- concrete normal-form producer may work with `imageV` valued directly
      -- in `BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n`, prove subtype openness of
      -- `imageV '' parameterBox`, then expose the ambient packet by composing
      -- with `Subtype.val`.
      def BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientVarietyLocalImageData.ofSubtype
          {P : Type*} [TopologicalSpace P]
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          {parameterBox : Set P}
          (parameterBox_open : IsOpen parameterBox)
          (parameterBox_connected : IsConnected parameterBox)
          {p0 : P}
          (p0_mem : p0 Ōłł parameterBox)
          {imageV : P -> BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n}
          (imageV_continuousOn : ContinuousOn imageV parameterBox)
          (center_eq : (imageV p0).1 = G0)
          (imageV_open : IsOpen (imageV '' parameterBox))
          (imageV_sub :
            BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyUnderlyingSet d n
                (imageV '' parameterBox) ŌŖå
              N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientVarietyLocalImageData
            (d := d) (n := n) (P := P) G0 N0 := by
        -- Checked in production Lean using
        -- `sourceOrientedVarietyUnderlyingSet_image`,
        -- `sourceOrientedVarietyUnderlyingSet_relOpen_of_isOpen`, and
        -- continuity of `Subtype.val`.
        exact
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientVarietyLocalImageData.ofSubtype
            (d := d) (n := n) parameterBox_open parameterBox_connected
            p0_mem imageV_continuousOn center_eq imageV_open imageV_sub

      -- The active theorem-2 route therefore uses this checked strengthening.
      -- The concrete Schur/residual construction must instantiate `P` with
      -- the same finite head/mixed/tail parameter box and prove the extra
      -- max-rank connectedness field.  This is not wrapper churn: it is exactly
      -- the topological fact needed to pass from connected source-patch domains
      -- to connected max-rank transition domains.
      theorem BHW.isConnected_image_inter_sourceOrientedMaxRank_of_parameter_slice
          {P : Type*} [TopologicalSpace P]
          {parameterBox parameterMaxRank : Set P}
          {image : P -> BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hparameter_sub : parameterMaxRank ŌŖå parameterBox)
          (hparameter_conn : IsConnected parameterMaxRank)
          (himage_eq :
            image '' parameterMaxRank =
              (image '' parameterBox) Ōł®
                {G | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G})
          (himage_cont : ContinuousOn image parameterBox) :
          IsConnected
            ((image '' parameterBox) Ōł®
              {G | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G}) := by
        -- Checked in production Lean: rewrite by `himage_eq`, then use
        -- `hparameter_conn.image image (himage_cont.mono hparameter_sub)`.
        exact
          BHW.isConnected_image_inter_sourceOrientedMaxRank_of_parameter_slice
            (d := d) (n := n) hparameter_sub hparameter_conn
            himage_eq himage_cont

      theorem BHW.image_inter_preimage_sourceOrientedMaxRank_eq
          {P : Type*}
          {parameterBox : Set P}
          {image : P -> BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n} :
          image '' (parameterBox Ōł®
              {p | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n (image p)}) =
            (image '' parameterBox) Ōł®
              {G | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G} := by
        -- Checked in production Lean by extensionality.
        exact
          BHW.image_inter_preimage_sourceOrientedMaxRank_eq
            (d := d) (n := n)

      theorem BHW.isConnected_image_inter_sourceOrientedMaxRank_of_connected_preimage
          {P : Type*} [TopologicalSpace P]
          {parameterBox : Set P}
          {image : P -> BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hparameter_conn :
            IsConnected (parameterBox Ōł®
              {p | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n (image p)}))
          (himage_cont : ContinuousOn image parameterBox) :
          IsConnected
            ((image '' parameterBox) Ōł®
              {G | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G}) := by
        -- Checked in production Lean by the previous two helpers.
        exact
          BHW.isConnected_image_inter_sourceOrientedMaxRank_of_connected_preimage
            (d := d) (n := n) hparameter_conn himage_cont

      structure BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientMaxRankLocalImageData
          {P : Type*} [TopologicalSpace P]
          (d n : Nat)
          (G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
          (N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)) where
        parameterBox : Set P
        parameterBox_open : IsOpen parameterBox
        parameterBox_connected : IsConnected parameterBox
        p0 : P
        p0_mem : p0 Ōłł parameterBox
        image : P -> BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n
        image_continuousOn : ContinuousOn image parameterBox
        center_eq : image p0 = G0
        image_relOpen :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n
            (image '' parameterBox)
        image_sub :
          image '' parameterBox ŌŖå
            N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n
        image_maxRank_connected :
          IsConnected
            ((image '' parameterBox) Ōł®
              {G | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G})

      def BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientVarietyLocalImageData
          .to_maxRankLocalImageData_of_connected_preimage
          {P : Type*} [TopologicalSpace P]
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (R :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientVarietyLocalImageData
              (d := d) (n := n) (P := P) G0 N0)
          (hpreimage_connected :
            IsConnected (R.parameterBox Ōł®
              {p | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n (R.image p)})) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientMaxRankLocalImageData
            (d := d) (n := n) (P := P) G0 N0 := by
        -- Checked in production Lean by reusing the fields of `R` and filling
        -- `image_maxRank_connected` with
        -- `isConnected_image_inter_sourceOrientedMaxRank_of_connected_preimage`.
        exact
          R.to_maxRankLocalImageData_of_connected_preimage
            hpreimage_connected

      def BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientMaxRankLocalImageData.ofSubtype
          {P : Type*} [TopologicalSpace P]
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          {parameterBox : Set P}
          (parameterBox_open : IsOpen parameterBox)
          (parameterBox_connected : IsConnected parameterBox)
          {p0 : P}
          (p0_mem : p0 Ōłł parameterBox)
          {imageV : P -> BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n}
          (imageV_continuousOn : ContinuousOn imageV parameterBox)
          (center_eq : (imageV p0).1 = G0)
          (imageV_open : IsOpen (imageV '' parameterBox))
          (imageV_sub :
            BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyUnderlyingSet d n
                (imageV '' parameterBox) ŌŖå
              N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hpreimage_connected :
            IsConnected (parameterBox Ōł®
              {p | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n (imageV p).1})) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientMaxRankLocalImageData
            (d := d) (n := n) (P := P) G0 N0 := by
        -- Checked in production Lean by composing `ofSubtype` with
        -- `to_maxRankLocalImageData_of_connected_preimage`.
        exact
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientMaxRankLocalImageData.ofSubtype
            (d := d) (n := n) parameterBox_open parameterBox_connected
            p0_mem imageV_continuousOn center_eq imageV_open imageV_sub
            hpreimage_connected

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedRankDeficientConnectedMaxRankPatchAt_of_localImageProducer
          (rankDeficientLocalImageAt :
            ŌłĆ {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n},
              G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ->
              BHW.SourceOrientedExceptionalRank d n G0 ->
              ŌłĆ {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)},
                IsOpen N0 -> G0 Ōłł N0 ->
                ╬Ż P : Type, ╬Ż _ : TopologicalSpace P,
                  BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientMaxRankLocalImageData
                    (d := d) (n := n) (P := P) G0 N0)
          {U : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n U)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0U : G0 Ōłł U)
          (hex : BHW.SourceOrientedExceptionalRank d n G0)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā V : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n),
            G0 Ōłł V Ōł¦
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n V Ōł¦
            V ŌŖå U Ōł® N0 Ōł¦
            IsConnected
              (V Ōł® {G | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G}) := by
        -- Checked in production Lean.  The proof opens `hU_rel` as
        -- `U = U0 Ōł® sourceOrientedGramVariety`, calls the producer on the
        -- ambient open shrink `U0 Ōł® N0`, and transports `image_sub` back to
        -- `V ŌŖå U Ōł® N0`.
        exact
          BHW.sourceOrientedRankDeficientConnectedMaxRankPatchAt_of_localImageProducer
            (d := d) (n := n) rankDeficientLocalImageAt
            hU_rel hG0U hex hN0_open hG0N0

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_varietyLocalImage
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hlow : ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          ╬Ż P : Type, ╬Ż _ : TopologicalSpace P,
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientVarietyLocalImageData
              (d := d) (n := n) (P := P) G0 N0 := by
        classical
        let N :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_algebraicNormalFormData
            (d := d) hd n hG0 hlow
        -- Choose finite coordinates for
        -- `SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n N.r N.hrD N.hrn`
        -- and a small connected open ball `P` around the encoded normal
        -- center.  Shrink it so the selected head Gram block remains
        -- invertible and so the inverse variety transport of the normal
        -- subtype image lies inside `N0`; the transported open-neighborhood
        -- and invertible-head shrink is now checked as
        -- `SourceOrientedRankDeficientAlgebraicNormalFormData.exists_normalParameterBall_image_subset_open_and_head`.
        rcases
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_algebraicParameterBall
            (d := d) hd n N hN0_open hG0N0 with
          Ō¤©m, encode, P, c0, ╬®nf, hP_open, hP_conn, hc0,
            h╬®nf_open, himage_N0, hlocal_surj, htoInv_cont,
            htoInv_c0, htoInv_memŌ¤®
        let normalV : (Fin m -> Ōäé) -> BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n :=
          fun c =>
            Ō¤©BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
                d n N.r N.hrD N.hrn (encode.symm c)),
              htoInv_mem cŌ¤®
        let toInv : (Fin m -> Ōäé) -> BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n :=
          fun c =>
            (N.varietyTransport.invFun (normalV c)).1
        have himage_relOpen :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n (toInv '' P) := by
          -- Forward direction: transport `G` to normal coordinates, form
          -- Schur residual data, factor the invertible head block, realize
          -- the shifted oriented tail by
          -- `sourceShiftedTailSmallRealization`, and
          -- encode the resulting `(head,mixed,tail)` parameter.  Reverse
          -- direction is `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur`
          -- plus preservation of the variety by `N.varietyTransport`.
          -- The checked abstract packet stores this final relative-openness
          -- result as `image_relOpen`.
          exact hlocal_surj.relOpen
        have himage_sub :
            toInv '' P ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
          -- This is the no-tube local-image inclusion: normal parameters in
          -- the shrunken box map into the prescribed ambient open `N0` and
          -- into the oriented source variety.
          exact himage_N0
        refine
          Ō¤©Fin m -> Ōäé, inferInstance,
            { parameterBox := P
              parameterBox_open := hP_open
              parameterBox_connected := hP_conn
              p0 := c0
              p0_mem := hc0
              image := toInv
              image_continuousOn := htoInv_cont
              center_eq := htoInv_c0
              image_relOpen := himage_relOpen
              image_sub := himage_sub }Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_maxRankVarietyLocalImage
          [NeZero d]
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hlow : ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          ╬Ż P : Type, ╬Ż _ : TopologicalSpace P,
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientMaxRankLocalImageData
              (d := d) (n := n) (P := P) G0 N0 := by
        classical
        -- Same normal-form and parameter-ball construction as
        -- `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_varietyLocalImage`.
        let N :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_algebraicNormalFormData
            (d := d) hd n hG0 hlow
        rcases
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_algebraicParameterBall
            (d := d) hd n N hN0_open hG0N0 with
          Ō¤©m, encode, P, c0, ╬®nf, hP_open, hP_conn, hc0,
            h╬®nf_open, himage_N0, hlocal_surj, htoInv_cont,
            htoInv_c0, htoInv_memŌ¤®
        let normalV : (Fin m -> Ōäé) -> BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n :=
          fun c =>
            Ō¤©BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
                d n N.r N.hrD N.hrn (encode.symm c)),
              htoInv_mem cŌ¤®
        let toInv : (Fin m -> Ōäé) -> BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n :=
          fun c =>
            (N.varietyTransport.invFun (normalV c)).1
        have himage_relOpen :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n (toInv '' P) := by
          exact hlocal_surj.relOpen
        have himage_sub :
            toInv '' P ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
          exact himage_N0
        have hparameter_maxRank_connected :
            IsConnected
              (P Ōł® {c | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n (toInv c)}) := by
          -- This is the remaining concrete exceptional local-image proof.
          -- Expand `toInv`, package the normal Schur point as a
          -- `SourceOrientedVariety`, absorb the source-change inverse by the
          -- checked theorem
          -- `SourceOrientedRankDeficientAlgebraicNormalFormData.originalNormalVarietyPoint_maxRank_iff_tail_rank`.
          -- On the invertible-head shrunken box, the max-rank condition is
          -- exactly that the shifted residual tail has rank `(d+1) - N.r`.
          -- Under `encode`, the set
          -- above is the product of:
          --
          -- * the connected head-gauge symmetric ball,
          -- * the connected mixed-coordinate ball,
          -- * the connected small symmetric residual cone at exact rank
          --   `(d+1) - N.r`.
          --
          -- The first two factors use `isConnected_symmetric_matrix_ball` and
          -- `isConnected_matrix_ball`; the last factor is the same theorem
          -- used in `sourceComplexGramVariety_local_rankExact_connected_basis_singular`,
          -- namely `matrixSymmetricRankExactCone_small_connected`.
          -- The checked variety-relative theorem
          -- `isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchur_varietyTransported_orientedMaxRank_preimage_of_eq`
          -- packages the product-connected Schur residual slice directly.
          -- The normal-form proof must only provide the subtype-valued normal
          -- parameter map, identify its underlying value with the principal
          -- Schur graph on `P`, prove `toInv` agrees with the inverse
          -- variety transport of that point, and then transport through
          -- `encode` by `isConnected_preimage_homeomorph`; the checked normal
          -- coordinate bridge is a homeomorphism, not a continuous-linear
          -- equivalence.
          --
          -- The determinant coordinates do not split this set into separate
          -- local branches: `SourceOrientedMaxRankAt` only reads `G.gram`, and
          -- every determinant coordinate in `toInv c` is a continuous
          -- polynomial/minor expression in the same source-vector parameter.
          -- Thus the connected parameter slice maps continuously onto the
          -- max-rank part of the oriented local image.
          exact
            BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_parameterMaxRank_connected
              (d := d) hn hd n N encode P c0 hP_open hP_conn
        have himage_maxRank_connected :
            IsConnected
              ((toInv '' P) Ōł®
                {G | BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G}) :=
          BHW.isConnected_image_inter_sourceOrientedMaxRank_of_connected_preimage
            (d := d) (n := n)
            (parameterBox := P) (image := toInv)
            hparameter_maxRank_connected htoInv_cont
        refine
          Ō¤©Fin m -> Ōäé, inferInstance,
            { parameterBox := P
              parameterBox_open := hP_open
              parameterBox_connected := hP_conn
              p0 := c0
              p0_mem := hc0
              image := toInv
              image_continuousOn := htoInv_cont
              center_eq := htoInv_c0
              image_relOpen := himage_relOpen
              image_sub := himage_sub
              image_maxRank_connected := himage_maxRank_connected }Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVariety_localConnectedPatch_rankDeficient
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hlow : ┬¼ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0)
          {N0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hG0N0 : G0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā P :
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyLocalConnectedPatchData d n G0,
            P.V ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
        classical
        rcases
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_varietyLocalImage
            (d := d) hd n hG0 hlow hN0_open hG0N0 with
          Ō¤©P, instP, CŌ¤®
        letI : TopologicalSpace P := instP
        rcases C.to_connectedRelOpenPatch with
          Ō¤©V, hcenter, hrel, hconn, hsubŌ¤®
        exact
          Ō¤©{ V := V
             center_mem := hcenter
             V_relOpen := hrel
             V_connected := hconn }, hsubŌ¤®
      ```

      The algebraic parameter-ball theorem in this transcript is precisely the
      no-tube part of the residual-polydisc construction: it chooses an
      ordinary finite-dimensional normal-form representative for a variety
      point, selects a connected open parameter ball, proves head-block
      invertibility on that ball, and proves local surjectivity onto
      `╬® Ōł® sourceOrientedGramVariety d n` by Schur residual extraction and
      `sourceShiftedTailSmallRealization`.  The compact set `K`,
      `toVec_mem : _ Ōłł ExtendedTube`, and compact boundedness/cluster-value
      fields are added only later, when the base point is known to lie in
      `ExtendedTube d n`.  This separation is now a proof-doc requirement for
      Lean: the local connected-basis theorem consumes the algebraic
      no-tube chart, while scalar descent at exceptional rank consumes the
