      ```lean
          ... := by
        classical
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
        let hChart :=
          BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart_of_figure24
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep
        let hSeed :=
          BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hChart hCompact
        let D : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n hSeed.Žä
        let q : NPointDomain d n -> BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n :=
          fun x =>
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (Q.symm
                (BHW.realEmbed
                  (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                    (d := d) (n := n)
                    (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)))
        have hq_cont : Continuous q := by
          simpa [q, Q] using
            BHW.continuous_os45QuarterTurnOrientedScalar
              (d := d) (n := n)
        have hqD : ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V, q x Ōłł D := by
          intro x hx
          have hxDswap :=
            BHW.os45QuarterTurnOrientedScalar_mem_double_of_orientedPath
              (d := d) hd n i hi (hOrientedPath x hx)
          simpa [D, q, Q, hSeed.Žä_eq] using hxDswap
        rcases hSeed.Wscal_component with Ō¤©Gbase, hGbaseW, hWcompŌ¤®
        rcases hV_nonempty with Ō¤©xbase, hxbaseVŌ¤®
        have hxbase_closure : xbase Ōłł closure V := subset_closure hxbaseV
        have hqbaseW : q xbase Ōłł hSeed.Wscal := by
          have hmem :
              q xbase Ōłł hSeed.Wscal := by
            simpa [hChart, hSeed, q, Q] using
              hSeed.wick_mem xbase ((by
                simpa [hChart, hSeed] using hV_sub) hxbaseV)
          exact hmem
        have hImage_conn : IsConnected (q '' closure V) :=
          hV_connected.closure.image q hq_cont.continuousOn
        have hImage_D : q '' closure V ŌŖå D := by
          intro G hG
          rcases hG with Ō¤©x, hx, rflŌ¤®
          exact hqD x hx
        have hqbase_image : q xbase Ōłł q '' closure V :=
          Ō¤©xbase, hxbase_closure, rflŌ¤®
        have hImage_component :
            q '' closure V ŌŖå connectedComponentIn D (q xbase) :=
          hImage_conn.isPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn
            hqbase_image hImage_D
        have hcomponent_eq :
            connectedComponentIn D (q xbase) = hSeed.Wscal := by
          have hqbase_component :
              q xbase Ōłł connectedComponentIn D Gbase := by
            simpa [hWcomp] using hqbaseW
          have hcomp :=
            connectedComponentIn_eq (F := D) (x := Gbase)
              (y := q xbase) hqbase_component
          simpa [hWcomp] using hcomp.symm
        intro x hx
        have hxComp : q x Ōłł connectedComponentIn D (q xbase) :=
          hImage_component Ō¤©x, hx, rflŌ¤®
        simpa [q, Q, hChart, hSeed, hcomponent_eq] using hxComp
      ```

      These oriented names are not optional aliases.  They are the only
      compatible consumers for `SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData`.
      Their proof scripts are the pure-Gram scripts with three substitutions:
      replace every `sourceMinkowskiGram` value by
      `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant`, replace every
      `sourcePermuteComplexGram` map by `sourcePermuteOrientedGram`, and
      replace the checked pure-Gram path field by
      `OS45Figure24OrientedPathField`.  The non-mechanical obligations are
      exactly the oriented identity principle, the oriented real uniqueness
      set from compact Wick pairings, and the source-oriented path field
      described above.

      Implementation-order correction for the oriented adjacent packet.  The
      theorem `os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`
      is a final orchestration theorem, not the first mathematical proof in
      the packet.  The compact OS-I equality first produces a real-patch
      seed, then the generic oriented corridor theorem propagates that seed
      across `Wscal`.  Production Lean must keep these as separate proof
      objects so the Figure-2-4 path theorem can still see the seed
      provenance.  In the production file order, the real-seed definitions
      and `*_of_realSeed` theorems below must appear before the public
      `*_of_compactWickPairingEq` wrappers displayed above; the wrappers are
      shown earlier only to record the public endpoint names.

      ```lean
      structure BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedRealSeed
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n F)
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart
              (d := d) n i hi hRep) where
        Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)
        Žä_eq : Žä = hChart.Žä
        realPatch : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ)
        realPatch_open : IsOpen realPatch
        realPatch_nonempty : realPatch.Nonempty
        realPatch_unique :
          BHW.sourceOrientedDistributionalUniquenessPatch d n realPatch
        Wseed : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
        Wseed_relOpen :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n Wseed
        Wseed_connected : IsConnected Wseed
        Wseed_nonempty : Wseed.Nonempty
        realPatch_mem_Wseed :
          ŌłĆ x Ōłł realPatch,
            BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n x Ōłł Wseed
        Wseed_subset_Wscal : Wseed ŌŖå hChart.Wscal
        Wseed_double :
          Wseed ŌŖå BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n Žä
        seed_eq :
          Set.EqOn
            (fun G => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žä G))
            hRep.Phi
            Wseed
        wick_mem :
          ŌłĆ x Ōłł BHW.os45Figure24SourcePatch (d := d) (n := n) i hi,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              ((BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
                (BHW.realEmbed
                  (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                    (d := d) (n := n)
                    (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x))) Ōłł hChart.Wscal

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_checkedRealPatch_of_anchor
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (hAnchor :
            BHW.SourceOrientedDistributionalAdjacentTubeAnchor
              (d := d) n i hi F)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch
            (d := d) n ŽĆ i hi (hAnchor.realPatch ŽĆ)

      theorem BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch.restrict_open
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {E0 E : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ)}
          (hPatch :
            BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch (d := d) n ŽĆ i hi E0)
          (hE_sub : E ŌŖå E0)
          (hE_open : IsOpen E)
          (hE_ne : E.Nonempty) :
          BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch (d := d) n ŽĆ i hi E := by
        refine
          { isOpen := hE_open
            nonempty := hE_ne
            permuted_jost := ?_ }
        intro y hy
        rcases hy with Ō¤©x, hx, rflŌ¤®
        exact hPatch.permuted_jost Ō¤©x, hE_sub hx, rflŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_checkedRealPatch_chartContact
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n F)
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart
              (d := d) n i hi hRep)
          (hAnchor :
            BHW.SourceOrientedDistributionalAdjacentTubeAnchor
              (d := d) n i hi F)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          Ōłā x0 Ōłł hAnchor.realPatch ŽĆ,
            BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (fun k => x0 (ŽĆ k)) Ōłł hChart.Wscal := by
        obtain Ō¤©x0, hx0Patch, u, hu, hreal_eqŌ¤® :=
          hAnchor.realPatch_commonEdge_contact ŽĆ
        refine Ō¤©x0, hx0Patch, ?_Ō¤®
        have hcommon := hChart.commonEdge_mem u hu
        simpa [hreal_eq] using hcommon

      theorem BHW.continuous_realSourcePermute_orientedInvariant
          (d n : Nat)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          Continuous
            (fun x : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ =>
              BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (fun k => x (ŽĆ k))) := by
        exact
          BHW.continuous_sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n |>.comp
            (BHW.realSourcePermuteHomeomorph d n ŽĆ).continuous

      theorem BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch.restrict_orientedOpen
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {E0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ)}
          (hPatch :
            BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch (d := d) n ŽĆ i hi E0)
          {W0 : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hW0_open : IsOpen W0)
          (hNonempty :
            (E0 Ōł®
              {x |
                BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                  (fun k => x (ŽĆ k)) Ōłł W0}).Nonempty) :
          BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch (d := d) n ŽĆ i hi
            (E0 Ōł®
              {x |
                BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                  (fun k => x (ŽĆ k)) Ōłł W0}) := by
        apply BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch.restrict_open
          (d := d) hd n ŽĆ i hi hPatch
        ┬Ę intro x hx
          exact hx.1
        ┬Ę exact hPatch.isOpen.inter
            (hW0_open.preimage
              (BHW.continuous_realSourcePermute_orientedInvariant d n ŽĆ))
        ┬Ę exact hNonempty

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_checkedRealPatch_shrink_into_orientedRelOpen
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (E0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ))
          (hPatch :
            BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch (d := d) n ŽĆ i hi E0)
          {W : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)}
          (hW_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n W)
          (hContact :
            Ōłā x0 Ōłł E0,
              BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (fun k => x0 (ŽĆ k)) Ōłł W) :
          Ōłā E1 : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ),
            E1 ŌŖå E0 Ōł¦
            IsOpen E1 Ōł¦
            E1.Nonempty Ōł¦
            BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch (d := d) n ŽĆ i hi E1 Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ y Ōłł {y | Ōłā x Ōłł E1, y = fun k => x (ŽĆ k)},
              BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n y Ōłł W) := by
        rcases hW_rel with Ō¤©W0, hW0_open, hW_eqŌ¤®
        rcases hContact with Ō¤©x0, hx0E0, hx0WŌ¤®
        let E1 : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ) :=
          E0 Ōł®
            {x |
              BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (fun k => x (ŽĆ k)) Ōłł W0}
        have hE1_sub : E1 ŌŖå E0 := by
          intro x hx
          exact hx.1
        have hE1_open : IsOpen E1 := by
          exact hPatch.isOpen.inter
            (hW0_open.preimage
              (BHW.continuous_realSourcePermute_orientedInvariant d n ŽĆ))
        have hx0W0 :
            BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (fun k => x0 (ŽĆ k)) Ōłł W0 := by
          have hx0W' :
              BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (fun k => x0 (ŽĆ k)) Ōłł
                  W0 Ōł® BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
            simpa [hW_eq] using hx0W
          exact hx0W'.1
        have hE1_ne : E1.Nonempty :=
          Ō¤©x0, hx0E0, hx0W0Ō¤®
        have hChecked1 :
            BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch (d := d) n ŽĆ i hi E1 :=
          BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch.restrict_orientedOpen
            (d := d) hd n ŽĆ i hi hPatch hW0_open hE1_ne
        refine Ō¤©E1, hE1_sub, hE1_open, hE1_ne, hChecked1, ?_Ō¤®
        intro y hy
        rcases hy with Ō¤©x, hxE1, rflŌ¤®
        rw [hW_eq]
        exact
          Ō¤©hxE1.2,
            BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_mem_variety d n
              (fun k => x (ŽĆ k))Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_realPatch_orientedRegularChartSubpatch
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n F)
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart
              (d := d) n i hi hRep)
          (hAnchor :
            BHW.SourceOrientedDistributionalAdjacentTubeAnchor
              (d := d) n i hi F)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          Ōłā E : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ),
            E ŌŖå hAnchor.realPatch ŽĆ Ōł¦
            IsOpen E Ōł¦
            E.Nonempty Ōł¦
            BHW.IsHWOrientedRealEnvironment d n
              {y | Ōłā x Ōłł E, y = fun k => x (ŽĆ k)} Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ y Ōłł {y | Ōłā x Ōłł E, y = fun k => x (ŽĆ k)},
              BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n y Ōłł
                hChart.Wscal) := by
        have hChecked :
            BHW.IsOS45Figure24CheckedRealPatch
              (d := d) n ŽĆ i hi (hAnchor.realPatch ŽĆ) :=
          BHW.os45Figure24_checkedRealPatch_of_anchor
            (d := d) hd n i hi hAnchor ŽĆ
        obtain Ō¤©x0, hx0Patch, hx0WŌ¤® :=
          BHW.os45Figure24_checkedRealPatch_chartContact
            (d := d) hd n i hi hRep hChart hAnchor ŽĆ
        obtain Ō¤©E1, hE1_sub, hE1_open, hE1_ne, hChecked1,
          hE1_WŌ¤® :=
          BHW.os45Figure24_checkedRealPatch_shrink_into_orientedRelOpen
            (d := d) hd n ŽĆ i hi (hAnchor.realPatch ŽĆ)
            hChecked hChart.Wscal_relOpen
            Ō¤©x0, hx0Patch, hx0WŌ¤®
        obtain Ō¤©E, hE_sub_E1, hE_open, hE_ne, hE_envŌ¤® :=
          BHW.os45Figure24_realPatch_orientedRegularSubpatch
            (d := d) hd n ŽĆ i hi E1 hChecked1
        refine
          Ō¤©E, subset_trans hE_sub_E1 hE1_sub, hE_open, hE_ne,
            hE_env, ?_Ō¤®
        intro y hy
        rcases hy with Ō¤©x, hx, rflŌ¤®
        exact hE1_W (fun k => x (ŽĆ k))
          Ō¤©x, hE_sub_E1 hx, rflŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.os45_realPatch_subset_anchorPatch
          (d n : Nat)
          {E0 E : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ)}
          (hE_sub : E ŌŖå E0)
          {ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)}
          {y : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (hy : y Ōłł {y | Ōłā x Ōłł E, y = fun k => x (ŽĆ k)}) :
          y Ōłł {y | Ōłā x Ōłł E0, y = fun k => x (ŽĆ k)} := by
        rcases hy with Ō¤©x, hxE, rflŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©x, hE_sub hxE, rflŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.os45_oriented_seedNeighborhood_from_realPatch
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n F)
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart
              (d := d) n i hi hRep)
          (hAnchor :
            BHW.SourceOrientedDistributionalAdjacentTubeAnchor
              (d := d) n i hi F)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (E : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ))
          (hE_sub : E ŌŖå hAnchor.realPatch ŽĆ)
          (hE_env :
            BHW.IsHWOrientedRealEnvironment d n
              {y | Ōłā x Ōłł E, y = fun k => x (ŽĆ k)})
          (hE_chart :
            ŌłĆ y Ōłł {y | Ōłā x Ōłł E, y = fun k => x (ŽĆ k)},
              BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n y Ōłł
                hChart.Wscal) :
          Ōłā Wseed : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n),
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceOrientedGramVariety d n Wseed Ōł¦
            IsConnected Wseed Ōł¦
            Wseed.Nonempty Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ y Ōłł {y | Ōłā x Ōłł E, y = fun k => x (ŽĆ k)},
              BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n y Ōłł Wseed) Ōł¦
            Wseed ŌŖå hChart.Wscal Ōł¦
            Wseed ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n hChart.Žä := by
        refine
          Ō¤©hChart.Wscal, hChart.Wscal_relOpen, hChart.Wscal_connected,
            ?_, hE_chart, subset_rfl, hChart.Wscal_doubleŌ¤®
        rcases hE_env.nonempty with Ō¤©y0, hy0Ō¤®
        exact
          Ō¤©BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n y0,
            hE_chart y0 hy0Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.sourceRealOrientedInvariant_left_mem_double_of_sectors
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (x : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ)
          (hleft :
            BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n ŽĆ)
          (hright :
            BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł
              BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n
                (ŽĆ * Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)) :
          BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
            (fun k => x (ŽĆ k)) Ōłł
            BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n
              (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)

      theorem BHW.sourceRealOrientedInvariant_adjacent_perm_eq
          (d n : Nat)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (x : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ) :
          BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
            (fun k => x ((ŽĆ * Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®) k)) =
          BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n
            (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)
            (BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (fun k => x (ŽĆ k)))

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedAdjacentTubeAnchor_of_compactWickPairingEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (hCompact :
            BHW.OS45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq
              (d := d) n i hi OS lgc) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedDistributionalAdjacentTubeAnchor
            (d := d) n i hi (bvt_F OS lgc n) := by
        exact
          { realPatch := hCompact.realPatch
            realPatch_open := hCompact.realPatch_open
            realPatch_nonempty := hCompact.realPatch_nonempty
            realPatch_jost := hCompact.realPatch_jost
            realPatch_left_sector := hCompact.realPatch_left_sector
            realPatch_right_sector := hCompact.realPatch_right_sector
            realPatch_commonEdge_contact :=
              hCompact.realPatch_commonEdge_contact
            oriented_left_mem := by
              intro ŽĆ x hx
              exact
                BHW.sourceRealOrientedInvariant_left_mem_double_of_sectors
                  (d := d) n i hi ŽĆ x
                  (hCompact.realPatch_left_sector ŽĆ x hx)
                  (hCompact.realPatch_right_sector ŽĆ x hx)
            oriented_right_eq_perm_left := by
              intro ŽĆ x hx
              exact
                BHW.sourceRealOrientedInvariant_adjacent_perm_eq
                  d n i hi ŽĆ x
            compact_branch_eq := hCompact.compact_branch_eq }

      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedRealSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart
              (d := d) n i hi hRep)
          (hCompact :
            BHW.OS45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq
              (d := d) n i hi OS lgc) :
          BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedRealSeed
            (d := d) n i hi hRep hChart := by
        let hAnchor :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedAdjacentTubeAnchor_of_compactWickPairingEq
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hCompact
        let ŽĆ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := 1
        obtain Ō¤©E, hE_sub, hE_open, hE_ne, hE_env,
          hE_chartŌ¤® :=
          BHW.os45Figure24_realPatch_orientedRegularChartSubpatch
            (d := d) hd n i hi hRep hChart hAnchor ŽĆ
        let EŽĆ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ) :=
          {y | Ōłā x Ōłł E, y = fun k => x (ŽĆ k)}
        have hUnique :
            BHW.sourceOrientedDistributionalUniquenessPatch d n EŽĆ :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedDistributionalUniquenessPatch_of_HWRealEnvironment
            d n hE_env
        obtain Ō¤©Wseed, hWseed_rel, hWseed_conn, hWseed_ne,
          hEŽĆ_mem, hWseed_sub, hWseed_doubleŌ¤® :=
          BHW.os45_oriented_seedNeighborhood_from_realPatch
            (d := d) hd n i hi hRep hChart hAnchor ŽĆ E hE_sub
            hE_env hE_chart
        have hF_holo_BHW :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
          simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
            bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
        have hF_cinv_BHW :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              bvt_F OS lgc n (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
              bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
          intro ╬ø z hz h╬øz
          exact
            bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
              (d := d) OS lgc n ╬ø z
              ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė hz)
              ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė h╬øz)
        have hPatchEq :
            ŌłĆ y Ōłł EŽĆ,
              hRep.Phi
                  (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n hChart.Žä
                    (BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n y)) =
                hRep.Phi
                  (BHW.sourceRealOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n y) := by
          intro y hy
          simpa [hChart.Žä_eq] using
            BHW.sourceOrientedScalarRepresentative_adjacent_seed_eq_on_patch
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              hF_holo_BHW hF_cinv_BHW
              hRep i hi hAnchor ŽĆ y
              (BHW.os45_realPatch_subset_anchorPatch
                (d := d) n hE_sub hy)
        have hWseed_subset_U : Wseed ŌŖå hRep.U := by
          intro G hG
          have hdouble :
              G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n hChart.Žä :=
            hWseed_double hG
          simpa [hRep.U_eq] using hdouble.1
        have hWseed_subset_permU :
            Wseed ŌŖå
              {G |
                BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n hChart.Žä G Ōłł hRep.U} := by
          intro G hG
          have hdouble :
              G Ōłł BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n hChart.Žä :=
            hWseed_double hG
          simpa [hRep.U_eq] using hdouble.2
        have h╬”_holo :
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n
              (fun G =>
                hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n hChart.Žä G))
              Wseed := by
          have hpre :
              BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n
                (fun G =>
                  hRep.Phi
                    (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n hChart.Žä G))
                {G |
                  BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n hChart.Žä G Ōłł
                    hRep.U} :=
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteOrientedGram
              (d := d) (n := n) hd hRep.Phi_holomorphic hChart.Žä
          exact
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen
              (d := d) (n := n) hpre hWseed_rel hWseed_subset_permU
        have h╬©_holo :
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n
              hRep.Phi Wseed :=
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen
            (d := d) (n := n) hRep.Phi_holomorphic
            hWseed_rel hWseed_subset_U
        have hSeedEq :
            Set.EqOn
              (fun G =>
                hRep.Phi
                  (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n hChart.Žä G))
              hRep.Phi
              Wseed := by
          exact
            hUnique.2 Wseed
              (fun G =>
                hRep.Phi
                  (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n hChart.Žä G))
              hRep.Phi
              hWseed_rel hWseed_conn hEŽĆ_mem
              h╬”_holo
              h╬©_holo
              hPatchEq
        exact
          { Žä := hChart.Žä
            Žä_eq := rfl
            realPatch := EŽĆ
            realPatch_open :=
              BHW.isOpen_realSourcePermuteImage d n ŽĆ hE_open
            realPatch_nonempty :=
              BHW.nonempty_realSourcePermuteImage d n ŽĆ hE_ne
            realPatch_unique := hUnique
            Wseed := Wseed
            Wseed_relOpen := hWseed_rel
            Wseed_connected := hWseed_conn
            Wseed_nonempty := hWseed_ne
            realPatch_mem_Wseed := hEŽĆ_mem
            Wseed_subset_Wscal := hWseed_sub
            Wseed_double := hWseed_double
            seed_eq := hSeedEq
            wick_mem := hChart.quarterTurn_mem }

      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSourceEq_of_realSeed
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n F)
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart
              (d := d) n i hi hRep)
          (hSeed :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedRealSeed
              (d := d) n i hi hRep hChart) :
          BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSourceEq
            (d := d) n i hi hRep hChart := by
        have hWscal_double :
            hChart.Wscal ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®) := by
          simpa [hChart.Žä_eq] using hChart.Wscal_double
        have hSeedEq :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            Set.EqOn
              (fun G => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žä G))
              hRep.Phi
              hSeed.Wseed := by
          simpa [hSeed.Žä_eq, hChart.Žä_eq] using hSeed.seed_eq
        exact
          BHW.os45AdjacentOrientedScalarEq_on_quarterTurnCorridor
            (d := d) (n := n) hd i hi hRep
            hSeed.Wseed hChart.Wscal
            hChart.Wscal_relOpen hChart.Wscal_connected
            hSeed.Wseed_relOpen hSeed.Wseed_nonempty
            hSeed.Wseed_subset_Wscal
            hWscal_double hSeedEq

      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarSeed_of_realSeed
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n F)
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart
              (d := d) n i hi hRep)
          (hRealSeed :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedRealSeed
              (d := d) n i hi hRep hChart) :
          BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarSeed
            (d := d) n i hi hRep := by
        refine
          { Žä := hChart.Žä
            Žä_eq := hChart.Žä_eq
            V := BHW.os45Figure24SourcePatch (d := d) (n := n) i hi
            V_open := BHW.isOpen_os45Figure24SourcePatch
              (d := d) (n := n) i hi
            V_nonempty := BHW.nonempty_os45Figure24SourcePatch
              (d := d) hd n i hi
            Wseed := hRealSeed.Wseed
            Wseed_relOpen := hRealSeed.Wseed_relOpen
            Wseed_nonempty := hRealSeed.Wseed_nonempty
            Wscal := hChart.Wscal
            Wscal_relOpen := hChart.Wscal_relOpen
            Wscal_connected := hChart.Wscal_connected
            Wseed_subset := hRealSeed.Wseed_subset_Wscal
            Wscal_double := by
              simpa [hChart.Žä_eq] using hChart.Wscal_double
            Wscal_component := by
              simpa [hChart.Žä_eq] using hChart.Wscal_component
            seed_eq := by
              simpa [hRealSeed.Žä_eq] using hRealSeed.seed_eq
            wick_mem := hRealSeed.wick_mem }
      ```

      The chart-compatible real-patch shrink is now explicit.  The raw
      anchor patch is first shown to contact the scalarization chart by
      `os45Figure24_checkedRealPatch_chartContact`; then
      `os45Figure24_checkedRealPatch_shrink_into_orientedRelOpen` restricts
      the checked real patch to the preimage of the relatively open
      `hChart.Wscal` while preserving the OS45 checked-patch data.
      Only after this restriction does
      `os45Figure24_realPatch_orientedRegularSubpatch` impose the
      determinant-regular oriented environment.  Consequently
      `os45_oriented_seedNeighborhood_from_realPatch` is no longer allowed to
      infer chart membership from raw anchor membership: it consumes the
      explicit `hE_chart` field and can choose `Wseed := hChart.Wscal`.  This
      is finite source-chart topology and checked Figure-2-4 real-patch
      geometry; it is not allowed to use local EOW, final locality, PET branch
      independence, or the already-proved final
      `OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSourceEq`.

      Deep Research route-risk audit.  Gemini Deep Research interaction
      `v1_ChczMGoxYWNQTEphYWlfdU1QeFlpRTJBSRIXMzBqMWFjUExKYWFpX3VNUHhZaUUyQUk`
      agreed with this theorem-shape gate: determinant-`1` proper complex
      Lorentz invariance plus holomorphy is not enough for full
      source-Gram descent in scalar rank `d + 1`; the determinant/pseudoscalar
      obstruction is real; and deriving
      `BHW.bvt_F_realOrthoChronousInvariant` from OS.E1 `SO(d+1)` and
      proper-complex invariance would silently add parity/improper
      invariance.  The audit therefore supports blocking production Lean on
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` until the route supplies a
      full-component source theorem, an oriented scalar-source API, or a
      paper-sourced proof that the obstruction vanishes for the specific OS
      scalar branch.  This audit is sanity-check evidence only; it is not a
      Lean proof and not an axiom approval.

      With this definition, the extended-tube improper equality has a real
      proof term rather than a wrapper.  If `z = ╬ōz ŌĆó p` and `w = ╬ōw ŌĆó q`
      are proper forward-tube preimages from the definition of
      `ExtendedTube`, and `w = A ŌĆó z` with `A` full-complex-Lorentz, then
      `q = ╬ōwŌü╗┬╣ A ╬ōz ŌĆó p` and the middle matrix is a full-complex-Lorentz
      element.  Since `p,q Ōłł ForwardTube`, `hF_full` applies to that full
      element and proves `F q = F p`; the two `extendF` values are then
      unfolded to those preimage values by `BHWCore.extendF_preimage_eq`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzInvariantOnExtendedTube_eq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hF_full :
            BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzInvariantOnForwardTube d n F)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz : z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (himproper :
            BHW.HWSameSourceGramImproperOrbitData d n z w) :
          BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w := by
        rcases hz with Ō¤©pz, hpz, ╬ōz, hz_eqŌ¤®
        rcases hw with Ō¤©qw, hqw, ╬ōw, hw_eqŌ¤®
        let A' : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d :=
          BHW.fullComplexLorentz_properInv_mul_full_mul_proper ╬ōw
            himproper.A ╬ōz
        have hA'_maps :
            BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzAction A' pz = qw :=
          BHW.fullComplexLorentz_preimage_map_eq
            (d := d) ╬ōw himproper.A ╬ōz pz qw hz_eq hw_eq
            himproper.maps
        have hF_qp : F qw = F pz := by
          simpa [hA'_maps] using hF_full A' pz hpz (hA'_maps.symm Ō¢Ė hqw)
        calc
          BHW.extendF F z = F pz := by
            exact BHW.extendF_eq_preimage_value
              (d := d) n F hF_cinv hpz ╬ōz hz_eq
          _ = F qw := hF_qp.symm
          _ = BHW.extendF F w := by
            exact (BHW.extendF_eq_preimage_value
              (d := d) n F hF_cinv hqw ╬ōw hw_eq).symm
      ```

      The helper names in the proof above are still proof-doc slots:
      `BHW.fullComplexLorentz_properInv_mul_full_mul_proper`,
      `BHW.fullComplexLorentz_preimage_map_eq`, and
      `BHW.extendF_eq_preimage_value`.  They are finite algebra/unfolding
      support, not new analytic assumptions.

      Equivalently, a future implementation may consume a sorry-free standard
      Hall-Wightman source theorem with exactly this full orthochronous
      invariant content.  What it may not do is apply
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension` as if every same-Gram full-rank
      frame lies in one `ComplexLorentzGroup d` orbit.  A rank-`d` frame has a
      one-dimensional orthogonal complement whose sign can repair the
      determinant; a rank-`d+1` full frame has no complement, so the determinant
      sign is fixed.  This keeps
      `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq` non-Lean-ready until the
      improper-generator source input is proved or imported as an exact
      ordinary Hall-Wightman boundary.

      Second, isolate the scalar-domain geometry:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Ōł¦
          IsConnected (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
      ```

      Mathematical content: this is Hall-Wightman's statement that the scalar
      image `S'_n` of the ordinary extended tube is a connected relatively open
      domain in the complex source Gram variety.  The connectedness half may
      use the checked local theorem `BHW.isConnected_extendedTube` and the
      continuous image under
      `(BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous`, because
      `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n` is exactly
      `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' BHW.ExtendedTube d n`.  The
      relative-openness half is not a continuity consequence; it is the BHW
      scalar-product neighborhood theorem (Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 in the
      local audit language), including the singular scalar-rank cases.

      Lean-shaped proof of the mechanical connectedness half:

      ```lean
      -- Requires importing
      -- `OSReconstruction.ComplexLieGroups.Connectedness.BHWPermutation.SourceComplexChart`
      -- for `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram`.
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected ... := by
        exact Ō¤©
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen (d := d) hd n,
          by
            have hGram_cont :
                Continuous (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n) :=
              (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous
            have hImage :
                IsConnected
                  ((BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n) ''
                    BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
              (BHW.isConnected_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)).image
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n) hGram_cont.continuousOn
            simpa [BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain] using hImageŌ¤®
      ```

      The only non-mechanical line in this theorem is therefore
      `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen`.  That line is
      the direct Lemma-3 relative-openness theorem described in the
      quantitative subpacket below; it may not be replaced by continuity of
      the Gram map or by connectedness of `ExtendedTube`.

      Third, perform the holomorphic descent through the source Gram map:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w) :
          Ōłā Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
            BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = BHW.extendF F w
      ```

      This is the actual descent theorem.  Its proof has two coupled parts.
      First, on `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n`, define the value by an
      ordinary extended-tube realization:
      if `Z = BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w`, set
      `Phi Z = BHW.extendF F w`; well-definedness is exactly
      `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq`.  Second, Hall-Wightman's
      scalar-product charts must provide an analytic-space holomorphic germ on
      the source Gram variety.  For each `Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n`,
      choose a realization `w0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n`, an ambient open
      neighborhood `U0` of `Z`, and a holomorphic scalar function on `U0`
      whose restriction to `U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n` agrees with
      the realized value above.  The chart-overlap compatibility is again the
      same-source-Gram branch law plus the Hall-Wightman identity/continuity
      argument on the Gram variety; it is not legitimate to infer equality of
      arbitrary ambient extensions off that variety.  Third, because the
      active `BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn` API asks only for equality
      on `U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, this local Hall-Wightman
      germ is exactly enough.  A `Classical.choice` of preimages with
      arbitrary values on the scalar domain is still not enough: the chosen
      value function must be locally represented by holomorphic scalar charts
      on the analytic variety.

      The descent proof uses one small, explicit compatibility helper.  It
      converts a local chart's `branch_eq` field into equality with the
      globally chosen scalar value `Phi` on the source Gram variety slice.
      This is not an overlap theorem and not an off-variety equality claim.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_eq_scalarValue
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          {Phi ╬© : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          {U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hPhi_branch :
            ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                BHW.extendF F w)
          (hU0_sub :
            U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (h╬©_branch :
            ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł U0 ->
                ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w) :
          Set.EqOn Phi ╬© (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
      ```

      Scratch-checked proof with `SourceExtension.lean` imported:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_eq_scalarValue ... := by
        intro Z hZ
        have hZU : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n :=
          hU0_sub hZ
        rcases hZU with Ō¤©w, hw, rflŌ¤®
        exact (hPhi_branch w hw).trans (h╬©_branch w hw hZ.1).symm
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the descent theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent ... := by
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch with
          Ō¤©Phi, hPhi_branch, hPhi_cont, hPhi_bddŌ¤®
        have hExceptionalAnalytic :
            BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z} :=
          BHW.hwSourceGramExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety (d := d) (n := n)
        have hMaxCharts :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
                  BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn Phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
          intro Z hZU hRank
          rcases BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at
              (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hZU hRank with
            Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub, h╬©_branchŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub,
            BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_eq_scalarValue
              (d := d) n F hPhi_branch hU0_sub h╬©_branchŌ¤®
        have hPhi_holo :
            BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) := by
          intro Z hZU
          by_cases hMax : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z
          ┬Ę rcases hMaxCharts Z hZU hMax with
              Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub, hEqŌ¤®
            exact Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hEq, hU0_subŌ¤®
          ┬Ę have hExc : BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z :=
              (BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                (d := d) (n := n)).2 hMax
            rcases BHW.hallWightman_removableScalarChart_at
                (d := d) hd n hPhi_cont hPhi_bdd
                hExceptionalAnalytic hMaxCharts hZU hExc with
              Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub, hEqŌ¤®
            exact Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hEq, hU0_subŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©Phi, hPhi_holo, hPhi_branchŌ¤®
      ```

      This proof is why the continuity/local-boundedness theorem is upstream
      of the exceptional chart.  The exceptional branch consumes the same
      `Phi` chosen by the branch law; it does not choose a new local value
      function and then try to glue it later.

      Fourth, assemble the data packet from the preceding pieces:

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hGeom :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Ōł¦
            IsConnected (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n))
          (hDesc :
            Ōłā Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
              BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi
                (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Ōł¦
              ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
                Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w) :
          BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData (d := d) n F
      ```

      This assembly is deliberately mechanical: `U` is exactly
      `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n`; `U_eq` is `rfl`;
      `U_relOpen` and `U_connected` are `hGeom`; `Phi`,
      `Phi_holomorphic`, and `branch_eq` are the descent output.

      The general Hall-Wightman source theorem is then only the composition of
      these four obligations:

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hF_full :
            BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzInvariantOnForwardTube d n F) :
          BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData (d := d) n F
      ```

      Conditional full-component specialization to the OS-II boundary-value
      function: `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` applies
      `BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData` to
      `F := bvt_F OS lgc n`, using the checked inputs
      `bvt_F_holomorphic` and
      `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` transported through
      `BHW_forwardTube_eq` to the `BHW.ForwardTube` convention, plus the
      still-missing source input
      `BHW.bvt_F_realOrthoChronousInvariant` used to construct the
      improper/full-complex-orthogonal invariant field.  Since that source
      input is not derivable from the current OS axiom surface, this
      specialization remains conditional and must not be ported to Lean on
      this branch unless the full-component decision is resolved.  If a later
      Lean port keeps
      the general Hall-Wightman input proposition-valued as an existence
      theorem, the extraction of the data object must still happen only after
      these same obligations, including the improper-component gate, are
      proved; it may not be hidden in a
      placeholder definition.

      Lean syntax note: data-valued surfaces in this block are
      `noncomputable def`s, not `theorem`s, because Lean rejects theorem
      declarations whose type is not a proposition.  If implementation chooses
      to keep a Hall-Wightman input as an existence theorem, it must have a
      propositional shape such as
      `Ōłā hRep : BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData (d := d) n F, True`, and
      any exported data term must be a `noncomputable def` obtained from that
      proved existence statement.

      This whole block is an external BHW/Hall-Wightman source theorem on the
      OS route.  It is not an axiom surface, not a locality theorem, not an EOW
      theorem, and not a substitute for the later adjacent branch-difference
      argument.

      Implementation gate: production Lean for
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` may start only after the
      Hall-Wightman scalar branch law, the `S'_n` relative-open/connected
      theorem, the variety-germ chart/gluing theorem, and the downstream
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData` consumers have been migrated to the
      germ predicate below, all with checked proof transcripts or an
      explicitly approved source-import boundary with these exact theorem
      statements.  The older domain-of-holomorphy/Siu/Cartan bridge below is
      archived only as an optional strong-API theorem if somebody later proves
      the extra SCV hypotheses independently; it is not a Hall-Wightman input
      and not a Lean-ready route gate.  Adding the theorem names as wrappers
      or production `sorry`s would not be progress on this route.

      In the current theorem-2 branch there is no approved production
      source-import boundary for this packet.  Therefore "source import" in
      this document is a proof-audit label only: before any Lean
      implementation may consume the packet, each named surface must either
      be a proved local theorem or be replaced by an already implemented,
      sorry-free local support theorem with the same mathematical content.
      It may not be introduced as a new `axiom`, as a theorem with `sorry` or
      `admit`, or as a data-valued `noncomputable def` whose proof obligation
      is hidden in a choice field.  A future user approval for a boundary
      would have to name the exact proposition-level statements below and
      still carry no theorem-2, locality, PET, EOW, or OS-specific content.

      Local production-code audit, 2026-05-01: the germ substrate has already
      been migrated in Lean, but the Hall-Wightman scalar representative has
      not.  `SourceExtension.lean` defines
      `BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn` and
      `BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData.Phi_holomorphic` already has the
      germ predicate.  The supporting source-variety continuation declarations
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle`,
      `BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram`, and
      `BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_differentiableOn_chart` are
      present in the local BHWPermutation infrastructure.  However, a search
      of `OSReconstruction/` finds no production declarations for the five
      ordinary scalar-source surfaces in this block:
      `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq`,
      `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`,
      `BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent`,
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw`, and
      `BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData`, and no
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`.  Thus the next Lean phase,
      when reached, must implement or import these exact source theorems; it
      may not treat the already checked germ/identity-principle infrastructure
      as the scalar representative itself.

      Lean-readiness decision for this checkpoint: the already checked
      substrate may be cited only as support for proof documents or for
      downstream consumers whose mathematical inputs are already proved.  It
      does not authorize production declarations named
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`,
      `BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData`,
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw`, or the adjacent
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrime...` producers.  The first production Lean target
      after this readiness gate must be the lowest dependency whose proof
      transcript is complete, for example a finite-dimensional
      Hall-Wightman support theorem or the generic OS-free Jost/Ruelle
      theorem, not a wrapper around the final scalar representative.

      Implementation-readiness ledger for this packet:

      ```lean
      /-- Proposition-valued ordinary Hall-Wightman source descent.  This is
      the only honest shape for a future source-import boundary for the
      ordinary scalar representative.  It is not active in the current branch;
      without explicit approval it must be proved locally through the
      component theorems below. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData_exists
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hF_full :
            BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzInvariantOnForwardTube d n F) :
          Ōłā hRep : BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData (d := d) n F, True
      ```

      This existence theorem would be only an approved import boundary for the
      **ordinary** Hall-Wightman source theorem.  It must not mention
      `OsterwalderSchraderAxioms`, Figure 2-4, EOW, PET, final locality,
      `bvt_W`, or the adjacent `S'_n` package.  If it is ever approved, the
      exported data object is then the mechanical choice

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData ... :=
        Classical.choose
          (BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData_exists
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hF_full)
      ```

      and the proof that this chosen object has the required fields is
      `Classical.choose_spec`.  In the current no-boundary route this theorem
      is replaced by the five explicit local obligations:
      `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq`,
      `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`,
      `BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent`,
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw`, and
      `BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData`.  The first three are
      genuine mathematical source theorems; the last two are assembly only
      after the first three have complete proofs.

      Lean must stop at this line unless the following audit questions all
      have affirmative answers in the blueprint:

      * Does the same-source-Gram branch law prove the high-rank Lorentz orbit
        theorem with the determinant orientation/improper-component split, and
        the low-rank singular contraction limit, using
        `BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv` and
        `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn` only after the Hall-Wightman geometric data
        are constructed?
      * Does Lemma 3 prove relative openness by local vector realization in
        `ExtendedTube d n`, rather than by openness of a continuous image?
      * Does the descent proof return the branch-defined scalar value `Phi`
        with variety-germ holomorphic charts, not a second glued ambient
        function and not equality off `sourceComplexGramVariety d n`?
      * Does the max-rank chart use the threshold `min (d + 1) n` and the
        invariant-PDE/power-series theorem, while the Lemma-2 orbit law uses
        the different threshold `min d n`?
      * Is the removable singularity step backed by
        `sourceComplexGramVariety_normal`,
        `sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank`, and
        `sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension`, with the symmetric
        determinantal normality packet proved or imported as genuine
        finite-dimensional SCV/algebraic geometry?
      * Is every future specialization to `bvt_F OS lgc n` only the transport
        of `bvt_F_holomorphic` and
        `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` across
        `BHW_forwardTube_eq`, plus a source-backed
        Hall-Wightman improper/space-inversion invariant input for full-rank
        same-Gram fibres, with no theorem-2 locality or adjacent branch
        information?

      Until all six answers are yes, `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`
      is not production-Lean-ready.

      Scalar-source implementation ledger.  This table is binding for the
      first future Lean pass; a theorem may move to production only when its
      row says "proved/local" or "approved source boundary", not merely
      because its name appears in the route.

      | Surface | Current status | First missing mathematical input |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq` | Conditional pure-Gram transcript pinned; not an active Lean target on the oriented route. | Hall-Wightman Lemma-2 branch law: high-rank span isometry/Witt extension with determinant orientation/improper-component split, and low-rank isotropic contraction limit, with continuity of `extendF` on `ExtendedTube`.  On the active route this is replaced by `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceOrientedInvariant_extendF_eq`. |
      | `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected` | Conditional pure-Gram transcript pinned; connectedness mechanical. | Relative openness consumes Lemma-3 local vector realization, including the adapted same-Gram representative and singular-rank residual-frame deletion.  On the active route this is replaced by `BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_relOpen_connected`. |
      | `BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent` | Conditional pure-Gram transcript pinned; not an active Lean target on the oriented route. | The germ API and mechanical glue are checked; Hall-Wightman max-rank scalar charts, branch-defined continuity/local boundedness, exceptional-rank removable chart, and compatibility of the branch-defined `Phi` supply the actual source proof.  On the active route this is replaced by `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent`. |
      | `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw` | Mechanically ready only after the preceding descent theorem exists. | No new mathematics; just `Classical.choose` from the proved descent existence and `hGeom`. |
      | `BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData` | Mechanically ready only after the first three rows exist. | Composition of branch law, scalar-domain geometry, and descent. |
      | `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` | Conditional only; not an active Lean target on the current strict proper-complex OS surface. | If the full-component route is sourced, transport `bvt_F_holomorphic` and `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` across `BHW_forwardTube_eq` and supply the Hall-Wightman space-inversion/improper invariant needed for full-rank scalar fibres.  If the oriented route is sourced instead, replace this pure-Gram surface by the oriented scalar-source API before migrating adjacent consumers. |

      This ledger separates real mathematical source work from assembly.
      The last three rows may not be implemented first as wrappers, because
      they would only hide the first three source obligations.  Conversely,
      the checked germ-support files (`SourceExtension.lean`,
      `SourceComplexDensity.lean`, and `SourceComplexLocalChart.lean`) may be
      consumed by the first three rows, but they do not themselves prove any
      row marked conditional or not an active Lean target.

      Local low-level source audit, 2026-05-01, updated 2026-05-02: the production
      `BHWPermutation` files contain substantial source-complex rank-variety
      and Schur-chart substrate, including `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety`,
      `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety`, local

      The rank-split and coefficient-map substrates are now checked in

      `BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank`, `BHW.sourceConfigGramMatrixRank`,

      `BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt`, `BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt`,
      `BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank`, `BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank`,
      `BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt`,
      `BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRankAt`, the rank complement/same-Gram
      transport lemmas, `BHW.sourceCoefficientEval`,
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap`,
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel`,
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_apply_sourceMinkowskiGram`,
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_single`,
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_zero_iff_eval_pair_eval_eq_zero`,
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_pair_eq_sum_gram`,
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_toLin_transpose`,
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_ker_le_gramKernel`,
      `BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap`,
      `BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical`, `BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank`, and
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_mem_restrictedMinkowskiRadical_iff`,
      `BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel_eq_eval_preimage_radical`,
      `BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap`,
      `BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_restrictedRank`, and
      `BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range`, plus
      `BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace`,
      `BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical_nontrivial_of_degenerate`,
      `BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank_lt_finrank_of_degenerate`,
      `BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientEval_le`,
      `BHW.finrank_restrictedSpan_le_d_of_degenerate`,
      `BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_orbitThreshold_of_range_degenerate`,
      `BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate`,
      `BHW.hw_highRank_eval_ker_eq_gramKernel`, and
      `BHW.hw_highRank_sourceCoefficientEval_ker_eq_of_sameSourceGram`, plus
      `BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometryOfKernelEq`,
      `BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometry_apply_eval`,
      `BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData`,
      `BHW.hw_highRank_spanIsometryData_of_sameSourceGram`, and
      `BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData_sourceGram_eq`.
      They do **not** currently contain production declarations named
      `BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup`,
      `BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzInvariantOnForwardTube`,
      `BHW.realFullLorentzAction`,
      `BHW.fullOrthochronousRealLorentz_preserves_forwardTube`,
      `BHW.fullComplexLorentz_properInv_mul_full_mul_proper`,
      `BHW.fullComplexLorentz_preimage_map_eq`,
      `BHW.extendF_eq_preimage_value`,
      `BHW.hallWightman_improperComponentInvariant_forwardTube`,
      `BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzInvariantOnExtendedTube_eq`,
      `BHW.bvt_F_realOrthoChronousInvariant`,
      `BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singularLimit_extendF_eq`,
      `BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative`,
      `BHW.hwLemma3_adapted_sourceGram_localVectorRealization`,
      `BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at`,
      `BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded`,
      `BHW.hwSourceGramExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety`,
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal`, or
      `BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension`.  Those names remain
      proof-doc theorem slots.  Any Lean pass that needs them must first
      create the genuine finite-dimensional Hall-Wightman/SCV mathematics
      behind them, or consume an already checked sorry-free theorem with the
      same content; it may not cite the existing rank-variety substrate as if
      these producer theorems already existed.

      Scratch-checked assembly after the analytic pieces are proved
      (with `SourceExtension.lean` imported):

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw ... := by
        rcases hGeom with Ō¤©hRelOpen, hConnŌ¤®
        let Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé := Classical.choose hDesc
        have hPhi_spec := Classical.choose_spec hDesc
        exact
          { U := BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n
            U_eq := rfl
            U_relOpen := hRelOpen
            U_connected := hConn
            Phi := Phi
            Phi_holomorphic := hPhi_spec.1
            branch_eq := hPhi_spec.2 }

      noncomputable def BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData ... := by
        have hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w := by
          intro z w hz hw hgram
          exact BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hF_full hz hw hgram
        have hGeom :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Ōł¦
            IsConnected (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected (d := d) hd n
        have hDesc :=
          BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch
        exact BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw
          (d := d) n F hGeom hDesc

      noncomputable def BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F ... := by
        have hF_holo_BHW :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
          simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
            bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
        have hF_cinv_BHW :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              bvt_F OS lgc n (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
                bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
          intro ╬ø z hz h╬øz
          exact bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
            (d := d) OS lgc n ╬ø z
            ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė hz)
            ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė h╬øz)
        have hF_full_BHW :
            BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzInvariantOnForwardTube d n
              (bvt_F OS lgc n) := by
          -- BLOCKER: this is the newly explicit improper-component source
          -- input.  It cannot be derived from determinant-`1`
          -- `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` alone.
          exact
            BHW.hallWightman_improperComponentInvariant_forwardTube
              (d := d) hd n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              hF_holo_BHW
              (BHW.bvt_F_realOrthoChronousInvariant OS lgc n)
        exact BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData
          (d := d) hd n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
          hF_holo_BHW hF_cinv_BHW hF_full_BHW
      ```

      The `Classical.choose` extraction in
      `sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw` is a Lean elimination
      necessity, not a hidden mathematical assumption: `hDesc` is a proved
      proposition-valued existence theorem, while the constructor returns the
      data type `SourceScalarRepresentativeData`.  A direct
      `rcases hDesc with Ō¤©Phi, ...Ō¤®` is rejected because a proof of `Exists`
      in `Prop` cannot be eliminated into a data-valued definition.

      Implementation-level source spine for this block:

      The proof source is Hall-Wightman, Section 1, Theorem I and Lemmas 1--7,
      plus the beginning of Section 2 for the topology of the scalar-product
      domain.  The Lean port must not compress those inputs into one opaque
      theorem.  The source theorem is complete only after the following
      proof-doc subpacket is either formalized or imported as one explicitly
      approved BHW source boundary with the same fields:

      Current readiness certificate for this block.  The public data constructor
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` is mathematically ready only
      when the proof documents contain, for every theorem named in the
      subpacket, all of the following items:

      * the exact Lean statement with the repo conventions for
        `ForwardTube`, `ExtendedTube`, `sourceMinkowskiGram`,
        `sourceComplexGramVariety`, and `ComplexLorentzGroup d`;
      * a proof transcript whose non-mechanical inputs are standard
        Hall-Wightman/BHW, finite-dimensional linear algebra, or SCV support
        theorems, and whose mechanical parts are reduced to local API names;
      * a provenance note identifying whether the theorem is proved locally,
        imported from an existing sorry-free support file, or still only a
        proof-doc obligation;
      * a negative dependency audit excluding global PET branch independence,
        theorem-2 locality, local EOW envelopes, pointwise permutation
        symmetry, and any downstream `bvt_W` construction.

      As of this pass, the first, second, and fourth bullets are largely
      pinned for the displayed theorem surfaces, but the third bullet still
      marks the Hall-Wightman branch law, Lemma-3 relative-openness theorem,
      max-rank scalar chart, scalar continuity/local-boundedness theorem, and
      normal analytic-space removable theorem as unimplemented proof-doc
      obligations.  This is the precise reason production Lean must still
      stop before `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`.

      Local source-text audit, 2026-05-01: the PDF
      `references/hall_wightman_invariant_analytic_functions_1957.pdf` was
      rechecked against this decomposition in the current session using the
      local extraction `/tmp/hw1957.txt`.  The source explicitly supports the
      route split used here: Lemma 1 gives complex Lorentz invariance and
      extended-tube single-valuedness; Lemma 2 is a rank-three/four orbit
      theorem plus rank-one/two limiting argument in four-vector notation;
      Lemma 3 is the scalar-product neighborhood theorem; Lemmas 4--7 give
      the infinitesimal equations, power-series scalar dependence, and
      non-exceptional scalar-product rank theorem; the final step invokes a
      removable-singularity theorem and records that the scalar image is open
      and connected in the scalar-product variety.  This audit confirms the
      theorem surfaces below, but it does not by itself close the Lean
      implementation gate: the dimension-general replacements and the normal
      analytic-space removable theorem still must be proved locally or
      accepted as an explicit source-import boundary.

      Source-to-Lean obligation matrix for the ordinary scalar representative.
      This table is the implementation-level expansion of "Hall-Wightman
      Theorem I" on the theorem-2 route.  A row marked proof-doc only is a
      mathematical gap, even if its theorem name already appears elsewhere in
      this blueprint.

      | Hall-Wightman source step | Lean-facing theorem/data surfaces | Readiness and exact remaining proof content |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Lemma 1: continuation to the extended tube and complex Lorentz invariance of the continuation. | Checked support surfaces: `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn`, `BHW.extendF_complex_lorentz_invariant`, and the direct `hF_cinv` bridge `BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv`. | Proper determinant-`1` bridge implemented in `ComplexInvariance/Extend.lean`: the proof unfolds `extendF`, chooses forward-tube preimages, and applies `BHW.extendF_preimage_eq_of_cinv`; it imports no PET, EOW, locality, final `bvt_W`, or scalar representative target. |
      | Lemma 2, high scalar-rank branch: same scalar products imply one complex Lorentz orbit. | Checked finite support: `BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt`, `BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData`, `BHW.hw_highRank_spanIsometryData_of_sameSourceGram`; orbit assembly transcript pinned for `BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit` and `BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit_or_improper`. | Coefficient quotient, common Gram kernel, restricted-span nondegeneracy, and span-isometry data are implemented in `BHWPermutation/SourceRank.lean`.  The full-complex group algebra, nondegenerate-complement Witt extension transcript, complex symmetric-form classification helper, full-frame determinant-ratio computation, orthogonal-complement nontriviality, nonisotropic-vector extraction, Householder determinant repair, and final vectorwise-to-configuration orbit conversion now have Lean-shaped checked transcripts.  No theorem-shape gap remains in the high-rank row; the full-rank pure-Gram case remains conditional on the full-component/improper input, while the active oriented fork proves determinant `1` before consuming the proper orbit theorem. |
      | Lemma 2, low scalar-rank branch: singular same-Gram points are limits of two orbit curves with a common endpoint. | Checked in `BHWPermutation/SourceHWSingularLimit.lean`, `SourceHWLowRankAlignment.lean`, `SourceHWCommonResidualFrame.lean`, `SourceHWMaximalIsotropicFrame.lean`, `SourceHWLowRankNormalForm.lean`, `SourceHWSeparateResidualFrame.lean`, and assembled in `SourceHWBranchLaw.lean` as `BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData`. | The production route no longer requires a compatible common residual maximal-isotropic frame.  From same Gram data, `HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrameData` and `HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment` produce the common selected base and residual families; `hw_lowRank_separateResidualFrameData` extends the left and right residual spans separately; `hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData_of_selectedAlignment` builds separate determinant-one null-boost orbit curves converging to the same selected base; and `hw_sameSourceGram_singularLimit_extendF_eq` gives endpoint equality. |
      | Lemma 3: scalar-product neighborhoods of the extended tube. | `BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative`, `BHW.hwLemma3_adapted_sourceGram_localVectorRealization`, `BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization`, `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen`. | Proof transcript pinned; production Lean not started.  The proof replaces an arbitrary realization by a same-Gram adapted realization, deletes residual isotropic-frame components in the singular case, and realizes all sufficiently near scalar-variety points inside `ExtendedTube d n`.  The connectedness half of `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected` is mechanical; relative openness consumes the Lemma-3 realization theorem. |
      | Lemma 4: infinitesimal complex-Lorentz invariant differential equations. | `BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator`, `BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent`, `BHW.SatisfiesLorentzInvariantPDE`, `BHW.hallWightman_lorentzInfinitesimalEquations`. | Proof transcript pinned; production Lean not started.  It differentiates the explicit complex Lorentz exponential curve at `0`, uses differentiability of `extendF` on the open extended tube, and uses extended-tube Lorentz invariance along that curve. |
      | Lemmas 5--7: invariant PDE solutions are scalar-product power series at non-exceptional points. | `BHW.sourceGramDifferential`, `BHW.sourceGramDifferential_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent_zero`, `BHW.sourceGramDifferential_kernel_eq_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent`, `BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_scalarDifferentials_span_PDE`, `BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_from_PDE_span`, `BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at`. | Proof transcript pinned; production Lean not started.  The implementation must prove the finite-dimensional kernel/rank theorem, the selected-row nondegenerate minor extraction, the auxiliary-coordinate split, and the power-series independence argument.  The rank here is the maximal scalar-chart rank `min (d + 1) n`, not the Lemma-2 orbit threshold `min d n`. |
      | Scalar value continuity/local boundedness before removability. | `BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded`. | Proof transcript pinned; production Lean not started.  This theorem defines the scalar value by a chosen extended-tube realization and uses Lemma 3 directly to transfer neighborhood control from `extendF`; it must not call the exceptional local chart theorem, because that theorem consumes continuity/local boundedness. |

      Consequence: the first future production Lean target cannot be
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` or any data-valued wrapper.
      It must be the lowest row of this matrix whose proof transcript is
      complete enough to port, or an explicitly approved proposition-valued
      source boundary with precisely the ordinary Hall-Wightman content listed
      here.

      Dimension and analytic-space audit for this source import:

      Hall-Wightman is printed for four-vectors.  The repo theorem surfaces
      are dimension-general with spacetime dimension `D = d + 1` and
      `hd : 2 <= d`.  The proof docs therefore cannot cite the printed
      `D = 4` theorem without also exposing the dimension-general linear
      algebra replacement.  Every occurrence of `4` in Lemmas 2, 3, 6, and 7
      is replaced by `D`; every occurrence of the direct-orbit threshold `3`
      is replaced by `D - 1 = d`; the maximal scalar-chart rank is
      `min D n = min (d + 1) n`.  The proof is the same Witt-extension,
      Schur-complement, and infinitesimal-invariant proof, but it must be
      recorded as a theorem about the repo's `ComplexLorentzGroup d`, not as
      an informal appeal to the four-dimensional paper.

      The second audit point is stronger: for `n > d + 1`, the scalar Gram
      variety has singular points.  Hall-Wightman's text proves ordinary
      power-series analyticity directly at non-exceptional points and
      continuity/local boundedness at singular points, then invokes the
      Bochner-Martin analytic-space notion.  This is a theorem about
      holomorphic germs on the scalar-product analytic variety, not a theorem
      that arbitrary local ambient extensions agree off the variety.

      This forces an API correction before production Lean.  The current
      production predicate `BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` is stronger than
      the Hall-Wightman output because it asks one total ambient function
      `Phi` to be holomorphic on full ambient neighborhoods.  That strong
      predicate can be recovered from the analytic-space germ only if a
      separate global ambient-extension theorem is supplied.  The local
      source audit of `references/hall_wightman_invariant_analytic_functions_1957.pdf`
      says `S'_n` is open and connected in the scalar-product variety, but it
      also says the set need not be the natural domain of analyticity for
      field-theory functions; therefore the proof docs must not assume, as a
      Hall-Wightman consequence, that `S'_n` is a Stein/domain-of-holomorphy
      subspace admitting global Cartan extension.  The active route is to
      refactor the source representative API to the honest analytic-space germ
      predicate below.  The Siu/Cartan ambient bridge remains documented only
      as an optional stronger support theorem if independently proved; it is
      not part of the current Lean-ready route.

      ```lean
      /-- Holomorphicity on the source Gram analytic variety.  A single value
      function `Phi` is stored on ambient scalar coordinates, but the local
      holomorphic representatives are required to agree with it only on the
      source Gram variety.  This is the Bochner-Martin/Hall-Wightman
      analytic-space notion and is strictly weaker than the old
      `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` predicate. -/
      def BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn
          (d n : Nat)
          (Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)) : Prop :=
        ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U ->
          Ōłā U0 ╬©,
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn Phi ╬©
              (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) Ōł¦
            U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn.to_germ
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn d n Phi U) :
          BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.continuousOn
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (hU_sub : U ŌŖå BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :
          ContinuousOn Phi U

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          {U V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
      ```
