          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (hV_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n V)
          (hV_sub : V ŌŖå U) :
          BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi V

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.sub
          {Phi Psi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (hPsi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Psi U) :
          BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n
            (fun Z => Phi Z - Psi Z) U

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n
            (fun Z => Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žā Z))
            {Z | BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žā Z Ōłł U}

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          {╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (hGramU :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' ╬® ŌŖå U) :
          DifferentiableOn Ōäé
            (fun z => Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)) ╬®

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_differentiableOn_chart
          (d n N : ŌäĢ)
          {Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          {D : Set (Fin N -> Ōäé)}
          {╬ō : (Fin N -> Ōäé) -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hPhi : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi U)
          (h╬ōU : ╬ō '' D ŌŖå U)
          (h╬ōvar :
            ╬ō '' D ŌŖå BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          (h╬ō_diff : DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬ō D) :
          DifferentiableOn Ōäé (fun q => Phi (╬ō q)) D

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle
          {U W : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          {H : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_conn : IsConnected U)
          (hW_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n W)
          (hW_ne : W.Nonempty)
          (hWU : W ŌŖå U)
          (hH_holo : BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n H U)
          (hH_zero : Set.EqOn H 0 W) :
          Set.EqOn H 0 U
      ```

      Production migration status: the field
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.Phi_holomorphic` now uses
      `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn`, not the old strong ambient predicate.
      Downstream identity-principle, uniqueness-set, and composition lemmas
      have been migrated to the germ predicate; this is not a wrapper refactor,
      because it removes an analytically false off-variety equality demand
      from the Hall-Wightman route.  The identity principle above is the
      analytic-space identity theorem on the connected normal source Gram
      variety: each local ambient representative vanishes on a nonempty
      relatively open subset of the regular locus, hence vanishes as a germ;
      overlap compatibility transports the zero germ across the connected
      relatively open domain.

      Local production-file locations are part of the implementation
      contract.  `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn`,
      `to_germ`, `continuousOn`, `of_subset_relOpen`, `sub`, and
      `precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram` are checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceExtension.lean`.  The pullback
      `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram` and the
      source-variety identity principle are checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean`.  The finite-coordinate
      chart pullback
      `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_differentiableOn_chart` is
      checked in `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexLocalChart.lean` and has the
      `Fin N -> Ōäé` signature displayed above; do not use an undocumented
      arbitrary-index variant in theorem-2 proofs.

      Lean-shaped proof of the basic germ support API:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn.to_germ ... := by
        intro Z hZU
        rcases hPhi Z hZU with
          Ō¤©U0, hU0_open, hZU0, hPhi_diff, hU0_subŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©U0, Phi, hU0_open, hZU0, hPhi_diff,
          (fun W hW => rfl), hU0_subŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.continuousOn ... := by
        rw [continuousOn_iff]
        intro Z hZU T hT_open hPhiZT
        rcases hPhi Z hZU with
          Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hEq, _hU0_subŌ¤®
        have hZvar : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := hU_sub hZU
        have h╬©ZT : ╬© Z Ōłł T := by
          simpa [hEq Ō¤©hZU0, hZvarŌ¤®] using hPhiZT
        rcases (continuousOn_iff.mp h╬©_diff.continuousOn)
            Z hZU0 T hT_open h╬©ZT with
          Ō¤©V, hV_open, hZV, hV_subŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©V Ōł® U0, hV_open.inter hU0_open, Ō¤©hZV, hZU0Ō¤®,
          by
            intro W hW
            have hWU : W Ōłł U := hW.2
            have hWvar : W Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := hU_sub hWU
            have hPhi_eq : Phi W = ╬© W := hEq Ō¤©hW.1.2, hWvarŌ¤®
            simpa [hPhi_eq] using hV_sub hW.1Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen ... := by
        intro Z hZV
        rcases hPhi Z (hV_sub hZV) with
          Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hEq, hU0_subŌ¤®
        rcases hV_rel with Ō¤©V0, hV0_open, hV_eqŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©U0 Ōł® V0, ╬©, hU0_open.inter hV0_open,
          by
            rw [hV_eq] at hZV
            exact Ō¤©hZU0, hZV.1Ō¤®,
          h╬©_diff.mono (by intro W hW; exact hW.1),
          by
            intro W hW
            exact hEq Ō¤©hW.1.1, hW.2Ō¤®,
          by
            intro W hW
            rw [hV_eq]
            exact Ō¤©hW.1.2, hW.2Ō¤®Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram ... := by
        intro z hz
        have hZU :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł U :=
          hGramU Ō¤©z, hz, rflŌ¤®
        rcases hPhi _ hZU with
          Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hEq, _hU0_subŌ¤®
        have h╬©_at :
            DifferentiableAt Ōäé ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) :=
          (h╬©_diff (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) hZU0).differentiableAt
            (hU0_open.mem_nhds hZU0)
        have hgram : Differentiable Ōäé (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n) :=
          (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).differentiable (by simp)
        have hcomp :
            DifferentiableWithinAt Ōäé
              (fun w => ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)) ╬® z :=
          (h╬©_at.comp z hgram.differentiableAt).differentiableWithinAt
        have heq :
            (fun w => Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)) =ßČĀ[­ØōØ[╬®] z]
              (fun w => ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)) := by
          have hGramU0_event :
              ŌłĆßČĀ y in ­ØōØ[╬®] z, BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y Ōłł U0 :=
            hgram.continuous.continuousAt.continuousWithinAt.preimage_mem_nhdsWithin
              (hU0_open.mem_nhds hZU0)
          filter_upwards [hGramU0_event] with y hyU0
          exact hEq Ō¤©hyU0, Ō¤©y, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®
        exact hcomp.congr_of_eventuallyEq_of_mem heq hz
      ```

      The proof uses the exact Mathlib congruence API
      `DifferentiableWithinAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq_of_mem`, as used in the
      checked `SourceComplexDensity.lean` proof.  The equality event is only
      on the within-filter `­ØōØ[╬®] z`, obtained by pulling back the open
      representative neighborhood `U0` along the continuous Gram map.  This
      is the crucial analytic-space point: all nearby Gram values are in the
      source Gram variety by the witness `Ō¤©w, rflŌ¤®`, so the local equality
      `hEq` is used only on `U0 Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety d n`; no equality
      of ambient representatives off the variety is assumed.

      Lean-facing standard-geometry inputs for these two audits:

      ```lean
      /-- Production pairing name.  The local source-complex infrastructure
      already defines the one-vector complex Minkowski pairing as
      `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner` in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexTangent.lean`, together with
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm`,
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_left`,
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_left`,
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_right`, and the nondegeneracy
      support lemmas.  Previous drafts used the shorthand
      `complexMinkowskiBilinear` for this same formula; production Lean should
      not introduce that as a duplicate API unless it is just a local notation
      inside a proof. -/

      /-- Nondegeneracy of the complexified Minkowski form restricted to a
      finite-dimensional subspace.  This is the precise hypothesis needed
      when the low-rank residual frame is placed in the orthogonal complement
      of the selected Hall-Wightman span. -/
      def BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) : Prop :=
        ŌłĆ x : M,
          (ŌłĆ y : M,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0) ->
          x = 0

      /-- Standard nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form after diagonalizing the
      complexified Minkowski form. -/
      def BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
          {D : Nat}
          (z w : Fin D -> Ōäé) : Ōäé :=
        Ōłæ ╬╝ : Fin D, z ╬╝ * w ╬╝

      /-- One-vector action underlying the existing configuration action
      `BHW.complexLorentzAction`. -/
      def BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction
          {d : Nat}
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun ╬╝ => Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1), ╬ø.val ╬╝ ╬Į * v ╬Į

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_apply_eq_vectorAction
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (i : Fin n) :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z i =
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (z i)

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction_add
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (u + v) =
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø u +
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø v

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction_smul
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (a : Ōäé) (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (fun ╬╝ => a * v ╬╝) =
            fun ╬╝ => a * BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø v ╬╝

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_add
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø (z + w) =
            BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z +
            BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø w

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_smul_vector
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (a : Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø (fun i ╬╝ => a i * q ╬╝) =
            fun i ╬╝ => a i * BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø q ╬╝

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_complexLorentzVectorAction
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø u)
            (BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø v) =
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d u v

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_cancel_left
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h : BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z =
                BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø w) :
          z = w

      /-- Existing local complex-linear change of coordinates identifying the
      complexified Minkowski bilinear form with the standard complex symmetric
      dot form.  This definition already lives in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexGlobalIdentity.lean`; the proof docs may
      call it the "orthogonal model", but production Lean should use this
      checked name instead of introducing a duplicate wrapper. -/
      def BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv
          (d : Nat) :
          (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_eq_dot_after_equiv
          (d : Nat) (z w : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d z w =
            BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d z)
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d w)

      /-- Dimension-general Witt extension for the nondegenerate complex
      symmetric form in spacetime dimension `d + 1`. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension
          (d : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M N : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (e : M ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] N)
          (he :
            ŌłĆ x y : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d x y =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (e x) (e y)) :
          Ōłā ╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ŌłĆ x : M,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
                (e x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 in dimension `d + 1`: near any realized
      source Gram point, every nearby point of the rank-bounded symmetric
      determinantal variety is realized by some ordinary extended-tube
      source tuple.  The proof first chooses an adapted same-Gram
      representative; it does not assert small perturbations around an
      arbitrary representative of the scalar point. -/
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          Ōłā O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen O Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Ōłł O Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł O Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              Ōłā z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
                z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z = Z

      /-- The source Gram variety is the existing repo symmetric
      determinantal variety of scalar matrices of rank at most `d + 1`,
      transported through the Minkowski-to-standard bilinear model.  The
      symmetry condition is part of the statement; replacing this by the bare
      `rank <= d + 1` subset of all matrices would be false. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n =
            BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1)

      /-- Rank-and-symmetry characterization of the same variety, matching the
      checked local API in `SourceComplexGlobalIdentity.lean`. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n =
            {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé |
              Z Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n Ōł¦
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j) <= d + 1}

      Active standard-geometry predicates used by the source-germ route:

      These declarations must live before the Hall-Wightman source descent
      theorem in the Lean port.  They are not QFT assumptions and they are not
      wrappers around the desired scalar representative.  They are the
      finite-dimensional SCV/algebraic-geometry vocabulary needed to state
      normality, analytic exceptional sets, and Riemann extension on the
      source Gram analytic space.

      ```lean
      /-- Relative openness inside an arbitrary finite-dimensional analytic
      subspace.  This must be polymorphic in the ambient type because the
      ordinary Gram space and the oriented Gram-plus-volume space are different
      finite-dimensional complex vector spaces. -/
      def BHW.IsRelOpenIn
          {E : Type*} [TopologicalSpace E]
          (V A : Set E) : Prop :=
        Ōłā ╬®0 : Set E, IsOpen ╬®0 Ōł¦ A = ╬®0 Ōł® V

      /-- Local boundedness on a finite-dimensional coordinate domain. -/
      def BHW.LocallyBoundedOn
          {E : Type*} [TopologicalSpace E]
          (f : E -> Ōäé)
          (U : Set E) : Prop :=
        ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł U ->
          Ōłā V : Set E,
            IsOpen V Ōł¦ x Ōłł V Ōł¦
            Ōłā C : ŌäØ, 0 <= C Ōł¦
              ŌłĆ y, y Ōłł V Ōł® U -> ŌĆ¢f yŌĆ¢ <= C

      /-- Analytic-subvariety predicate relative to an ambient analytic set.
      The concrete implementation may use the repo's eventual SCV sheaf API;
      mathematically the subset is locally cut out, inside `V`, by finitely
      many ambient holomorphic equations. -/
      def BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
          {E : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace Ōäé E]
          [CompleteSpace E] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
          (V A : Set E) : Prop :=
        A ŌŖå V Ōł¦
        ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
          Ōłā U0 : Set E,
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ x Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            Ōłā m : Nat, Ōłā g : Fin m -> E -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ a, DifferentiableOn Ōäé (g a) U0) Ōł¦
              A Ōł® U0 = {y | y Ōłł V Ōł® U0 Ōł¦ ŌłĆ a, g a y = 0}

      /-- Standard SCV predicate: `V` is a reduced closed analytic subvariety
      and every analytic local ring `­ØÆ¬_{V,Z}` is integrally closed.  The Lean
      implementation may encode this through the eventual analytic-local-ring
      API; it must not be a vacuous placeholder. -/
      def BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
          {E : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace Ōäé E]
          [CompleteSpace E] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
          (V : Set E) : Prop :=
        BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn (Set.univ : Set E) V Ōł¦
        ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.AnalyticLocalRingNormal V x

      /-- Standard commutative-algebra predicate for analytic local rings.
      This is used only to invoke Serre's criterion for the symmetric
      determinantal variety. -/
      def BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
          {E : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace Ōäé E]
          [CompleteSpace E] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
          (V : Set E) : Prop :=
        BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn (Set.univ : Set E) V Ōł¦
        ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.AnalyticLocalRingCohenMacaulay V x

      /-- Serre `R1`: the singular locus has codimension at least two, or
      equivalently `V` is regular in codimension one. -/
      def BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne
          {E : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace Ōäé E]
          [CompleteSpace E] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
          (V : Set E) : Prop :=
        BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn (Set.univ : Set E) V Ōł¦
        BHW.SingularLocusCodimAtLeast V 2
      ```

      The auxiliary names `AnalyticLocalRingNormal`,
      `AnalyticLocalRingCohenMacaulay`, `AnalyticLocalRingRegular`,
      `SingularLocusCodimAtLeast`, `ReducedAnalyticSubvariety`,
      `ComplexAnalyticDimension`, `AnalyticCodimensionIn`, and
      `AnalyticLocalProductModelAt` are intentionally standard
      finite-dimensional analytic/algebraic-geometry predicates, also
      polymorphic in the ambient complex vector space.  If the repo has no such API
      when the Lean port reaches this layer, it must add the genuine
      local-ring/stratification definitions or import them from a local SCV
      support file; replacing them by `True` or by the target normality
      theorem would violate the no-wrapper/no-placeholder rule.

      /-- Normality of the symmetric determinantal source Gram variety.  The
      proof is the standard finite-dimensional algebraic-geometry theorem:
      symmetric determinantal varieties over `Ōäé` are irreducible, reduced,
      Cohen-Macaulay, regular in codimension one, hence normal by Serre's
      criterion.  Locally, the Schur-complement charts reduce the rank-`<= r`
      statement to a smaller symmetric rank cone, giving the induction used
      in the Lean port. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- The maximal scalar-rank locus is dense in every relatively open
      piece of the source Gram variety.  This source-specific density input is
      needed by the normal-space removable theorem; the exceptional locus
      being analytic is not by itself enough for the extension argument. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
          U ŌŖå closure
            (U Ōł® {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z})

      /-- Symmetric determinantal normality reduced to the standard
      algebraic-geometry package.  These are not QFT inputs; they are the
      finite-dimensional commutative-algebra facts behind
      `sourceComplexGramVariety_normal`. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)

      The normality invocation is Lean-ready only after the following
      determinantal subpacket is present.  These names are still
      finite-dimensional analytic geometry, not BHW locality content; their
      purpose is to prevent `sourceComplexGramVariety_normal` from becoming a
      black-box assertion that hides singular-rank work.

      ```lean
      /-- The ordinary analytic singular locus of an analytic subvariety,
      defined by non-regularity of the analytic local ring. -/
      def BHW.AnalyticSingularLocus
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)) :
          Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :=
        {Z | Z Ōłł V Ōł¦
          ┬¼ BHW.AnalyticLocalRingRegular V Z}

      /-- Symmetric rank-bounded matrix cone on an arbitrary finite index
      type.  This avoids pretending that a Schur-complement block indexed by
      `q` is definitionally a `Fin (Fintype.card q)` source matrix.  Whenever
      a `Fin`-indexed version is needed, transport through a chosen
      equivalence `q Ōēā Fin (Fintype.card q)`. -/
      def BHW.symmetricRankLEMatrixSet
          (q : Type*) [Fintype q] [DecidableEq q]
          (D : Nat) : Set (Matrix q q Ōäé) :=
        {S | SßĄĆ = S Ōł¦ S.rank <= D}

      /-- Local Schur product model for the symmetric rank-bounded variety.
      Around a point whose selected principal block has size `r` and
      invertible determinant, the rank-`<= D` variety is analytically
      isomorphic to a smooth factor `(A,B)` times the smaller symmetric
      rank-`<= D-r` cone in the Schur-complement coordinate `S`.  The
      implementation uses the checked graph
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph` and its checked membership theorem
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_mem_rankLE_iff`; the theorem below is
      the genuine analytic-local-product packaging of those checked
      coordinate equalities. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_schurLocalProductModel
          (n D : Nat)
          {r q : Type*} [Fintype r] [Fintype q]
          [DecidableEq r] [DecidableEq q]
          (e : Fin n Ōēā r ŌŖĢ q)
          {Z0 : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hZ0_rank :
            Z0 Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum n (Fintype.card r))
          (hA0_unit :
            IsUnit
              ((((Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z0 i j).reindex e e)
                .toBlocksŌéüŌéü).det))
          (hrD : Fintype.card r <= D) :
          BHW.AnalyticLocalProductModelAt
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)
            Z0
            (BHW.symmetricRankLEMatrixSet q (D - Fintype.card r))
            (0 : Matrix q q Ōäé)

      /-- In the nontrivial range `0 < D < n`, the analytic singular locus of
      the symmetric rank-`<= D` variety is exactly the lower-rank variety
      rank `<= D - 1`.  For `D = 0` the variety is a point, and for
      `n <= D` it is the full symmetric vector space; those easy cases are
      handled separately by the normality proof. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_singularLocus_eq_lowerRank
          (n D : Nat) (hD0 : 0 < D) (hDlt : D < n) :
          BHW.AnalyticSingularLocus
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) =
            BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (D - 1)

      /-- Complex dimension of the rank-exact symmetric stratum.  This is the
      Hall-Wightman count `n*D - D*(D-1)/2`, proved through the selected
      Schur chart dimension theorem already documented for the global source
      identity principle. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum_complexDimension
          (n D : Nat) (hDle : D <= n) :
          BHW.ComplexAnalyticDimension
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum n D) =
            n * D - (D * (D - 1)) / 2

      /-- Codimension of the lower-rank singular locus inside the
      rank-`<= D` symmetric determinantal variety.  In the singular range
      `0 < D < n` this is `n - D + 1`, hence at least two. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim
          (n D : Nat) (hD0 : 0 < D) (hDlt : D < n) :
          BHW.AnalyticCodimensionIn
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (D - 1)) =
            n - D + 1

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim_ge_two
          (n D : Nat) (hD0 : 0 < D) (hDlt : D < n) :
          2 <=
            BHW.AnalyticCodimensionIn
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (D - 1))

      /-- Reduced Cohen-Macaulay theorem for symmetric determinantal
      varieties over `Ōäé`.  This may be implemented from the standard
      straightening-law/Eagon-Northcott/Jozefiak-Pragacz package, but it may
      not be replaced by a predicate that is definitionally true. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.ReducedAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) Ōł¦
            BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D)
      ```

      Normal/removable production-readiness gate.  The exceptional-rank
      Hall-Wightman chart may enter Lean only after the normal source-Gram
      analytic-space support has been proved in the following dependency
      order:

      ```lean
      -- 1. Identify the source Gram variety with the symmetric rank-bounded
      -- determinantal variety.  This is symmetry plus rank <= d+1 and the
      -- converse finite Gram realization, not a bare rank statement.
      have hVariety :
          BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n =
            BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1) :=
        BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety
          (d := d) (n := n)

      -- 2. Prove normality through the genuine symmetric determinantal
      -- package: Schur local product, singular locus, codimension, and
      -- reduced Cohen-Macaulay coordinate ring.
      have hNormal :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :=
        BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal d n

      -- 3. Prove density of the maximal scalar-rank locus in every relatively
      -- open source-variety patch.
      have hDense :
          U ŌŖå closure
            (U Ōł® {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z}) :=
        BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank
          (d := d) (n := n) hU_rel

      -- 4. Apply the normal-space Riemann theorem to the same branch-defined
      -- scalar value `phi`; do not choose a new value function at the
      -- exceptional locus.
      have hExt :
          ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U ->
            Ōłā U0 ╬©,
              IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
              DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
              U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U Ōł¦
              Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :=
        BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension
          (d := d) hd n hU_rel hphi_cont hphi_bdd
          hExceptionalAnalytic hDense hregular
      ```

      Step 2 is not a placeholder for "normality of the target".  It must
      contain `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_schurLocalProductModel`,
      `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_singularLocus_eq_lowerRank`,
      `sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum_complexDimension`,
      `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim_ge_two`,
      `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay`, and
      `normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre`, or consume an already implemented
      sorry-free theorem with exactly that finite-dimensional
      algebraic-geometry content.  Step 4 must consume the OS-free
      `normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension`/Riemann
      theorem and return the domain-control field
      `U0 Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U`.  None of this support may
      mention OS, Wightman fields, extended tubes beyond the source-domain
      input, EOW, PET, theorem-2 locality, `bvt_W`, or
      `sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`.  If any of those appear in the
      normality/removable proof term, the exceptional-rank chart is still not
      production-Lean-ready.

      Implementation-level algebraic source of the last theorem.  The Lean
      port may either prove this in the SCV/algebraic-geometry support layer
      or consume an already implemented sorry-free theorem with exactly this
      content; it may not introduce it as an axiom or as a theorem-specific
      placeholder.  Work in the polynomial ring whose variables are indexed by
      unordered pairs of source indices, build the generic symmetric matrix,
      and let `sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal n D` be the ideal generated by all
      `(D+1) x (D+1)` minors of that matrix.  The algebraic input is the
      standard symmetric determinantal theorem:

      ```lean
      def BHW.sourceSymmetricCoordIndex (n : Nat) : Type :=
        Sym2 (Fin n)

      def BHW.sourceGenericSymmetricMatrix
          (n : Nat) :
          Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n)
            (MvPolynomial (BHW.sourceSymmetricCoordIndex n) Ōäé)

      def BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal
          (n D : Nat) :
          Ideal (MvPolynomial (BHW.sourceSymmetricCoordIndex n) Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal_zeroLocus
          (n D : Nat) :
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé |
              Z Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n Ōł¦
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j) <= D}
            =
          BHW.zeroLocus
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal n D)

      /-- Standard Jozefiak-Pragacz/straightening-law theorem for the
      symmetric determinantal coordinate ring over an algebraically closed
      characteristic-zero field, specialized here to `Ōäé`. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLE_coordinateRing_prime_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal n D).IsPrime Ōł¦
          BHW.QuotientRingCohenMacaulay
            (MvPolynomial (BHW.sourceSymmetricCoordIndex n) Ōäé)
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal n D)

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.ReducedAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) Ōł¦
            BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) := by
        have hzero :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal_zeroLocus n D
        have halg :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLE_coordinateRing_prime_cohenMacaulay n D
        exact
          BHW.reduced_cohenMacaulay_analyticSubvariety_of_prime_CM_ideal
            hzero halg.1 halg.2
      ```

      The analytic translation theorem
      `reduced_cohenMacaulay_analyticSubvariety_of_prime_CM_ideal` is pure
      finite-dimensional SCV/commutative algebra: prime gives reduced
      analytic local rings, and the Cohen-Macaulay quotient-ring theorem
      localizes to all analytic local rings.  It carries no extended-tube,
      Wightman, OS, EOW, PET, or locality content.  If this coordinate-ring
      theorem is not available in local support, the normal-removability part
      of `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` is not Lean-ready.

      The Serre assembly is then mechanical and must stay visible:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) :=
        (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay n D).2

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) := by
        by_cases hD0 : D = 0
        ┬Ę exact BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_point_regularInCodimOne
            (n := n) (by simpa [hD0])
        by_cases hDfull : n <= D
        ┬Ę exact
            BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_fullSpace_regularInCodimOne
              (n := n) (D := D) hDfull
        have hD0' : 0 < D := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hD0
        have hDlt : D < n := Nat.lt_of_not_ge hDfull
        have hsing :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_singularLocus_eq_lowerRank
            n D hD0' hDlt
        have hcodim :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim_ge_two
            n D hD0' hDlt
        exact
          BHW.regularInCodimensionOne_of_singularLocus_codim_ge_two
            hsing hcodim

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
        have hCM :
            BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1)) :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_cohenMacaulay n (d + 1)
        have hR1 :
            BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1)) :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne n (d + 1)
        have hnormal :
            BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1)) :=
          BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre hCM hR1
        simpa [BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety]
          using hnormal
      ```

      theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre
          {n : Nat}
          {V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hCM : BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hR1 : BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne V) :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V

      Archived optional strong-ambient bridge:

      The following Stein/Siu/Cartan surfaces are **not** part of the active
      source-scalar route.  They document the additional SCV theorem package
      that would be needed only if the old production predicate
      `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` were kept unchanged.  The local
      Hall-Wightman audit does not prove that `S'_n` is a domain of
      holomorphy or that it admits a global Stein ambient neighborhood; it
      proves the analytic-space germ statement formalized above.  Therefore no
      theorem in this archived block may be moved to production as a wrapper
      around Hall-Wightman, and no implementation of
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` may depend on it unless the
      extra SCV hypotheses are independently proved and explicitly reopened.
      Gemini Deep Research interaction
      `v1_ChZIeHYwYWE5cng1Mzd4Z19ldGRDQURREhZIeHYwYWE5cng1Mzd4Z19ldGRDQURR`
      judged this stronger bridge mathematically sound if Step 1 is sourced
      not as a bare Hall-Wightman assertion, but as the additional
      Bochner-Martin/invariant-quotient theorem that the scalar image `S'_n`
      is a domain of holomorphy in the normal Gram analytic space (with the
      extended-tube/Stein perspective related to Zhou's theorem).  This
      validates the bridge as optional SCV support, not as an already
      Lean-ready consequence of the local Hall-Wightman source import.

      /-- A Stein open set in the finite ambient scalar-coordinate space.
      The Lean port should implement this as the chosen SCV API's Stein
      predicate; mathematically the fields are open, holomorphically
      separable, holomorphically convex on compact subsets, and equivalently
      the existence of a smooth strictly plurisubharmonic exhaustion. -/
      structure BHW.SteinOpen
          {n : Nat}
          (╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)) : Prop where
        isOpen : IsOpen ╬®
        holomorphically_separable :
          ŌłĆ Z Ōłł ╬®, ŌłĆ W Ōłł ╬®, Z ŌēĀ W ->
            Ōłā f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
              DifferentiableOn Ōäé f ╬® Ōł¦ f Z ŌēĀ f W
        holomorphically_convex :
          ŌłĆ K : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsCompact K -> K ŌŖå ╬® ->
              Ōłā L : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
                IsCompact L Ōł¦ L ŌŖå ╬® Ōł¦ K ŌŖå L Ōł¦
                ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł ╬® -> Z Ōłē L ->
                  Ōłā f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
                    DifferentiableOn Ōäé f ╬® Ōł¦
                    (ŌłĆ W, W Ōłł K -> ŌĆ¢f WŌĆ¢ <= 1) Ōł¦
                    2 <= ŌĆ¢f ZŌĆ¢

      /-- Closed analytic subvariety in the finite source-Gram coordinate
      space, stated only at the level needed by the BHW source route. -/
      structure BHW.ClosedAnalyticSubvariety
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)) : Prop where
        isClosed : IsClosed V
        locally_cut_out :
          ŌłĆ Z, Ōłā U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            Ōłā m : Nat, Ōłā g : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ a, DifferentiableOn Ōäé (g a) U0) Ōł¦
              V Ōł® U0 = {W | W Ōłł U0 Ōł¦ ŌłĆ a, g a W = 0}

      /-- Coherent ideal sheaf cutting out a closed analytic subvariety.  The
      Lean port can replace this with the eventual SCV sheaf API, but the
      theorem-2 route must keep the finite local generator condition visible. -/
      structure BHW.CoherentIdealSheaf
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)) : Prop where
        local_finite_presentation :
          ŌłĆ Z, Ōłā U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            Ōłā m r : Nat,
            Ōłā g : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
            Ōłā a : Fin r -> Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ i, DifferentiableOn Ōäé (g i) U0) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ b i, DifferentiableOn Ōäé (a b i) U0) Ōł¦
              V Ōł® U0 = {W | W Ōłł U0 Ōł¦ ŌłĆ i, g i W = 0} Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ b W, W Ōłł U0 ->
                Ōłæ i : Fin m, a b i W * g i W = 0) Ōł¦
              ŌłĆ h : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ i, DifferentiableOn Ōäé (h i) U0) ->
                (ŌłĆ W, W Ōłł U0 ->
                  Ōłæ i : Fin m, h i W * g i W = 0) ->
                ŌłĆ P, P Ōłł U0 ->
                  Ōłā V0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
                    IsOpen V0 Ōł¦ P Ōłł V0 Ōł¦ V0 ŌŖå U0 Ōł¦
                    Ōłā c : Fin r -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
                      (ŌłĆ b, DifferentiableOn Ōäé (c b) V0) Ōł¦
                      ŌłĆ i, Set.EqOn (h i)
                        (fun W => Ōłæ b : Fin r, c b W * a b i W) V0

      /-- Stein analytic subspace structure on a relatively open subset of a
      closed analytic subvariety.  The fields deliberately name the
      analytic-space Stein theorem rather than pretending that relative
      openness alone gives a Stein ambient set. -/
      structure BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
          {n : Nat}
          (V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)) : Prop where
        subset : U ŌŖå V
        relOpen : Ōłā ╬®0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé), IsOpen ╬®0 Ōł¦ U = ╬®0 Ōł® V
        holomorphically_separable :
          ŌłĆ Z Ōłł U, ŌłĆ W Ōłł U, Z ŌēĀ W ->
            Ōłā f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ P, P Ōłł U ->
                Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                  IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ P Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                  DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                  Set.EqOn f ╬© (U0 Ōł® V)) Ōł¦
              f Z ŌēĀ f W
        holomorphically_convex :
          ŌłĆ K : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsCompact K -> K ŌŖå U ->
              Ōłā L : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
                IsCompact L Ōł¦ L ŌŖå U Ōł¦ K ŌŖå L Ōł¦
                ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U -> Z Ōłē L ->
                  Ōłā f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
                    (ŌłĆ P, P Ōłł U ->
                      Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                        IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ P Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                        DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                        Set.EqOn f ╬© (U0 Ōł® V)) Ōł¦
                    (ŌłĆ W, W Ōłł K -> ŌĆ¢f WŌĆ¢ <= 1) Ōł¦
                    2 <= ŌĆ¢f ZŌĆ¢

      The generic relative-open predicate `BHW.IsRelOpenIn` is declared in
      the active standard-geometry block above, before the normal Riemann
      theorem.  The archived Stein/Cartan bridge reuses that same predicate;
      it must not redeclare a second incompatible relative-openness notion.

      /-- A scalar-space holomorphic function analytically continues across a
      boundary point of a relatively open analytic-space domain if it has an
      ambient holomorphic representative on a neighborhood of that boundary
      point which agrees with the original germ on a nonempty relatively open
      piece of the old domain. -/
      structure BHW.AnalyticallyContinuesAcross
          {n : Nat}
          (V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé))
          (f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) : Prop where
        ╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
        ╬© : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé
        ╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®
        center_mem : Z Ōłł ╬® Ōł® V
        ╬©_hol : DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© ╬®
        agreement_set : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
        agreement_relOpen : BHW.IsRelOpenIn V agreement_set
        agreement_nonempty : agreement_set.Nonempty
        agreement_subset : agreement_set ŌŖå U Ōł® ╬® Ōł® V
        agreement : Set.EqOn f ╬© agreement_set

      /-- Domain-of-holomorphy predicate for a relatively open subset of the
      source Gram analytic space.  In this blueprint it is archived support
      for the optional strong ambient API only.  It is deliberately not
      recorded as a Hall-Wightman consequence: the paper gives openness and
      connectedness of `S'_n` and the invariant analytic germs, while also
      warning that this set need not be the natural domain of analyticity for
      field-theory functions. -/
      structure BHW.DomainOfHolomorphyIn
          {n : Nat}
          (V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)) : Prop where
        subset : U ŌŖå V
        relOpen : Ōłā ╬®0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé), IsOpen ╬®0 Ōł¦ U = ╬®0 Ōł® V
        maximal_for_scalar_holomorphic_functions :
          ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł frontier U Ōł® V ->
            Ōłā f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ P, P Ōłł U ->
                Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                  IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ P Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                  DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                  Set.EqOn f ╬© (U0 Ōł® V)) Ōł¦
              ┬¼ BHW.AnalyticallyContinuesAcross V U f Z

      /-- Closed analytic-subspace structure of the source Gram variety.  This
      packages the already checked closedness and the polynomial defining
      equations: symmetry equations `Z i j - Z j i = 0` plus the
      `(d + 2) x (d + 2)` minors in the strict rank-bounded case. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.ClosedAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Optional strong-API theorem, not active: if one independently proves
      that `S'_n` is a domain of holomorphy in the source Gram analytic space,
      then the Stein/Cartan bridge below can be used to recover the old total
      ambient-function API.  This statement is not currently justified as a
      Hall-Wightman source import. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_domainOfHolomorphy
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.DomainOfHolomorphyIn
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      /-- Cartan-Thullen/Bochner-Martin theorem on normal analytic spaces:
      a domain of holomorphy in a finite-dimensional normal analytic space is
      Stein. -/
      theorem BHW.domainOfHolomorphy_steinAnalyticSubspace
          {n : Nat}
          {V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hV_normal : BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hU_domain : BHW.DomainOfHolomorphyIn V U) :
          BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace V U

      /-- Optional strong-API consequence, not active: if the independent
      domain-of-holomorphy theorem above is proved, then the scalar image is
      Stein as an open analytic subspace of the closed source Gram variety.
      The active germ route does not use this theorem. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) := by
        exact BHW.domainOfHolomorphy_steinAnalyticSubspace
          (V := BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          (U := BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal d n)
          (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_domainOfHolomorphy
            (d := d) hd n)
      ```

      /-- Siu's Stein-neighborhood theorem in the finite ambient scalar space:
      a Stein analytic subspace of a complex space admits an open Stein
      neighborhood realizing it by intersection with the ambient closed
      analytic subvariety. -/
      theorem BHW.siu_steinNeighborhood_sourceGramSubspace
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_stein :
            BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U) :
          Ōłā ╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen ╬® Ōł¦ BHW.SteinOpen ╬® Ōł¦
            U = ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n

      /-- Optional Stein-neighborhood support for the source Gram domain.
      This is needed only for the archived total ambient-function API. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAmbientNeighborhood
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          Ōłā ╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen ╬® Ōł¦ BHW.SteinOpen ╬® Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n =
              ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n

      /-- Coherence of the ideal sheaf of the source Gram variety.  In this
      concrete finite-dimensional setting the generators are the symmetry
      equations and the Gram minors, so coherence is the Oka coherence theorem
      for finitely generated ideals of holomorphic functions. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramIdealSheaf_coherent
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.CoherentIdealSheaf
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Generic Cartan-B restriction theorem in the exact shape consumed
      below: for a coherent ideal sheaf cutting out a closed analytic
      subvariety `V` inside a Stein open set `╬®`, every holomorphic section of
      the structure sheaf on `╬® Ōł® V` is the restriction of a holomorphic
      function on `╬®`. -/
      theorem BHW.cartanB_restriction_surjective_for_coherentIdeal
          {n : Nat}
          {╬® V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hV_closedAnalytic : BHW.ClosedAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hV_ideal : BHW.CoherentIdealSheaf V)
          (h╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®)
          (h╬®_stein : BHW.SteinOpen ╬®)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hphi_germ :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł ╬® Ōł® V ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦ U0 ŌŖå ╬® Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬© (U0 Ōł® V)) :
          Ōłā Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé Phi ╬® Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn Phi phi (╬® Ōł® V)

      /-- Cartan theorem B / Oka extension in the form used by the source Gram
      variety: restriction from holomorphic functions on a Stein ambient
      neighborhood to holomorphic sections on the closed analytic subvariety is
      surjective.  The ideal sheaf is coherent because the source Gram variety
      is cut out by finitely many Gram minors. -/
      theorem BHW.cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (h╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®)
          (h╬®_stein : BHW.SteinOpen ╬®)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hphi_germ :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦ U0 ŌŖå ╬® Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)) :
          Ōłā Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé Phi ╬® Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn Phi phi
              (╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Archived Cartan/Oka extension bridge forced only by the old
      production API `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn`.  A holomorphic scalar germ
      on the closed
      source Gram analytic subspace, given by local ambient representatives
      that agree as germs on the variety, can be represented by one total
      ambient function `Phi` near the relatively open scalar domain.  The proof
      uses a Stein neighborhood of the source Gram domain in the finite ambient
      scalar-coordinate space and Cartan theorem B for the ideal sheaf of the
      closed analytic subvariety.  This is not part of the current
      Hall-Wightman route unless the Stein-neighborhood input is separately
      proved. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceVariety_holomorphicGerm_cartanAmbientExtension
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_stein :
            Ōłā ╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
              IsOpen ╬® Ōł¦ BHW.SteinOpen ╬® Ōł¦
              U = ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hlocal :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U) :
          Ōłā Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
            BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn d n Phi U Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn Phi phi U

      Archived Lean-shaped proof of the optional Stein/Cartan bridge:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAmbientNeighborhood
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) := by
        have hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected
            (d := d) hd n).1
        have hU_stein :
            BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace
            (d := d) hd n
        exact BHW.siu_steinNeighborhood_sourceGramSubspace
          (d := d) (n := n) hU_rel hU_stein

      theorem BHW.cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety ... := by
        -- Let `V := sourceComplexGramVariety d n`.
        -- `sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic` gives the coherent ideal
        -- sheaf `I_V` generated by the symmetry equations and Gram minors.
        -- The sequence `0 -> I_V -> O_╬® -> O_{╬® Ōł® V} -> 0` is exact.
        -- Since `╬®` is Stein, Cartan theorem B gives `H^1(╬®, I_V) = 0`;
        -- hence global sections of `O_╬®` surject onto global sections of
        -- `O_{╬® Ōł® V}`.  The local data `hphi_germ` is exactly the section of
        -- `O_{╬® Ōł® V}` represented by `phi`, so choose its ambient lift
        -- `Phi╬®`.  Extend `Phi╬®` arbitrarily outside `╬®`; the conclusion only
        -- asks for `DifferentiableOn Ōäé Phi ╬®` and equality on `╬® Ōł® V`.
        exact BHW.cartanB_restriction_surjective_for_coherentIdeal
          (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic d n)
          (BHW.sourceGramIdealSheaf_coherent d n)
          h╬®_open h╬®_stein hphi_germ

      theorem BHW.sourceVariety_holomorphicGerm_cartanAmbientExtension
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_stein :
            Ōłā ╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
              IsOpen ╬® Ōł¦ BHW.SteinOpen ╬® Ōł¦
              U = ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hlocal :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U) := by
        rcases hU_stein with Ō¤©╬®, h╬®_open, h╬®_stein, hU_eqŌ¤®
        have hphi_germ :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦ U0 ŌŖå ╬® Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
          intro Z hZ
          have hZU : Z Ōłł U := by simpa [hU_eq] using hZ
          rcases hlocal Z hZU with
            Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hEq, hU0_subŌ¤®
          refine Ō¤©U0 Ōł® ╬®, ╬©, hU0_open.inter h╬®_open,
            Ō¤©hZU0, hZ.1Ō¤®, ?_, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
          ┬Ę exact fun W hW => hW.2
          ┬Ę exact h╬©_diff.mono (by intro W hW; exact hW.1)
          ┬Ę intro W hW
            exact hEq Ō¤©hW.1.1, hW.2Ō¤®
        rcases BHW.cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety
            (d := d) (n := n) h╬®_open h╬®_stein hphi_germ with
          Ō¤©Phi, hPhi_diff, hPhi_eq_phiŌ¤®
        refine Ō¤©Phi, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
        ┬Ę intro Z hZU
          have hZ╬®V : Z Ōłł ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
            simpa [hU_eq] using hZU
          refine Ō¤©╬®, h╬®_open, hZ╬®V.1, hPhi_diff, ?_Ō¤®
          intro W hW
          simpa [hU_eq] using hW
        ┬Ę intro Z hZU
          have hZ╬®V : Z Ōłł ╬® Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
            simpa [hU_eq] using hZU
          exact hPhi_eq_phi hZ╬®V
      ```

      /-- Riemann extension on the normal source Gram variety, in exactly the
      form needed by Hall-Wightman's Bochner-Martin conclusion.  A scalar
      germ which is ambient holomorphic on the maximal-rank regular locus and
      continuous or locally bounded on the whole `S'_n` domain has a strong
      local ambient holomorphic representative at every point.  Implement
      `BHW.LocallyBoundedOn` before this theorem; its exact definition is
      displayed again in the chart packet below. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
          (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
          (hExceptionalAnalytic :
            BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z})
          (hMaxDense :
            U ŌŖå closure
              (U Ōł® {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z}))
          (hregular :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)) :
          ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U ->
            Ōłā U0 ╬©,
              IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
              DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
              U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U Ōł¦
              Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Generic normal analytic-space Riemann extension theorem in a
      finite-dimensional complex ambient vector space.  This is the SCV theorem
      consumed by both the ordinary Gram route and the oriented Gram-plus-volume
      route.  It is allowed as a standard complex-analytic support theorem only;
      it carries no QFT or locality content. -/
      theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension
          {E : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace Ōäé E]
          [CompleteSpace E] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
          {V U A : Set E}
          {phi : E -> Ōäé}
          (hV_normal : BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenIn V U)
          (hA_analytic : BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn V A)
          (hDense : U ŌŖå closure (U \ A))
          (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
          (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
          (hregular :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł U \ A ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ x Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® V ŌŖå U Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬© (U0 Ōł® V)) :
          ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł U ->
            Ōłā U0 ╬©,
              IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ x Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
              DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
              U0 Ōł® V ŌŖå U Ōł¦
              Set.EqOn phi ╬© (U0 Ōł® V)

      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.to_isRelOpenIn
          {d n : Nat}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hU : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenIn (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U
      ```

      Proof transcript for these standard-geometry inputs:

      1. The finite coordinate change diagonalizing the complexified
         Minkowski form is the already-implemented
         `BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv`; its preservation theorem is
         the already-implemented
         `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_eq_dot_after_equiv`.  Over `Ōäé`,
         signs are removed by multiplying the time coordinate by `I`; the
         linear equivalence is explicit in coordinates and the preservation
         theorem is a direct finite sum calculation.  Production Lean should
         not introduce a second "orthogonal model" definition for the same
         map.
      2. `complexMinkowski_wittExtension` is Witt extension for a
         nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form over an algebraically closed
         field of characteristic not two, transported back through the model
         above.  In Lean this can be proved by a Gram-Schmidt/Witt basis
         construction: split off hyperbolic pairs for the radical, extend an
         isometry on the nondegenerate quotient, then add matching orthogonal
         complements.
         Local audit: Mathlib/local search does not expose a ready theorem
         named `Witt`, `wittExtension`, or `extend_isometry` for bilinear
         forms.  Therefore this is a real ordinary finite-dimensional
         support theorem, not an import-name lookup.

         The production theorem must be the general subspace-isometry form
         first:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_wittExtension
             {D : Nat}
             {M N : Submodule Ōäé (Fin D -> Ōäé)}
             (T : M ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] N)
             (hT :
               ŌłĆ x y : M,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   ((T x : N) : Fin D -> Ōäé)
                   ((T y : N) : Fin D -> Ōäé) =
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (x : Fin D -> Ōäé) (y : Fin D -> Ōäé)) :
             Ōłā A : (Fin D -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin D -> Ōäé),
               (ŌłĆ u v,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear (A u) (A v) =
                   BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear u v) Ōł¦
               ŌłĆ x : M, A (x : Fin D -> Ōäé) = (T x : Fin D -> Ōäé)
         ```

         Its proof is not a one-theorem import.  The Lean port should first
         expose the standard form as a `LinearMap.BilinForm` so Mathlib's
         orthogonal-complement API can be used directly:

         ```lean
         def BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm (D : Nat) :
             LinearMap.BilinForm Ōäé (Fin D -> Ōäé)

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_apply
             (D : Nat) (u v : Fin D -> Ōäé) :
             BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D u v =
               BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear u v

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_isRefl
             (D : Nat) :
             (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).IsRefl

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_isSymm
             (D : Nat) :
             (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).IsSymm

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_nondegenerate
             (D : Nat) :
             (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).Nondegenerate

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_dualVectorOfLinearFunctional
             (D : Nat)
             (ell : (Fin D -> Ōäé) ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé)
             (v : Fin D -> Ōäé) :
             BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
               (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricDualVectorOfLinearFunctional D ell)
               v = ell v
         ```

         The nondegeneracy and dual-vector proofs are finite coordinate
         calculations against `Pi.basisFun`, exactly parallel to the checked
         source-Minkowski theorem
         `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_dualVectorOfLinearFunctional`.
         The Mathlib APIs pinned for the proof are
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.orthogonal`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.mem_orthogonal_iff`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.isCompl_span_singleton_orthogonal`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_orthogonal_spanSingleton`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.toDual`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.apply_toDual_symm_apply`,
         `Submodule.exists_isCompl`, `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl`,
         `Submodule.linearProjOfIsCompl`, `LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton`,
         `LinearMap.ofIsCompl`, and `LinearMap.exists_extend`.
         Local API audit, 2026-05-01: projection and complement maps should be
         used in method form where possible:
         `(Ōäé ŌłÖ u).linearProjOfIsCompl C hC`,
         `Submodule.linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_left hC`,
         `Submodule.linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_right hC`,
         `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl_symm_apply_left hC`,
         `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl_symm_apply_right hC`,
         `LinearMap.ofIsCompl hC Žå Žł`,
         `LinearMap.ofIsCompl_left_apply hC`, and
         `LinearMap.ofIsCompl_right_apply hC`.  The extension theorem has
         exact shape
         `LinearMap.exists_extend (f : M ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé) :
          Ōłā g : U ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé, g.comp M.subtype = f`
         for `M : Submodule Ōäé U`.  The dual-vector orientation is
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.apply_toDual_symm_apply`:
         if `p0 := (BU.toDual hU).symm ellU`, then
         `BU p0 v = ellU v`; any proof needing `BU v p0` must explicitly use
         symmetry of the restricted form.

         The induction theorem should be proved in the restricted ambient form
         below, then specialized to `U = U' = ŌŖż`.  This avoids an otherwise
         false same-ambient induction step: after splitting an anisotropic
         line or hyperbolic plane, the domain and codomain induction ambients
         are different orthogonal complements.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_wittExtension_restricted
             {D : Nat}
             {U U' : Submodule Ōäé (Fin D -> Ōäé)}
             (hU :
               ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U).
                 Nondegenerate)
             (hU' :
               ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U').
                 Nondegenerate)
             (hdimUU' :
               Module.finrank Ōäé U = Module.finrank Ōäé U')
             {M : Submodule Ōäé U} {N : Submodule Ōäé U'}
             (T : M ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] N)
             (hT :
               ŌłĆ x y : M,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (((T x : N) : U') : Fin D -> Ōäé)
                   (((T y : N) : U') : Fin D -> Ōäé) =
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (((x : M) : U) : Fin D -> Ōäé)
                   (((y : M) : U) : Fin D -> Ōäé)) :
             Ōłā A : U ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] U',
               (ŌłĆ u v : U,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   ((A u : U') : Fin D -> Ōäé)
                   ((A v : U') : Fin D -> Ōäé) =
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (u : Fin D -> Ōäé) (v : Fin D -> Ōäé)) Ōł¦
               ŌłĆ x : M, A (x : U) = (T x : U')
         ```

         The restricted theorem proceeds by well-founded induction on
         `Module.finrank Ōäé M`, with these finite-dimensional sublemmas.  They
         remain required before the Witt packet is Lean-ready:

         * `standardComplexSymmetric_anisotropic_line_split`: for
           `u : U` with `B u u ŌēĀ 0`, the line `Ōäé ŌłÖ u` is nondegenerate and
           complementary to its restricted orthogonal complement.

           ```lean
           theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_anisotropic_line_split
               {D : Nat}
               {U : Submodule Ōäé (Fin D -> Ōäé)}
               (hU :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U).
                   Nondegenerate)
               {u : U}
               (hu : ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U)
                   u u ŌēĀ 0) :
               let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
               let L : Submodule Ōäé U := Ōäé ŌłÖ u
               (BU.restrict L).Nondegenerate Ōł¦
               IsCompl L (BU.orthogonal L) Ōł¦
               (BU.restrict (BU.orthogonal L)).Nondegenerate
           ```

           Proof: convert `hu` to
           `┬¼ BU.IsOrtho u u`, apply
           `LinearMap.BilinForm.isCompl_span_singleton_orthogonal`, obtain
           nondegeneracy of the line from
           `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal`
           and nondegeneracy of the orthogonal complement from
           `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_orthogonal_spanSingleton
           BU hU BU_isRefl`.  The only form-specific input is
           `standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_isRefl`.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_hyperbolic_plane_split`: if
           `B u u = 0`, `B p p = 0`, and `B u p = 1`, then
           `H = span Ōäé {u, p}` is nondegenerate and complementary to `HßŚ«`.

           ```lean
           theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_hyperbolic_plane_split
               {D : Nat}
               {U : Submodule Ōäé (Fin D -> Ōäé)}
               (hU :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U).
                   Nondegenerate)
               {u p : U}
               (huu :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U)
                   u u = 0)
               (hpp :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U)
                   p p = 0)
               (hup :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U)
                   u p = 1) :
               let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
               let H : Submodule Ōäé U :=
                 Submodule.span Ōäé ({u, p} : Set U)
               (BU.restrict H).Nondegenerate Ōł¦
               IsCompl H (BU.orthogonal H) Ōł¦
               (BU.restrict (BU.orthogonal H)).Nondegenerate
           ```

           Proof transcript: first prove `u ŌēĀ 0` by pairing `u = 0` with `p`,
           and `p ŌēĀ 0` similarly.  Prove
           `LinearIndependent Ōäé ![u, p]`: if `a ŌĆó u + b ŌĆó p = 0`, pair with
           `p` to get `a = 0` from `huu`, `hpp`, `hup`, and symmetry; pair
           with `u` to get `b = 0`.  For nondegeneracy, take
           `x : H` with `ŌłĆ y : H, BU x y = 0`.  Write
           `(x : U) = a ŌĆó u + b ŌĆó p` using the `Fin 2` span
           coordinates from the linear-independent pair.  Orthogonality to
           `p` gives `a = 0`; orthogonality to `u` gives `b = 0`; hence
           `x = 0`.  The right-kernel side follows from symmetry.  Then
           `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal
           BU_isRefl` gives `IsCompl H (BU.orthogonal H)`, and the
           orthogonal-complement restriction is nondegenerate by applying the
           same theorem to that complement, using `BU.orthogonal_orthogonal`
           with `hU` and reflexivity.
         * `standardComplexSymmetricRestrictedRadical`: define the radical of
           the restricted form on a subspace by

           ```lean
           def BHW.standardComplexSymmetricRestrictedRadical
               {D : Nat} (U : Submodule Ōäé (Fin D -> Ōäé))
               (M : Submodule Ōäé U) : Submodule Ōäé M :=
             { x : M | ŌłĆ y : M,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (((x : M) : U) : Fin D -> Ōäé)
                   (((y : M) : U) : Fin D -> Ōäé) = 0 }
           ```

         * `standardComplexSymmetric_all_isotropic_imp_restrictedRadical_top`:
           if `ŌłĆ x : M, B x x = 0`, then the restricted radical is `ŌŖż`.
           The proof is the polarization identity over `Ōäé`:
           `0 = B (x + y) (x + y) =
             B x x + B x y + B y x + B y y = 2 * B x y`;
           `two_ne_zero` gives `B x y = 0`.  This replaces the earlier
           polynomial-existence sketch and is the exact Lean route.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_subspace_has_anisotropic_or_radical`:
           if `M ŌēĀ ŌŖź`, then either
           `Ōłā u : M, u ŌēĀ 0 Ōł¦ B u u ŌēĀ 0`, or
           `Ōłā u : M, u ŌēĀ 0 Ōł¦ u Ōłł
             BHW.standardComplexSymmetricRestrictedRadical U M`.
           In Lean, use `Submodule.exists_mem_ne_zero_of_ne_bot`; if the
           anisotropic alternative is false, the previous polarization lemma
           puts every nonzero vector of `M` in the restricted radical.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_radical_partner_of_isCompl`: if
           `u : M` is nonzero and lies in the restricted radical, and
           `hC : IsCompl (Ōäé ŌłÖ u) C` in `M`, construct a hyperbolic partner
           `p : U` orthogonal to `C`.  This is the exact theorem surface; it
           is the key place where the proof uses nondegeneracy of the current
           induction ambient `U`, not nondegeneracy of the full coordinate
           space.

           ```lean
           theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_radical_partner_of_isCompl
               {D : Nat}
               {U : Submodule Ōäé (Fin D -> Ōäé)}
               (hU :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U).
                   Nondegenerate)
               {M : Submodule Ōäé U}
               {u : M}
               (hu_ne : u ŌēĀ 0)
               (hu_rad :
                 u Ōłł BHW.standardComplexSymmetricRestrictedRadical U M)
               {C : Submodule Ōäé M}
               (hC : IsCompl (Ōäé ŌłÖ u) C) :
               let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
               Ōłā p : U,
                 BU ((u : M) : U) p = 1 Ōł¦
                 BU p p = 0 Ōł¦
                 (ŌłĆ c : C, BU ((c : M) : U) p = 0)
           ```

           The coefficient functional is not informal, and the representing
           vector is obtained inside the current nondegenerate induction
           ambient `U`:

           ```lean
           let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
           let L : Submodule Ōäé M := Ōäé ŌłÖ u
           let coeffLine : L ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé :=
             (LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton Ōäé M u hu_ne).symm.toLinearMap
           let coord : M ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé :=
             coeffLine.comp (L.linearProjOfIsCompl C hC)
           obtain Ō¤©ellU, hellUŌ¤® := LinearMap.exists_extend coord
           let p0 : U := (BU.toDual hU).symm ellU
           let p : U := p0 - ((BU p0 p0) / 2) ŌĆó ((u : M) : U)
           ```

           The proof obligations are:

           ```lean
           have hcoord_u : coord u = 1 := by
             -- `L.linearProjOfIsCompl C hC` is identity on `L`, and
             -- `LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton_symm_apply_self` gives
             -- coefficient `1` on `u`.
           have hcoord_C : ŌłĆ c : C, coord (c : M) = 0 := by
             intro c
             -- `Submodule.linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_right hC c`
           have hp0_u_right :
               BU p0 ((u : M) : U) = 1 := by
             -- `apply_toDual_symm_apply` plus `hellU` and `hcoord_u`
           have hp0_C_right :
               ŌłĆ c : C, BU p0 ((c : M) : U) = 0 := by
             intro c
             -- `apply_toDual_symm_apply` plus `hellU` and `hcoord_C c`
           ```

           Symmetry of `BU` turns these into the left-oriented pairings
           needed by the theorem statement.  Since `u` is radical in `M`,
           `BU ((u : M) : U) ((u : M) : U) = 0` and
           `BU ((u : M) : U) ((c : M) : U) = 0` for every `c : C`; symmetry
           gives the reversed pairings.  Therefore the adjustment
           `p = p0 - (BU p0 p0 / 2) ŌĆó u` preserves
           `BU u p = 1` and `BU c p = 0`, while
           `BU p p = 0` is the explicit calculation
           `B00 - (B00 / 2) * 1 - (B00 / 2) * 1 + 0 = 0`, closed by `ring`
           after rewriting `2 ŌēĀ 0` in `Ōäé`.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_isometry_step_anisotropic`: choose
           `u : M` with `B u u ŌēĀ 0`.  Then `T u` has the same nonzero norm.
           The complement used for the recursive subproblem is not an
           unspecified set intersection; it is the restricted orthogonal
           complement inside `M`:

           ```lean
           let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
           let BU' := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U'
           let uU : U := (u : M)
           let TuU' : U' := ((T u : N) : U')
           let L : Submodule Ōäé U := Ōäé ŌłÖ uU
           let L' : Submodule Ōäé U' := Ōäé ŌłÖ TuU'
           let U1 : Submodule Ōäé U := BU.orthogonal L
           let U1' : Submodule Ōäé U' := BU'.orthogonal L'
           let C : Submodule Ōäé M :=
             { x : M | ((x : M) : U) Ōłł U1 }
           let C' : Submodule Ōäé N :=
             { y : N | ((y : N) : U') Ōłł U1' }
           ```

           Because `BU uU uU ŌēĀ 0`, every `x : M` decomposes by the explicit
           projection formula
           `x = ((BU ((x:M):U) uU / BU uU uU) ŌĆó u) +
             (x - (BU ((x:M):U) uU / BU uU uU) ŌĆó u)`,
           and the second summand lies in `C`.  This gives
           `hMC : IsCompl (Ōäé ŌłÖ u) C` inside `M`.  The same construction gives
           `hNC : IsCompl (Ōäé ŌłÖ T u) C'` inside `N`.  The preservation
           hypothesis gives

           ```lean
           let fC : C ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] C' :=
             (T.toLinearMap.comp C.subtype).codRestrict C' (by
               intro c
               -- use `hT c u` to rewrite orthogonality to `Tu`)
           let gC : C' ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] C :=
             (T.symm.toLinearMap.comp C'.subtype).codRestrict C (by
               intro c'
               -- use `hT (T.symm c') u` and `T.apply_symm_apply`)
           let T_C : C ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] C' :=
             LinearEquiv.ofLinear fC gC
               (by ext c; simp [fC, gC])
               (by ext c'; simp [fC, gC])
           ```

           In the recursive call, `C` and `C'` are viewed as submodules of
           `U1` and `U1'` by codomain restriction of their subtype maps; the
           proofs that their images land in the orthogonal ambients are their
           defining membership fields.  The ambient nondegeneracy hypotheses
           are supplied by
           `standardComplexSymmetric_anisotropic_line_split`.  The ambient
           finrank equality is

           ```lean
           have hdimU1 :
               Module.finrank Ōäé U1 = Module.finrank Ōäé U1' := by
             rw [LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal hU L,
                 LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal hU' L']
             have hL : Module.finrank Ōäé L = 1 := by
               simpa using
                 (LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton Ōäé U uU huU_ne).finrank_eq
             have hL' : Module.finrank Ōäé L' = 1 := by
               simpa using
                 (LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton Ōäé U' TuU' hTu_ne).finrank_eq
             omega
           ```

           The line map is
           `(LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton Ōäé U uU huU_ne).symm Ōē¬Ōē½ŌéŚ
             LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton Ōäé U' TuU' hTu_ne`.
           After the recursive equivalence `A1 : U1 ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] U1'` is obtained,
           the global extension is the product-conjugate

           ```lean
           let decU := Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl L U1 hLU
           let decU' := Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl L' U1' hLU'
           let A : U ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] U' :=
             decU.symm.trans ((Aline.prod A1).trans decU')
           ```

           `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl_symm_apply_left/right` proves that
           `A` sends `u` to `T u` and agrees with the recursive map on `C`;
           the product orthogonality and the two component isometry proofs
           prove preservation of `BU`.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_isometry_step_radical`: choose
