           `u : M` in the restricted radical and a complement
           `hC : IsCompl (Ōäé ŌłÖ u) C` from `Submodule.exists_isCompl`.
           Transport `C` to `C' = C.map T.toLinearMap` and use the partner
           theorem to obtain `p` for `(u, C)` and `p'` for
           `(T u, C')`.  The map on hyperbolic planes is
           `u Ōå” T u`, `p Ōå” p'`; it is constructed from the two ordered bases
           of `H = span Ōäé {uU, p}` and
           `H' = span Ōäé {TuU', p'}` obtained in
           `standardComplexSymmetric_hyperbolic_plane_split`.  The Gram
           matrix in both bases is `!![0, 1; 1, 0]`, so this plane map is an
           isometry.

           The recursive subspaces are explicitly the images of `C` and `C'`
           inside the orthogonal complements:

           ```lean
           let H : Submodule Ōäé U := Submodule.span Ōäé ({uU, p} : Set U)
           let H' : Submodule Ōäé U' := Submodule.span Ōäé ({TuU', p'} : Set U')
           let U1 : Submodule Ōäé U := BU.orthogonal H
           let U1' : Submodule Ōäé U' := BU'.orthogonal H'
           let C_in_U1 : Submodule Ōäé U1 :=
             (ŌŖż : Submodule Ōäé C).map
               (LinearMap.codRestrict U1
                 (M.subtype.comp C.subtype)
                 (by
                   intro c
                   -- orthogonal to `u` by radicality, orthogonal to `p` by
                   -- the partner theorem, hence orthogonal to all of `H`))
           ```

           Define `C'_in_U1'` analogously.  The restricted map
           `T_C : C_in_U1 ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] C'_in_U1'` is induced by `T` and the
           definition `C' = C.map T.toLinearMap`; preservation of the form is
           the original `hT` restricted to `C`.  The recursive ambient finrank
           equality is again a direct subtraction:

           ```lean
           have hdimU1 :
               Module.finrank Ōäé U1 = Module.finrank Ōäé U1' := by
             rw [LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal hU H,
                 LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal hU' H']
             have hH : Module.finrank Ōäé H = 2 := by
               -- ordered hyperbolic basis `{u,p}`
             have hH' : Module.finrank Ōäé H' = 2 := by
               -- ordered hyperbolic basis `{T u,p'}`
             omega
           ```

           After induction gives `A1 : U1 ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] U1'`, combine it with the
           hyperbolic-plane isometry exactly as in the anisotropic branch:

           ```lean
           let decU := Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl H U1 hHU1
           let decU' := Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl H' U1' hHprimeU1prime
           let A : U ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] U' :=
             decU.symm.trans ((AH.prod A1).trans decU')
           ```

           `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl_symm_apply_left/right` proves the
           agreement on `H` and on the recursive complement.  Agreement with
           the original `T` on all of `M` follows from the decomposition
           `hC : IsCompl (Ōäé ŌłÖ u) C`: the line component is handled by
           `AH u = T u`, and the complement component by the recursive
           agreement on `C`.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_wittExtension_induction`: combines the
           anisotropic and radical cases.  The measure drops because in each
           branch the recursive subspace is a complement to a nonzero line in
           `M`; use `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl` to identify
           `M ŌēāŌéŚ (Ōäé ŌłÖ u) ├Ś C` and close
           `Module.finrank Ōäé C < Module.finrank Ōäé M` by
           `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq`, `Module.finrank_prod`, and
           `LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton`.

         After `standardComplexSymmetric_wittExtension`, the Minkowski theorem
         is mechanical: conjugate `T` by
         `BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d`, apply the standard
         extension, conjugate the resulting global isometry back, and package
         its matrix as `ComplexLorentzGroup d` using the preservation
         equation.  This packaging is valid only when the extended global
         isometry has determinant `1`.  If the source subspace has codimension
         one, choose the sign on the orthogonal complement to force determinant
         `1`; if it is the whole ambient space, the determinant is already
         fixed by `T`, and same scalar products alone do not force it to be
         `1`.  Consequently the full-rank high-branch consumer must either
         carry Hall-Wightman's improper-component invariance or call a
         full-complex-orthogonal BHW source theorem.  The determinant-`1`
         shortcut `complexMinkowski_wittExtension` is obtained from
         `complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry` only for the
         determinant-`1` case, by reversing the equality orientation if needed.
      3. `hwLemma3_adaptedBase_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube` is
         the quantitative Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 in Schur-complement
         coordinates.  The public theorem first replaces the given
         extended-tube representative by an adapted same-Gram representative
         whose vector span has dimension equal to the scalar rank; this is the
         exact first reduction in Hall-Wightman's proof and avoids the false
         operation of killing radical tail vectors by an invertible source
         change.  For the adapted base, choose a principal rank `r` minor,
         move it to the upper-left block, normalize it to the identity, and
         write nearby rank-`<= d + 1` matrices as
         `[[A, B], [BßĄĆ, BßĄĆ AŌü╗┬╣ B + S]]` with `S` symmetric of rank
         `<= d + 1 - r`.  Realize `A` and `B` by analytic perturbations of the
         first `r` source vectors and realize `S` by vectors in the
         `(d + 1 - r)`-dimensional orthogonal complement via
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail`.
         The estimates are Hall-Wightman's inequality (48), with `4 - r`
         replaced by `d + 1 - r`.  Choose `╬Ą` smaller than the coordinate tube
         radius at the adapted representative; then all realized perturbations
         remain in the ordinary extended tube.
      4. `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety` follows
         from the same Schur realization: every source Gram matrix is
         symmetric and has rank at most `d + 1`; conversely every complex
         symmetric matrix of rank `<= d + 1` is congruent to
         `diag(1, ..., 1, 0, ..., 0)` and is realized by `d + 1` standard
         complex source vectors.  The existing checked companion
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le` is always the conjunction of
         symmetry and the rank bound, never the bare rank condition.
      5. `sourceComplexGramVariety_normal` is not a QFT theorem.  The Lean port
         should prove or import the standard symmetric-determinantal package in
         this order:

         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_schurLocalProductModel`: choose a
           rank-`r` principal block by symmetric Gaussian elimination and
           reindex it by `e : Fin n Ōēā r ŌŖĢ q`.  On the determinant-unit patch,
           use the checked coordinate equality
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch`:
           membership in `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D` is equivalent to
           the Schur coordinate
           `S = C - BßĄĆ AŌü╗┬╣ B` being symmetric and having rank
           `<= D-r`.  The inverse chart is
           `Z Ōå” (A(Z), B(Z), S(Z))`; the forward chart is
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph n e A B S`.  The block-recovery lemmas
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_toBlocksŌéüŌéü`,
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_toBlocksŌéüŌéé`, and
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_schurComplement` give
           `to_inv_on` and `inv_to_on`.  All coordinate maps are holomorphic
           on the determinant-unit patch because matrix inverse is
           holomorphic there and the remaining operations are polynomial.
         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_singularLocus_eq_lowerRank`: in the
           nontrivial range `0 < D < n`, a rank-`D` point has residual cone
           `rank <= 0`, hence the Schur product is smooth.  A rank
           `r < D` point has residual cone `Sym_{n-r}^{<= D-r}` at `0`, with
           `0 < D-r < n-r`; all defining `(D-r+1)`-minors have vanishing
           linear part at `0`, so the Zariski tangent space is the whole
           symmetric ambient space while the cone has smaller dimension.
           Therefore those and only those points are singular.  The cases
           `D = 0` and `n <= D` are separated before this theorem: a point and
           the full symmetric vector space are smooth.
         * `sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum_complexDimension`: the
           determinant-unit Schur chart at rank `D` has free symmetric
           `A`-coordinates and rectangular `B`-coordinates, with
           `S = 0`; its dimension is
           `D*(D+1)/2 + D*(n-D) = n*D - D*(D-1)/2`.  In Lean this reuses the
           already documented checked dimension theorem
           `finrank_sourceSelectedComplexSymCoordSubspace` and the arithmetic
           packet
           `sourceRankExactChartDim_sub_previous`.
         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim`: subtract the same
           dimension formula for ranks `D` and `D-1` to get
           `n - D + 1`; because the singular case has `D < n`, this is at
           least two.  This is the exact proof of
           `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne`, not a phrase
           "singular locus has codimension at least two".
         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay`: use the
           standard symmetric-determinantal straightening-law theorem over
           `Ōäé` (equivalently the Jozefiak-Pragacz resolution) to get reduced
           and Cohen-Macaulay local rings.  If the Lean port imports this as a
           standard algebraic-geometry theorem, the imported theorem must be
           stated for symmetric determinantal varieties over an algebraically
           closed field of characteristic zero and specialized to `Ōäé`; it may
           not mention OS, Wightman fields, locality, extended tubes, or the
           scalar representative target.
         * Apply `normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre` to
           `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_cohenMacaulay` and
           `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne`; finally rewrite
           through
           `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety`.

         The local Schur chart is the same algebraic chart family used in the
         checked rank-exact source infrastructure, but here its role is
         commutative algebra/normality, not analytic continuation.
      6. `sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank` is
         the density/properness input for the removable theorem.  In the hard
         range `d + 1 < n`, it is the checked rank-exact density theorem
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_rankExact`,
         because max rank is `d + 1`; in the easy range `n <= d + 1`, it is
         the elementary density of invertible symmetric matrices inside the
         full symmetric coordinate space.  This theorem is what prevents the
         exceptional analytic set from becoming an unconstrained component.

         Implementation-level density proof.  Work first on the symmetric
         rank-bounded model `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D`.

         ```lean
         /-- Rank-exact points are dense in the symmetric rank-`<= D`
         determinantal variety.  At a point of rank `r <= D`, choose a
         determinant-unit principal `r`-block and use the Schur product model.
         In the residual symmetric cone, add the diagonal path
         `t ŌĆó diag(1,...,1,0,...)` of rank `D-r`; its Schur graph has total
         rank `D`, stays in rank `<= D`, and tends to the original point as
         `t -> 0`.  The cases `r = D`, `D = 0`, and `n <= D` are immediate. -/
         theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_rankExact_dense
             (n D : Nat) :
             BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D ŌŖå
               closure (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum n (min D n))

         theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_maxRank_dense
             (d n : Nat) :
             BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
               closure {Z | Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Ōł¦
                 BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z} := by
           have hVar :
               BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n =
                 BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1) :=
             BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety
               (d := d) (n := n)
           have hRankSet :
               {Z | Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Ōł¦
                 BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z} =
               BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum n (min (d + 1) n) := by
             ext Z
             simp [hVar, BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank,
               BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank]
           simpa [hVar, hRankSet] using
             BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_rankExact_dense n (d + 1)

         theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank
             (d n : Nat)
             {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
             (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
             U ŌŖå closure
               (U Ōł® {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z}) := by
           rcases hU_rel with Ō¤©╬®, h╬®_open, hU_eqŌ¤®
           intro Z hZU
           have hZV : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
             simpa [hU_eq] using hZU.2
           have hZ╬® : Z Ōłł ╬® := by
             simpa [hU_eq] using hZU.1
           refine
             BHW.closure_inter_open_of_mem_open
               h╬®_open hZ╬®
               (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_maxRank_dense
                 (d := d) (n := n) hZV)
               (by
                 ext W
                 simp [hU_eq, Set.inter_assoc, Set.left_comm,
                   Set.inter_left_comm])
         ```

         The last helper
         `BHW.closure_inter_open_of_mem_open` is the standard topological
         lemma: if `x Ōłł ╬®` and `x Ōłł closure A`, with `╬®` open, then
         `x Ōłł closure (╬® Ōł® A)`.  Here `A` is the maximal-rank subset of the
         source Gram variety, so rewriting `U = ╬® Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety`
         gives the required relative-open statement.  The proof does not use
         extended tubes, OS data, or the scalar function `phi`.
      7. `sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension` is the analytic Riemann
         extension theorem on normal analytic spaces, specialized to the
         source Gram variety.  Its inputs must include the analytic exceptional
         set and the dense maximal-rank complement, in addition to
         `hphi_cont`, `hphi_bdd`, and the max-rank local representatives.
         Its proof first rewrites `hU_rel` as a relative-open hypothesis
         `BHW.IsRelOpenIn (sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U`; then it calls the
         generic SCV theorem
         `BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension` with
         `V := sourceComplexGramVariety d n` and
         `A := {Z | HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z}`.  The regular input
         on `U \ A` is exactly `hregular`, because
         `HWSourceGramMaxRank` is the complement of
         `HWSourceGramExceptionalRank`.  Local boundedness near the exceptional
         set is supplied by `hphi_bdd`, while continuity and the dense
         complement ensure that the extended section agrees with the original
         scalar value `phi` on exceptional points.  The resulting holomorphic
         section of the normal analytic space is represented locally by an
         ambient holomorphic function because
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic` identifies the source Gram
         variety as a closed analytic subvariety of the finite scalar-coordinate
         space.  Both the regular-locus chart input and the conclusion must
         also carry the local-domain field `U0 Ōł® V ŌŖå U`; otherwise the
         asserted equality with `phi` would mention values outside the
         relatively open scalar domain.

         The generic theorem is itself implemented through the following
         standard SCV support, with no QFT or locality content:

         ```lean
         /-- Weakly holomorphic local extension on a normal analytic subvariety.
         Here `f` is represented by compatible ambient holomorphic charts on
         `U \ A`, is locally bounded on `U`, and `U \ A` is dense in `U`.
         Normality converts this weakly holomorphic section into a genuine
         structure-sheaf section near `x`, chosen inside the relative domain. -/
         theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension
             {E : Type*}
             [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace Ōäé E]
             [CompleteSpace E] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
             {V U A : Set E}
             {phi : E -> Ōäé}
             (hV_normal : BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V)
             (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenIn V U)
             (hA_analytic : BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn V A)
             (hDense : U ŌŖå closure (U \ A))
             (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
             (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
             (hregular :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł U \ A ->
                 Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                   IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ x Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                   DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                   U0 Ōł® V ŌŖå U Ōł¦
                   Set.EqOn phi ╬© (U0 Ōł® V))
             {x : E}
             (hxU : x Ōłł U) :
             Ōłā U0 ╬©,
               IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ x Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
               DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
               U0 Ōł® V ŌŖå U Ōł¦
               Set.EqOn phi ╬© (U0 Ōł® V)

         theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension ... := by
           intro x hxU
           exact
             BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension
               hV_normal hU_rel hA_analytic hDense
               hphi_cont hphi_bdd hregular hxU
         ```

         Proof transcript for
         `normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension`:

         * Use `hU_rel` to choose an ambient open set `╬®U` with
           `U = ╬®U Ōł® V`; shrink every returned neighborhood inside `╬®U`, so
           `U0 Ōł® V ŌŖå U` is a theorem field rather than an informal
           convention.
         * On `U \ A`, the `hregular` charts define a holomorphic section of
           the structure sheaf of the analytic space `V`; overlap equality
           follows because every chart equals the same scalar function `phi`
           on its intersection with `V`.
         * `hphi_bdd` gives local boundedness of this section near points of
           `A`, and `hDense` ensures `U \ A` is the dense complement in every
           relative neighborhood used in the extension argument.
         * Apply the standard normal-space Riemann theorem:
           a locally bounded weakly holomorphic function on a normal analytic
           space extends uniquely across a nowhere-dense analytic subset.
           In Lean this theorem should live in the SCV support layer with a
           finite-dimensional-polymorphic statement not mentioning Wightman,
           OS, locality, Gram maps, or fields.  The ordinary source Gram space
           and the oriented Gram-plus-volume space must both be applications
           of this one SCV theorem, not separate black boxes.
         * Choose an ambient holomorphic representative of the resulting
           structure-sheaf germ using the closed analytic embedding of `V` in
           the finite coordinate space.  Equality with `phi` at exceptional
           points is obtained from continuity of both sides and equality on
           the dense set `U \ A`; no value is assigned by arbitrary choice.

         Lean-shaped specialization:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension ... := by
           have hV_normal :
               BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
                 (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :=
             BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal d n
           have hU_rel' :
               BHW.IsRelOpenIn (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U :=
             BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.to_isRelOpenIn hU_rel
           have hDense' :
               U ŌŖå closure
                 (U \ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z}) := by
             have hSet :
                 U Ōł® {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z} =
                   U \ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z} := by
               ext Z
               constructor
               ┬Ę rintro Ō¤©hZU, hMaxŌ¤®
                 refine Ō¤©hZU, ?_Ō¤®
                 intro hExc
                 exact
                   ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                     (d := d) (n := n)).1 hExc) hMax
               ┬Ę rintro Ō¤©hZU, hNotExcŌ¤®
                 refine Ō¤©hZU, ?_Ō¤®
                 by_contra hNotMax
                 exact hNotExc
                   ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                     (d := d) (n := n)).2 hNotMax)
             simpa [hSet] using hMaxDense
           have hregular' :
               ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U \ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z} ->
                 Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                   IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                   DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                   U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U Ōł¦
                   Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                     (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
             intro Z hZ
             have hZU : Z Ōłł U := hZ.1
             have hMax : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z :=
               by
                 by_contra hnotMax
                 exact hZ.2
                   ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                     (d := d) (n := n)).2 hnotMax)
             exact hregular Z hZU hMax
           exact
             BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension
               hV_normal hU_rel' hExceptionalAnalytic hDense'
               hphi_cont hphi_bdd hregular'
         ```
      8. `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAmbientNeighborhood` and
         `cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety` are archived
         Siu/Cartan support for the old total ambient-function API only.  The
         first would be a four-step theorem, not a consequence of relative
         openness alone: an independent theorem would have to supply
         `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_domainOfHolomorphy`; the normality of
         the source Gram variety would upgrade this to
         `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace`; the source domain
         is already relatively open by
         `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`; Siu's theorem would
         then give a Stein ambient neighborhood `╬®` with
         `S'_n = ╬® Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety`.  The second uses the coherent
         ideal sheaf generated by the symmetry equations and Gram minors,
         applies the exact sequence
         `0 -> I_V -> O_╬® -> O_{╬®Ōł®V} -> 0`, and invokes Cartan theorem B on
         the Stein open `╬®` to prove surjectivity of
         `╬ō(╬®, O_╬®) -> ╬ō(╬® Ōł® V, O_{╬®Ōł®V})`.
      9. `sourceVariety_holomorphicGerm_cartanAmbientExtension` is not a
         Hall-Wightman theorem and is not active.  It is only the bridge from
         the usual analytic-space output to the repo's stronger total-ambient
         `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` predicate.  The active route instead
         changes `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.Phi_holomorphic` to the germ
         predicate, so this bridge is unnecessary for theorem-2 production
         Lean unless the old strong API is explicitly retained and the
         independent Stein input is proved.

      ```lean
      /-- Rank of a source Gram matrix in Hall-Wightman's scalar-product
      coordinates.  This is a predicate on scalar products, not on the
      dimension of the source-vector span. -/
      def BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank
          (n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) : Nat :=
        (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j).rank

      def BHW.sourceConfigGramMatrixRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) : Nat :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      /-- Dimension-general Hall-Wightman orbit-rank condition.  For the
      original four-vector theorem, `d = 3`, so this says rank
      `min 3 n` or higher: rank three or four in the hard arity range, and
      nonsingular scalar matrices in the small-arity range. -/
      def BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        min d n <= BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z

      def BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z < min d n

      def BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank d n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      def BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank d n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      /-- Hall-Wightman's non-exceptional scalar-rank condition for the
      local holomorphic chart part of Theorem I.  This is a different
      threshold from the Lemma-2 orbit condition: it is maximal possible
      scalar rank in spacetime dimension `d + 1`. -/
      def BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        min (d + 1) n <= BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z

      def BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z < min (d + 1) n

      def BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGram_lowRank_iff_not_orbitRank
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé} :
          BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank d n Z Ōåö
            ┬¼ BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank d n Z := by
        constructor
        ┬Ę intro hlow horbit
          exact (not_lt_of_ge horbit) hlow
        ┬Ę intro hnot
          exact Nat.lt_of_not_ge hnot

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé} :
          BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z Ōåö
            ┬¼ BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z := by
        constructor
        ┬Ę intro hlow hmax
          exact (not_lt_of_ge hmax) hlow
        ┬Ę intro hnot
          exact Nat.lt_of_not_ge hnot

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGramOrbitRankAt_of_sourceGram_eq
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z Ōåö
            BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n w := by
        simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt, hgram]

      /- These rank-predicate lemmas are only arithmetic and definitional
      bookkeeping.  The hypothesis `hd` on
      `hwSourceGram_lowRank_iff_not_orbitRank` is retained only to match the
      Hall-Wightman theorem surfaces where it is consumed; the proof itself is
      `Nat.lt_of_not_ge` and `not_lt_of_ge`.  Thus none of the real
      Hall-Wightman content is hidden in the rank split: the genuine work
      starts with the high-rank span isometry and the low-rank isotropic
      contraction data below. -/

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma-2 low-rank normal form before taking limits.
      After applying `╬ø0` to the left configuration, the two equal-Gram
      configurations differ by vectors in a common totally isotropic residual
      subspace orthogonal to the common selected span.  In four spacetime
      dimensions this residual subspace is at most one-dimensional, giving the
      printed Hall-Wightman formula with one null vector.  For the repo's
      dimension-general surfaces it must be kept as a finite isotropic frame;
      replacing it by one `q` would be false in higher dimension. -/
      structure BHW.HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm
          (d n : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) where
        ╬ø0 : ComplexLorentzGroup d
        ╬Š : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        s : Nat
        aLeft : Fin n -> Fin s -> Ōäé
        aRight : Fin n -> Fin s -> Ōäé
        q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        left_eq :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø0 z =
            fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, aLeft i c * q c ╬╝
        right_eq :
          w = fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, aRight i c * q c ╬╝
        base_mem :
          ╬Š Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        q_pair_zero :
          ŌłĆ c c',
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0
        qDual_pair_zero :
          ŌłĆ c c',
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0
        q_dual :
          ŌłĆ c c',
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
              if c = c' then (1 : Ōäé) else 0
        q_orth :
          ŌłĆ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (╬Š i) = 0
        qDual_orth :
          ŌłĆ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (╬Š i) = 0
        contract : ŌäØ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d
        contract_fix_╬Š :
          ŌłĆ t i ╬╝,
            (Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬Į * ╬Š i ╬Į) = ╬Š i ╬╝
        contract_scale_q :
          ŌłĆ t c ╬╝,
            (Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬Į * q c ╬Į) =
              Real.exp (-t) * q c ╬╝
        contract_scale_qDual :
          ŌłĆ t c ╬╝,
            (Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1),
              (contract t).val ╬╝ ╬Į * qDual c ╬Į) =
              Real.exp t * qDual c ╬╝
        contracted_left_mem :
          ŌłĆ t,
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        contracted_right_mem :
          ŌłĆ t,
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        contracted_left_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ŌäØ =>
              fun i ╬╝ =>
                ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c ╬╝)
            atTop (nhds ╬Š)
        contracted_right_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ŌäØ =>
              fun i ╬╝ =>
                ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c ╬╝)
            atTop (nhds ╬Š)

      /-- Linear algebra inside the low-rank branch: after choosing a nonzero
      principal scalar block, the equal Gram data determine the same
      nondegenerate selected span, scalar-coordinate coefficients against that
      span, and residual radical pairings. -/
      structure BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame
          (d n r : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n) where
        I_injective : Function.Injective I
        principal_minor_ne :
          BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) ŌēĀ 0
        selected_gram_eq :
          ŌłĆ a b,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z (I a) (I b) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w (I a) (I b)
        coeff : Fin n -> Fin r -> Ōäé
        left_residual_orth :
          ŌłĆ i a,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó z (I b))
              (z (I a)) = 0
        right_residual_orth :
          ŌłĆ i a,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (w i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó w (I b))
              (w (I a)) = 0
        residual_pairing_eq :
          ŌłĆ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó z (I b))
              (z j - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ŌĆó z (I b)) =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (w i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó w (I b))
              (w j - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ŌĆó w (I b))
        left_residual_pair_zero :
          ŌłĆ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó z (I b))
              (z j - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ŌĆó z (I b)) = 0
        right_residual_pair_zero :
          ŌłĆ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (w i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó w (I b))
              (w j - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ŌĆó w (I b)) = 0

      def BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé :=
        Matrix.of fun a b => G (I a) (I b)

      noncomputable def BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (i : Fin n) :
          Fin r -> Ōäé :=
        fun b =>
          Ōłæ a : Fin r,
            G i (I a) *
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G)Ōü╗┬╣ a b

      theorem BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_projection_eq
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G).det)
          (i : Fin n) (a : Fin r) :
          Ōłæ b : Fin r,
            BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G i b *
              G (I b) (I a) =
            G i (I a)

      theorem BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_selected_eq
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G).det)
          (a b : Fin r) :
          BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G (I a) b =
            if a = b then 1 else 0

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_selected
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) ŌēĀ 0)
          (a : Fin r) :
          BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) z (I a) = 0

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_head
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) ŌēĀ 0)
          (i : Fin n) (a : Fin r) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) z i)
            (z (I a)) = 0

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedComplement_residual_inner_residual_eq_schur
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hI : Function.Injective I)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) ŌēĀ 0)
          (u v : BHW.selectedIndexComplement I) :
          let G := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z
          let e := BHW.selectedIndexSumEquiv I hI
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I G z
              (e.symm (Sum.inr u)))
            (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I G z
              (e.symm (Sum.inr v))) =
          BHW.reindexedRectSchurComplement
            (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => G i j) e e u v

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_residual_eq_zero
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) = r)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) ŌēĀ 0) :
          ŌłĆ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) z i)
              (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) z j) = 0

      def BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanFrame_of_sameSourceGram
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrameData d n z w

      def BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace
          (d : Nat)
          (R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) : Prop :=
        ŌłĆ x y : R,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0

      /-- Data obtained after the selected nondegenerate spans have first been
      matched by a Witt-extension Lorentz transformation.  The common selected
      span is the `w` selected span; only after applying `╬øsel` to the left
      endpoint do the two projected residual families live in the same
      orthogonal complement. -/
      structure BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment
          (d n r : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I) where
        M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
        ╬øsel : ComplexLorentzGroup d
        ╬Š : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        leftResidual : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        rightResidual : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        M_eq :
          M = Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range (fun a : Fin r => w (I a)))
        M_nondeg : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M
        selected_mem : ŌłĆ a, w (I a) Ōłł M
        ╬øsel_selected :
          ŌłĆ a,
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬øsel (z (I a)) = w (I a)
        ╬Š_eq :
          ŌłĆ i, ╬Š i = Ōłæ b : Fin r, S.coeff i b ŌĆó w (I b)
        ╬Š_mem : ŌłĆ i, ╬Š i Ōłł M
        leftResidual_eq :
          ŌłĆ i,
            leftResidual i =
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬øsel (z i) - ╬Š i
        rightResidual_eq :
          ŌłĆ i, rightResidual i = w i - ╬Š i
        left_decomp :
          ŌłĆ i,
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬øsel (z i) =
              ╬Š i + leftResidual i
        right_decomp : ŌłĆ i, w i = ╬Š i + rightResidual i
        left_residual_orth_M :
          ŌłĆ i (m : M),
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (leftResidual i)
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0
        right_residual_orth_M :
          ŌłĆ i (m : M),
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (rightResidual i)
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0
        left_residual_pair_zero :
          ŌłĆ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (leftResidual i) (leftResidual j) = 0
        right_residual_pair_zero :
          ŌłĆ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (rightResidual i) (rightResidual j) = 0

      noncomputable def BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanAlignment_of_selectedSpanFrame
          (d n r : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I)
          (hproper : r < d + 1) :
          BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment d n r z w I S

      /-- Once the selected spans are aligned, the projected residual vectors
      on both sides lie in the orthogonal complement of the common selected
      span and span totally isotropic subspaces. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_after_selectedAlignment
          {d n r : Nat}
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          {S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I}
          (A : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment d n r z w I S) :
          Ōłā (Rleft Rright : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)),
            Rleft =
              Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range A.leftResidual) Ōł¦
            Rright =
              Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range A.rightResidual) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x : Rleft, ŌłĆ m : A.M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x : Rright, ŌłĆ m : A.M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0) Ōł¦
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rleft Ōł¦
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rright

      /-- Finite-dimensional Witt geometry in the orthogonal complement of the
      selected span, packaged as the final geometric frame-extension packet.
      It supplies a compatible maximal isotropic frame in `A.MßŚ«` containing the
      right residuals, plus a determinant-one Lorentz correction fixing the
      selected nondegenerate span and moving the left residuals into that same
      frame span. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_residualFrameExtensionData_of_selectedAlignment
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          {S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I}
          (A : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment d n r z w I S) :
          BHW.HWLowRankResidualFrameExtensionData A

      The proof of this producer is a finite-dimensional Witt
      construction in the orthogonal complement of `A.M`; it must not be left
      as a black-box "put both residual spaces in the same null frame".  The
      required support packet is:

      ```lean
      def BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) :
          Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
        { carrier := {v | ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0}
          zero_mem' := by simp
          add_mem' := by
            intro x y hx hy m
            simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_left, hx m, hy m]
          smul_mem' := by
            intro c x hx m
            simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_left, hx m] }

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
          (d : Nat)
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)

      theorem BHW.subspace_le_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
          {d : Nat}
          {M R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hR_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0) :
          R Ōēż BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M

      structure BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn
          (d : Nat)
          (N : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) where
        s : Nat
        q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        q_mem : ŌłĆ c, q c Ōłł N
        q_independent : LinearIndependent Ōäé q
        q_pair_zero :
          ŌłĆ c c',
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0
        maximal :
          ŌłĆ R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            R Ōēż N ->
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R ->
            Module.finrank Ōäé R Ōēż s

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_exists
          {N : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          Nonempty
            (BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending
          {N R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hR_le : R Ōēż N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          Ōłā F : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N,
            R Ōēż Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range F.q)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {N R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (F : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N)
          (hR_le : R Ōēż N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          Ōłā E : R ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            Function.Injective E Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x : R,
              E x Ōłł Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range F.q)) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x y : R,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E x)
                (E y)
              =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))

      theorem BHW.span_frame_orthogonal_to_subspace
          (d s : Nat)
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hq_orth :
            ŌłĆ c (m : M),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          {v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hv : v Ōłł Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)) :
          ŌłĆ m : M,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              v (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_disjoint_of_nondegenerate_orthogonal
          {d : Nat}
          {M R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0) :
          Disjoint M R

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_orthogonalComplement_after_selectedAlignment
          {d n r : Nat}
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          {S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I}
          (A : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment d n r z w I S) :
          Ōłā (Rleft Rright : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)),
            Rleft =
              Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range A.leftResidual) Ōł¦
            Rright =
              Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range A.rightResidual) Ōł¦
            Rleft Ōēż BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d A.M Ōł¦
            Rright Ōēż BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d A.M Ōł¦
            Disjoint A.M Rleft Ōł¦
            Disjoint A.M Rright Ōł¦
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rleft Ōł¦
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rright

      noncomputable def BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
          {d : Nat}
          (M R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (E : R ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hE_inj : Function.Injective E)
          (hE_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E x) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          : Ōåź(M ŌŖö R) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōåź(M ŌŖö LinearMap.range E)

      theorem BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_preserves
          {d : Nat}
          {M R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          {E : R ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hE_inj : Function.Injective E)
          (hE_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E x) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hE_preserves :
            ŌłĆ x y : R,

            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              ((T x : Ōåź(M ŌŖö LinearMap.range E)) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              ((T y : Ōåź(M ŌŖö LinearMap.range E)) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_maps_right
          {d : Nat}
          {M R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          {E : R ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hE_inj : Function.Injective E)
          (hE_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E x) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (x : R) :
          ((BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
              (d := d) M R E hM hR_orth hE_inj hE_orth)
            Ō¤©(x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé), Submodule.mem_sup_right x.2Ō¤® :
              Ōåź(M ŌŖö LinearMap.range E)) =
            Ō¤©E x, Submodule.mem_sup_right Ō¤©x, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_maps_left
          {d : Nat}
          {M R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          {E : R ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hE_inj : Function.Injective E)
          (hE_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E x) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (m : M) :
          ((BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
              (d := d) M R E hM hR_orth hE_inj hE_orth)
            Ō¤©(m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé), Submodule.mem_sup_left m.2Ō¤® :
              Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = m
      ```

      Proof transcript for this packet: `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule`
      is a submodule because the bilinear form is linear in the first
      variable.  If `M` is nondegenerate in the ambient nondegenerate complex
      Minkowski space, the standard finite-dimensional decomposition
      `ambient = M ŌŖö MßŚ«` gives nondegeneracy of `MßŚ«`; in Lean this is the
      same Gram-matrix block diagonal argument used in
      `complexMinkowski_wittExtension`.  A maximal isotropic frame in a
      finite-dimensional complex vector space is now obtained in production
      Lean by choosing a totally isotropic subspace of maximal finite
      dimension among all totally isotropic subspaces contained in the ambient
      subspace, then taking a finite basis of that subspace.  The checked
      namespace lemmas `span_le`, `span_isotropic`, `finrank_span`, and
      `maximal_span` expose the frame span with its dimension and maximality
      bound; `complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_maximalFrame`
      and `directSum_identity_sum_isotropicMaximalFrameEmbedding` feed that
      bound directly into the residual embedding/direct-sum packet.  The final
      hard theorem can now target
      `BHW.HWLowRankResidualFrameExtensionData`; the checked conversion
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_residualAlignmentData_of_frameExtensionData` supplies
      `HWLowRankResidualAlignmentData`.  The strengthened constructor
      `complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending` is now checked:
      it extends a given totally isotropic subspace `R` to a global maximal
      isotropic subspace by the relative-orthogonal quotient/rank-nullity
      argument, then packages a basis of that enlarged subspace as a maximal
      frame.  Its output is essential here: the right residual subspace `Rw`
      already lies in the final frame span before the left residual subspace is
      moved.

      For `complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame`, choose a
      basis of `R`.  The maximality field gives
      `finrank R <= F.s`, so inject that basis index into `Fin F.s` and send
      the basis vectors of `R` to the corresponding `F.q` vectors.  Since the
      bilinear form vanishes identically on `R` and on the span of the frame,
      the map preserves pairings automatically; the selected `F.q` subfamily
      is independent, so the map is injective.  The theorem deliberately
      returns an injective map into the frame span, not an equivalence onto the
      full frame span: `R` may have smaller dimension than the chosen maximal
      frame.

      This embedding step is now checked as:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame
          {d s : Nat}
          {R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hq_independent : LinearIndependent Ōäé q)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ŌłĆ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R)
          (hdim : Module.finrank Ōäé R <= s) :
          Ōłā E : R ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            Function.Injective E Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x : R,
              E x Ōłł Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)) Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ x y : R,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (E x) (E y) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
      ```

      The coefficient extraction for the eventual normal-form fields is also
      checked:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.coefficients_of_family_mem_span_finite_frame
          {d n s : Nat}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hv : ŌłĆ i,
            v i Ōłł Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)) :
          Ōłā a : Fin n -> Fin s -> Ōäé,
            ŌłĆ i, v i = Ōłæ c : Fin s, a i c ŌĆó q c
      ```

      Finally, the frame-to-direct-sum composition is checked:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicFrameEmbedding
          {d s : Nat}
          {M R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hq_independent : LinearIndependent Ōäé q)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ŌłĆ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth_M :
            ŌłĆ c (m : M),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R)
          (hdim : Module.finrank Ōäé R <= s) :
          Ōłā (E : R ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
            (hE_inj : Function.Injective E)
            (hE_orth :
              ŌłĆ x : R, ŌłĆ m : M,
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (E x)
                  (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0),
            (ŌłĆ x : R,
              E x Ōłł Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x y : R,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (E x) (E y) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) Ōł¦
            let T := BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
              (d := d) M R E hM hR_orth hE_inj hE_orth
            ŌłĆ x y : Ōåź(M ŌŖö R),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                ((T x : Ōåź(M ŌŖö LinearMap.range E)) :
                  Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                ((T y : Ōåź(M ŌŖö LinearMap.range E)) :
                  Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
      ```

      The right-residual containment theorem in this part of the low-rank route
      is now checked.  The production statement is slightly stronger than the
      earlier proof sketch: it does not require a separate nondegeneracy
      hypothesis on `N`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending
          {d : Nat}
          {N R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hR_le : R Ōēż N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          Ōłā F : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N,
            R Ōēż Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range F.q)
      ```

      This theorem is stronger than the older checked
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_exists`.  A frame of
      globally maximal Witt dimension is not enough unless its span contains the
      actual right residual subspace.  The checked proof extends the given
      totally isotropic subspace to a global maximal totally isotropic subspace
      by the relative-orthogonal quotient/rank-nullity argument, then takes a
      basis of that enlarged subspace.  The final production declarations are:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicSubspaceIn_extending
          {d : Nat}
          {N R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hR_le : R Ōēż N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          Ōłā F : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicSubspaceIn d N,
            R Ōēż F.Q

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending
          {d : Nat}
          {N R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hR_le : R Ōēż N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          Ōłā F : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N,
            R Ōēż Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range F.q)
      ```

      The subtype/relative-orthogonal API in the first lines is now checked in
      `SourceHWMaximalIsotropicFrame.lean` as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmoduleIn`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalIn`, and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmoduleIn_le_relativeOrthogonalIn_of_totallyIsotropic`;
      the kernel condition for quotient descent is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmoduleInRelativeOrthogonal` and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmoduleIn_comap_le_relativeOrthogonalIn_restrict_ker`.
      The quotient form itself is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientForm`, with the `mk`
      computation theorem
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientForm_mk` and symmetry
      theorem `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientForm_isSymm`.
      The dimension bookkeeping for the retyped copies of `R` is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmoduleIn_finrank_eq_of_le`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmoduleInRelativeOrthogonal_finrank_eq_of_le`,
      and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmoduleInRelativeOrthogonal_finrank_eq_of_totallyIsotropic`.
      The quotient radical bridge is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientForm_nondegenerate_of_restrict_ker_le`:
      after the restricted kernel on `Rperp` is shown to lie in the retyped
      `R`, the quotient form is nondegenerate.  The required
      double-orthogonal computation is now checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalIn_eq_orthogonal`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonal_restrict_ker_le_submoduleInRelativeOrthogonal`,
      and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientForm_nondegenerate`.
      The quotient route is now checked.  It uses only ordinary
      finite-dimensional linear algebra, quotient induced forms, and
      rank-nullity.  The quotient-preimage carriers and their ambient
      containment are now checked in
      `SourceHWMaximalIsotropicQuotient.lean` as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimageInRperp`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimageInN`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimage`, and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimage_le`.  The
      quotient-zero containment of the original `R` is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmoduleInRelativeOrthogonal_mkQ_mem_quotientPreimage`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmodule_mem_relativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimage`,
      and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubmodule_le_relativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimage`.
      The quotient-isotropy pullback is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimageInRperp_pair_zero`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimageInN_pair_zero`,
      and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimage_isotropic`,
      using the pointwise quotient-subspace predicate
      `BHW.BilinFormTotallyIsotropicSubspace`.  The maximal-quotient choice is
      checked generically as
      `BHW.bilinForm_maximalTotallyIsotropicSubspace_exists` and specialized as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotient_maximalIsotropicSubspace_exists`.
      The pairing-map half of the rank-nullity comparison is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingToSubmoduleDual`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingToSubmoduleDual_range_le_dualAnnihilator`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingToSubmoduleDual_range_add_inter_finrank_le`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingToSubmoduleDual_mem_ker_iff`, and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingKerToRelativeOrthogonal`.  The quotient-image
      dimension half for an arbitrary isotropic `S Ōēż N` is checked: the generic
      quotient-image API is
      `BHW.linearMapQuotientImageCarrier`,
      `BHW.linearMapToQuotientImageCarrier`,
      `BHW.linearMapToQuotientImageCarrier_surjective`,
      `BHW.linearMapToQuotientImageCarrier_mem_ker_iff`,
      `BHW.linearMapToQuotientImageCarrier_ker_eq_comap`, and
      `BHW.linearMapQuotientImageCarrier_finrank_add_ker`; the maximal-bound
      and arithmetic hooks are checked as
      `BHW.linearMapQuotientImageCarrier_isotropic_of_pair_zero`,
      `BHW.linearMapQuotientImageCarrier_finrank_le_of_maximal_isotropic`,
      `BHW.quotientPreimage_finrank_eq_add`,
      `BHW.quotientPreimageCarrier_finrank_eq_add`, and
      `BHW.quotient_rankNullity_cancel_le`; the concrete
      dependent carrier and isotropy proof are checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingKernelQuotientImage` and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingKernelQuotientImage_isotropic`.  The generic
      quotient-image dimension comparison is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingKerToRelativeOrthogonal_mem_Rin_iff`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingKerToRelativeOrthogonalComapEquivInter`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingKerToRelativeOrthogonal_comap_finrank_eq_inter`,
      and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingKer_genericQuotientImage_finrank_add_inter`;
      the source-coordinate quotient-preimage finrank formula is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiRelativeOrthogonalQuotientPreimage_finrank_eq_add`;
      the maximal-`Qbar` bound and `finrank T` cancellation are checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiPairingKernelQuotientImage_finrank_le_of_maximal`,
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubspace_finrank_le_of_quotient_maximal`, and
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiSubspace_global_maximal_of_quotient_maximal`; and
      the final raw quotient-preimage wrappers are checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicSubspaceIn_extending` and
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending`.
      The remaining producer must not replace the determinant-one residual
      extension with a generic degenerate
      `complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry` theorem, because that
      would hide exactly the compatibility and dimension comparison needed by
      the residual-frame route.

      The remaining determinant-one extension theorem must expose exactly the
      data used by the residual-frame producer.  It extends the checked
      identity-plus-residual isometry on the degenerate partial domain
      `M ŌŖö Rleft` by adjoining compatible hyperbolic dual frames to the residual
      blocks and then building an ambient determinant-one form-preserving
      equivalence.  The theorem statement is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedResidualHyperbolicExtension
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M Rleft Qleft : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hRleft_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : Rleft, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hQleft_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : Qleft, ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hM_disjoint_left : Disjoint M Rleft)
          (hM_disjoint_Qleft : Disjoint M Qleft)
          (T : Ōåź(M ŌŖö Rleft) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōåź(M ŌŖö Qleft))
          (hT_maps_M :
            ŌłĆ m : M,
              ((T Ō¤©(m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                  Submodule.mem_sup_left m.2Ō¤® : Ōåź(M ŌŖö Qleft)) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hT_maps_Rleft_Qleft :
            ŌłĆ x : Rleft,
              ((T Ō¤©(x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                  Submodule.mem_sup_right x.2Ō¤® : Ōåź(M ŌŖö Qleft)) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) Ōłł Qleft)
          (hT_preserves :
            ŌłĆ x y : Ōåź(M ŌŖö Rleft),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                ((T x : Ōåź(M ŌŖö Qleft)) : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                ((T y : Ōåź(M ŌŖö Qleft)) : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          {s : Nat}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hq_independent : LinearIndependent Ōäé q)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ŌłĆ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_mem :
            ŌłĆ c, q c Ōłł BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hQleft_le_Qspan :
            Qleft Ōēż Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)) :
          Ōłā ╬øfix : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            (ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬øfix
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ x : Rleft,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬øfix
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) Ōłł
                Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)
      ```

      The final packaging substep of this theorem is checked as
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedResidualHyperbolicExtension_of_ambientLinearEquiv`:
      once the hyperbolic-basis construction has produced an ambient linear
      equivalence preserving `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner`, with determinant
      `1`, fixing `M`, and sending `Rleft` into the frame span, it packages that
      equivalence as `╬øfix : ComplexLorentzGroup d` and proves exactly the
      `╬øfix_M` and `left_span` fields needed below.

      The nondegenerate-block extension substep is also checked in
      `SourceHWResidualWittExtension.lean`.  The theorem
      `BHW.exists_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalComplementIsometry_of_finrank_eq`
      gives a form-preserving equivalence between orthogonal complements of
      same-finrank nondegenerate subspaces.  The theorem
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_nondegenerateSubspaceEquiv_ambientExtension`
      then extends any pairing-preserving equivalence `T : K ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] L`
      between nondegenerate complex-Minkowski subspaces to an ambient
      pairing-preserving linear equivalence that agrees with `T` on `K`.
      The determinant-one repair for such nondegenerate block extensions is now
      checked as
      `BHW.fullComplexLorentz_det_neg_reflection_fixing_nondegenerate_subspace`
      and
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_nondegenerateSubspaceEquiv_detOne_orbitExtension`:
      if the raw ambient extension has determinant `-1`, a Householder
      reflection fixing the proper target block corrects the determinant without
      changing the prescribed block action.  This proves the proper completed
      target-block branch.  It is not, by itself, the full determinant story:
      the hyperbolic completion of `M ŌŖö Qleft` can be the whole ambient space
      when the residual isotropic subspace is maximal in `MßŚ«`.  In that full
      block case there is no orthogonal-complement reflection left to use; the
      compatible dual-frame construction must instead choose the completed
      hyperbolic block map with determinant `1` directly, or supply an ambient
      determinant-one equivalence to the already checked packaging lemma
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedResidualHyperbolicExtension_of_ambientLinearEquiv`.

      Therefore the checked proper-block consumer is a sufficient branch, not
      the final theorem surface for all cases.  It is recorded as
      `BHW.HWSelectedResidualHyperbolicBlockExtensionData` and
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedResidualHyperbolicExtension_of_blockExtensionData`:
      the data records proper nondegenerate blocks `K,L`, inclusions
      `M Ōēż K` and `Rleft Ōēż K`, a pairing-preserving block equivalence, pointwise
      fixing of `M`, and the image of `Rleft` in `Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)`.
      Once such data is produced, the proper Lorentz element and the required
      `╬øfix_M`/`left_span` fields are mechanical.  The checked def
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_residualFrameExtensionData_of_blockExtensionData`
      packages this proper-branch Lorentz correction with a compatible maximal
      frame and right-residual span containment into
      `BHW.HWLowRankResidualFrameExtensionData`.  The full
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedResidualHyperbolicExtension` producer must
      be a two-branch construction:

      * if the completed target hyperbolic block is proper, produce
        `BHW.HWSelectedResidualHyperbolicBlockExtensionData` and invoke the
        checked proper-block consumer;
      * if the completed block is all of `Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé`, prove from the
        chosen source and target hyperbolic dual bases that the resulting
        ambient block equivalence has determinant `1`, then invoke
        `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedResidualHyperbolicExtension_of_ambientLinearEquiv`
        directly.

      This is the exact remaining determinant-one content; the docs must not
      assume the proper-block branch covers the full-block case.  The
      immediate mechanical full-block bridge is now Lean-shaped separately:

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.fullBlockAmbientEquivOfTopBlock
          (hKtop : K = ŌŖż) (hLtop : L = ŌŖż)
          (Tblock : K ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] L) :
          (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.fullBlockAmbientEquivOfTopBlock_apply
          (hKtop : K = ŌŖż) (hLtop : L = ŌŖż)
          (Tblock : K ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] L)
          (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.fullBlockAmbientEquivOfTopBlock hKtop hLtop Tblock x =
            (Tblock Ō¤©x, by rw [hKtop]; trivialŌ¤® :
              Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedResidualHyperbolicExtension_of_fullBlockData
          (hKtop : K = ŌŖż) (hLtop : L = ŌŖż)
          (Tblock : K ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] L)
          (hT :
            ŌłĆ x y : K,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (Tblock x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (Tblock y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hdet :
            LinearMap.det
              (BHW.fullBlockAmbientEquivOfTopBlock
                hKtop hLtop Tblock).toLinearMap = 1)
          (hK_M : M Ōēż K) (hK_left : Rleft Ōēż K)
          (hT_M :
            ŌłĆ m : M,
              (Tblock Ō¤©(m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé), hK_M m.2Ō¤® :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hT_left_span :
            ŌłĆ x : Rleft,
              (Tblock Ō¤©(x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé), hK_left x.2Ō¤® :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) Ōłł
              Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)) :
          Ōłā ╬øfix : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            (ŌłĆ m : M,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬øfix
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ x : Rleft,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬øfix
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) Ōłł
              Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)
      ```

      This theorem is not the oriented determinant proof; it merely says that
      once the full-block determinant equality is supplied, the old ambient
      determinant-one packaging lemma finishes the branch.  The next checked
      file should add this bridge before the source-oriented volume/family
      determinant theorem is attempted.

      The next checked sublemma is now the hyperbolic basis supply, not another
      wrapper around `╬øfix`.  Its Lean-level target is:

      ```lean
      structure BHW.ComplexMinkowskiIsotropicSubspaceBasisDualFrameData
          (d : Nat)
          (N R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) where
        k : Nat
        frame : Fin k -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        dual : Fin k -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        k_eq : k = Module.finrank Ōäé R
        frame_mem_R : ŌłĆ c, frame c Ōłł R
        frame_span_eq : Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range frame) = R
        frame_mem_N : ŌłĆ c, frame c Ōłł N
        dual_mem_N : ŌłĆ c, dual c Ōłł N
        frame_independent : LinearIndependent Ōäé frame
        frame_pair_zero :
          ŌłĆ c c', BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (frame c) (frame c') = 0
        dual_pair_zero :
          ŌłĆ c c', BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (dual c) (dual c') = 0
        frame_dual :
          ŌłĆ c c',
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (frame c) (dual c') =
              if c = c' then (1 : Ōäé) else 0

      noncomputable def BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicSubspace_basisDualFrameData
          {d : Nat}
          {N R : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hN : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N)
          (hR_le : R Ōēż N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiIsotropicSubspaceBasisDualFrameData d N R
      ```

      Proof sketch: set `k := Module.finrank Ōäé R` and
      `frame c := (Module.finBasis Ōäé R c : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)`.  The basis
      gives `LinearIndependent Ōäé frame` after applying the injective subtype
      map and proves `span range frame = R` by the basis representation formula.
      The total-isotropy hypothesis gives the `frame_pair_zero` field.  The
      inclusion `R Ōēż N` supplies `frame_mem_N`, so the checked theorem
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicFrame_dualFrameIn` returns the isotropic
      dual frame inside `N`.  This is checked in
      `SourceHWResidualHyperbolicCompletion.lean`, together with
      `frame_orthogonal_to_subspace`, `dual_orthogonal_to_subspace`, and
      `selected_sup_hyperbolic_nondegenerate`.  The later block-data theorem
      will apply this packet once to `Rleft` and once to
      `LinearMap.range Eleft`, then build the hyperbolic-block equivalence
      mapping each source basis vector to its target image and each source dual
      to the matching target dual.  The span-level bridge is
      `BHW.linearEquiv_spanOfLinearIndependentFrames`, defined from
      `LinearIndependent.linearCombinationEquiv`, with an application theorem
      proving that the canonical equivalence sends `v i` to `w i`.  The
      checked preservation lemmas
      `BHW.linearEquiv_spanOfLinearIndependentFrames_preserves_isotropic` and
      `BHW.linearEquiv_spanOfLinearIndependentFrames_preserves_dual_pairing`
      show that these span maps preserve the zero pairings on each isotropic
      half and the Kronecker cross-pairing between the two halves.  The target
      side should use
      `BHW.ComplexMinkowskiIsotropicSubspaceBasisDualFrameData.imageFrame_dualFrameIn`:
      given the source basis packet and the injective pairing-preserving
      residual embedding `Eleft`, it builds the target basis as
      `Eleft (sourceFrame c)` and returns a target isotropic dual frame with
      the same `Fin source.k` index type, avoiding a later finrank cast.  The
      direct-sum and block layers are also checked:
      `BHW.directSum_congr_sup_equiv_between` combines source and target
      complementary suprema, `BHW.complexMinkowski_hyperbolicFrameSpanEquiv_between`
      preserves the hyperbolic-span pairing and maps the source frame span into
      the target frame span, and
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicBlockEquiv_between` lifts this
      map across the selected block by the identity on `M`.  The constructor
      `BHW.hw_selectedResidualHyperbolicBlockExtensionData_of_hyperbolicFrames`
      packages these facts into
      `BHW.HWSelectedResidualHyperbolicBlockExtensionData`, and
      `BHW.ComplexMinkowskiIsotropicSubspaceBasisDualFrameData.selectedResidualHyperbolicBlockExtensionData`
      specializes it to the source basis-dual packet.  Thus the remaining
      implementation issue is no longer the `Tblock` itself; it is the final
      proper/full branch split needed to supply `L_proper` for this constructor
      or bypass it with a direct full-ambient determinant-one extension.  The
      finrank gate for this split is checked:
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_submodule_eq_top_of_not_finrank_lt_spacetime`
      turns `┬¼ Module.finrank Ōäé L < d + 1` into `L = ŌŖż`, and
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_submodule_eq_top_of_linearEquiv_full` carries
      fullness back across the block equivalence to the source block.  The
      full-block determinant branch is an oriented-invariant branch, not a
      repair branch: preserving the target maximal isotropic span leaves only
      determinant-one stabilizer freedom (`A` on the span and `(AßĄĆ)Ōü╗┬╣` on a
      dual), so a determinant-`-1` block map cannot be fixed by flipping a
      target basis vector or by a hidden reflection.  The full-block proof must
      show that the oriented source-volume/family invariant forces the
      constructed full ambient block map to have determinant one already.

      Lean-shaped proof of the residual-frame-extension producer after the
      support packet exists:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_residualFrameExtensionData_of_selectedAlignment
          ... (A : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment d n r z w I S) :
          BHW.HWLowRankResidualFrameExtensionData A := by
        rcases
          BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_orthogonalComplement_after_selectedAlignment
            (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (w := w)
