            (I := I) (S := S) A with
          Ō¤©Rleft, Rright, hRleft_eq, hRright_eq,
            hRleft_le_orth, hRright_le_orth, hM_disjoint_left,
            hM_disjoint_right, hRleft_iso, hRright_isoŌ¤®
        let N := BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d A.M
        have hN_nondeg :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
            (d := d) hd A.M_nondeg
        have hRleft_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : Rleft, ŌłĆ m : A.M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 := by
          intro x m
          exact
            (BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_iff
              d A.M (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)).1
              (hRleft_le_orth x.2) m
        have hRright_orth :
            ŌłĆ x : Rright, ŌłĆ m : A.M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 := by
          intro x m
          exact
            (BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_iff
              d A.M (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)).1
              (hRright_le_orth x.2) m
        have hRleft_le : Rleft Ōēż N := hRleft_le_orth
        have hRright_le : Rright Ōēż N := hRright_le_orth
        rcases BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending
            (d := d) (N := N) (R := Rright) hRright_le hRright_iso with
          Ō¤©F, hRright_QŌ¤®
        rcases
          BHW.complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame
            (d := d) hd F hRleft_le hRleft_iso with
          Ō¤©Eleft, hEleft_inj, hEleft_Q, hEleft_preservesŌ¤®
        let Qspan : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range F.q)
        let Qleft : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          LinearMap.range Eleft
        have hQleft_le_Qspan : Qleft Ōēż Qspan := by
          rintro v Ō¤©x, rflŌ¤®
          exact hEleft_Q x
        have hEleft_orth_M :
            ŌłĆ x : Rleft, ŌłĆ m : A.M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (Eleft x) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 := by
          intro x m
          exact
            BHW.span_frame_orthogonal_to_subspace
              (d := d)
              (M := A.M)
              (q := F.q)
              (by
                intro c m
                exact F.q_mem c m)
              (hEleft_Q x) m
        have hQleft_orth_M :
            ŌłĆ x : Qleft, ŌłĆ m : A.M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 := by
          intro x m
          rcases x.2 with Ō¤©r, hrŌ¤®
          simpa [hr] using hEleft_orth_M r m
        have hM_disjoint_Qleft : Disjoint A.M Qleft :=
          BHW.complexMinkowski_disjoint_of_nondegenerate_orthogonal
            A.M_nondeg hQleft_orth_M
        let Domain : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) := A.M ŌŖö Rleft
        let Target : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) := A.M ŌŖö Qleft
        let T : ŌåźDomain ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] ŌåźTarget :=
          BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
            (d := d) A.M Rleft Eleft A.M_nondeg hRleft_orth
            hEleft_inj hEleft_orth_M
        have hT_maps_M :
            ŌłĆ m : A.M,
              ((T Ō¤©(m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                  Submodule.mem_sup_left m.2Ō¤® : ŌåźTarget) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) := by
          intro m
          exact
            BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_maps_left
              (d := d) A.M_nondeg hRleft_orth hEleft_inj
              hEleft_orth_M m
        have hT_maps_Rleft_Qleft :
            ŌłĆ x : Rleft,
              ((T Ō¤©(x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                  Submodule.mem_sup_right x.2Ō¤® : ŌåźTarget) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) Ōłł Qleft := by
          intro x
          have hx :=
            BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_maps_right
              (d := d) A.M_nondeg hRleft_orth hEleft_inj
              hEleft_orth_M x
          exact Ō¤©x, by simpa [Qleft] using (congrArg Subtype.val hx).symmŌ¤®
        have hT_preserves :
            ŌłĆ x y : ŌåźDomain,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                ((T x : ŌåźTarget) : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                ((T y : ŌåźTarget) : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_preserves
            (d := d) A.M_nondeg hRleft_orth hEleft_inj
            hEleft_orth_M hEleft_preserves
        rcases BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedResidualHyperbolicExtension
            (d := d) hd (M := A.M) (Rleft := Rleft)
            (Qleft := Qleft) A.M_nondeg hRleft_orth hQleft_orth_M
            hM_disjoint_left hM_disjoint_Qleft T hT_maps_M
            hT_maps_Rleft_Qleft hT_preserves F.q_independent
            F.q_pair_zero F.q_mem hQleft_le_Qspan with
          Ō¤©╬øfix, h╬øfix_M, h╬øfix_left_QŌ¤®
        refine
          { F := F
            ╬øfix := ╬øfix
            ╬øfix_M := h╬øfix_M
            left_span := ?_
            right_span := ?_ }
        ┬Ę intro i
          exact h╬øfix_left_Q
            Ō¤©A.leftResidual i, by
              simpa [hRleft_eq] using Submodule.subset_span Ō¤©i, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®
        ┬Ę intro i
          exact hRright_Q
            Ō¤©A.rightResidual i, by
              simpa [hRright_eq] using Submodule.subset_span Ō¤©i, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®
      ```

      The checked theorem
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_residualAlignmentData_of_frameExtensionData` converts this
      packet into `BHW.HWLowRankResidualAlignmentData`, deriving `q_orth_M`
      from `F.q_mem` and `mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_iff`.  Then
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_commonIsotropicFrameData_of_residualAlignmentData`
      extracts coefficient functions from `left_span` and `right_span` and
      proves the two expansion equations by expanding
      `complexLorentzAction_mul`, `A.left_decomp`, `A.right_decomp`, and
      `╬øfix_M`.

      The pseudocode uses the proof-local helper
      `directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding` and its `preserves` /
      `maps_right` projections.  These are not new route assumptions: they are
      the finite linear map on `A.M ŌŖö Rleft` which is the identity on `A.M`
      and `Eleft` on `Rleft`, with codomain `A.M ŌŖö LinearMap.range Eleft`, not the
      whole maximal frame span.  The direct-sum decomposition is justified by
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_disjoint_of_nondegenerate_orthogonal`: an element
      in `A.M Ōł® Rleft` lies in `A.M` and is orthogonal to all of `A.M`, hence
      is zero by nondegeneracy of `A.M`; the same proof applies to
      `A.M Ōł® LinearMap.range Eleft` after
      `BHW.span_frame_orthogonal_to_subspace` transports frame-span
      orthogonality to `A.M`.  The proof that the map preserves the bilinear
      form is exactly the block calculation using nondegeneracy of `A.M`,
      orthogonality of `Rleft` and the frame span to `A.M`, and total isotropy
      of both residual blocks.  If the Lean implementation avoids naming this
      helper, it must still perform the same disjointness and block-pairing
      calculation before applying the determinant-one hyperbolic-basis
      residual extension.

      theorem BHW.exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame
          {d s : Nat}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hv : v Ōłł Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)) :
          Ōłā a : Fin s -> Ōäé, v = Ōłæ c : Fin s, a c ŌĆó q c

      /-- After the selected nondegenerate spans have been matched by Witt
      extension, the low-rank discrepancy is contained in a finite totally
      isotropic residual frame in the orthogonal complement.  For `d = 3` this
      frame has length at most one, recovering Hall-Wightman's displayed
      vector `w`; for general `d` the frame length may be larger. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_alignedResidualFrame_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz : z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzLow : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          Ōłā (M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
            (╬ø0 : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
            (╬Š : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (s : Nat)
            (aLeft aRight : Fin n -> Fin s -> Ōäé)
            (q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ i, ╬Š i Ōłł M) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ c (m : M),
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
                  0) Ōł¦
              LinearIndependent Ōäé q Ōł¦
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø0 z =
                (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, aLeft i c * q c ╬╝) Ōł¦
              w = (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, aRight i c * q c ╬╝) Ōł¦
              ╬Š Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ c c',
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ c i,
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (╬Š i) = 0)

      /-- Witt-complement input used by the low-rank contraction.  A finite
      totally isotropic residual frame in the orthogonal complement of the
      common base admits a dual isotropic frame, with the stated pairings,
      after possibly discarding zero residual directions in the normal-form
      construction.  This is the dimension-general replacement for the
      one-null-vector boost in the printed four-dimensional proof. -/
	      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicDualFrame_of_residualFrame
	          {n s : Nat}
	          {╬Š : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
	          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hM_nondeg :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (h╬Š_mem : ŌłĆ i, ╬Š i Ōłł M)
          (hq_orth_M :
            ŌłĆ c (m : M),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ŌłĆ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_independent :
            LinearIndependent Ōäé q) :
          Ōłā qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
            (ŌłĆ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : Ōäé) else 0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ c (m : M),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (qDual c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ c i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (╬Š i) = 0)

	      The dual-frame theorem must be proved through the same finite
	      nondegenerate-space machinery as the Witt packet, not by choosing
	      arbitrary ambient dual vectors.  The implementation-level support
	      theorem is split into two pieces.  First construct a raw dual frame
	      `y` inside the nondegenerate orthogonal complement, with
	      `Ō¤¬q c, y c'Ō¤½ = ╬┤ c c'`.  Then apply the checked cleanup theorem below,
	      which subtracts half the raw-dual Gram matrix along the totally
	      isotropic `q`-frame and makes the dual frame itself totally isotropic
	      without changing the `q`-dual pairings:

	      ```lean
	      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicDualFrame_of_rawDualFrame
	          {d s : Nat}
	          {N : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
	          {q y : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
	          (hq_mem : ŌłĆ c, q c Ōłł N)
	          (hy_mem : ŌłĆ c, y c Ōłł N)
	          (hq_pair_zero :
	            ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
	          (hy_dual :
	            ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (y c') =
	                if c = c' then (1 : Ōäé) else 0) :
	          Ōłā qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
	            (ŌłĆ c, qDual c Ōłł N) Ōł¦
	            (ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0) Ōł¦
	            ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
	                if c = c' then (1 : Ōäé) else 0
	      ```

	      The remaining raw-dual producer has the following target:

	      ```lean
	      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_rawDualFrameIn
	          {d s : Nat}
	          {N : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
	          (hN :
	            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N)
	          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
	          (hq_mem : ŌłĆ c, q c Ōłł N)
	          (hq_independent : LinearIndependent Ōäé q)
	          :
	          Ōłā y : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
	            (ŌłĆ c, y c Ōłł N) Ōł¦
	            ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (y c') =
	                if c = c' then (1 : Ōäé) else 0

	      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicFrame_dualFrameIn
	          {d s : Nat}
	          {N : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
	          (hN :
	            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N)
	          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
	          (hq_mem : ŌłĆ c, q c Ōłł N)
	          (hq_independent : LinearIndependent Ōäé q)
	          (hq_pair_zero :
	            ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0) :
	          Ōłā qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
	            (ŌłĆ c, qDual c Ōłł N) Ōł¦
	            (ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0) Ōł¦
	            (ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
	                if c = c' then (1 : Ōäé) else 0)
	      ```

	      Proof transcript for `complexMinkowski_isotropicFrame_dualFrameIn`:
	      work with the restricted bilinear form on `N`.  Coerce
	      `q` to `qN : Fin s -> N`; linear independence is obtained from
	      `hq_independent` by applying `Fintype.linearIndependent_iff` and then
	      coercing finite sums through `N.subtype`.  Extend `qN` to a basis of
	      `N`, or equivalently extend the coordinate functionals on
	      `Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range qN)` to `Module.Dual Ōäé N` using
	      `Submodule.dualLift`.  Nondegeneracy of `(sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d).restrict N`
	      gives representatives `y c : N` for those extended functionals via
	      `LinearMap.BilinForm.toDual.symm`, so
	      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (y c') = if c = c' then 1 else 0`.
	      Applying
	      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicDualFrame_of_rawDualFrame` inside `N`
	      then replaces `y` by
	      `qDual c = y c - (2 : Ōäé)Ōü╗┬╣ ŌĆó Ōłæ k, Ō¤¬y c, y kŌ¤½ ŌĆó q k`.  The checked
	      cleanup theorem proves `qDual_mem`, the zero `qDual`-`qDual` block, and
	      the unchanged Kronecker `q`-`qDual` block.

	      To apply this theorem to
	      `complexMinkowski_isotropicDualFrame_of_residualFrame`, take
	      `N := BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M`.  The previous
	      support theorem gives nondegeneracy of `N`, `hq_orth_M` gives
	      `hq_mem : ŌłĆ c, q c Ōłł N`, and the returned `qDual_mem` gives
	      `qDual_orth` because membership in `N` is precisely orthogonality to
	      `M`; since every `╬Š i Ōłł M`, this yields the displayed
	      `qDual_orth` field.  This wrapper is now checked in
	      `SourceHWCommonResidualFrame.lean`; no independent choice of dual
	      vectors outside the selected-span orthogonal complement is permitted.

	      /-- The checked null boost fixing the selected span and contracting
	      every vector in the totally isotropic residual frame.  The proof
	      chooses a dual isotropic frame `qDual`, forms the nondegenerate
	      subspace `K = M ŌŖö span q ŌŖö span qDual`, scales `q` by `exp (-t)` and
	      `qDual` by `exp t`, extends the resulting isometry by the identity on
	      `KßŚ«`, and packages the result as a determinant-one complex Lorentz
	      element.  This route deliberately avoids a general degenerate
	      `complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry`: the low-rank boost
	      is extended from a nondegenerate hyperbolic block. -/
	      noncomputable def BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContractionFamily
	          {s : Nat}
	          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
	          (hM :
	            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
	          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
	          {qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
	          (hq_orth_M :
	            ŌłĆ c (m : M),
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
	                (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
	          (hqDual_orth_M :
	            ŌłĆ c (m : M),
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
	                (qDual c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0)
	          (hq_pair_zero :
	            ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
	          (hqDual_pair_zero :
	            ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0)
	          (hq_dual :
	            ŌłĆ c c',
	              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
	                if c = c' then (1 : Ōäé) else 0)
	          (t : ŌäØ) :
	          ComplexLorentzGroup d

	      Checked action fields:
	      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContractionFamily_vectorAction`
	      identifies the packaged Lorentz vector action with the global linear
	      equivalence; `_maps_M` fixes every `m : M`; `_maps_q` scales `q c` by
	      `((Real.exp (-t) : ŌäØ) : Ōäé)`; and `_maps_qDual` scales `qDual c` by
	      `((Real.exp t : ŌäØ) : Ōäé)`.  Therefore any selected base family
	      `╬Š : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé` with `ŌłĆ i, ╬Š i Ōłł M` is fixed
	      pointwise by applying `_maps_M` to each `╬Š i`.

      /-- Hall-Wightman's second and third remarks after Lemma 2,
      finite-frame version: if one coefficient choice along a finite totally
      isotropic frame orthogonal to the base lies in the ordinary extended
      tube, then the zero coefficient choice does too.  This does not assert
      any `ForwardTube` conclusion for the original base. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_base_mem_extendedTube_of_endpoint
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {╬Š : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> Ōäé}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h╬Šq :
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, a i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ŌłĆ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ŌłĆ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (╬Š i) = 0) :
          ╬Š Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n

      /-- Hall-Wightman's first consequence of the second and third remarks
      after Lemma 2, finite-frame version.  Once one coefficient tuple along a
      totally isotropic frame lies in the ordinary extended tube, every
      coefficient tuple along the same frame lies in the ordinary extended
      tube, including the zero tuple.  The dual frame used later for the
      null-boost contraction is not part of this membership theorem. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {╬Š : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> Ōäé}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h╬Šq :
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, a i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ŌłĆ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ŌłĆ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (╬Š i) = 0)
          :
          ╬Š Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
          ŌłĆ b : Fin n -> Fin s -> Ōäé,
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, b i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n

	      Checked block-level declarations:
	      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContraction` is the linear
	      equivalence of the submodule
	      `M ŌŖö (Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q) ŌŖö
	      Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range qDual))`.  The theorems
	      `_maps_M`, `_maps_q`, and `_maps_qDual` give its action on the three
	      summands; `_preserves` proves preservation of the complex Minkowski
	      form on this nondegenerate block; and `_det` proves
	      `LinearMap.det T.toLinearMap = 1`.

	      Checked global declarations:
	      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContraction_globalLinearEquiv`
	      is the ambient linear equivalence obtained by splitting the
	      nondegenerate block from its orthogonal complement and extending by
	      identity.  The theorems `_apply_block`, `_maps_M`, `_maps_q`, and
	      `_maps_qDual` read off its action; `_preserves` proves form
	      preservation on the ambient space; `_det` proves determinant one; and
	      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContractionFamily` packages
	      this global equivalence as a `ComplexLorentzGroup d`.

      Proof transcript for the contraction family.  For each fixed `t`, put
      `Q := Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)`, `Qd := Submodule.span Ōäé
      (Set.range qDual)`, and let `K := M ŌŖö Q ŌŖö Qd` be the submodule generated
      by the selected nondegenerate block and the hyperbolic residual pair.
      The duality equations imply:

      1. `q` is linearly independent, because pairing
         `Ōłæ c, a c ŌĆó q c = 0` with `qDual c0` gives `a c0 = 0`.
      2. `qDual` is linearly independent by the same argument, using symmetry
         of `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner`.
      3. `Q Ōł® M = ŌŖź`: an element of `Q Ōł® M` pairs trivially with every
         `qDual c` by `hqDual_orth_M`, while the duality equation reads off
         its `q`-coordinates.
      4. `Qd Ōł® (M ŌŖö Q) = ŌŖź`: pair with every `m : M` using
         `hqDual_orth_M`, and pair with every `q c` using
         `hq_pair_zero`, `hq_dual`, and symmetry of the bilinear form.

      These four facts give a direct-sum coordinate decomposition of the
      hyperbolic block `K`.  The form on `K` is nondegenerate: it is the
      orthogonal direct sum of the already nondegenerate selected block `M`
      and the hyperbolic pair `Q ŌŖĢ Qd`, whose Gram matrix is
      `0 I; I 0` in the `q/qDual` bases.
      Define `T_t` on `K` by
      `m + qpart + qdpart Ōå” m + (Real.exp (-t) : Ōäé) ŌĆó qpart +
      (Real.exp t : Ōäé) ŌĆó qdpart`.  The displayed pairings show that `T_t`
      preserves the complex Minkowski form on all pairs of generators:
      the `M`-`M` block is fixed, the `M`-`Q` and `M`-`Qd` blocks are zero,
      the `Q`-`Q` and `Qd`-`Qd` blocks are zero, and the `Q`-`Qd` block is
      multiplied by `Real.exp (-t) * Real.exp t = 1`.  The inverse is the
      same construction with `-t`, so this is a linear equivalence
      `K ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] K`.  Its determinant is
      `(Real.exp (-t) : Ōäé) ^ s * (Real.exp t : Ōäé) ^ s = 1`, because `M` is
      fixed and the two residual diagonal blocks have reciprocal determinants.
      This proves `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContraction` and
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContraction_det`.

      Next use nondegeneracy of `K` and
      `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal` to
      split the ambient complex Minkowski space as `K ŌŖö KßŚ«`.  Extend `T_t` by
      the identity on `KßŚ«` using `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl`.  The product
      map preserves the bilinear form by the same block calculation, and its
      determinant is still `1` because the orthogonal-complement factor is the
      identity.  This gives
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContraction_globalLinearEquiv`
      and `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContraction_globalLinearEquiv_det`.

      Finally package the global determinant-one form-preserving linear
      equivalence by
      `BHW.complexLorentzGroupOfLinearEquivPreservesInner`, and read its
      coordinate action with
      `BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction_ofLinearEquivPreservesInner`.  Since
      each `╬Š i Ōłł M`, the selected block is fixed; `q` and `qDual` are scaled
      by construction.  No continuity of the chosen map `t Ōå” contract t` is
      used later.  All low-rank limits come from the checked finite helper
      `BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg_base`, the scalar limit
      `Real.tendsto_exp_neg_atTop_nhds_zero`, the continuous coercion
      `ŌäØ -> Ōäé`, and finite-sum continuity.

      Proof transcript for the tube-stability theorems.  The base-membership
      theorem is Hall-Wightman's second remark after Lemma 2.  Its conclusion
      is deliberately `ExtendedTube`, not `ForwardTube`: in complex null-frame
      coordinates, deleting the null residual can move the imaginary part of
      a forward-tube representative to the forward-cone boundary.  The proof
      is still finite-dimensional.  First transport the endpoint to a
      forward-tube representative, then handle one null residual direction at
      a time by Hall-Wightman's normal-form and scaling remarks.  The precise
      Hall-Wightman content is subtler than "delete the residual and stay in
      the forward tube": that
      statement is false.  If `q = e1 + i e2`, `╬Ę = T e0` is future timelike,
      and `╬Š = i╬Ę + iR q` with `R > T`, then `╬Š - iR q` is in the one-point
      forward tube and `q` is null and orthogonal to `╬Š`, but `Im ╬Š =
      T e0 + R e1` is not timelike.  Hall-Wightman's second remark instead
      rewrites a forward-tube point with a null residual as
      `╬Ę + ╬▓ q`, where `╬Ę` is another forward-tube point and the null vector
      has the standard real spacelike normal form relative to `╬Ę`.  The third
      remark then shows that all coefficient choices along this null vector
      are in the ordinary extended tube.  This is exactly the invariant
      conclusion needed downstream.

      The base-membership theorem should therefore be implemented through
      Hall-Wightman's second and third remarks in one null direction, finite
      coefficient replacement, and then Lorentz transport.  The dual frame is
      not part of this coefficient-freedom theorem and enters only later, in
      the simultaneous contraction family used for the singular analytic
      limit.  The nontrivial Hall-Wightman normal-form lemmas below assume
      `[NeZero n]`: for `n = 0` the finite-frame coefficient theorem is
      handled separately by the unique empty configuration, and no claim is
      made that an arbitrary complex null vector has spacelike normal form
      from vacuous tube data.

      Implementation ladder for this block.  The first Lean file is a small
      API bridge, not the Hall-Wightman geometry itself.  It introduces only
      consequences of existing definitions and checked Lorentz invariance:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_add
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø
              (fun i ╬╝ => z i ╬╝ + w i ╬╝) =
            fun i ╬╝ =>
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z i ╬╝ +
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø w i ╬╝

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_smul_vector
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (c : Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø
              (fun i ╬╝ => c i * v ╬╝) =
            fun i ╬╝ => c i * BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø v ╬╝

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_cancel_left
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h :
            BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z =
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø w) :
          z = w

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_complexLorentzVectorAction
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø u)
              (BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø v) =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d u v

      theorem BHW.tendsto_singleResidual_exp_neg_zero
          (b : Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ŌäØ =>
              fun i ╬╝ => (Real.exp (-t) : Ōäé) * b i * q ╬╝)
            atTop
            (nhds (0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
      ```

      These are now checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceHWTubeCoefficient.lean`.  The same file also
      introduces the production definitions
      `BHW.HWNullResidualNormalForm` and `BHW.realNullCollinear`, and checks
      the first elementary null-split helpers:
      `BHW.complexNull_iff_real_imag_equal_orthogonal`,
      `BHW.real_imag_nonzero_of_complex_ne_zero`,
      `BHW.realNullCollinear_commonVector`,
      `BHW.realNullCollinear_scalar_ne_zero_of_nonzero_direction`, and
      `BHW.sum_sq_eq_zero`.  The same checked elementary cone layer now also
      includes `BHW.abs_sum_mul_le_sqrt_mul_sqrt`,
      `BHW.realNull_abs_time_eq_spatial_norm`,
      `BHW.realNullCollinear_orthogonal_forwardTube_differences`,
      `BHW.realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone`, and
      `BHW.nonzero_realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone_differences`.
      The time-orientation helper
      `BHW.forwardCone_timeOrientation_constant_on_timelike_segment` is
      checked there as well.  The null-plane relation
      `BHW.hw_nullPlane_orthogonality_relation` is also checked there, with
      the corrected required hypothesis `╬▒ ŌēĀ 0`; so is
      `BHW.imag_difference_orthogonal_realVector`, and
      `BHW.imag_nullNormalForm_coefficients`.  The equation-(41) cone
      removal theorem `BHW.forwardCone_remove_spacelikeOrthogonal_twoPlane`
      and the tube part of the second remark
      `BHW.hw_secondRemark_eta_mem_forwardTube` are checked there as well.
      The proofs are purely
      definitional/topological: they use `complexLorentzVectorAction_add`,
      `complexLorentzVectorAction_smul`, `complexLorentzAction_inv`,
      `sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction` specialized to two source
      labels, and `Real.tendsto_exp_atBot.comp
      tendsto_neg_atTop_atBot` with finite product continuity.  After this
      bridge, the first genuine Hall-Wightman theorem is
      `BHW.hw_secondRemark_forwardTube_singleNullResidual_normalForm`; its
      proof is decomposed below into the real/imaginary null split,
      lightlike-collinear exclusion, spacelike two-plane normalization,
      coefficient projection, and the equation-(41) cone-removal lemma.

      ```lean
      /-- Normal form used in Hall-Wightman's second and third remarks after
      Lemma 2.  Either `q = 0`, or `q = ╬▒ (u + i v)` with `u,v` real
      spacelike orthonormal vectors and every source vector `╬Ę i` orthogonal
      to both `u` and `v`.  With the repo's mostly-positive convention the
      spacelike normalizations are `u.u = 1` and `v.v = 1`; Hall-Wightman's
      printed `-1` signs come from the opposite convention. -/
      def BHW.HWNullResidualNormalForm
          (d n : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (╬Ę : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        q = 0 Ōł©
        Ōłā (╬▒ : Ōäé) (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ),
          ╬▒ ŌēĀ 0 Ōł¦
          q = fun ╬╝ => ╬▒ * ((u ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) Ōł¦
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1 Ōł¦
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1 Ōł¦
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0 Ōł¦
          (ŌłĆ i,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)) (╬Ę i) = 0) Ōł¦
          (ŌłĆ i,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) (╬Ę i) = 0)

      def BHW.realNullCollinear
          (d : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (x y : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ) : Prop :=
        Ōłā (Ōäō : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ) (a b : ŌäØ),
          Ōäō ŌēĀ 0 Ōł¦
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Ōäō Ōäō = 0 Ōł¦
          x = fun ╬╝ => a * Ōäō ╬╝ Ōł¦
          y = fun ╬╝ => b * Ōäō ╬╝

      theorem BHW.complexNull_iff_real_imag_equal_orthogonal
          [NeZero d]
          (q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hq : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q q = 0) :
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).re) (fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).re) =
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).im) (fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).im) Ōł¦
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).re) (fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).im) = 0

      theorem BHW.real_imag_nonzero_of_complex_ne_zero
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hq : q ŌēĀ 0) :
          (fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).re) ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© (fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).im) ŌēĀ 0

      theorem BHW.realNullCollinear_commonVector
          [NeZero d]
          {x y : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (hcoll : BHW.realNullCollinear d x y)
          (hxy_ne : x ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© y ŌēĀ 0) :
          Ōłā Ōäō : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ,
            Ōäō ŌēĀ 0 Ōł¦
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Ōäō Ōäō = 0 Ōł¦
            (Ōłā a : ŌäØ, x = fun ╬╝ => a * Ōäō ╬╝) Ōł¦
            (Ōłā b : ŌäØ, y = fun ╬╝ => b * Ōäō ╬╝)

      theorem BHW.realNullCollinear_scalar_ne_zero_of_nonzero_direction
          {qre qim Ōäō : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          {a b : ŌäØ}
          (hqre : qre = fun ╬╝ => a * Ōäō ╬╝)
          (hqim : qim = fun ╬╝ => b * Ōäō ╬╝)
          (hq_ne : qre ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© qim ŌēĀ 0) :
          (a : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (b : Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0 := by
        intro h
        have ha : a = 0 := by
          exact_mod_cast congrArg Complex.re h
        have hb : b = 0 := by
          have := congrArg Complex.im h
          simpa [ha] using this
        rcases hq_ne with hqre_ne | hqim_ne
        ┬Ę exact hqre_ne (by ext ╬╝; simp [hqre, ha])
        ┬Ę exact hqim_ne (by ext ╬╝; simp [hqim, hb])

      theorem BHW.sum_sq_eq_zero
          {╬╣ : Type} [Fintype ╬╣]
          (f : ╬╣ -> ŌäØ)
          (h : Ōłæ i, f i ^ 2 = 0) :
          ŌłĆ i, f i = 0 := by
        intro i
        exact
          sq_eq_zero_iff.mp
            (Finset.sum_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg
              (fun j _ => sq_nonneg (f j))).mp h i (Finset.mem_univ i)

      theorem BHW.realNull_abs_time_eq_spatial_norm
          [NeZero d]
          {Ōäō : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (hŌäō_null : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Ōäō Ōäō = 0) :
          |Ōäō 0| = Real.sqrt (Ōłæ i : Fin d, Ōäō i.succ ^ 2) := by
        have hsq :
            Ōäō 0 ^ 2 = Ōłæ i : Fin d, Ōäō i.succ ^ 2 := by
          simpa [MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner, BHW.minkowski_sum_decomp]
            using hŌäō_null
        rw [ŌåÉ Real.sqrt_sq_eq_abs, hsq]
        exact (Real.sqrt_sq_eq_abs _).symm

      theorem BHW.realNullCollinear_orthogonal_forwardTube_differences
          [NeZero d]
          {╬Č : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {qre qim Ōäō : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (hq_orth :
            ŌłĆ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => (qre ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (qim ╬╝ : Ōäé))
                (╬Č i) = 0)
          (hq_ne : qre ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© qim ŌēĀ 0)
          (hqre : Ōłā a : ŌäØ, qre = fun ╬╝ => a * Ōäō ╬╝)
          (hqim : Ōłā b : ŌäØ, qim = fun ╬╝ => b * Ōäō ╬╝) :
          ŌłĆ k : Fin n,
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Ōäō
              (fun ╬╝ =>
                ((╬Č k ╬╝) -
                  (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                   else ╬Č Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤® ╬╝)).im) = 0 := by
        rcases hqre with Ō¤©a, hqreŌ¤®
        rcases hqim with Ō¤©b, hqimŌ¤®
        have hscalar_ne : (a : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (b : Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0 :=
          BHW.realNullCollinear_scalar_ne_zero_of_nonzero_direction
            (qre := qre) (qim := qim) (Ōäō := Ōäō) hqre hqim hq_ne
        have hŌäō_orth_point :
            ŌłĆ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => (Ōäō ╬╝ : Ōäé)) (╬Č i) = 0 := by
          intro i
          have hqi := hq_orth i
          have hfactor :
              (fun ╬╝ => (qre ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (qim ╬╝ : Ōäé)) =
              fun ╬╝ => ((a : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (b : Ōäé)) * (Ōäō ╬╝ : Ōäé) := by
            ext ╬╝
            simp [hqre, hqim, mul_add, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
          rw [hfactor,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_left] at hqi
          exact mul_eq_zero.mp hqi |>.resolve_left hscalar_ne
        intro k
        by_cases hk : k.val = 0
        ┬Ę simpa [hk] using
            congrArg Complex.im (hŌäō_orth_point k)
        ┬Ę have hprev :=
            hŌäō_orth_point Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤®
          have hcurr := hŌäō_orth_point k
          have hdiff :
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => (Ōäō ╬╝ : Ōäé))
                (fun ╬╝ => ╬Č k ╬╝ - ╬Č Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤® ╬╝) = 0 := by
            simpa [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right, hcurr, hprev]
          simpa [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner,
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner, hk] using
            congrArg Complex.im hdiff

      theorem BHW.realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone
          [NeZero d]
          {Ōäō ╬Ę : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (h╬Ę : BHW.InOpenForwardCone d ╬Ę)
          (hŌäō_ne : Ōäō ŌēĀ 0)
          (hŌäō_null : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Ōäō Ōäō = 0) :
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Ōäō ╬Ę ŌēĀ 0 := by
        have h╬Ę_gap := BHW.inOpenForwardCone_iff_timePos_gapPos.mp h╬Ę
        have h╬Ę_spatial_lt :
            Real.sqrt (Ōłæ i : Fin d, ╬Ę i.succ ^ 2) < ╬Ę 0 :=
          BHW.spatial_norm_lt_time h╬Ę
        have hŌäō_time_abs :
            |Ōäō 0| = Real.sqrt (Ōłæ i : Fin d, Ōäō i.succ ^ 2) :=
          BHW.realNull_abs_time_eq_spatial_norm (d := d) hŌäō_null
        have hŌäō_time_ne : Ōäō 0 ŌēĀ 0 := by
          intro h0
          have hsp0 : Ōłæ i : Fin d, Ōäō i.succ ^ 2 = 0 := by
            simpa [h0, BHW.minkowski_sum_decomp] using hŌäō_null
          have hsp_each : ŌłĆ i : Fin d, Ōäō i.succ = 0 :=
            BHW.sum_sq_eq_zero.mp hsp0
          apply hŌäō_ne
          ext ╬╝
          cases ╬╝ using Fin.cases with
          | zero => exact h0
          | succ i => exact hsp_each i
        intro horth
        have hdot_eq :
            (Ōłæ i : Fin d, Ōäō i.succ * ╬Ę i.succ) = Ōäō 0 * ╬Ę 0 := by
          simpa [MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner,
            BHW.minkowski_sum_decomp] using horth
        have hcs :
            |Ōłæ i : Fin d, Ōäō i.succ * ╬Ę i.succ| <=
              Real.sqrt (Ōłæ i : Fin d, Ōäō i.succ ^ 2) *
              Real.sqrt (Ōłæ i : Fin d, ╬Ę i.succ ^ 2) :=
          Real.abs_sum_mul_le_sqrt_mul_sqrt _ _ _
        have habs_eq :
            |Ōłæ i : Fin d, Ōäō i.succ * ╬Ę i.succ| = |Ōäō 0| * ╬Ę 0 := by
          rw [hdot_eq, abs_mul, abs_of_pos h╬Ę.1]
        have hle :
            |Ōäō 0| * ╬Ę 0 <=
              |Ōäō 0| *
              Real.sqrt (Ōłæ i : Fin d, ╬Ę i.succ ^ 2) := by
          simpa [habs_eq, hŌäō_time_abs] using hcs
        exact
          not_le_of_gt h╬Ę_spatial_lt
            (le_of_mul_le_mul_left hle (abs_pos.mpr hŌäō_time_ne))

      theorem BHW.nonzero_realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone_differences
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          {╬Č : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {Ōäō : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (h╬Č : ╬Č Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hŌäō_ne : Ōäō ŌēĀ 0)
          (hŌäō_null : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Ōäō Ōäō = 0)
          (hŌäō_orth :
            ŌłĆ k : Fin n,
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Ōäō
                (fun ╬╝ =>
                  ((╬Č k ╬╝) -
                    (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                     else ╬Č Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤® ╬╝)).im) = 0) :
          False := by
        let k : Fin n := 0
        have hkFT : BHW.InOpenForwardCone d
            (fun ╬╝ =>
              ((╬Č k ╬╝) -
                (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                 else ╬Č Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤® ╬╝)).im) :=
          h╬Č k
        exact
          BHW.realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone
            (d := d) hkFT hŌäō_ne hŌäō_null (hŌäō_orth k)

      theorem BHW.realImag_null_not_collinear_to_spacelike_orthonormal
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {qre qim : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (h_ne : qre ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© qim ŌēĀ 0)
          (hnull_real :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qre qre =
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qim qim Ōł¦
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qre qim = 0)
          (hnot_collinear : ┬¼ BHW.realNullCollinear d qre qim) :
          Ōłā (╬▒ : Ōäé) (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ),
            ╬▒ ŌēĀ 0 Ōł¦
            (fun ╬╝ => (qre ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (qim ╬╝ : Ōäé)) =
              fun ╬╝ => ╬▒ * ((u ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) Ōł¦
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1 Ōł¦
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1 Ōł¦
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0

      Lean proof plan for
      `BHW.realImag_null_not_collinear_to_spacelike_orthonormal`.
      Set
      `A := MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qre qre`; then
      `hnull_real.1` rewrites `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qim qim`
      to `A`, and `hnull_real.2` gives the orthogonality.  The proof is the
      Lorentz-index-one dichotomy:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.realNull_orthogonal_collinear
          [NeZero d]
          {x y : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (hx_null : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d x x = 0)
          (hy_null : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d y y = 0)
          (hxy : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d x y = 0)
          (hne : x ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© y ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.realNullCollinear d x y

      theorem BHW.real_orthogonal_equalNorm_not_collinear_positive
          [NeZero d]
          {x y : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (hne : x ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© y ŌēĀ 0)
          (heq :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d x x =
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d y y)
          (hxy : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d x y = 0)
          (hnot : ┬¼ BHW.realNullCollinear d x y) :
          0 < MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d x x
      ```

      Proof of `realNull_orthogonal_collinear`: use
      `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_decomp` on `x.x`, `y.y`, and `x.y`.
      The null equations give
      `|x 0| = sqrt (Ōłæ i, x_i^2)` and
      `|y 0| = sqrt (Ōłæ i, y_i^2)` via the checked
      `BHW.realNull_abs_time_eq_spatial_norm`; the orthogonality equation
      gives `Ōłæ i, x_i y_i = x 0 * y 0`.  Apply the checked finite
      Cauchy-Schwarz inequality to the spatial parts.  Equality in
      Cauchy-Schwarz is then forced.  Use the standard finite-dimensional
      equality case, exposed as

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.real_cauchy_eq_collinear_of_abs_dot_eq_norms
          {╬╣ : Type} [Fintype ╬╣]
          {x y : ╬╣ -> ŌäØ}
          (heq :
            |Ōłæ i, x i * y i| =
              Real.sqrt (Ōłæ i, x i ^ 2) *
                Real.sqrt (Ōłæ i, y i ^ 2)) :
          Ōłā a b : ŌäØ,
            (a ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© b ŌēĀ 0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ i, b * x i = a * y i)
      ```

      This equality case is not a black-box appeal to Hilbert-space
      equality.  It is a finite-sum calculation with the following checked
      expansion target:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sum_sq_linear_combination_expand
          {╬╣ : Type} [Fintype ╬╣]
          (x y : ╬╣ -> ŌäØ) (A B : ŌäØ) :
          Ōłæ i, (A * x i - B * y i) ^ 2 =
            A ^ 2 * (Ōłæ i, x i ^ 2)
              - 2 * A * B * (Ōłæ i, x i * y i)
              + B ^ 2 * (Ōłæ i, y i ^ 2)
      ```

      The proof of `real_cauchy_eq_collinear_of_abs_dot_eq_norms` is:
      set
      `xy := Ōłæ i, x i * y i`,
      `xx := Ōłæ i, x i ^ 2`, and
      `yy := Ōłæ i, y i ^ 2`.  Nonnegativity of `xx` and `yy` is
      `Finset.sum_nonneg (fun i _ => sq_nonneg _)`.  Squaring `heq` gives

      ```lean
      have hsq : xy ^ 2 = xx * yy := by
        have h := congrArg (fun r : ŌäØ => r ^ 2) heq
        simpa [xy, xx, yy, sq_abs, mul_pow,
          Real.sq_sqrt hxx_nonneg, Real.sq_sqrt hyy_nonneg] using h
      ```

      If `xx = 0`, then `BHW.sum_sq_eq_zero x` gives `x = 0`; choose
      `(a,b) = (0,1)`.  If `xx ŌēĀ 0`, choose `(a,b) = (xx,xy)`.  The
      expansion with `A = xy` and `B = xx`, followed by `ring_nf` and
      `rw [hsq]`, proves

      ```lean
      have hsum0 :
          Ōłæ i, (xy * x i - xx * y i) ^ 2 = 0 := by
        have hexpand :=
          BHW.sum_sq_linear_combination_expand x y xy xx
        rw [hexpand]
        simp [xy, xx, yy] at hsq ŌŖó
        ring_nf
        rw [hsq]
        ring
      ```

      Then `BHW.sum_sq_eq_zero (fun i => xy * x i - xx * y i)` gives
      `xy * x i = xx * y i` for every coordinate, exactly the desired
      `b * x i = a * y i`.  Together with the time-coordinate equations
      this produces one real null vector `Ōäō` and scalars `a,b` satisfying
      the definition of `BHW.realNullCollinear`.  The zero-spatial-vector
      subcases are closed by `BHW.sum_sq_eq_zero`, which forces the whole
      null vector to vanish; the hypothesis `hne` then selects the nonzero
      common direction.

      The remaining synchronization for
      `realNull_orthogonal_collinear` is also finite algebra.  From
      `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_decomp` obtain

      ```lean
      hx_sp  : (Ōłæ i : Fin d, x i.succ ^ 2) = x 0 ^ 2
      hy_sp  : (Ōłæ i : Fin d, y i.succ ^ 2) = y 0 ^ 2
      hxy_sp : (Ōłæ i : Fin d, x i.succ * y i.succ) = x 0 * y 0
      ```

      The null-vector square-root lemma and `hxy_sp` give the Cauchy
      equality for the spatial parts, so the previous theorem returns
      `a b : ŌäØ`, `a ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© b ŌēĀ 0`, and
      `hsp : ŌłĆ i : Fin d, b * x i.succ = a * y i.succ`.  Summing
      `hsp i * x i.succ` and `hsp i * y i.succ` gives

      ```lean
      hxrel_sum :
        b * (Ōłæ i : Fin d, x i.succ ^ 2) =
          a * (Ōłæ i : Fin d, x i.succ * y i.succ)
      hyrel_sum :
        b * (Ōłæ i : Fin d, x i.succ * y i.succ) =
          a * (Ōłæ i : Fin d, y i.succ ^ 2)
      ```

      Rewriting these by `hx_sp`, `hy_sp`, and `hxy_sp` yields
      `x 0 * (b * x 0 - a * y 0) = 0` and
      `y 0 * (b * x 0 - a * y 0) = 0`.  If both time coordinates vanish,
      the two null equations and `BHW.sum_sq_eq_zero` force `x = 0` and
      `y = 0`, contradicting `hne`; otherwise one factor cancels and gives
      `htime : b * x 0 = a * y 0`.

      Finally split on `a = 0`.  If `a = 0`, then `b ŌēĀ 0`; `hsp` and
      `htime` force `x = 0`, so choose the common null vector `Ōäō = y` with
      coefficients `(0,1)`.  If `a ŌēĀ 0`, then `x ŌēĀ 0`; choose
      `Ōäō = x` with coefficients `(1, b / a)`, and use `hsp` plus `htime`
      and `field_simp [a_ne]` to prove every coordinate of
      `y = (b/a) ŌĆó x`.  This finite Cauchy bridge and the
      null-collinearity theorem are production-checked in
      `SourceHWTubeCoefficient.lean` as
      `BHW.sum_sq_linear_combination_expand`,
      `BHW.real_cauchy_eq_collinear_of_abs_dot_eq_norms`, and
      `BHW.realNull_orthogonal_collinear`.

      Proof of
      `real_orthogonal_equalNorm_not_collinear_positive`: set
      `A := MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d x x` and use
      `lt_trichotomy A 0`.
      If `A = 0`, then `heq.symm` rewrites
      `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d y y = 0`; the checked
      `BHW.realNull_orthogonal_collinear hx_null hy_null hxy hne`
      contradicts `hnot`.  If `A < 0`, then `x` is timelike.  First prove
      `x 0 ŌēĀ 0`: if `x 0 = 0`, the decomposition
      `A = -(x 0)^2 + Ōłæ i, x_i^2` and nonnegativity of the spatial square
      contradict `A < 0`.  Split on the sign of `x 0`.

      For `0 < x 0`, apply
      `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_orthogonal_to_timelike_nonneg`
      to `╬Š = y` and `╬Ę = x`, with

      ```lean
      htimelike :
        MinkowskiSpace.IsTimelike d x := by
        simpa [MinkowskiSpace.IsTimelike,
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiNormSq] using hA_neg
      hfuture : MinkowskiSpace.IsFutureDirected d x := hx0_pos
      horth_yx :
        MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d y x = 0 := by
        simpa [MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_comm] using hxy
      ```

      This gives `0 Ōēż MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d y y`, while
      `heq` rewrites it to `A < 0`, contradiction.  For `x 0 < 0`, use
      `╬Ę = fun ╬╝ => -x ╬╝`; its time component is positive, its norm square
      is still `A`, and `y` is orthogonal to it by bilinearity (or by
      unfolding `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner`).  The same nonnegativity
      contradiction closes the negative-time branch.  Therefore the only
      trichotomy branch left is `0 < A`.  This theorem is production-checked
      in `SourceHWTubeCoefficient.lean` as
      `BHW.real_orthogonal_equalNorm_not_collinear_positive`.

      With `hA_pos : 0 < A`, define
      `s : ŌäØ := Real.sqrt A`, `╬▒ : Ōäé := (s : Ōäé)`,
      `u := fun ╬╝ => qre ╬╝ / s`, and `v := fun ╬╝ => qim ╬╝ / s`.  The
      checked positivity gives `s ŌēĀ 0` and `s^2 = A`.  Bilinearity of
      `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner` gives
      `u.u = A / s^2 = 1`, `v.v = A / s^2 = 1`, and
      `u.v = 0 / s^2 = 0`.  Finally extensionality and field simplification
      give
      `fun ╬╝ => (qre ╬╝ : Ōäé) + I * (qim ╬╝ : Ōäé) =
       fun ╬╝ => ╬▒ * ((u ╬╝ : Ōäé) + I * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé))`.  In Lean, the
      bilinear scaling equalities factor `1 / s^2` out of the finite
      Minkowski sums with `Finset.sum_div`; the theorem is production-checked
      in `SourceHWTubeCoefficient.lean` as
      `BHW.realImag_null_not_collinear_to_spacelike_orthonormal`.

      theorem BHW.forwardCone_timeOrientation_constant_on_timelike_segment
          [NeZero d]
          {╬│ : ŌäØ -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (h╬│_cont : Continuous ╬│)
          (h╬│_timelike :
            ŌłĆ s Ōłł Set.Icc (0 : ŌäØ) 1,
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d (╬│ s) (╬│ s) < 0)
          (h╬│_one_time : (╬│ 1) 0 > 0) :
          (╬│ 0) 0 > 0 := by
        by_contra hnot
        have h0_nonpos : (╬│ 0) 0 <= 0 := le_of_not_gt hnot
        have htime_path :
            ContinuousOn (fun s : ŌäØ => (╬│ s) 0) (Set.Icc 0 1) :=
          (continuous_apply 0).comp_continuousOn h╬│_cont.continuousOn
        have hzero :
            Ōłā s Ōłł Set.Icc (0 : ŌäØ) 1, (╬│ s) 0 = 0 :=
          intermediate_value_Icc h0_nonpos (le_of_lt h╬│_one_time)
            htime_path
        rcases hzero with Ō¤©s, hs, htime0Ō¤®
        have hquad_nonneg :
            0 <= MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d (╬│ s) (╬│ s) := by
          -- With time component zero, the Minkowski square is a sum of
          -- spatial squares.
          simp [MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner, htime0,
            Fin.sum_univ_succ]
        exact not_lt_of_ge hquad_nonneg (h╬│_timelike s hs)

      theorem BHW.hw_nullPlane_orthogonality_relation
          [NeZero d]
          {q ╬Š : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {╬▒ : Ōäé} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (h╬▒ : ╬▒ ŌēĀ 0)
          (hq :
            q = fun ╬╝ => ╬▒ * ((u ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (horth : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q ╬Š = 0) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) ╬Š =
            Complex.I *
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)) ╬Š

      theorem BHW.imag_difference_orthogonal_realVector
          [NeZero d]
          {╬Ę : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {u : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (hu╬Ę :
            ŌłĆ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)) (╬Ę i) = 0)
          (k : Fin n) :
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
            (fun ╬╝ =>
              (╬Ę k ╬╝ -
                (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                 else ╬Ę Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤® ╬╝)).im) u = 0

      theorem BHW.imag_nullNormalForm_coefficients
          [NeZero d]
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {╬▒ ╬│ : Ōäé}
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (hq :
            q = fun ╬╝ => ╬▒ * ((u ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé))) :
          Ōłā p r : ŌäØ,
            (fun ╬╝ => (╬│ * q ╬╝).im) =
              fun ╬╝ => p * u ╬╝ + r * v ╬╝

      /-- The real two-plane calculation in Hall-Wightman's equation (41).
      With the repo's mostly-positive convention, if `Y + p u + r v` is in
      the open forward cone, while `u,v` are spacelike orthonormal and
      orthogonal to `Y`, then `Y` is also in the open forward cone.  The path
      `s Ōå” Y + s p u + s r v`, `0 <= s <= 1`, stays timelike because its
      Minkowski square is `Y.Y + s^2 (p^2 + r^2)`, which is bounded above by
      the negative value at `s = 1`; the time orientation cannot change along
      a continuous timelike path. -/
      theorem BHW.forwardCone_remove_spacelikeOrthogonal_twoPlane
          [NeZero d]
          {Y u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          {p r : ŌäØ}
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (hYu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y u = 0)
          (hYv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y v = 0)
          (hYpr :
            BHW.InOpenForwardCone d
              (fun ╬╝ => Y ╬╝ + p * u ╬╝ + r * v ╬╝)) :
          BHW.InOpenForwardCone d Y := by
        have hsquare_decomp :
            ŌłĆ s : ŌäØ,
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => Y ╬╝ + s * p * u ╬╝ + s * r * v ╬╝)
                (fun ╬╝ => Y ╬╝ + s * p * u ╬╝ + s * r * v ╬╝)
              =
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y Y +
                s ^ 2 * (p ^ 2 + r ^ 2) := by
          intro s
          -- Expand bilinearly; cross terms vanish by `hYu`, `hYv`, `huv`.
          ring_nf
          simp [MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul,
            huu, hvv, huv, hYu, hYv,
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_comm]
        have hneg_at_one :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y Y + (p ^ 2 + r ^ 2) < 0 := by
          simpa [BHW.InOpenForwardCone, hsquare_decomp 1] using hYpr.2
        have hYY_neg : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y Y < 0 := by
          nlinarith [sq_nonneg p, sq_nonneg r, hneg_at_one]
        have hpath_timelike :
            ŌłĆ s Ōłł Set.Icc (0 : ŌäØ) 1,
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => Y ╬╝ + s * p * u ╬╝ + s * r * v ╬╝)
                (fun ╬╝ => Y ╬╝ + s * p * u ╬╝ + s * r * v ╬╝) < 0 := by
          intro s hs
          rw [hsquare_decomp s]
          have hs_sq : s ^ 2 <= 1 := by nlinarith [hs.1, hs.2]
          nlinarith [sq_nonneg p, sq_nonneg r, hs_sq, hneg_at_one]
        have htime_pos :
            Y 0 > 0 :=
          BHW.forwardCone_timeOrientation_constant_on_timelike_segment
            (d := d)
            (╬│ := fun s ╬╝ => Y ╬╝ + s * p * u ╬╝ + s * r * v ╬╝)
            (h╬│_cont := by
              exact continuous_pi fun ╬╝ =>
                continuous_const.add
                  ((continuous_id.mul continuous_const).mul continuous_const).add
                    ((continuous_id.mul continuous_const).mul continuous_const))
            (h╬│_timelike := hpath_timelike)
            (h╬│_one_time := by simpa using hYpr.1)
        exact Ō¤©htime_pos, hYY_negŌ¤®

      Production status: checked in `SourceHWTubeCoefficient.lean` as
      `BHW.forwardCone_remove_spacelikeOrthogonal_twoPlane`.  The checked
      proof uses `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_add_right`,
      `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_smul_right`, and
      `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_comm` for the path-square expansion,
      plus a local bridge
      `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Z Z =
       Ōłæ ╬╝, minkowskiSignature d ╬╝ * Z ╬╝ ^ 2`
      to match the `BHW.InOpenForwardCone` definition.

      /-- Algebraic split of one complex null vector.  If `q ŌēĀ 0`,
      `B(q,q)=0`, and `q` is orthogonal to a forward-tube configuration in
      the sense needed by the second remark, then `q` is a nonzero scalar
      multiple of `u + i v` with real spacelike orthonormal `u,v`.  The
      excluded case is the Hall-Wightman lightlike-collinear alternative:
      a nonzero real null vector orthogonal to every strict forward-tube
      imaginary difference is impossible. -/
      theorem BHW.complexNullOrthogonal_forwardTube_spacelikeNormalForm
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {╬Č : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h╬Č : ╬Č Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hq_ne : q ŌēĀ 0)
          (hq_null : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q q = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ŌłĆ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q (╬Č i) = 0) :
          Ōłā (╬▒ : Ōäé) (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ),
            ╬▒ ŌēĀ 0 Ōł¦
            q = fun ╬╝ => ╬▒ * ((u ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) Ōł¦
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1 Ōł¦
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1 Ōł¦
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0 := by
        let qre : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ := fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).re
        let qim : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ := fun ╬╝ => (q ╬╝).im
        have hnull_real :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qre qre =
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qim qim Ōł¦
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qre qim = 0 :=
          BHW.complexNull_iff_real_imag_equal_orthogonal
            (d := d) (q := q) hq_null
        have hqreim_ne : qre ŌēĀ 0 Ōł© qim ŌēĀ 0 :=
          BHW.real_imag_nonzero_of_complex_ne_zero (q := q) hq_ne
        have hnot_lightlike_collinear :
            ┬¼ BHW.realNullCollinear d qre qim := by
          intro hcoll
          rcases BHW.realNullCollinear_commonVector hcoll hqreim_ne with
            Ō¤©Ōäō, hŌäō_ne, hŌäō_null, hqre, hqimŌ¤®
          have hŌäō_orth_diffs :
              ŌłĆ k : Fin n,
                MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Ōäō
                  (fun ╬╝ =>
                    ((╬Č k ╬╝) -
                      (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                       else ╬Č Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤® ╬╝)).im) = 0 :=
            BHW.realNullCollinear_orthogonal_forwardTube_differences
              (d := d)
              (by simpa [qre, qim] using hq_orth)
              hqreim_ne
              hqre hqim
          exact
            BHW.nonzero_realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone_differences
              (d := d) h╬Č hŌäō_ne hŌäō_null hŌäō_orth_diffs
        rcases
          BHW.realImag_null_not_collinear_to_spacelike_orthonormal
            (d := d) hd hqreim_ne hnull_real hnot_lightlike_collinear with
          Ō¤©╬▒, u, v, h╬▒, hq_reim, huu, hvv, huvŌ¤®
        refine Ō¤©╬▒, u, v, h╬▒, ?_, huu, hvv, huvŌ¤®
        ext ╬╝
        simpa [qre, qim] using congrFun hq_reim ╬╝

      Production status: checked in `SourceHWTubeCoefficient.lean` as
      `BHW.complexNullOrthogonal_forwardTube_spacelikeNormalForm`.  In the
      production file this theorem is intentionally placed after
      `BHW.nonzero_realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone_differences`,
      because the lightlike-collinear exclusion uses that contradiction
      theorem.

      /-- Hall-Wightman's coefficient split in the second remark.  Once
      `q = ╬▒ (u + i v)`, every vector `╬Š i` orthogonal to `q` decomposes
      uniquely as `╬Ę i + ╬▓ i q`, where `╬Ę i` is orthogonal to both real
      spacelike vectors `u` and `v`. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_secondRemark_coefficients_orthogonalTo_nullPlane
          [NeZero d]
          {╬Š : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {╬▒ : Ōäé} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (h╬▒ : ╬▒ ŌēĀ 0)
          (hq :
            q = fun ╬╝ => ╬▒ * ((u ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ŌłĆ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q (╬Š i) = 0) :
          Ōłā (╬Ę : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (╬▓ : Fin n -> Ōäé),
            (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ╬Š i ╬╝ = ╬Ę i ╬╝ + ╬▓ i * q ╬╝) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)) (╬Ę i) = 0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) (╬Ę i) = 0) := by
        let cu : (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé :=
          fun x => BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)) x
        let cv : (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé :=
          fun x => BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) x
        let ╬▓ : Fin n -> Ōäé :=
          fun i => cu (╬Š i) / ╬▒
        let ╬Ę : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ - ╬▓ i * q ╬╝
        refine Ō¤©╬Ę, ╬▓, ?_, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
        ┬Ę intro i ╬╝
          simp [╬Ę]
        ┬Ę intro i
          have hcu_q : cu q = ╬▒ := by
            simp [cu, hq, huu, huv]
          have hcv_q : cv q = Complex.I * ╬▒ := by
            simp [cv, hq, hvv, huv, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]
          simp [╬Ę, ╬▓, cu, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right, hcu_q, h╬▒]
        ┬Ę intro i
          have hcu_q : cu q = ╬▒ := by
            simp [cu, hq, huu, huv]
          have hcv_q : cv q = Complex.I * ╬▒ := by
            simp [cv, hq, hvv, huv, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]
          have horth_i := hq_orth i
          -- The real and imaginary parts of `hq_orth i`, expanded with
          -- `hq`, give `cv (╬Š i) = Complex.I * cu (╬Š i)`.
          have hcv_relation : cv (╬Š i) = Complex.I * cu (╬Š i) :=
            BHW.hw_nullPlane_orthogonality_relation
              (d := d) h╬▒ hq huu hvv huv horth_i
          simp [╬Ę, ╬▓, cv, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right, hcv_q,
            hcv_relation, h╬▒]

      Production status: checked in `SourceHWTubeCoefficient.lean` as
      `BHW.hw_secondRemark_coefficients_orthogonalTo_nullPlane`.  The checked
      proof introduces `U := fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)` and
      `V := fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)`, casts `huu`, `huv`, and `hvv` into
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner` identities for `U,V`, and then computes
      `cu q = ╬▒` and `cv q = I * ╬▒` from right-linearity.

      /-- The tube part of Hall-Wightman's second remark after the coefficient
      split: if `╬Š + a q` is in the forward tube and
      `╬Š = ╬Ę + ╬▓ q` is the orthogonal-null-plane split, then `╬Ę` is in the
      forward tube.  This is exactly equation (41) applied to each successive
      imaginary difference. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_secondRemark_eta_mem_forwardTube
          [NeZero d]
          {╬Š ╬Ę : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {a ╬▓ : Fin n -> Ōäé}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {╬▒ : Ōäé} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (h╬Š_split : ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ╬Š i ╬╝ = ╬Ę i ╬╝ + ╬▓ i * q ╬╝)
          (hq :
            q = fun ╬╝ => ╬▒ * ((u ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (hu╬Ę :
            ŌłĆ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)) (╬Ę i) = 0)
          (hv╬Ę :
            ŌłĆ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) (╬Ę i) = 0)
          (h╬Šaq :
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + a i * q ╬╝) Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n) :
          ╬Ę Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
        intro k
        let prev╬Ę : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          if h : k.val = 0 then 0 else ╬Ę Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤®
        let ╬┤╬Ę : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé := fun ╬╝ => ╬Ę k ╬╝ - prev╬Ę ╬╝
        let ╬│ : Ōäé :=
          (╬▓ k + a k) -
            (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
             else ╬▓ Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤® +
                  a Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤®)
        have hdiff_tube :
            (fun ╬╝ =>
              (let prev : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
                if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                else (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + a i * q ╬╝)
                  Ō¤©k.val - 1, by omegaŌ¤®
              ((fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + a i * q ╬╝) k ╬╝ - prev ╬╝).im))
            =
            fun ╬╝ => (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝ + ╬│ * q ╬╝).im := by
          ext ╬╝
          by_cases hk : k.val = 0
          ┬Ę simp [╬┤╬Ę, prev╬Ę, h╬Š_split, ╬│, hk, add_comm, add_left_comm,
              add_assoc, sub_eq_add_neg]
            ring
          ┬Ę simp [╬┤╬Ę, prev╬Ę, h╬Š_split, ╬│, hk, add_comm, add_left_comm,
              add_assoc, sub_eq_add_neg]
            ring
        have horth_u :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ => (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝).im) u = 0 := by
          by_cases hk : k.val = 0
          ┬Ę simpa [╬┤╬Ę, prev╬Ę, hk] using
              BHW.imag_difference_orthogonal_realVector (d := d) hu╬Ę k
          ┬Ę simpa [╬┤╬Ę, prev╬Ę, hk] using
              BHW.imag_difference_orthogonal_realVector (d := d) hu╬Ę k
        have horth_v :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ => (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝).im) v = 0 := by
          by_cases hk : k.val = 0
          ┬Ę simpa [╬┤╬Ę, prev╬Ę, hk] using
              BHW.imag_difference_orthogonal_realVector (d := d) hv╬Ę k
          ┬Ę simpa [╬┤╬Ę, prev╬Ę, hk] using
              BHW.imag_difference_orthogonal_realVector (d := d) hv╬Ę k
        have hcone_with_plane :
            BHW.InOpenForwardCone d (fun ╬╝ => (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝ + ╬│ * q ╬╝).im) := by
          have hcone_raw := h╬Šaq k
          dsimp [BHW.ForwardTube] at hcone_raw
          convert hcone_raw using 1
          ext ╬╝
          by_cases hk : k.val = 0
          ┬Ę simp [╬┤╬Ę, prev╬Ę, ╬│, hk, h╬Š_split, add_comm, add_left_comm,
              add_assoc, sub_eq_add_neg]
            ring
          ┬Ę simp [╬┤╬Ę, prev╬Ę, ╬│, hk, h╬Š_split, add_comm, add_left_comm,
              add_assoc, sub_eq_add_neg]
            ring
        rcases
          BHW.imag_nullNormalForm_coefficients
            (d := d) (╬│ := ╬│) hq with
          Ō¤©p, r, himag_eqŌ¤®
        have hcone_plane_real :
            BHW.InOpenForwardCone d
              (fun ╬╝ => (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝).im + p * u ╬╝ + r * v ╬╝) := by
          have hfun :
              (fun ╬╝ => (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝ + ╬│ * q ╬╝).im) =
                fun ╬╝ => (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝).im + p * u ╬╝ + r * v ╬╝ := by
            ext ╬╝
            calc
              (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝ + ╬│ * q ╬╝).im =
                  (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝).im + (╬│ * q ╬╝).im := by
                rw [Complex.add_im]
              _ = (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝).im + (p * u ╬╝ + r * v ╬╝) := by
                rw [congrFun himag_eq ╬╝]
              _ = (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝).im + p * u ╬╝ + r * v ╬╝ := by
                ring
          exact hfun Ō¢Ė hcone_with_plane
        have hremove :=
          BHW.forwardCone_remove_spacelikeOrthogonal_twoPlane
            (d := d) (Y := fun ╬╝ => (╬┤╬Ę ╬╝).im)
            (u := u) (v := v) (p := p) (r := r)
            huu hvv huv horth_u horth_v
            hcone_plane_real
        simpa [BHW.ForwardTube, ╬┤╬Ę, prev╬Ę] using hremove

      Production status: checked in `SourceHWTubeCoefficient.lean` as
      `BHW.hw_secondRemark_eta_mem_forwardTube`.  The checked proof does not
      rely on an informal rewrite of `ForwardTube`: it introduces the
      let-bound previous point from the definition, aligns it with `╬┤╬Ę` and
      `╬│` by `by_cases hk : k.val = 0`, and uses `convert hcone_raw using 1`
      before applying the checked equation-(41) cone-removal theorem.

      /-- Hall-Wightman's second remark after Lemma 2.  A forward-tube point
      with one orthogonal complex-null residual can be rewritten as a
      forward-tube point `╬Ę` plus another coefficient family along the same
      null vector, and in that representation the null vector has the standard
      real spacelike normal form.  This theorem does **not** say that the
      original base `╬Š` is in the forward tube. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_secondRemark_forwardTube_singleNullResidual_normalForm
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {╬Š : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {a : Fin n -> Ōäé}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h╬Šq :
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + a i * q ╬╝) Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hq_null :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q q = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ŌłĆ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q (╬Š i) = 0) :
          Ōłā (╬Ę : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (╬▓ : Fin n -> Ōäé),
            ╬Ę Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n Ōł¦
            BHW.HWNullResidualNormalForm d n q ╬Ę Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ╬Š i ╬╝ = ╬Ę i ╬╝ + ╬▓ i * q ╬╝) := by
        by_cases hq0 : q = 0
        ┬Ę refine Ō¤©╬Š, 0, ?_, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
          ┬Ę simpa [hq0] using h╬Šq
          ┬Ę exact Or.inl hq0
          ┬Ę intro i ╬╝
            simp [hq0]
        ┬Ę rcases
            BHW.complexNullOrthogonal_forwardTube_spacelikeNormalForm
              (d := d) hd h╬Šq hq0 hq_null ?horthForwardPoint with
            Ō¤©╬▒, u, v, h╬▒, hq, huu, hvv, huvŌ¤®
          ┬Ę rcases
              BHW.hw_secondRemark_coefficients_orthogonalTo_nullPlane
                (d := d) (╬Š := ╬Š) (q := q)
                h╬▒ hq huu hvv huv hq_orth with
              Ō¤©╬Ę, ╬▓, h╬Š_split, hu╬Ę, hv╬ĘŌ¤®
            have h╬ĘFT :
                ╬Ę Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n :=
              BHW.hw_secondRemark_eta_mem_forwardTube
                (d := d) h╬Š_split hq huu hvv huv hu╬Ę hv╬Ę h╬Šq
            refine Ō¤©╬Ę, ╬▓, h╬ĘFT, ?_, h╬Š_splitŌ¤®
            exact Or.inr Ō¤©╬▒, u, v, h╬▒, hq, huu, hvv, huv, hu╬Ę, hv╬ĘŌ¤®
          ┬Ę intro i
            -- The normal-form lemma is applied to the actual forward-tube
            -- point `╬Č i = ╬Š i + a i q`.  Orthogonality follows from
            -- `B(q, ╬Š i) = 0` and `B(q,q) = 0`.
            rw [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right,
              hq_orth i, hq_null]
            ring

      Production status: checked in `SourceHWTubeCoefficient.lean` as
      `BHW.hw_secondRemark_forwardTube_singleNullResidual_normalForm`.  The
      nonzero branch applies the normal-form theorem to the actual forward
      point `fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + a i * q ╬╝`; the required orthogonality is a
      checked right-linearity calculation, not an implicit reuse of
      `hq_orth`.

      /-- Hall-Wightman's third remark after Lemma 2.  In the standard null
      normal form, changing the coefficients of the null residual keeps the
      configuration in the ordinary extended tube. -/
      /-- Explicit complex rotation in the spacelike two-plane of the third
      remark.  Write `cu x = B(u,x)` and `cv x = B(v,x)`.  Since
      `B(u,u)=B(v,v)=1` and `B(u,v)=0`, the vector
      `x - cu x ŌĆó u - cv x ŌĆó v` is orthogonal to both `u` and `v`.  The
      operator below fixes that orthogonal complement and applies the
      two-by-two complex orthogonal matrix from Hall-Wightman equation (42)
      on the `u,v` plane. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation
          [NeZero d]
          (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ)
          (t : ŌäØ)
          (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        let cu : Ōäé :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)) x
        let cv : Ōäé :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) x
        fun ╬╝ =>
          x ╬╝
            + cu * (((Real.cosh t : Ōäé) - 1) * (u ╬╝ : Ōäé) +
                Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : Ōäé) * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé))
            + cv * (-Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : Ōäé) * (u ╬╝ : Ōäé) +
                (((Real.cosh t : Ōäé) - 1) * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)))

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_linear
          [NeZero d]
          (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ)
          (t : ŌäØ) :
          IsLinearMap Ōäé (BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t)

      In production this is packaged as the reusable linear map
      `BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotationLinearMap`; its `toFun` is
      definitionally `BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation`, with additivity
      and scalar compatibility proved by expanding right-linearity of
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner`.

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_apply_orthogonal
          [NeZero d]
          {u v x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {ur vr : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          {t : ŌäØ}
          (hu :
            u = fun ╬╝ => (ur ╬╝ : Ōäé))
          (hv :
            v = fun ╬╝ => (vr ╬╝ : Ōäé))
          (hux : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d u x = 0)
          (hvx : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v x = 0) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) ur vr t x = x := by
        ext ╬╝
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, hu, hv, hux, hvx]

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_apply_u
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          {t : ŌäØ}
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t
              (fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)) =
            fun ╬╝ =>
              (Real.cosh t : Ōäé) * (u ╬╝ : Ōäé) +
              Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : Ōäé) * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé) := by
        ext ╬╝
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, huu, huv,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_apply_v
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          {t : ŌäØ}
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t
              (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)) =
            fun ╬╝ =>
              -Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : Ōäé) * (u ╬╝ : Ōäé) +
              (Real.cosh t : Ōäé) * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé) := by
        ext ╬╝
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, hvv, huv,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_preserves_inner
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          {t : ŌäØ}
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (x y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t x)
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t y) =
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d x y := by
        let U : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé := fun ╬╝ => (u ╬╝ : Ōäé)
        let V : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé := fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)
        let cu : (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé :=
          fun z => BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d U z
        let cv : (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé :=
          fun z => BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d V z
        let xperp : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun ╬╝ => x ╬╝ - cu x * U ╬╝ - cv x * V ╬╝
        let yperp : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun ╬╝ => y ╬╝ - cu y * U ╬╝ - cv y * V ╬╝
        have hxperp_u : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d U xperp = 0 := by
          simp [xperp, cu, cv, U, V, huu, huv]
        have hxperp_v : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d V xperp = 0 := by
          simp [xperp, cu, cv, U, V, hvv, huv,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]
        have hyperp_u : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d U yperp = 0 := by
          simp [yperp, cu, cv, U, V, huu, huv]
        have hyperp_v : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d V yperp = 0 := by
          simp [yperp, cu, cv, U, V, hvv, huv,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]
        have hdecomp_x :
            x = fun ╬╝ => xperp ╬╝ + cu x * U ╬╝ + cv x * V ╬╝ := by
          ext ╬╝
          simp [xperp, sub_eq_add_neg, add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc]
        have hdecomp_y :
            y = fun ╬╝ => yperp ╬╝ + cu y * U ╬╝ + cv y * V ╬╝ := by
          ext ╬╝
          simp [yperp, sub_eq_add_neg, add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc]
        -- Expand both sides using the decompositions.  Orthogonal-complement
        -- cross terms vanish, and the two-plane block is preserved by
        -- `(cosh t)^2 - (sinh t)^2 = 1`; the mixed `u,v` terms cancel.
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, hdecomp_x, hdecomp_y,
          hxperp_u, hxperp_v, hyperp_u, hyperp_v, U, V, huu, hvv, huv,
          Real.cosh_sq_sub_sinh_sq]
        ring

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_scale_null
          [NeZero d]
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {╬▒ : Ōäé} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          {t : ŌäØ}
          (hq :
            q = fun ╬╝ => ╬▒ * ((u ╬╝ : Ōäé) + Complex.I * (v ╬╝ : Ōäé)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t q =
            fun ╬╝ => (Real.exp t : Ōäé) * q ╬╝ := by
        rw [hq]
        ext ╬╝
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, huu, hvv, huv,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm, Complex.ofReal_exp,
          Real.exp_eq_cosh_add_sinh]
        ring

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_preserving_toMatrix'_metric
          [NeZero d]
          (L :
            (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé]
              (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hpres :
            ŌłĆ x y,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (L x) (L y) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d x y) :
          ŌłĆ ╬╝ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1),
            Ōłæ ╬▒ : Fin (d + 1),
              (minkowskiSignature d ╬▒ : Ōäé) *
                (LinearMap.toMatrix' L) ╬▒ ╬╝ *
                (LinearMap.toMatrix' L) ╬▒ ╬Į =
            if ╬╝ = ╬Į then (minkowskiSignature d ╬╝ : Ōäé) else 0 := by
        intro ╬╝ ╬Į
        have h := hpres (Pi.single ╬╝ (1 : Ōäé)) (Pi.single ╬Į (1 : Ōäé))
        simpa [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner, LinearMap.toMatrix'_apply,
          Pi.single_apply, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm] using h

      theorem BHW.det_complexTwoPlaneRotation_of_adaptedBasis
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ}
          (t : ŌäØ)
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          (LinearMap.toMatrix'
            ({ toFun := BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t
               map_add' := by
                 intro x y
                 ext ╬╝
                 simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation,
                   BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right,
