      ```lean
        have hright_val :
            ŌłĆ t, BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_right t) =
              BHW.extendF F w := by
          intro t
          rcases hsingular.curve_right_orbit t with Ō¤©╬ø, h╬øŌ¤®
          rw [h╬ø]
          exact BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv ╬ø hw
        have hleft_lim :
            Tendsto
              (fun t : ŌäØ => BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_left t))
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          (hExtend_cont.continuousWithinAt hsingular.base_mem).tendsto.comp
            (tendsto_nhdsWithin_iff.mpr
              Ō¤©hsingular.curve_left_tendsto_base,
                Eventually.of_forall hsingular.curve_left_memŌ¤®)
        have hright_lim :
            Tendsto
              (fun t : ŌäØ => BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_right t))
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          (hExtend_cont.continuousWithinAt hsingular.base_mem).tendsto.comp
            (tendsto_nhdsWithin_iff.mpr
              Ō¤©hsingular.curve_right_tendsto_base,
                Eventually.of_forall hsingular.curve_right_memŌ¤®)
        have hz_lim :
            Tendsto (fun _ : ŌäØ => BHW.extendF F z)
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          Filter.Tendsto.congr'
            (Eventually.of_forall fun t => hleft_val t) hleft_lim
        have hw_lim :
            Tendsto (fun _ : ŌäØ => BHW.extendF F w)
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          Filter.Tendsto.congr'
            (Eventually.of_forall fun t => hright_val t) hright_lim
        have hz_eq_base :
            BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F hsingular.base :=
          tendsto_nhds_unique tendsto_const_nhds hz_lim
        have hw_eq_base :
            BHW.extendF F w = BHW.extendF F hsingular.base :=
          tendsto_nhds_unique tendsto_const_nhds hw_lim
        exact hz_eq_base.trans hw_eq_base.symm
      ```

      The generic limit proof has been checked independently against Mathlib:
      from `hf : ContinuousOn f S`, `base Ōłł S`, curves `curve_left`,
      `curve_right` with `ŌłĆ t, curve_* t Ōłł S`, both tending to `base`, and
      identities `f (curve_left t) = f z`,
      `f (curve_right t) = f w`, the accepted Lean proof is precisely
      `(hf.continuousWithinAt hbase).tendsto.comp
      (tendsto_nhdsWithin_iff.mpr Ō¤©hcurve_tendsto,
      Eventually.of_forall hcurve_memŌ¤®)`, then
      `Filter.Tendsto.congr'` and `tendsto_nhds_unique`.

      Lean-shaped proof of the Hall-Wightman Lemma-2 alternative:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_fiberAlternative ... := by
        by_cases hzOrbit : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z
        ┬Ę rcases
            BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit_or_improper
              (d := d) hd (n := n) hz hw hzOrbit hgram)
            with hproper | himproper
          ┬Ę exact Or.inl hproper
          ┬Ę exact Or.inr (Or.inl himproper)
        ┬Ę exact Or.inr (Or.inr
            (BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData
              (d := d) hd (n := n) hz hw
              (by
                simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt,
                  BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt] using
                  ((BHW.hwSourceGram_lowRank_iff_not_orbitRank
                    (d := d) (n := n) hd).2 hzOrbit))
              hgram))
      ```

      Rank convention audit: Hall-Wightman is written for four-vectors.  In
      the outline of Lemma 2 the direct orbit case is scalar-product matrix
      rank three or four in the hard arity range, with the corresponding
      nonsingular-matrix exception for small arity; ranks below that threshold
      require the limiting argument.  Lemma 3 repeats the same rank split for
      neighborhoods.  In the repo notation the spacetime vector dimension is
      `d + 1`, so the dimension-general direct orbit threshold is scalar rank
      at least `min d n`.  Equivalently: if `n >= d`, the orbit cases are rank
      `d` or `d + 1`; if `n < d`, the orbit case is rank `n`.  The lower branch
      is rank `< min d n`.  This is why the proof-doc predicate is
      `HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt`; it must not be replaced by
      `SourceComplexGramRegularAt`.  The latter is still correct for the later
      local-image theorem that chooses a regular source point, but it is not
      Hall-Wightman's Lemma-2 scalar-rank split.

      The proof of `hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit` is the
      finite-dimensional complex orthogonal linear algebra in Hall-Wightman
      Lemma 2, with the coefficient quotient made explicit.  Let
      `evalZ` and `evalW` be the coefficient-evaluation maps
      `a Ōå” Ōłæ i, a i ŌĆó z i` and `a Ōå” Ōłæ i, a i ŌĆó w i`.  Equal source Gram
      matrices give the same coefficient Gram bilinear form.  In the high
      scalar-rank range, Hall-Wightman's rank computation proves
      `ker evalZ = sourceCoefficientGramKernel G = ker evalW`; this is the
      theorem `hw_highRank_sourceCoefficientEval_ker_eq_of_sameSourceGram`.
      Therefore the assignment induced by the identity map on coefficient
      space descends to a linear equivalence between `range evalZ` and
      `range evalW`, sends each `z i` to `w i`, and preserves the complex
      Minkowski bilinear form.  This is packaged as
      `HWHighRankSpanIsometryData`.  The consumer must then keep the
      determinant gate visible.  With
      `HWSameSourceGramSOOrientationCompatible`, the determinant-sensitive
      theorem `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_detOne_of_sourceSpan`
      returns a `ComplexLorentzGroup d` element carrying every `z i` to
      `w i`.  Without that compatibility, the only honest output is the
      alternative
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_or_improper_of_sourceSpan`, whose
      right branch records the improper full-complex-orthogonal orbit data.
      No source-map regularity theorem is used in this high-rank branch.  The
      Lean proof of the public high-rank alternative is only the conversion
      from vectorwise source images to the configuration action; all
      determinant-sensitive mathematics remains in the two Witt-extension
      producers named below.

      Determinant-repair transcript for
      `complexMinkowski_wittExtension_detOne_of_sourceSpan`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_detOne_of_sourceSpan ... := by
        rcases
          BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_full_of_sourceSpan
            (d := d) hd S with
          Ō¤©A, hAŌ¤®
        by_cases hproper :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1
        ┬Ę by_cases hdetA :
            BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A = 1
          ┬Ę rcases
              BHW.fullComplexLorentz_to_complexLorentzGroup_of_det_one A
                hdetA with
              Ō¤©╬ø, h╬øAŌ¤®
            exact Ō¤©╬ø, fun i => by rw [h╬øA, hA i]Ō¤®
          ┬Ę rcases
              BHW.fullComplexLorentz_det_neg_reflection_fixing_sourceSpan
                (d := d) hd S hproper with
              Ō¤©R, hRdet, hRfixŌ¤®
            let AR :=
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzMul A R
            have hARdet :
                BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet AR = 1 := by
              have hA_sq :=
                BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentz_det_sq_eq_one A
              have hA_neg :
                  BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A = -1 :=
                BHW.det_eq_neg_one_of_sq_one_of_ne_one hA_sq hdetA
              simp [AR, BHW.fullComplexLorentz_mul_det, hA_neg, hRdet]
            rcases
              BHW.fullComplexLorentz_to_complexLorentzGroup_of_det_one AR
                hARdet with
              Ō¤©╬ø, h╬øARŌ¤®
            refine Ō¤©╬ø, ?_Ō¤®
            intro i
            calc
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (z i)
                  =
                BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction AR (z i) := by
                  rw [h╬øAR]
              _ =
                BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A
                  (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction R
                    (z i)) := by
                  rw [AR, BHW.fullComplexLorentz_mul_vectorAction]
              _ =
                BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A (z i) := by
                  rw [hRfix Ō¤©z i, S.z_mem iŌ¤®]
              _ = w i := hA i
        ┬Ę have hfull :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) = d + 1 :=
            le_antisymm
              (BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_le_spacetime_of_sourceGram
                (d := d) (n := n) z)
              (Nat.le_of_not_lt hproper)
          have hdetA :
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A = 1 := by
            have hdet_frame :=
              BHW.fullComplexLorentz_det_eq_frameMapDet_of_fullRank
                (d := d) hd hfull
                (BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometryData_sourceGram_eq
                  (d := d) (n := n) S)
                A hA
            rcases hSO with hlt | hone
            ┬Ę exact False.elim ((not_lt_of_ge (le_of_eq hfull)) hlt)
            ┬Ę exact hdet_frame.trans hone
          rcases
            BHW.fullComplexLorentz_to_complexLorentzGroup_of_det_one A
              hdetA with
            Ō¤©╬ø, h╬øAŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©╬ø, fun i => by rw [h╬øA, hA i]Ō¤®
      ```

      The proof starts with ordinary Witt extension into the full complex
      orthogonal group, not directly into `ComplexLorentzGroup d`.  If the
      source span is proper, the orthogonal complement is nonzero; the
      reflection theorem supplies a determinant-`-1` full-complex-Lorentz
      element fixing that span, so multiplying by it flips the determinant
      without moving any source vector.  If the source span has full ambient
      rank, no complement remains; the determinant is the selected full-frame
      determinant ratio, so the proof must use the right disjunct of
      `HWSameSourceGramSOOrientationCompatible`.  This is the exact place
      where the oriented invariant eliminates the improper full-rank branch.

      The ordinary full-complex Witt extension used in the first line above
      is itself finite-dimensional linear algebra and must not remain an
      unnamed black box.  In the high-rank row the domain and target source
      spans are already nondegenerate, so the proof does not need the fully
      degenerate version of Witt's theorem.  It extends the checked
      `S.T : S.M ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] S.N` by choosing an isometry between the two
      nondegenerate orthogonal complements and then assembling the direct-sum
      map.

      Lean-shaped proof transcript for
      `complexMinkowski_wittExtension_full_of_sourceSpan`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm_isSymm
          (d : Nat) :
          (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d).IsSymm := by
        intro x y
        exact BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm d x y

      theorem BHW.exists_complex_unit_orthogonal_basis
          {U : Type*}
          [AddCommGroup U] [Module Ōäé U] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé U]
          (B : LinearMap.BilinForm Ōäé U)
          (hB_symm : B.IsSymm)
          (hB_nd : B.Nondegenerate) :
          Ōłā b : Basis (Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé U)) Ōäé U,
            B.iIsOrtho b Ōł¦ ŌłĆ i, B (b i) (b i) = 1 := by
        -- Import boundary:
        --   Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.QuadraticForm.Basic
        --   Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.BilinearForm.Orthogonal
        --   Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Basis.SMul
        --   Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.Basic
        have hsymm' : LinearMap.IsSymm B :=
          LinearMap.BilinForm.isSymm_iff.mp hB_symm
        rcases
          LinearMap.BilinForm.exists_orthogonal_basis
            (K := Ōäé) (V := U) hsymm' with
          Ō¤©b0, hb0_orthŌ¤®
        have hdiag_ne :
            ŌłĆ i, B (b0 i) (b0 i) ŌēĀ 0 := by
          intro i
          exact
            LinearMap.BilinForm.iIsOrtho
              .not_isOrtho_basis_self_of_nondegenerate
              hb0_orth hB_nd i
        choose r hr using
          fun i =>
            IsAlgClosed.exists_pow_nat_eq
              (B (b0 i) (b0 i)) (by norm_num : 0 < 2)
        have hr_ne : ŌłĆ i, r i ŌēĀ 0 := by
          intro i hzero
          have : B (b0 i) (b0 i) = 0 := by
            simpa [hzero] using (hr i).symm
          exact hdiag_ne i this
        let c : Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé U) -> Ōäé :=
          fun i => (r i)Ōü╗┬╣
        have hc_unit : ŌłĆ i, IsUnit (c i) := by
          intro i
          exact isUnit_iff_ne_zero.mpr (inv_ne_zero (hr_ne i))
        let b : Basis (Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé U)) Ōäé U :=
          b0.isUnitSMul hc_unit
        refine Ō¤©b, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
        ┬Ę intro i j hij
          have hzero : B (b0 i) (b0 j) = 0 := hb0_orth hij
          change B (b i) (b j) = 0
          rw [Module.Basis.isUnitSMul_apply,
            Module.Basis.isUnitSMul_apply]
          simp [c, hzero]
        ┬Ę intro i
          rw [Module.Basis.isUnitSMul_apply]
          simp [c]
          field_simp [hr_ne i]
          simpa [pow_two] using (hr i).symm

      theorem BHW.bilinForm_eq_sum_coords_of_unitOrthogonalBasis
          {╬╣ U : Type*}
          [Fintype ╬╣] [DecidableEq ╬╣]
          [AddCommGroup U] [Module Ōäé U]
          (B : LinearMap.BilinForm Ōäé U)
          (b : Basis ╬╣ Ōäé U)
          (horth : B.iIsOrtho b)
          (hdiag : ŌłĆ i, B (b i) (b i) = 1)
          (x y : U) :
          B x y = Ōłæ i, b.equivFun x i * b.equivFun y i := by
        -- Import boundary: Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Basis.Bilinear.
        have h1 :
            B x y =
              Ōłæ i, Ōłæ j,
                b.repr x i *
                  (b.repr y j * B (b i) (b j)) := by
          have hsum :=
            (LinearMap.sum_repr_mul_repr_mul
              b b (B := B) x y).symm
          rw [hsum]
          rw [Finsupp.sum_fintype]
          ┬Ę congr
            ext i
            rw [Finsupp.sum_fintype]
            ┬Ę simp [smul_eq_mul]
            ┬Ę intro j
              simp
          ┬Ę intro i
            simp
        rw [h1]
        simp only [Basis.equivFun_apply]
        apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
        intro i hi
        calc
          Ōłæ j,
              b.repr x i *
                (b.repr y j * B (b i) (b j))
              =
            b.repr x i *
              (b.repr y i * B (b i) (b i)) := by
                rw [ŌåÉ Finset.mul_sum]
                congr 1
                apply Finset.sum_eq_single i
                ┬Ę intro j _ hji
                  have hz : B (b i) (b j) = 0 :=
                    horth hji.symm
                  simp [hz]
                ┬Ę intro hi_not
                  simp at hi_not
          _ = b.repr x i * b.repr y i := by
                rw [hdiag i]
                ring

      theorem BHW.nondegenerate_complexSymmetricBilinForm_linearEquiv_of_finrank_eq
          {U V : Type*}
          [AddCommGroup U] [Module Ōäé U] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé U]
          [AddCommGroup V] [Module Ōäé V] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé V]
          (BU : LinearMap.BilinForm Ōäé U)
          (BV : LinearMap.BilinForm Ōäé V)
          (hBU_symm : BU.IsSymm)
          (hBV_symm : BV.IsSymm)
          (hBU_nd : BU.Nondegenerate)
          (hBV_nd : BV.Nondegenerate)
          (hfin : Module.finrank Ōäé U = Module.finrank Ōäé V) :
          Ōłā E : U ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V,
            ŌłĆ x y, BV (E x) (E y) = BU x y := by
        rcases
          BHW.exists_complex_unit_orthogonal_basis
            BU hBU_symm hBU_nd with
          Ō¤©bU, hUorth, hUdiagŌ¤®
        rcases
          BHW.exists_complex_unit_orthogonal_basis
            BV hBV_symm hBV_nd with
          Ō¤©bV0, hV0orth, hV0diagŌ¤®
        let e╬╣ :
            Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé U) Ōēā
              Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé V) :=
          finCongr hfin
        let bV : Basis (Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé U)) Ōäé V :=
          bV0.reindex e╬╣.symm
        have hVorth : BV.iIsOrtho bV := by
          intro i j hij
          change BV (bV i) (bV j) = 0
          have hij' : e╬╣ i ŌēĀ e╬╣ j :=
            fun h => hij (e╬╣.injective h)
          simpa [bV, Basis.reindex_apply] using hV0orth hij'
        have hVdiag : ŌłĆ i, BV (bV i) (bV i) = 1 := by
          intro i
          simp [bV, Basis.reindex_apply, hV0diag]
        let E : U ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V := bU.equiv bV (Equiv.refl _)
        refine Ō¤©E, ?_Ō¤®
        intro x y
        have hcoordx : bV.equivFun (E x) = bU.equivFun x := by
          have hmap : bU.map E = bV := by
            simpa [E] using
              Basis.map_equiv bU bV (Equiv.refl _)
          rw [ŌåÉ hmap, Basis.map_equivFun]
          exact congrArg bU.equivFun (E.symm_apply_apply x)
        have hcoordy : bV.equivFun (E y) = bU.equivFun y := by
          have hmap : bU.map E = bV := by
            simpa [E] using
              Basis.map_equiv bU bV (Equiv.refl _)
          rw [ŌåÉ hmap, Basis.map_equivFun]
          exact congrArg bU.equivFun (E.symm_apply_apply y)
        rw [
          BHW.bilinForm_eq_sum_coords_of_unitOrthogonalBasis
            BV bV hVorth hVdiag (E x) (E y)]
        rw [
          BHW.bilinForm_eq_sum_coords_of_unitOrthogonalBasis
            BU bU hUorth hUdiag x y]
        rw [hcoordx, hcoordy]

      theorem BHW.exists_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalComplementIsometry
          [NeZero d]
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w) :
          Ōłā C :
              BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
                BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.N,
            ŌłĆ x y,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                ((C x :
                  BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.N) :
                    Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                ((C y :
                  BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.N) :
                    Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) := by
        let B := BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d
        have hB_nd : B.Nondegenerate :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm_nondegenerate d
        have hB_symm : B.IsSymm :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm_isSymm d
        have hOM_nd :
            (B.restrict
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M)).Nondegenerate :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace_to_restrict
            d _ (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
              (d := d) S.M S.M_nondegenerate)
        have hON_nd :
            (B.restrict
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.N)).Nondegenerate :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace_to_restrict
            d _ (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
              (d := d) S.N S.N_nondegenerate)
        have hfin_MN : Module.finrank Ōäé S.M = Module.finrank Ōäé S.N :=
          S.T.finrank_eq
        have hfin_comp :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
                (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M) =
              Module.finrank Ōäé
                (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.N) := by
          have hM :=
            LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal
              (B := B) hB_nd S.M
          have hN :=
            LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal
              (B := B) hB_nd S.N
          omega
        rcases
          BHW.nondegenerate_complexSymmetricBilinForm_linearEquiv_of_finrank_eq
            (B.restrict
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M))
            (B.restrict
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.N))
            (BHW.restrict_isSymm_of_isSymm hB_symm _)
            (BHW.restrict_isSymm_of_isSymm hB_symm _)
            hOM_nd hON_nd hfin_comp with
          Ō¤©C, hCŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©C, by
          intro x y
          simpa [LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm] using hC x yŌ¤®

      def BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup.ofLinearEquiv
          [NeZero d]
          (L : (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hL :
            ŌłĆ u v,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (L u) (L v) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d u v) :
          BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d :=
        { val :=
            LinearMap.toMatrix
              (Pi.basisFun Ōäé (Fin (d + 1)))
              (Pi.basisFun Ōäé (Fin (d + 1))) L.toLinearMap
          metric_preserving := by
            exact
              BHW.metric_preserving_components_of_preserves_sourceInner
                d L hL }

      theorem BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup.ofLinearEquiv_vectorAction
          [NeZero d]
          (L : (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hL :
            ŌłĆ u v,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (L u) (L v) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d u v)
          (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction
              (BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup.ofLinearEquiv
                L hL) x = L x := by
        ext ╬╝
        simp [BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup.ofLinearEquiv,
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction,
          LinearMap.toMatrix, Matrix.mulVec, Pi.basisFun]

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_full_of_sourceSpan
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w) :
          Ōłā A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ŌłĆ i,
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A (z i) =
                w i := by
        let B := BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d
        rcases
          BHW.exists_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalComplementIsometry
            (d := d) S with
          Ō¤©C, hCŌ¤®
        have hSM_rest : (B.restrict S.M).Nondegenerate :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace_to_restrict
            d S.M S.M_nondegenerate
        have hSN_rest : (B.restrict S.N).Nondegenerate :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace_to_restrict
            d S.N S.N_nondegenerate
        have hSM_compl : IsCompl S.M (B.orthogonal S.M) :=
          (LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal
            (B := B) (W := S.M)
            (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm_isRefl d)).mp hSM_rest
        have hSN_compl : IsCompl S.N (B.orthogonal S.N) :=
          (LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal
            (B := B) (W := S.N)
            (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm_isRefl d)).mp hSN_rest
        let eM :
            S.M ├Ś BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M
              ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl S.M
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M)
            (by simpa [BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule, B]
              using hSM_compl)
        let eN :
            S.N ├Ś BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.N
              ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl S.N
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.N)
            (by simpa [BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule, B]
              using hSN_compl)
        let P :
            S.M ├Ś BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M
              ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
            S.N ├Ś BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.N :=
          S.T.prod C
        let L : (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
            (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          eM.symm.trans (P.trans eN)
        have hL :
            ŌłĆ u v,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (L u) (L v) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d u v := by
          intro u v
          exact
            BHW.sourceInner_preserved_prodEquiv_of_span_and_orthogonal
              (d := d) S C hC hSM_compl hSN_compl u v
        let A :=
          BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup.ofLinearEquiv
            (d := d) L hL
        refine Ō¤©A, ?_Ō¤®
        intro i
        calc
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A (z i)
              = L (z i) := by
                exact
                  BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup
                    .ofLinearEquiv_vectorAction L hL (z i)
          _ = w i := by
                simpa [L, P, eM, eN,
                  Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl_symm_apply_left,
                  S.T_z i]
      ```

      This closes the former standard-boundary placeholder
      `complex_symmetric_nondegenerate_forms_isometric_of_finrank_eq` at
      proof-doc level.  The local proof uses only finite-dimensional
      bilinear algebra: Mathlib's orthogonal-basis theorem for symmetric
      bilinear forms, nondegeneracy to make each diagonal entry nonzero,
      algebraic closedness of `Ōäé` for square roots, basis rescaling by
      `Basis.isUnitSMul`, and `LinearMap.sum_repr_mul_repr_mul` to reduce
      both forms to the same coordinate sum.  No new axiom is authorized or
      needed for this helper.

      Proof transcript for
      `fullComplexLorentz_det_eq_frameMapDet_of_fullRank`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_det_eq_frameMapDet_of_fullRank
          ... := by
        rcases
          BHW.exists_sourceFullFrameDet_ne_zero_of_sourceGramRank_eq_spacetime
            d n z hfull with
          Ō¤©╬╣, h╬╣Ō¤®
        have hdet_action :
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ w =
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A *
                BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z := by
          calc
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ w
                =
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣
                (fun i =>
                  BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A
                    (z i)) := by
                congr
                ext i
                exact (hA i).symm
            _ =
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A *
                BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z := by
                exact
                  BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_fullComplexLorentzAction
                    (d := d) A n ╬╣ z
        have hratio :
            BHW.HWFullRankSameGramFrameMapDet d n z w =
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ w /
                BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z :=
          BHW.hwFullRankSameGramFrameMapDet_eq_det_ratio_of_fullFrame
            d n hfull hgram ╬╣ h╬╣
        calc
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A
              =
            (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A *
                BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z) /
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z := by
                rw [mul_div_cancel_rightŌéĆ _ h╬╣]
          _ =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ w /
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ╬╣ z := by
                rw [ŌåÉ hdet_action]
          _ = BHW.HWFullRankSameGramFrameMapDet d n z w := hratio.symm
      ```

      This determinant action packet is now checked in
      `SourceFullComplexLorentzFrame.lean`.  The proof is finite matrix
      algebra: the selected frame matrix of `A ŌĆó z` is the selected frame
      matrix of `z` multiplied by the transpose of the full Lorentz matrix, so
      `Matrix.det_mul` and `Matrix.det_transpose` give multiplication by
      `hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A`.  This theorem is the full-group
      analog of the determinant-`1` lemma used for
      `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_complexLorentzAction`.

      Implementation-level full-complex Lorentz ledger.  The determinant
      repair theorem may not start by adding an opaque "full Lorentz" object.
      The first Lean slice in this row is now checked in
      `SourceFullComplexLorentz.lean` and consists of the following purely
      finite matrix support:

      ```lean
      /-- Full complex Lorentz group `O(1,d;Ōäé)`: preserve the same complex
      Minkowski matrix as `ComplexLorentzGroup d`, but carry no determinant
      condition. -/
      structure BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup (d : Nat) where
        val : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé
        metric_preserving : ŌłĆ ╬╝ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1),
          Ōłæ ╬▒ : Fin (d + 1),
            (minkowskiSignature d ╬▒ : Ōäé) * val ╬▒ ╬╝ * val ╬▒ ╬Į =
          if ╬╝ = ╬Į then (minkowskiSignature d ╬╝ : Ōäé) else 0

      def BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun ╬╝ => Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1), A.val ╬╝ ╬Į * v ╬Į

      def BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzAction
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun i => BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A (z i)

      def BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d) : Ōäé :=
        A.val.det

      def BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun ╬╝ => Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1), ╬ø.val ╬╝ ╬Į * v ╬Į

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_apply_eq_vectorAction
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (i : Fin n) :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z i =
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (z i) := rfl

      theorem BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentz_metric_preserving_matrix
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d) :
          A.val.transpose * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d) * A.val =
            ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d)

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzEta_det_ne_zero (d : Nat) :
          (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d)).det ŌēĀ 0 := by
        rw [ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé, Matrix.det_diagonal]
        apply Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr
        intro i _
        by_cases hi : i = 0
        ┬Ę simp [minkowskiSignature, hi]
        ┬Ę simp [minkowskiSignature, hi]

      theorem BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentz_det_sq_eq_one
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d) :
          (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A) ^ 2 = 1 := by
        have hmat :=
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentz_metric_preserving_matrix A
        have hdet := congrArg Matrix.det hmat
        have h╬Ę :
            (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d)).det ŌēĀ 0 :=
          BHW.complexLorentzEta_det_ne_zero d
        rw [Matrix.det_mul, Matrix.det_mul, Matrix.det_transpose] at hdet
        have hdet' :
            A.val.det * A.val.det *
                (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d)).det =
              1 * (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d)).det := by
          calc
            A.val.det * A.val.det *
                (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d)).det
                =
              A.val.det * (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d)).det *
                A.val.det := by ring
            _ = (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d)).det := hdet
            _ = 1 * (ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé (d := d)).det := by ring
        have hcancel := mul_right_cancelŌéĆ h╬Ę hdet'
        simpa [BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet, pow_two] using hcancel

      theorem BHW.det_eq_neg_one_of_sq_one_of_ne_one
          {a : Ōäé} (hsq : a ^ 2 = 1) (hne : a ŌēĀ 1) : a = -1 := by
        have hfactor : (a - 1) * (a + 1) = 0 := by
          calc
            (a - 1) * (a + 1) = a ^ 2 - 1 := by ring
            _ = 0 := by rw [hsq]; ring
        rcases mul_eq_zero.mp hfactor with hminus | hplus
        ┬Ę exact False.elim (hne (sub_eq_zero.mp hminus))
        ┬Ę exact eq_neg_of_add_eq_zero_left hplus

      def BHW.complexLorentzGroup_to_fullComplexLorentz
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d) :
          BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d :=
        { val := ╬ø.val
          metric_preserving := ╬ø.metric_preserving }

      def BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzMul
          (A B : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d) :
          BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d where
        val := A.val * B.val
        metric_preserving := by
          apply ComplexLorentzGroup.of_metric_preserving_matrix
          rw [Matrix.transpose_mul]
          calc
            B.val.transpose * A.val.transpose *
                ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé * (A.val * B.val)
                =
              B.val.transpose *
                  (A.val.transpose * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé * A.val) *
                B.val := by
                  simp only [Matrix.mul_assoc]
            _ = B.val.transpose * ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé * B.val := by
                  rw [BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentz_metric_preserving_matrix A]
            _ = ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé :=
                  BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentz_metric_preserving_matrix B

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_mul_det
          (A B : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet
              (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzMul A B) =
            BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A *
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet B := by
        simp [BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet,
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzMul, Matrix.det_mul]

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_mul_vectorAction
          (A B : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction
              (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzMul A B) v =
            BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A
              (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction B v) := by
        ext ╬╝
        simp only [BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction,
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzMul, Matrix.mul_apply]
        simp_rw [Finset.sum_mul]
        rw [Finset.sum_comm]
        congr 1
        ext ╬Į
        simp_rw [mul_assoc]
        rw [ŌåÉ Finset.mul_sum]

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_mul_configAction
          (A B : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzAction
              (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzMul A B) z =
            BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzAction A
              (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzAction B z) := by
        ext i ╬╝
        simpa [BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzAction] using
          congrFun (BHW.fullComplexLorentz_mul_vectorAction A B (z i)) ╬╝

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_to_complexLorentzGroup_of_det_one
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (hdet : BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A = 1) :
          Ōłā ╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ŌłĆ v,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø v =
                BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A v := by
        let ╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d :=
          { val := A.val
            metric_preserving := A.metric_preserving
            proper := hdet }
        exact Ō¤©╬ø, fun v => rflŌ¤®
      ```

      The proof of
      `hallWightmanFullComplexLorentz_metric_preserving_matrix` is the same
      componentwise-to-matrix calculation already used in
      `ComplexLorentzGroup.metric_preserving_matrix`, with the determinant
      field omitted.  The determinant-square proof above was scratch-checked
      against Lean's matrix API: take determinants in `AßĄĆ ╬Ę A = ╬Ę`, rewrite
      with `Matrix.det_mul` and `Matrix.det_transpose`, prove `det ╬Ę ŌēĀ 0`
      from the diagonal `┬▒1` entries of `ComplexLorentzGroup.╬ĘŌäé`, and cancel
      `det ╬Ę`.

      The matrix equation, determinant square, multiplication, vector action,
      tuple action, determinant-one conversion, `ofLinearMap` constructor, and
      `ofLinearMap_vectorAction` lemmas above are checked in production Lean.
      They are the only full-group algebra allowed before the Witt theorem.
      They do not mention `extendF`, scalar representatives, theorem-2
      locality, PET, EOW, or OS fields.

      The orthogonal-complement bridge must be stated through Mathlib's
      bilinear-form API, but the production pairing remains the already
      implemented `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner`.

      ```lean
      def BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm
          (d : Nat) :
          LinearMap.BilinForm Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) where
        toFun := fun u =>
          { toFun := fun v => BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d u v
            map_add' := BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right d u
            map_smul' := by
              intro a v
              exact BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right d a u v }
        map_add' := by
          intro u v
          ext w
          simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_left]
        map_smul' := by
          intro a u
          ext w
          simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_left]

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm_isRefl
          (d : Nat) :
          (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d).IsRefl := by
        intro x y hxy
        simpa [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm d y x] using hxy

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm_nondegenerate
          (d : Nat) :
          (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d).Nondegenerate := by
        apply LinearMap.BilinForm.Nondegenerate.ofSeparatingLeft
        intro w hw
        apply BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_left_nonDegenerate d
        intro v
        simpa [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm] using hw v

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace_to_restrict
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          ((BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d).restrict M).Nondegenerate := by
        apply LinearMap.BilinForm.Nondegenerate.ofSeparatingLeft
        intro x hx
        apply hM x
        intro y
        simpa [LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm] using hx y

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace_of_restrict
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hM :
            ((BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d).restrict M).Nondegenerate) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M := by
        intro x hx
        have hxker :
            x Ōłł LinearMap.ker
              ((BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d).restrict M) := by
          rw [LinearMap.mem_ker]
          ext y
          simpa [LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm] using hx y
        have hker := LinearMap.BilinForm.Nondegenerate.ker_eq_bot hM
        have : x Ōłł (ŌŖź : Submodule Ōäé M) := by
          simpa [hker] using hxker
        simpa using this

      def BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) :
          Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
        (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d).orthogonal M

      theorem BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_iff
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          {u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé} :
          u Ōłł BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M Ōåö
            ŌłĆ x : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) u = 0 := by
        rw [BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule,
          LinearMap.BilinForm.mem_orthogonal_iff]
        constructor
        ┬Ę intro h x
          simpa [LinearMap.BilinForm.IsOrtho,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm] using
            h (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) x.2
        ┬Ę intro h x hx
          simpa [LinearMap.BilinForm.IsOrtho,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm] using
            h Ō¤©x, hxŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
          (d : Nat)
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M) := by
        let O := BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M
        intro x horth
        apply Subtype.ext
        apply BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_left_nonDegenerate d
        intro v
        let R := BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap d M
        have hR_inj : Function.Injective R := by
          rw [ŌåÉ LinearMap.ker_eq_bot]
          exact
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical_eq_bot_of_nondegenerate d hM
        have hdim :
            Module.finrank Ōäé M = Module.finrank Ōäé (Module.Dual Ōäé M) := by
          rw [Subspace.dual_finrank_eq]
        have hR_surj : Function.Surjective R :=
          (LinearMap.injective_iff_surjective_of_finrank_eq_finrank
            hdim).1 hR_inj
        obtain Ō¤©m, hmŌ¤® :=
          hR_surj (BHW.complexMinkowskiToSubmoduleDual d M v)
        have hvm_orth : v - (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) Ōłł O := by
          rw [BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_iff]
          intro y
          have hmy :
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                v (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) := by
            have h := congrArg (fun f : Module.Dual Ōäé M => f y) hm
            simpa [R, BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap,
              BHW.complexMinkowskiToSubmoduleDual] using h
          rw [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_left, hmy, sub_self]
        have hxm :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 :=
          (BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_iff d M
            (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)).1 x.property m
        have hxvm :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (v - (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) = 0 :=
          horth Ō¤©v - (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé), hvm_orthŌ¤®
        rw [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right, hxm, sub_zero] at hxvm
        exact hxvm

      theorem BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank_eq_finrank_of_nondegenerate
          (d : Nat)
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M = Module.finrank Ōäé M := by
        have hrad_bot :
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M = ŌŖź := by
          apply le_antisymm
          ┬Ę intro x hx
            have hx0 : x = 0 := by
              apply hM x
              intro y
              have hxker :
                  BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap d M x = 0 := by
                simpa [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical] using hx
              exact congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe hxker) y
            simp [hx0]
          ┬Ę exact bot_le
        have hbotfin :
            Module.finrank Ōäé (ŌŖź : Submodule Ōäé M) = 0 :=
          finrank_bot Ōäé M
        rw [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank, hrad_bot, hbotfin,
          Nat.sub_zero]

      theorem BHW.sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_nontrivial_of_proper
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w)
          (hproper :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M ŌēĀ ŌŖź := by
        apply
          BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_ne_bot_of_finrank_lt d
        rw [BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData.M_finrank_eq_sourceGramRank
          d n S]
        exact hproper
      ```

      The proof of
      `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate` is now the direct
      quotient-dual proof above, not a dependency on a heavier Mathlib
      orthogonal-complement theorem.  The proof of
      `restrictedMinkowskiRank_eq_finrank_of_nondegenerate` first proves the
      restricted radical is bottom from `S.M_nondegenerate`, then unfolds
      `restrictedMinkowskiRank`.  The proof of
      `sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_nontrivial_of_proper` uses the checked
      `HWHighRankSpanIsometryData.M_finrank_eq_sourceGramRank` equation and
      then applies
      `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_ne_bot_of_finrank_lt`.  Its
      vector-valued version is the one-line
      `Submodule.exists_mem_ne_zero_of_ne_bot` extraction.

      Finally, the reflection theorem is not a sign-choice black box.  It is
      the explicit Householder map.  The nonisotropic vector is produced from
      nondegeneracy by polarization, and the determinant is computed by the
      standard line/quotient determinant formula for a module reflection.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_nonisotropic_in_nondegenerate_subspace
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          [Nontrivial M]
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          Ōłā u : M,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0 := by
        by_contra hnone
        push Not at hnone
        obtain Ō¤©x, hx_neŌ¤® := exists_ne (0 : M)
        have hx_zero : x = 0 := by
          apply hM x
          intro y
          have hxy_diag := hnone (x + y)
          have hx_diag := hnone x
          have hy_diag := hnone y
          have hexpand :
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  ((x + y : M) : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  ((x + y : M) : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                    (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                    (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) +
                  BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                    (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                    (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) +
                  BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                    (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                    (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) +
                  BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                    (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                    (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) := by
            simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_left,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right]
            ring
          have hsum :
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) +
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 := by
            rw [hexpand, hx_diag, hy_diag] at hxy_diag
            simpa using hxy_diag
          have hsym :
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm d
              (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          have htwo :
              (2 : Ōäé) * BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 := by
            calc
              (2 : Ōäé) * BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) +
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) := by ring
              _ =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) +
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) := by
                    rw [hsym]
              _ = 0 := hsum
          exact
            (mul_eq_zero.mp htwo).resolve_left
              (two_ne_zero (╬▒ := Ōäé))
        exact hx_ne hx_zero

      def BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hu : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0) :
          (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌåÆŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
        LinearMap.id -
          ((2 / BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) ŌĆó
            LinearMap.BilinForm.toLinHomFlip
              (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d)
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)).smulRight
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_apply
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hu : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0)
          (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear u hu v =
            v -
              ((2 * BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v
                  (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) /
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                  (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)) ŌĆó
                (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) := by
        simp [BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear,
          LinearMap.BilinForm.toLin'Flip_apply,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm,
          div_eq_mul_inv, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_preserves_inner
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hu : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0)
          (v w : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear u hu v)
              (BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear u hu w) =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v w := by
        rw [BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_apply u hu v,
          BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_apply u hu w]
        rw [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_left,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right]
        simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_left,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right]
        have hwu_sym :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) w =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d w
                (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm d
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) w
        rw [hwu_sym]
        field_simp [hu]
        ring

      theorem BHW.det_eq_of_conj_by_linearEquiv
          {V W : Type*}
          [AddCommGroup V] [Module Ōäé V]
          [AddCommGroup W] [Module Ōäé W]
          [FiniteDimensional Ōäé V] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé W]
          (L : V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] W)
          (T : W ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] W)
          (S : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V)
          (h :
            L.symm.toLinearMap.comp (T.comp L.toLinearMap) = S) :
          LinearMap.det T = LinearMap.det S := by
        have hdet := LinearMap.det_conj (A := Ōäé) T L.symm
        simpa [LinearMap.comp_assoc, h] using hdet.symm

      theorem BHW.det_restrict_reflection_span
          {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module Ōäé V]
          [FiniteDimensional Ōäé V]
          (x : V) (hx : x ŌēĀ 0)
          (f : Module.Dual Ōäé V)
          (hf : f x = 2)
          {e : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V}
          (he :
            e =
              ((Module.reflection (x := x) (f := f) hf :
                V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V))
          (W : Submodule Ōäé V)
          (hWdef : W = Ōäé ŌłÖ x)
          (hW : W Ōēż W.comap e) :
          LinearMap.det (e.restrict hW) = -1 := by
        subst e
        subst W
        let L : Ōäé ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Ōäé ŌłÖ x) :=
          LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton Ōäé V x hx
        have hconj :
            L.symm.toLinearMap.comp
                (((Module.reflection (x := x) (f := f) hf :
                  V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V).restrict hW).comp
                  L.toLinearMap =
              (-1 : Ōäé) ŌĆó LinearMap.id := by
          ext a
          -- Use `LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton_apply`,
          -- `Module.reflection_apply_self hf`, and
          -- `map_smul`; both sides are the scalar `-a`.
          simp [L, Module.reflection_apply_self hf]
        calc
          LinearMap.det
              (((Module.reflection (x := x) (f := f) hf :
                V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V).restrict hW)
              =
            LinearMap.det ((-1 : Ōäé) ŌĆó
              (LinearMap.id : Ōäé ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé)) := by
                exact BHW.det_eq_of_conj_by_linearEquiv
                  L _ _ hconj
          _ = -1 := by
                simp [LinearMap.det_smul, Module.finrank_self]

      theorem BHW.det_quotient_reflection_span
          {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module Ōäé V]
          [FiniteDimensional Ōäé V]
          (x : V) (_hx : x ŌēĀ 0)
          (f : Module.Dual Ōäé V)
          (hf : f x = 2)
          {e : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V}
          (he :
            e =
              ((Module.reflection (x := x) (f := f) hf :
                V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V))
          (W : Submodule Ōäé V)
          (hWdef : W = Ōäé ŌłÖ x)
          (hW : W Ōēż W.comap e) :
          LinearMap.det (W.mapQ W e hW) = 1 := by
        subst e
        subst W
        have hmap :
            (Ōäé ŌłÖ x).mapQ (Ōäé ŌłÖ x)
                ((Module.reflection (x := x) (f := f) hf :
                  V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) hW =
              LinearMap.id := by
          ext q
          induction q using Quotient.inductionOn with
          | h y =>
              rw [Submodule.mapQ_apply]
              apply Quotient.sound
              change
                ((Module.reflection (x := x) (f := f) hf :
                  V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) y - y) Ōłł Ōäé ŌłÖ x
              rw [Module.reflection_apply]
              change -(f y) ŌĆó x Ōłł Ōäé ŌłÖ x
              exact Submodule.smul_mem _ (-(f y))
                (Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x)
        rw [hmap, LinearMap.det_id]

      theorem BHW.det_moduleReflection_of_nonzero
          {V : Type*} [AddCommGroup V] [Module Ōäé V]
          [FiniteDimensional Ōäé V]
          (x : V) (hx : x ŌēĀ 0)
          (f : Module.Dual Ōäé V)
          (hf : f x = 2) :
          LinearMap.det
              ((Module.reflection (x := x) (f := f) hf :
                V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) = -1 := by
        let e :=
          ((Module.reflection (x := x) (f := f) hf :
            V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V)
        let W : Submodule Ōäé V := Ōäé ŌłÖ x
        have hW : W Ōēż W.comap e := by
          intro y hy
          rw [Submodule.mem_comap]
          rcases Submodule.mem_span_singleton.mp hy with Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®
          rw [map_smul, Module.reflection_apply_self hf]
          exact W.smul_mem (-a) (Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x)
        have hdet := LinearMap.det_eq_det_mul_det W e hW
        have hres :
            LinearMap.det (e.restrict hW) = -1 := by
          -- Conjugate `e.restrict hW` by
          -- `LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton Ōäé V x hx`; the conjugate is
          -- scalar multiplication by `-1` on `Ōäé`.
          exact BHW.det_restrict_reflection_span
            (x := x) hx f hf rfl W rfl hW
        have hquot :
            LinearMap.det (W.mapQ W e hW) = 1 := by
          -- On the quotient by `Ōäé ŌłÖ x`, `e y = y - f y ŌĆó x` is equal to
          -- `y`; use `Submodule.mapQ_apply`, quotient induction, and
          -- `LinearMap.det_id`.
          exact BHW.det_quotient_reflection_span
            (x := x) hx f hf rfl W rfl hW
        rw [hdet, hres, hquot, mul_one]

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_det
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hu : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0) :
          LinearMap.det
              (BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear u hu) = -1 := by
        let V := Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        let B := BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d
        let q :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (u : V) (u : V)
        have hu0 : (u : V) ŌēĀ 0 := by
          intro h0
          exact hu (by simp [q, h0])
        let f : Module.Dual Ōäé V :=
          (2 / q) ŌĆó LinearMap.BilinForm.toLinHomFlip B (u : V)
        have hf : f (u : V) = 2 := by
          simp [f, B, q, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm]
          field_simp [hu]
        have hR_eq :
            BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear u hu =
              ((Module.reflection (x := (u : V)) (f := f) hf :
                V ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) : V ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] V) := by
          ext v
          simp [BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_apply,
            Module.reflection_apply, f, q, B]
        rw [hR_eq]
        exact BHW.det_moduleReflection_of_nonzero
          (x := (u : V)) hu0 f hf

      def BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hu : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection_fix_subspace
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hu : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0)
          (x : M) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction
              (BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection u hu) x = x
      ```

      The nonisotropic-vector proof above was scratch-checked: assuming
      `B(x,x)=0` for every `x`, applying the assumption to `x+y` and using
      bilinearity gives `B(x,y)+B(y,x)=0`; symmetry turns this into
      `(2 : Ōäé) * B(x,y)=0`, and `two_ne_zero` gives `B(x,y)=0`, contradicting
      nondegeneracy on any chosen nonzero `x`.

      `fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear`,
      `fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_apply`,
      `fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_preserves_inner`,
      `fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_fix_subspace`,
      `fullComplexLorentzReflection`, `fullComplexLorentzReflection_det`,
      and `fullComplexLorentzReflection_fix_subspace` are now checked in
      `SourceFullComplexLorentz.lean`.  The proper-span consumer
      `fullComplexLorentz_det_neg_reflection_fixing_sourceSpan` is also
      checked there: it only combines the checked nonisotropic complement
      vector with this reflection packet.

      For `fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_preserves_inner`, expand
      `R v = v - (2 * B v u / B u u) ŌĆó u` and
      `R w = w - (2 * B w u / B u u) ŌĆó u`, use symmetry
      `B u w = B w u`, and cancel the two linear correction terms against
      the quadratic correction term using `hu`.  This expansion was
      scratch-checked with the local
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_left`,
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right`,
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_left`, and
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right` lemmas.

      The determinant proof is the standard determinant computation for a
      module reflection, not an undeclared orientation choice.  Let
      `W := Ōäé ŌłÖ u` and
      `e := (Module.reflection (x := u) (f := (2 / B u u) ŌĆó B(-,u)) hf)`
      with `hf : f u = 2`.  The line `W` is invariant and `e` acts on it by
      `-1`, proved after conjugating the restriction by
      `LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton Ōäé _ u hu0`.  On the quotient by
      `W`, `e y = y - f y ŌĆó u` is equal to `y`, because the correction term
      lies in `W`; quotient induction and `Submodule.mapQ_apply` identify the
      quotient map with `LinearMap.id`.  Finally
      `LinearMap.det_eq_det_mul_det W e hW` gives
      `det e = (-1) * 1 = -1`.  This is equivalent to decomposing into the
      reflection line plus an invariant complement, but it uses the exact
      quotient API Lean already exposes.

      The fix-subspace theorem uses `u Ōłł MßŚ«` to rewrite `B x u = 0`, so the
      Householder correction term is zero.
      The definition `fullComplexLorentzReflection` uses
      `LinearMap.toMatrix (Pi.basisFun Ōäé _) (Pi.basisFun Ōäé _)` applied to
      `fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear u hu`; its `metric_preserving`
      field is exactly
      `fullComplexLorentzReflectionLinear_preserves_inner` rewritten through
      the matrix/vector-action convention.

      These reflection details are load-bearing.  Replacing them by a theorem
      that merely says "choose the sign on the complement" would reintroduce
      the old determinant gap.

      Proof transcript for the reflection support:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_det_neg_reflection_fixing_sourceSpan
          ... := by
        have hMnondeg :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d S.M :=
          BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometryData_domain_nondegenerate (d := d) S
        have hcomp_nontrivial :
            Nontrivial
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M) :=
          BHW.sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_nontrivial_of_proper
            (d := d) hd S hproper
        have hcomp_nondeg :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M) :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
            (d := d) S.M hMnondeg
        rcases
          BHW.exists_nonisotropic_in_nondegenerate_subspace
            (d := d)
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M)
            hcomp_nondeg with
          Ō¤©u, huŌ¤®
        refine
          Ō¤©BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection u hu,
            BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection_det u hu,
            ?_Ō¤®
        intro x
        exact BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection_fix_subspace u hu x
      ```

      `sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_nontrivial_of_proper` is the dimension
      step: high-rank gives a nondegenerate source span, while
      `sourceGramMatrixRank < d + 1` says its dimension is strictly below the
      ambient dimension, so the orthogonal complement is nonzero.  The
      immediate vector-valued form is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_nonzero_mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_of_finrank_lt
          (d : Nat)
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hfin : Module.finrank Ōäé M < d + 1) :
          Ōłā v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
            v Ōłł BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M Ōł¦ v ŌēĀ 0 := by
        exact
          Submodule.exists_mem_ne_zero_of_ne_bot
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_ne_bot_of_finrank_lt
              d hfin)

      theorem BHW.exists_nonzero_mem_sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_of_orbitRank_rank_lt_spacetime
          (d n : Nat)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hzRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z)
          (hlt :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) <
              d + 1) :
          Ōłā v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
            v Ōłł
              BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) Ōł¦
            v ŌēĀ 0 := by
        exact
          Submodule.exists_mem_ne_zero_of_ne_bot
            (BHW.sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_nontrivial_of_orbitRank_rank_lt_spacetime
              d n hzRank hlt)

      theorem BHW.sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_nontrivial_of_proper
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w)
          (hproper :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) <
              d + 1) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M ŌēĀ ŌŖź := by
        apply
          BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_ne_bot_of_finrank_lt d
        rw [BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData.M_finrank_eq_sourceGramRank
          d n S]
        exact hproper

      theorem BHW.exists_nonzero_mem_sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_of_proper
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w)
          (hproper :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) <
              d + 1) :
          Ōłā v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
            v Ōłł BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M Ōł¦ v ŌēĀ 0 :=
        Submodule.exists_mem_ne_zero_of_ne_bot
          (BHW.sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_nontrivial_of_proper
            d n S hproper)

      theorem BHW.exists_nonisotropic_mem_sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_of_proper
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w)
          (hproper :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) <
              d + 1) :
          Ōłā u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēĀ 0 := by
        have hcomp_ne :
            BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M ŌēĀ ŌŖź :=
          BHW.sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_nontrivial_of_proper
            d n S hproper
        letI : Nontrivial
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M) :=
          (Submodule.nontrivial_iff_ne_bot).2 hcomp_ne
        exact
          BHW.exists_nonisotropic_in_nondegenerate_subspace d
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M)
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
              d S.M_nondegenerate)
      ```

      The complement is nondegenerate by the quotient-dual decomposition
      theorem above.  A nonzero nondegenerate complex symmetric space contains
      a nonisotropic vector: if
      `B(u,u) = 0` for every `u`, polarization gives
      `B(u,v) = 0` for all `u,v`, contradicting nondegeneracy.  The reflection
      is the explicit Householder map
      `v Ōå” v - (2 * B(v,u) / B(u,u)) ŌĆó u`; it preserves the form, has
      determinant `-1`, and fixes `S.M` because `u Ōłł S.MßŚ«`.

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate`: let
      `M := LinearMap.range (sourceCoefficientEval d n z)`.  The source Gram
      matrix is the matrix of the restricted complex Minkowski form pulled
      back along the surjection `sourceCoefficientEval d n z : (Fin n -> Ōäé) ->
      M`.  Therefore the first required bridge is
      `sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range`: quotient the
      coefficient space by `ker evalZ`, identify the quotient with `M` by
      `evalZ.quotKerEquivRange`, and observe that the pulled-back Gram
      bilinear form has radical
      `sourceCoefficientGramKernel`; its quotient rank is exactly
      `finrank M - finrank (restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M)`.

      The first bridge is implemented through three explicit support facts,
      not by identifying two different notions of rank informally:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_toLin_transpose
          (n : Nat)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n G =
            Matrix.toLin'
              ((Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => G i j)ßĄĆ) := by
        ext a j
        simp [BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap, Matrix.toLin'_apply,
          Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct, Matrix.transpose, mul_comm]

      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap
          (n : Nat)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G =
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n G))

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_mem_restrictedMinkowskiRadical_iff
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (a : Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
          let M := LinearMap.range evalZ
          (Ō¤©evalZ a, Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® : M) Ōłł
              BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M Ōåö
            ŌłĆ b : Fin n -> Ōäé,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (evalZ a) (evalZ b) = 0 := by
        intro evalZ M
        rw [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical, LinearMap.mem_ker]
        constructor
        ┬Ę intro h b
          have happ :=
            congrFun (show
              (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap d M)
                (Ō¤©evalZ a, Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® : M) = 0 from h)
              (Ō¤©evalZ b, Ō¤©b, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® : M)
          simpa [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap] using happ
        ┬Ę intro h
          ext y
          rcases y with Ō¤©yv, b, rflŌ¤®
          simpa [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap] using h b

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel_eq_eval_preimage_radical
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
            ((BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))).map
              (Submodule.subtype
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)))).comap
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z) := by
        let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
        let M := LinearMap.range evalZ
        let rad := BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M
        ext a
        have hmap :
            evalZ a Ōłł rad.map (Submodule.subtype M) Ōåö
              (Ō¤©evalZ a, Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® : M) Ōłł rad := by
          constructor
          ┬Ę rintro Ō¤©x, hx, hxvalŌ¤®
            have hx_eq : x = (Ō¤©evalZ a, Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® : M) :=
              Subtype.ext hxval
            simpa [hx_eq] using hx
          ┬Ę intro hx
            exact Ō¤©(Ō¤©evalZ a, Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® : M), hx, rflŌ¤®
        change
          a Ōłł LinearMap.ker
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)) Ōåö
            evalZ a Ōłł rad.map (Submodule.subtype M)
        rw [LinearMap.mem_ker, hmap,
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_zero_iff_eval_pair_eval_eq_zero,
          BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_mem_restrictedMinkowskiRadical_iff]

      theorem BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_restrictedRank
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Module.finrank Ōäé
            (LinearMap.range
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))) =
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))
      ```

      Proof transcript for the first bridge:
      `sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap` is the
      standard finite-matrix identification of the rank of the matrix
      `(G i j)` with the finrank of the range of the associated linear map
      `a Ōå” (j Ōå” Ōłæ i, a i * G i j)`: first prove
      `sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_toLin_transpose`, then use
      `Matrix.rank_transpose` and
      `Matrix.rank_eq_finrank_range_toLin` with
      `Pi.basisFun Ōäé (Fin n)` on both sides.

      For the preimage-radical theorem, unfold all three maps but do not leave
      the finite-sum step implicit.  The exact bridge is
      `sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_zero_iff_eval_pair_eval_eq_zero`: a
      coefficient vector `a` is in the scalar Gram kernel iff
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (sourceCoefficientEval d n z a)
        (sourceCoefficientEval d n z b) = 0`
      for every coefficient vector `b`.  The forward direction expands the
      right coefficient vector by
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_right` and uses the zero
      coordinates of `sourceCoefficientGramMap`; the reverse direction tests
      against `b = Pi.single j 1` and uses `sourceCoefficientEval_single`.
      Since every element of
      `M := LinearMap.range (sourceCoefficientEval d n z)` is some
      `sourceCoefficientEval d n z b`, this iff is precisely membership in
      `restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M`.

      Lean-shaped proof of the quotient rank computation:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_restrictedRank
          ... := by
        let E := Fin n -> Ōäé
        let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
        let M := LinearMap.range evalZ
        let gramMap :=
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        have hK_le : LinearMap.ker evalZ Ōēż LinearMap.ker gramMap :=
          BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_ker_le_gramKernel (d := d) n z
        let liftGram : (E Ō¦Ė LinearMap.ker evalZ) ŌåÆŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin n -> Ōäé) :=
          (LinearMap.ker evalZ).liftQ gramMap hK_le
        have hrange_lift :
            LinearMap.range liftGram = LinearMap.range gramMap := by
          simpa [liftGram] using
            Submodule.range_liftQ (p := LinearMap.ker evalZ)
              (f := gramMap) hK_le
        have hker_lift :
            LinearMap.ker liftGram =
              (LinearMap.ker gramMap).map
                (LinearMap.ker evalZ).mkQ := by
          simpa [liftGram] using
            Submodule.ker_liftQ (p := LinearMap.ker evalZ)
              (f := gramMap) hK_le
        have hker_pre :
            LinearMap.ker gramMap =
              ((BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M).map
                (Submodule.subtype M)).comap evalZ :=
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel_eq_eval_preimage_radical
            (d := d) n z
        have hquot :
            (E Ō¦Ė LinearMap.ker evalZ) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] M :=
          evalZ.quotKerEquivRange
        have hrad_quot :
            LinearMap.ker liftGram ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
              BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M := by
          -- Combine `hker_lift`, `hker_pre`, and `hquot`: the scalar Gram
          -- radical after quotienting by `ker evalZ` is exactly the
          -- restricted radical in `M`.
          exact BHW.scalarGramKernel_quot_equiv_restrictedRadical
            (d := d) n z hker_pre hker_lift hquot
        have hnullity :
            Module.finrank Ōäé (LinearMap.range liftGram) +
              Module.finrank Ōäé (LinearMap.ker liftGram) =
            Module.finrank Ōäé (E Ō¦Ė LinearMap.ker evalZ) :=
          LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker liftGram
        have hquot_finrank :
            Module.finrank Ōäé (E Ō¦Ė LinearMap.ker evalZ) =
              Module.finrank Ōäé M :=
          hquot.finrank_eq
        have hrad_finrank :
            Module.finrank Ōäé (LinearMap.ker liftGram) =
              Module.finrank Ōäé (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M) :=
          hrad_quot.finrank_eq
        have hrank :
            Module.finrank Ōäé (LinearMap.range gramMap) =
              Module.finrank Ōäé M -
                Module.finrank Ōäé
                  (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M) := by
          -- Rewrite rank-nullity for `liftGram` by `hrange_lift`,
          -- `hquot_finrank`, and `hrad_finrank`, then solve the
          -- natural-number subtraction.
          exact
            BHW.finrank_range_eq_sub_finrank_ker_of_rank_nullity
              hnullity hrange_lift hquot_finrank hrad_finrank
        simpa [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank] using hrank

      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range
          ... := by
        rw [BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap]
        exact
          BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_restrictedRank
            (d := d) n z
      ```

      The helper names
      `scalarGramKernel_quot_equiv_restrictedRadical` and
      `finrank_range_eq_sub_finrank_ker_of_rank_nullity` are proof-local
      linear algebra helpers if the implementation needs them; they are not
      new mathematical route surfaces.  Their content is exactly quotienting
      by `ker evalZ` with `Submodule.liftQ`, using
      `Submodule.range_liftQ`, `Submodule.ker_liftQ`,
      `LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker`, and transporting the radical
      through `evalZ.quotKerEquivRange`.

      The second bridge is
      `sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_orbitThreshold_of_range_degenerate`.  If the
      restricted radical is nonzero, then `M` is not the whole ambient space:
      otherwise ambient nondegeneracy of complex Minkowski space would force
      the radical vector to be zero.  Since the ambient vector space has
      dimension `d + 1`, a proper subspace has dimension at most `d`.  Also,
      `M` is generated by `n` source vectors, so `finrank M <= n`.  Hence a
      nonzero radical gives
      `restrictedMinkowskiRank d M <= finrank M - 1 < min d n`, and the rank
      bridge gives
      `sourceGramMatrixRank n (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < min d n`.
      This contradicts `HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z`.  Thus the restricted
      radical is zero, which is exactly
      `ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M`.  This is the
      finite-dimensional rank-radical step in Hall-Wightman Lemma 2; it is
      not the later source-map regularity theorem.

      The rank-radical bridge is itself decomposed into the following
      finite-dimensional support facts:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical_nontrivial_of_degenerate
          (d : Nat)
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hdeg :
            ┬¼ BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          0 < Module.finrank Ōäé
            (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M)

      theorem BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank_lt_finrank_of_degenerate
          (d : Nat)
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hdeg :
            ┬¼ BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M <
            Module.finrank Ōäé M

      theorem BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientEval_le
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Module.finrank Ōäé
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) <= n

      theorem BHW.finrank_restrictedSpan_le_d_of_degenerate
          (d : Nat)
          {M : Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hdeg :
            ┬¼ BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          Module.finrank Ōäé M <= d
      ```
