      Proof transcript for these support facts:
      `restrictedMinkowskiRadical_nontrivial_of_degenerate` unfolds
      `ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace` and
      `restrictedMinkowskiRadical`; the negated universal statement gives
      `x : M` with `x ŌēĀ 0` and zero pairing against every `y : M`, hence a
      nonzero element of the radical and positive finrank by
      `Submodule.one_le_finrank_iff`.  The range bound is the standard
      finite-dimensional inequality `LinearMap.finrank_range_le`, followed by
      `Module.finrank_fin_fun` for `Fin n -> Ōäé`.
      `restrictedMinkowskiRank_lt_finrank_of_degenerate` is then the arithmetic
      statement
      `finrank M - finrank radical < finrank M`: the radical is a subspace of
      `M`, so `Submodule.finrank_le` bounds its finrank by `finrank M`, and
      the positive radical finrank lets `omega` or `Nat.sub_lt` close the
      subtraction.
      For `finrank_restrictedSpan_le_d_of_degenerate`, first prove
      `M ŌēĀ ŌŖż`; if `M = ŌŖż`, transport the ambient nondegeneracy of the complex
      Minkowski form through the subtype equivalence and contradict `hdeg`.
      The ambient nondegeneracy input is the checked
      `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_left_nonDegenerate`: given
      `x : M` orthogonal to every `y : M`, use `M = ŌŖż` to test against every
      ambient vector `v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé`.  A proper submodule of the
      `(d + 1)`-dimensional ambient space then has finrank `< d + 1` by
      `Submodule.finrank_lt`, hence `<= d` by `Nat.lt_succ_iff`.

      Lean-shaped support proofs:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical_nontrivial_of_degenerate
          ... := by
        have hrad_ne :
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M ŌēĀ ŌŖź := by
          intro hbot
          apply hdeg
          intro x horth
          have hx_rad :
              x Ōłł BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M := by
            rw [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical, LinearMap.mem_ker]
            ext y
            simpa [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap] using horth y
          have hx_bot : x Ōłł (ŌŖź : Submodule Ōäé M) := by
            simpa [hbot] using hx_rad
          simpa using hx_bot
        exact Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le Nat.zero_lt_one
          ((Submodule.one_le_finrank_iff).2 hrad_ne)

      theorem BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank_lt_finrank_of_degenerate
          ... := by
        have hrad_pos :
            0 < Module.finrank Ōäé
              (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M) :=
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical_nontrivial_of_degenerate
            (d := d) hdeg
        have hrad_le :
            Module.finrank Ōäé (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M) <=
              Module.finrank Ōäé M :=
          Submodule.finrank_le _
        simp [BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank]
        omega

      theorem BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientEval_le
          ... := by
        have h :=
          LinearMap.finrank_range_le
            (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)
        simpa [Module.finrank_fin_fun] using h

      theorem BHW.finrank_restrictedSpan_le_d_of_degenerate
          ... := by
        have hM_ne_top : M ŌēĀ ŌŖż := by
          intro htop
          apply hdeg
          intro x horth
          apply Subtype.ext
          apply BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_left_nonDegenerate d
          intro v
          have hvM : v Ōłł M := by
            rw [htop]
            trivial
          exact horth Ō¤©v, hvMŌ¤®
        have hlt :
            Module.finrank Ōäé M <
              Module.finrank Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          Submodule.finrank_lt hM_ne_top
        have hlt' : Module.finrank Ōäé M < d + 1 := by
          simpa [Module.finrank_fin_fun] using hlt
        exact Nat.lt_succ_iff.mp hlt'
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the rank-drop theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_orbitThreshold_of_range_degenerate
          ... := by
        let M := LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)
        have hM_le_n : Module.finrank Ōäé M <= n :=
          BHW.finrank_range_sourceCoefficientEval_le
            d n z
        have hM_le_d : Module.finrank Ōäé M <= d :=
          BHW.finrank_restrictedSpan_le_d_of_degenerate
            (d := d) hdeg
        have hrank_lt_M :
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M <
              Module.finrank Ōäé M :=
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank_lt_finrank_of_degenerate
            (d := d) hdeg
        have hrank_lt_d :
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M < d :=
          lt_of_lt_of_le hrank_lt_M hM_le_d
        have hrank_lt_n :
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M < n :=
          lt_of_lt_of_le hrank_lt_M hM_le_n
        rw [BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range
          (d := d) n z]
        exact lt_min hrank_lt_d hrank_lt_n
      ```

      This packet is now implemented in `BHWPermutation/SourceRank.lean`
      without `[NeZero d]` or `hd : 2 <= d`: the rank-radical argument uses
      only finite-dimensional linear algebra and ambient nondegeneracy of
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner`.  No analyticity, connectedness, or BHW
      permutation machinery is involved in this rank-drop theorem.

      Lean-shaped proof of the nondegeneracy theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate ... := by
        by_contra hdeg
        have hlt :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) <
              min d n :=
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_orbitThreshold_of_range_degenerate
            d n z hdeg
        have hge :
            min d n <=
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) := by
          simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt,
            BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank] using hzRank
        exact not_lt_of_ge hge hlt
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the basic coefficient-kernel inclusion:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_ker_le_gramKernel ... := by
        intro a ha
        rw [LinearMap.mem_ker] at ha
        change
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a = 0
        ext j
        rw [BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_apply_sourceMinkowskiGram
          (d := d) n z a j]
        simp [ha, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner]
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the high-rank coefficient-kernel equality:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_eval_ker_eq_gramKernel ... := by
        let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
        apply le_antisymm
        ┬Ę exact BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_ker_le_gramKernel (d := d) n z
        ┬Ę intro a ha
          have hNondeg :
              BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
                (LinearMap.range evalZ) :=
            BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate
              (d := d) (n := n) hzRank
          have horth :
              ŌłĆ v : LinearMap.range evalZ,
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (evalZ a) (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 := by
            intro v
            rcases v with Ō¤©_, b, rflŌ¤®
            have ha0 :
                BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a = 0 :=
              by
                simpa [BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel] using
                  (LinearMap.mem_ker.mp ha)
            exact
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_zero_iff_eval_pair_eval_eq_zero
                d n z a).1 ha0 b
          have hEval_mem : evalZ a Ōłł LinearMap.range evalZ := Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®
          have hEval_zero :
              (Ō¤©evalZ a, hEval_memŌ¤® :
                LinearMap.range evalZ) = 0 :=
            hNondeg Ō¤©evalZ a, hEval_memŌ¤® horth
          simpa [LinearMap.mem_ker] using
            congrArg Subtype.val hEval_zero
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the kernel transport theorem used by the quotient
      span isometry:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_sourceCoefficientEval_ker_eq_of_sameSourceGram
          ... := by
        let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
        let evalW := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w
        have hwRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n w :=
          by
            simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt,
              BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank, hgram] using hzRank
        calc
          LinearMap.ker evalZ
              =
            BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) :=
                BHW.hw_highRank_eval_ker_eq_gramKernel
                  (d := d) (n := n) hzRank
          _ =
            BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) := by
                rw [hgram]
          _ = LinearMap.ker evalW :=
                (BHW.hw_highRank_eval_ker_eq_gramKernel
                  (d := d) (n := n) hwRank).symm
      ```

      Thus the assignment on coefficient classes is genuinely well-defined:
      the identity map on coefficient space descends only after the two kernel
      equalities have been proved.  The proof does not use local source-map
      regularity, connectedness of the scalar domain, or any adjacent
      `S'_n`/PET information.

      The proof of
      `hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData` is the paper's
      null-contraction construction: put the common lower-rank span into a
      standard frame, write the remaining discrepancy in a totally isotropic
      residual frame orthogonal to that span, choose a complex Lorentz
      one-parameter family contracting the whole residual frame, and define the
      two curves so that each parameter is in the ordinary extended tube and
      each curve stays in the complex Lorentz orbit of its own endpoint.  In
      four spacetime dimensions this residual frame has length at most one,
      matching Hall-Wightman's displayed `a_i w`; the dimension-general Lean
      surface must not assume that collapse.  No analytic equality is part of
      either geometric theorem.

      Lean-shaped proof of the high-rank orbit and low-rank geometric
      theorems:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometry_apply_eval
          ... :
          BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometryOfKernelEq (d := d) (n := n)
            (z := z) (w := w) hker
            (Ō¤©BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z a, Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® :
              LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) =
            (Ō¤©BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w a, Ō¤©a, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® :
              LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w)) := by
        apply Subtype.ext
        simp [BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometryOfKernelEq,
          LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange_symm_apply_image,
          LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange_apply_mk,
          Submodule.quotEquivOfEq_mk]

      noncomputable def BHW.hw_highRank_spanIsometryData_of_sameSourceGram
          ... := by
        let evalZ := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
        let evalW := BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w
        have hker : LinearMap.ker evalZ = LinearMap.ker evalW :=
          BHW.hw_highRank_sourceCoefficientEval_ker_eq_of_sameSourceGram
            (d := d) (n := n) (z := z) (w := w) hgram hzRank
        let T : LinearMap.range evalZ ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] LinearMap.range evalW :=
          BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometryOfKernelEq (d := d) (n := n)
            (z := z) (w := w) hker
        refine
          { M := LinearMap.range evalZ
            N := LinearMap.range evalW
            M_eq_range := rfl
            N_eq_range := rfl
            M_nondegenerate :=
              BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate
                (d := d) (n := n) (z := z) hzRank
            N_nondegenerate :=
              BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate
                (d := d) (n := n) (z := w)
                (by
                  simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt,
                    BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank, hgram] using hzRank)
            z_mem := by
              intro i
              exact Ō¤©Pi.single i (1 : Ōäé), by
                simpa [evalZ] using
                  BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_single d n z iŌ¤®
            w_mem := by
              intro i
              exact Ō¤©Pi.single i (1 : Ōäé), by
                simpa [evalW] using
                  BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_single d n w iŌ¤®
            T := T
            T_preserves := by
              intro x y
              rcases x with Ō¤©xv, hxvŌ¤®
              rcases hxv with Ō¤©ax, haxŌ¤®
              rcases y with Ō¤©yv, hyvŌ¤®
              rcases hyv with Ō¤©ay, hayŌ¤®
              subst xv
              subst yv
              have hTx :
                  T Ō¤©evalZ ax, Ō¤©ax, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® =
                    Ō¤©evalW ax, Ō¤©ax, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® :=
                BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometry_apply_eval
                  (d := d) (n := n) (z := z) (w := w)
                  hker ax
              have hTy :
                  T Ō¤©evalZ ay, Ō¤©ay, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® =
                    Ō¤©evalW ay, Ō¤©ay, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® :=
                BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometry_apply_eval
                  (d := d) (n := n) (z := z) (w := w)
                  hker ay
              rw [hTx, hTy]
              rw [BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_pair_eq_sum_gram
                (d := d) n z ax ay]
              rw [BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_pair_eq_sum_gram
                (d := d) n w ax ay]
              apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
              intro j _
              congr 1
              simp [BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap, hgram]
            T_z := by
              intro i
              simpa [BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_single (d := d) n z i,
                BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_single (d := d) n w i] using
                BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometry_apply_eval
                  (d := d) (n := n) (z := z) (w := w)
                  hker
                  (Pi.single i (1 : Ōäé)) }

      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit ... := by
        let S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w :=
          BHW.hw_highRank_spanIsometryData_of_sameSourceGram
            (d := d) (n := n) hzOrbit hgram
        rcases
          BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_detOne_of_sourceSpan
            (d := d) hd S hSO with
          Ō¤©╬ø, h╬øŌ¤®
        refine Ō¤©╬ø, by
          ext i ╬╝
          have h╬øi :
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (z i) = w i :=
            h╬ø i
          exact
            (by
              simpa [BHW.complexLorentzAction_apply_eq_vectorAction] using
                congrArg (fun v => v ╬╝) h╬øi).symmŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit_or_improper ... := by
        let S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w :=
          BHW.hw_highRank_spanIsometryData_of_sameSourceGram
            (d := d) (n := n) hzOrbit hgram
        rcases
          BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_or_improper_of_sourceSpan
            (d := d) hd S with hproper | himproper
        ┬Ę rcases hproper with Ō¤©╬ø, h╬øŌ¤®
          exact Or.inl Ō¤©╬ø, by
            ext i ╬╝
            have h╬øi :
                BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction ╬ø (z i) = w i :=
              h╬ø i
            exact
              (by
                simpa [BHW.complexLorentzAction_apply_eq_vectorAction] using
                  congrArg (fun v => v ╬╝) h╬øi).symmŌ¤®
        ┬Ę exact Or.inr himproper

      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData ... := by
        have N :=
          BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_sameSourceGram
            (d := d) hd (n := n) hz hw hzLow hgram
        exact BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData
          (d := d) hd hz hw N
      ```

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_sameSourceGram`: let
      `r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)`.
      Because the source Gram matrix is symmetric, the checked theorem
      `BHW.exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank` gives an
      injective `I : Fin r -> Fin n` with nonzero principal minor on the common
      Gram matrix.  This is the Lean replacement for the paper's implicit
      "choose independent scalar products" step.  The nonzero principal block
      identifies a nondegenerate rank-`r` selected span; equality of the two
      full Gram matrices gives the same scalar-coordinate coefficients and the
      same residual radical pairings for the remaining vectors.  Use the
      selected-span Gram equality to build the isometry of the two
      nondegenerate spans.  Because this is the low-rank singular-contraction
      normal-form packet, the selected-span extension is allowed only after
      fixing the determinant on the orthogonal complement: apply the
      determinant-sensitive extension with the automatic
      `sourceGramMatrixRank < d + 1` orientation compatibility supplied by
      the leftover complement, or equivalently use the subspace-isometry Witt
      theorem plus the explicit complement sign repair before packaging the
      result as a `ComplexLorentzGroup d` element.

      Lean-shaped selected-span coefficient construction:

      ```lean
      def BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanFrame_of_sameSourceGram ... := by
        classical
        let G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z
        let r : ŌäĢ := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G
        have hGsym : G Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n := by
          intro i j
          exact BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_symm d n z i j
        have hrank :
            (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => G i j).rank = r := by
          have hM : (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => G i j) = G := by
            ext i j
            rfl
          rw [hM]
          simp [BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank, r]
        let hExists :=
          BHW.exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank
            hGsym hrank
        let I : Fin r -> Fin n := Classical.choose hExists
        have hI_inj : Function.Injective I :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hExists).1
        have hminor : BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I G ŌēĀ 0 :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hExists).2
        let coeff : Fin n -> Fin r -> Ōäé :=
          BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G
        have hminorW :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) ŌēĀ 0 := by
          simpa [G, hgram] using hminor
        have hrankZ :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) = r := by
          rfl
        have hrankW :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = r := by
          simpa [G, r] using
            congrArg (BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n) hgram.symm
        have hsel :
            ŌłĆ a b,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z (I a) (I b) =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w (I a) (I b) := by
          intro a b
          simpa [G] using congrFun (congrFun hgram (I a)) (I b)
        have hleft_orth :
            ŌłĆ i a,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (z i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó z (I b))
                (z (I a)) = 0 := by
          simpa [coeff, G] using
            BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_head
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (I := I)
              hminor
        have hright_orth :
            ŌłĆ i a,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (w i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó w (I b))
                (w (I a)) = 0 := by
          simpa [coeff, G, hgram] using
            BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_head
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := w) (I := I)
              hminorW
        have hleft_pair_zero :
            ŌłĆ i j,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (z i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó z (I b))
                (z j - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ŌĆó z (I b)) = 0 := by
          simpa [coeff, G] using
            BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_residual_eq_zero
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (I := I)
              hrankZ hminor
        have hright_pair_zero :
            ŌłĆ i j,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (w i - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff i b ŌĆó w (I b))
                (w j - Ōłæ b : Fin r, coeff j b ŌĆó w (I b)) = 0 := by
          intro i j
          simpa [coeff, G, hgram] using
            BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_residual_eq_zero
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := w) (I := I)
              hrankW hminorW i j
        exact
          { r := r
            I := I
            rank_eq := rfl
            frame :=
              { I_injective := hI_inj
                principal_minor_ne := by simpa [G] using hminor
                selected_gram_eq := hsel
                coeff := coeff
                left_residual_orth := hleft_orth
                right_residual_orth := hright_orth
                residual_pairing_eq := by
                  intro i j
                  exact (hleft_pair_zero i j).trans
                    (hright_pair_zero i j).symm
                left_residual_pair_zero := hleft_pair_zero
                right_residual_pair_zero := hright_pair_zero } }
      ```

      The support theorem
      `BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_projection_eq` is just
      `row_i * AŌü╗┬╣ * A = row_i` for the selected principal Gram matrix
      `A`; `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_head` expands the
      bilinear pairing and applies that identity.  The residual pairing
      equality theorem expands both residual pairings into a finite polynomial
      in the common source Gram entries and the common coefficient formula,
      then rewrites by `hgram`.  Thus no source regularity, connectedness, or
      analytic continuation is used in this selected-span step.
      The zero residual-pairing theorem is the Schur-complement rank step:
      with the selected principal block invertible and the full source Gram
      matrix having rank exactly `r`, the Schur complement of that block is
      zero.  This must be implemented as an indexed statement, not as an
      informal block-matrix slogan.  From `hminor` obtain
      `hI : Function.Injective I` by
      `BHW.sourceMatrixMinor_ne_zero_left_injective`, set
      `e := BHW.selectedIndexSumEquiv I hI`, and obtain the determinant unit
      for the upper-left block from
      `BHW.isUnit_selectedIndexSumEquiv_toBlocksŌéüŌéü_det hI hI hminor`.  The
      rank hypothesis rewrites to
      `(Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => G i j).rank = Fintype.card (Fin r)`, so
      the checked theorem
      `BHW.rank_eq_card_iff_reindexed_rect_schur_complement_eq_zero` gives
      `BHW.reindexedRectSchurComplement (Matrix.of fun i j => G i j) e e = 0`.
      For arbitrary `i j : Fin n`, case-split on `e i` and `e j`.  If either
      index is `Sum.inl a`, rewrite that original index to `I a` using
      `BHW.selectedIndexSumEquiv_apply_selected` and close by
      `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_selected`.  If both are complementary
      indices `Sum.inr u` and `Sum.inr v`, use
      `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedComplement_residual_inner_residual_eq_schur` and the
      zero Schur-complement entry.  Thus the residual-residual Gram matrix is
      zero at every pair of original indices, with no hidden complement choice.

      Production Lean now checks this core in
      `SourceHWSelectedProjection.lean` as
      `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_residual_eq_zero`; the transcript is:

      ```lean
      have hI : Function.Injective I :=
        BHW.sourceMatrixMinor_ne_zero_left_injective
          (I := I) (J := I) hminor
      let G := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z
      let M : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé := Matrix.of fun i j => G i j
      let e := BHW.selectedIndexSumEquiv I hI
      have hunit :
          IsUnit
            (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G).det := by
        apply isUnit_iff_ne_zero.mpr
        simpa [G, BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix, BHW.sourceMatrixMinor]
          using hminor
      have hA :
          IsUnit ((M.reindex e e).toBlocksŌéüŌéü.det) := by
        simpa [M, G, e] using
          BHW.isUnit_selectedIndexSumEquiv_toBlocksŌéüŌéü_det
            (I := I) (J := I) hI hI hminor
      have hrankM : M.rank = Fintype.card (Fin r) := by
        simpa [M, G, BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank] using hrank
      have hschur :
          BHW.reindexedRectSchurComplement M e e = 0 :=
        (BHW.rank_eq_card_iff_reindexed_rect_schur_complement_eq_zero
          (Z := M) (er := e) (ec := e) hA).mp hrankM
      intro i j
      rcases hi : e i with a | u
      ┬Ę have hiI : i = I a := by
          calc
            i = e.symm (e i) := (e.symm_apply_apply i).symm
            _ = e.symm (Sum.inl a) := by rw [hi]
            _ = I a := by
              rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
              exact (BHW.selectedIndexSumEquiv_apply_selected I hI a).symm
        subst i
        simp [BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_selected hminor a]
      ┬Ę rcases hj : e j with b | v
        ┬Ę have hjI : j = I b := by
            calc
              j = e.symm (e j) := (e.symm_apply_apply j).symm
              _ = e.symm (Sum.inl b) := by rw [hj]
              _ = I b := by
                rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
                exact (BHW.selectedIndexSumEquiv_apply_selected I hI b).symm
          subst j
          simp [BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_selected hminor b]
        ┬Ę have hpair :=
            BHW.hwLemma3_selectedComplement_residual_inner_residual_eq_schur
              (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (I := I)
              hI hminor u v
          have hentry :
              BHW.reindexedRectSchurComplement M e e u v = 0 := by
            simpa [M, e] using congrFun (congrFun hschur u) v
          simpa [G, M, e, hi, hj] using hpair.trans hentry
      ```

      The aligned residual-frame theorem is then a finite-dimensional Witt
      alignment statement, not a notation choice, and it has two separate
      stages.

      This is now checked as
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanAlignment_of_selectedSpanFrame`.  Let
      `Mz := Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range fun a : Fin r => z (I a))` and
      `Mw := Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range fun a : Fin r => w (I a))`.  The
      nonzero principal minor gives linear independence of the selected
      vectors on both sides and nondegeneracy of the restricted form on both
      selected spans.  The field `S.selected_gram_eq` defines the linear
      isometry `Tsel : Mz ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] Mw` by
      `Tsel Ō¤©z (I a), _Ō¤® = Ō¤©w (I a), _Ō¤®`; in production this is routed through
      the checked selected-tuple rank theorem and high-rank span-isometry
      packet.  The determinant-one selected-span orbit theorem gives
      `╬øsel : ComplexLorentzGroup d` with
      `╬øsel ŌĆó z (I a) = w (I a)`.  Now set
      `M := Mw`,
      `╬Š i := Ōłæ b : Fin r, S.coeff i b ŌĆó w (I b)`,
      `leftResidual i := ╬øsel ŌĆó z i - ╬Š i`, and
      `rightResidual i := w i - ╬Š i`.  The orthogonality fields in
      `HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame`, transported by `╬øsel` on the left, prove
      both residual families are orthogonal to `M`; the Schur-zero fields,
      again transported by `╬øsel` on the left, prove both residual families
      have zero mutual pairings.  This is why
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_after_selectedAlignment` takes the
      alignment object as input: before `╬øsel` is applied, there is no common
      selected span in which the statement is true.

      Lean-shaped selected-alignment skeleton:

      ```lean
      let A :=
        BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanAlignment_of_selectedSpanFrame
          (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (w := w)
          (I := I) S hproper
      have hleft_residual_mem :
          ŌłĆ i,
            A.leftResidual i Ōłł
              Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range A.leftResidual) := by
        intro i
        exact Submodule.subset_span Ō¤©i, rflŌ¤®
      have hright_residual_mem :
          ŌłĆ i,
            A.rightResidual i Ōłł
              Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range A.rightResidual) := by
        intro i
        exact Submodule.subset_span Ō¤©i, rflŌ¤®
      ```

      Second prove the common residual-frame-extension producer
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_residualFrameExtensionData_of_selectedAlignment`.
      Apply `BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_after_selectedAlignment A` to
      get `Rleft` and `Rright` inside `A.MßŚ«`.  The proof of the alignment
      theorem is the explicit Witt-basis argument in the nondegenerate
      quotient/complement of `A.M`: choose a maximal totally isotropic
      subspace `Q` of `A.MßŚ«` containing `Rright`; choose an injective linear
      map from `Rleft` into `Q` (possible because both are totally isotropic
      and `Q` has maximal Witt dimension in the complement); the direct sum of
      the identity on `A.M` and this zero-form isometry on `Rleft` preserves
      the complex Minkowski form on its domain, because both residual
      subspaces are orthogonal to `A.M` and have zero internal form.  The
      remaining extension must **not** call a generic degenerate
      `complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry`.  Instead it must be
      the specific hyperbolic-basis construction for
      `A.M ŌŖö Rleft ŌŖö RleftDual` and
      `A.M ŌŖö Q_sub ŌŖö Q_subDual`, extended by identity/sign-repaired on the
      nondegenerate complement, with determinant-one output.  That theorem
      returns `╬øfix`, fixing `A.M`, with `╬øfix(Rleft) ŌŖå Q` and
      `Rright ŌŖå Q`.  The producer returns this as
      `HWLowRankResidualFrameExtensionData`.  The checked conversion
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_residualAlignmentData_of_frameExtensionData` then exposes
      the finite frame fields needed by
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_commonIsotropicFrameData_of_residualAlignmentData`, where
      membership in `Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)` is converted to
      coefficient functions by `BHW.exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame`;
      this step is explicit so that `aLeft` and `aRight` are not hidden choices.

      Lean-shaped residual-frame extraction:

      ```lean
      rcases
        BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_orthogonalComplement_after_selectedAlignment
          (d := d) (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (w := w)
          (I := I) (S := S) A with
        Ō¤©Rleft, Rright, hRleft_eq, hRright_eq,
          hRleft_le_orth, hRright_le_orth, hM_disjoint_left,
          hM_disjoint_right, hRleft_iso, hRright_isoŌ¤®
      have hRleft_orth :
          ŌłĆ x : Rleft, ŌłĆ m : A.M,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 := by
        intro x m
        exact
          (BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_iff
            d A.M (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)).1
            (hRleft_le_orth x.2) m
      have hRright_orth :
          ŌłĆ x : Rright, ŌłĆ m : A.M,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) = 0 := by
        intro x m
        exact
          (BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_iff
            d A.M (x : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)).1
            (hRright_le_orth x.2) m
      let E := BHW.hw_lowRank_residualFrameExtensionData_of_selectedAlignment
          (d := d) hd
          (n := n) (r := r) (z := z) (w := w)
          (I := I) (S := S) hproper A
      let D :=
        BHW.hw_lowRank_residualAlignmentData_of_frameExtensionData
          (d := d) (A := A) E
      let C :=
        BHW.hw_lowRank_commonIsotropicFrameData_of_residualAlignmentData
          (d := d) A D
      exact
        BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_commonFrameData
          (d := d) hd hz hw A C
      ```

      In production Lean,
      `hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_orthogonalComplement_after_selectedAlignment`
      is the preferred packet:
      `hRleft_le_orth` and `hRright_le_orth` are the inputs for work inside
      `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d A.M`; `hM_disjoint_left` and
      `hM_disjoint_right` are the direct-sum witnesses used when defining the
      partial identity-plus-residual isometry.  If a proof still needs the
      pointwise orthogonality hypotheses, recover them by using
      `mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_iff` on the inclusions, rather
      than running a second extraction path.

      The checked consumer
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_commonFrameData` now performs the
      rest of this paragraph in production Lean.  From `C.left_eq` and
      `C.right_eq` it has the two endpoint expansions in the common frame.
      It chooses a dual isotropic frame `qDual` by
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicDualFrame_of_residualFrame`, packages the
      determinant-one null boost by
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_selectedHyperbolicContractionFamily`, proves the
      two orbit-curve identities by
      `BHW.complexLorentzAction_residualExpansion`, obtains extended-tube
      membership from `BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube`,
      and proves convergence by `BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg_base`.
      Thus the only unchecked low-rank finite-dimensional content is the
      producer of `HWLowRankResidualAlignmentData`; coefficient extraction,
      the common-data expansion equations, the dual frame, contraction family,
      tube membership, and convergence fields are no longer part of the
      blocker.

      The `base_mem`, `contracted_left_mem`, and `contracted_right_mem` fields
      of `HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm` are not independent assumptions.  They
      are filled from
      `BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube`, once for the
      endpoint `BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø0 z` with coefficients `aLeft`, and
      once for the endpoint `w` with coefficients `aRight`.  The base obtained
      from the two applications is the same `╬Š`; the contracted configurations
      are the special coefficient choices
      `fun i c => Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c` and
      `fun i c => Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c`.

      Lean-shaped final assembly inside
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_sameSourceGram` after the
      selected span, residual frame, dual frame, and contraction family have
      been constructed:

      ```lean
      have hleft_endpoint :
          (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, aLeft i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
            BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        simpa [left_eq] using
          BHW.complexLorentzAction_mem_extendedTube
            (d := d) n ╬ø0 hz
      have hright_endpoint :
          (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, aRight i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
            BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        simpa [right_eq] using hw
      rcases BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          (d := d) hd hleft_endpoint q_pair_zero q_orth with
        Ō¤©hbase_mem, hleft_allŌ¤®
      rcases BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          (d := d) hd hright_endpoint q_pair_zero q_orth with
        Ō¤©_, hright_allŌ¤®
      have hleft_contract_mem :
          ŌłĆ t,
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        intro t
        simpa [mul_assoc] using
          hleft_all (fun i c => Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c)
      have hright_contract_mem :
          ŌłĆ t,
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        intro t
        simpa [mul_assoc] using
          hright_all (fun i c => Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c)
      have hleft_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ŌäØ =>
              fun i ╬╝ =>
                ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c ╬╝)
            atTop (nhds ╬Š) := by
        -- finite-coordinate continuity plus
        -- `Real.tendsto_exp_neg_atTop_nhds_zero`
	        exact BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg_base
	          (d := d) (╬Š := ╬Š) (a := aLeft) (q := q)
      have hright_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ŌäØ =>
              fun i ╬╝ =>
                ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c ╬╝)
            atTop (nhds ╬Š) := by
	        exact BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg_base
	          (d := d) (╬Š := ╬Š) (a := aRight) (q := q)
      exact
        { ╬ø0 := ╬ø0
          ╬Š := ╬Š
          s := s
          aLeft := aLeft
          aRight := aRight
          q := q
          qDual := qDual
          left_eq := left_eq
          right_eq := right_eq
          base_mem := hbase_mem
          q_pair_zero := q_pair_zero
          qDual_pair_zero := qDual_pair_zero
          q_dual := q_dual
          q_orth := q_orth
          qDual_orth := qDual_orth
          contract := contract
          contract_fix_╬Š := contract_fix_╬Š
          contract_scale_q := contract_scale_q
          contract_scale_qDual := contract_scale_qDual
          contracted_left_mem := hleft_contract_mem
          contracted_right_mem := hright_contract_mem
          contracted_left_tendsto := hleft_tendsto
          contracted_right_tendsto := hright_tendsto }
      ```

      The helper `BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg_base` is pure finite
      topology: each coordinate is a finite sum of constants multiplied by
	      `Real.exp (-t)`, so it tends to zero; adding the constant coordinate
	      `╬Š i ╬╝` gives convergence to `╬Š`.  This helper is now checked in
	      `SourceHWCommonResidualFrame.lean`.

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData`: define
        `base := N.╬Š`,
        `curve_left t :=
          fun i ╬╝ =>
            N.╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin N.s,
              Real.exp (-t) * N.aLeft i c * N.q c ╬╝`,
        and
        `curve_right t :=
          fun i ╬╝ =>
            N.╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin N.s,
              Real.exp (-t) * N.aRight i c * N.q c ╬╝`.
        The left and right curves are in `BHW.ExtendedTube d n` by
        `N.contracted_left_mem t` and `N.contracted_right_mem t`.  The base
        point is in the extended tube by the `N.base_mem` field, which was
        produced upstream by
        `BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube`; it is not
        inferred from closedness of `ExtendedTube`.  The two curves tend to
        `base` by `N.contracted_left_tendsto` and
        `N.contracted_right_tendsto`.  For each `t`, the Lorentz transform
        `N.contract t * N.╬ø0` carries `z` to `curve_left t`, and
        `N.contract t` carries `w` to `curve_right t`, by `N.left_eq`,
        `N.right_eq`, `N.contract_fix_╬Š`, and `N.contract_scale_q`.  These are
        exactly the two orbit fields consumed by the analytic limiting proof.

      Lean-shaped proof of
      `BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData`:

      ```lean
      def BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData ... := by
        let curve_left : ŌäØ -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun t i ╬╝ =>
            N.╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin N.s,
              Real.exp (-t) * N.aLeft i c * N.q c ╬╝
        let curve_right : ŌäØ -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun t i ╬╝ =>
            N.╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin N.s,
              Real.exp (-t) * N.aRight i c * N.q c ╬╝
        refine
          { base := N.╬Š
            curve_left := curve_left
            curve_right := curve_right
            base_mem := N.base_mem
            curve_left_mem := N.contracted_left_mem
            curve_right_mem := N.contracted_right_mem
            curve_left_tendsto_base := by
              simpa [curve_left] using N.contracted_left_tendsto
            curve_right_tendsto_base := by
              simpa [curve_right] using N.contracted_right_tendsto
            curve_left_orbit := by
              intro t
              refine Ō¤©N.contract t * N.╬ø0, by
                ext i ╬╝
                calc
                  curve_left t i ╬╝
                      =
                    BHW.complexLorentzAction (N.contract t)
                      (BHW.complexLorentzAction N.╬ø0 z) i ╬╝ := by
                        -- Expand `N.left_eq`, distribute the finite sum
                        -- through the matrix action, and use
                        -- `N.contract_fix_╬Š` plus `N.contract_scale_q`.
                        simp [curve_left, BHW.complexLorentzAction,
                          N.left_eq, N.contract_fix_╬Š,
                          N.contract_scale_q, Finset.mul_sum,
                          Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm,
                          mul_comm]
                  _ =
                    BHW.complexLorentzAction
                      (N.contract t * N.╬ø0) z i ╬╝ := by
                        simpa using
                          (BHW.complexLorentzAction_mul
                            (d := d) (n := n) (N.contract t) N.╬ø0 z).symmŌ¤®
            curve_right_orbit := by
              intro t
              refine Ō¤©N.contract t, by
                ext i ╬╝
                -- Same finite-coordinate calculation, using `N.right_eq`.
                simp [curve_right, BHW.complexLorentzAction, N.right_eq,
                  N.contract_fix_╬Š, N.contract_scale_q, Finset.mul_sum,
                  Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]Ō¤® }
      ```

      Proof transcript for `BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv`:
      unfold the ordinary extended tube representation
      `z = complexLorentzAction ╬øŌéĆ wŌéĆ` with `wŌéĆ Ōłł ForwardTube d n`; then
      `complexLorentzAction ╬ø z =
      complexLorentzAction (╬ø * ╬øŌéĆ) wŌéĆ`.  Unfold `BHW.extendF` at both
      points and use the checked preimage-independence lemma for `extendF`
      in the current BHW core support with `hF_cinv`.  The exact Lean call is
      to `BHWCore.extendF_preimage_eq`, not to the production
      `BHW.extendF_preimage_eq`: the latter is the older theorem whose
      signature takes restricted real invariance and derives complex
      invariance internally.  The definitions
      `BHW.extendF`, `BHW.ForwardTube`, `BHW.ExtendedTube`, and
      `BHW.complexLorentzAction` are definitionally equal to their
      `BHWCore` counterparts, so this bridge is a `change`/`simpa` step, not a
      new mathematical theorem.
      This is the same proof shape as the existing restricted-Lorentz theorem
      `extendF_complex_lorentz_invariant`, but using the already-complex
      invariance hypothesis directly.

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (_hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz : z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          BHW.extendF F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
            BHW.extendF F z := by
        classical
        rcases Set.mem_iUnion.mp hz with Ō¤©╬ø0, w0, hw0, hz_eqŌ¤®
        have hex_z :
            Ōłā (w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              w Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n Ōł¦
                Ōłā ╬ō : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
                  z = BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ō w :=
          Ō¤©w0, hw0, ╬ø0, hz_eqŌ¤®
        have hex_Lz :
            Ōłā (w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              w Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n Ōł¦
                Ōłā ╬ō : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
                  BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z =
                    BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ō w := by
          exact Ō¤©w0, hw0, ╬ø * ╬ø0, by
            rw [hz_eq, BHW.complexLorentzAction_mul]Ō¤®
        simp only [BHW.extendF]
        rw [dif_pos hex_Lz, dif_pos hex_z]
        rcases hex_Lz.choose_spec with Ō¤©hwL, ╬ōL, h╬ōLŌ¤®
        rcases hex_z.choose_spec with Ō¤©hwZ, ╬ōZ, h╬ōZŌ¤®
        change F hex_Lz.choose = F hex_z.choose
        exact
          BHWCore.extendF_preimage_eq (d := d) n F
            (by
              intro ╬ō y hy h╬ōy
              exact hF_cinv ╬ō y hy h╬ōy)
            hwL hwZ
            (╬øŌéü := ╬ōL) (╬øŌéé := ╬ø * ╬ōZ)
            (by
              calc
                BHWCore.complexLorentzAction ╬ōL hex_Lz.choose
                    = BHWCore.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z := h╬ōL.symm
                _ =
                  BHWCore.complexLorentzAction (╬ø * ╬ōZ) hex_z.choose := by
                    exact
                      (congrArg (BHWCore.complexLorentzAction ╬ø) h╬ōZ).trans
                        (BHWCore.complexLorentzAction_mul
                          ╬ø ╬ōZ hex_z.choose).symm)
      ```

      The final `congrArg` step is intentionally used instead of
      `rw [h╬ōZ]`: `hex_z.choose` is dependent on the chosen proof of the
      preimage existential, and unrestricted rewriting of `z` can make Lean
      abstract a motive in which that choice no longer typechecks.  The
      `congrArg`/`trans` form has been checked in a scratch Lean theorem
      against `SourceExtension.lean`.

      The hypotheses `hd` and `_hF_holo` are present because the theorem is
      consumed beside `extendF_holomorphicOn` in the Hall-Wightman branch-law
      proof; the invariance calculation itself only needs `hF_cinv`,
      `ExtendedTube` as the complex Lorentz orbit of `ForwardTube`, and
      `BHWCore.extendF_preimage_eq`.  If Lean does not unfold the definitional
      equalities automatically, insert:

      ```lean
      change BHWCore.extendF F (BHWCore.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
        BHWCore.extendF F z
      ```

      before unfolding `extendF`.

      Lean-shaped proof of the branch law after these Lemma-2 subfacts:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singularLimit_extendF_eq ... := by
        have hExtend_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          (BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n F hF_holo hF_cinv).continuousOn
        have hleft_val :
            ŌłĆ t, BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_left t) =
              BHW.extendF F z := by
          intro t
          rcases hsingular.curve_left_orbit t with Ō¤©╬ø, h╬øŌ¤®
          rw [h╬ø]
          exact BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv ╬ø hz
        have hright_val :
            ŌłĆ t, BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_right t) =
              BHW.extendF F w := by
          intro t
          rcases hsingular.curve_right_orbit t with Ō¤©╬ø, h╬øŌ¤®
          rw [h╬ø]
          exact BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv ╬ø hw
        have hleft_lim :
            Tendsto
              (fun t : ŌäØ => BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_left t))
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          (hExtend_cont.continuousWithinAt hsingular.base_mem).tendsto.comp
            (tendsto_nhdsWithin_iff.mpr
              Ō¤©hsingular.curve_left_tendsto_base,
                Eventually.of_forall hsingular.curve_left_memŌ¤®)
        have hright_lim :
            Tendsto
              (fun t : ŌäØ => BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_right t))
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          (hExtend_cont.continuousWithinAt hsingular.base_mem).tendsto.comp
            (tendsto_nhdsWithin_iff.mpr
              Ō¤©hsingular.curve_right_tendsto_base,
                Eventually.of_forall hsingular.curve_right_memŌ¤®)
        have hz_lim :
            Tendsto (fun _ : ŌäØ => BHW.extendF F z)
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          Filter.Tendsto.congr'
            (Eventually.of_forall fun t => hleft_val t) hleft_lim
        have hw_lim :
            Tendsto (fun _ : ŌäØ => BHW.extendF F w)
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          Filter.Tendsto.congr'
            (Eventually.of_forall fun t => hright_val t) hright_lim
        have hz_base :
            BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F hsingular.base :=
          tendsto_nhds_unique tendsto_const_nhds hz_lim
        have hw_base :
            BHW.extendF F w = BHW.extendF F hsingular.base :=
          tendsto_nhds_unique tendsto_const_nhds hw_lim
        exact hz_base.trans hw_base.symm

      theorem BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq ... := by
        classical
        rcases BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_fiberAlternative
            (d := d) hd (n := n) hz hw hgram with horbit | himproper_or_singular
        ┬Ę rcases horbit with Ō¤©╬ø, hw_eqŌ¤®
          calc
            BHW.extendF F z
                = BHW.extendF F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) := by
                    exact (BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
                      (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv ╬ø hz).symm
            _ = BHW.extendF F w := by simpa [hw_eq]
        ┬Ę rcases himproper_or_singular with himproper | hsingular
          ┬Ę exact
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzInvariantOnExtendedTube_eq
                (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hF_full hz hw himproper
          ┬Ę exact BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singularLimit_extendF_eq
              (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hz hw hgram hsingular
      ```

      The singular-limit proof itself is not optional.  Its transcript is:
      put the two equal-Gram configurations into Hall-Wightman Lemma-2 normal
      form; obtain the finite totally isotropic residual frame `q`, the two
      coefficient families `aLeft` and `aRight`, a common base `╬Š`, and a
      one-parameter complex Lorentz family `A ╬Ė` with `A ╬Ė q_c -> 0` for every
      residual-frame vector while `A ╬Ė` fixes `╬Š`.  Build the two extended-tube
      curves in the Lorentz orbits of `z` and `w`, both tending to `╬Š`; use
      `BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv` to show each curve has the
      same value as its endpoint; pass both limits to the common base using
      continuity from `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn`.  This is the low-rank
      continuity step in Hall-Wightman, not a new theorem route.

      Lemma-2 production-readiness ledger.  This is the first real
      scalar-source blocker.  The final branch law
      `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq` is only an assembly theorem
      after every row below is implemented as a proof term or replaced by a
      checked local support theorem with the same statement.  In this ledger
      "transcript pinned" means the mathematical proof route and Lean-sized
      support surfaces are specified; it does **not** mean the corresponding
      production Lean declaration exists.

      | Lemma-2 subpacket | Current status | Implementation-level proof content |

      | Coefficient maps and scalar-kernel bridge: `sourceCoefficientEval`, `sourceCoefficientGramMap`, `sourceCoefficientGramKernel`, `sourceCoefficientGramMap_apply_sourceMinkowskiGram`, `sourceCoefficientEval_single`, `sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_zero_iff_eval_pair_eval_eq_zero`, `sourceCoefficientEval_pair_eq_sum_gram`, `sourceCoefficientEval_ker_le_gramKernel` | Implemented and exact-file checked in `BHWPermutation/SourceCoefficient.lean`; umbrella import checked in `BHWPermutation.lean`. | Finite-sum linear maps, the checked local pairing `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner`, `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_left`/`_right`, and `sourceMinkowskiGram_apply_eq_complexInner`; no analytic input. |
