      The oriented max-rank continuation layer consumes the following public
      corollary of the determinant/Witt row:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceOrientedInvariant_maxRank_properOrbit
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hzmax :
            BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z))
          (hinv :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w) :
          Ōłā ╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            w = BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z
      ```

      Its proof unfolds the oriented invariant into same ordinary Gram data
      plus equality of all selected full-frame determinants.  Same Gram data
      gives `HWHighRankSpanIsometryData`.  In the proper-span branch,
      determinant repair gives a determinant-`1` extension directly.  In the
      full-ambient-rank branch, choose a selected full frame with nonzero
      determinant from `hzmax`; the determinant action formula plus `hinv`
      forces the full-frame extension determinant to be `1`, excluding the
      improper branch.  Convert the determinant-`1` full Lorentz element to
      `ComplexLorentzGroup d` and rewrite vectorwise equality to the displayed
      configuration equality.

      After the theorem-2 proof-doc gate is fully closed, the first possible
      remaining production Lean target in this Lemma-2 block is therefore the
      high-rank determinant-Witt row or one of the later
      low-rank geometric rows above, not
      `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq` itself.  This is a future
      implementation-order note, not permission to start production Lean while
      the oriented scalar-source producer ledger remains open.  A
      direct implementation of the final branch law before the
      coefficient-kernel, high-rank Witt, low-rank residual-frame, and
      tube-stability rows exist would only recreate the same missing
      mathematics behind a wrapper.

      The scalar-domain geometry is split similarly:

      ```lean
      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 / Section 2: every scalar point in `S'_n`
      has a relatively open scalar neighborhood inside the Gram image of the
      ordinary extended tube. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_at
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hZ : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :
          Ōłā O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen O Ōł¦ Z Ōłł O Ōł¦
            O Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_subset_sourceComplexGramVariety
          [NeZero d] (n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ŌŖå
            BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_connected
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) :
          IsConnected (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      -- Requires importing
      -- `OSReconstruction.ComplexLieGroups.Connectedness.BHWPermutation.SourceComplexChart`
      -- for `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram`; it is not exported by
      -- `SourceExtension.lean` alone.
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_subset_sourceComplexGramVariety
          [NeZero d] (n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ŌŖå
            BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
        intro Z hZ
        rcases hZ with Ō¤©z, hzET, rflŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_connected ... := by
        simpa [BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain] using
          (BHW.isConnected_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)).image
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n)
            (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous.continuousOn

      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected ... := by
        exact Ō¤©
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen (d := d) hd n,
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_connected (d := d) hd nŌ¤®
      ```

      The subset proof uses only the repo definitions:
      `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n` is the image of `ExtendedTube d n`
      under `sourceMinkowskiGram d n`, while
      `sourceComplexGramVariety d n` is the full range of that same Gram map.
      The witness `hzET` is deliberately unused; extended-tube membership is
      needed for scalar-domain membership, not for variety membership.  This
      is a definitional support lemma, not Hall-Wightman content.

      The proof of `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen` is the
      Hall-Wightman neighborhood theorem: for
      `Z = sourceMinkowskiGram d n z`, Lemma 3 gives an ambient scalar
      neighborhood `O` of `Z` such that
      `O Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå sourceMinkowskiGram d n ''
      ExtendedTube d n`; taking the union of these `O` over `Z Ōłł S'_n`
      gives the relative-open witness.  This is where singular scalar-rank
      fibers are handled; no openness of a continuous image is used.

      Import boundary: the connectedness line has been scratch-checked with
      `SourceComplexChart.lean` imported.  A future scalar-representative file
      should import the existing chart file for
      `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram`, not duplicate the polynomial
      continuity proof in `SourceExtension.lean`.

      Lean-facing local realization theorem:

      ```lean
      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 around an adapted representative.  The
      adaptedness hypothesis says that the source-vector span has dimension
      equal to the scalar Gram rank; only under this hypothesis can the
      zero-tail canonical normal form be reached by invertible source and
      Lorentz transformations. -/
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_adapted_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {╬Č0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h╬Č0 : ╬Č0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hspan :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Č0)) =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0))
          {Vsrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hVsrc_open : IsOpen Vsrc)
          (h╬Č0Vsrc : ╬Č0 Ōłł Vsrc)
          (hVsrc_sub : Vsrc ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          Ōłā O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen O Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0 Ōłł O Ōł¦
            O Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Vsrc

      /-- Public relative-open form of Lemma 3.  For an arbitrary
      extended-tube representative `z0`, first replace it by the adapted
      same-Gram representative supplied by
      `hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative`; the realizing
      vectors for nearby scalar points need only lie in the extended tube,
      not in a prescribed neighborhood of the original representative. -/
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          Ōłā O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen O Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Ōłł O Ōł¦
            O Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n
      ```

      The adapted local theorem is obtained from the quantitative
      small-perturbation theorem by a finite-dimensional open-ball shrink:

      ```lean
      /-- Finite-product topology helper: every open neighborhood of a source
      configuration contains a coordinate sup-norm perturbation box.  The
      output is phrased for additive perturbations because that is the exact
      shape returned by Hall-Wightman's quantitative Lemma 3. -/
      theorem BHW.exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen
          {d n : Nat}
          {x0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hx0U : x0 Ōłł U) :
          Ōłā ╬Ą : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ą Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢v i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) ->
              (fun i ╬╝ => x0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) Ōłł U

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_smallPerturbation_to_adapted_localVectorRealization
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {╬Č0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h╬Č0 : ╬Č0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hspan :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Č0)) =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0))
          {Vsrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hVsrc_open : IsOpen Vsrc)
          (h╬Č0Vsrc : ╬Č0 Ōłł Vsrc)
          (hVsrc_sub : Vsrc ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          Ōłā O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen O Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0 Ōłł O Ōł¦
            O Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Vsrc
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_smallPerturbation_to_adapted_localVectorRealization
          ... := by
        rcases BHW.exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen
            (x0 := ╬Č0) hVsrc_open h╬Č0Vsrc with
          Ō¤©╬Ą, h╬Ą_pos, hball_subŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_adaptedBase_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube
            (d := d) hd n h╬Č0 hspan h╬Ą_pos with
          Ō¤©O, hO_open, hZO, hO_realizeŌ¤®
        refine Ō¤©O, hO_open, hZO, by
          intro Z hZ
          rcases hO_realize Z hZ.2 hZ.1 with
            Ō¤©v, hv_small, hET, hGramŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©fun i ╬╝ => ╬Č0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝,
            hball_sub hv_small, hGramŌ¤®Ō¤®
      ```

      Here `exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen` is only the standard
      finite-product metric fact that an open neighborhood of `╬Č0` contains a
      coordinate sup-norm ball around `╬Č0`.  It is the only topology used to
      pass from Hall-Wightman's quantitative `╬Ą` statement to an arbitrary
      source neighborhood `Vsrc`.  The Lean proof is scratch-checked with
      the existing Pi sup-norm API; no separate norm-equivalence theorem is
      needed:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen
          {d n : Nat}
          {x0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hx0U : x0 Ōłł U) :
          Ōłā ╬Ą : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ą Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢v i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) ->
              (fun i ╬╝ => x0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) Ōłł U := by
        rcases Metric.mem_nhds_iff.mp (hU_open.mem_nhds hx0U) with
          Ō¤©Žü, hŽü_pos, hŽü_subŌ¤®
        refine Ō¤©Žü, hŽü_pos, by
          intro v hv
          have hv_row : ŌłĆ i : Fin n, ŌĆ¢v iŌĆ¢ < Žü := by
            intro i
            exact (pi_norm_lt_iff hŽü_pos).2 (hv i)
          have hv_norm : ŌĆ¢vŌĆ¢ < Žü :=
            (pi_norm_lt_iff hŽü_pos).2 hv_row
          have hdiff : (fun i ╬╝ => x0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) - x0 = v := by
            ext i ╬╝
            simp
          exact hŽü_sub (by
            simpa [Metric.mem_ball, dist_eq_norm, hdiff] using hv_norm)Ō¤®
      ```

      The quantitative adapted-base theorem
      `BHW.hwLemma3_adaptedBase_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube` is
      the real Hall-Wightman Lemma-3 content after the same-Gram adapted
      representative has been chosen.  Its implementation must be decomposed
      into the following finite-dimensional sublemmas, with `D := d + 1`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedBlock_sqrt_near_identity
          (r : Nat)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬┤ : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬┤ Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ B : Fin r -> Fin r -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ i j, B i j = B j i) ->
              (ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢B i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) ->
              Ōłā P : Fin r -> Fin r -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ i j, P i j = P j i) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ i j,
                  ŌĆ¢P i j - (if i = j then (1 : Ōäé) else 0)ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ i j,
                  Ōłæ k : Fin r, P i k * P j k =
                    (if i = j then (1 : Ōäé) else 0) + B i j)

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_schurComplement_rank_bound
          (D r m : Nat)
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {C : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
          (hA_symm : A.transpose = A)
          (hA_inv : IsUnit A.det)
          (hRank :
            Matrix.rank
              (Matrix.fromBlocks A B.transpose B C) <= D) :
          Matrix.rank (C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose) <= D - r

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (m k : Nat)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬┤ : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬┤ Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ S : Fin m -> Fin m -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ i j, S i j = S j i) ->
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.of fun i j => S i j) <= k ->
              k <= d + 1 ->
              (ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢S i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                ŌłĆ i j,
                  BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q i) (q j) = S i j

      /-- Explicit finite entry-size majorant used to avoid mixing the
      singular-value estimate with any ambient matrix norm instance. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound
          (m : Nat) (S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) : ŌäØ :=
        Ōłæ p : Fin m ├Ś Fin m, ŌĆ¢S p.1 p.2ŌĆ¢

      theorem BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound_nonneg
          (m : Nat) (S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) :
          0 <= BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S

      theorem BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound_lt_of_entry_bound
          (m : Nat)
          {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
          {╬┤ : ŌäØ} (h╬┤ : 0 < ╬┤)
          (hS : ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢S i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) :
          BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S <
            (((m * m : Nat) : ŌäØ) + 1) * ╬┤

      theorem BHW.exists_pos_mul_sqrt_lt
          {╬Ą C : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) (hC : 0 < C) :
          Ōłā ╬┤ : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬┤ Ōł¦ Real.sqrt (C * ╬┤) < ╬Ą

      theorem BHW.real_sqrt_lt_of_lt_mul_bound
          {x C ╬┤ ╬Ą : ŌäØ}
          (hx : 0 <= x)
          (hxlt : x < C * ╬┤)
          (hsmall : Real.sqrt (C * ╬┤) < ╬Ą) :
          Real.sqrt x < ╬Ą

      theorem BHW.matrix_unitary_entry_norm_le_one
          (m : Nat)
          (U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé)
          (i a : Fin m) :
          ŌĆ¢(U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) i aŌĆ¢ <= 1

      theorem BHW.matrix_unitary_entry_mul_real_sqrt_norm_le_sqrt
          (m : Nat)
          (U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé)
          (i a : Fin m)
          {Žā L : ŌäØ}
          (hŽā_le : Žā <= L) :
          ŌĆ¢(U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) i a *
            (Real.sqrt Žā : Ōäé)ŌĆ¢ <= Real.sqrt L

      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_takagi_factor_from_supportEmbedding
          (m k : Nat)
          {U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé}
          {Žā : Fin m -> ŌäØ}
          (hŽā_nonneg : ŌłĆ a, 0 <= Žā a)
          (e : {a : Fin m // Žā a ŌēĀ 0} Ōå¬ Fin k)
          (S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé)
          (hTakagi :
            S =
              (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) *
                Matrix.diagonal (fun a => (Žā a : Ōäé)) *
                (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé).transpose) :
          Ōłā A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé,
            S = A * A.transpose Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ {L : ŌäØ}, 0 <= L -> (ŌłĆ a, Žā a <= L) ->
              ŌłĆ i b, ŌĆ¢A i bŌĆ¢ <= Real.sqrt L

      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_entryL1_of_takagiDiagonalData
          (m k : Nat)
          {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
          {U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé}
          {Žā : Fin m -> ŌäØ}
          (hŽā_nonneg : ŌłĆ a, 0 <= Žā a)
          (hTakagi :
            S =
              (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) *
                Matrix.diagonal (fun a => (Žā a : Ōäé)) *
                (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé).transpose)
          (hcard : Fintype.card {a : Fin m // Žā a ŌēĀ 0} <= k)
          (hŽā_bound : ŌłĆ a, Žā a <= BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S) :
          Ōłā A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé,
            S = A * A.transpose Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ i a, ŌĆ¢A i aŌĆ¢ <= Real.sqrt (BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S)

      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_entryL1_of_takagiDiagonalData_rankSupport
          (m k : Nat)
          {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
          {U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé}
          {Žā : Fin m -> ŌäØ}
          (hŽā_nonneg : ŌłĆ a, 0 <= Žā a)
          (hTakagi :
            S =
              (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) *
                Matrix.diagonal (fun a => (Žā a : Ōäé)) *
                (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé).transpose)
          (hrankSupport :
            Fintype.card {a : Fin m // Žā a ŌēĀ 0} = Matrix.rank S)
          (hRank : Matrix.rank S <= k)
          (hŽā_bound : ŌłĆ a, Žā a <= BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S) :
          Ōłā A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé,
            S = A * A.transpose Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ i a, ŌĆ¢A i aŌĆ¢ <= Real.sqrt (BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S)

      /-- Finite-dimensional Takagi/Bargmann factorization in the standard
      complex symmetric form, with the smallness estimate needed in
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 3.  This is pure matrix linear algebra: if a complex
      symmetric matrix has rank at most `k` and all entries are sufficiently
      small, then it is `A * AßĄĆ` with all entries of `A` small. -/
      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi_entryL1
          (m k : Nat)
          {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
          (hSym : S.transpose = S)
          (hRank : Matrix.rank S <= k) :
          Ōłā A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé,
            S = A * A.transpose Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ i a, ŌĆ¢A i aŌĆ¢ <=
              Real.sqrt (BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S))

      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE_of_entryL1
          (m k : Nat)
          (hentry :
            ŌłĆ {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé},
              S.transpose = S ->
              Matrix.rank S <= k ->
              Ōłā A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé,
                S = A * A.transpose Ōł¦
                ŌłĆ i a,
                  ŌĆ¢A i aŌĆ¢ <= Real.sqrt (BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S))
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬┤ : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬┤ Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé,
              S.transpose = S ->
              Matrix.rank S <= k ->
              (ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢S i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) ->
              Ōłā A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ i a, ŌĆ¢A i aŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                S = A * A.transpose

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE_of_entryL1
          (m k : Nat)
          (hentry :
            ŌłĆ {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé},
              S.transpose = S ->
              Matrix.rank S <= k ->
              Ōłā A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé,
                S = A * A.transpose Ōł¦
                ŌłĆ i a,
                  ŌĆ¢A i aŌĆ¢ <= Real.sqrt (BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S))
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬┤ : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬┤ Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ S : Fin m -> Fin m -> Ōäé,
              S Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace m ->
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin m => S i j) <= k ->
              (ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢S i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin k -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ i a, ŌĆ¢q i aŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceComplexDotGram k m q = S

      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_takagi_rankLE
          (m k : Nat)
          {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
          (hSym : S.transpose = S)
          (hRank : Matrix.rank S <= k) :
          Ōłā A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé,
            S = A * A.transpose

      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE
          (m k : Nat)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬┤ : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬┤ Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé,
              S.transpose = S ->
              Matrix.rank S <= k ->
              (ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢S i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) ->
              Ōłā A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ i a, ŌĆ¢A i aŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                S = A * A.transpose

      /-- The tail subspace in the diagonalized complex Minkowski model:
      vectors whose first `r` standard orthogonal coordinates vanish.  This is
      the orthogonal complement used after the selected Hall-Wightman block
      has been normalized to the first `r` standard coordinates. -/
      def BHW.standardCoordinateTailSubspace
          (D r : Nat) :
          Submodule Ōäé (Fin D -> Ōäé) :=
        { carrier := {u | ŌłĆ ╬╝ : Fin D, ╬╝.val < r -> u ╬╝ = 0}
          zero_mem' := by simp
          add_mem' := by
            intro u v hu hv ╬╝ h╬╝
            simp [hu ╬╝ h╬╝, hv ╬╝ h╬╝]
          smul_mem' := by
            intro c u hu ╬╝ h╬╝
            simp [hu ╬╝ h╬╝] }

      def BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace
          (d r : Nat) :
          Submodule Ōäé (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
        (BHW.standardCoordinateTailSubspace (d + 1) r).comap
          (BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d).toLinearMap

      theorem BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace_iff
          (d r : Nat) {v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé} :
          v Ōłł BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace d r Ōåö
          ŌłĆ ╬╝ : Fin (d + 1), ╬╝.val < r ->
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d v) ╬╝ = 0

      theorem BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace_orthogonal
          (d r : Nat) {v w : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hv : v Ōłł BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace d r)
          (hw :
            ŌłĆ ╬╝, r <= ╬╝.val ->
              (BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d w) ╬╝ = 0) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v w = 0

      theorem BHW.exists_finTailEmbedding
          (D r k : Nat)
          (hkr : k <= D - r) :
          Ōłā ╬╣ : Fin k -> Fin D,
            Function.Injective ╬╣ Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ a, r <= (╬╣ a).val

      theorem BHW.exists_finTailEmbedding ... := by
        refine Ō¤©fun a => Ō¤©r + a.val, by omegaŌ¤®, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
        ┬Ę intro a b hab
          apply Fin.ext
          exact Nat.add_left_cancel (Fin.ext_iff.mp hab)
        ┬Ę intro a
          simp

      def BHW.tailEmbeddedFactorVectors
          (D r m k : Nat)
          (╬╣ : Fin k -> Fin D)
          (A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé) :
          Fin m -> Fin D -> Ōäé :=
        fun i ╬╝ =>
          if h : Ōłā a : Fin k, ╬╣ a = ╬╝ then
            A i (Classical.choose h)
          else 0

      theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear_tailEmbeddedFactor
          (D r m k : Nat)
          {╬╣ : Fin k -> Fin D}
          (h╬╣_inj : Function.Injective ╬╣)
          (h╬╣_tail : ŌłĆ a, r <= (╬╣ a).val)
          (A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé) :
          (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ╬╝.val < r ->
            BHW.tailEmbeddedFactorVectors D r m k ╬╣ A i ╬╝ = 0) Ōł¦
          (ŌłĆ i j,
            BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
              (BHW.tailEmbeddedFactorVectors D r m k ╬╣ A i)
              (BHW.tailEmbeddedFactorVectors D r m k ╬╣ A j) =
            (A * A.transpose) i j)

      theorem BHW.tailEmbeddedFactorVectors_coord_bound
          (D r m k : Nat)
          {╬╣ : Fin k -> Fin D}
          {A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé}
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ}
          (hA_small : ŌłĆ i a, ŌĆ¢A i aŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) :
          ŌłĆ i ╬╝,
            ŌĆ¢BHW.tailEmbeddedFactorVectors D r m k ╬╣ A i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv_symm_coord_bound
          (d : Nat) :
          Ōłā C : ŌäØ, 0 < C Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ ╬╝, ŌĆ¢(BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d).symm v ╬╝ŌĆ¢ <=
                C * Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1), ŌĆ¢v ╬ĮŌĆ¢)

      /-- Tail version of the residual realization theorem.  This is the form
      consumed by Lemma 3 after Schur complement reduction: the residual
      vectors are small and lie in the orthogonal complement of the selected
      normalized block, so adding them changes only the residual Schur
      complement scalar products. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (r m k : Nat)
          (hr : r <= d + 1)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬┤ : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬┤ Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ S : Fin m -> Fin m -> Ōäé,
              (ŌłĆ i j, S i j = S j i) ->
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.of fun i j => S i j) <= k ->
              k <= d + 1 - r ->
              (ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢S i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) ->
              Ōłā q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢q i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ i, q i Ōłł
                  BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace d r) Ōł¦
                ŌłĆ i j,
                  BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q i) (q j) = S i j

      def BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource
          (d n r : Nat) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé

      def BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram
          (d n r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) (hrn : r <= n) :
          Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé

      theorem BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_hwLemma3CanonicalSource
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) (hrn : r <= n) :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
            (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r) =
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hrn

      /-- Source-index linear change on an `n`-tuple of spacetime vectors.
      This is the exact operation used in Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 before the
      canonical block argument. -/
      def BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange
          (d n : Nat)
          (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun i ╬╝ => Ōłæ j : Fin n, A i j * z j ╬╝

      /-- The corresponding congruence action on source Gram matrices. -/
      def BHW.sourceGramCongruence
          (n : Nat)
          (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé :=
        fun i j =>
          Ōłæ a : Fin n, Ōłæ b : Fin n, A i a * Z a b * A j b

      theorem BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_sourceTupleLinearChange
          (d n : Nat)
          (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
            (BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n A z) =
          BHW.sourceGramCongruence n A
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      theorem BHW.sourceGramCongruence_mem_variety_iff
          (d n : Nat)
          {A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé}
          (hA : IsUnit A.det)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramCongruence n A Z Ōłł
              BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Ōåö
            Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n

      /-- Algebraic normalizing congruence for an invertible complex symmetric
      block.  This is the arbitrary-base analogue of the near-identity square
      root used later for perturbations. -/
      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_invertible_congruence_to_identity
          (r : Nat)
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          (hSym : A.transpose = A)
          (hInv : IsUnit A.det) :
          Ōłā P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé,
            IsUnit P.det Ōł¦
            P * A * P.transpose = 1

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexDotGram_matrix_eq_mul_transpose
          (D n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin D -> Ōäé) :
          Matrix.of (BHW.sourceComplexDotGram D n z) =
            (Matrix.of fun i a => z i a) *
              (Matrix.of fun i a => z i a).transpose

      def BHW.sourceHeadMetricSquareRoot
          (d r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé

      theorem BHW.sourceHeadMetricSquareRoot_det_isUnit
          (d r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          IsUnit (BHW.sourceHeadMetricSquareRoot d r hrD).det

      theorem BHW.sourceHeadMetricSquareRoot_congruence
          (d r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) :
          BHW.sourceHeadMetricSquareRoot d r hrD * 1 *
              (BHW.sourceHeadMetricSquareRoot d r hrD).transpose =
            BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD

      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_invertible_congruence_to_sourceHeadMetric
          (d r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1)
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          (hSym : A.transpose = A)
          (hInv : IsUnit A.det) :
          Ōłā P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé,
            IsUnit P.det Ōł¦
            P * A * P.transpose = BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.complexSymmetric_invertible_congruence_to_identity`:

      1. Let `Z i j := A i j`.  The symmetry hypothesis gives
         `Z Ōłł sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace r`, and `hInv` gives
         `(Matrix.of Z).rank = r` by `Matrix.rank_of_isUnit`; therefore
         `Z Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum r r`.
      2. Apply the checked exact-rank factorization theorem
         `BHW.exists_fullRank_sourceComplexDotGram_of_rankExact` to get
         `Qfun : Fin r -> Fin r -> Ōäé` with
         `BHW.sourceComplexDotGram r r Qfun = Z`.  With
         `Q := Matrix.of fun i a => Qfun i a`, the checked matrix identity
         `BHW.sourceComplexDotGram_matrix_eq_mul_transpose` rewrites this as
         `A = Q * Q.transpose`.
      3. Taking determinants gives
         `A.det = Q.det * Q.det`.  Since `A.det` is a unit, `Q.det ŌēĀ 0`, hence
         `IsUnit Q.det`.
      4. Take `P := QŌü╗┬╣`.  The inverse identities
         `Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul`, `Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv`, and
         `Matrix.transpose_nonsing_inv` give
         `QŌü╗┬╣ * A * (QŌü╗┬╣).transpose = 1`; `P.det` is a unit by
         `Matrix.isUnit_nonsing_inv_det`.
      5. For the canonical source head metric, define
         `BHW.sourceHeadMetricSquareRoot d r hrD` as the diagonal matrix whose
         entries are `complexSquareRootChoice` of the corresponding
         `MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature` entry.  Then
         `sourceHeadMetricSquareRoot * 1 * sourceHeadMetricSquareRootßĄĆ =
         sourceHeadMetric d r hrD`, and its determinant is a unit because
         every signature entry is `┬▒1`.
      6. Compose the identity normalizer with this diagonal square-root:
         if `P0 * A * P0ßĄĆ = 1`, then
         `(D * P0) * A * (D * P0)ßĄĆ = sourceHeadMetric d r hrD`.

      Lean-shaped proof skeleton:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_invertible_congruence_to_identity
          ... := by
        let Z : Fin r -> Fin r -> Ōäé := fun i j => A i j
        have hZ : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum r r := by
          constructor
          ┬Ę intro i j
            change A i j = A j i
            have hij := congrFun (congrFun hSym j) i
            simpa [Matrix.transpose_apply] using hij
          ┬Ę change A.rank = r
            have hAunit : IsUnit A :=
              (Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det A).mpr hInv
            simpa using Matrix.rank_of_isUnit A hAunit
        rcases BHW.exists_fullRank_sourceComplexDotGram_of_rankExact hZ with
          Ō¤©Qfun, _hQfull, hQeqŌ¤®
        let Q : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé :=
          Matrix.of fun i a => Qfun i a
        have hAeq : A = Q * Q.transpose := by
          change Matrix.of Z = Q * Q.transpose
          have h := congrArg Matrix.of hQeq
          exact h.symm.trans (by
            simpa [Q] using
              BHW.sourceComplexDotGram_matrix_eq_mul_transpose r r Qfun)
        have hQdet : IsUnit Q.det := by
          apply isUnit_iff_ne_zero.mpr
          intro hzero
          have hdet_eq : A.det = Q.det * Q.det := by
            rw [hAeq, Matrix.det_mul, Matrix.det_transpose]
          exact hInv.ne_zero (by simpa [hdet_eq, hzero])
        refine Ō¤©QŌü╗┬╣, Matrix.isUnit_nonsing_inv_det Q hQdet, ?_Ō¤®
        rw [hAeq]
        calc
          QŌü╗┬╣ * (Q * Q.transpose) * (QŌü╗┬╣).transpose =
              (QŌü╗┬╣ * Q) * (Q.transpose * (QŌü╗┬╣).transpose) := by
                simp [Matrix.mul_assoc]
          _ = 1 * (Q.transpose * (QŌü╗┬╣).transpose) := by
                rw [Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul (A := Q) hQdet]
          _ = Q.transpose * (Q.transpose)Ōü╗┬╣ := by
                simp [Matrix.transpose_nonsing_inv]
          _ = 1 := by
                rw [Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv (A := Q.transpose)
                  (Matrix.isUnit_det_transpose Q hQdet)]
      ```

      This is now checked in
      `OSReconstruction/ComplexLieGroups/Connectedness/BHWPermutation/SourceNormalFormTransport.lean`;
      the old orthogonal-basis decomposition is no longer a production target
      for the base head-block normalizer.

      /-- Hall-Wightman's first reduction in Lemma 3: an extended-tube
      scalar point admits a same-Gram extended-tube representative whose
      source-vector span has dimension equal to the scalar Gram rank.

      This is the step which prevents an invalid attempt to map an arbitrary
      representative with extra radical vectors to the zero-tail canonical
      tuple by an invertible source-coordinate change.  In the low-rank case
      it is exactly Hall-Wightman's null-residual observation: after the
      residual-frame normal form, the coefficients of the null residual frame
      may be changed, in particular set to zero, while remaining in the
      extended tube and without changing scalar products. -/
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          Ōłā ╬Č0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
            ╬Č0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0 =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Ōł¦
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Č0)) =
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0)

      -- Checked support layer:
      -- OSReconstruction/ComplexLieGroups/Connectedness/BHWPermutation/
      --   SourceExtendedTubeRank.lean

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_self_re_im
          (d : Nat)
          (v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v v).re =
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝).re) (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝).re) -
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝).im) (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝).im) Ōł¦
          (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v v).im =
            2 *
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
                (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝).re) (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝).im)

      theorem BHW.matrix_eq_zero_of_rank_eq_zero_source
          {m n : Type*} [Fintype m] [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n]
          (A : Matrix m n Ōäé) (hA : A.rank = 0) :
          A = 0

      theorem BHW.openForwardCone_imag_complexSelfInner_ne_zero
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          {v : Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hv_im :
            BHW.InOpenForwardCone d (fun ╬╝ => (v ╬╝).im)) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v v ŌēĀ 0

      theorem BHW.matrix_rank_pos_of_nonzero_entry
          {m n : Type*} [Fintype m] [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n]
          (M : Matrix m n Ōäé)
          {i : m} {j : n}
          (hij : M i j ŌēĀ 0) :
          0 < M.rank

      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_pos_of_forwardTube
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n : Nat} [NeZero n]
          {w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hw : w Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n) :
          0 < BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)

      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_pos_of_mem_extendedTube
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n : Nat} [NeZero n]
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          0 < BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0)

      -- Checked support layer:
      -- OSReconstruction/ComplexLieGroups/Connectedness/BHWPermutation/
      --   SourceHWSelectedProjection.lean

      def BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé :=
        fun a b => G (I a) (I b)

      theorem BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix_det_isUnit_of_sourceMatrixMinor_ne_zero
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (hminor : BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I G ŌēĀ 0) :
          IsUnit (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G).det

      def BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (i : Fin n) :
          Fin r -> Ōäé :=
        fun a =>
          Ōłæ b : Fin r,
            G i (I b) * (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G)Ōü╗┬╣ b a

      theorem BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_projection_eq
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (hminor : BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I G ŌēĀ 0)
          (i : Fin n)
          (a : Fin r) :
          (Ōłæ b : Fin r,
              BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G i b *
                BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G b a) =
            G i (I a)

      theorem BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_selected
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (hminor : BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I G ŌēĀ 0)
          (a b : Fin r) :
          BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G (I a) b =
            if a = b then 1 else 0

      def BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun i =>
          Ōłæ a : Fin r,
            BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G i a ŌĆó z0 (I a)

      def BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun i ╬╝ =>
          z0 i ╬╝ -
            BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I G z0 i ╬╝

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_selected
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ŌēĀ 0)
          (a : Fin r) :
          BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 (I a) =
            z0 (I a)

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_inner_head
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ŌēĀ 0)
          (i : Fin n)
          (a : Fin r) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 i)
              (z0 (I a)) =
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 i (I a)

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_head
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ŌēĀ 0)
          (i : Fin n)
          (a : Fin r) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 i)
              (z0 (I a)) = 0

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_head_inner
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ŌēĀ 0)
          (a : Fin r)
          (i : Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z0 (I a))
              (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 i) = 0

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_gram_eq
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0))
              = r)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ŌēĀ 0) :
          ŌłĆ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 i)
              (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 j) =
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 i j

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_span_finrank_eq_rank
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0))
              = r)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ŌēĀ 0) :
          Module.finrank Ōäé
            (LinearMap.range
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n
                (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0))) =
          r

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_isotropicFrameData
          (d n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) = r)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ŌēĀ 0) :
          Ōłā (s : Nat)
            (q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
            (a : Fin n -> Fin s -> Ōäé),
            LinearIndependent Ōäé q Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ c e,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q e) = 0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ c i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c)
                (BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 i) = 0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ i ╬╝,
              BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 i ╬╝ =
              Ōłæ c : Fin s, a i c * q c ╬╝) Ōł¦
            (fun i ╬╝ =>
              BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 i ╬╝ +
              Ōłæ c : Fin s, a i c * q c ╬╝) = z0

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGramMaxRankAt_span_finrank_eq_sourceGramMatrixRank
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0Rank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0) :
          Module.finrank Ōäé
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z0)) =
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0)

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative`:

      1. Put `G := sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0` and
         `r := sourceGramMatrixRank n G`.  If `n = 0`, take `╬Č0 := z0`;
         the source span and scalar rank are both zero.  If `n > 0`, the
         rank-zero case cannot occur for an ordinary extended-tube point:
         destruct `hz0` as a complex Lorentz image of a forward-tube point,
         use Lorentz invariance of the Gram matrix, and evaluate any diagonal
         scalar product indexed by `i0 : Fin n`.  The imaginary part of
         `w i0` lies in the open forward cone, so
         `BHW.openForwardCone_imag_complexSelfInner_ne_zero` gives
         `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (w i0) (w i0) ŌēĀ 0`.  That theorem
         is the elementary Lorentz-cone fact: if
         `v = x + i╬Ę`, `╬Ę Ōłł V_+`, and `B(v,v)=0`, then the imaginary part of
         `B(v,v)`, computed by
         `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_self_re_im`, gives `B(x,╬Ę)=0`,
         while the real part gives `B(x,x)=B(╬Ę,╬Ę)`.  In the checked
         `MinkowskiSpace` sign convention, the existing theorem
         `MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_orthogonal_to_timelike_nonneg` gives
         `0 <= B(x,x)`, while the open-cone hypothesis gives
         `B(╬Ę,╬Ę) < 0`; contradiction.  A nonzero diagonal matrix entry gives
         positive matrix rank by `BHW.matrix_rank_pos_of_nonzero_entry`,
         yielding
         `BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_pos_of_forwardTube`.  The extended-tube
         theorem then rewrites through the chosen Lorentz preimage by
         `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction`; the scalar rank then
         rewrites along the resulting Gram-matrix equality, so no closure or
         boundary-value argument enters this positivity step.
      2. For `0 < r`, use
         `BHW.exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank` on
         the symmetric matrix `G` to choose
         `I : Fin r -> Fin n` with
         `sourceMatrixMinor n r I I G ŌēĀ 0`.  This is the same principal-block
         choice as in Hall-Wightman's first paragraph of Lemma 3.
      3. Define the selected projection
         `╬Š := BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I G z0`, where the
         coefficients are the checked
         `BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G`.  Define the residual
         `Žü := z0 - ╬Š`.  The checked identities
         `BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_projection_eq` and
         `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_head` give
         `B(Žü i, z0 (I a)) = 0` for every `i,a`; the symmetric order is
         `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_head_inner`.  The selected-label
         projection identity is already checked as
         `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_selected`.
      4. The Schur-complement rank theorem gives
         `B(Žü i, Žü j) = 0` for every `i,j`: the full Gram matrix has rank
         exactly `r` and the selected principal block is invertible, so the
         Schur complement of that block is zero.  Expanding the Schur
         complement in the selected coefficients is precisely the residual
         pairing.  Consequently the projection tuple has the same scalar Gram
         entries as `z0`; this is exposed as the checked entrywise theorem
         `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_gram_eq`.
      5. The vectors `╬Š i` lie in the span of the selected vectors
         `z0 (I a)`, so their source span has finrank at most `r` by the
         finite `r`-generator bound.  The checked Gram equality and the
         general rank bound
         `sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range` give the
         lower bound `r <= finrank (range (sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Š))`.
         Hence the source span of `╬Š` has finrank exactly `r`.  This is
         `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_span_finrank_eq_rank`.
      6. Checked in `SourceHWSelectedProjection.lean`: choose the finite
         independent frame `q` by applying
         `Submodule.exists_fun_fin_finrank_span_eq` to
         `Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range Žü)`, where
         `Žü := BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I G z0`.  Each `q c` is
         literally some residual vector.  The coefficient functions
         `a : Fin n -> Fin s -> Ōäé` are obtained from
         `BHW.exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame` after rewriting
         `Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range q)` to the residual span.  The checked
         theorem `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_residual_eq_zero` gives
         `B(q c, q e)=0`, and
         `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_projection` gives
         `B(q c, ╬Š i)=0`.  The final source decomposition is just
         `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_add_residual` plus the chosen
         coefficient equality.  This is the package
         `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_isotropicFrameData`; it is now
         production Lean, finite linear algebra, and has no `[NeZero d]`,
         `hd`, or extended-tube hypothesis.
      7. Since
         `z0 = fun i => ╬Š i + Ōłæ c, a i c ŌĆó q c` is in the extended tube,
         Hall-Wightman's second/third remarks after Lemma 2, in the local
         finite-frame form already used by the contraction proof, give
         `╬Š Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n`: apply
         `BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube` to the
         endpoint `z0`, the frame `q`, and the coefficient family `a`, then
         specialize its returned all-coefficients field to the zero
         coefficient family.  Take `╬Č0 := ╬Š`.

      Lean-shaped proof skeleton:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative ... := by
        by_cases hn : n = 0
        ┬Ę subst hn
          exact Ō¤©z0, hz0, rfl, by
            simp [BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank, BHW.sourceCoefficientEval]Ō¤®
        haveI : NeZero n := Ō¤©hnŌ¤®
        let G := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0
        let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G
        have hr_pos : 0 < r := by
          simpa [r, G] using
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_pos_of_mem_extendedTube
              (d := d) hd (n := n) hz0
        have hGsym : G Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n := by
          intro i j
          exact BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_symm d n z0 i j
        have hrank : (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => G i j).rank = r := rfl
        rcases BHW.exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank
            hGsym hrank with
          Ō¤©I, hI_inj, hminorŌ¤®
        let ╬Š :=
          BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection d n r I G z0
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_isotropicFrameData
            d n r I z0 (by rfl) hminor with
          Ō¤©s, q, a, hq_li, hq_pair_zero, hq_orth_╬Š,
            hres_coeff, hz0_decompŌ¤®
        have hendpoint :
            (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Š i ╬╝ + Ōłæ c : Fin s, a i c * q c ╬╝) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
          simpa [╬Š] using hz0_decomp.symm Ō¢Ė hz0
        rcases BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
            (d := d) hd hendpoint hq_pair_zero hq_orth_╬Š with
          Ō¤©hbase_mem, _hall_coefficientsŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©╬Š, hbase_mem,
          by
            ext i j
            simpa [╬Š, G, BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_apply_eq_complexInner] using
              BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_gram_eq
                d n r I z0 (by rfl) hminor i j,
          by
            simpa [╬Š, G, r] using
              BHW.hwLemma3_selectedProjection_span_finrank_eq_rank
                d n r I z0 (by rfl) hminorŌ¤®
      ```

      The call to `hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube` uses
      only the residual isotropy and base-orthogonality supplied by
      `BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual_isotropicFrameData`.  No dual frame is
      needed for this membership-only step; dual frames remain part of the
      Lemma-2 contraction packet, where they supply the actual null boosts.

      /-- Finite-dimensional normal-form transport used in Hall-Wightman
      Lemma 3.  It records the source-index linear change and ambient
      Minkowski isometry that carry the chosen base tuple to the canonical
      `r`-block tuple, together with the corresponding congruence map on
      source Gram matrices, the induced variety-relative oriented
      Gram-plus-determinant transport for the source-index change, and the
      smallness estimates needed to transport perturbations back. -/
      structure BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport
          [NeZero d]
          (d n r : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) where
        rank_lt_spacetime : r < d + 1
        rank_le_arity : r <= n
        toNormalVec :
          (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
            (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
        toNormalGram :
          (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
        z0_to_canonical :
          toNormalVec z0 = BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r
        gram_commute :
          ŌłĆ z,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (toNormalVec z) =
              toNormalGram (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        gram_variety_iff :
          ŌłĆ Z,
            toNormalGram Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n Ōåö
              Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n
        sourceMatrixVarietyTransport :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n
        oriented_commute :
          ŌłĆ z,
            (Ō¤©BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toNormalVec z),
                Ō¤©toNormalVec z, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® :
              BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n) =
              sourceMatrixVarietyTransport.toFun
                (Ō¤©BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z,
                    Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® :
                  BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n)
        oriented_symm_commute :
          ŌłĆ z,
            (Ō¤©BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toNormalVec.symm z),
                Ō¤©toNormalVec.symm z, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® :
              BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n) =
              sourceMatrixVarietyTransport.invFun
                (Ō¤©BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z,
                    Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® :
                  BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n)
        gram_z0 :
          toNormalGram (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) =
            BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r rank_lt_spacetime rank_le_arity
        gram_ball_to_normal_ball :
          ŌłĆ {╬ĘN : ŌäØ}, 0 < ╬ĘN ->
            Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
              ŌłĆ Z,
                (ŌłĆ i j,
                  ŌĆ¢Z i j - BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 i jŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
                (ŌłĆ i j,
                  ŌĆ¢toNormalGram Z i j -
                    BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r
                      rank_lt_spacetime rank_le_arity i jŌĆ¢ < ╬ĘN)
        perturb_back_small :
          ŌłĆ {╬Ą : ŌäØ}, 0 < ╬Ą ->
            Ōłā ╬ĄN : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬ĄN Ōł¦
              ŌłĆ vN : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢vN i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬ĄN) ->
                Ōłā v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
                  (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢v i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                  toNormalVec (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) =
                    fun i ╬╝ =>
                      BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r i ╬╝ + vN i ╬╝

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormTransportData
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (r : Nat)
          (hr :
            r = BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0))
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hspan :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z0)) = r) :
          Ōłā T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r z0, True

      theorem BHW.exists_sourcePermutation_movingPrincipalBlockToHead
          (n r : Nat)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hI : Function.Injective I) :
          Ōłā Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n),
            ŌłĆ a : Fin r, Žā (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a) = I a

      def BHW.sourceBlockMatrix
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (C : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé

      def BHW.sourceHeadHeadBlock
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé :=
        fun a b => G (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
          (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b)

      def BHW.sourceTailHeadBlock
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé :=
        fun u a => G (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)
          (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)

      def BHW.sourceTailTailBlock
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé :=
        fun u v => G (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)
          (BHW.finSourceTail hrn v)

      theorem BHW.sourceBlockMatrix_of_headTailBlocks
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n) :
          BHW.sourceBlockMatrix n r hrn
            (BHW.sourceHeadHeadBlock n r hrn G)
            (BHW.sourceTailHeadBlock n r hrn G)
            (BHW.sourceTailTailBlock n r hrn G) = G

      theorem BHW.sourceHeadHeadBlock_symm_of_sourceSymmetric
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n) :
          (BHW.sourceHeadHeadBlock n r hrn G).transpose =
            BHW.sourceHeadHeadBlock n r hrn G

      def BHW.sourceLinearBlockMatrix
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (X : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (Y : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé)
          (Z : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (W : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé

      theorem BHW.sourceGramCongruence_eq_matrix_mul
          (n : Nat)
          (M : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          Matrix.of (BHW.sourceGramCongruence n M Z) =
            M * Matrix.of Z * M.transpose

      theorem BHW.sourceGramCongruence_mul
          (n : Nat)
          (M N : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramCongruence n (M * N) Z =
            BHW.sourceGramCongruence n M
              (BHW.sourceGramCongruence n N Z)

      theorem BHW.sourceLinearBlockMatrix_det_eq_fromBlocks_det
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (X : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (Y : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé)
          (Z : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (W : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé) :
          (BHW.sourceLinearBlockMatrix n r hrn X Y Z W).det =
            (Matrix.fromBlocks X Y Z W).det

      def BHW.hwLemma3_projectionSourceChangeMatrix
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé

      def BHW.hwLemma3_extendHeadMatrix
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé

      def BHW.sourcePermutationMatrix
          (n : Nat) (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé

      def BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé) :
          Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé :=
        BHW.hwLemma3_extendHeadMatrix n r hrn P *
          BHW.hwLemma3_projectionSourceChangeMatrix n r hrn A B *
          BHW.sourcePermutationMatrix n Žā

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_projectionSourceChangeMatrix_det_isUnit
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
          (B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé) :
          IsUnit
            (BHW.hwLemma3_projectionSourceChangeMatrix n r hrn A B).det

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_projectionSourceChangeMatrix_congruence
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {C : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé}
          (hA : IsUnit A.det)
          (hAsym : A.transpose = A) :
          BHW.sourceGramCongruence n
            (BHW.hwLemma3_projectionSourceChangeMatrix n r hrn A B)
            (BHW.sourceBlockMatrix n r hrn A B C) =
          BHW.sourceBlockMatrix n r hrn A 0
            (C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose)

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_extendHeadMatrix_det_isUnit
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          {P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          (hP : IsUnit P.det) :
          IsUnit (BHW.hwLemma3_extendHeadMatrix n r hrn P).det

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_extendHeadMatrix_congruence
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé} :
          BHW.sourceGramCongruence n
            (BHW.hwLemma3_extendHeadMatrix n r hrn P)
            (BHW.sourceBlockMatrix n r hrn A 0 0) =
          BHW.sourceBlockMatrix n r hrn (P * A * P.transpose) 0 0

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram_eq_sourceBlockMatrix
          (d n r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) (hrn : r <= n) :
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hrn =
            BHW.sourceBlockMatrix n r hrn
              (BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD) 0 0

      theorem BHW.sourcePermutationMatrix_det_isUnit
          (n : Nat) (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          IsUnit (BHW.sourcePermutationMatrix n Žā).det

      theorem BHW.sourceGramCongruence_sourcePermutationMatrix
          (n : Nat) (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramCongruence n (BHW.sourcePermutationMatrix n Žā) Z =
            BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žā Z

      theorem BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange_sourcePermutationMatrix
          (d n : Nat) (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n
              (BHW.sourcePermutationMatrix n Žā) z =
            fun i => z (Žā i)

      theorem BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange_mul
          (d n : Nat)
          (M N : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n (M * N) z =
            BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n M
              (BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n N z)

      def BHW.sourceTupleLinearEquivOfMatrix
          (d n : Nat)
          (M : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé)
          (hM : IsUnit M.det) :
          (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
            (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_sourcePermuteComplexGram
          (n : Nat) (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
            (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žā G) =
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G

      theorem BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_mem_sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace
          (n : Nat) (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hG : G Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n) :
          BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žā G Ōłł
            BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_schurComplement_eq_zero_of_rank_eq
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {C : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé}
          (hGsym : G Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n)
          (hBlock : G = BHW.sourceBlockMatrix n r hrn A B C)
          (hRank : BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G = r)
          (hA : IsUnit A.det) :
          C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose = 0

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix_det_isUnit
          (n r : Nat) (hrn : r <= n)
          (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          (hA : IsUnit A.det)
          (hP : IsUnit P.det) :
          IsUnit
            (BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix
              n r hrn Žā A B P).det

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix_canonicalGram
          (d n r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          {Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)}
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {C : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé}
          {P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          (hBlock :
            BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žā G =
              BHW.sourceBlockMatrix n r hrn A B C)
          (hA : IsUnit A.det)
          (hAsym : A.transpose = A)
          (hSchur : C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose = 0)
          (hP : P * A * P.transpose = BHW.sourceHeadMetric d r hrD) :
          BHW.sourceGramCongruence n
            (BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix
              n r hrn Žā A B P) G =
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hrn

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_projectedTail_zero_of_adapted
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hr :
            r = BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0))
          (hspan :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z0)) =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0))
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ŌēĀ 0) :
          BHW.hwLemma3_selectedResidual d n r I
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) z0 = 0

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiNondegenerate_eval_range_of_adapted
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hadapt :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) =
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_canonicalGram_tail_zero_of_adapted
          (d n r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) (hrn : r <= n)
          {w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hGram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w =
              BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hrn)
          (hadapt :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w)) =
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)) :
          ŌłĆ u : Fin (n - r), w (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u) = 0

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_range_sourceTupleLinearChange_eq
          (d n : Nat)
          {M : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé}
          (hM : IsUnit M.det)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          LinearMap.range
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n
                (BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n M z)) =
            LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)

      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_sourceGramCongruence
          (n : Nat) {M : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé}
          (hM : IsUnit M.det)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceGramCongruence n M Z) =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z

      theorem BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange_adapted_of_isUnit
          (d n : Nat)
          {M : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé}
          (hM : IsUnit M.det)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hadapt :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) =
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)) :
          Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range
                (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n
                  (BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n M z))) =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                (BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n M z))

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChange_tail_zero_of_adapted
          (d n r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) (hrn : r <= n)
          {Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)}
          {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          {P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
          (hA : IsUnit A.det)
          (hP : IsUnit P.det)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hGram :
            BHW.sourceGramCongruence n
              (BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix
                n r hrn Žā A B P)
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
            BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hrn)
          (hadapt :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) =
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)) :
          ŌłĆ u : Fin (n - r),
            BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n
              (BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix
                n r hrn Žā A B P) z
              (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u) = 0

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_canonicalGram_exists_complexLorentz_to_canonicalSource_of_adapted
          (d n r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) (hrn : r <= n)

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChange_exists_complexLorentz_to_canonicalSource_of_adapted
          (d n r : Nat) (hrD : r < d + 1) (hrn : r <= n)

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurSurjective
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat)
          (hrn : r <= n)

          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)

              Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              (ŌłĆ i j,
                ŌĆ¢Z i j - BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 i jŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->

      /-- Finite source-Gram coordinate ball used to turn the quantitative
      Lemma-3 estimate into an open scalar neighborhood. -/
      def BHW.sourceGramCoordBall
          (n : Nat)

          (n : Nat)

      /-- The final quantitative Lemma-3 bridge for an adapted base: choose
      the perturbation radius small enough that the vector perturbation
      produced by the normal-form realization remains in the ordinary
      extended tube. -/
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_adaptedBase_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {╬Č0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (h╬Č0 : ╬Č0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hspan :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Č0)) =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0))
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé,
              Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              Z Ōłł BHW.sourceGramCoordBall n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0) ╬Ę ->
              Ōłā v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
                (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢v i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Č0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) Ōłł
                  BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (fun i ╬╝ => ╬Č0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝) = Z
      ```

      Proof transcript for the residual small-realization theorem:

      0. Prove `hwLemma3_selectedBlock_sqrt_near_identity` by a finite
         matrix binomial square-root construction, not by an unnamed analytic
         choice.  This support packet is pure Banach-algebra matrix analysis;
         it imports only
         `Mathlib.Analysis.Analytic.Binomial`,
         `Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix.Normed`,
         `Mathlib.Analysis.Normed.Ring.InfiniteSum`, and
         `Mathlib.Topology.Instances.Matrix`.  All estimates in this packet
         are made under
         `open scoped Matrix.Norms.Operator`, so `Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé`
         carries the `LŌł×` operator norm
         `Matrix.linfty_opNorm_def`, not the elementwise sup norm.

         The case `r = 0` is discharged first: take any positive `╬┤` and the
         unique empty matrix `P`.  All quantified entry and product
         conditions are over `Fin 0`, hence vacuous.  The analytic construction
         below is used only in the branch `0 < r`, where Lean has
         `haveI : Nonempty (Fin r) := Fin.pos_iff_nonempty.mp hr` and the
         scoped operator-norm ring instances are available without any empty
         matrix edge case.

         First expose the finite norm conversion lemmas.  The first is the
         operator-norm estimate from `Matrix.linfty_opNorm_def`; the second
         is the entry estimate obtained from the same row-sum formula.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.matrix_opNorm_le_card_mul_sup_entry
             (r : Nat) [Nonempty (Fin r)]
             (B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé) :
             ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢ <= (r : ŌäØ) *
               ((Finset.univ : Finset (Fin r)).sup fun i =>
                 (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin r)).sup fun j =>
                   ŌĆ¢B i jŌĆ¢ŌéŖ : ŌäØ)

         theorem BHW.matrix_norm_lt_of_entry_bound
             (r : Nat) [Nonempty (Fin r)]
             {Žü : ŌäØ} (hŽü : 0 < Žü) :
             Ōłā ╬┤ : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬┤ Ōł¦
               ŌłĆ B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé,
                 (ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢B i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) -> ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢ < Žü

         theorem BHW.matrix_entry_norm_le_linfty_opNorm
             (r : Nat) [Nonempty (Fin r)]
             (B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
             (i j : Fin r) :
             ŌĆ¢B i jŌĆ¢ <= ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢
         ```

         For `matrix_norm_lt_of_entry_bound`, choose for example
         `╬┤ = Žü / (r + 1 : ŌäØ)`.  Since every row has `r` entries and
         `r / (r + 1) < 1`, `Matrix.linfty_opNorm_def` gives
         `ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢ <= r * ╬┤ < Žü`.  For
         `matrix_entry_norm_le_linfty_opNorm`, bound the selected entry by
         the selected row sum, and the row sum by the finite supremum in
         `Matrix.linfty_opNorm_def`.

         Define the coefficients and the matrix series:

         ```lean
         def BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff (m : Nat) : Ōäé :=
           Ring.choose (1 / 2 : Ōäé) m

         noncomputable def BHW.matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero
             (r : Nat) [Nonempty (Fin r)]
             (B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé) :
             Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé :=
           Ōłæ' m : Nat, BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff m ŌĆó (B ^ m)
         ```

         The scalar convergence theorem is deliberately scalar.  It uses
         Mathlib's binomial radius theorem only for the scalar series over
         `Ōäé`, then compares matrix terms by submultiplicativity:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.summable_norm_sqrtBinomialCoeff_mul_pow
             {Žü : ŌäØ} (hŽü_nonneg : 0 <= Žü) (hŽü_lt : Žü < 1) :
             Summable fun m : Nat =>
               ŌĆ¢BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff mŌĆ¢ * Žü ^ m

         theorem BHW.summable_norm_matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero_terms
             (r : Nat) [Nonempty (Fin r)]
             {B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
             (hB : ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢ < 1) :
             Summable fun m : Nat =>
               ŌĆ¢BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff m ŌĆó (B ^ m)ŌĆ¢

         theorem BHW.summable_matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero_terms
             (r : Nat) [Nonempty (Fin r)]
             {B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
             (hB : ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢ < 1) :
             Summable fun m : Nat =>
               BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff m ŌĆó (B ^ m)
         ```

         The first theorem follows from
         `binomialSeries_radius_ge_one` and
         `FormalMultilinearSeries.summable_norm_mul_pow` for the scalar
         binomial series.  The second uses
         `ŌĆ¢c ŌĆó B^mŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢cŌĆ¢ * ŌĆ¢B^mŌĆ¢`,
         `norm_pow_le`, and `ŌĆ¢B^mŌĆ¢ <= ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢^m`; the third is
         `Summable.of_norm`.

         The square identity is a Cauchy-product calculation, not a new
         spectral theorem.  The exact coefficient identity is
         Chu-Vandermonde in `RingTheory.Binomial`:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff_antidiagonal_sum
             (n : Nat) :
             (Ōłæ kl Ōłł Finset.Nat.antidiagonal n,
                BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff kl.1 *
                  BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff kl.2) =
             Ring.choose (1 : Ōäé) n := by
           have hhalf : (1 / 2 : Ōäé) + (1 / 2 : Ōäé) = 1 := by
             norm_num
           simpa [BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff, hhalf]
             using
               (Ring.add_choose_eq (R := Ōäé) n
                 (Commute.all (1 / 2 : Ōäé) (1 / 2 : Ōäé)))

         theorem BHW.sqrtBinomialCoeff_one_coeff
             (n : Nat) :
             Ring.choose (1 : Ōäé) n =
               if n = 0 then 1 else if n = 1 then 1 else 0
         ```

         The second line is just `Ring.choose_natCast` with `1 : Nat`,
         `Nat.choose_zero_right`, `Nat.choose_one_right`, and
         `Nat.choose_eq_zero_of_lt`.  Then
         `tsum_mul_tsum_eq_tsum_sum_antidiagonal_of_summable_norm` from
         `Mathlib.Analysis.Normed.Ring.InfiniteSum` gives:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero_sq
             (r : Nat) [Nonempty (Fin r)]
             {B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
             (hB : ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢ < 1) :
             BHW.matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero r B *
               BHW.matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero r B =
             1 + B
         ```

         Proof transcript: apply the absolute Cauchy-product theorem to
         `f m := sqrtBinomialCoeff m ŌĆó B^m`.  Reassociate every
         antidiagonal term with
         `B^p * B^q = B^(p+q)` using `pow_add` and the fact that all powers
         of the same matrix commute.  Since `(p,q) Ōłł antidiagonal n` means
         `p + q = n`, factor out `B^n` from the finite antidiagonal sum.
         Rewrite the scalar coefficient by
         `sqrtBinomialCoeff_antidiagonal_sum`, then by
         `sqrtBinomialCoeff_one_coeff`; all terms with `n >= 2` vanish, and
         the remaining `n = 0,1` terms are exactly `1 + B`.

         Transpose compatibility is also explicit:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero_transpose
             (r : Nat) [Nonempty (Fin r)]
             {B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
             (hB : ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢ < 1)
             (hBsymm : BßĄĆ = B) :
             (BHW.matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero r B)ßĄĆ =
               BHW.matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero r B
         ```

         Use `Matrix.transpose_tsum`,
         `Matrix.transpose_smul`, and `Matrix.transpose_pow`.  Each term
         rewrites as
         `(sqrtBinomialCoeff m ŌĆó B^m)ßĄĆ =
          sqrtBinomialCoeff m ŌĆó (BßĄĆ)^m =
          sqrtBinomialCoeff m ŌĆó B^m`.
         The convergence hypothesis `hB` supplies summability for the tsum on
         both sides.

         Finally record the continuity-at-zero estimate as a standard
         power-series support theorem, with its proof included rather than
         hidden behind a phrase like "analytic choice":

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.scalarPowerSeries_tendsto_at_zero
             {A : Type*} [NormedRing A] [CompleteSpace A]
             [NormedAlgebra Ōäé A]
             {c : Nat -> Ōäé}
             (hconv :
               ŌłĆ Žü : ŌäØ, 0 <= Žü -> Žü < 1 ->
                 Summable fun m : Nat => ŌĆ¢c mŌĆ¢ * Žü ^ m)
             (hc0 : c 0 = 1) :
             Tendsto
               (fun x : A => Ōłæ' m : Nat, c m ŌĆó (x ^ m))
               (­ØōØ 0) (­ØōØ 1)

         theorem BHW.matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero_tendsto_identity
             (r : Nat) [Nonempty (Fin r)] :
             Tendsto
               (fun B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé =>
                 BHW.matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero r B)
               (­ØōØ 0) (­ØōØ 1)
         ```
