         For `scalarPowerSeries_tendsto_at_zero`, fix `0 < Žü < 1`.  The
         shifted majorant
         `MŽü := Ōłæ' m : Nat, ŌĆ¢c (m+1)ŌĆ¢ * Žü^m` is finite by `hconv`.
         If `ŌĆ¢xŌĆ¢ < Žü`, then
         `ŌĆ¢Ōłæ' m, c m ŌĆó x^m - c 0 ŌĆó 1ŌĆ¢`
         is bounded by
         `ŌĆ¢xŌĆ¢ * MŽü`; choose `ŌĆ¢xŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą / max MŽü 1`.  This proof uses only
         `Summable.of_norm`, `tsum_sub`, `tsum_mul_left`, `norm_tsum_le_tsum_norm`,
         `norm_pow_le`, and the scalar comparison theorem above.  Applying it
         with `c = sqrtBinomialCoeff` gives the matrix theorem.

         The selected-block theorem is then a direct ╬┤-selection:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.hwLemma3_selectedBlock_sqrt_near_identity
             (r : Nat)
             {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
             Ōłā ╬┤ : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬┤ Ōł¦
               ŌłĆ B0 : Fin r -> Fin r -> Ōäé,
                 (ŌłĆ i j, B0 i j = B0 j i) ->
                 (ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢B0 i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) ->
                 Ōłā P : Fin r -> Fin r -> Ōäé,
                   (ŌłĆ i j, P i j = P j i) Ōł¦
                   (ŌłĆ i j,
                     ŌĆ¢P i j - (if i = j then (1 : Ōäé) else 0)ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                   (ŌłĆ i j,
                     Ōłæ k : Fin r, P i k * P j k =
                       (if i = j then (1 : Ōäé) else 0) + B0 i j)
         ```

         In the `0 < r` branch, use
         `matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero_tendsto_identity r` to get
         `╬Ę > 0` such that `ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę` implies
         `ŌĆ¢matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero r B - 1ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą`.  Use
         `matrix_norm_lt_of_entry_bound r (min_pos ╬Ę_pos zero_lt_one)` to
         shrink the entrywise radius so that the same hypothesis also gives
         `ŌĆ¢BŌĆ¢ < 1`.  Let
         `B : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé := fun i j => B0 i j` and
         `P := matrixSqrtOneAddNearZero r B`.  Symmetry of `B0` gives
         `BßĄĆ = B`, hence `PßĄĆ = P` by the transpose theorem.  The square
         theorem gives `P * P = 1 + B`, and after rewriting with `PßĄĆ = P`,
         the matrix entry identity is exactly
         `Ōłæ k, P i k * P j k = (if i = j then 1 else 0) + B0 i j`.
         The entrywise closeness follows from
         `matrix_entry_norm_le_linfty_opNorm r (P - 1) i j`.  This closes
         the selected-block square-root theorem with no spectral import and
         no Hall-Wightman-specific assumption.
      0b. Prove `hwLemma3_schurComplement_rank_bound` by Mathlib's actual
          Schur-complement block factorization, not by handwaving a block
          Gaussian step.  The support imports are
          `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.SchurComplement`,
          `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.Rank`, and
          `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.ToLin`.  For an invertible selected
          block `A`, put
          `S := C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose`.  Use
          `letI := Matrix.invertibleOfIsUnitDet hA_inv`, and convert
          `Ōģ¤A` to `AŌü╗┬╣` with `Matrix.invOf_eq_nonsing_inv`.

          The exact factorization to use is already in Mathlib:

          ```lean
          Matrix.fromBlocks_eq_of_invertibleŌéüŌéü
            (A := A) (B := B.transpose) (C := B) (D := C)
          ```

          After rewriting `Ōģ¤A = AŌü╗┬╣`, it gives

          ```lean
          Matrix.fromBlocks A B.transpose B C =
            Matrix.fromBlocks 1 0 (B * AŌü╗┬╣) 1 *
              Matrix.fromBlocks A 0 0
                (C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose) *
            Matrix.fromBlocks 1 (AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose) 0 1
          ```

          The triangular factors are invertible by the propositional
          block-triangular APIs
          `Matrix.isUnit_fromBlocks_zeroŌéüŌéé` and
          `Matrix.isUnit_fromBlocks_zeroŌééŌéü`, applied to the two identity
          diagonal blocks, then converted to determinant-unit hypotheses by
          `Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det.mp`.  Therefore their multiplication
          does not change rank by the checked rank lemmas:

          ```lean
          Matrix.rank_mul_eq_right_of_isUnit_det
          Matrix.rank_mul_eq_left_of_isUnit_det
          ```

          The remaining block-diagonal rank additivity must be its own pure
          theorem, because no route step may silently assume it:

          ```lean
          theorem BHW.matrix_rank_fromBlocks_zero_zero
              (A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
              (S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) :
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.fromBlocks A 0 0 S) =
                Matrix.rank A + Matrix.rank S
          ```

          Proof transcript for this helper: rewrite all ranks by
          `Matrix.rank_eq_finrank_range_toLin` using product bases.  Identify
          the linear map represented by `Matrix.fromBlocks A 0 0 S` with
          `(Matrix.toLin' A).prodMap (Matrix.toLin' S)` via
          `LinearMap.toMatrix_prodMap` and the `Pi.basisFun` product basis.
          Then use `LinearMap.range_prodMap` and
          `Module.finrank_prod` to get the sum of the two component ranks.

          The Schur rank equality is then:

          ```lean
          theorem BHW.matrix_rank_fromBlocks_schurComplement_eq
              {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
              {B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin r) Ōäé}
              {C : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
              (hA_inv : IsUnit A.det) :
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.fromBlocks A B.transpose B C) =
                r + Matrix.rank (C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose) := by
            have hA_rank :
                Matrix.rank A = r := by
              simpa [Fintype.card_fin] using
                Matrix.rank_of_isUnit A
                  (Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det.mpr hA_inv)
            -- factor by `fromBlocks_eq_of_invertibleŌéüŌéü`, drop the two
            -- invertible triangular factors by rank invariance, use
            -- `matrix_rank_fromBlocks_zero_zero`, and rewrite `hA_rank`.
          ```

          The theorem does not mathematically need symmetry of `A`; the
          surrounding Hall-Wightman block is symmetric, but the rank argument
          only uses invertibility.  The public Lemma-3 bound may keep the
          `hA_symm` hypothesis if it is already available upstream, but the
          proof must not use it to hide any rank algebra:

          ```lean
          theorem BHW.hwLemma3_schurComplement_rank_bound
              (D r m : Nat)
              {A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé}
              {B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin r) Ōäé}
              {C : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
              (hA_symm : A.transpose = A)
              (hA_inv : IsUnit A.det)
              (hRank :
                Matrix.rank
                  (Matrix.fromBlocks A B.transpose B C) <= D) :
              Matrix.rank (C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose) <= D - r := by
            have hEq :=
              BHW.matrix_rank_fromBlocks_schurComplement_eq
                (A := A) (B := B) (C := C) hA_inv
            have hAdd :
                r + Matrix.rank (C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose) <= D := by
              simpa [hEq] using hRank
            exact Nat.le_sub_of_add_le hAdd
          ```

          This is pure finite matrix algebra.  It is the exact proof of the
          Schur-rank theorem used in Hall-Wightman Lemma 3.
      1. Prove the entry-controlled Autonne-Takagi factor theorem
         `complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi_entryL1` first; the
         older theorem `complexSymmetric_takagi_rankLE` is only its first
         projection.  The proof is the standard finite-dimensional
         Autonne-Takagi theorem for complex symmetric matrices: there is a
         unitary matrix `U` and nonnegative singular values `Žā a` such that
         `S = U * Matrix.diagonal (fun a => (Žā a : Ōäé)) * U.transpose`.
         The singular values are the square roots of the eigenvalues of the
         positive Hermitian matrix `S * Sß┤┤`; Mathlib supplies the
         Hermitian spectral theorem through `Matrix.IsHermitian.eigenvectorUnitary`
         in `Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix.Spectrum`.
         The symmetric condition `SßĄĆ = S` is the additional Autonne step which
         chooses the phases so the right unitary is `U.transpose`, not an
         unrelated unitary.  The local support theorem should expose this as:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi
             (m : Nat)
             {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S) :
             Ōłā (U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé)
               (Žā : Fin m -> ŌäØ),
                (ŌłĆ a, 0 <= Žā a) Ōł¦
                S =
                  (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) *
                    Matrix.diagonal (fun a => (Žā a : Ōäé)) *
                    (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé).transpose Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ a, Žā a <= BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S) Ōł¦
                Fintype.card {a : Fin m // Žā a ŌēĀ 0} = Matrix.rank S
         ```

         The quantitative clause is deliberately stated with the explicit
         finite entry sum
         `BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S = Ōłæ p : Fin m ├Ś Fin m, ŌĆ¢S p.1 p.2ŌĆ¢`,
         not with `ŌĆ¢SŌĆ¢`, so the proof is independent of the particular matrix
         norm instance in scope.  The support theorem is:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.takagi_singularValue_le_entryL1Bound
             (m : Nat)
             {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             {U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé}
             {Žā : Fin m -> ŌäØ}
             (hŽā_nonneg : ŌłĆ a, 0 <= Žā a)
             (hTakagi :
               S =
                 (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) *
                   Matrix.diagonal (fun a => (Žā a : Ōäé)) *
                 (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé).transpose) :
             ŌłĆ a, Žā a <= BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S
         ```

         This support theorem is now checked in
         `SourceComplexTakagiEntry.lean` by direct diagonal extraction, not by
         the older Frobenius-norm detour.  The checked local helpers are:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.matrix_unitary_transpose_mul_star_transpose
             (m : Nat) (U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé) :
             ((U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé).transpose) *
               star ((U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé).transpose) = 1

         theorem BHW.takagi_diagonal_extraction
             (m : Nat)
             {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             {U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé}
             {Žā : Fin m -> ŌäØ}
             (hTakagi :
               S =
                 (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) *
                   Matrix.diagonal (fun a => (Žā a : Ōäé)) *
                   (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé).transpose)
             (a : Fin m) :
             (Žā a : Ōäé) =
               Ōłæ p : Fin m ├Ś Fin m,
                 S p.2 p.1 *
                   (star ((U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) p.1 a) *
                     star ((U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) p.2 a))
         ```

         The extraction proof rewrites
         `star U * S * star UßĄĆ` by `hTakagi`, uses
         `star U * U = 1` and `UßĄĆ * star UßĄĆ = 1`, and expands the `(a,a)`
         entry.  Taking norms of the extracted double sum gives
         `Žā a <= Ōłæ p, ŌĆ¢S p.2 p.1ŌĆ¢` because both unitary column entries have
         norm at most `1`; the final sum is reindexed by `Equiv.prodComm` to
         become `BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S`.

         The proof of `complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi` is not allowed to be
         an unnamed spectral-theory appeal.  The finite-dimensional route is:

         ```lean
         def BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap
             (m : Nat)
             (S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) :
             (Fin m -> Ōäé) -> (Fin m -> Ōäé) :=
           fun v => S.mulVec (star v)

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap_add
             (m : Nat) (S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé)
             (v w : Fin m -> Ōäé) :
             BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S (v + w) =
               BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S v +
               BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S w

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap_smul
             (m : Nat) (S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé)
             (c : Ōäé) (v : Fin m -> Ōäé) :
             BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S (c ŌĆó v) =
               star c ŌĆó BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S v

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap_sq
             (m : Nat)
             {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S) :
             (fun v =>
               BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S
                 (BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S v)) =
             fun v => (S * Sß┤┤).mulVec v
         ```

         The square is the positive Hermitian linear map `S * Sß┤┤`, using
         `Sß┤┤ = star S` under `S.transpose = S`.  Apply
         `Matrix.IsHermitian.eigenvectorUnitary` to `H := S * Sß┤┤`, whose
         Hermitian proof is `(Matrix.isHermitian_mul_conjTranspose_self S)`
         after rewriting orientations.  The eigenspace split must expose the
         following support, because the Autonne step depends on the
         conjugate-linear map, not only on the linear spectral theorem:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap_commutes_square
             (m : Nat)
             {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S) :
             (fun v =>
               BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S
                 ((S * Sß┤┤).mulVec v)) =
             fun v =>
               (S * Sß┤┤).mulVec
                 (BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S v)

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap_mem_eigenspace
             (m : Nat)
             {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {╬╗ : ŌäØ} {v : Fin m -> Ōäé}
             (hv : (S * Sß┤┤).mulVec v = (╬╗ : Ōäé) ŌĆó v) :
             (S * Sß┤┤).mulVec
                 (BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S v) =
               (╬╗ : Ōäé) ŌĆó
                 BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S v

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap_zero_eigenspace_eq_zero
             (m : Nat)
             {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {v : Fin m -> Ōäé}
             (hv : (S * Sß┤┤).mulVec v = 0) :
             BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S v = 0

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap_positive_eigenspace_conjugation
             (m : Nat)
             {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {lambda : ŌäØ} (hlambda : 0 < lambda) :
             Ōłā J :
               (LinearMap.eigenspace
                   (Matrix.toLin' (S * Sß┤┤)) (lambda : Ōäé)) ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé]
                 (LinearMap.eigenspace
                   (Matrix.toLin' (S * Sß┤┤)) (lambda : Ōäé)),
               (ŌłĆ x, J (J x) = x) Ōł¦
               ŌłĆ x,
                 (J x : Fin m -> Ōäé) =
                   (Real.sqrt lambda : Ōäé)Ōü╗┬╣ ŌĆó
                     BHW.takagiConjugateLinearMap m S (x : Fin m -> Ōäé)
         ```

         The first theorem is just `C Ōłś C^2 = C^2 Ōłś C`.  The second uses
         that Hermitian eigenvalues are real and `J` is conjugate-linear, so
         conjugating `(╬╗ : Ōäé) ŌĆó v` leaves the scalar `(╬╗ : Ōäé)` fixed.  For
         the zero eigenspace,
         `C^2 v = 0` implies `C v = 0` because
         `ŌĆ¢C vŌĆ¢^2 = re Ō¤¬C^2 v, vŌ¤½`; these basis vectors receive singular
         value `0`.  For `0 < ╬╗`, put `Žā = Real.sqrt ╬╗`.  On the eigenspace
         `E╬╗`, the map `J := ŽāŌü╗┬╣ ŌĆó C` is conjugate-linear, maps `E╬╗` to
         itself by the eigenspace theorem, squares to the identity by
         `C^2 = ╬╗ ŌĆó id`, and is isometric because
         `ŌĆ¢C vŌĆ¢^2 = ╬╗ * ŌĆ¢vŌĆ¢^2`.

         The remaining finite-dimensional theorem is:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.conjugateLinear_isometricInvolution_fixedOrthonormalBasis
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
             (J : E -> E)
             (hJ_add : ŌłĆ x y, J (x + y) = J x + J y)
             (hJ_smul : ŌłĆ c x, J (c ŌĆó x) = star c ŌĆó J x)
             (hJ_sq : ŌłĆ x, J (J x) = x)
             (hJ_norm : ŌłĆ x, ŌĆ¢J xŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢xŌĆ¢) :
             Ōłā b : OrthonormalBasis (Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé E)) Ōäé E,
               ŌłĆ i, J (b i) = b i
         ```

         This fixed-basis theorem is not a black box.  Its generic real-form
         and basis construction is now checked in
         `SourceComplexTakagiFixed.lean` through the bundled
         semilinear-isometry API from
         `Mathlib.Analysis.Normed.Operator.LinearIsometry`.  The
         positive-eigenspace phase task is also now checked in
         `SourceComplexTakagiPhase.lean`: for each `lambda > 0`, the
         eigenspace of `S * Sß┤┤` is packaged as a submodule of
         `EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m)`, the norm identity for
         `v Ōå” S *ßĄź star v` is proved there, and the normalized map is bundled
         as the required `Jiso : E╬╗ ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E╬╗`.  The subsequent global
         assembly is now checked in `SourceComplexTakagiGlobal.lean`: it
         collects those fixed positive-eigenspace bases together with the
         zero-eigenspace basis, reindexes to `Fin m`, proves the diagonal
         Takagi identity, and derives the quantitative rank-support bound.
         The checked generic layer is:

         ```lean
         def BHW.conjugationFixedSubmodule
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             (J : E ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E) :
             Submodule ŌäØ E

         instance BHW.conjugationFixedSubmodule_innerProductSpaceReal
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             (J : E ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E) :
             InnerProductSpace ŌäØ (BHW.conjugationFixedSubmodule J)

         theorem BHW.conjugateLinearIsometry_inner_map_map
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             (J : E ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E) (x y : E) :
             inner Ōäé (J x) (J y) = star (inner Ōäé x y)

         theorem BHW.conjugationFixed_decomposition_apply
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             (J : E ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E)
             (hJ_sq : ŌłĆ x, J (J x) = x) (x : E) :
             (BHW.conjugationFixedRealPart J hJ_sq x : E) +
               Complex.I ŌĆó (BHW.conjugationFixedImagPart J hJ_sq x : E) = x

         theorem BHW.conjugationFixed_decomposition_injective
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             (J : E ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E) :
             Function.Injective
               (fun p :
                 BHW.conjugationFixedSubmodule J ├Ś
                   BHW.conjugationFixedSubmodule J =>
                 (p.1 : E) + Complex.I ŌĆó (p.2 : E))

         theorem BHW.conjugationFixedSubmodule_finrank
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
             (J : E ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E)
             (hJ_sq : ŌłĆ x, J (J x) = x) :
             Module.finrank ŌäØ (BHW.conjugationFixedSubmodule J) =
               Module.finrank Ōäé E

         theorem BHW.conjugationFixed_inner_im_eq_zero
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             (J : E ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E)
             (x y : BHW.conjugationFixedSubmodule J) :
             (Ō¤¬(x : E), (y : E)Ō¤½_Ōäé).im = 0

         theorem BHW.conjugationFixed_complexOrthonormal
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
             (J : E ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E)
             (hJ_sq : ŌłĆ x, J (J x) = x) :
             Orthonormal Ōäé
               (fun i : Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé E) =>
                 (BHW.conjugationFixedRealOrthonormalBasis J hJ_sq i : E))

         noncomputable def BHW.conjugationFixedComplexOrthonormalBasis
             {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace Ōäé E]
             [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
             (J : E ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé] E)
             (hJ_sq : ŌłĆ x, J (J x) = x) :
             OrthonormalBasis (Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé E)) Ōäé E
         ```

         The checked positive-eigenspace phase layer is:

         ```lean
         def BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace
             (m : ŌäĢ) (S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) (lambda : ŌäØ) :
             Submodule Ōäé (EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m))

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap_norm_sq_of_eigen
             (m : ŌäĢ) {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé} (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {lambda : ŌäØ} {v : EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m)}
             (hv : (S * Sß┤┤) *ßĄź (v : Fin m ŌåÆ Ōäé) =
               (lambda : Ōäé) ŌĆó (v : Fin m ŌåÆ Ōäé)) :
             ŌĆ¢BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap m S vŌĆ¢ ^ 2 =
               lambda * ŌĆ¢vŌĆ¢ ^ 2

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap_normalized_norm_of_eigen
             (m : ŌäĢ) {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé} (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {lambda : ŌäØ} (hlambda : 0 < lambda)
             {v : EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m)}
             (hv : (S * Sß┤┤) *ßĄź (v : Fin m ŌåÆ Ōäé) =
               (lambda : Ōäé) ŌĆó (v : Fin m ŌåÆ Ōäé)) :
             ŌĆ¢(((Real.sqrt lambda)Ōü╗┬╣ : ŌäØ) : Ōäé) ŌĆó
               BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap m S vŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢vŌĆ¢

         noncomputable def BHW.takagiPositiveEigenspaceConjugation
             (m : ŌäĢ) {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {lambda : ŌäØ} (hlambda : 0 < lambda) :
             BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace m S lambda ŌēāŌéŚßĄóŌŗå[Ōäé]
               BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace m S lambda

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap_eq_sqrt_smul_of_positive_fixed
             (m : ŌäĢ) {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé} (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {lambda : ŌäØ} (hlambda : 0 < lambda)
             (x : BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace m S lambda)
             (hx :
               BHW.takagiPositiveEigenspaceConjugation
                 (m := m) (S := S) hSym hlambda x = x) :
             BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap m S
                 (x : EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m)) =
               (Real.sqrt lambda : Ōäé) ŌĆó (x : EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m))

         noncomputable def BHW.takagiPositiveEigenspaceFixedBasis
             (m : ŌäĢ) {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {lambda : ŌäØ} (hlambda : 0 < lambda) :
             OrthonormalBasis
               (Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé
                 (BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace m S lambda))) Ōäé
               (BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace m S lambda)

         theorem BHW.takagiPositiveEigenspace_fixedBasis_col_eigen
             (m : ŌäĢ) {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {lambda : ŌäØ} (hlambda : 0 < lambda)
             (i : Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé
               (BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace m S lambda))) :
             BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap m S
                 ((BHW.takagiPositiveEigenspaceFixedBasis
                   (m := m) (S := S) hSym hlambda i :
                   BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace m S lambda) :
                   EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m)) =
               (Real.sqrt lambda : Ōäé) ŌĆó
                 ((BHW.takagiPositiveEigenspaceFixedBasis
                   (m := m) (S := S) hSym hlambda i :
                   BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace m S lambda) :
                   EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m))

         theorem BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap_zero_eigenspace_eq_zero
             (m : ŌäĢ) {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé} (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             {v : EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m)}
             (hv : (S * Sß┤┤) *ßĄź (v : Fin m ŌåÆ Ōäé) = 0) :
             BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap m S v = 0

         theorem BHW.takagiZeroEigenspace_col_eigen
             (m : ŌäĢ) {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             (hSym : S.transpose = S)
             (v : BHW.takagiHermitianEigenspace m S 0) :
             BHW.takagiConjugateLinearEuclideanMap m S
                 (v : EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m)) =
               (0 : Ōäé) ŌĆó (v : EuclideanSpace Ōäé (Fin m))
         ```

         Proof transcript for the checked helpers:

         * `conjugateLinearIsometry_inner_map_map` expands both sides by
           `inner_eq_sum_norm_sq_div_four`.  The semilinear identities
           `J (x + y) = J x + J y`, `J (x - y) = J x - J y`,
           `J (x + I ŌĆó y) = J x - I ŌĆó J y`, and
           `J (x - I ŌĆó y) = J x + I ŌĆó J y`, together with
           `LinearIsometryEquiv.norm_map`, turn the left-hand polarization
           formula into the conjugate of the right-hand formula.
         * For `conjugationFixed_decomposition_apply`, the inverse map sends
           `x : E` to
           `u = (1 / 2 : ŌäØ) ŌĆó (x + J x)` and
           `v = (1 / 2 : ŌäØ) ŌĆó (Complex.I ŌĆó (J x - x))`.  The identities
           `J u = u`, `J v = v`, and `u + I ŌĆó v = x` are direct algebra from
           semilinearity, `J (I ŌĆó y) = -I ŌĆó J y`, and `hJ_sq`.
         * `conjugationFixedSubmodule_finrank` compares real dimensions in
           the bijective real-linear map
           `fixed ├Ś fixed -> E`, `p Ōå” p.1 + I ŌĆó p.2`.  The codomain is `E`
           as a real vector space; Mathlib gives
           `Module.finrank ŌäØ E = 2 * Module.finrank Ōäé E`, while
           `Module.finrank_prod` gives twice the real dimension of the fixed
           subspace.  Cancel the factor `2`.
         * `conjugationFixed_inner_im_eq_zero` uses antiunitarity:
           `Ō¤¬J x, J yŌ¤½ = star Ō¤¬x,yŌ¤½`.  Since `x` and `y` are fixed, the
           inner product equals its conjugate, hence its imaginary part is
           zero.
         * `conjugationFixedComplexOrthonormalBasis` chooses
           `stdOrthonormalBasis ŌäØ (BHW.conjugationFixedSubmodule J)`, reindexes
           it by `conjugationFixedSubmodule_finrank`, proves the coerced
           family is complex-orthonormal from
           `conjugationFixed_inner_eq_ofReal_real_inner`, then invokes
           `OrthonormalBasis.mk`.  The complex span condition is discharged
           by `hon.linearIndependent.span_eq_top_of_card_eq_finrank'`, because
           the index type is already `Fin (Module.finrank Ōäé E)`.

         Applying this on every positive eigenspace and adjoining a
         zero-eigenspace basis is now factored through the checked bridge
         `BHW.complexSymmetric_takagi_exists_unitary_of_fixedHermitianEigenbasis`:
         it takes an orthonormal Hermitian-square eigenbasis `b`, nonnegative
         eigenvalue labels `lambda`, the positive fixedness condition, and
         proves the Takagi unitary with singular values `Real.sqrt Ōłś lambda`.
         The entry-controlled version
         `BHW.complexSymmetric_entryL1_of_fixedHermitianEigenbasis` composes
         this bridge with `BHW.complexSymmetric_entryL1_of_autonneTakagiDiagonalization`.
         Thus the remaining construction is exactly the global collected ONB
         and its label/rank-support bookkeeping.  If `U` is the unitary matrix
         with columns `u a`, the entrywise identity
         `S i j = Ōłæ a, U i a * (Žā a : Ōäé) * U j a` is exactly
         `S = U * diagonal Žā * U.transpose`; this algebraic endpoint is
         checked as `BHW.complexSymmetric_takagi_matrix_eq_of_col_eigen`, and
         the orthonormal-basis-to-unitary wrapper is checked as
         `BHW.complexSymmetric_takagi_exists_unitary_of_orthonormalBasis_col_eigen`.
         The rank-support equality says
         `Žā a ŌēĀ 0` exactly on the positive eigenspaces of `S * Sß┤┤`, whose
         total dimension is `Matrix.rank S`; the bound
         `Žā a <= BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S` is now the direct checked
         diagonal-extraction estimate
         `BHW.takagi_singularValue_le_entryL1Bound`.

         From this, use `hRank : Matrix.rank S <= k` to inject the finite
         nonzero singular-value index subtype into `Fin k`.  The exact
         finite-cardinality API is
         `Fintype.nonempty_of_card_le` from
         `Mathlib.Data.Fintype.EquivFin`:

         ```lean
         rcases BHW.complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi (m := m) hSym with
           Ō¤©U, Žā, hŽā_nonneg, hTakagi, hŽā_le_entry, hrankSupportŌ¤®
         let nz : Type := {a : Fin m // Žā a ŌēĀ 0}
         have hnz_card : Fintype.card nz <= Fintype.card (Fin k) := by
           simpa [nz, hrankSupport] using hRank
         rcases Fintype.nonempty_of_card_le hnz_card with
           Ō¤©e : nz Ōå¬ Fin kŌ¤®
         ```

         The rectangular factor is then not a choice-free informal column
         selection; it is the following finite matrix:

         ```lean
         let A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé :=
           fun i b =>
             if hb : Ōłā a : nz, e a = b then
               let a : nz := Classical.choose hb
               (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) i a.1 *
                 (Real.sqrt (Žā a.1) : Ōäé)
             else 0
         ```

         The factor identity uses the following support lemma:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_takagi_factor_from_supportEmbedding
             (m k : Nat)
             {U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé}
             {Žā : Fin m -> ŌäØ}
             (hŽā_nonneg : ŌłĆ a, 0 <= Žā a)
             (e : {a : Fin m // Žā a ŌēĀ 0} Ōå¬ Fin k)
             (S :
               Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé)
             (hTakagi :
               S =
                 (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) *
                   Matrix.diagonal (fun a => (Žā a : Ōäé)) *
                   (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé).transpose)
             (A : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin k) Ōäé)
             (hA :
               ŌłĆ i b,
                 A i b =
                   if hb : Ōłā a : {a : Fin m // Žā a ŌēĀ 0}, e a = b then
                     let a := Classical.choose hb
                     (U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) i a.1 *
                       (Real.sqrt (Žā a.1) : Ōäé)
                   else 0) :
             S = A * A.transpose
         ```

         Proof: expand the `(i,j)` entry of `A * A.transpose` as a sum over
         `Fin k`.  Split the sum into `Set.range e` and its complement; the
         complement is zero by the definition of `A`.  Reindex the range sum
         along the embedding `e` using `Finset.sum_embedding`/`Finset.sum_bij`
         and `e.injective`.  For each nonzero singular index `a`,
         `Real.sq_sqrt (hŽā_nonneg a.1)` rewrites
         `(sqrt Žāa : Ōäé) * (sqrt Žāa : Ōäé)` to `(Žāa : Ōäé)`.  The omitted indices
         have `Žā a = 0`, so the diagonal Takagi sum over all `Fin m` equals
         the nonzero-support sum.  This proves the factor identity entrywise.

         The norm estimate is also a separate finite lemma, not hidden in the
         factor theorem:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.matrix_unitary_entry_norm_le_one
             (m : Nat)
             (U : Matrix.unitaryGroup (Fin m) Ōäé)
             (i a : Fin m) :
             ŌĆ¢(U : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé) i aŌĆ¢ <= 1
         ```

         It follows by applying the unit-column identity
         `Ōłæ i, ŌĆ¢U i aŌĆ¢^2 = 1`; the selected summand is nonnegative, so it is
         at most the sum.  Consequently, if
         `Žā a <= BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S`, then

         ```lean
         ŌĆ¢U i a * (Real.sqrt (Žā a) : Ōäé)ŌĆ¢
           <= Real.sqrt (BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S)
         ```

         by `norm_mul`, `Complex.norm_ofReal`, monotonicity of `Real.sqrt`,
         and `0 <= Žā a`.  This proves
         `complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi_entryL1`.
      2. Prove `complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE` from the entry-controlled
         theorem.  This reduction is now checked in
         `SourceComplexSmallFactor.lean` as
         `complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE_of_entryL1` and
         `sourceComplexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE_of_entryL1`; the remaining
         unproved mathematical input is exactly
         `complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi_entryL1`.  The finite
         smallness helper is:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound_lt_of_entry_bound
             (m : Nat)
             {S : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin m) Ōäé}
             {╬┤ : ŌäØ} (h╬┤ : 0 < ╬┤)
             (hS : ŌłĆ i j, ŌĆ¢S i jŌĆ¢ < ╬┤) :
             BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S <
               (((m * m : Nat) : ŌäØ) + 1) * ╬┤
         ```

         The extra `+ 1` makes the empty `m = 0` case automatic:
         the entry sum is `0`, while the right side is positive.  For
         `0 < m`, expand the finite sum over `Fin m ├Ś Fin m`; every summand
         is `< ╬┤`, and the number of summands is `m * m`.

         Given `╬Ą > 0`, put `C_m = ((m * m : Nat) : ŌäØ) + 1` and choose
         `╬┤ > 0` with `Real.sqrt (C_m * ╬┤) < ╬Ą`.  The entrywise bound on `S`
         gives
         `Real.sqrt (BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S) < ╬Ą`, and the entry bound
         from `complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi_entryL1` gives
         `ŌĆ¢A i aŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą`.  This step is finite matrix linear algebra only; it
         has no BHW, OS, or locality content.
      3. Define `standardCoordinateTailSubspace (d + 1) r` as
         `{u | ŌłĆ ╬╝, ╬╝.val < r -> u ╬╝ = 0}`, and define
         `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace d r` as its `comap` under
         `(BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d).toLinearMap`.  The membership
         theorem is therefore `Iff.rfl` after unfolding.  Orthogonality to the
         selected block is then the ordinary standard-bilinear calculation
         `Ōłæ ╬╝, u ╬╝ * w ╬╝ = 0`: the tail vector has zero coordinates for
         `╬╝.val < r`, while a selected-block vector has zero coordinates for
         `r <= ╬╝.val`.  Split the finite sum by
         `Finset.filter (fun ╬╝ => ╬╝.val < r)` and its complement, close each
         summand by one of the two support hypotheses, and transport the
         equality back with
         `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_eq_dot_after_equiv`.
      4. For
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail`, first
         apply `complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE` with
         `k <= d + 1 - r`.  Embed the resulting `Fin k` columns into the tail
         coordinates using `exists_finTailEmbedding` and
         `tailEmbeddedFactorVectors`; all other diagonalized coordinates are
         zero.  The theorem
         `standardComplexSymmetricBilinear_tailEmbeddedFactor` proves both
         support in the coordinate tail and
         `standardComplexSymmetricBilinear q_i q_j = (A * AßĄĆ) i j`.
         The pointwise coordinate estimate is the direct helper
         `tailEmbeddedFactorVectors_coord_bound`.
         The embedding theorem is explicit:
         `╬╣ a = Ō¤©r + a.val, by omegaŌ¤®`; injectivity is
         `Nat.add_left_cancel`, and the tail inequality is `Nat.le_add_right`.
         In `standardComplexSymmetricBilinear_tailEmbeddedFactor`, split the
         finite coordinate sum into the range of `╬╣` and its complement.
         Outside the range every embedded coordinate is zero by definition.
         On the range, use `h╬╣_inj` to identify the unique chosen index, then
         reindex by `Fin k`; the resulting sum is precisely
         `(A * A.transpose) i j`.
         Transport the vectors back by
         `(BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d).symm`.  Finite-dimensional norm
         equivalence for this fixed linear equivalence is exposed as
         `complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv_symm_coord_bound`, so the Takagi
         factorization is requested with `╬Ą / C`; after transport every
         ambient coordinate is `< ╬Ą`.  The support proof is exactly the
         inequality `r <= (╬╣ a).val` from `exists_finTailEmbedding`.
      5. The older theorem
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE` is the special case
         `r = 0` of the tail theorem.  The normalized Schur-surjectivity proof
         must use the tail theorem with `k = d + 1 - r`; otherwise it would
         realize the residual matrix in directions that might not be
         orthogonal to the selected block.

      Lean-shaped proof of the small Takagi factor and tail realization:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_takagi_rankLE ... := by
        rcases BHW.complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi_entryL1
            (m := m) (k := k) hSym hRank with
          Ō¤©A, hA_factor, _hA_entryŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©A, hA_factorŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE ... := by
        let C : ŌäØ := (((m * m : Nat) : ŌäØ) + 1)
        have hC_pos : 0 < C := by positivity
        rcases BHW.exists_pos_mul_sqrt_lt h╬Ą hC_pos with
          Ō¤©╬┤, h╬┤_pos, h╬┤_smallŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©╬┤, h╬┤_pos, by
          intro S hSym hRank hS_entry
          rcases BHW.complexSymmetric_autonneTakagi_entryL1
              (m := m) (k := k) hSym hRank with
            Ō¤©A, hA_factor, hA_entryŌ¤®
          have hS_entryL1_small :
              Real.sqrt (BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S) < ╬Ą := by
            have hL1 :
                BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S < C * ╬┤ :=
              BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound_lt_of_entry_bound
                (m := m) h╬┤_pos hS_entry
            exact BHW.real_sqrt_lt_of_lt_mul_bound
              (BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound_nonneg m S) hL1 h╬┤_small
          exact Ō¤©A, fun i a => lt_of_le_of_lt (hA_entry i a) hS_entryL1_small,
            hA_factorŌ¤®Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail
          ... := by
        rcases BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv_symm_coord_bound
            (d := d) with
          Ō¤©C, hC_pos, hC_boundŌ¤®
        let ╬Ą0 : ŌäØ := ╬Ą / (C * (d + 1) + 1)
        have h╬Ą0_pos : 0 < ╬Ą0 := by positivity
        rcases BHW.complexSymmetric_factorSmall_rankLE
            (m := m) (k := k) h╬Ą0_pos with
          Ō¤©╬┤, h╬┤_pos, hfactorŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©╬┤, h╬┤_pos, by
          intro S hSym hRank hk_tail hS_small
          rcases hfactor (Matrix.of fun i j => S i j)
              (by ext i j; exact hSym j i)
              hRank
              (by simpa using hS_small) with
            Ō¤©A, hA_small, hA_factorŌ¤®
          rcases BHW.exists_finTailEmbedding (D := d + 1) (r := r) (k := k)
              hk_tail with
            Ō¤©╬╣, h╬╣_inj, h╬╣_tailŌ¤®
          let u : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
            BHW.tailEmbeddedFactorVectors (d + 1) r m k ╬╣ A
          let q : Fin m -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
            fun i => (BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d).symm (u i)
          have hu_tail_and_pair :=
            BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear_tailEmbeddedFactor
              (D := d + 1) (r := r) (m := m) (k := k)
              h╬╣_inj h╬╣_tail A
          have hu_small : ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢u i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą0 :=
            BHW.tailEmbeddedFactorVectors_coord_bound
              (D := d + 1) (r := r) (m := m) (k := k) hA_small
          exact Ō¤©q,
            by
              intro i ╬╝
              have hsum :
                  Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1), ŌĆ¢u i ╬ĮŌĆ¢ < (d + 1) * ╬Ą0 := by
                exact BHW.sum_norm_lt_card_mul_of_coord_bound hu_small
              exact lt_of_le_of_lt (hC_bound (u i) ╬╝)
                (by dsimp [╬Ą0]; nlinarith [hC_pos, hsum, h╬Ą]),
            by
              intro i
              -- Unfold the transported tail subspace; the first component of
              -- `hu_tail_and_pair` says `u i` vanishes on the selected
              -- coordinates.
              exact BHW.mem_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace_of_model_tail
                (d := d) (r := r) (u := u i) hu_tail_and_pair.1,
            by
              intro i j
              calc
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q i) (q j)
                    =
                  BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear (u i) (u j) := by
                    simpa [q] using
                      BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_eq_dot_after_equiv
                        (d := d) (q i) (q j)
                _ = (A * A.transpose) i j := hu_tail_and_pair.2 i j
                _ = S i j := by
                    simpa using congrArg (fun M => M i j) hA_factor.symmŌ¤®Ō¤®
      ```

      The proof-local arithmetic helpers in this skeleton are elementary:
      `exists_pos_mul_sqrt_lt` chooses `╬┤` so `Real.sqrt (C * ╬┤)` is below
      the requested factor radius; `matrixEntryL1Bound_lt_of_entry_bound`
      converts entrywise smallness into
      `BHW.matrixEntryL1Bound m S < C * ╬┤`;
      `real_sqrt_lt_of_lt_mul_bound` applies monotonicity of `Real.sqrt`;
      and `sum_norm_lt_card_mul_of_coord_bound` is the finite sum estimate
      `Ōłæ╬Į ŌĆ¢u╬ĮŌĆ¢ < card * ╬Ą0`.  They carry no BHW route content.

      Normalized Schur-surjectivity is the block construction in
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 3.  After fixing `hrn : r <= n`, use the order
      equivalence
      `Fin n Ōēā Fin r ŌŖĢ Fin (n - r)` to write a nearby scalar matrix as
      `Z = [[A, BßĄĆ], [B, C]]`, where the canonical base is
      `[[1, 0], [0, 0]]`.  The theorem is implemented by the following
      proof-local block lemma, not by an unspecified inverse-function
      theorem:

      ```lean
      def BHW.finSourceHead {n r : Nat} (hrn : r <= n) :
          Fin r -> Fin n :=
        fun a => Ō¤©a.val, lt_of_lt_of_le a.isLt hrnŌ¤®

      def BHW.finSourceTail {n r : Nat} (hrn : r <= n) :
          Fin (n - r) -> Fin n :=
        fun u => Ō¤©r + u.val, by omegaŌ¤®

      theorem BHW.finSourceHead_tail_cases
          {n r : Nat} (hrn : r <= n)
          (i : Fin n) :
          (Ōłā a : Fin r, i = BHW.finSourceHead hrn a) Ōł©
          (Ōłā u : Fin (n - r), i = BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurBlockRealization
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          {╬Ą : ŌäØ} (h╬Ą : 0 < ╬Ą) :
          Ōłā ╬Ę : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ę Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé,
              Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              (ŌłĆ i j,
                ŌĆ¢Z i j - BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hrn i jŌĆ¢ < ╬Ę) ->
              Ōłā (P : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé)
                (L : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé)
                (q : Fin (n - r) -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
                (v : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                  (ŌłĆ a b, P a b = P b a) Ōł¦
                  (ŌłĆ a b,
                    Ōłæ c : Fin r, P a c * P b c =
                      Z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
                        (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b)) Ōł¦
                  (ŌłĆ u a,
                    Ōłæ b : Fin r, L u b *
                      Z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b)
                        (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a) =
                      Z (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)
                        (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)) Ōł¦
                  (ŌłĆ u v',
                    BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q u) (q v') =
                      Z (BHW.finSourceTail hrn u)
                        (BHW.finSourceTail hrn v') -
                      Ōłæ a : Fin r, Ōłæ b : Fin r,
                        L u a *
                          Z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
                            (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b) *
                          L v' b) Ōł¦
                  (ŌłĆ u, q u Ōłł
                    BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace d r) Ōł¦
                  (ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢v i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą) Ōł¦
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                    (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r + v) = Z
      ```

      Here `BHW.finSourceHead hrn : Fin r -> Fin n` and
      `BHW.finSourceTail hrn : Fin (n - r) -> Fin n` are the standard finite
      order embeddings selecting the first `r` and last `n-r` source
      indices.  They are proof-local indexing helpers; their only content is
      the equivalence `Fin n Ōēā Fin r ŌŖĢ Fin (n-r)`.

      Proof transcript for `hwLemma3_normalizedSchurBlockRealization`:

      1. Let `A` be the selected block of `Z` and write
         `A = 1 + B1`.  Since `Z` is `╬Ę`-close to the canonical Gram matrix,
         `B1` is small and symmetric.  Apply
         `hwLemma3_selectedBlock_sqrt_near_identity` to obtain a symmetric
         `P` close to `1` with `P * PßĄĆ = A`.  The first `r` source vectors
         are `x_a := Ōłæ b, P a b ŌĆó e_b`, where `e_b` are the first `r`
         standard orthogonal coordinates in the diagonalized complex
         Minkowski model.
      2. Because `A` is close to `1`, shrink `╬Ę` so `A.det` is a unit and
         `AŌü╗┬╣` is close to `1`.  Set
         `L u b := Ōłæ a, Z (tail u) (head a) * (AŌü╗┬╣) a b`.  Then
         `Ōłæ b, L u b * A b a = Z (tail u) (head a)`, so
         `p_u := Ōłæ b, L u b ŌĆó x_b` has the required selected cross
         scalar products.
      3. Define the residual Schur complement
         `S u v := Z (tail u) (tail v) -
           Ōłæ a b, L u a * A a b * L v b`.  By
         `hwLemma3_schurComplement_rank_bound`, using
         `Z Ōłł sourceComplexGramVariety d n` and
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le`, plus symmetry of the selected
         block `A`, `rank S <= d + 1 - r`.
         Smallness of `S` follows from smallness of `Z - Gcan`,
         `P - 1`, `AŌü╗┬╣ - 1`, and `L`.
      4. Apply
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail` to
         realize `S` by small `q_u` in the orthogonal tail.  Tail membership
         gives `<q_u, x_a> = 0`, while the theorem gives
         `<q_u, q_v> = S u v`.
      5. For a tail source index set
         `y_u := p_u + q_u`; for a head index use `x_a`.  The perturbation
         `v` is `x_a - e_a` on the head and `y_u` on the tail.  The three
         displayed identities prove the selected-selected,
         tail-selected, and tail-tail entries of
         `sourceMinkowskiGram d n (hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r + v) = Z`.
         Shrinking `╬Ę` one final time makes all coordinates of `v` smaller
         than `╬Ą`.

      Lean-shaped proof of normalized Schur-surjectivity:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurSurjective ... := by
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurBlockRealization
            (d := d) hd n r hrn hrD h╬Ą with
          Ō¤©╬Ę, h╬Ę_pos, hblockŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©╬Ę, h╬Ę_pos, by
          intro Z hZvar hZsmall
          rcases hblock Z hZvar hZsmall with
            Ō¤©P, L, q, v, hP_symm, hP_block, hL_cross,
              hq_schur, hq_tail, hv_small, hgramŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©v, hv_small, hgramŌ¤®Ō¤®
      ```

      Proof transcript for the quantitative theorem:

      1. The base used for the neighborhood argument is not an arbitrary
         extended-tube representative.  Given
         `z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n`, first apply
         `BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative` and replace
         `z0` by a same-Gram representative `╬Č0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n`
         whose source-vector span has dimension exactly
         `r := sourceGramMatrixRank n (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0)`.
         This is faithful to the first paragraph of Hall-Wightman Lemma 3,
         where the vectors representing a rank-`r` scalar matrix are chosen
         to span an `r`-dimensional manifold before the neighborhood
         construction begins.  It is also load-bearing in Lean: an invertible
         source-coordinate change cannot send nonzero radical tail vectors to
         zero, so the zero-tail canonical reduction is valid only for this
         adapted representative.  The low-rank construction of `╬Č0` uses the
         residual-frame theorem from Lemma 2 and the already documented
         `BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube`, setting
         the null residual coefficients to zero while preserving both
         extended-tube membership and the scalar Gram matrix.
      2. Let `G╬Č := sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0` and
         `r := sourceGramMatrixRank n G╬Č`.  By finite matrix rank bounds and
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le`, `r <= n` and `r <= D`; by
         adaptedness,
         `Module.finrank Ōäé (LinearMap.range
           (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Č0)) = r`.
      3. Choose a nonzero principal `r ├Ś r` minor using the checked
         `exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank`; after
         a finite permutation of labels, treat this block as the upper-left
         block.  The `r = 0` case is the same argument with the selected block
         empty and the whole nearby matrix handled by the residual realization
         theorem.
      4. Use the selected block to put the adapted base configuration in
         Hall-Wightman normal form: the first `r` source vectors have Gram
         matrix `sourceHeadMetric d r hrD`, the remaining source vectors have selected coordinates
         removed.  Since `╬Č0` is adapted, those residual tail vectors are
         actually zero: they lie both in the source span and in the radical of
         the restricted form, whose dimension is zero after adaptedness.  The
         base Gram becomes `hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hrn`.  The transformations
         used here are invertible finite-dimensional source-coordinate
         changes, congruences on symmetric Gram matrices, and one ambient
         complex Minkowski isometry, so they send neighborhoods to
         neighborhoods and preserve the rank-bounded source variety.
      5. For a nearby scalar matrix `Z = G╬Č + E`, write the normalized block
         decomposition as `[[1 + B1, BßĄĆ], [B, C]]`.  For `E` small,
         `1 + B1` is invertible and its analytic square root is supplied by
         `hwLemma3_selectedBlock_sqrt_near_identity`; this chooses small
         perturbations of the first `r` source vectors.
      6. Orthogonalize the remaining perturbations against the first `r`
         perturbed vectors exactly as in Hall-Wightman's equation (46).  The
         coefficients are `B * (1 + B1)Ōü╗┬╣`, and the inverse is close to `1`;
         hence this change and its inverse preserve the chosen smallness
         bounds after shrinking `╬Ę`.
      7. The lower-right residual is the Schur complement
         `S := C - B * (1 + B1)Ōü╗┬╣ * BßĄĆ`.  Because the full block matrix has
         rank at most `D` and the selected block has rank `r`,
         `hwLemma3_schurComplement_rank_bound` gives `rank S <= D - r`.
      8. Realize this small symmetric residual by small vectors in the
         normalized orthogonal tail using
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail`.
         This is the dimension-general form of Hall-Wightman's estimate (48),
         with `4 - r` replaced by `D - r`.  The tail membership is
         load-bearing: it ensures that the residual vectors do not change the
         selected block or the selected/residual cross scalar products after
         the orthogonalization step.
      9. Add the selected-block perturbations and residual vectors, undo the
         orthogonalization and the initial normal-form transport, and shrink
         `╬Ę` one final time so every coordinate of the resulting perturbation
         is `< ╬Ą`.

      The normal-form transport in steps 3--4 is exposed as
      `BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport`, not hidden in prose.  Its proof is
      finite-dimensional: choose the nonzero principal `r ├Ś r` minor, apply an
      invertible source-index change so the selected block is first, subtract
      the selected-span projection from every tail vector, apply
      `BHW.complexSymmetric_invertible_congruence_to_sourceHeadMetric` to normalize
      the selected Gram block to `sourceHeadMetric d r hrD`, and use Witt extension to carry the
      normalized selected source vectors to the first `r` standard orthogonal
      coordinates.  The adaptedness hypothesis proves the projected tail
      vectors are zero: their span is the radical of the restricted form on
      `LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Č0)`, and that radical
      has finrank `0` because the restricted-rank theorem identifies the
      scalar Gram rank with the span dimension.  The induced congruence on
      Gram matrices is `toNormalGram`; `gram_commute` is the direct bilinear
      calculation from
      `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_sourceTupleLinearChange`, and
      `gram_variety_iff` follows from
      `BHW.sourceGramCongruence_mem_variety_iff`.  The oriented transport is
      built at the same time: source-index changes act on full-frame
      determinant coordinates by the exterior-power matrix of ordered
      `(d+1)`-source frames, and the ambient `ComplexLorentzGroup d` factor
      has determinant `1`, so it preserves those determinant coordinates.
      The two estimate fields are finite-dimensional continuity/norm
      equivalence for the fixed linear equivalences.

      The oriented-transport construction is the following finite matrix
      theorem.  It is deliberately tied to the source matrix used by the
      normal-form producer and lives on the oriented variety subtype; it is
      not a theorem about an arbitrary linear equivalence of the source-vector
      Pi space and not an ambient determinant-coordinate homeomorphism.

      ```lean
      noncomputable def
        BHW.sourceOrientedVarietySourceMatrixTransportEquivOfMatrix
          (d n : Nat)
          {M : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) Ōäé}
          (hM : IsUnit M.det) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n
      ```

      Corrected proof transcript: the function-indexed determinant transform
      is the checked row-multilinearity/Cauchy-Binet formula on actual
      determinant coordinates, not a standalone linear equivalence of the full
      ordered-embedding coordinate space.  The checked theorem is now
      `BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_sourceTupleLinearChange`, proved by applying
      `Matrix.detRowAlternating.toMultilinearMap.map_sum` to expand rows,
      `map_smul_univ` to extract the coefficient product, and
      `AlternatingMap.map_eq_zero_of_not_injective` to kill non-injective
      functions.  Consequently
      `BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_sourceTupleLinearChange` and
      `BHW.sourceOrientedGramDataSourceMatrixTransform_mem_variety` are
      checked, and invertible source matrices act by the checked subtype
      equivalence
      `BHW.sourceOrientedGramVarietySourceMatrixEquivOfMatrix`.

      The previous ambient-homeomorphism shortcut is not allowed: the
      transform
      `sourceFullFrameDetSourceMatrixTransform d n M` has `T_1 = id` on all
      ordered-coordinate functions and is correct on actual alternating
      determinant coordinates, but it is not functorial/invertible on arbitrary
      independent ordered-frame coordinates.  Therefore the old target
      `sourceOrientedInvariantTransportEquiv_of_sourceChange_lorentz` is no
      longer the route target for source changes.  The chosen route is the
      checked variety-relative interface
      `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv`, with source matrices supplied
      by `BHW.sourceOrientedVarietySourceMatrixTransportEquivOfMatrix`.  This
      has a subtype homeomorphism, inverse map, and max-rank preservation, all
      proved from actual tuple-level inverse source changes and Gram-rank
      congruence.  Downstream normal-form local-image code must consume
      relative open sets and connected max-rank slices on the oriented variety,
      not arbitrary ambient determinant-coordinate extensions.  The complex
      Lorentz factor remains harmless:
      `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_complexLorentzAction ╬ø` leaves both
      Gram and determinant coordinates unchanged because `╬ø` is determinant
      `1`.

      Implementation transcript for
      `BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormTransportData`:

      1. Let `G0 := sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0` and
         `r := sourceGramMatrixRank n G0`.  The adaptedness hypothesis says
         `finrank (range (sourceCoefficientEval d n z0)) = r`.
      2. Choose an injective principal block
         `I : Fin r -> Fin n` with nonzero determinant by
         `exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank`.
         Use `exists_sourcePermutation_movingPrincipalBlockToHead` to obtain a
         source permutation `Žā` moving that block to the first `r` source
         labels.  Its matrix is invertible, and its Gram action is
         `sourcePermuteComplexGram`.
      3. In the permuted coordinates write the base Gram matrix as
         `sourceBlockMatrix n r hrn A B C`, where `A` is the selected
         `r ├Ś r` block, `B` is the tail-head block, and `C` is the tail-tail
         block.  The selected determinant is a unit.  Since the full Gram rank
         is `r`, the Schur complement satisfies
         `C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose = 0`.
      4. Apply `hwLemma3_projectionSourceChangeMatrix n r hrn A B`.  This is
         the lower triangular source-coordinate matrix with identity on the
         selected block and `-B * AŌü╗┬╣` in the tail-selected block.  Its
         determinant is a unit, and its congruence sends the base Gram to
         `[[A,0],[0,0]]`.
      5. Apply `complexSymmetric_invertible_congruence_to_sourceHeadMetric`
         to `A`, obtaining `P` with
         `P * A * PßĄĆ = sourceHeadMetric d r hrD`.  Extend `P` by the identity on
         the tail source coordinates and compose with the previous source
         changes.  The resulting invertible source matrix `M` satisfies
         `sourceGramCongruence n M G0 =
         hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hr_le_n`.
      6. On vectors, `sourceTupleLinearChange d n M z0` has canonical Gram.
         The checked theorem
         `hwLemma3_canonicalGram_tail_zero_of_adapted` proves that any
         canonical-Gram tuple whose coefficient-span dimension equals its
         scalar Gram rank has zero tail: adaptedness makes the restricted
         Minkowski form on the source span nondegenerate, and each canonical
         tail vector is orthogonal to the whole source span.  The source-change
         wrapper `hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChange_tail_zero_of_adapted` is also
         checked: it uses
         `sourceCoefficientEval_range_sourceTupleLinearChange_eq` and
         `sourceGramMatrixRank_sourceGramCongruence` to preserve adaptedness
         under the invertible source matrix `M`, then applies the canonical-tail
         theorem.  The first `r` vectors have Gram matrix
         `sourceHeadMetric d r hrD`.
      7. Use the checked
         `hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChange_exists_complexLorentz_to_canonicalSource_of_adapted`
         to obtain `╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d` carrying
         `sourceTupleLinearChange d n M z0` to the standard canonical source
         frame.  Internally it applies
         `complexMinkowski_detOneWittExtension_to_canonicalHeadFrame` to the
         canonical head frame and the checked adapted tail-zero theorem to the
         tail.  Define
         `toNormalVec z := complexLorentzAction ╬ø
           (sourceTupleLinearChange d n M z)`.
      8. Define `toNormalGram` as
         `sourceGramCongruenceLinearEquivOfMatrix n M hM_unit`.  The field
         `gram_commute` is
         `sourceMinkowskiGram_sourceTupleLinearChange` followed by Lorentz
         invariance of `sourceMinkowskiGram`; `gram_variety_iff` is
         `sourceGramCongruence_mem_variety_iff`; `gram_z0` is step 5.
      9. Construct `sourceMatrixVarietyTransport` by
         `sourceOrientedVarietySourceMatrixTransportEquivOfMatrix` applied to
         the same `M` and `hM_unit`.  The ambient Lorentz factor contributes no
         determinant-coordinate transport because
         `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_complexLorentzAction` leaves the
         oriented invariant unchanged.  The commute fields stored on
         `HWLemma3NormalFormTransport` are subtype equalities: their underlying
         values are
         `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_sourceTupleLinearChange` followed
         by Lorentz invariance, and the proofs that both sides lie in the
         variety are the canonical range witnesses `Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®`.
      10. The estimate fields are now purely finite-dimensional:
         `gram_ball_to_normal_ball` is
         `linearEquiv_coord_ball_preimage T.toNormalGram`, where
         `toNormalGram` is built from
         `sourceGramCongruenceLinearEquivOfMatrix n M hM_unit`, and
         `perturb_back_small` is
         `linearEquiv_perturb_back_coord_bound T.toNormalVec`, after rewriting
         the target equation by `z0_to_canonical`.

      Lean-shaped producer skeleton:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormTransportData ... := by
        let G0 := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0
        have hr_le_n : r <= n := by
          rw [hr]
          exact BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_le_arity (n := n) G0
        have hr_lt_D : r < d + 1 := hrD
        have hG0sym : G0 Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n := by
          intro i j
          exact BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_symm d n z0 i j
        rcases BHW.exists_sourcePrincipalMinor_ne_zero_of_sourceSymmetricRank
            hG0sym
            (by simpa [G0] using hr.symm) with
          Ō¤©I, hI_inj, hminorŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.exists_sourcePermutation_movingPrincipalBlockToHead
            n r hr_le_n hI_inj with
          Ō¤©Žā, hŽā_headŌ¤®
        let Gp := BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žā G0
        let A : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) Ōäé :=
          BHW.sourceHeadHeadBlock n r hr_le_n Gp
        let B : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) Ōäé :=
          BHW.sourceTailHeadBlock n r hr_le_n Gp
        let C : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin (n - r)) Ōäé :=
          BHW.sourceTailTailBlock n r hr_le_n Gp
        have hGpsym : Gp Ōłł BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n := by
          simpa [Gp] using
            BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_mem_sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace
              (n := n) Žā hG0sym
        have hBlock :
            Gp = BHW.sourceBlockMatrix n r hr_le_n A B C := by
          simpa [A, B, C] using
            (BHW.sourceBlockMatrix_of_headTailBlocks n r hr_le_n Gp hGpsym).symm
        have hGp_rank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Gp = r := by
          rw [Gp, BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_sourcePermuteComplexGram]
          exact hr.symm
        have hA_symm : A.transpose = A :=
          BHW.sourceHeadHeadBlock_symm_of_sourceSymmetric
            n r hr_le_n hGpsym
        have hA_unit : IsUnit A.det := by
          simpa [A, Gp, hŽā_head] using hminor
        have hSchur : C - B * AŌü╗┬╣ * B.transpose = 0 :=
          BHW.hwLemma3_schurComplement_eq_zero_of_rank_eq
            (n := n) (r := r) hr_le_n hGpsym hBlock
            hGp_rank hA_unit
        rcases BHW.complexSymmetric_invertible_congruence_to_sourceHeadMetric
            (d := d) (r := r) (hrD := hrD) (A := A)
            hA_symm
            hA_unit with
          Ō¤©P, hP_unit, hP_congrŌ¤®
        let M :=
          BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix
            n r hr_le_n Žā A B P
        have hM_unit : IsUnit M.det :=
          BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix_det_isUnit
            n r hr_le_n Žā hA_unit hP_unit
        have hM_canon :
            BHW.sourceGramCongruence n M G0 =
              BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hr_le_n := by
          simpa [M] using
            BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChangeMatrix_canonicalGram
              d n r hrD hr_le_n (G := G0) (Žā := Žā)
              (A := A) (B := B) (C := C) (P := P)
              (by simpa [Gp] using hBlock)
              hA_unit hA_symm hSchur hP_congr
        let y := BHW.sourceTupleLinearChange d n M z0
        have hy_gram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y =
              BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hr_le_n := by
          calc
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y =
                BHW.sourceGramCongruence n M G0 := by
              simpa [y, G0] using
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_sourceTupleLinearChange
                  d n M z0
            _ = BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r hrD hr_le_n := hM_canon
        rcases
          BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormSourceChange_exists_complexLorentz_to_canonicalSource_of_adapted
            d n r hrD hr_le_n
            (Žā := Žā) (A := A) (B := B) (P := P)
            hA_unit hP_unit
            (z := z0)
            (by simpa [M, G0] using hM_canon)
            (by simpa [G0] using hspan) with
          Ō¤©╬ø, h╬øyŌ¤®
        let Lvec : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
            (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          BHW.sourceTupleLinearEquivOfMatrix d n M hM_unit |>.trans
            (BHW.complexLorentzActionLinearEquiv d n ╬ø)
        let Lgram : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) ŌēāŌéŚ[Ōäé]
            (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :=
          BHW.sourceGramCongruenceLinearEquivOfMatrix n M hM_unit
        let Qsource :
            BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedVarietySourceMatrixTransportEquivOfMatrix
            d n M hM_unit
        refine Ō¤©{
          toNormalVec := Lvec
          toNormalGram := Lgram
          z0_to_canonical := by simpa [Lvec, y] using h╬øy
          gram_commute := by
            intro z
            simp [Lvec, Lgram,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_sourceTupleLinearChange,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction]
          gram_variety_iff := by
            intro Z
            simpa [Lgram] using
              BHW.sourceGramCongruence_mem_variety_iff
                (d := d) (n := n) hM_unit Z
          sourceMatrixVarietyTransport := Qsource
          oriented_commute := by
            intro z
            -- Underlying equality is
            -- `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_sourceTupleLinearChange`
            -- plus `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_complexLorentzAction`.
            ext <;> simp [Qsource, Lvec,
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_sourceTupleLinearChange,
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_complexLorentzAction]
          oriented_symm_commute := by
            intro z
            -- Same calculation for the inverse source matrix and
            -- `╬øŌü╗┬╣`; the subtype proof witnesses are range witnesses.
            ext <;> simp [Qsource, Lvec,
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_sourceTupleLinearChange,
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_complexLorentzAction]
          gram_z0 := by simpa [Lgram] using hM_canon
          gram_ball_to_normal_ball := by
            intro ╬ĘN h╬ĘN
            rcases BHW.linearEquiv_coord_ball_preimage Lgram G0 h╬ĘN with
              Ō¤©╬Ę, h╬Ę, hsmallŌ¤®
            exact Ō¤©╬Ę, h╬Ę, by
              intro Z hZ
              simpa [Lgram, hM_canon] using hsmall Z hZŌ¤®
          perturb_back_small := by
            intro ╬Ą h╬Ą
            rcases BHW.linearEquiv_perturb_back_coord_bound Lvec h╬Ą with
              Ō¤©╬ĄN, h╬ĄN, hboundŌ¤®
            exact Ō¤©╬ĄN, h╬ĄN, by
              intro vN hvN
              exact Ō¤©Lvec.symm vN, hbound vN hvN, by
                calc
                  Lvec (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + Lvec.symm vN i ╬╝) =
                      Lvec z0 + vN := by
                    ext i ╬╝
                    simp
                  _ =
                      fun i ╬╝ =>
                        BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r i ╬╝ + vN i ╬╝ := by
                    ext i ╬╝
                    simpa [Lvec, y] using congrFun (congrFun h╬øy i) ╬╝Ō¤®Ō¤® }, trivialŌ¤®
      ```

      Lean-shaped transport proof of
      `BHW.hwLemma3_transport_from_normalForm`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_transport_from_normalForm ... := by
        let G0 := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0
        let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G0
        have hr : r = BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G0 := rfl
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormTransportData
            (d := d) hd n (z0 := z0) r hr hrD hspan with
          Ō¤©T, _Ō¤®
        rcases T.perturb_back_small h╬Ą with
          Ō¤©╬ĄN, h╬ĄN_pos, hbackŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_normalizedSchurSurjective
            (d := d) hd n r T.rank_le_arity T.rank_lt_spacetime h╬ĄN_pos with
          Ō¤©╬ĘN, h╬ĘN_pos, hnormŌ¤®
        rcases T.gram_ball_to_normal_ball h╬ĘN_pos with
          Ō¤©╬Ę, h╬Ę_pos, h╬Ę_to_normalŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©╬Ę, h╬Ę_pos, by
          intro Z hZvar hZsmall
          have hZnorm_var :
              T.toNormalGram Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
            (T.gram_variety_iff Z).2 hZvar
          have hZnorm_small :
              ŌłĆ i j,
                ŌĆ¢T.toNormalGram Z i j -
                  BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalGram d n r
                    T.rank_lt_spacetime T.rank_le_arity i jŌĆ¢ < ╬ĘN :=
            h╬Ę_to_normal Z hZsmall
          rcases hnorm (T.toNormalGram Z) hZnorm_var hZnorm_small with
            Ō¤©vN, hvN_small, hgramNŌ¤®
          rcases hback vN hvN_small with Ō¤©v, hv_small, hvec_backŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©v, hv_small, by
            apply T.toNormalGram.injective
            calc
              T.toNormalGram
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                    (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝))
                  =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (T.toNormalVec (fun i ╬╝ => z0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝)) := by
                    rw [T.gram_commute]
                    rfl
              _ =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (fun i ╬╝ =>
                    BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r i ╬╝ + vN i ╬╝) := by
                    rw [hvec_back]
              _ = T.toNormalGram Z := hgramN.symmŌ¤®Ō¤®
      ```

      The adapted small-perturbation Lemma-3 theorem has one additional
      topological shrink that must be explicit in Lean.  Its perturbation
      version is stated only for the adapted representative `╬Č0`, not for an
      arbitrary representative of the same scalar point.  Since
      `BHW.ExtendedTube d n` is open and `h╬Č0 : ╬Č0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n`,
      `BHW.exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen` gives an
      `╬ĄET > 0` such that every coordinate perturbation of `╬Č0` smaller than
      `╬ĄET`
      stays inside the extended tube.  Apply
      `BHW.hwLemma3_transport_from_normalForm` with
      `╬Ą' := min ╬Ą ╬ĄET / 2`, then use
      `BHW.sourceGramCoordBall` as the scalar neighborhood.  This is the only
      place where extended-tube membership is recovered from the quantitative
      normal-form algebra.

      Lean-shaped final quantitative assembly:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_adaptedBase_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube
          ... := by
        rcases BHW.exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen
            (x0 := ╬Č0)
            (U := BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
            (BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)) h╬Č0 with
          Ō¤©╬ĄET, h╬ĄET_pos, hET_ballŌ¤®
        let ╬Ą' : ŌäØ := min ╬Ą ╬ĄET / 2
        have h╬Ą'_pos : 0 < ╬Ą' := by
          positivity
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_transport_from_normalForm
            (d := d) hd n (z0 := ╬Č0) hspan h╬Ą'_pos with
          Ō¤©╬Ę, h╬Ę_pos, hrealizeŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©╬Ę, h╬Ę_pos, by
          intro Z hZvar hZball
          rcases hrealize Z hZvar hZball with Ō¤©v, hv_small, hgramŌ¤®
          have hv_target : ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢v i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬Ą := by
            intro i ╬╝
            exact lt_of_lt_of_le (hv_small i ╬╝) (by
              dsimp [╬Ą']; nlinarith [min_le_left ╬Ą ╬ĄET])
          have hv_ET : ŌłĆ i ╬╝, ŌĆ¢v i ╬╝ŌĆ¢ < ╬ĄET := by
            intro i ╬╝
            exact lt_of_lt_of_le (hv_small i ╬╝) (by
              dsimp [╬Ą']; nlinarith [min_le_right ╬Ą ╬ĄET])
          exact Ō¤©v, hv_target, hET_ball v hv_ET, hgramŌ¤®Ō¤®

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
          ... := by
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative
            (d := d) hd n hz0 with
          Ō¤©╬Č0, h╬Č0, h╬Č0Gram, h╬Č0spanŌ¤®
        let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
          (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0)
        have hspan :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Č0)) = r := by
          simpa [r, h╬Č0Gram] using h╬Č0span
        have h╬Ą : 0 < (1 : ŌäØ) := by norm_num
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_adaptedBase_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube
            (d := d) hd n h╬Č0 hspan h╬Ą with
          Ō¤©╬Ę, h╬Ę_pos, hrealizeŌ¤®
        let O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :=
          BHW.sourceGramCoordBall n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0) ╬Ę
        exact Ō¤©O, BHW.isOpen_sourceGramCoordBall n _ h╬Ę_pos,
          by
            intro i j
            simp [O, BHW.sourceGramCoordBall, h╬Č0Gram]
            exact h╬Ę_pos,
          by
            intro Z hZ
            rcases hrealize Z hZ.2 hZ.1 with
              Ō¤©v, _hv_small, hzeta_v, hgramŌ¤®
            exact Ō¤©fun i ╬╝ => ╬Č0 i ╬╝ + v i ╬╝, hzeta_v, hgramŌ¤®Ō¤®
      ```

      The orbit-rank and low-rank wrappers are only organizational views of
      this same Lemma-3 theorem, matching Hall-Wightman's discussion after
      Lemma 2.  The low-rank branch is the delicate Schur-complement/residual
      realization above; it is not a source-map regularity theorem.  Existing
      local rank-exact Schur charts may be used only inside this realization
      theorem after the ordinary extended-tube preimage has been produced;
      they cannot replace Lemma 3.

      Lemma-3 production-readiness ledger.  This is the scalar-domain
      companion to the Lemma-2 ledger: `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen`
      may move to Lean only after the finite-dimensional local-realization
      rows below are proved or replaced by sorry-free local support with the
      same content.

      | Lemma-3 subpacket | Current status | Implementation-level proof content |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Definitional subset and connectedness of `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain` | Mechanically ready with checked support. | Use the image definition of `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`, the range definition of `sourceComplexGramVariety`, `BHW.isConnected_extendedTube`, and `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram`. |
      | Pointwise-to-global relative-open assembly | Mechanically ready after local realization. | Choose explicit neighborhoods from `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_at`, form the subtype-indexed union, and use `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_subset_sourceComplexGramVariety`; no Hall-Wightman geometry occurs here. |
      | Adapted same-Gram representative `hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative` | Checked in `SourceHWAdaptedTubeRepresentative.lean`. | Assembles the checked residual-frame/all-coefficients extended-tube theorem, selected projection/selected-orthogonality support, Schur-zero residual theorem, and span-rank equality; the proof explicitly handles `n = 0` and forbids source-changing an arbitrary representative to zero tail. |
      | Principal block, projection, Schur-zero residual algebra, and residual-frame data | Checked in `SourceHWSelectedProjection.lean`. | The checked layer defines the principal block, selected coefficients, projection, and residual; proves selected rows are fixed, residuals are orthogonal to selected vectors and selected projections, complementary residual pairings are the reindexed rectangular Schur complement, rank-`r` residual pairings vanish over all original labels, original vectors are orthogonal to residuals, the selected projection preserves the full scalar Gram matrix, the projected source span has finrank `r`, every vector in a finite-frame span has explicit coordinates, and the selected residuals admit a finite linearly independent totally isotropic coordinate frame orthogonal to the selected projection tuple.  The checked theorem names are `hwLemma3_selectedResidual_selected`, `hwLemma3_selectedProjection_inner_residual`, `hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_projection`, `hwLemma3_selectedComplement_residual_inner_residual_eq_schur`, `hwLemma3_selectedResidual_inner_residual_eq_zero`, `hwLemma3_selectedProjection_gram_eq`, `hwLemma3_selectedProjection_span_finrank_eq_rank`, `exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame`, and `hwLemma3_selectedResidual_isotropicFrameData`. |
      | Normal-form source transport | Source-level normal-form data with actual `toOriginal` is checked, together with the core finite source-change, head-normalization, adaptedness preservation, tail-zero, selected-head Witt/Lorentz orbit layer, variety-relative source-matrix transport, sliced Schur-window/local-image assembly, finite-dimensional IFT producer, hard-range connectedness consumer, small-arity branch, and finite-overlap constructors checked across the `Source*` BHWPermutation files. | The checked layer now includes `hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative`, `SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.ofSourceMatrixLorentz`, `SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.exists_ofAdaptedBase`, `SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.exists_ofExtendedTube`, `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_tubeResidualPolydisc_hardRange`, `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_tubeResidualPolydisc_smallArity`, `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_tubeResidualPolydisc`, `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_tubeResidualPolydiscProducer`, and `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChartProducer`.  It builds the adapted ET representative, rank-deficient same-oriented-invariant conversion, actual continuous `toOriginal`, explicit original-coordinate residual-polydisc data, Schur image-surjectivity, and max-rank density without using an ambient determinant-coordinate transport.  This local-realization input is no longer a blocker; the next theorem-2 blocker is downstream source-oriented scalar representative/descent and the OS45 direct source-patch carrier, not an unproved residual-chart wrapper. |
      | Near-identity selected-block square root | Proof transcript pinned; pure matrix analysis. | Define the finite matrix binomial series for `(1 + B)^(1/2)`, prove convergence via a scalar power-series majorant, transpose compatibility, square identity, and entrywise smallness via `matrix_opNorm_le_card_mul_sup_entry`. |
      | Schur-rank bound and Takagi residual factorization | Proof transcript pinned; pure finite linear algebra. | Prove block Gaussian rank equality, Autonne-Takagi with rank support and explicit entry-L1 control, small factorization, and tail embedding with coordinate estimates. |
      | Orthogonal-tail residual realization | Proof transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | Transport the Takagi factors into `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalTailSubspace`, prove they pair to the Schur complement and are orthogonal to the selected block, then transport back through the Minkowski orthogonal model with norm control. |
      | Normalized Schur realization and transport back | Canonical normal-form transport-to-center, subtype-valued normal-image transport, and invertible-head principal-Schur equality are checked; production local-image producer not started. | Assemble selected vectors, cross coefficients, residual tail vectors, prove the oriented Gram/determinant equality with `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur`, identify the normal point with `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVarietyPoint`, rewrite the invertible-head box by `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVarietyPoint_eq_sourcePrincipalSchurGraph`, and transport it back as `N.originalNormalVarietyPoint` through the checked `SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv`. |
