      | Extended-tube shrink and arbitrary-neighborhood conversion | Proof transcript pinned; topology helper standard. | `exists_coord_supnorm_ball_subset_of_isOpen` is used with openness of `ExtendedTube`; `hwLemma3_adaptedBase_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube` combines the shrink with the transported realization. |
      | Existing Schur-chart/local-connectedness infrastructure | Checked local support only. | `sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis*` and `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch` may help path/corridor arguments and chart bookkeeping, but they do not prove Lemma-3 relative openness of the extended-tube Gram image. |

      After the theorem-2 proof-doc gate is fully closed, the first possible
      production Lean target in the Lemma-3 block would be one of the
      finite-dimensional support rows above, not
      `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen`,
      `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`, or the scalar
      representative constructor.  This is a future implementation-order note,
      not permission to start production Lean while the scalar-source decision
      remains open.

      The pointwise relative-open theorem is now mechanical:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_at ... := by
        rcases hZ with Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
            (d := d) hd n hz0 with
          Ō¤©O, hO_open, hZO, hO_sub_imageŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©O, hO_open, hZO, by
          intro W hW
          exact hO_sub_image hWŌ¤®
      ```

      Scratch-checked relative-open assembly.  The explicit `hLocal`
      wrapper is important: choosing directly from the theorem whose scalar
      point is an implicit argument gives `O` indexed by the proof argument
      alone, which makes the subtype-indexed union awkward.  Making the
      scalar point explicit gives the intended `O Z hZ` family.

      ```lean
      -- Requires importing
      -- `OSReconstruction.ComplexLieGroups.Connectedness.BHWPermutation.SourceExtension`
      -- for the scalar-domain definitions.
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen ... := by
        classical
        have hLocal :
            ŌłĆ Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé,
              Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
                Ōłā O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
                  IsOpen O Ōł¦ Z Ōłł O Ōł¦
                  O Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
                    BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
          intro Z hZ
          exact BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_at
            (d := d) hd (n := n) hZ
        choose O hO_open hZO hO_sub using hLocal
        refine Ō¤©Ōŗā Z : {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé //
            Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n}, O Z.1 Z.2,
          isOpen_iUnion (fun Z => hO_open Z.1 Z.2), by
            ext W
            constructor
            ┬Ę intro hWdomain
              constructor
              ┬Ę exact Set.mem_iUnion.mpr
                  Ō¤©Ō¤©W, hWdomainŌ¤®, hZO W hWdomainŌ¤®
              ┬Ę exact
                BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_subset_sourceComplexGramVariety
                  (d := d) (n := n) hWdomain
            ┬Ę intro hW
              rcases hW with Ō¤©hWU0, hWvarŌ¤®
              rcases Set.mem_iUnion.mp hWU0 with Ō¤©Z, hWOŌ¤®
              exact hO_sub Z.1 Z.2 Ō¤©hWO, hWvarŌ¤®Ō¤®
      ```

      Finally, the descent theorem must be sourced from the analytic part of
      Hall-Wightman Theorem I:

      ```lean
      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7 in the exact active form: the
      single-valued scalar function on `S'_n` becomes a holomorphic
      analytic-space germ on the source Gram variety.  The output is the germ
      predicate; no Stein-neighborhood or total ambient extension is used. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w) :
          Ōłā Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
            BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = BHW.extendF F w)
      ```

      The descent theorem is itself split into the local Hall-Wightman chart
      theorem and the gluing/removable-singularity theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hZU : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :
          Ōłā U0 ╬©,
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
            U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł U0 ->
                ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)

      /-- Optional proof-local compatible scalar-product germ atlas produced by
      Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7.  If used in Lean, this packet should be
      `private` or inlined inside
      `sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent`; it is not an additional
      public source theorem surface.  The `overlap_eq` field is equality of
      analytic germs on the scalar Gram variety.  It is deliberately not full
      ambient-open equality: arbitrary ambient representatives of the same
      analytic-space germ can differ by functions vanishing on the variety. -/
      structure BHW.HallWightmanScalarGermAtlas
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé) where
        U0 :
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) ->
            Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
        ╬© :
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) ->
            Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé
        U0_open :
          ŌłĆ Z hZ, IsOpen (U0 Z hZ)
        center_mem :
          ŌłĆ Z hZ, Z Ōłł U0 Z hZ
        ╬©_diff :
          ŌłĆ Z hZ, DifferentiableOn Ōäé (╬© Z hZ) (U0 Z hZ)
        U0_sub :
          ŌłĆ Z hZ,
            U0 Z hZ Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n
        branch_eq :
          ŌłĆ Z hZ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł U0 Z hZ ->
              ╬© Z hZ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                BHW.extendF F w
        overlap_eq :
          ŌłĆ Z hZ W hW,
            Set.EqOn (╬© Z hZ) (╬© W hW)
              ((U0 Z hZ Ōł® U0 W hW) Ōł®
                BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Compatibility of two Hall-Wightman scalar charts is not an extra
      off-variety uniqueness theorem.  Once each chart's variety slice is
      contained in `S'_n` and each chart agrees with `extendF F` on every
      realized extended-tube point in that slice, equality on the overlap of
      the source Gram variety follows by choosing an ordinary extended-tube
      realization of the overlap scalar point. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_overlap_eq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          {U V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          {╬© ╬¦ : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hU_sub :
            U Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hV_sub :
            V Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (h╬©_branch :
            ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł U ->
                ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)
          (h╬¦_branch :
            ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł V ->
                ╬¦ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w) :
          Set.EqOn ╬© ╬¦
            ((U Ōł® V) Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      Scratch-checked proof of the overlap helper.  The symmetric
      `hV_sub` hypothesis is kept in the public theorem statement because
      both charts are required to have domain control, but the proof only
      needs one side to realize the scalar point as an extended-tube Gram
      point; the other side is used through its branch equality.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_overlap_eq ... := by
        intro G hG
        have hG_Uvar : G Ōłł U Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
          Ō¤©hG.1.1, hG.2Ō¤®
        have hG_domain : G Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n :=
          hU_sub hG_Uvar
        rcases hG_domain with Ō¤©w, hw, rflŌ¤®
        exact
          (h╬©_branch w hw hG.1.1).trans
            (h╬¦_branch w hw hG.1.2).symm
      ```

      /-- Optional proof-local sheaf-style gluing on the source Gram analytic
      subspace.  This formalizes choice-independence of compatible chart
      representatives, but the production route should not expose it as a
      second public descent theorem: the direct descent proof below uses the
      branch-defined `Phi` from
      `hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded` and proves the germ
      predicate for that same `Phi`. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_scalarGermAtlas_glue
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanScalarGermAtlas hd n F) :
          Ōłā phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
            (ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
                  BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = BHW.extendF F w)
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of `BHW.hallWightman_scalarGermAtlas_glue`: let
      `I := ╬Ż Z, Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n`, set
      `U i := A.U0 i.1 i.2` and `╬©i i := A.╬© i.1 i.2`, and let
      `╬®V := Ōŗā i : I, U i Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n`.  Define
      `phi W := ╬©i (Classical.choose hW) W` if `hW : W Ōłł ╬®V`, and `0`
      otherwise.  If
      `W Ōłł (U i Ōł® U j) Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, then
      `A.overlap_eq i.1 i.2 j.1 j.2` proves `╬©i i W = ╬©i j W`, so `phi` is
      choice-independent on the scalar variety and
      `Set.EqOn phi (╬©i i) (U i Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)`.  The
      local fields `A.U0_open`, `A.center_mem`, `A.╬©_diff`, and `A.U0_sub`
      give the analytic-space local representative clause.  The branch
      equality is `A.branch_eq`
      applied to the index
      `Ō¤©BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w, Ō¤©w, hw, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®`.

      `BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_at` is Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7 in
      local form.  It must not reuse the Lemma-2 orbit/low split.  The
      power-series chart is at non-exceptional scalar points, i.e. maximal
      possible scalar rank `min (d + 1) n`; the exceptional lower-rank stratum
      uses boundedness/continuity plus the removable-singularity theorem on
      the scalar-product variety.  The overlap
      equality for two local scalar charts is not an extra assumption: on any
      common scalar point realized by extended-tube configurations, `hBranch`
      equates the two values; analytic continuation on the connected
      relatively open chart overlap extends equality as a germ on the scalar
      Gram variety.  A private gluing theorem can define a scalar-space
      function by these compatible variety germs, but the active public
      descent theorem must use the already branch-defined `Phi` from
      `hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded`.  It must not
      introduce a second scalar value function and then silently identify it
      with the branch-law choice.

      The API-critical point is now separated cleanly.  Hall-Wightman gives
      equality of scalar-product germs on `S'_n`, i.e. equality on the source
      Gram variety.  That does not imply equality of arbitrary ambient
      extensions on full open overlaps.  Therefore the active production API
      must consume `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn`; keeping the old
      `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` field would require the archived
      Cartan/Oka bridge and an independent Stein-domain theorem.

      If the implementation uses the optional atlas as local proof
      organization, its construction is the following private Hall-Wightman
      chart-packet producer:

      ```lean
      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7 plus the scalar-product power-series
      overlap uniqueness on the scalar Gram analytic subspace. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.hallWightman_consistentScalarGermAtlas
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w) :
          BHW.HallWightmanScalarGermAtlas hd n F
      ```

      Proof transcript for the private
      `BHW.hallWightman_consistentScalarGermAtlas`:

      1. For each `Z Ōłł sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n`, split by
         `BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z`.  In the max-rank case, use
         `BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at`; in the exceptional
         case, use
         `BHW.hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at`, whose proof
         is the removable-singularity construction below.  These choices fill
         `U0`, `╬©`, `U0_open`, `center_mem`, `╬©_diff`, `U0_sub`, and
         `branch_eq`.
      2. Prove `overlap_eq` uniformly with
         `BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_overlap_eq`.  Given a scalar point
         in `(U0 Z hZ Ōł® U0 W hW) Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, the first
         chart's `U0_sub` field realizes it as `sourceMinkowskiGram d n w`
         for some `w Ōłł ExtendedTube d n`; the two `branch_eq` fields then
         identify both chart values with `extendF F w`.  This is equality on
         the source Gram variety only, exactly the germ-overlap field.
      3. If the private glue theorem is used, it is purely formal once
         variety-germ `overlap_eq` is a field of the atlas; it may not re-prove
         or assume equality of arbitrary ambient extensions.  The public
         descent theorem still returns the branch-defined `Phi`, not an
         unrelated glued choice.

      Lean-facing local-chart split:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_localScalarChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hZU : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z) :
          Ōłā U0 ╬©,
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
            U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł U0 ->
                ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hZU : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z) :
          Ōłā U0 ╬©,
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
            U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł U0 ->
                ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_at ... := by
        by_cases hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z
        ┬Ę exact BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_localScalarChart_at
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hZU hRank
        ┬Ę exact BHW.hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hZU
            ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
              (d := d) (n := n)).2 hRank)
      ```

      The max-rank chart is proved from the Hall-Wightman invariant
      power-series theorem near a non-exceptional scalar point.  The
      exceptional-rank chart is not a formal gluing wrapper: it is the
      theorem's removable singularity step, using boundedness/continuity from
      Lemmas 1--7 and the analytic structure of the exceptional locus in the
      scalar-product variety.  Existing repo rank-exact identity-principle
      lemmas may help with the final analytic continuation on relatively open
      scalar charts, but they do not replace the Hall-Wightman bounded
      extension at the exceptional locus.

      The proof of the two chart lemmas is itself part of the source route.
      It must be decomposed as follows; otherwise
      `hallWightman_localScalarChart_at` is still only a name for the missing
      Hall-Wightman theorem.

      The predicates `BHW.LocallyBoundedOn` and
      `BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn` are declared in the active
      standard-geometry block above, before they are consumed by
      `sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension`; the chart packet below only
      adds the continuity and PDE support lemmas used to prove those inputs.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.continuousOn_of_open_nhdsWithin_control
          {X Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace Y]
          {f : X -> Y} {U : Set X}
          (h :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł U ->
              ŌłĆ T : Set Y, IsOpen T -> f x Ōłł T ->
                Ōłā O : Set X, IsOpen O Ōł¦ x Ōłł O Ōł¦
                  ŌłĆ y, y Ōłł O -> y Ōłł U -> f y Ōłł T) :
          ContinuousOn f U

      theorem BHW.continuousOn_openDomain_preimage_nhds
          {X Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace Y]
          {f : X -> Y} {╬® : Set X}
          (hf : ContinuousOn f ╬®)
          {x : X} (hx╬® : x Ōłł ╬®)
          {T : Set Y} (hT_open : IsOpen T)
          (hxT : f x Ōłł T) :
          Ōłā V : Set X, IsOpen V Ōł¦ x Ōłł V Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ y, y Ōłł ╬® -> y Ōłł V -> f y Ōłł T

      -- Scratch-checked proof body for
      -- `continuousOn_of_open_nhdsWithin_control`.
      theorem BHW.continuousOn_of_open_nhdsWithin_control ... := by
        rw [continuousOn_iff]
        intro x hx T hT_open hxT
        rcases h x hx T hT_open hxT with
          Ō¤©O, hO_open, hxO, hO_controlŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©O, hO_open, hxO, by
          intro y hy
          exact hO_control y hy.1 hy.2Ō¤®

      -- Scratch-checked proof body for
      -- `continuousOn_openDomain_preimage_nhds`.
      theorem BHW.continuousOn_openDomain_preimage_nhds ... := by
        rw [continuousOn_iff] at hf
        rcases hf x hx╬® T hT_open hxT with
          Ō¤©V, hV_open, hxV, hV_subŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©V, hV_open, hxV, by
          intro y hy╬® hyV
          exact hV_sub Ō¤©hyV, hy╬®Ō¤®Ō¤®

      /-- Lean-facing structure for the concrete finite linear PDE system in
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 4.  Its single field is the infinitesimal generator;
      the equations themselves are the vanishing conditions in
      `SatisfiesLorentzInvariantPDE`, indexed by these generators and obtained
      by differentiating the complex Lorentz action at the identity. -/
      structure BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator (d : Nat) where
        val : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) Ōäé
        skew :
          ŌłĆ ╬╝ ╬Į,
            (MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d ╬╝ : Ōäé) * val ╬╝ ╬Į +
            (MinkowskiSpace.metricSignature d ╬Į : Ōäé) * val ╬Į ╬╝ = 0

      def BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent
          (d n : Nat)
          (A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun i ╬╝ => Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1), A.val ╬╝ ╬Į * z0 i ╬Į

      def BHW.SatisfiesLorentzInvariantPDE
          (d n : Nat)
          (G : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        ŌłĆ A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d,
          fderiv Ōäé G z0 (BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0) = 0

      /-- Convert the componentwise skew-Minkowski generator used by the
      Hall-Wightman PDE into the existing complex Lorentz Lie-algebra
      predicate.  The proof expands the diagonal matrix `╬ĘŌäé`: the `(╬╝,╬Į)`
      entry of `A.val.transpose * ╬ĘŌäé + ╬ĘŌäé * A.val` is exactly
      `metricSignature ╬Į * A.val ╬Į ╬╝ + metricSignature ╬╝ * A.val ╬╝ ╬Į`,
      hence zero by `A.skew ╬╝ ╬Į` and commutativity of addition. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiSkewGenerator_isInLieAlgebra
          (A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d) :
          ComplexLorentzGroup.IsInLieAlgebra A.val

      /-- The one-parameter complex Lorentz subgroup generated by `A`.  This
      uses the existing public exponential constructor
      `ComplexLorentzGroup.expLieAlg`; the metric-preservation and determinant
      proofs stay inside the complex Lorentz infrastructure rather than being
      reintroduced in the source proof. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator.expCurve
          (A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d)
          (t : Ōäé) :
          ComplexLorentzGroup d :=
        ComplexLorentzGroup.expLieAlg ((t : Ōäé) ŌĆó A.val)
          (ComplexLorentzGroup.isInLieAlgebra_smul t
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiSkewGenerator_isInLieAlgebra A))

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiSkewGenerator_expCurve_val
          (A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d)
          (t : Ōäé) :
          (BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator.expCurve A t).val =
            NormedSpace.exp (t ŌĆó A.val)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiSkewGenerator_expCurve_zero
          (A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator.expCurve A 0 = 1

      theorem BHW.complexLorentzAction_expCurve_mem_extendedTube
          (A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d)
          (t : Ōäé)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction
              (BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator.expCurve A t) z0 Ōłł
            BHW.ExtendedTube d n

      /-- Derivative at the identity of the one-parameter Lorentz action.
      Componentwise this is the derivative of
      `Ōłæ ╬Į, (exp (t ŌĆó A.val)) ╬╝ ╬Į * z0 i ╬Į` at `0`; use
      `hasDerivAt_exp_smul_const A.val 0`, simplify `exp 0 = 1`, and sum the
      coordinate derivatives. -/
      theorem BHW.complexLorentz_expCurve_action_hasDerivAt
          (A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          HasDerivAt
            (fun t : Ōäé =>
              BHW.complexLorentzAction
                (BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator.expCurve A t) z0)
            (BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0)
            0

      /-- `extendF F` is differentiable at an ordinary extended-tube point.
      This is the open-domain conversion of
      `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n F hF_holo hF_cinv`; production must either
      export the currently local `isOpen_extendedTube` support or reprove the
      open-union argument from `BHWCore.ExtendedTube` at this source boundary.
      -/
      theorem BHW.differentiableAt_extendF_of_mem_extendedTube
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          DifferentiableAt Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) z0

      theorem BHW.extendF_expCurve_invariant
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (t : Ōäé) :
          BHW.extendF F
              (BHW.complexLorentzAction
                (BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator.expCurve A t) z0) =
            BHW.extendF F z0

      /-- Lean-facing linear algebra assertion in
      Hall-Wightman Lemmas 6--7: the scalar-product differentials account for
      all local solutions of the infinitesimal Lorentz-invariant PDE at `z0`.
      This is not a new assumption; it is the rank computation in the paper. -/
      def BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (i j : Fin n) :
          (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé :=
        fderiv Ōäé (fun z => BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z i j) z0

      def BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) : Prop :=
        ŌłĆ Ōäō : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé,
          (ŌłĆ A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d,
            Ōäō (BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0) = 0) ->
          Ōłā c : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé,
            Ōäō =
              Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                c i j ŌĆó BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z0 i j

      def BHW.sourceGramDifferential
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ->L[Ōäé]
            (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :=
        fderiv Ōäé (fun z => BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) z0

      theorem BHW.sourceGramDifferential_apply
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 X : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0 X =
            fun i j =>
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (X i) (z0 j) +
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (z0 i) (X j)

      theorem BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential_apply
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 X : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (i j : Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z0 i j X =
            BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0 X i j

      theorem BHW.sourceGramDifferential_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent_zero
          (d n : Nat)
          (A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0
              (BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0) = 0

      /-- Infinitesimal Witt extension for the complex Minkowski form.  Given
      independent source vectors `u` spanning a nondegenerate subspace and
      prescribed first-order displacements `X` satisfying the skew compatibility
      `B(X a, u b) + B(u a, X b) = 0`, extend the partial infinitesimal
      isometry `u a Ōå” X a` to a global complex Lorentz Lie-algebra element.
      This is the infinitesimal analogue of the checked Witt-extension theorem
      used in Hall-Wightman Lemma 2. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_infinitesimalWittExtension
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {r : Nat}
          {u X : Fin r -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hu_indep : LinearIndependent Ōäé u)
          (hu_nondeg :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
              (Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range u)))
          (hcompat :
            ŌłĆ a b : Fin r,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (X a) (u b) +
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (u a) (X b) = 0) :
          Ōłā A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d,
            ŌłĆ a : Fin r,
              (fun ╬╝ => Ōłæ ╬Į : Fin (d + 1), A.val ╬╝ ╬Į * u a ╬Į) = X a

      /-- Selected-row data extracted from a maximal scalar-rank source
      configuration.  The selected rows are independent, their span is
      nondegenerate, and in the range `d + 1 <= n` they span the whole complex
      source spacetime.  In the range `n <= d + 1`, the selector is a bijection
      of the source labels. -/
      structure BHW.HWMaxRankSelectedRowsData
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) where
        I : Fin (min n (d + 1)) -> Fin n
        I_injective : Function.Injective I
        selected_independent :
          LinearIndependent Ōäé (fun a => z0 (I a))
        selected_span_nondeg :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
            (Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range (fun a => z0 (I a))))
        selected_span_top_of_spacetime_le_points :
          d + 1 <= n ->
            Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range (fun a => z0 (I a))) = ŌŖż
        I_surjective_of_points_le_spacetime :
          n <= d + 1 -> Function.Surjective I

      theorem BHW.hwMaxRank_selectedRowsData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0Rank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0) :
          BHW.HWMaxRankSelectedRowsData d n z0

      theorem BHW.hwMaxRank_kernel_row_relation_transfer
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 X : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (S : BHW.HWMaxRankSelectedRowsData d n z0)
          (hDq : BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0 X = 0)
          (hD_le_n : d + 1 <= n)
          {r : Fin n}
          {c : Fin (min n (d + 1)) -> Ōäé}
          (hrel :
            z0 r = Ōłæ a : Fin (min n (d + 1)), c a ŌĆó z0 (S.I a)) :
          X r = Ōłæ a : Fin (min n (d + 1)), c a ŌĆó X (S.I a)

      theorem BHW.sourceGramDifferential_kernel_eq_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0Rank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0)
          (X : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0 X = 0 Ōåö
            Ōłā A : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator d,
              X = BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0

      Lean-shaped proof of the kernel theorem.  This is the
      Hall-Wightman Lemmas 6--7 rank computation; it is not an analytic
      continuation statement.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceGramDifferential_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent_zero
          ... := by
        ext i j
        simp [BHW.sourceGramDifferential_apply,
          BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner, Finset.sum_add_distrib,
          Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm,
          mul_comm]
        -- After collecting the double sum, each `(╬╝,╬Į)` coefficient is
        -- `metric ╬╝ * A.val ╬╝ ╬Į + metric ╬Į * A.val ╬Į ╬╝`, hence zero by
        -- `A.skew ╬╝ ╬Į`.
        exact BHW.sum_skew_minkowski_generator_pair_zero A z0 i j

      theorem BHW.sourceGramDifferential_kernel_eq_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent
          ... := by
        constructor
        ┬Ę intro hDq
          let S := BHW.hwMaxRank_selectedRowsData
            (d := d) hd n hz0Rank
          have hcompat :
              ŌłĆ a b : Fin (min n (d + 1)),
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (X (S.I a)) (z0 (S.I b)) +
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (z0 (S.I a)) (X (S.I b)) =
                  0 := by
            intro a b
            have h := congrFun (congrFun hDq (S.I a)) (S.I b)
            simpa [BHW.sourceGramDifferential_apply] using h
          rcases BHW.complexMinkowski_infinitesimalWittExtension
              (d := d) hd
              (u := fun a : Fin (min n (d + 1)) => z0 (S.I a))
              (X := fun a : Fin (min n (d + 1)) => X (S.I a))
              S.selected_independent S.selected_span_nondeg hcompat with
            Ō¤©A, hA_selectedŌ¤®
          refine Ō¤©A, ?_Ō¤®
          ext r ╬╝
          by_cases hD_le_n : d + 1 <= n
          ┬Ę have hspan_top :=
              S.selected_span_top_of_spacetime_le_points hD_le_n
            rcases
              BHW.exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame
                (q := fun a : Fin (min n (d + 1)) => z0 (S.I a))
                (v := z0 r)
                (by
                  simpa [hspan_top] : z0 r Ōłł
                    Submodule.span Ōäé
                      (Set.range (fun a : Fin (min n (d + 1)) =>
                        z0 (S.I a)))) with
              Ō¤©c, hrelŌ¤®
            have hXrel :
                X r =
                  Ōłæ a : Fin (min n (d + 1)), c a ŌĆó X (S.I a) :=
              BHW.hwMaxRank_kernel_row_relation_transfer
                (d := d) hd n S hDq hD_le_n hrel
            calc
              X r ╬╝ =
                  (Ōłæ a : Fin (min n (d + 1)), c a ŌĆó X (S.I a)) ╬╝ := by
                    rw [hXrel]
              _ =
                  (Ōłæ a : Fin (min n (d + 1)), c a ŌĆó
                    BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0 (S.I a)) ╬╝ := by
                    simp [hA_selected]
              _ =
                  BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0 r ╬╝ := by
                    -- `lorentzInfinitesimalTangent` is linear in `z0`;
                    -- rewrite `z0 r` by the coefficient relation `hrel`.
                    simpa [BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent, hrel,
                      Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc,
                      mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
          ┬Ę have hn_le_D : n <= d + 1 := Nat.le_of_not_ge hD_le_n
            rcases S.I_surjective_of_points_le_spacetime hn_le_D r with
              Ō¤©a, haŌ¤®
            subst r
            simpa using congrFun (hA_selected a) ╬╝
        ┬Ę rintro Ō¤©A, rflŌ¤®
          exact
            BHW.sourceGramDifferential_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent_zero
              (d := d) (n := n) A z0
      ```

      The helper
      `BHW.hwMaxRank_kernel_row_relation_transfer` is purely linear algebra.
      If `z0 r = Ōłæ a, c a ŌĆó z0 (S.I a)`, pair
      `X r - Ōłæ a, c a ŌĆó X (S.I a)` with every selected vector `z0 (S.I b)`;
      the equality `Dq X = 0`, the selected relation, and bilinearity show all
      pairings vanish.  Since the selected span is nondegenerate and, in the
      case `d + 1 <= n`, is the whole ambient space, the vector itself is
      zero.  In the case `n <= d + 1`, the selected index map is surjective,
      so there are no unselected rows.  This is the point where maximal
      scalar rank is used essentially.

      theorem BHW.continuousLinearFunctional_factor_through_of_vanishes_on_ker
          {E F : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace Ōäé E]
          [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace Ōäé F]
          [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E] [FiniteDimensional Ōäé F]
          (L : E ->L[Ōäé] F)
          (Ōäō : E ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé)
          (hker : ŌłĆ x, L x = 0 -> Ōäō x = 0) :
          Ōłā Žł : F ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé, Ōäō = Žł.comp L

      Lean-shaped proof of the factor-through support theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.continuousLinearFunctional_factor_through_of_vanishes_on_ker
          ... := by
        let Llin : E ŌåÆŌéŚ[Ōäé] F := L.toLinearMap
        let elllin : E ŌåÆŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé := Ōäō.toLinearMap
        have hker_le :
            LinearMap.ker Llin Ōēż LinearMap.ker elllin := by
          intro x hx
          exact hker x hx
        let lifted : (E Ō¦Ė LinearMap.ker Llin) ŌåÆŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé :=
          (LinearMap.ker Llin).liftQ elllin hker_le
        let ŽłRange : LinearMap.range Llin ŌåÆŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé :=
          lifted.comp Llin.quotKerEquivRange.symm.toLinearMap
        rcases ŽłRange.exists_extend with Ō¤©Žłlin, hŽłlinŌ¤®
        let Žł : F ŌåÆL[Ōäé] Ōäé :=
          Ō¤©Žłlin, LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional ŽłlinŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©Žł, by
          ext x
          have hxrange :
              (Ō¤©Llin x, Ō¤©x, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® : LinearMap.range Llin) =
                Llin.quotKerEquivRange (Submodule.Quotient.mk x) := by
            ext
            simp [LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange_apply_mk]
          have hŽł_apply :
              Žłlin (Llin x) = ŽłRange Ō¤©Llin x, Ō¤©x, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® := by
            have h :=
              congrArg
                (fun f : LinearMap.range Llin ŌåÆŌéŚ[Ōäé] Ōäé =>
                  f Ō¤©Llin x, Ō¤©x, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®)
                hŽłlin
            simpa [LinearMap.comp_apply] using h
          calc
            Ōäō x = elllin x := rfl
            _ = lifted (Submodule.Quotient.mk x) := by
                  rw [Submodule.liftQ_apply]
            _ = ŽłRange
                  (Llin.quotKerEquivRange (Submodule.Quotient.mk x)) := by
                  simp [ŽłRange]
            _ = ŽłRange Ō¤©Llin x, Ō¤©x, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® := by
                  rw [ŌåÉ hxrange]
            _ = Žłlin (Llin x) := hŽł_apply.symm
            _ = Žł (L x) := rflŌ¤®
      ```

      The proof is algebraic until the final coercion to a continuous linear
      map: `LinearMap.exists_extend` extends the functional from
      `LinearMap.range Llin` to the ambient codomain, and
      `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` supplies continuity of the
      extension.  The quotient step is `Submodule.liftQ` through
      `LinearMap.ker Llin`, then `LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange`.

      theorem BHW.sourceGramDifferential_dual_coordinate_expansion
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (Žł : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé) :
          Ōłā c : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé,
            Žł.comp (BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0) =
              Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                c i j ŌĆó BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z0 i j

      Lean-shaped proof of the finite dual expansion:

      ```lean
      private def BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate (n : Nat) (i j : Fin n) :
          (Fin n ŌåÆ Fin n ŌåÆ Ōäé) ŌåÆL[Ōäé] Ōäé :=
        (ContinuousLinearMap.proj
          (R := Ōäé) (╬╣ := Fin n) (Žå := fun _ : Fin n => Ōäé) j).comp
          (ContinuousLinearMap.proj
            (R := Ōäé) (╬╣ := Fin n)
            (Žå := fun _ : Fin n => Fin n ŌåÆ Ōäé) i)

      private theorem BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate_expand
          (n : Nat)
          (Žł : (Fin n ŌåÆ Fin n ŌåÆ Ōäé) ŌåÆL[Ōäé] Ōäé) :
          Žł =
            Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
              (Žł (Pi.single i (Pi.single j (1 : Ōäé)))) ŌĆó
                BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate n i j := by
        ext Z
        have hrow : ŌłĆ i : Fin n,
            Z i =
              Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                (Z i j) ŌĆó
                  (Pi.single (M := fun _ : Fin n => Ōäé) j (1 : Ōäé)) := by
          intro i
          ext j
          rw [Finset.sum_apply]
          calc
            Z i j = Ōłæ x : Fin n, if j = x then Z i x else 0 := by
              simp
            _ =
                Ōłæ x : Fin n,
                  Z i x *
                    (Pi.single (M := fun _ : Fin n => Ōäé) x (1 : Ōäé)) j := by
              apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
              intro x hx
              by_cases h : j = x
              ┬Ę subst x
                simp
              ┬Ę simp [h]
        have hZ :
            Z =
              Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                (Z i j) ŌĆó
                  (Pi.single i (Pi.single j (1 : Ōäé)) :
                    Fin n ŌåÆ Fin n ŌåÆ Ōäé) := by
          ext i j
          rw [Finset.sum_apply]
          rw [Finset.sum_eq_single i]
          ┬Ę simpa using congrFun (hrow i) j
          ┬Ę intro b hb hbi
            by_cases h : i = b
            ┬Ę exact False.elim (hbi h.symm)
            ┬Ę simp [h]
          ┬Ę intro hi
            simp at hi
        calc
          Žł Z =
              Žł (Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                (Z i j) ŌĆó
                  (Pi.single i (Pi.single j (1 : Ōäé)) :
                    Fin n ŌåÆ Fin n ŌåÆ Ōäé)) := by
            rw [ŌåÉ hZ]
          _ =
              Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                (Z i j) *
                  Žł (Pi.single i (Pi.single j (1 : Ōäé))) := by
            simp [map_sum, map_smul]
          _ =
              (Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                (Žł (Pi.single i (Pi.single j (1 : Ōäé)))) ŌĆó
                  BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate n i j) Z := by
            simp [BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate, mul_comm]

      private theorem BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential_eq_targetCoordinate_comp
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n ŌåÆ Fin (d + 1) ŌåÆ Ōäé)
          (i j : Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z0 i j =
            (BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate n i j).comp
              (BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0) := by
        ext X
        simpa [BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate]
          using BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential_apply d n z0 X i j

      theorem BHW.sourceGramDifferential_dual_coordinate_expansion
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n ŌåÆ Fin (d + 1) ŌåÆ Ōäé)
          (Žł : (Fin n ŌåÆ Fin n ŌåÆ Ōäé) ŌåÆL[Ōäé] Ōäé) :
          Ōłā c : Fin n ŌåÆ Fin n ŌåÆ Ōäé,
            Žł.comp (BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0) =
              Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                c i j ŌĆó BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z0 i j := by
        let c : Fin n ŌåÆ Fin n ŌåÆ Ōäé :=
          fun i j => Žł (Pi.single i (Pi.single j (1 : Ōäé)))
        exact Ō¤©c, by
          have hŽł := BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate_expand n Žł
          ext X
          calc
            (Žł.comp (BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0)) X =
                Žł (BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0 X) := rfl
            _ =
                (Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                  c i j ŌĆó BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate n i j)
                  (BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0 X) := by
              rw [hŽł]
            _ =
                (Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                  c i j ŌĆó
                    (BHW.sourceGramTargetCoordinate n i j).comp
                      (BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0)) X := by
              simp [c]
            _ =
                (Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                  c i j ŌĆó
                    BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z0 i j) X := by
              simp [BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential_eq_targetCoordinate_comp]Ō¤®
      ```

      This proof has no Hall-Wightman content hidden inside it: the only
      ingredient is the finite coordinate-basis expansion of the ambient Gram
      target.  The coefficient is explicitly
      `c i j = Žł (Pi.single i (Pi.single j 1))`; after composition with
      `sourceGramDifferential`, the coordinate projection is identified with
      `sourceGramCoordinateDifferential` by extensionality and
      `BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential_apply`.

      /-- Coordinate split used in Hall-Wightman Lemma 5.  The vector
      variables near `z0` are written as scalar-product coordinates `u`
      together with auxiliary fiber coordinates `v`; in these coordinates the
      selected source scalar products are exactly the `u` coordinates. -/
      structure BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit
          (d n e a : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) where
        Uvec : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
        U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
        Ucoord : Set ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé))
        coord :
          Uvec ŌēāŌé£ Ucoord
        coordMap :
          (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ->
            ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé))
        coordSymmMap :
          ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)) ->
            (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
        z0_mem : z0 Ōłł Uvec
        Uvec_open : IsOpen Uvec
        Uvec_sub_extendedTube : Uvec ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        U0_open : IsOpen U0
        gram_z0_mem : BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Ōłł U0
        sourceGram_Uvec_sub :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Uvec ŌŖå U0
        Ucoord_open : IsOpen Ucoord
        Ucoord_product :
          Ōłā Us Ua, IsOpen Us Ōł¦ IsOpen Ua Ōł¦
            IsConnected Ua Ōł¦ Ua.Nonempty Ōł¦
            Ucoord = Set.prod Us Ua
        coordMap_diff :
          DifferentiableOn Ōäé coordMap Uvec
        coordSymmMap_diff :
          DifferentiableOn Ōäé coordSymmMap Ucoord
        coordMap_eq_coord :
          ŌłĆ z : Uvec, coordMap z.1 = (coord z).1
        coordSymmMap_eq_coord_symm :
          ŌłĆ p hp, coordSymmMap p = (coord.symm Ō¤©p, hpŌ¤®).1
        coordSymmMap_mem :
          ŌłĆ p, p Ōłł Ucoord -> coordSymmMap p Ōłł Uvec
        coordMap_coordSymmMap :
          ŌłĆ p, p Ōłł Ucoord -> coordMap (coordSymmMap p) = p
        coordSymmMap_coordMap :
          ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec -> coordSymmMap (coordMap z) = z
        scalarCoord : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Fin e -> Ōäé
        scalarCoord_diff : Differentiable Ōäé scalarCoord
        sourceGram_selected :
          ŌłĆ z : Uvec,
            scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
              (coord z).1.1
        sourceGramDifferentials_selected_local :
          ŌłĆ z : Uvec,
            ŌłĆ i j : Fin n,
              Ōłā c : Fin e -> Ōäé,
                BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z.1 i j =
                  Ōłæ a : Fin e, c a ŌĆó
                    fderiv Ōäé (fun y =>
                      scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) a) z.1
        span_local :
          ŌłĆ z : Uvec,
            BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z
        scalarCoord_injective_on_U0_variety :
          ŌłĆ Z W,
            Z Ōłł U0 ->
            W Ōłł U0 ->
            Z Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
            W Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
            scalarCoord Z = scalarCoord W ->
            Z = W

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 5 coordinate preparation: at a maximal
      scalar-rank point where the scalar differentials span the invariant PDE
      covectors, choose functionally independent scalar products and
      complementary vector variables giving a local analytic coordinate split.
      -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_coordinateSplit
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hz0Rank :
            BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0)
          (hSpan :
            BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z0) :
          Ōłā e a,
            BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit d n e a z0

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_coord_pullback_extendF
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e a : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hF_holo_ext :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (C : BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit d n e a z0) :
          Ōłā g : ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)) -> Ōäé,
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé g C.Ucoord Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ z : C.Uvec,
              g (C.coord z).1 = BHW.extendF F z

      Scratch-checked Lean pattern for this coordinate pullback:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_coord_pullback_extendF ... := by
        let g :
            ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)) -> Ōäé :=
          fun p => BHW.extendF F (C.coordSymmMap p)
        have hmap :
            Set.MapsTo C.coordSymmMap C.Ucoord
              (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) := by
          intro p hp
          exact C.Uvec_sub_extendedTube (C.coordSymmMap_mem p hp)
        have hg_diff : DifferentiableOn Ōäé g C.Ucoord := by
          exact hF_holo_ext.comp C.coordSymmMap_diff hmap
        exact Ō¤©g, hg_diff, by
          intro z
          have hsymm :
              C.coordSymmMap (C.coord z).1 = z.1 := by
            rw [ŌåÉ C.coordMap_eq_coord z]
            exact C.coordSymmMap_coordMap z.1 z.2
          simpa [g, hsymm]Ō¤®
      ```

      The equality proof uses the ambient map equation
      `C.coordMap_eq_coord z` to rewrite the subtype coordinate
      `(C.coord z).1` back to `C.coordMap z.1`, then applies
      `C.coordSymmMap_coordMap`.  It should not use the subtype
      homeomorphism inverse directly unless the production structure exposes
      the needed ambient equality; otherwise the proof loses the link between
      `coordSymmMap` and the subtype-valued `coord.symm`.

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_auxiliaryDerivative_zero
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e a : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hF_holo_ext :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (C : BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit d n e a z0)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          {g : ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)) -> Ōäé}
          (hg_branch :
            ŌłĆ z : C.Uvec,
              g (C.coord z).1 = BHW.extendF F z) :
          ŌłĆ p, p Ōłł C.Ucoord ->
            ŌłĆ v : Fin a -> Ōäé,
              fderiv Ōäé g p (0, v) = 0

      /-- Product-neighborhood shrink used inside the inverse-function chart.
      From an arbitrary open coordinate target around `(u0, v0)`, choose a
      smaller product target whose auxiliary factor is an open connected ball.
      The proof is purely finite-dimensional topology:
      `isOpen_prod_iff.mp hU_open u0 v0 hp` gives open factors inside `U`;
      then `Metric.mem_nhds_iff` shrinks the second factor to a ball and
      `isConnected_ball` supplies connectedness. -/
      theorem BHW.exists_sourceCoord_product_with_connected_aux_subset_open
          {e a : Nat}
          {U : Set ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé))}
          {p : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)}
          (hU_open : IsOpen U) (hp : p Ōłł U) :
          Ōłā Us Ua,
            IsOpen Us Ōł¦ IsOpen Ua Ōł¦
            p.1 Ōłł Us Ōł¦ p.2 Ōłł Ua Ōł¦
            IsConnected Ua Ōł¦ Ua.Nonempty Ōł¦
            Set.prod Us Ua ŌŖå U

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_sourceCoord_product_with_connected_aux_subset_open
          ... := by
        rcases isOpen_prod_iff.mp hU_open p.1 p.2 hp with
          Ō¤©Us0, Ua0, hUs0_open, hUa0_open, hp1, hp2, hprod_subŌ¤®
        rcases Metric.mem_nhds_iff.mp (hUa0_open.mem_nhds hp2) with
          Ō¤©r, hr, hball_subŌ¤®
        refine Ō¤©Us0, Metric.ball p.2 r,
          hUs0_open, Metric.isOpen_ball,
          hp1, Metric.mem_ball_self hr,
          isConnected_ball hr,
          Ō¤©p.2, Metric.mem_ball_self hrŌ¤®, by
            intro q hq
            exact hprod_sub Ō¤©hq.1, hball_sub hq.2Ō¤®Ō¤®
      ```

      The inverse-function theorem proof uses this helper only to choose the
      smaller target product.  It must then restrict the local equivalence to
      the preimage of `Set.prod Us Ua`, rather than leaving the original
      target and recording a subset.

      /-- Finite-dimensional calculus support: a differentiable complex
      function with zero Frechet derivative on an open connected set is
      constant there.  This is used only to turn the zero auxiliary
      derivatives in Hall-Wightman Lemma 5 into independence of the auxiliary
      variable on one connected product polydisc. -/
      theorem BHW.eqOn_of_fderiv_eq_zero_of_isConnected_open
          {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace Ōäé E]
          [FiniteDimensional Ōäé E]
          {╬® : Set E} (h╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®) (h╬®_conn : IsConnected ╬®)
          {f : E -> Ōäé}
          (hf : DifferentiableOn Ōäé f ╬®)
          (hzero : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł ╬® -> fderiv Ōäé f x = 0) :
          ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł ╬® -> ŌłĆ y, y Ōłł ╬® -> f x = f y

      Lean-shaped proof of this support theorem should use the checked
      Mathlib API directly:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.eqOn_of_fderiv_eq_zero_of_isConnected_open ... := by
        intro x hx y hy
        exact h╬®_open.is_const_of_fderiv_eq_zero
          h╬®_conn.isPreconnected
          hf
          (fun z hz => hzero z hz)
          hx hy
      ```

      The needed theorem is
      `IsOpen.is_const_of_fderiv_eq_zero` from
      `Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.MeanValue`; it takes an `IsPreconnected`
      open set, so the local helper only converts `IsConnected ╬®` to
      `h╬®_conn.isPreconnected`.  Production may inline this Mathlib call
      instead of exporting the helper if that keeps the support file cleaner.

      /-- Holomorphic functions on a product coordinate polydisc whose
      derivatives in all auxiliary directions vanish are pulled back from the
      scalar coordinate factor.  This is the analytic core of
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 5 after the coordinate split. -/
      theorem BHW.holomorphic_product_independent_of_auxiliary
          {e a : Nat}
          {U : Set ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé))}
          {g : ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)) -> Ōäé}
          (hU_product :
            Ōłā Us Ua, IsOpen Us Ōł¦ IsOpen Ua Ōł¦
              IsConnected Ua Ōł¦ Ua.Nonempty Ōł¦
              U = Set.prod Us Ua)
          (hg : DifferentiableOn Ōäé g U)
          (haux :
            ŌłĆ p, p Ōłł U ->
              ŌłĆ v : Fin a -> Ōäé,
                fderiv Ōäé g p (0, v) = 0) :
          Ōłā Us Ua, Ōłā ╬©s : (Fin e -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
            IsOpen Us Ōł¦ IsOpen Ua Ōł¦ IsConnected Ua Ōł¦ Ua.Nonempty Ōł¦
            U = Set.prod Us Ua Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬©s Us Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ p, p Ōłł U -> g p = ╬©s p.1

      Lean-shaped proof of the product-independence theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.holomorphic_product_independent_of_auxiliary ... := by
        rcases hU_product with
          Ō¤©Us, Ua, hUs_open, hUa_open, hUa_conn, hUa_ne, rflŌ¤®
        rcases hUa_ne with Ō¤©vbase, hvbaseŌ¤®
        let ╬©s : (Fin e -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé := fun u => g (u, vbase)
        have h╬©s_diff : DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬©s Us := by
          -- Slice `g` along the differentiable affine map
          -- `u Ōå” (u, vbase)`.  Use `hasFDerivAt_prodMk_left`/`inl`
          -- or the corresponding `fun_prop` differentiability proof, and
          -- the inclusion `(u, vbase) Ōłł Us ├Ś╦ó Ua`.
          exact
            hg.comp
              ((differentiable_id.prodMk
                (differentiable_const vbase)).differentiableOn)
              (by intro u hu; exact Ō¤©hu, hvbaseŌ¤®)
        have hconst_aux :
            ŌłĆ u, u Ōłł Us -> ŌłĆ v, v Ōłł Ua -> g (u, v) = ╬©s u := by
          intro u hu v hv
          let gu : (Fin a -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé := fun v => g (u, v)
          have hgu_diff : DifferentiableOn Ōäé gu Ua := by
            -- Slice `g` along `v Ōå” (u, v)` using
            -- `hasFDerivAt_prodMk_right`/`inr`.
            exact
              hg.comp
                (((differentiable_const u).prodMk
                  differentiable_id).differentiableOn)
                (by intro v hv; exact Ō¤©hu, hvŌ¤®)
          have hgu_zero :
              ŌłĆ v, v Ōłł Ua -> fderiv Ōäé gu v = 0 := by
            intro v hv
            have hslice :
                fderiv Ōäé gu v =
                  (fderiv Ōäé g (u, v)).comp
                    (ContinuousLinearMap.inr Ōäé
                      (Fin e -> Ōäé) (Fin a -> Ōäé)) := by
              -- Chain rule for `gu = g Ōłś fun v => (u, v)`.
              -- Since `Us ├Ś╦ó Ua` is open, `hg` gives differentiability at
              -- `(u, v)`, and
              -- `hasFDerivAt_prodMk_right` gives the derivative of the slice.
              have hg_at :
                  DifferentiableAt Ōäé g (u, v) :=
                hg.differentiableAt
                  ((hUs_open.prod hUa_open).mem_nhds Ō¤©hu, hvŌ¤®)
              have hslice_at :
                  HasFDerivAt (fun v : Fin a -> Ōäé => (u, v))
                    (ContinuousLinearMap.inr Ōäé
                      (Fin e -> Ōäé) (Fin a -> Ōäé)) v :=
                hasFDerivAt_prodMk_right u v
              simpa [gu, hslice_at.fderiv] using
                (fderiv_comp'
                  (x := v) (g := g)
                  (f := fun v : Fin a -> Ōäé => (u, v))
                  hg_at hslice_at.differentiableAt)
            apply ContinuousLinearMap.ext
            intro dv
            rw [hslice]
            simpa [ContinuousLinearMap.comp_apply] using
              haux (u, v) Ō¤©hu, hvŌ¤® dv
        exact
          (BHW.eqOn_of_fderiv_eq_zero_of_isConnected_open
            hUa_open hUa_conn hgu_diff hgu_zero v hv vbase hvbase).trans
            rfl
        exact Ō¤©Us, Ua, ╬©s, hUs_open, hUa_open, hUa_conn,
          Ō¤©vbase, hvbaseŌ¤®, rfl, h╬©s_diff, by
            intro p hp
            exact hconst_aux p.1 hp.1 p.2 hp.2Ō¤®
      ```

      The displayed proof intentionally fixes `u` and applies constancy only
      on the auxiliary factor `Ua`.  It never asserts that zero auxiliary
      derivatives make `g` constant on the full product, and it never compares
      different connected components of an auxiliary open set.

      /-- Reinflate a holomorphic function of the selected independent scalar
      coordinates to a holomorphic ambient scalar function on a neighborhood
      of the full source Gram point.  The differentiability hypothesis is only
      local: Hall-Wightman Lemma 5 gives `╬©s` on the scalar projection of the
      product coordinate polydisc, not on a global selected-coordinate image. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_selectedScalarFunction_to_fullGramChart
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e a : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (C : BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit d n e a z0)
          {Us : Set (Fin e -> Ōäé)}
          {Ua : Set (Fin a -> Ōäé)}
          {╬©s : (Fin e -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hprod : C.Ucoord = Set.prod Us Ua)
          (hUs_open : IsOpen Us)
          (h╬©s_diff :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬©s Us) :
          Ōłā U0 ╬©,
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            U0 ŌŖå C.U0 Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              ╬© Z = ╬©s (C.scalarCoord Z)

      /-- Local implementation structure for the finite rank selection in
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 5.  Its fields say that `scalarCoord` is a projection
      to selected source Gram coordinates and that their differentials form a
      basis of the image of `fderiv (sourceMinkowskiGram) z0`. -/
      structure BHW.SelectedScalarCoordinatesBasis
          (d n e : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (scalarCoord : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Fin e -> Ōäé) : Prop where
        card_eq_expected :
          e = BHW.sourceGramExpectedDim d n
        selected_linear :
          Ōłā L : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) ->L[Ōäé] (Fin e -> Ōäé),
            scalarCoord = L
        spans_sourceGram_differentials :
          ŌłĆ i j : Fin n,
            Ōłā c : Fin e -> Ōäé,
              BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z0 i j =
                Ōłæ a : Fin e, c a ŌĆó
                  fderiv Ōäé (fun z =>
                    scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a) z0
        independent_selected_differentials :
          LinearIndependent Ōäé
            (fun a : Fin e =>
              fderiv Ōäé (fun z =>
                scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a) z0)

      /-- Local implementation data for completing the selected scalar
      coordinates to a vector-space coordinate system. -/
      structure BHW.HWVectorCoordinateSplitData
          (d n e a : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (scalarCoord : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Fin e -> Ōäé)
          (auxCoord : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Fin a -> Ōäé) : Prop where
        coordMap_hasFDerivAt_equiv :
          Ōłā E : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ŌēāL[Ōäé]
              ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)),
            HasFDerivAt
              (fun z =>
                (scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z), auxCoord z))
              E z0
        vertical_sourceGram_zero :
          ŌłĆ X,
            (ŌłĆ a : Fin e,
              fderiv Ōäé (fun z =>
                scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a) z0 X = 0) ->
            ŌłĆ i j : Fin n,
              BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z0 i j X = 0

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_independentScalarCoordinates
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0Rank :
            BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0)
          (hSpan :
            BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z0) :
          Ōłā e,
          Ōłā scalarCoord : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Fin e -> Ōäé,
            Differentiable Ōäé scalarCoord Ōł¦
            BHW.SelectedScalarCoordinatesBasis
              d n e z0 scalarCoord

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_sourceVectorCoordinateSplit
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {scalarCoord : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Fin e -> Ōäé}
          (hscalar :
            BHW.SelectedScalarCoordinatesBasis d n e z0 scalarCoord) :
          Ōłā a,
          Ōłā auxCoord : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Fin a -> Ōäé,
            BHW.HWVectorCoordinateSplitData
              d n e a z0 scalarCoord auxCoord

      theorem BHW.exists_open_selectedMinor_ne_zero_neighborhood
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hz0Rank :
            BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0)
          {scalarCoord : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Fin e -> Ōäé}
          (hscalar :
            BHW.SelectedScalarCoordinatesBasis d n e z0 scalarCoord) :
          Ōłā Uvec : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen Uvec Ōł¦ z0 Ōłł Uvec Ōł¦
            Uvec ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              LinearIndependent Ōäé
                (fun a : Fin e =>
                  fderiv Ōäé (fun y =>
                    scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) a) z))

      theorem BHW.sourceGramDifferential_image_basis_of_selected_minor
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          {Uvec : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          {scalarCoord : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Fin e -> Ōäé}
          (hscalar :
            BHW.SelectedScalarCoordinatesBasis d n e z0 scalarCoord)
          (hU_rank :
            ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec -> BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
          (hz : z Ōłł Uvec)
          (hU_selected_indep :
            ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              LinearIndependent Ōäé
                (fun a : Fin e =>
                  fderiv Ōäé (fun y =>
                    scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) a) z))
          (i j : Fin n) :
          Ōłā c : Fin e -> Ōäé,
            BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z i j =
              Ōłæ a : Fin e, c a ŌĆó
                fderiv Ōäé (fun y =>
                  scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) a) z

      /-- Constant-rank shrink for the selected scalar coordinates.  This is
      the local finite-dimensional rank theorem that fills the two pointwise
      fields of `HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit`: selected scalar differentials
      span the source-Gram differential image, and Hall-Wightman Lemmas 6--7
      give the invariant-PDE span at every point of the same chart. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_selectedScalarDifferentials_local
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hz0Rank :
            BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0)
          {scalarCoord : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Fin e -> Ōäé}
          (hscalar :
            BHW.SelectedScalarCoordinatesBasis d n e z0 scalarCoord) :
          Ōłā Uvec : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen Uvec Ōł¦
            z0 Ōłł Uvec Ōł¦
            Uvec ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              ŌłĆ i j : Fin n,
                Ōłā c : Fin e -> Ōäé,
                  BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z i j =
                    Ōłæ a : Fin e, c a ŌĆó
                      fderiv Ōäé (fun y =>
                        scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) a) z) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z)

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_coordinateSplit_inverseFunction
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e a : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hz0Rank :
            BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0)
          {scalarCoord : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Fin e -> Ōäé}
          {auxCoord : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Fin a -> Ōäé}
          (hdata :
            BHW.HWVectorCoordinateSplitData
              d n e a z0 scalarCoord auxCoord) :
          BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit d n e a z0

      noncomputable def BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent
          [NeZero d]
          {n e a : Nat}
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (C : BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit d n e a z0)
          (p : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé))
          (hp : p Ōłł C.Ucoord)
          (v : Fin a -> Ōäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fderiv Ōäé C.coordSymmMap p (0, v)

      theorem BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent_selectedScalarDeriv_zero
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e a : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (C : BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit d n e a z0)
          (p : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé))
          (hp : p Ōłł C.Ucoord)
          (v : Fin a -> Ōäé) :
          ŌłĆ b : Fin e,
            fderiv Ōäé (fun y =>
              C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) b)
              (C.coordSymmMap p)
              (BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent C p hp v) = 0

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_auxiliaryTangent_sourceGramDifferentials_zero
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e a : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (C : BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit d n e a z0)
          (p : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé))
          (hp : p Ōłł C.Ucoord)
          (v : Fin a -> Ōäé) :
          ŌłĆ i j : Fin n,
            BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n
              (C.coordSymmMap p) i j
              (BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent C p hp v) = 0

      theorem BHW.fderiv_coord_pullback_extendF
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n e a : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hF_holo_ext :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (C : BHW.HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit d n e a z0)
          {g : ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)) -> Ōäé}
          (hg_branch :
            ŌłĆ z : C.Uvec,
              g (C.coord z).1 = BHW.extendF F z)
          (p : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé))
          (hp : p Ōłł C.Ucoord)
          (v : Fin a -> Ōäé) :
          fderiv Ōäé g p (0, v) =
            fderiv Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) (C.coordSymmMap p)
              (BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent C p hp v)

      Lean-shaped proof of this derivative comparison:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.fderiv_coord_pullback_extendF ... := by
        let pull : ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)) -> Ōäé :=
          fun q => BHW.extendF F (C.coordSymmMap q)
        have hpull_eventually : g =ßČĀ[­ØōØ p] pull := by
          filter_upwards [C.Ucoord_open.mem_nhds hp] with q hq
          let z : C.Uvec :=
            Ō¤©C.coordSymmMap q, C.coordSymmMap_mem q hqŌ¤®
          have hcoord : (C.coord z).1 = q := by
            rw [ŌåÉ C.coordMap_eq_coord z]
            exact C.coordMap_coordSymmMap q hq
          simpa [pull, z, hcoord] using hg_branch z
        have hsymm_at :
            DifferentiableAt Ōäé C.coordSymmMap p :=
          C.coordSymmMap_diff.differentiableAt
            (C.Ucoord_open.mem_nhds hp)
        have hExt_at :
            DifferentiableAt Ōäé (BHW.extendF F)
              (C.coordSymmMap p) :=
          hF_holo_ext.differentiableAt
            ((BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)).mem_nhds
              (C.Uvec_sub_extendedTube (C.coordSymmMap_mem p hp)))
        have hfderiv_eq :
            fderiv Ōäé g p = fderiv Ōäé pull p :=
          Filter.EventuallyEq.fderiv_eq hpull_eventually
        have hchain :
            fderiv Ōäé pull p =
              (fderiv Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) (C.coordSymmMap p)).comp
                (fderiv Ōäé C.coordSymmMap p) := by
          simpa [pull] using
            (fderiv_comp'
              (x := p) (g := BHW.extendF F)
              (f := C.coordSymmMap) hExt_at hsymm_at)
        calc
          fderiv Ōäé g p (0, v)
              = fderiv Ōäé pull p (0, v) := by rw [hfderiv_eq]
          _ = fderiv Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) (C.coordSymmMap p)
                (BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent C p hp v) := by
                rw [hchain]
                rfl
      ```

      This theorem does not infer a derivative identity from branch equality
      alone.  Branch equality supplies only the eventual equality
      `g =ßČĀ[­ØōØ p] pull`; the chain-rule identification of the pullback
      derivative uses the explicit holomorphy input `hF_holo_ext` and
      `C.coordSymmMap_diff`.

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 4: infinitesimal complex Lorentz invariance.
      The differential of an invariant holomorphic function annihilates every
      tangent vector obtained by applying a complex skew-Minkowski generator to
      the source tuple. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_lorentzInfinitesimalEquations
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          BHW.SatisfiesLorentzInvariantPDE d n (BHW.extendF F) z0

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemmas 6--7 in dimension `d + 1`: at a maximal scalar
      rank point, the differentials of the source scalar products span the
      solution space of the infinitesimal invariant PDE from Lemma 4. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_scalarDifferentials_span_PDE
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0Rank :
            BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0) :
          BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z0

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 5: if the scalar-product differentials span the
      invariant PDE solutions at a maximal scalar-rank point `z0`, every
      holomorphic invariant branch is, near `z0`, a convergent power series in
      a functionally independent set of source scalar products.  The maximal
      rank hypothesis is part of the theorem surface: it is what lets the
      coordinate split be shrunk so the selected scalar differentials span the
      full source-Gram differential image at every point of the product chart.
      -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_from_PDE_span
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0 : z0 Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hz0Rank :
            BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hF_holo_ext :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (hSpan :
            BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z0) :
          Ōłā Uvec U0 ╬©,
            IsOpen Uvec Ōł¦ z0 Ōłł Uvec Ōł¦
            Uvec ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł U0 ->
                ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
                  BHW.extendF F z)

      Lean-shaped proof of `BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_from_PDE_span`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_from_PDE_span ... := by
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_coordinateSplit
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hz0Rank hSpan with
          Ō¤©e, a, CŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_coord_pullback_extendF
            (d := d) hd n e a F hF_holo_ext C with
          Ō¤©g, hg_diff, hg_branchŌ¤®
        have haux :
            ŌłĆ p, p Ōłł C.Ucoord ->
              ŌłĆ v : Fin a -> Ōäé,
                fderiv Ōäé g p (0, v) = 0 := by
          exact BHW.hallWightman_auxiliaryDerivative_zero
            (d := d) hd n e a F hF_holo_ext C
              hF_holo hF_cinv hg_branch
        rcases BHW.holomorphic_product_independent_of_auxiliary
            C.Ucoord_product hg_diff haux with
          Ō¤©Us, Ua, ╬©s, hUs_open, hUa_open, hUa_conn, hUa_ne,
            hprod, h╬©s_diff, h╬©s_eqŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_selectedScalarFunction_to_fullGramChart
            (d := d) hd n e a C hprod hUs_open h╬©s_diff with
          Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, hU0_sub_C, h╬©_diff,
            h╬©_eq_selectedŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©C.Uvec Ōł® BHW.ExtendedTube d n, U0, ╬©,
          C.Uvec_open.inter (BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)),
          Ō¤©C.z0_mem, hz0Ō¤®, Set.inter_subset_right,
          hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, by
            intro z hz hGramU0
            have hscalar :
                ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
                  ╬©s (C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)) := by
              exact h╬©_eq_selected _ Ō¤©hGramU0, Ō¤©z, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®
            calc
              ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
                  = ╬©s (C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)) := hscalar
              _ = ╬©s (C.coord Ō¤©z, hz.1Ō¤®).1.1 := by
                    rw [C.sourceGram_selected Ō¤©z, hz.1Ō¤®]
              _ = g (C.coord Ō¤©z, hz.1Ō¤®).1 := by
                    exact (h╬©s_eq _ (by
                      simpa [hprod] using (C.coord Ō¤©z, hz.1Ō¤®).2)).symm
