              _ = BHW.extendF F z := hg_branch Ō¤©z, hz.1Ō¤®Ō¤®
      ```

      The auxiliary product theorem has a real connectedness hypothesis.  Zero
      derivative in auxiliary directions would only make `g u ┬Ę` constant on
      each connected component of `Ua`; it would not identify two different
      components.  Hall-Wightman's coordinate polydisc is therefore recorded as
      `C.Ucoord = Us ├Ś Ua` with `Ua` connected and nonempty.  The proof chooses
      `vbase Ōłł Ua`, defines `╬©s u := g (u, vbase)`, proves differentiability
      on `Us` by restricting `g` to the continuous linear slice
      `u Ōå” (u, vbase)`, and uses the finite-dimensional theorem that a
      differentiable function with zero derivative on an open connected set is
      constant to show `g (u,v) = ╬©s u` for all `v Ōłł Ua`.

      The support lemmas in this proof are the actual Hall-Wightman Lemma 5
      content.  `hallWightman_coord_pullback_extendF` is the analytic
      coordinate-change proof for `extendF F` in the split variables.
      `hallWightman_auxiliaryDerivative_zero` is the finite-dimensional
      duality step: auxiliary coordinate tangent vectors are exactly the
      directions annihilated by the selected scalar-product differentials.
      The local selected-differential span then makes every source-Gram
      differential vanish on those tangents; the local `span_local` field
      writes the invariant branch differential as a combination of those
      source-Gram differentials, and Lemma 4 supplies the pointwise PDE input.
      These support lemmas must
      be proved before the theorem above; they are not wrappers around the
      final scalar representative.

      Max-rank chart production-readiness gate.  The theorem
      `BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_from_PDE_span` is not allowed to enter
      production Lean as a single opaque Hall-Wightman Lemma-5 name.  Its
      proof term must already contain the following checked subproofs, in this
      order:

      ```lean
      -- 1. Lemma 4: differentiate the actual complex-Lorentz exp curve.
      have hPDE :
          BHW.SatisfiesLorentzInvariantPDE d n (BHW.extendF F) z :=
        BHW.hallWightman_lorentzInfinitesimalEquations
          (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hz

      -- 2. Lemmas 6--7: identify the kernel of the Gram differential with
      -- infinitesimal Lorentz tangents, then factor and expand covectors.
      have hSpan :
          BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z :=
        BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_scalarDifferentials_span_PDE
          (d := d) hd n hzRank

      -- 3. Lemma 5: build an actual differentiable product chart, not merely
      -- a topological local equivalence.
      rcases BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_coordinateSplit
          (d := d) hd n hz hzRank hSpan with
        Ō¤©e, a, CŌ¤®

      -- 4. Pull back the extended branch through `C.coordSymmMap`, prove the
      -- auxiliary derivative vanishes by the local selected-span fields, and
      -- descend through the connected auxiliary factor.
      rcases BHW.hallWightman_coord_pullback_extendF
          (d := d) hd n e a F hF_holo_ext C with
        Ō¤©g, hg_diff, hg_branchŌ¤®
      have haux :
          ŌłĆ p, p Ōłł C.Ucoord ->
            ŌłĆ v : Fin a -> Ōäé, fderiv Ōäé g p (0, v) = 0 :=
        BHW.hallWightman_auxiliaryDerivative_zero
          (d := d) hd n e a F hF_holo_ext C
          hF_holo hF_cinv hg_branch
      rcases BHW.holomorphic_product_independent_of_auxiliary
          C.Ucoord_product hg_diff haux with
        Ō¤©Us, Ua, ╬©s, hUs_open, hUa_open, hUa_conn, hUa_ne,
          hprod, h╬©s_diff, h╬©s_eqŌ¤®

      -- 5. Reinflate from selected scalar coordinates to an ambient scalar
      -- Gram chart on the source variety.
      rcases BHW.hallWightman_selectedScalarFunction_to_fullGramChart
          (d := d) hd n e a C hprod hUs_open h╬©s_diff with
        Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, hU0_sub_C, h╬©_diff,
          h╬©_eq_selectedŌ¤®
      ```

      Each line above is a mathematical obligation, not an interface shim.
      In particular, step 1 must use the explicit Lie-algebra membership and
      `expCurve` derivative; step 2 must prove the infinitesimal Witt-extension
      kernel equality before factoring covectors; step 3 must construct the
      local product chart with `C.Ucoord = Set.prod Us Ua`, connected
      nonempty auxiliary factor, differentiable coordinate maps, and local
      selected-span fields; step 4 must prove constancy only on that connected
      auxiliary factor; and step 5 must prove the selected-coordinate chart on
      `sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, including the domain-control field used
      later by the branch-law transport helper.  If any one of these items is
      still only a theorem name or imported as theorem-2 content, the max-rank
      Hall-Wightman chart is not production-Lean-ready.
      In production Lean, declare the local implementation structures
      `SelectedScalarCoordinatesBasis` and `HWVectorCoordinateSplitData` before
      `hallWightman_powerSeries_coordinateSplit`; they are shown in this block
      at the point where their fields become relevant.

      Descent/max-rank/removable production-readiness ledger.  This table is
      binding after the Lemma-3 gate: the descent theorem
      `BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent` is an assembly
      target only after every row that supplies local scalar charts or
      removability has been proved or replaced by sorry-free ordinary
      Hall-Wightman support.

      | Descent subpacket | Current status | Implementation-level proof content |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Germ API and local chart-to-`Phi` compatibility | Checked API plus scratch-pinned helper. | `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn` is already the right predicate; `hallWightman_localScalarChart_eq_scalarValue` only realizes a variety point as an extended-tube Gram and rewrites both sides by branch equality. |
      | Branch-defined scalar value, continuity, and local boundedness | Component transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | `sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn` defines the branch value on the subtype `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n`, and `sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn_branch_eq` proves branch independence from `extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq`; the continuity/local-boundedness proof uses Lemma 3 directly and may not call the exceptional local chart theorem. |
      | Lemma 4 infinitesimal PDE | Proof transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | Build `ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator`, prove Lie-algebra membership, define the complex-Lorentz `expCurve`, compute the action derivative at `0`, obtain differentiability of `extendF` from `extendF_holomorphicOn`, and use extended-tube invariance along the curve. |
      | Lemmas 6--7 kernel and dual span | Proof transcript pinned; two finite helpers scratch-pinned. | Prove `sourceGramDifferential_kernel_eq_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent` using selected rows and infinitesimal Witt extension, then factor covectors through the Gram differential and expand finite product duals in coordinate probes. |
      | Max-rank selected scalar coordinates and vector coordinate split | Proof transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | Extract a nonzero selected scalar minor at rank `min (d + 1) n`, prove constant-rank persistence, complete the selected scalar coordinates by auxiliary vector coordinates, and apply the finite-dimensional inverse function theorem. |
      | Product target and auxiliary independence | Product-slice transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | Shrink the IFT target so `C.Ucoord = Set.prod Us Ua` with connected nonempty `Ua`, prove auxiliary derivatives vanish using local selected-span fields, and apply `IsOpen.is_const_of_fderiv_eq_zero` only on the fixed auxiliary slice. |
      | Reinflate selected scalar coordinates to a full source-Gram chart | Lean-shaped transcript pinned; depends on coordinate split implementation. | Define `U0 := C.U0 Ōł® {Z | C.scalarCoord Z Ōłł Us}` and `╬© Z := ╬©s (C.scalarCoord Z)`, prove differentiability on this exact open, and carry the source-variety domain-control field. |
      | Max-rank scalar chart assembly | Assembly transcript pinned; not a first Lean target. | Combine the product chart with Lemma 3's adapted local realization and the branch-law transport helper; this theorem cannot be implemented before the preceding coordinate/PDE rows. |
      | Exceptional locus and dense max-rank complement | Determinantal transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | Prove the exceptional rank locus is the analytic determinantal subset cut out by maximal scalar minors and prove `sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank` for relatively open scalar domains. |
      | Normal analytic-space removability | SCV support transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | Prove or import as sorry-free ordinary SCV support the normality of the source Gram determinantal variety and the normal analytic-space Riemann extension theorem with continuity/local boundedness hypotheses. |
      | `sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent` | Mechanical only after all rows above. | Use the branch-defined `Phi`, max-rank charts, removable exceptional charts, and the exact `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn` constructor fields; do not introduce a second glued scalar value. |

      After the theorem-2 proof-doc gate is fully closed, the first possible
      production Lean target in this descent block would be a lower support
      theorem such as the finite dual factorization, coordinate expansion,
      exp-curve derivative support, or selected-row kernel theorem, not
      `BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent`,
      `BHW.hallWightman_localScalarChart_at`, or
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`.  This is a future
      implementation-order note, not permission to start production Lean while
      the oriented scalar-source producer ledger remains open.

      Lean-shaped proof of the coordinate split:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_coordinateSplit ... := by
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_independentScalarCoordinates
            (d := d) hd n hz0Rank hSpan with
          Ō¤©e, scalarCoord, hscalar_diff, hscalar_basisŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_sourceVectorCoordinateSplit
            (d := d) hd n e hscalar_basis with
          Ō¤©a, auxCoord, hcoord_dataŌ¤®
        exact
          BHW.hallWightman_coordinateSplit_inverseFunction
            (d := d) hd n e a hz0 hz0Rank hcoord_data
      ```

      The proof of
      `BHW.hallWightman_coordinateSplit_inverseFunction` is the finite
      dimensional inverse-function theorem applied to
      `z Ōå” (scalarCoord (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z), auxCoord z)`.  Shrink the
      vector neighborhood to the ordinary extended tube using
      `BHW.isOpen_extendedTube`; keep both the subtype homeomorphism `coord`
      and the ambient differentiable maps `coordMap`/`coordSymmMap`; shrink
      the coordinate target by first applying
      `BHW.exists_sourceCoord_product_with_connected_aux_subset_open` to the
      inverse-function-theorem target open, and then restricting the local
      homeomorphism to the preimage of the resulting product
      `Us ├Ś Ua`.  The final `C.Ucoord` is definitionally this product, so the
      field `C.Ucoord_product` is proved by `Ō¤©Us, Ua, hUs_open, hUa_open,
      hUa_conn, hUa_nonempty, rflŌ¤®`.  This restriction step is mandatory:
      it is not enough to know a product lies inside the IFT image while
      keeping the old larger coordinate target, because
      `BHW.holomorphic_product_independent_of_auxiliary` needs equality
      `C.Ucoord = Set.prod Us Ua`; define
      `U0` as a scalar neighborhood on which selected scalar coordinates are
      injective on `sourceComplexGramVariety d n`; and record
      `sourceGram_selected` by the first projection of the coordinate map.
      The maximal-rank hypothesis is used here, not hidden in the later
      derivative proof: after shrinking, the full source-Gram differential
      image is generated by the selected scalar differentials at every vector
      point in `Uvec`, giving
      `sourceGramDifferentials_selected_local`; Hall-Wightman Lemmas 6--7 give
      `span_local` at every vector point in the same `Uvec`.
      Both pointwise fields are supplied by
      `BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_selectedScalarDifferentials_local` and then
      intersected with the inverse-function-theorem coordinate neighborhood.
      The `scalarCoord_injective_on_U0_variety` field is not a convenience:
      it is the local scalar-variety chart assertion from Hall-Wightman Lemma
      5, proved from the same inverse-function chart plus Lemma 3's local image
      theorem.

      Lean-shaped proof of the constant-rank local field theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_selectedScalarDifferentials_local
          ... := by
        -- The selected differentials are independent at `z0`; independence of
        -- a finite determinant/minor is open, and the ordinary extended tube is
        -- open, so shrink to a neighborhood where the same selected minor is
        -- nonzero and all points remain in the extended tube.
        rcases BHW.exists_open_selectedMinor_ne_zero_neighborhood
            (d := d) hd n e hz0 hz0Rank hscalar with
          Ō¤©Uvec, hU_open, hz0U, hU_ET, hU_rank, hU_selected_indepŌ¤®
        have hselected_span :
            ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              ŌłĆ i j : Fin n,
                Ōłā c : Fin e -> Ōäé,
                  BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z i j =
                    Ōłæ a : Fin e, c a ŌĆó
                      fderiv Ōäé (fun y =>
                        scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) a) z := by
          intro z hz i j
          exact
            BHW.sourceGramDifferential_image_basis_of_selected_minor
              (d := d) hd n e hscalar hU_rank z hz hU_selected_indep i j
        have hPDE_span :
            ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z := by
          intro z hz
          exact
            BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_scalarDifferentials_span_PDE
              (d := d) hd n (hU_rank z hz)
        exact Ō¤©Uvec, hU_open, hz0U, hU_ET,
          hU_rank, hselected_span, hPDE_spanŌ¤®
      ```

      The two internal finite-dimensional helpers in that transcript are the
      ordinary constant-rank/minor facts: the first shrinks to a nonvanishing
      selected scalar minor inside `ExtendedTube`; the second says that on such
      a patch the selected scalar differentials are a basis for the image of
      the source Gram differential.  The `SelectedScalarCoordinatesBasis`
      field `card_eq_expected` is essential in the second helper: independence
      alone only gives a subspace of the image, while `card_eq_expected`
      together with `HWSourceGramMaxRankAt` supplies the dimension equality
      needed to conclude spanning.  They are linear algebra and inverse
      function theorem support, not source-representative wrappers.

      The two differentiability helpers used below are part of this chart
      construction, not extra assumptions.  For
      `g p := extendF F (C.coordSymmMap p)` on `C.Ucoord`,
      `BHW.hallWightman_coord_pullback_extendF` proves differentiability by
      `C.coordSymmMap_diff`, `C.coordSymmMap_mem`, and
      `hF_holo_ext.mono C.Uvec_sub_extendedTube`; its branch equality follows
      from `C.coordSymmMap_coordMap` and `C.coordMap_eq_coord`.  The tangent
      helper defines
      `auxiliaryCoordinateTangent C p hp v = fderiv Ōäé C.coordSymmMap p (0,v)`.
      Differentiating `C.coordMap (C.coordSymmMap q) = q` at `p` inside the
      open set `C.Ucoord` shows that all selected scalar-coordinate
      derivatives of this tangent are zero; then
      `C.sourceGramDifferentials_selected_local` upgrades this to vanishing of
      every source Gram coordinate differential.

      Lean-shaped proof of the two auxiliary tangent helpers:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent_selectedScalarDeriv_zero
          ... := by
        intro b
        let X := BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent C p hp v
        have hright :
            (fun q => (C.coordMap (C.coordSymmMap q)).1 b) =ßČĀ[­ØōØ p]
              (fun q : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé) => q.1 b) := by
          filter_upwards [C.Ucoord_open.mem_nhds hp] with q hq
          simp [C.coordMap_coordSymmMap q hq]
        have hcoord_first :
            fderiv Ōäé (fun q =>
              (C.coordMap (C.coordSymmMap q)).1 b) p (0, v) = 0 := by
          have hfderiv_eq :
              fderiv Ōäé (fun q =>
                (C.coordMap (C.coordSymmMap q)).1 b) p =
              fderiv Ōäé
                (fun q : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé) => q.1 b) p :=
            Filter.EventuallyEq.fderiv_eq hright
          have hproj_zero :
              fderiv Ōäé
                (fun q : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé) => q.1 b)
                p (0, v) = 0 := by
            let L1 :
                ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)) ->L[Ōäé]
                  (Fin e -> Ōäé) :=
              ContinuousLinearMap.fst Ōäé (Fin e -> Ōäé) (Fin a -> Ōäé)
            let L2 : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé :=
              ContinuousLinearMap.proj b
            let L :
                ((Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé)) ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé :=
              L2.comp L1
            have hL :
                (fun q : (Fin e -> Ōäé) ├Ś (Fin a -> Ōäé) => q.1 b) =
                  L := by
              funext q
              rfl
            rw [hL, L.fderiv]
            rfl
          rw [hfderiv_eq]
          exact hproj_zero
        have hselected_eventually :
            (fun q =>
              C.scalarCoord
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (C.coordSymmMap q)) b)
                =ßČĀ[­ØōØ p]
              (fun q => (C.coordMap (C.coordSymmMap q)).1 b) := by
          filter_upwards [C.Ucoord_open.mem_nhds hp] with q hq
          have hmem := C.coordSymmMap_mem q hq
          have hsource :=
            C.sourceGram_selected Ō¤©C.coordSymmMap q, hmemŌ¤®
          simpa [C.coordMap_eq_coord Ō¤©C.coordSymmMap q, hmemŌ¤®] using hsource
        have hbase_at :
            DifferentiableAt Ōäé
              (fun y =>
                C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) b)
              (C.coordSymmMap p) := by
          exact
            (ContinuousLinearMap.proj b).hasFDerivAt.differentiableAt.comp
              (C.coordSymmMap p)
              (C.scalarCoord_diff.differentiableAt.comp
                (C.coordSymmMap p)
                (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).differentiableAt)
        have hsymm_at :
            DifferentiableAt Ōäé C.coordSymmMap p :=
          C.coordSymmMap_diff.differentiableAt
            (C.Ucoord_open.mem_nhds hp)
        have hchain :
            fderiv Ōäé
              (fun y =>
                C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) b)
              (C.coordSymmMap p) X =
            fderiv Ōäé
              (fun q =>
                C.scalarCoord
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (C.coordSymmMap q)) b)
              p (0, v) := by
          have hchain_op :
              fderiv Ōäé
                (fun q =>
                  C.scalarCoord
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (C.coordSymmMap q)) b) p =
              (fderiv Ōäé
                (fun y =>
                  C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) b)
                (C.coordSymmMap p)).comp
                (fderiv Ōäé C.coordSymmMap p) := by
            simpa using
              (fderiv_comp'
                (x := p)
                (g := fun y =>
                  C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) b)
                (f := C.coordSymmMap) hbase_at hsymm_at)
          rw [hchain_op]
          rfl
        calc
          fderiv Ōäé
              (fun y =>
                C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) b)
              (C.coordSymmMap p) X
              = fderiv Ōäé
                  (fun q =>
                    C.scalarCoord
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (C.coordSymmMap q)) b)
                  p (0, v) := hchain
          _ = fderiv Ōäé
                  (fun q =>
                    (C.coordMap (C.coordSymmMap q)).1 b)
                  p (0, v) := by
                  exact congrArg (fun L => L (0, v))
                    (Filter.EventuallyEq.fderiv_eq hselected_eventually)
          _ = 0 := hcoord_first

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_auxiliaryTangent_sourceGramDifferentials_zero
          ... := by
        intro i j
        let zval := C.coordSymmMap p
        have hzval : zval Ōłł C.Uvec := C.coordSymmMap_mem p hp
        let z : C.Uvec := Ō¤©zval, hzvalŌ¤®
        let X := BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent C p hp v
        have hselzero :
            ŌłĆ b : Fin e,
              fderiv Ōäé (fun y =>
                C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) b)
                z.1 X = 0 :=
          BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent_selectedScalarDeriv_zero
            (d := d) hd n e a C p hp v
        rcases C.sourceGramDifferentials_selected_local z i j with
          Ō¤©c, hcŌ¤®
        calc
          BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z.1 i j X
              = (Ōłæ b : Fin e, c b ŌĆó
                  fderiv Ōäé (fun y =>
                    C.scalarCoord (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n y) b)
                    z.1) X := by rw [hc]
          _ = 0 := by simp [hselzero]
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of
      `BHW.hallWightman_auxiliaryDerivative_zero`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_auxiliaryDerivative_zero ... := by
        intro p hp v
        let zval := C.coordSymmMap p
        have hzval : zval Ōłł C.Uvec := C.coordSymmMap_mem p hp
        let z : C.Uvec := Ō¤©zval, hzvalŌ¤®
        let X := BHW.auxiliaryCoordinateTangent C p hp v
        have hG_deriv :
            fderiv Ōäé g p (0, v) =
              fderiv Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) z.1 X := by
          exact BHW.fderiv_coord_pullback_extendF
            (d := d) hd n e a F hF_holo_ext C hg_branch p hp v
        have hPDE_z :
            BHW.SatisfiesLorentzInvariantPDE d n (BHW.extendF F) z.1 :=
          BHW.hallWightman_lorentzInfinitesimalEquations
            (d := d) hd n F
            hF_holo hF_cinv (C.Uvec_sub_extendedTube z.2)
        have hSpan_z :
            BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z.1 :=
          C.span_local z
        have hExt_covector :
            Ōłā c : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé,
              fderiv Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) z.1 =
                Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                  c i j ŌĆó
                    BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z.1 i j := by
          exact hSpan_z (fderiv Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) z.1)
            (by intro A; exact hPDE_z A)
        rcases hExt_covector with Ō¤©c, hcŌ¤®
        have hvertical :
            ŌłĆ i j,
              BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z.1 i j X = 0 :=
          BHW.hallWightman_auxiliaryTangent_sourceGramDifferentials_zero
            (d := d) hd n e a C p hp v
        calc
          fderiv Ōäé g p (0, v)
              = fderiv Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) z.1 X := hG_deriv
          _ = (Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                c i j ŌĆó
                  BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z.1 i j) X := by
                rw [hc]
          _ = 0 := by
                simp [hvertical]
      ```

      The displayed proof is why `HWPowerSeriesCoordinateSplit` carries
      both local fields: `sourceGramDifferentials_selected_local` kills all
      source-Gram differentials on auxiliary tangents, and `span_local` writes
      the covector `fderiv (extendF F)` as a linear combination of those
      differentials at the current chart point.  Lemma 4 is derived pointwise from
      `BHW.hallWightman_lorentzInfinitesimalEquations`; no separate stronger
      PDE theorem is introduced.

      Lean-shaped proof of
      `BHW.hallWightman_lorentzInfinitesimalEquations`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_lorentzInfinitesimalEquations ... := by
        intro A
        let ╬│ : Ōäé -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun t =>
            BHW.complexLorentzAction
              (BHW.ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator.expCurve A t) z0
        have h╬│0 : ╬│ 0 = z0 := by
          simp [╬│, BHW.complexMinkowskiSkewGenerator_expCurve_zero,
            BHW.complexLorentzAction_one]
        have h╬│_deriv :
            HasDerivAt ╬│
              (BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0) 0 := by
          simpa [╬│] using
            BHW.complexLorentz_expCurve_action_hasDerivAt
              (d := d) (n := n) A z0
        have hExtDiff :
            DifferentiableAt Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) z0 :=
          BHW.differentiableAt_extendF_of_mem_extendedTube
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hz0
        have hcomp :
            HasDerivAt (fun t : Ōäé => BHW.extendF F (╬│ t))
              (fderiv Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) z0
                (BHW.lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0)) 0 := by
          exact hExtDiff.hasFDerivAt.comp_hasDerivAt_of_eq
            0 h╬│_deriv h╬│0
        have hconst :
            (fun t : Ōäé => BHW.extendF F (╬│ t)) =ßČĀ[nhds 0]
              fun _ : Ōäé => BHW.extendF F z0 := by
          exact Filter.Eventually.of_forall fun t =>
            BHW.extendF_expCurve_invariant
              (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv A hz0 t
        have hzero :
            HasDerivAt (fun t : Ōäé => BHW.extendF F (╬│ t)) 0 0 := by
          exact
            (hasDerivAt_const (0 : Ōäé) (BHW.extendF F z0)).congr_of_eventuallyEq
              hconst
        exact hcomp.unique hzero
      ```

      The support theorem
      `BHW.complexLorentz_expCurve_action_hasDerivAt` is not an analytic
      continuation argument: it is the finite-dimensional matrix-exponential
      derivative at `0`.  The analytic input in Lemma 4 is exactly
      `BHW.differentiableAt_extendF_of_mem_extendedTube`, obtained from
      `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn`, and the constancy input is exactly
      `BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv` along the generated
      complex Lorentz curve.  This prevents the PDE step from smuggling in a
      stronger invariant-function theorem.

      Lean-shaped proof of
      `BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_scalarDifferentials_span_PDE`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_scalarDifferentials_span_PDE ... := by
        intro Ōäō hŌäō_lorentz
        let Dq := BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0
        have hker :
            ŌłĆ X, Dq X = 0 -> Ōäō X = 0 := by
          intro X hX
          rcases
              (BHW.sourceGramDifferential_kernel_eq_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent
                (d := d) hd n hz0Rank X).1 hX with
            Ō¤©A, rflŌ¤®
          exact hŌäō_lorentz A
        rcases
            BHW.continuousLinearFunctional_factor_through_of_vanishes_on_ker
              Dq Ōäō hker with
          Ō¤©Žł, hŽłŌ¤®
        rcases
            BHW.sourceGramDifferential_dual_coordinate_expansion
              (d := d) n z0 Žł with
          Ō¤©c, hcŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©c, by
          calc
            Ōäō = Žł.comp Dq := hŽł
            _ = Ōłæ i : Fin n, Ōłæ j : Fin n,
                  c i j ŌĆó BHW.sourceGramCoordinateDifferential d n z0 i j := hcŌ¤®
      ```

      The kernel theorem in that proof is Hall-Wightman Lemmas 6--7 in
      finite-dimensional form.  Its proof is:

      1. Define `Dq := BHW.sourceGramDifferential d n z0` and use
         `BHW.sourceGramDifferential_apply`:
         `Dq X i j = B(X i, z0 j) + B(z0 i, X j)`.
      2. For every skew generator `A`,
         `Dq (lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0) = 0`; this is
         `BHW.sourceGramDifferential_lorentzInfinitesimalTangent_zero`,
         proved by expanding the same coordinate formula and applying
         `A.skew`.
      3. Conversely, assume `Dq X = 0` and obtain
         `S : BHW.HWMaxRankSelectedRowsData d n z0` from
         `BHW.hwMaxRank_selectedRowsData`.  If `n <= d + 1`, the selector
         `S.I` is surjective, so the selected rows are all source rows.  If
         `d + 1 <= n`, the selected rows span the whole complex source space.
      4. On the selected span, prescribe the partial infinitesimal map
         `z0 (S.I a) Ōå” X (S.I a)`.  The equation `Dq X = 0` is exactly the skew
         compatibility
         `B(X (S.I a), z0 (S.I b)) + B(z0 (S.I a), X (S.I b)) = 0`.
      5. Apply `BHW.complexMinkowski_infinitesimalWittExtension` to obtain a
         global skew-Minkowski generator `A` matching those selected
         displacements; its inputs are `S.selected_independent`,
         `S.selected_span_nondeg`, and the compatibility from step 4.
      6. In the `n <= d + 1` case the selector is bijective, so the match is
         already all rows.  In the `d + 1 <= n` case, for any relation
         `Ōłæ a, c a ŌĆó z0 (S.I a) = z0 r`, the helper
         `BHW.hwMaxRank_kernel_row_relation_transfer` proves
         `X r = Ōłæ a, c a ŌĆó X (S.I a)` by pairing the difference with every
         selected row; `Dq X = 0` and the selected-row relation make all
         pairings zero, and `S.selected_span_top_of_spacetime_le_points`
         forces the difference to be zero.
         Hence `A z0 r = X r` for every row `r`.
      7. This proves
         `ker (sourceGramDifferential d n z0) =
         range (A Ōå” lorentzInfinitesimalTangent d n A z0)`.  The
         finite-dimensional annihilator step then factors any covector
         vanishing on Lorentz tangents through `Dq`; expanding a functional on
         the finite product target in the coordinate dual basis gives the
         displayed scalar-product differential sum.

      The proof of `BHW.complexMinkowski_infinitesimalWittExtension` is the
      standard finite-dimensional skew-extension argument and is not an
      additional analytic input.  Let
      `E = Submodule.span Ōäé (Set.range u)` and use `hu_indep` to define a
      linear map `T0 : E ->ŌéŚ[Ōäé] (Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)` by `T0 (u a) = X a`.
      The compatibility hypothesis extends by bilinearity to
      `B(T0 e, f) + B(e, T0 f) = 0` for all `e f : E`.  Since `E` is
      nondegenerate, write the ambient space as `E ŌŖĢ EßŚ«`; decompose
      `T0 e = S e + R e` with `S e Ōłł E` and `R e Ōłł EßŚ«`.  The previous identity
      says that `S` is skew on `E`.  For every `q Ōłł EßŚ«`, the functional
      `e Ōå” -B(q, R e)` on `E` has a unique representing vector
      `Rsharp q Ōłł E` by nondegeneracy of `E`.  Define the ambient linear map
      `A(e + q) = S e + R e + Rsharp q`.  Pairing two `E` vectors uses skewness
      of `S`; pairing two `EßŚ«` vectors is zero by orthogonality; pairing
      `e Ōłł E` with `q Ōłł EßŚ«` is zero by the defining equation for `Rsharp`.
      Thus `A` is skew-Minkowski, hence gives a
      `ComplexMinkowskiSkewGenerator`, and by construction `A (u a) = X a`.

      The proof of `BHW.hwMaxRank_selectedRowsData` is the determinant-minor
      extraction from the maximal scalar Gram rank condition.  Use the rank
      theorem for the scalar Gram matrix to choose
      `I : Fin (min n (d + 1)) -> Fin n` with nonzero selected Gram determinant.
      The nonzero Gram determinant gives linear independence of the selected
      source rows and nondegeneracy of their restricted complex Minkowski form.
      If `d + 1 <= n`, the selected family has `d + 1` independent vectors in a
      `(d + 1)`-dimensional space, so its span is `ŌŖż`.  If `n <= d + 1`, then
      `min n (d + 1) = n`, and an injective map `Fin n -> Fin n` is surjective,
      giving `S.I_surjective_of_points_le_spacetime`.

      The proof of `BHW.hwMaxRank_kernel_row_relation_transfer` is the row
      relation calculation needed in the `d + 1 <= n` branch.  Put
      `Y := X r - Ōłæ a, c a ŌĆó X (S.I a)`.  For each selected row `S.I b`, the
      coordinate formula for `sourceGramDifferential` and `hDq` give
      `B(X r, z0 (S.I b)) = -B(z0 r, X (S.I b))` and the analogous identities
      for each selected row.  Rewriting `z0 r` by `hrel` makes
      `B(Y, z0 (S.I b)) = 0` for all `b`.  Since the selected rows span `ŌŖż`
      under `hD_le_n`, the vector `Y` is orthogonal to the whole complex
      Minkowski space; nondegeneracy of the ambient form gives `Y = 0`.

      The max-rank condition used here is the local scalar-chart maximum rank
      `min (d + 1) n`, not the Lemma-2 same-source-Gram orbit threshold
      `min d n` and not the later `SourceComplexGramRegularAt` predicate.  This
      keeps the Hall-Wightman PDE-span lemma separate from both the Lemma-2
      orbit alternative and the regular-source-map chart machinery.

      Lean-shaped proof of
      `BHW.hallWightman_selectedScalarFunction_to_fullGramChart`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_selectedScalarFunction_to_fullGramChart
          ... := by
        -- The theorem hypotheses include
        -- `hprod : C.Ucoord = Set.prod Us Ua` and
        -- `hUs_open : IsOpen Us`.
        let p0 := (C.coord Ō¤©z0, C.z0_memŌ¤®).1
        have hp0 : p0 Ōłł C.Ucoord :=
          (C.coord Ō¤©z0, C.z0_memŌ¤®).2
        have hp0_prod : p0 Ōłł Set.prod Us Ua := by
          simpa [p0, hprod] using hp0
        have hp0_Us : p0.1 Ōłł Us := by
          exact hp0_prod.1
        let U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) :=
          C.U0 Ōł® {Z | C.scalarCoord Z Ōłł Us}
        let ╬© : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé :=
          fun Z => ╬©s (C.scalarCoord Z)
        have hZ0U0 :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Ōłł U0 := by
          exact Ō¤©C.gram_z0_mem, by
            simpa [p0, C.sourceGram_selected Ō¤©z0, C.z0_memŌ¤®] using
              hp0_UsŌ¤®
        have hU0_open : IsOpen U0 :=
          C.U0_open.inter
            (hUs_open.preimage C.scalarCoord_diff.continuous)
        have h╬©_diff : DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 := by
          have hscalar_into :
              C.scalarCoord '' U0 ŌŖå Us := by
            intro u hu
            rcases hu with Ō¤©Z, hZU0, rflŌ¤®
            exact hZU0.2
          exact h╬©s_diff.comp
            C.scalarCoord_diff.differentiableOn hscalar_into
        exact Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZ0U0, Set.inter_subset_left,
          h╬©_diff, by
            intro Z hZ
            rflŌ¤®
      ```

      The intermediate `hp0_prod` is necessary in Lean: before rewriting
      `C.Ucoord` by `hprod`, a proof `hp0 : p0 Ōłł C.Ucoord` is not a product
      membership proof and has no `.1` projection.  The same pattern should be
      used anywhere a membership proof in `C.Ucoord` is projected into scalar
      and auxiliary coordinates.

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_powerSeriesChart_branch_eq_of_sameGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Uvec : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          {U0 O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          {╬© : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (h╬©_branch_vec :
            ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uvec ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł U0 ->
                ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
                  BHW.extendF F z)
          (hUvec_sub : Uvec ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hO_sub_image :
            O Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Uvec)
          {w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hw : w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hwU :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł
              (U0 Ōł® O) Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :
          ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = BHW.extendF F w

      Lean-shaped proof of the branch-law transport helper:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_powerSeriesChart_branch_eq_of_sameGram
          ... := by
        rcases hO_sub_image Ō¤©hwU.1.2, hwU.2Ō¤® with
          Ō¤©z, hzUvec, hzGramŌ¤®
        have hzET : z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := hUvec_sub hzUvec
        have hzU0 :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł U0 := by
          simpa [hzGram] using hwU.1.1
        calc
          ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
              = ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) := by
                  rw [hzGram]
          _ = BHW.extendF F z :=
                  h╬©_branch_vec z hzUvec hzU0
          _ = BHW.extendF F w :=
                  hBranch hzET hw hzGram
      ```

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemmas 5--7, source-coordinate form: at a
      non-exceptional scalar point there are `sourceGramExpectedDim d n`
      functionally independent scalar products, and the invariant extended-tube
      branch is a convergent power series in those scalar products. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hZU : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z) :
          Ōłā U0 ╬©,
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
            U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł U0 ->
                ╬© (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)

      /-- Branch-defined scalar value on the ordinary source Gram image,
      stated on the subtype carrying the proof of membership in `S'_n`.
      This is the mathematically honest object: it chooses an ordinary
      extended-tube realization of the given scalar point and evaluates the
      single-valued BHW continuation.  The branch law is the separate theorem
      proving this definition does not depend on the chosen realization. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn
          (d n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (Z : {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé //
            Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n}) : Ōäé :=
        BHW.extendF F (Classical.choose Z.2)

      theorem BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn_branch_eq
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hw : w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn d n F
              Ō¤©BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w, Ō¤©w, hw, rflŌ¤®Ō¤® =
            BHW.extendF F w

      theorem BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn_branch_eq ... := by
        classical
        have hZ :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := Ō¤©w, hw, rflŌ¤®
        have hchoose_mem :
            Classical.choose hZ Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hZ).1
        have hchoose_gram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Classical.choose hZ) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hZ).2
        change BHW.extendF F (Classical.choose hZ) =
          BHW.extendF F w
        exact hBranch hchoose_mem hw hchoose_gram

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemmas 1--7 before the final removability step:
      the scalar germ defined by the branch law is single-valued, continuous,
      and locally bounded on `S'_n`. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ŌłĆ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé},
              z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w) :
          Ōłā phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé,
            (ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = BHW.extendF F w) Ōł¦
            ContinuousOn phi (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) Ōł¦
            BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      Lean-shaped proof of continuity and local boundedness.  This proof must
      not call `hallWightman_localScalarChart_at`, because the exceptional
      chart theorem consumes continuity/local boundedness.  Instead it uses
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 directly: every sufficiently near scalar-variety
      point has a realization in an arbitrarily small ordinary extended-tube
      neighborhood of the chosen realization.  The initial subtype value and
      branch-equality proof have been scratch-checked against
      `SourceExtension.lean`: `Classical.choose hZ` has fields
      `(Classical.choose_spec hZ).1 : _ Ōłł ExtendedTube d n` and
      `(Classical.choose_spec hZ).2 : sourceMinkowskiGram d n _ = Z`, so the
      branch law rewrites the chosen representative to any other realization.
      The exported proof-local helper
      `BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn_branch_eq` packages exactly this
      well-definedness calculation; it is not a replacement for the branch
      law, because it consumes `hBranch`.  The total function `phi` below is a
      theorem-local totalization needed only because the existing continuity
      and germ APIs take total functions on the ambient scalar coordinate
      space.  Its `else 0` branch is never mathematical content and must not
      be exported as a production scalar representative.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded ... := by
        classical
        -- Totalize the subtype-defined scalar value only for this ambient
        -- function API.  All later uses are restricted to `S'_n`.
        let phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé :=
          fun Z =>
            if hZ : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n then
              BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn d n F Ō¤©Z, hZŌ¤®
            else 0
        have hphi_branch :
            ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                BHW.extendF F w := by
          intro w hw
          have hZ :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł
                BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := Ō¤©w, hw, rflŌ¤®
          simpa [phi, hZ] using
            BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn_branch_eq
              (d := d) n F hBranch hw
        have hU_sub :
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_subset_sourceComplexGramVariety
            (d := d) (n := n)
        have hExt_holo :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n F hF_holo hF_cinv
        have hExt_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          hExt_holo.continuousOn
        have hcont :
            ContinuousOn phi (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          BHW.continuousOn_of_open_nhdsWithin_control <| by
            intro Z hZU T hT_open hphiZT
            rcases hZU with Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤®
            have hz0_value :
                phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) =
                  BHW.extendF F z0 :=
              hphi_branch z0 hz0
            have hExt_in_T : BHW.extendF F z0 Ōłł T := by
              simpa [hz0_value] using hphiZT
            rcases BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative
                (d := d) hd n hz0 with
              Ō¤©╬Č0, h╬Č0, h╬Č0Gram, h╬Č0spanŌ¤®
            have h╬Č0_value :
                phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0) =
                  BHW.extendF F ╬Č0 :=
              hphi_branch ╬Č0 h╬Č0
            have hExt╬Č_in_T : BHW.extendF F ╬Č0 Ōłł T := by
              have hsame :
                  phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0) =
                    phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) := by
                rw [h╬Č0Gram]
              simpa [h╬Č0_value, hz0_value] using
                hsame.symm Ō¢Ė hphiZT
            rcases
              BHW.continuousOn_openDomain_preimage_nhds
                hExt_cont h╬Č0 hT_open hExt╬Č_in_T with
              Ō¤©Vsrc, hVsrc_open, h╬Č0Vsrc, hVsrc_sub_TŌ¤®
            let Vtube := Vsrc Ōł® BHW.ExtendedTube d n
            have hVtube_open : IsOpen Vtube :=
              hVsrc_open.inter (BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n))
            have h╬Č0Vtube : ╬Č0 Ōłł Vtube := Ō¤©h╬Č0Vsrc, h╬Č0Ō¤®
            have hVtube_sub : Vtube ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n := Set.inter_subset_right
            let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0)
            have hspan :
                Module.finrank Ōäé
                  (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Č0)) =
                r := by
              simpa [r, h╬Č0Gram] using h╬Č0span
            rcases BHW.hwLemma3_adapted_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
                (d := d) hd n h╬Č0 hspan
                hVtube_open h╬Č0Vtube hVtube_sub with
              Ō¤©O, hO_open, hZO, hO_imageŌ¤®
            exact Ō¤©O, hO_open, by simpa [h╬Č0Gram] using hZO, by
              intro G hGO hGdomain
              have hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
                hU_sub hGdomain
              rcases hO_image Ō¤©hGO, hGvarŌ¤® with
                Ō¤©z, hzVtube, hGzŌ¤®
              have hzET : z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := hzVtube.2
              have hphiG : phi G = BHW.extendF F z := by
                simpa [hGz] using hphi_branch z hzET
              simpa [hphiG] using hVsrc_sub_T z hzET hzVtube.1Ō¤®
        have hbdd :
            BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) := by
          intro Z hZU
          rcases hZU with Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤®
          let C : ŌäØ := ŌĆ¢BHW.extendF F z0ŌĆ¢ + 1
          have hC_nonneg : 0 <= C := by positivity
          have hC_pos : ŌĆ¢BHW.extendF F z0ŌĆ¢ < C := by linarith
          have hNorm_open : IsOpen {c : Ōäé | ŌĆ¢cŌĆ¢ < C} :=
            isOpen_lt continuous_norm continuous_const
          rcases BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative
              (d := d) hd n hz0 with
            Ō¤©╬Č0, h╬Č0, h╬Č0Gram, h╬Č0spanŌ¤®
          have h╬Č_bound : ŌĆ¢BHW.extendF F ╬Č0ŌĆ¢ < C := by
            have hsame_value :
                BHW.extendF F ╬Č0 = BHW.extendF F z0 := by
              exact hBranch h╬Č0 hz0 h╬Č0Gram
            simpa [hsame_value] using hC_pos
          rcases
            BHW.continuousOn_openDomain_preimage_nhds
              hExt_cont h╬Č0 hNorm_open h╬Č_bound with
            Ō¤©Vsrc, hVsrc_open, h╬Č0Vsrc, hVsrc_boundŌ¤®
          let Vtube := Vsrc Ōł® BHW.ExtendedTube d n
          have hVtube_open : IsOpen Vtube :=
            hVsrc_open.inter (BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n))
          have h╬Č0Vtube : ╬Č0 Ōłł Vtube := Ō¤©h╬Č0Vsrc, h╬Č0Ō¤®
          have hVtube_sub : Vtube ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n := Set.inter_subset_right
          let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ╬Č0)
          have hspan :
              Module.finrank Ōäé
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ╬Č0)) =
              r := by
            simpa [r, h╬Č0Gram] using h╬Č0span
          rcases BHW.hwLemma3_adapted_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
              (d := d) hd n h╬Č0 hspan
              hVtube_open h╬Č0Vtube hVtube_sub with
            Ō¤©O, hO_open, hZO, hO_imageŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©O, hO_open, by simpa [h╬Č0Gram] using hZO, C,
            hC_nonneg, by
              intro G hG
              have hGvar : G Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
                hU_sub hG.2
              rcases hO_image Ō¤©hG.1, hGvarŌ¤® with
                Ō¤©z, hzVtube, hGzŌ¤®
              have hzET : z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := hzVtube.2
              have hphiG : phi G = BHW.extendF F z := by
                simpa [hGz] using hphi_branch z hzET
              exact le_of_lt (by
                simpa [hphiG] using hVsrc_bound z hzET hzVtube.1)Ō¤®
        exact Ō¤©phi, hphi_branch, hcont, hbddŌ¤®
      ```

      /-- Determinantal description of the exceptional locus in the
      scalar-product variety: locally it is the analytic subset cut out by the
      maximal scalar minors.  This is the input needed by the removable
      singularity theorem. -/
      theorem BHW.hwSourceGramExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
            {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z}

      /-- Normal analytic-space removable theorem in the exact form consumed
      by the Hall-Wightman exceptional-rank chart.  The input gives local
      ambient representatives on the maximal-rank locus of a relatively open
      source-variety domain and continuity/local boundedness on the full
      domain.  Since the exceptional locus is an analytic subvariety of the
      normal source Gram variety, the representatives extend across it as
      analytic-space germs. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_removableAlongExceptionalRank
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
          (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
          (hExceptionalAnalytic :
            BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z})
          (hMaxCharts :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)) :
          ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U ->
            Ōłā U0 ╬©,
              IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
              DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
              U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U Ōł¦
              Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Hall-Wightman's final removable-singularity step: a scalar germ that
      is holomorphic on the non-exceptional part of a scalar neighborhood and
      continuous/locally bounded on the whole neighborhood extends to an
      ambient holomorphic representative at an exceptional scalar point. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_removableScalarChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hphi_cont :
            ContinuousOn phi (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n))
          (hphi_bdd :
            BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n))
          (hExceptionalAnalytic :
            BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z})
          (hMaxCharts :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
                  BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n))
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hZU : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z) :
          Ōłā U0 ╬©,
            IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
            U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn phi ╬©
              (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the exceptional removable chart:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_removableScalarChart_at ... := by
        have hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen (d := d) hd n
        exact
          BHW.sourceGramVariety_removableAlongExceptionalRank
            (d := d) hd n hU_rel hphi_cont hphi_bdd
            hExceptionalAnalytic hMaxCharts Z hZU
      ```

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.sourceGramVariety_removableAlongExceptionalRank`: this theorem must
      be a direct consumer of the named normal analytic-space Riemann theorem
      `BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension`, not a second
      removability black box.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_removableAlongExceptionalRank ... := by
        have hregular :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł U ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
          intro Z hZU hMax
          rcases hMaxCharts Z hZU hMax with
            Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub, hEqŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub, hEqŌ¤®
        have hMaxDense :
            U ŌŖå closure
              (U Ōł® {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z}) :=
          BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank
            (d := d) (n := n) hU_rel
        have hall :=
          BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension
            (d := d) hd n hU_rel hphi_cont hphi_bdd
            hExceptionalAnalytic hMaxDense hregular
        intro Z hZU
        exact hall Z hZU
      ```

      The mathematical input behind
      `BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension` is exactly the normal
      analytic-space Riemann extension theorem recorded earlier in this
      section.  By `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal`, each relatively open
      piece of the source Gram variety is normal.  The exceptional set is the
      analytic subvariety cut out by maximal scalar minors via
      `BHW.hwSourceGramExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety`; its complement is
      the maximal-rank locus.  On that complement the `hMaxCharts` fields give
      a structure-sheaf section represented by ambient holomorphic functions,
      and chart compatibility is equality on the source variety.  Continuity
      and local boundedness of `phi` on all of `U` provide the boundedness
      hypothesis for the normal-space extension theorem.  The normal Riemann
      theorem itself uses the relative-open witness for `U` to choose the
      returned neighborhood inside the source domain, so the subset field
      `U0 Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå U` is part of the theorem output
      rather than a later shrink hidden in this removable wrapper.

      The max-rank chart theorem is the direct formalization of
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 5 together with Lemmas 6--7: choose a realization
      `z0 Ōłł ExtendedTube` of `Z`; maximal scalar rank gives the required
      number `sourceGramExpectedDim d n` of functionally independent scalar
      products; the implicit-function theorem expresses the remaining vector
      variables analytically in terms of those scalar products and auxiliary
      variables; invariance kills the auxiliary derivatives, so the branch is
      a convergent power series in scalar products alone.  The result is the
      ambient scalar chart `╬©`.

      Lean-shaped proof of `BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at ... := by
        rcases hZU with Ō¤©z0, hz0, rflŌ¤®
        have hz0Rank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0 := by
          simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt] using hRank
        have hExt_holo :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n F hF_holo hF_cinv
        have hSpan :
            BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z0 :=
          BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_scalarDifferentials_span_PDE
            (d := d) hd n hz0Rank
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_from_PDE_span
            (d := d) hd n F hz0 hz0Rank hF_holo hF_cinv hExt_holo hSpan with
          Ō¤©Uvec, U0, ╬©,
            hUvec_open, hz0Uvec, hUvec_sub,
            hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, h╬©_branch_vecŌ¤®
        -- Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 shrinks the scalar chart so every nearby
        -- scalar-variety point has an extended-tube realization inside `Uvec`.
        have hspan_adapted :
            Module.finrank Ōäé
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z0)) =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) :=
          BHW.hwSourceGramMaxRankAt_span_finrank_eq_sourceGramMatrixRank
            (d := d) hd n hz0Rank
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_adapted_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hspan_adapted
            hUvec_open hz0Uvec hUvec_sub with
          Ō¤©O, hO_open, hZO, hO_sub_imageŌ¤®
        let Uscalar := U0 Ōł® O
        exact Ō¤©Uscalar, ╬©, hU0_open.inter hO_open,
          Ō¤©hZU0, hZOŌ¤®, h╬©_diff.mono (Set.inter_subset_left),
          by
            intro G hG
            rcases hO_sub_image Ō¤©hG.1.2, hG.2Ō¤® with
              Ō¤©z, hzUvec, rflŌ¤®
            exact Ō¤©z, hUvec_sub hzUvec, rflŌ¤®,
          by
            intro w hw hwUscalar
            have hwU_helper :
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł
                  (U0 Ōł® O) Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
              Ō¤©hwUscalar, Ō¤©w, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®
            exact BHW.hallWightman_powerSeriesChart_branch_eq_of_sameGram
              (d := d) hd n F hBranch h╬©_branch_vec hUvec_sub hO_sub_image
              hw hwU_helperŌ¤®
      ```

      The final helper in the last line is not a new source theorem: it is the
      branch-law transport step.  Given `sourceMinkowskiGram w Ōłł O`, Lemma 3
      returns `z Ōłł Uvec` with the same Gram; `h╬©_branch_vec` identifies
      `╬© (sourceMinkowskiGram z)` with `extendF F z`, and `hBranch` identifies
      `extendF F z` with `extendF F w`.

      The exceptional theorem is the final paragraph of Hall-Wightman's proof.
      First use the same-source-Gram branch law to define the scalar germ
      `phi` on `S'_n`.  Lemmas 1--7 give its single-valuedness, continuity, and
      local boundedness.  On the complement of the exceptional determinantal
      locus, `hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at` supplies ambient
      holomorphic representatives.  Since the exceptional locus is locally an
      analytic subvariety of the source Gram variety, the removable
      singularity theorem extends the scalar representative through `Z`.
      This is the precise mathematical content of
      `hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at`.

      The compatible germ-atlas theorem must remember which local chart was
      obtained at each scalar point and must prove its `overlap_eq` field by
      the Hall-Wightman power-series/removable-germ uniqueness argument.  Do
      not replace that field by a generic predicate saying that arbitrary
      ambient holomorphic extensions agree when they agree on the
      rank-bounded Gram variety; that statement is false in general and would
      be assumption-smuggling.

      Lean-shaped proof of the chart split after these subfacts:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_localScalarChart_at ... := by
        exact BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at
          (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hZU hRank

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at ... := by
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch with
          Ō¤©phi, hphi_branch, hphi_cont, hphi_bddŌ¤®
        have hMaxCharts :
            ŌłĆ Z, Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              Ōłā U0 ╬©,
                IsOpen U0 Ōł¦ Z Ōłł U0 Ōł¦
                DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬© U0 Ōł¦
                U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ŌŖå
                  BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n Ōł¦
                Set.EqOn phi ╬©
                  (U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
          intro W hWU hWmax
          rcases BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at
              (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hWU hWmax with
            Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hWU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub, h╬©_branchŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hWU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub, by
            intro G hG
            have hGdomain : G Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n :=
              hU0_sub hG
            rcases hGdomain with Ō¤©w, hw, rflŌ¤®
            exact (hphi_branch w hw).trans (h╬©_branch w hw hG.1).symmŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_removableScalarChart_at
            (d := d) hd n hphi_cont hphi_bdd
            (BHW.hwSourceGramExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety d n)
            hMaxCharts hZU hRank with
          Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub, hphi_eqŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©U0, ╬©, hU0_open, hZU0, h╬©_diff, hU0_sub, by
          intro w hw hwU0
          have hG : BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w Ōłł
              U0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
            Ō¤©hwU0, Ō¤©w, rflŌ¤®Ō¤®
          exact (hphi_eq hG).symm.trans (hphi_branch w hw)Ō¤®
      ```

      Do not add a separate production descent-core forwarding theorem merely
      to package the local facts into the descent theorem.  The active Lean
      target is the direct
      proof of `sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent` above, where
      the branch-defined `Phi`, max-rank charts, and exceptional removable
      chart are in the same proof term.  If the implementation uses
      `HallWightmanScalarGermAtlas` internally for organization, the atlas
      construction should be inlined or kept private; it is not an additional
      public theorem surface on the route.

      This is the mathematically complete Hall-Wightman output needed by the
      corrected source representative.  Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7 supply the
      local power-series/implicit-function expansions in scalar products; the
      overlap compatibility comes from the same branch law above, and the
      singular points use the bounded/continuous extension and removable
      singularity step in the proof of Theorem I.  The proof never promotes
      equality on the scalar variety to equality on a full ambient open set.

      Once this local source predicate is available on every point of
      `closure E`, the compact common-germ theorem is a finite gluing theorem:

      The two source-supplier lemmas used by the adjacent OS45 packet are
      identity-route lemmas, not a general arbitrary-`Žü` theorem.  The
      Figure-2-4 selector below only constructs the active identity OS45
      common edge and the adjacent swap branch over that same edge.  A later
      generic common-boundary theorem may consume explicit source-germ
      hypotheses for any branch label, but the Figure-2-4 supplier itself must
      stay at the exact active surface:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_id
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ŌäĢ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hUfig_source :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hVcl : closure V ŌŖå Ufig) :
          ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)

      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ŌäĢ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hUfig_source :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hV_swap_ordered_closure :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
              (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žä)
          (hV_figPath_closure :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
              let y :=
                BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
              Ōłā (╬ō ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                Continuous ╬ō Ōł¦
                Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
                ╬ō (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōł¦
                ╬ō (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ t, ╬ō t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ t,
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                    BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t))))
          (hVcl : closure V ŌŖå Ufig) :
          ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)
      ```

      Both displayed suppliers should factor through the same one-branch
      source theorem.  This theorem is the exact OS I ┬¦4.5/BHW input needed
      upstream of EOW:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_oneBranch_sourcePatch
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ŌäĢ) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (U : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hU_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł U,
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          (hU_horiz_bhw :
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł U,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0U : x0 Ōłł U)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0)) :
          BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
            (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
            (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0)
      ```

      Implementation restriction: this arbitrary-`╬▓` theorem is only an
      assembly target because it consumes `hScalar`.  The scalar packet is the
      real mathematical source input.  The current theorem-2 route needs only
      two public scalar-packet suppliers: `╬▓ = 1` and
      `╬▓ = Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®`.  If the generic theorem is
      implemented before the full arbitrary-permutation Hall-Wightman source
      theorem, it must remain this private-style assembly lemma consuming the
      explicit scalar source packet returned by the identity or adjacent scalar
      source suppliers, not a theorem claiming that `hU_open`, `hU_ordered`,
      and `hU_horiz_bhw` alone prove arbitrary permutation source
      compatibility.  Jost and ordinary extended-tube fields live upstream in
      the scalar-packet suppliers; once `hScalar` is present, this assembly
      step does not need to carry them.

      Mathematical content of this one-branch theorem:

      1. Put `Q := os45QuarterTurnCLE` and
         `W╬▓ z := permAct ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)`.
      2. Use `hU_ordered x0 hx0U` and the checked
         `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered` to put the
         horizontal base point in `os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓`.  Use
         `hU_horiz_bhw x0 hx0U` to put the same base point in
         `os45PulledRealBranchDomain ╬▓`.  Both are open domains, so shrink to
         a chart neighborhood contained in their intersection.  On that
         neighborhood, `Q.symm z Ōłł ForwardTube` and `W╬▓ z Ōłł ExtendedTube`.
      3. On this neighborhood the BHW-side representative is definitionally
         `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z)`.
      4. The ACR-side equality

         ```lean
         extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z) =
           bvt_F OS lgc n (W╬▓ z)
         ```

         on `os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓` is the genuine one-branch BHW
         source/edge theorem: the OS-II ACR branch and the Hall-Wightman BHW
         branch are two continuations of the same forward-tube germ through the
         local Streater-Wightman Figure-2-4 real environment.  It may use the
         one-branch scalar-product representative and local source uniqueness
         for the branch `╬▓`, but it may not use adjacent branch equality,
         `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`, final `bvt_W` locality, or global
         PET branch independence.
      5. Package the branch-specific neighborhood and the two agreement fields
         as `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`.

      The identity supplier applies this theorem not to bare `Ufig`, but to
      the ordered open patch

      ```lean
      Uid := Ufig Ōł®
        EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
          (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
      ```

      with `╬▓ = 1` and `x0 = x`.  For a closure point
      `x Ōłł closure V`, this is an open neighborhood of `x`; the required
      membership in `Uid` is obtained from `hVcl` and
      `hV_ordered_closure`.  Apply
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id` with `V := Uid`: the Jost,
      ordinary extended-tube, and identity horizontal pulled-domain hypotheses
      are the corresponding fields of `hUfig_source` restricted along
      `Uid ŌŖå Ufig`.  Then apply the generic one-branch source-patch assembly
      with the same `Uid` and the resulting scalar packet.  No extra shrink is
      needed at this layer unless a later compactness consumer explicitly asks
      for one; the branch-BV compact shrink is a separate downstream step.

      For the adjacent supplier, do not try to use the original `V` as the
      scalar source patch for a boundary point `x Ōłł closure V`.  First choose a
      local open patch around that closure point in the original identity
      coordinates:

      ```lean
      Ux := Ufig Ōł®
        EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
          (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) Ōł®
        {u | (fun k => u (Žä k)) Ōłł
          EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žä} Ōł®
        {u | BHW.realEmbed
          (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) u) Ōłł
          BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä}
      ```

      The point `x` lies in `Ux` by `hVcl`, `hV_ordered_closure`,
      `hV_swap_ordered_closure`, and the closure-level adjacent pulled-domain
      field supplied by the source patch selector.  Openness comes from
      `hUfig_open`, ordered-sector openness, continuity of
      `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint`/`realEmbed`, and
      `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`.  The pointwise
      `hfig_x0` input for the adjacent scalar source theorem is exactly
      `hV_figPath_closure x hx`.

      The adjacent scalar theorem is applied on `Ux` and returns a scalar packet
      at the identity common edge

      ```lean
      Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
      y0 := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
        (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
      hScalar_y0 :
        BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket (d := d) OS lgc n Žä y0
      ```

      The one-branch source-patch assembly theorem is not applied to `Ux`
      directly, because its ordered input for branch `Žä` is an ordered
      `Žä`-configuration.  Apply it to the relabelled patch

      ```lean
      UŽä := {u | (fun k => u (Žä k)) Ōłł Ux}
      ```

      The relabelled patch transport is part of the theorem, not an informal
      convention.  This preimage formulation is the Lean-facing definition; it
      is equivalent to the image formulation
      `{u | Ōłā v Ōłł Ux, u = fun k => v (Žä k)}` because `Žä` is an adjacent swap.
      For the base point,

      ```lean
      let xŽä : NPointDomain d n := fun k => x (Žä k)
      have hxŽäUŽä : xŽä Ōłł UŽä := by
        change (fun k => xŽä (Žä k)) Ōłł Ux
        simpa [xŽä, Žä] using hxUx
      ```

      For a general `u Ōłł UŽä`, put `v := fun k => u (Žä k)`.  Then
      `hvUx : v Ōłł Ux` is exactly `hu`, and

      ```lean
      have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (Žä k) := by
        ext k ╬╝
        simp [v, Žä]
      ```

      The transported source-patch fields are then:

      - openness of `UŽä`, by continuity of the finite permutation map
        `u Ōå” fun k => u (Žä k)` and the openness of `Ux`;
      - Jost membership, from the `Ufig` Jost field applied to `v` and
        `BHW.jostSet_permutation_invariant`;
      - branch-`Žä` ordered-sector membership, exactly the swapped ordered field
        in the definition of `Ux`;
      - ordinary extended-tube membership of `u`, from the adjacent ET field of
        `hUfig_source` applied to `v`;
      - horizontal pulled-BHW membership, by rewriting `u` with `hu_eq` and then
        rewriting `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint Žä (v Ōłś Žä)` to
        `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 v` using
        `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq`, then applying the
        adjacent horizontal field of `hUfig_source` to `v`.

      The Lean-facing transport transcript is:

      ```lean
      have hperm_cont :
          Continuous (fun u : NPointDomain d n => fun k => u (Žä k)) := by
        exact continuous_pi (fun k =>
          continuous_pi (fun ╬╝ =>
            (continuous_apply ╬╝).comp (continuous_apply (Žä k))))

      have hUŽä_open : IsOpen UŽä := by
        simpa [UŽä] using hUx_open.preimage hperm_cont

      have hUŽä_jost : ŌłĆ u Ōłł UŽä, u Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (Žä k)
        have hvUx : v Ōłł Ux := by simpa [UŽä, v] using hu
        have hvJ : v Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n := hUx_jost v hvUx
        have hŽäJ : (fun k => v (Žä k)) Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n :=
          BHW.jostSet_permutation_invariant (d := d) (n := n) Žä hvJ
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (Žä k) := by
          ext k ╬╝
          simp [v, Žä]
        simpa [hu_eq] using hŽäJ

      have hUŽä_ordered :
          ŌłĆ u Ōłł UŽä,
            u Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žä := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (Žä k)
        have hvUx : v Ōłł Ux := by simpa [UŽä, v] using hu
        have hv_ordered :
            (fun k => v (Žä k)) Ōłł
              EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žä :=
          hUx_swap_ordered v hvUx
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (Žä k) := by
          ext k ╬╝
          simp [v, Žä]
        simpa [hu_eq] using hv_ordered

      have hUŽä_ET :
