      ```lean
          ŌłĆ u Ōłł UŽä, BHW.realEmbed u Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (Žä k)
        have hvUx : v Ōłł Ux := by simpa [UŽä, v] using hu
        have hvET :
            BHW.realEmbed (fun k => v (Žä k)) Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          hUx_swap_ET v hvUx
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (Žä k) := by
          ext k ╬╝
          simp [v, Žä]
        simpa [hu_eq] using hvET

      have hUŽä_horiz :
          ŌłĆ u Ōłł UŽä,
            BHW.realEmbed
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) Žä u) Ōłł
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (Žä k)
        have hvUx : v Ōłł Ux := by simpa [UŽä, v] using hu
        have hbase := hUx_horiz v hvUx
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (Žä k) := by
          ext k ╬╝
          simp [v, Žä]
        have hedge :
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) Žä u =
              BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) v := by
          rw [hu_eq]
          simpa [Žä] using
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
              (d := d) (n := n) i hi (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) v
        simpa [hedge] using hbase
      ```

      The basepoint rewrite must be explicit.  Put

      ```lean
      have hedge_eq :
        BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) Žä xŽä = y0 := by
        simpa [Žä, xŽä, y0] using
          BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
            (d := d) (n := n) i hi (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x

      have hScalar_relabelled :
        BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
          (d := d) OS lgc n Žä
          (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) Žä xŽä) := by
        simpa [hedge_eq] using hScalar_y0
      ```

      Applying the one-branch theorem with `╬▓ = Žä`, `U = UŽä`, and `x0 = xŽä`
      consumes `hScalar_relabelled` and yields the adjacent source germ at
      `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint Žä xŽä`.  The final displayed supplier rewrites
      this predicate back to `y0` by the same `hedge_eq`.  This is only
      basepoint transport in the source-germ predicate; it is not equality
      between the identity and adjacent branch values.

      Their proof is not an extra route, but it also must not silently promote
      domain geometry into branch-value equality.  The fields of
      `hUfig_source` say only that the selected Figure-2-4 patch is Jost, that
      the identity and adjacent real configurations lie in the ordinary
      extended tube, and that the two OS45 horizontal chart points lie in the
      corresponding pulled BHW domains.  These fields are enough to choose
      neighborhoods and to evaluate the two formulas.  They do **not** prove
      that the formulas agree.

      For the identity supplier, after `╬▓ = 1`, the branch-specific pulled BHW
      argument is definitionally `Q.symm z`.  For the adjacent supplier, the
      proof must work on the relabelled ordered configuration `x Ōłś Žä`, apply
      the same one-branch OS I ┬¦4.5 / BHW source construction there with the
      pointwise Figure-2-4 path supplied by `hV_figPath_closure x hx`, and
      then transport the result back to the identity common edge by
      `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq` and
      `BHW.os45QuarterTurnConfig_reindexed_realBranch_eq`.  It must not prove
      or assume equality between the identity and adjacent BHW branches.

      Lean implementation rule: `os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent`
      may call only the branch-local OS I ┬¦4.5/BHW source construction for the
      relabelled one-branch packet, plus the geometric reindexing identities.
      If a proof calls the downstream
      `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarGerm_of_OSII_Figure24`, the downstream
      `BHW.bvt_F_adjacent_extendF_edgeDistribution_eq_of_osEOWDifferenceEnvelope`,
      `hallWightman_source_permutedBranch_compatibility_of_distributionalAnchor`,
      `BHW.permutedExtendedTube_singleValued_of_forwardTube_symmetry`, or a
      corollary of them, it has skipped Slot 1 and is off the OS-II route.

      Fork compatibility guard for the whole branch-local source block.  The
      theorem surfaces
      `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_oneBranch_sourcePatch`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_sourcePatch`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`,
      `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalSourceEq_of_BHWJost`, and
      `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalBranchCompatibility_of_BHWJost`
      are pure-Gram theorem surfaces.  They may consume
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData` only after the pure-Gram source gate
      has been closed by a proved or explicitly sourced
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`, including the missing
      full-component/improper Hall-Wightman input.  Since the strict OS ┬¦4.5
      source decision is now closed on the proper-complex/oriented
      representative, none of these pure-Gram names may be implemented as
      compatibility wrappers around the oriented theorem.  The whole block
      must be restated with the oriented source API before Lean:

      The oriented packet shape is the earlier
      `BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket` display in the
      source-decision guard; do not introduce a second incompatible structure
      with the same name.  Its branch-value consumer has the same role as the
      pure-Gram `os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq`, but with the
      oriented invariant in every scalar slot:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedSourceEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (y0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ y0) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let W╬▓ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
          Ōłā Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen Uy Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł Uy Ōł¦
            Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Ōł®
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn
              (fun z => BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z))
              (fun z =>
                BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
              Uy
      ```

      The oriented consumer is Lean-ready only after the following algebraic
      reduction is made explicit.  The branch label in the pulled BHW
      representative is `╬▓.symm`, so the oriented invariant of the pulled
      point is rewritten by
      `BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_permAct
      (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓.symm`; the scalar
      equality packet is then applied to the unpermuted quarter-turn
      invariant.  Reversing `╬▓` and `╬▓.symm`, or putting
      `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (W╬▓ z)` directly into the packet
      field, would double-count the branch permutation.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedScalarEq
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hUy_acr :
            Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          (hUy_bhw :
            Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          (hOrient :
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uy ->
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n ╬▓.symm
                  (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                    (Q.symm z))) =
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (Q.symm z))) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let W╬▓ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
          Set.EqOn
            (fun z => BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z))
            (fun z =>
              BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
            Uy
      ```

      Proof transcript for the oriented reduction:

      1. For `z Ōłł Uy`, put `u := Q.symm z` and
         `w := W╬▓ z = BHW.permAct ╬▓.symm u`.
         From `hUy_acr hz`, unfolded through `os45ACRBranchDomain`, obtain
         `hu_ft : u Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n`.  From `hUy_bhw hz`, unfolded
         through `os45PulledRealBranchDomain`, obtain
         `hw_et : w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n`.  Set
         `hu_et := BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hu_ft`.
      2. Apply the oriented representative twice:
         `(hRep.branch_eq w hw_et).symm` rewrites the pulled BHW branch to
         `hRep.Phi (sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w)`, and
         `hRep.branch_eq u hu_et` rewrites the unpermuted scalar value back
         to `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u`.
      3. Rewrite the pulled invariant using the exact finite permutation
         lemma:
         ```lean
         BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w =
           BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n ╬▓.symm
             (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u)
         ```
         by `BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_permAct
         (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓.symm u`.
      4. Apply `hOrient z hz` to pass from the permuted oriented invariant to
         the unpermuted one.  This is the only scalar equality used in the
         oriented consumer.
      5. Since `u Ōłł ForwardTube`, use
         `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` with the checked
         `bvt_F_holomorphic` and
         `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` inputs, transported
         through `BHW_forwardTube_eq`, to rewrite
         `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u` to `bvt_F OS lgc n u`.
      6. Unfold `BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative`; it is
         `bvt_F OS lgc n w`.  The checked total permutation symmetry
         `bvt_F_perm OS lgc n ╬▓.symm u` gives
         `bvt_F OS lgc n w = bvt_F OS lgc n u`, so the final step uses this
         equality in the reverse direction.

      Lean-oriented calculation:

      ```lean
      intro z hz
      let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
      let u : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé := Q.symm z
      let w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm u
      have hu_ft : u Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
        simpa [BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain, Q, u] using hUy_acr hz
      have hw_et : w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        simpa [BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain, Q, u, w] using hUy_bhw hz
      have hu_et : u Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
        BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hu_ft
      have hbranch_w :
          BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w =
            hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w) :=
        (hRep.branch_eq w hw_et).symm
      have horient_w :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w =
            BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n ╬▓.symm
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u) := by
        simpa [w] using
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_permAct
            d n ╬▓.symm u
      have hbranch_u :
          hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u) =
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u :=
        hRep.branch_eq u hu_et
      have hF_holo_BHW :
          DifferentiableOn Ōäé (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
        simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
          bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
      have hF_restricted_BHW :
          ŌłĆ (╬ø : RestrictedLorentzGroup d)
            (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
            bvt_F OS lgc n
              (fun k ╬╝ => Ōłæ ╬Į, (╬ø.val.val ╬╝ ╬Į : Ōäé) * z k ╬Į) =
            bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
        intro ╬ø z hz
        exact bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
          (d := d) OS lgc n ╬ø z
          ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė hz)
      have hext_u :
          BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u = bvt_F OS lgc n u :=
        BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
          hF_holo_BHW hF_restricted_BHW u hu_ft
      have hperm_u :
          bvt_F OS lgc n w = bvt_F OS lgc n u := by
        simpa [w, BHW.permAct] using bvt_F_perm OS lgc n ╬▓.symm u
      calc
        BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w
            = hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w) := hbranch_w
        _ = hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n ╬▓.symm
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u)) := by
          rw [horient_w]
        _ = hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u) := by
          simpa [Q, u] using hOrient z hz
        _ = BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u := hbranch_u
        _ = bvt_F OS lgc n u := hext_u
        _ = BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z := by
          simpa [BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative, Q, u, w] using
            hperm_u.symm
      ```

      The proof of
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedSourceEq`
      is then only the unpacking of the packet plus this algebraic reduction:

      ```lean
      rcases hScalar with
        Ō¤©hRep, Wscal, Uy, hW_relOpen, hUy_open, hy0Uy,
          hUy_sub, hUy_scalar, hW_double, hPhi_eqŌ¤®
      have hUy_acr :
          Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ := by
        intro z hz
        exact (hUy_sub hz).1
      have hUy_bhw :
          Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ := by
        intro z hz
        exact (hUy_sub hz).2
      have hOrient :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uy ->
            hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n ╬▓.symm
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                  (Q.symm z))) =
            hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (Q.symm z)) := by
        intro z hz
        exact hPhi_eq (hUy_scalar z hz)
      refine Ō¤©Uy, hUy_open, hy0Uy, hUy_sub, ?_Ō¤®
      exact
        BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedScalarEq
          (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ hRep Uy hUy_acr hUy_bhw hOrient
      ```

      The fields `hW_relOpen` and `hW_double` are intentionally unused in the
      last unpacking step: their role is upstream, where the identity or
      adjacent source supplier proves the equality `hPhi_eq` by a
      source-variety identity theorem on the oriented double domain.  They
      must remain in the packet so the producer has no hidden analytic
      continuation assumption.

      This oriented ACR agreement is not yet the horizontal branch-germ
      predicate consumed by the common-boundary envelope.  The envelope
      deliberately consumes the representation-free predicate
      `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`, because after a one-branch source
      theorem has identified the ACR formula and the pulled BHW formula, the
      EOW step should not remember whether that identification came from the
      pure-Gram or oriented scalar packet.  On the active route, however, the
      only admissible producer of that neutral predicate is the oriented one:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_orientedSourceEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (y0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ y0) :
          BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
            (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ y0 := by
        classical
        let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
        let W╬▓ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) ->
            Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun z => BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
        obtain Ō¤©Uy, hUy_open, hy0Uy, hUy_sub, hACR_eqŌ¤® :=
          BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedSourceEq
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n ╬▓ y0 hScalar
        have hPulled_mem :
            ŌłĆ z Ōłł Uy, W╬▓ z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
          intro z hz
          exact
            BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain_pulled_mem
              (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ z (hUy_sub hz).2
        have hF_holo_BHW :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
          simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
            bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
        have hF_cinv_BHW :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              bvt_F OS lgc n (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
                bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
          intro ╬ø z hz h╬øz
          exact
            bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
              (d := d) OS lgc n ╬ø z
              ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė hz)
              ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė h╬øz)
        have hExtend_cont :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé
              (fun z =>
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z))
              Uy := by
          have hExtend_holo :
              DifferentiableOn Ōäé (BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n))
                (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
            BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              hF_holo_BHW hF_cinv_BHW
          exact
            hExtend_holo.comp_differentiableOn
              (BHW.differentiable_permAct_comp_quarterTurn_symm
                (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
              hPulled_mem
        refine Ō¤©Uy, hUy_open, ?_, hPulled_mem, hExtend_cont, ?_, ?_Ō¤®
        ┬Ę simpa [y0] using hy0Uy
        ┬Ę intro z hz hz_acr
          exact hACR_eq hz
        ┬Ę intro z hz hz_bhw
          -- The pulled BHW representative is definitionally the same
          -- branch-specific `extendF` formula on its pulled branch domain.
          simpa [BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative, BHW.os45PulledRealBranch,
            W╬▓, Q] using
            (BHW.os45PulledRealBranchRepresentative_eq_extendF
              (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z hz_bhw).symm
      ```

      The helper names in this proof are part of the required implementation
      surface.  `BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain_pulled_mem` is the domain
      projection saying that membership in the pulled BHW branch domain means
      the branch-specific argument `W╬▓ z` lies in `ExtendedTube`.
      `BHW.differentiable_permAct_comp_quarterTurn_symm` is the finite
      continuous-linear differentiability of
      `z Ōå” permAct ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)`.  The representative rewrite
      `BHW.os45PulledRealBranchRepresentative_eq_extendF` is definitional
      bookkeeping for `os45BHWBranchRepresentative`; it must not contain any
      source equality, adjacent branch comparison, PET independence, EOW
      output, or final locality.

      There is no generic placeholder structure called
      `OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceInput` in the production route.  The active
      Figure-2-4 suppliers are the two concrete one-branch packet producers,
      and they must build the oriented scalar packet before the neutral
      branch-germ predicate is exposed:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalOrientedSourceGermAt_of_figure24_id
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ŌäĢ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hUfig_source :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hVcl : closure V ŌŖå Ufig) :
          ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)

      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalOrientedSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ŌäĢ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_nonempty : V.Nonempty)
          (hV_connected : IsConnected V)
          (hV_sourcePatch :
            V ŌŖå BHW.os45Figure24SourcePatch (d := d) n i hi)
          (hUfig_source :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hV_swap_ordered_closure :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
              (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žä)
          (hV_orientedPath_closure :
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
              BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField (d := d) n i hi x)
          (hVcl : closure V ŌŖå Ufig) :
          ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)
      ```

      Their proofs are short only after the scalar packet suppliers have done
      the real work.  The identity supplier calls
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_id` at
      `╬▓ = 1` after deriving the base point's identity ACR and pulled-BHW
      domain memberships.  The adjacent supplier first constructs the
      oriented scalar representative, obtains the upstream
      `OS45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq` compact Wick packet for the selected
      Figure-2-4 patch, and combines that compact packet with
      the closure-level oriented path field `hV_orientedPath_closure` through
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq`.
      Only that seed/path packet is then passed to
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_adjacent`, together with
      the adjacent ACR/pulled-domain memberships and the quarter-turn scalar
      membership supplied on the closure by
      `hPath.quarterTurn_mem_closure`.  The supplier finally
      transports the base point back with
      `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq`.  Both suppliers finish
      by calling
      `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_orientedSourceEq`.  If either
      scalar packet supplier is not available, the supplier theorem is not
      Lean-ready; there is no abstract Figure-2-4 input record that may be
      filled trivially.

      Implementation transcript for the adjacent supplier:

      ```lean
      intro x hx
      let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
      let y0 :=
        BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
          (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
      let hRep :=
        BHW.sourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n
      let hCompact :=
        BHW.os45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq_of_OSI45
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi
      have hV_sub_seed :
          let hChart :=
            BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart_of_figure24
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep
          let hSeed :=
            BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hChart hCompact
          V ŌŖå hSeed.V := by
        -- `hSeed.V` is the canonical Figure-2-4 source patch; the theorem
        -- surface carries exactly this inclusion as `hV_sourcePatch`.
        simpa [BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq,
          BHW.os45Figure24SourcePatch_eq_canonical] using hV_sourcePatch
      have hQuarterTurn_mem_closure :
          let hChart :=
            BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart_of_figure24
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep
          let hSeed :=
            BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hChart hCompact
          ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              ((BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
                (BHW.realEmbed
                  (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                    (d := d) (n := n)
                    (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x))) Ōłł hSeed.Wscal :=
        BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedQuarterTurnMem_closure_of_figure24Path
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hCompact
          V hV_open hV_nonempty hV_connected hV_sub_seed
          hV_orientedPath_closure
      let hPath :=
        BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hCompact
          V hV_open hV_nonempty hV_sub_seed
          hQuarterTurn_mem_closure hV_orientedPath_closure
      have hy_acr_swapped :
          BHW.realEmbed
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) Žä
                (fun k => x (Žä k))) Ōłł
            BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä :=
        BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered
          (d := d) (n := n) Žä
          (hV_swap_ordered_closure x hx)
      have hedge_eq :
          BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) Žä
              (fun k => x (Žä k)) = y0 := by
        simpa [Žä, y0] using
          BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
            (d := d) (n := n) i hi 1 x
      have hy_acr :
          BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł
            BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä := by
        simpa [hedge_eq] using hy_acr_swapped
      have hy_bhw :
          BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł
            BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä := by
        exact (hUfig_source.2.2.2.2 x (hVcl hx))
      have hy_scalar :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
            ((BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
              (BHW.realEmbed y0)) Ōłł hPath.seed.Wscal := by
        simpa [y0] using hPath.quarterTurn_mem_closure x hx
      have hScalar :
          BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket
            (d := d) OS lgc n Žä y0 :=
        BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_adjacent
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hPath y0
          hy_acr hy_bhw hy_scalar
      simpa [Žä, y0] using
        BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_orientedSourceEq
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n Žä y0 hScalar
      ```

      The identity supplier is the same construction with no compact Wick
      seed.  For `x Ōłł closure V`, set `y0 := os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x`,
      obtain `hy_acr` from
      `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered 1
      (hV_ordered_closure x hx)`, obtain `hy_bhw` from the identity pulled
      branch field in `hUfig_source` and `hVcl hx`, call
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_id` with the same
      `hRep`, and finally call
      `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_orientedSourceEq`.  No
      adjacent equality, EOW output, or PET branch-independence theorem is
      used in either supplier.

      The two oriented packet suppliers themselves must be local source-input
      theorems, not consequences of the downstream branch-germ predicate.  In
      both cases the chart neighborhood is pulled back from an ambient open
      representative of a relatively open oriented scalar set, then
      intersected with the two branch domains:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram_one
          (d n : Nat)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :
          BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) G = G := by
        apply BHW.SourceOrientedGramData.ext
        ┬Ę simpa [BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram] using
            BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one n G.gram
        ┬Ę funext ╬╣
          have h╬╣ :
              ╬╣.trans (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)).toEmbedding = ╬╣ := by
            ext a
            simp
          simp [BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram, h╬╣]

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain_one_eq
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) =
            BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n := by
        ext G
        simp [BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain,
          BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram_one]

      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_id
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (y0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hy_acr :
            BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł
              BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hy_bhw :
            BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) :
          BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket
            (d := d) OS lgc n (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) y0
      ```

      Proof transcript for the identity supplier.  Choose
      `Wscal := hRep.U`.  Destructure `hRep.U_relOpen` as
      `Ō¤©O, hO_open, hU_eq_relŌ¤®`.  Let
      `scalarMap z :=
        BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (Q.symm z)`, and define

      ```lean
      let Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
        (scalarMap Ōü╗┬╣' O) Ōł®
          BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) Ōł®
          BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
      ```

      The set `Uy` is open by
      `BHW.continuous_sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant`,
      continuity of `Q.symm`, `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain`, and
      `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`.  The base point belongs to
      `Uy`: `hy_acr` unfolds to
      `Q.symm (realEmbed y0) Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n`, hence the oriented
      scalar value lies in `hRep.U` by `hRep.U_eq` and
      `BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube`; `hU_eq_rel` gives membership in
      `O`, while `hy_acr` and `hy_bhw` give the two branch-domain fields.
      For arbitrary `z Ōłł Uy`, the same ACR-domain argument puts
      `Q.symm z` in the forward tube, hence
      `scalarMap z Ōłł hRep.U = Wscal`.  The double-domain field follows from
      `BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain_one_eq` and `hRep.U_eq`.
      The equality field is the identity simplification:

      ```lean
      have hEq_id :
          Set.EqOn
            (fun G =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) G))
            hRep.Phi
            hRep.U := by
        intro G hG
        simp [BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram_one]
      ```

      The adjacent supplier consumes the oriented `S'_n` seed/path package,
      because that package is the first object that knows both the adjacent
      oriented double domain and the Figure-2-4 path provenance.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_adjacent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (hPath :
            BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSeedFigure24Path
              (d := d) n i hi hRep)
          (y0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hy_acr :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł
              BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä)
          (hy_bhw :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä)
          (hy_scalar :
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0)) Ōłł hPath.seed.Wscal) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket
            (d := d) OS lgc n Žä y0
      ```

      Proof transcript for the adjacent supplier.  Set
      `Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®`, `Wscal := hPath.seed.Wscal`, and
      destruct `hPath.seed.Wscal_relOpen` as
      `Ō¤©O, hO_open, hW_eq_relŌ¤®`.  Define `scalarMap` and `Uy` exactly as in
      the identity proof, but with branch label `Žä`.  The open-neighborhood
      proof is identical.  The base-point scalar membership in `O` is obtained
      from `hy_scalar` and `hW_eq_rel`; the two domain memberships are
      `hy_acr` and `hy_bhw`.

      For `z Ōłł Uy`, `scalarMap z Ōłł Wscal` follows from the ambient open
      membership in `O` plus the automatic variety membership of
      `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant`; it does not use the branch-domain
      hypotheses.  The double-domain field is exactly the seed field, after
      rewriting the stored permutation:

      ```lean
      have hW_double :
          Wscal ŌŖå
            BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n Žä.symm := by
        intro G hG
        have hG' :
            G Ōłł
              BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n hPath.seed.Žä :=
          hPath.seed.Wscal_double hG
        simpa [Žä, hPath.seed.Žä_eq] using hG'
      ```

      The equality field is the corridor equality stored in `hPath`, again
      rewritten through the fact that the adjacent transposition is its own
      inverse:

      ```lean
      have hEq_adj :
          Set.EqOn
            (fun G =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n Žä.symm G))
            hRep.Phi
            Wscal := by
        intro G hG
        have hcorr := hPath.corridor_eq G hG
        simpa [Žä, hPath.seed.Žä_eq] using hcorr
      ```

      In the Figure-2-4 branch supplier, `hy_scalar` is supplied by
      `hPath.quarterTurn_mem_closure` for the local closure point, after
      rewriting the base point with
      `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq` and the checked
      quarter-turn reindexing identity.  This is the precise place where the
      closure-level oriented path provenance enters the adjacent one-branch
      packet; it must not be replaced by the pure-Gram `hV_figPath_closure`.

      The oriented replacement names are
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_id`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_adjacent`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_sourcePatch`,
      `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_orientedSourceEq`,
      `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalOrientedSourceGermAt_of_figure24_id`,
      `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalOrientedSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent`,
      `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalOrientedSourceEq_of_BHWJost`, and
      `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalOrientedBranchCompatibility_of_BHWJost`.
      Their proofs must replace every use of `sourceMinkowskiGram`,
      `sourcePermuteComplexGram`, `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain`, and
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.branch_eq` by the oriented
      counterparts listed in the source-decision section above.  A mixed proof
      whose source representative is oriented but whose downstream packet is
      `OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket` is mathematically invalid.

      Archived pure-Gram readiness hardening for the one-branch theorem:

      The following one-branch notes are retained only to document the
      conditional ordinary pure-Gram fork and to explain why the old packet
      names cannot be revived as wrappers.  They are not the active theorem-2
      Lean route.  On the strict proper-complex route, replace the scalar
      packet below by `BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket`, replace the
      source coordinates by `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant` and
      `sourcePermuteOrientedGram`, and use the oriented conversion theorems
      displayed immediately above.

      In that archived pure-Gram fork, the displayed
      `os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_oneBranch_sourcePatch` theorem must
      not be implemented as if its source-patch geometry hypotheses alone
      implied value agreement.  Those hypotheses give only:

      - a real source patch `U`;
      - ordered-sector membership, which supplies the ACR-side base point;
      - horizontal membership in the branch-specific pulled BHW domain.

      They are enough to choose neighborhoods and evaluate formulas.  They are
      not enough to identify the ACR branch and the pulled BHW branch.  They
      also do not replace the Jost and ordinary extended-tube hypotheses used
      upstream to produce the scalar packet.  The value-identification part
      must be isolated as the following genuine one-branch source theorem,
      whose value input is the scalar packet:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_sourcePatch
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (U : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hU_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł U ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          (hU_horiz_bhw :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł U ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0U : x0 Ōłł U)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0)) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let W╬▓ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
          Ōłā Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen Uy Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0) Ōłł Uy Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uy -> W╬▓ z Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé
              (fun z =>
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z))
              Uy Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uy ->
              z Ōłł BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ ->
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z) =
                  BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
      ```

      This theorem is not a wrapper: its conclusion is exactly the
      branch-local ACR/BHW equality supplied by `hScalar`, together with the
      domain and holomorphy fields needed by the source germ.  After it is
      proved, `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt` is packaged mechanically by
      adding the definitional BHW-side equality on
      `os45PulledRealBranchDomain ╬▓`.

      For Lean implementation, the arbitrary-`╬▓` theorem above must not be
      exposed as a public theorem from only `hU_open`, `hU_ordered`, and
      `hU_horiz_bhw`.  The display below is the private-style algebraic
      assembly from an already constructed scalar packet; it is not a license
      to prove arbitrary permutation source compatibility from domain geometry.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (y0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ y0) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let W╬▓ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
          Ōłā Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen Uy Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł Uy Ōł¦
            Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Ōł®
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn
              (fun z => BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z))
              (fun z =>
                BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
              Uy
      ```

      The hard mathematical work is producing `hScalar` by the identity or
      adjacent OS I ┬¦4.5/BHW source theorem.  This assembly theorem then
      converts the scalar packet to the ACR/BHW equality by the algebraic
      reduction below.  The broader
      `os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_sourcePatch` theorem adds the
      source-patch domain hypotheses, records differentiability of the common
      representative using `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch_holomorphicOn`, and
      packages the same neighborhood for `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`.

      The source input itself has one algebraic reduction that must be used in
      Lean before any harder Hall-Wightman continuation is attempted.  Once a
      local scalar-product representative has supplied equality of the two
      Gram arguments, the OS45 equality is just calculation:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
          (hUy_acr :
            Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          (hUy_bhw :
            Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          (hGram :
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uy ->
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╬▓.symm
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z))) =
                hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z))) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let W╬▓ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
          Set.EqOn
            (fun z => BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z))
            (fun z =>
              BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
            Uy
      ```

      Proof transcript for this reduction:

      1. For `z Ōłł Uy`, put `u := Q.symm z` and `w := W╬▓ z`.
         Unfolding `BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain`, `hUy_acr` gives
         `hu_ft : u Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n`; unfolding
         `BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain`, `hUy_bhw` gives
         `hw_et : w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n`.
      2. Use `(hRep.branch_eq w hw).symm` to rewrite
         `BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w` as
         `hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)`.
      3. Use `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm` to rewrite
         `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w` as
         `BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╬▓.symm
           (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u)`, then use `hGram z hz` to replace
         this scalar value by
         `hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u)`.
      4. Set `hu_et := BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hu_ft`.  Use
         `hRep.branch_eq u hu_et` to rewrite the scalar value as
         `BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u`.
      5. Use `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` to rewrite
         `BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u` as `bvt_F OS lgc n u`.  The
         current production theorem consumes `bvt_F_holomorphic`, transported
         to `BHW.ForwardTube`, and
         `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`; the stronger checked
         `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` remains the input for
         the Hall-Wightman same-source-Gram branch law, not for this
         forward-tube simplification.
      6. Unfold `BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative`.  It is
         `bvt_F OS lgc n w`; the total permutation field
         `bvt_F_perm OS lgc n ╬▓.symm u` gives
         `bvt_F OS lgc n w = bvt_F OS lgc n u`, so the final equality is this
         equality in the reverse direction.

      Lean-oriented calculation:

      ```lean
      intro z hz
      let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
      let u : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé := Q.symm z
      let w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm u
      have hu_ft : u Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
        simpa [BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain, Q, u] using hUy_acr hz
      have hw_et : w Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        simpa [BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain, Q, u, w] using hUy_bhw hz
      have hu_et : u Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
        BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hu_ft
      have hbranch_w :
          BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w =
            hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :=
        (hRep.branch_eq w hw_et).symm
      have hgram_w :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w =
            BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╬▓.symm
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u) := by
        simpa [w, BHW.permAct] using
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓.symm u
      have hbranch_u :
          hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u) =
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u :=
        hRep.branch_eq u hu_et
      have hF_holo_BHW :
          DifferentiableOn Ōäé (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
        simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
          bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
      have hF_restricted_BHW :
          ŌłĆ (╬ø : RestrictedLorentzGroup d)
            (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
            bvt_F OS lgc n
              (fun k ╬╝ => Ōłæ ╬Į, (╬ø.val.val ╬╝ ╬Į : Ōäé) * z k ╬Į) =
            bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
        intro ╬ø z hz
        exact bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
          (d := d) OS lgc n ╬ø z
          ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė hz)
      have hext_u :
          BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u = bvt_F OS lgc n u :=
        BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
          hF_holo_BHW hF_restricted_BHW u hu_ft
      have hperm_u :
          bvt_F OS lgc n w = bvt_F OS lgc n u := by
        simpa [w, BHW.permAct] using bvt_F_perm OS lgc n ╬▓.symm u
      calc
        BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w
            = hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) := hbranch_w
        _ = hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╬▓.symm
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u)) := by rw [hgram_w]
        _ = hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n u) := by
          simpa [Q, u] using hGram z hz
        _ = BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u := hbranch_u
        _ = bvt_F OS lgc n u := hext_u
        _ = BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z := by
          simpa [BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative, Q, u, w] using hperm_u.symm
      ```

      Thus the only hard part of `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput`
      is not an OS45 coordinate calculation: it is the local
      Hall-Wightman scalar equality `hGram`.  That equality must be supplied
      from the one-branch OS I ┬¦4.5 / Streater-Wightman-Hall-Wightman source
      theorem on the selected Figure-2-4 real environment.  It must not be
      replaced by the later global PET branch-independence theorem or by the
      downstream real-edge equality extracted from an
      `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`.

      Do not try to prove `hGram` from `SourceScalarRepresentativeData` alone.
      That data only says that the ordinary extended-tube branch is represented
      by `Phi` on `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`; it does not assert any
      permutation invariance of `Phi`.  On this archived pure-Gram fork, the
      missing input is a local Hall-Wightman theorem for the symmetric `S'_n`
      datum producing the packet `BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket`.  There
      is no public arbitrary-`╬▓` theorem that produces this packet from the
      five source-domain hypotheses alone.  The only non-wrapper pure-Gram
      surfaces would be:

      - `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id`, the specialization
        `╬▓ = 1`;
      - `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`, the
        specialization `╬▓ = Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®`;
      - at most a private assembly helper
        `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_of_scalarCorridor`, whose hypotheses
        already include the appropriate scalar corridor and seed equality.

      These two public pure-Gram surfaces would therefore have to be written
      explicitly on that separate fork.  The identity source input is the
      harmless normalization case:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hV_ET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_horiz_id :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V) :
          let ╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := 1
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0
          BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
            (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ y0
      ```

      Its proof is only the identity simplification **after** the pure-Gram
      scalar-source fork has been approved and proved.  Obtain `hRep` from
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` only in that fork.  On the
      active theorem-2 route, replace this theorem surface by the oriented
      one-branch packet before implementation.  On the pure-Gram fork, use
      `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered` and
      `hV_horiz_id x0 hx0V` to define
      `Uy` as the finite intersection of the two open branch domains and the
      preimage of `hRep.U` under
      `z Ōå” sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)`.  Take
      `Wscal := hRep.U`, using `hRep.U_relOpen`.  The base point belongs to
      `Uy` because the ordered hypothesis puts `Q.symm (realEmbed y0)` in the
      forward tube, hence in the extended tube, and `hV_horiz_id` puts the
      branch-undone point in the pulled BHW domain.  Since `╬▓ = 1`,
      `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╬▓.symm` is reduced by the named
      helper
      `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq`; this is a `simp`
      consequence of the actual definition and
      `BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one`, not a definitional equality to be
      left implicit.  The equality field is then `rfl` after
      simp-normalizing `╬▓` and `sourcePermuteComplexGram_one`; no
      connectedness, path, or adjacent source theorem is used in the identity
      case.

      Lean normalization used in the identity supplier:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) =
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
        ext Z
        simp [BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain,
          BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one]

      -- Inside `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id`.
      let ╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := 1
      have hDouble_one :
          BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) =
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n :=
        BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq (d := d) (n := n)
      have hW_double :
          hRep.U ŌŖå
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╬▓.symm := by
        intro Z hZU
        have hZU' : Z Ōłł BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
          simpa [hRep.U_eq] using hZU
        have hZdouble :
            Z Ōłł BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) := by
          simpa [hDouble_one] using hZU'
        simpa [╬▓] using hZdouble
      have hEq_id :
          Set.EqOn
            (fun Z =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╬▓.symm Z))
            hRep.Phi
            hRep.U := by
        intro Z hZU
        simp [╬▓, BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one]
      ```

      The adjacent source input is the genuine OS I ┬¦4.5 source theorem.  It
      must carry the pointwise Figure-2-4 path field `hfig_x0`; without that
      field the quarter-turn scalar point is not connected to the `S'_n` seed
      inside the adjacent double scalar domain.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žä)
          (hV_horiz_swap :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V)
          (hfig_x0 :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            let y0 :=
              BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x0
            Ōłā (╬ō ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              Continuous ╬ō Ōł¦
              Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
              ╬ō (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) Ōł¦
              ╬ō (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ t, ╬ō t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ t,
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                  BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t)))) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x0
          BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
            (d := d) OS lgc n Žä y0
      ```

      Proof transcript for the adjacent surface: obtain `hRep` as in the
      identity case.  Apply
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq` with
      the same selected patch `V`, the swapped ordering field, the adjacent
      lift extended-tube field, and the pointwise path field `hfig_x0`; this
      gives the `S'_n` seed
      equality and the scalar path from the seed to the OS45 quarter-turn
      scalar point.  Apply
      `BHW.os45AdjacentQuarterTurnScalarCorridor_of_figure24` to thicken that
      path inside `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä`; the horizontal
      pulled-domain field is consumed here to put the quarter-turn real edge
      in the BHW adjacent branch domain.  This obtains the connected
      relatively open `Wscal` and the chart neighborhood `Uy`.
      The equality of
      `Z Ōå” hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z)` and `hRep.Phi` on all
      of `Wscal` is then the source-variety identity theorem: both functions
      are holomorphic on the double scalar domain, they agree on the nonempty
      relatively open seed `Wseed ŌŖå Wscal`, and `Wscal` is connected.  This is
      the only analytic continuation step in the adjacent source supplier.
      It is not local EOW and it does not mention `bvt_W`.

      There is no route permission to prove an arbitrary-permutation public
      theorem here until the same `S'_n` seed and BHW corridor have been
      supplied for arbitrary `╬▓`.

      Proof transcript for the two active source inputs:

      1. Obtain `hRep` from
         `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`, **only after** the
         full-component pure-Gram decision has been closed.  Its upstream proof
         is then the five-stage ordinary Hall-Wightman source descent:
         `BHW.extendedTube_same_sourceGram_extendF_eq`,
         `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`,
         `BHW.sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent`,
         `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_of_branchLaw`, and
         `BHW.hallWightman_sourceScalarRepresentativeData`, specialized using
         `bvt_F_holomorphic` and
         `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` after the
         `BHW_forwardTube_eq` convention conversion, plus the sourced
         improper/space-inversion invariant needed for full source-Gram
         fibres.  On the active theorem-2 route, this step is replaced by the
         oriented representative and every occurrence
         of `sourceMinkowskiGram`, `sourcePermuteComplexGram`, and
         `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.branch_eq` in this local source input
         is replaced by the oriented analog before any production Lean starts.
      2. Put
         `Z0 := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
           (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0))`.
         The ordered hypothesis gives
         `Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0) Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n`; the horizontal
         BHW hypothesis gives
         `BHW.permAct ╬▓.symm (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0)) Ōłł
           BHW.ExtendedTube d n`.  Hence `Z0` lies in the ordinary scalar
         domain and `sourcePermuteComplexGram n ╬▓.symm Z0` lies in it as well,
         after rewriting by `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`.
      3. For `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id`, set `╬▓ = 1`.
         Use `Wscal := hRep.U`; the packet requires relative openness and
         equality on `Wscal`, not connectedness or a smaller scalar
         corridor.  The chart neighborhood `Uy` is the open intersection of
         the two branch domains with the preimage of this `Wscal` under
         `z Ōå” sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)`.  Since
         `sourcePermuteComplexGram n 1 = id`,
         `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq` rewrites
         `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n 1` to the ordinary scalar
         domain, and the equality
         `hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n 1 Z) = hRep.Phi Z` is by
         `simp [BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one]`.
      4. For `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`, set
         `Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®` and apply
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq` to
         obtain the anchored local `S'_n` scalar seed `Wseed`, its seed
         equality, and the Figure-2-4 scalar path from the distinguished
         seed point to the OS45 quarter-turn scalar point, using the pointwise
         `hfig_x0` supplied on the theorem surface.  Then
         apply `BHW.os45AdjacentQuarterTurnScalarCorridor_of_figure24` with
         the double-domain relative-openness proof supplied by
         `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`
         from `hRep.U_relOpen` and `hRep.U_eq`.
      5. In the adjacent case, propagate equality of the two scalar
         representatives
         `Z Ōå” hRep.Phi Z` and
         `Z Ōå” hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z)` from
         `Wseed` to `Wscal` by the source-variety identity theorem.  This is
         not a real-edge equality of the two `extendF` branches, and it does
         not use `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`.
      6. Pull the relatively open scalar neighbourhood back through the
         continuous polynomial map
         `z Ōå” BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)` and intersect with the
         two open branch domains.  Continuity is supplied by
         `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram` and continuity of `Q.symm`; the
         branch-domain openings are
         `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain` and
         `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`.  This produces `Uy` and the
         displayed `hGram` carrier.

      The outputs of
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id` and
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent` feed
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq`, which in turn feeds
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput`.  This ordering keeps the
      scalar source theorem explicit and prevents boundary-functional
      demotion: the final `bvt_W` distribution is not replaced by a local
      scalar functional, and no real-edge adjacent equality is used upstream.

      Lean-ready internal decomposition of
      the two active scalar source inputs:

      **(A) Quarter-turn pullback geometry.**  First choose the OS45 chart
      neighbourhood and prove that its scalar image lies in the adjacent double
      scalar domain.  This step has no function equality and no real
      uniqueness environment.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchQuarterTurnPullbackGeometry_of_sourcePatch
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (U : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hU_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł U ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          (hU_horiz_bhw :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł U ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0U : x0 Ōłł U) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0
          Ōłā Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              IsOpen Uy Ōł¦
              BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł Uy Ōł¦
              Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Ōł®
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uy ->
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z) Ōłł
                  BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n ╬▓.symm)
      ```

      For the identity supplier, the double-domain condition is the
      `╬▓ = 1` specialization of
      `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq` after rewriting
      `╬▓.symm = 1`; it should be discharged by `simp [╬▓,
      BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_one_eq]`.
      For the adjacent supplier, apply the same geometry to the relabelled
      ordered patch `x Ōłś Žä`.  The scalar packet is first produced at the
      identity common edge `y0`; before this geometry theorem is consumed by
      the one-branch source-patch assembly, rewrite the packet's basepoint to
      `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint Žä (x Ōłś Žä)` using
      `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq`, and after packaging
      rewrite the resulting source-germ predicate back to `y0`.  The proof uses
      `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered`, the horizontal BHW
      domain field, `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain`,
      `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`,
      `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`, and
      `BHW.mem_sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_iff_exists_realizations`.

      **(B) Wick-side scalar seed and BHW corridor.**  The seed must not be
      stated as equality on `sourceRealGramComplexify n G` for a real
      Minkowski Gram environment.  At the OS45 horizontal edge,

      ```lean
      let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
      Q.symm
        (BHW.realEmbed
          (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x))
        =
      BHW.os45QuarterTurnConfig (d := d) (n := n)
        (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (╬▓ k)))
      ```

      and the scalar point
      `sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (realEmbed ...))` is generally a
      genuinely complex point of the Hall-Wightman scalar variety.  It is not
      `sourceRealGramComplexify n (sourceRealMinkowskiGram d n x)`.  Therefore
      the old schematic `hSeedPatch` formulation is rejected: real-environment
      uniqueness cannot be applied directly at the quarter-turn scalar point.

      The OS I ┬¦4.5 proof instead supplies two pieces, both before any local
      EOW real-edge equality:

      1. an `S'_n` seed open set in scalar-product coordinates, obtained from
         Euclidean symmetry and the OS-II continuation package; and
      2. a Hall-Wightman/BHW scalar corridor, inside the adjacent double scalar
         domain, connecting that `S'_n` seed to the OS45 quarter-turn scalar
         neighbourhood.

      On the active theorem-2 path this is needed only for the identity branch
      and one adjacent transposition.  Do not promote an arbitrary-`╬▓`
      production theorem unless the same `S'_n` seed and BHW corridor have
      been proved for that `╬▓`.

      The adjacent `S'_n` seed has two internal support packets.  Keeping
      these packets explicit prevents the old real-environment mistake from
      re-entering the proof: first prove equality in a genuine complex
      source-neighborhood, then pass to scalar-product coordinates through the
      checked source-Gram local-image infrastructure.

      The scalarization chart theorem is the precise OS I ┬¦4.5/BHW input that
      relates the OS-II Wick branch functions to the scalar representative
      `hRep.Phi` on `S'_n`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              ŌłĆ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x t Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          let ╬”0 :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé =>
              hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
          let ╬”Žä :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
          Ōłā (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)),
              IsOpen V0 Ōł¦ IsConnected V0 Ōł¦ x0 Ōłł V0 Ōł¦ V0 ŌŖå V Ōł¦
              IsOpen Usrc Ōł¦ IsConnected Usrc Ōł¦ Usrc.Nonempty Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 ->
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł Usrc) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ x : NPointDomain d n,
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł Usrc Ōåö x Ōłł V0) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Usrc ->
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł
                  BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä) Ōł¦
              DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬”0 Usrc Ōł¦
              DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬”Žä Usrc Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 ->
                ╬”0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
                  bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n,
                HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
                tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå V0 ->
                  Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                      ╬”Žä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * Žå x
                    =
                  Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                      bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k =>
                          wickRotatePoint
                            (x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k))) *
                        Žå x)
      ```

      Proof transcript: shrink the selected Jost/ordered patch around the
      specified base point `x0` to one Figure-2-4 `S'_n` source chart `V0`
      with `x0 Ōłł V0`.  The chart's scalar image is contained in the adjacent
      double scalar-product domain.  The identity branch scalarization is the
      ordinary forward-tube case: it uses `hRep.branch_eq`,
      `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube`, `bvt_F_holomorphic`,
      `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`, and the ordered Wick
      membership of `x`.

      **Important adjacent-trace correction.**  The adjacent Wick trace
      `fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Žä k))` is not a forward-tube trace under
      the Figure-2-4 hypotheses.  The local hypothesis
      `hV_swap_ordered` says
      `(fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector Žä`; since
      `EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector Žä` means that a further `Žä`
      relabelling is ordered, and `Žä = Žä.symm`, applying
      `wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector` to this
      hypothesis gives the identity Wick trace `wick x`, not the adjacent raw
      trace `wick (x Ōłś Žä)`.  Therefore the adjacent scalarization must not be
      proved by `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` at `wick (x Ōłś Žä)`, and the
      source chart must not export a field named `wick_tau_forwardTube`.

      The missing adjacent step is a genuine OS-II/BHW scalar-trace theorem,
      but its first usable form is **distributional**, not pointwise.  On the
      Figure-2-4 `S'_n` source chart, the Hall-Wightman source scalar
      representative evaluated at the permuted source Gram has the same
      compact Wick pairings as the OS-II Euclidean adjacent Wick trace.  This
      theorem is not a wrapper and not pointwise permutation symmetry; it is
      the source-backed BHW/Jost comparison that OS I ┬¦4.5 uses before the
      corridor and real-edge EOW stage.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          let ╬”Žä :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
          ŌłĆ Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n,
            HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
            tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0 ->
              Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                  ╬”Žä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * Žå x
                =
              Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                  bvt_F OS lgc n
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Žä k))) * Žå x
      ```

      The tempting pointwise theorem

      ```lean
      ╬”Žä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
        bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Žä k)))
      ```

      must not be used as an input to
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`.  In that
      position it is circular: it already asserts the selected adjacent
      `S'_n` scalarization on the Wick real section, while the purpose of the
      compact-Wick-to-source step is precisely to prove the adjacent `S'_n`
      source equality.  If a pointwise version is later needed, it may only be
      derived after an independent adjacent `S'_n` source theorem supplies
      continuity and boundary identification for the raw adjacent trace; it is
      not part of the seed proof.

      Proof obligations for the distributional theorem:

      1. Use only the OS-II ACR(1) continuation datum selected in
         `bvt_F_acrOne_package`, OS Euclidean permutation symmetry on
         zero-diagonal compact tests, and the Hall-Wightman/BHW source
         representative `hRep`.  Do not invoke the global pointwise theorem
         `bvt_F_perm` as a replacement for the local source theorem.
      2. The source side is the holomorphic function
         `z Ōå” hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
         (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))` on `hChart.Usrc`, with holomorphy
         supplied by
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram`
         and
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram`.
      3. The OS-II trace side is the adjacent Wick trace appearing in
         `os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector`.  The proof
         compares pairings against compact tests supported in `hChart.V0`; it
         does not require, and must not assume, independent pointwise
         continuity of the raw adjacent total function
         `x Ōå” bvt_F OS lgc n (wick (x Ōłś Žä))`.
      4. On the source side, do **not** choose an arbitrary existential
         `╬ö_x` under the integral.  The Figure-2-4 path-stability theorem must
         export the canonical rotated adjacent lift
         `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift hd Žä x t :=
         BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd
           (BHW.permAct Žä (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath x t))`
         with membership in `ExtendedTube` on the selected chart and the
         canonical source-Gram identity.  Then `hRep.branch_eq` rewrites
         `╬”Žä (wick x)` as `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
         (hChart.adjLift x 0)` pointwise on `hChart.V0`; outside `hChart.V0`
