            simpa [zraw, Žä] using
              BHW.os45Figure24_permutedWick_mem_extendedTube_zero
                (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart x hx
          simpa [BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector] using hET
        have hzraw_tau :
            zraw Ōłł BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n Žä := by
          have hwick_ET :
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
            BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube
              (hChart.wick_id_forwardTube x hx)
          simpa [zraw, Žä, BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector,
            BHW.permAct, Equiv.swap_inv] using hwick_ET
        have hbranch :=
          BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalBranchCompatibility_of_BHWJost
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart x hx
            hzraw_id hzraw_tau
        simpa [zraw, Žä, BHW.permAct, Equiv.swap_inv] using hbranch
      ```

      This keeps the adjacent `S'_n` proof local.  The later global
      `SourceDistributionalAdjacentTubeAnchor`/`S''_n` package may reuse the
      same OS-I ┬¦4.5 source mathematics, but it is not an input to this raw
      Figure-2-4 comparison theorem.

      After this downstream source-evaluation theorem is available, the
      left-side raw compact theorem is mechanical:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          ŌłĆ (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
            (_hŽå_comp :
              HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
            (hŽå_supp :
              tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0),
            let ŽåZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) Žå
                hŽå_suppŌ¤®
            let ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) Žä.symm ŽåZ
            Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * Žå x
              =
            OS.S n ŽłZ
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the mechanical left-side theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
          ... := by
        classical
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        intro Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp
        let ŽåZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
            (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
            (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) Žå hŽå_suppŌ¤®
        let ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) Žä.symm ŽåZ
        have hbranch :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł hChart.V0 ->
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                  (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) =
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) :=
          BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_eq_identityWick_of_BHWJost
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
        have hF_holo_BHW :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
          simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
            bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
        have hF_restricted_BHW :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : RestrictedLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              bvt_F OS lgc n
                (fun k ╬╝ => Ōłæ ╬Į, (╬ø.val.val ╬╝ ╬Į : Ōäé) * z k ╬Į) =
              bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
          intro ╬ø z hz
          exact bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
            (d := d) OS lgc n ╬ø z
            ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė hz)
        have hto_identity :
            Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * Žå x
              =
            Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * Žå x := by
          exact integral_congr_ae (by
            filter_upwards with x
            by_cases hx : x Ōłł hChart.V0
            ┬Ę have hwick_FT :
                  (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n :=
                hChart.wick_id_forwardTube x hx
              have hid :
                  BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
                    bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) :=
                BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  hF_holo_BHW hF_restricted_BHW
                  _ hwick_FT
              rw [hbranch x hx, hid]
            ┬Ę have hŽåx : Žå x = 0 := by
                exact
                  (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                    (fun hxSupp => hx (hŽå_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
              simp [hŽåx])
        have hŽåZ :
            OS.S n ŽåZ =
              Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * Žå x := by
          simpa [ŽåZ] using
            bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n ŽåZ
        have hE3 : OS.S n ŽåZ = OS.S n ŽłZ := by
          exact OS.E3_symmetric (n := n) (Žā := Žä.symm) ŽåZ ŽłZ (by
            intro x
            change
              (permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz
                (d := d) (n := n) Žä.symm ŽåZ).1 x =
              ŽåZ.1 (fun i => x (Žä.symm i))
            simp [ŽłZ])
        exact hto_identity.trans (hŽåZ.symm.trans hE3)
      ```

      Once the downstream pointwise source-evaluation theorem and its
      mechanical compact corollary are proved, the displayed comparison theorem
      is the transitive composition of this left-side equality with
      `BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_bvtF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.  If
      both lemmas are kept private in the implementation file, the public
      comparison theorem still exposes the correct compact-support boundary
      content without pretending that the raw adjacent point lies in ACR(1).

      Downstream proof transcript for the raw compact theorem:

      1. Start from the already-established local scalar source equality on
         `hChart.Usrc`.  This equality was obtained from the canonical compact
         theorem and compact-support separation; it is not proved here.
      2. Use `hRep.branch_eq` on the raw adjacent ordinary-extended-tube point
         and on the identity Wick forward-tube point, with the source-Gram
         algebra `sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`, to obtain the pointwise raw
         branch equality on `hChart.V0`.
      3. Under the compact-support hypothesis, replace the raw left integrand
         by the identity Wick integrand on `hChart.V0`, and replace it by zero
         off `hChart.V0`.
      4. Rewrite the identity Wick compact pairing by
         `bvt_euclidean_restriction`; if the theorem is oriented toward the
         permuted test, use `OS.E3_symmetric` for the single zero-diagonal
         compact-test equality `OS.S n ŽåZ = OS.S n ŽłZ`.  This is compact-test
         Schwinger symmetry, not pointwise permutation symmetry of final
         Wightman boundary distributions.
      5. Do **not** prove this comparison by invoking the existing
         `W_analytic_BHW` package for a completed `WightmanFunctions` object:
         that construction consumes `Wfn.locally_commutative`, which is exactly
         the theorem-2 locality output.  The allowed upstream source has
         already been isolated as the canonical-lift compact theorem: Euclidean
         symmetry and ACR(1) branch selection on the identity Wick trace,
         symmetric analytic continuation to the permuted tube,
         Bargmann-Hall-Wightman single-valued continuation, and Jost
         real-environment uniqueness.

      The public raw Schwinger-valued theorem below is then a mechanical
      consequence of this comparison theorem plus the right-side support
      theorem
      `BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_bvtF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.
      Like the comparison theorem, it must **not** take
      `hRep : SourceScalarRepresentativeData ...`; the scalar representative
      enters only in the downstream source-coordinate rewrite.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_permutedWickExtendF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          ŌłĆ (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
            (_hŽå_comp :
              HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
            (hŽå_supp :
              tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0),
            let ŽåZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) Žå
                hŽå_suppŌ¤®
            let ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) Žä.symm ŽåZ
            Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * Žå x
              =
            OS.S n ŽłZ
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the raw Schwinger-valued theorem after the BHW/Jost
      comparison theorem is available:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_permutedWickExtendF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger ... := by
        classical
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        intro Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp
        let ŽåZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
            (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
            (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) Žå hŽå_suppŌ¤®
        let ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) Žä.symm ŽåZ
        have hcompare :
            Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * Žå x
              =
            Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * Žå x :=
          BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_extendF_pairing_eq_bvt_F
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp
        have hbvt :
            Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * Žå x
              =
            OS.S n ŽłZ :=
          BHW.os45SPrime_rawAdjacentWick_bvtF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
            (d := d) OS lgc n i hi hChart.V0
            (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx))
            Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp
        exact hcompare.trans hbvt
      ```

      Thus there is no remaining non-mechanical raw input.  The raw
      Schwinger-valued theorem is a downstream extraction from the source
      equality and the finite Euclidean rewrite; it must not be used to prove
      the source equality, the adjacent trace theorem, or the canonical lift.

      The canonical Figure-2-4 lift pairing theorem is the primitive
      OS-I ┬¦4.5/BHW/Jost compact-boundary theorem.  Like the downstream raw
      comparison and raw Schwinger-valued theorems, it does not take
      `hRep`; the scalar representative enters only in the downstream
      source-pullback rewrite.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          ŌłĆ (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
            (_hŽå_comp :
              HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
            (hŽå_supp :
              tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0),
            let ŽåZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) Žå
                hŽå_suppŌ¤®
            let ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) Žä.symm ŽåZ
            Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x
              =
            OS.S n ŽłZ
      ```

      The two `ExtendedTube` hypotheses are deliberately explicit.  The
      checked Figure-2-4 source patch supplies them, while `hV_jost` records
      the real Jost environment for uniqueness.  The proof must not infer
      `BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n` from the bare local
      `JostSet` predicate unless a separate checked lemma with exactly that
      content is available.  This is what makes the later domain packet's
      field `╬® ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n` honest rather than a hidden
      strengthening.

      Its statement is deliberately about the deterministic canonical
      Figure-2-4 rotated lift, not an arbitrary existence witness.  This is
      the exact compact-support theorem that must be proved from OS-I ┬¦4.5 /
      Bargmann-Hall-Wightman before any adjacent `S'_n` Lean implementation
      starts.  After this theorem is established, the source-pullback, raw
      permuted-Wick, and any convenience canonical aliases are mechanical
      consequences of this theorem plus `hRep.branch_eq`,
      `hChart.adjLift_sourceGram`, and the Lorentz normalization theorem
      above.  The proof of the canonical theorem itself is the source
      boundary gate: it packages OS I equations (4.1), (4.12), and (4.14),
      Euclidean symmetry on the compact test `ŽłZ`, BHW continuation of the
      symmetric adjacent branch, and Jost real-environment uniqueness for the
      selected Figure-2-4 chart.  It may use the deterministic lift geometry
      and Lorentz normalization, but it must not call the raw pointwise branch
      comparison or any theorem derived from the chart-local source equality.
      Do not add a duplicate canonical theorem name; this theorem remains the
      source boundary gate.

      Canonical-lift compact-boundary readiness gate.  Production Lean may
      introduce
      `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger` only after
      all of the following have source proofs in the same dependency
      direction:

      ```lean
      -- A. Compact-test orientation: build the exact zero-diagonal tests.
      let ŽåZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
        Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
          (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
          (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) Žå hŽå_suppŌ¤®
      let ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
        permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) Žä.symm ŽåZ

      -- B. OS-I ┬¦4.5 source data for this deterministic lift, with no scalar
      -- representative or local source equality as input.
      have D :
          BHW.OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData
            hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
            Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp :=
        BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
          hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
          Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp

      -- C. OS-free Jost/Ruelle uniqueness on the connected analytic branch
      -- domain carried by `D.jr`.
      have huniq :=
        BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary
          (d := d) hd n Žå hŽå_comp D.jr

      -- D. The public theorem is only the final integrand rewrite and
      -- compact-support integration.
      have hord :
          Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x
            =
          Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
              D.jr.ordinaryBranch
                (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x := by
        exact integral_congr_ae (by
          filter_upwards with x
          by_cases hx : x Ōłł hChart.V0
          ┬Ę rw [D.ordinary_eq_extendF_on_lift x hx]
          ┬Ę have hŽåx : Žå x = 0 := by
              exact
                (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                  (fun hxSupp => hx (hŽå_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
            simp [hŽåx])
      exact
        hord.trans
          ((by simpa [D.jr_lift_eq] using huniq).trans
            D.adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger)
      ```

      Step B is the single allowed OS-specific source frontier here.  Its
      proof must fill the displayed `D.jr` fields from OS I equations (4.1),
      (4.12), and (4.14), Euclidean symmetry of `ŽłZ`, BHW continuation, and
      the Figure-2-4 real Jost boundary comparison.  Step C is the only
      allowed generic Jost/Ruelle theorem and must be OS-free.  Neither step
      may depend on `SourceScalarRepresentativeData`, the adjacent trace
      theorem, `os45SPrime_figure24LocalSourceEq_of_BHWJost`, the raw
      pointwise comparison, final `bvt_W` locality,
      `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`, or global PET branch independence.
      If any of these dependencies appears in the proof term, the adjacent
      `S'_n` seed package is still not production-Lean-ready.

the honest lower support layer for the compact source-patch theorem.  It stores two holomorphic branches on the same connected hull, their Wick traces, and their real source traces; the checked constructors restrict such a carrier to smaller open source patches, especially the canonical Figure-2-4 source patch.  The checked Wick integrability support proves the identity Wick trace integrable on an ordered source patch and every permuted Wick trace integrable there by `bvt_F_perm`; the checked real-source integrability support proves the corresponding `extendF` traces integrable from extended-tube membership, continuity of real embedding/permutation, and `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn`; the checked canonical specializations discharge these hypotheses on `os45Figure24SourcePatch`.  The checked theorems subtract the branches, rewrite equality of the two compact Wick pairings as zero of the Wick branch-difference pairing under explicit integrability hypotheses, convert the checked OS45 Euclidean-edge pairing theorem into canonical Wick branch-difference zero, upgrade compact-test Wick-trace distributional zero to pointwise zero of `P.difference` on the connected hull by the existing Wick-real-section identity theorem, rewrite this as real-source `P.difference` distributional zero, translate it to the explicit `extendF` source difference, turn zero of that integrated real difference into equality of the compact real source pairings, package that equality into `BHW.OS45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq`, and fold a full adjacent family into both selected and direct source-anchor surfaces.  The exact-source-patch variants leave only the pair carrier on `BHW.os45Figure24SourcePatch` as input; the broader canonical variants leave the pair carrier plus canonical source-patch containment.  The layer does not construct the pair from OS I ┬¦4.5; that carrier producer is the remaining direct BHW/Jost source obligation. |
      | `BHW.OS45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq`, `BHW.os45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq_of_sourcePatchPairing`, `BHW.sourceDistributionalAdjacentTubeAnchor_of_compactWickPairingEq`, `BHW.bvt_F_selectedAdjacentDistributionalJostAnchorData_of_compactWickPairingEq`, and `BHW.bvt_F_distributionalJostAnchor_of_compactWickPairingEq` | Checked in `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45Compact.lean`. | This is a packaging layer after the direct source-patch compact theorem, not a proof of that theorem.  The constructor uses `os45CompactRealPatch_pullbackSchwartz` to move arbitrary `ŽĆ`-labelled compact tests to the canonical source patch; a full adjacent family then produces the existing `SourceDistributionalAdjacentTubeAnchor` by using the Gram images of the unlabelled left source patches as uniqueness environments.  The selected-witness bridge takes the identity-labelled compact patch as the base patch and reuses the same compact equality, so downstream OS-II code can consume either the older `SelectedAdjacentDistributionalJostAnchorData` surface or the direct source anchor.  It supplies ordinary source-distributional anchor data; the oriented adjacent `S'_n` real-seed layer still requires determinant-regular shrinking and oriented source equality. |
      | `BHW.os45CommonBoundary_wickTrace_zero_of_compactPairing_zero` and `BHW.os45CommonBoundary_identity_of_wickTrace_zero` | Proof transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | Genuine analytic extraction from the common-boundary envelope: universal compact Wick branch-difference vanishing gives pointwise zero on the Wick trace by `SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn`, then holomorphic identity on the connected common chart by `BHW.identity_theorem_totally_real_product` after the complex-linear Wick-time chart calculation. |
      | `BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45` | Assembly transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | Field-by-field construction of `JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData`, `jr_lift_eq`, ordinary lift equality on the deterministic lift, and adjacent lift pairing `= OS.S n ŽłZ`, after the domain, ordinary-branch, adjacent-branch, and real-boundary packets above. It no longer carries individual source-to-Schwinger or lift-to-source PET-overlap fields. |
      | `BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary` | OS-free analytic theorem; transcript pinned; production Lean not started. | `SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn`, `BHW.identity_theorem_totally_real_product`, support restriction via `tsupport`, and no OS/theorem-2 inputs. |

      This ledger is intentionally asymmetric.  The checked Euclidean edge
      theorem proves only the compact Wick/Schwinger equality on the real
      ordered edge; it does not create the adjacent analytic branch, the
      connected Jost/Ruelle domain, or the common real-boundary distribution.
      Conversely, the ordinary branch packet is nearly mechanical once the
      domain packet exists, but it cannot be used to bypass the OS-I adjacent
      branch producer.

      Exact checked API for the Euclidean edge theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x Ōłł V, x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (Žü : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (_hV_ordered : ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
            x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žü)
          (_hV_swap_ordered : ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
            (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
              EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                ((Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®).symm * Žü))
          (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
          (hŽå_tsupport :
            tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå V) :
          Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
              bvt_F OS lgc n
                (fun k =>
                  wickRotatePoint
                    (x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k))) * Žå x
            =
          Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
              bvt_F OS lgc n
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * Žå x
      ```

      Thus downstream calls should restrict the support proof to the selected
      `V0` and pass `Žü := 1`.  The ordered input for the swapped configuration
      must be rewritten from the chart's local adjacent transposition
      `Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®` to the theorem's label
      `(Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®).symm * 1` by `Equiv.swap_inv` and
      `mul_one`.  The theorem does not take `hd`, `hV_open`, or a separate
      compact-support hypothesis; compact support enters the later
      distributional separation theorem, not this checked Euclidean edge
      equality.

      The private local helper `integral_perm_eq_self_locality` used in the
      checked proof must not be imported by name.  If a downstream public
      theorem needs the same finite permutation change of variables, use the
      already public theorem
      `integral_perm_eq_self` from
      `WickRotation/SchwingerAxioms.lean`:

      ```lean
      theorem integral_perm_eq_self (Žā : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (h : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) :
          Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n, h (fun i => x (Žā i)) =
          Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n, h x
      ```

      The proof transcript for this gate has one non-mechanical analytic
      compact-boundary input and deterministic Figure-2-4 geometry.  The
      analytic input is not a raw-Wick theorem; it is the OS-I ┬¦4.5 statement
      for the canonical lift itself.  The implementation must not contain an
      `exact ?...` line here.  It must first build the zero-diagonal tests,
      establish the four source subclaims below, then use the private
      `OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData` and the generic
      `jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary` skeleton displayed
      after item 4.  The production proof must establish the following
      subclaims explicitly:

      1. `ŽåZ` is the zero-diagonal compact test supported in `hChart.V0`, and
         `ŽłZ := permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz Žä.symm ŽåZ` is the permuted
         zero-diagonal test.  The exact Euclidean restriction API is

         ```lean
         have hŽł_restrict :
             OS.S n ŽłZ =
               Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                   Žå (fun k => x (Žä k)) := by
           simpa [ŽłZ, ŽåZ, Žä] using
             bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n ŽłZ
         ```

         If the OS-I argument is oriented through `ŽåZ`, insert exactly one
         call to `OS.E3_symmetric (Žā := Žä.symm)` to prove
         `OS.S n ŽåZ = OS.S n ŽłZ`.  This is the only Euclidean permutation
         symmetry used here; it is not `bvt_F_perm` and not pointwise
         Wightman locality.

      2. The OS-I ┬¦4.1 construction identifies the same compact Euclidean
         branch in reduced variables.  Equation (4.1) supplies the
         translation-reduced Schwinger distribution on the ordered positive
         time sector for the selected zero-diagonal test; equation (4.12)
         identifies its Fourier-Laplace transform with the forward-tube
         analytic branch represented in Lean by `bvt_F OS lgc n`; equation
         (4.14) gives the infinitesimal Lorentz-invariance equations for that
         transform.  In Lean this subclaim must not be a new axiom: it must be
         proved from the existing `bvt_F_holomorphic`,
         `bvt_euclidean_restriction`, and the local BHW/Jost infrastructure,
         or be left as the single honest theorem-level production frontier
         only after the exact statement is audited.

      3. Bargmann-Hall-Wightman is then applied to the OS-I branch from step
         2, not to a completed `WightmanFunctions` object.  The BHW conclusion
         is the single-valued complex-Lorentz continuation of this branch to
         the ordinary extended tube.  The deterministic Figure-2-4 geometry
         supplies the point of evaluation:

         ```lean
         have hET :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł hChart.V0 ->
               hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
           fun x hx => hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube x hx 0
         have hLift_cont :
             ContinuousOn
               (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                 BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)))
               hChart.V0 := by
           have hF_holo_BHW :
               DifferentiableOn Ōäé (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                 (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
             simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
               bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
           have hF_cinv_BHW :
               ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
                 (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                 z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
                 BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
                 bvt_F OS lgc n (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
                   bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
             intro ╬ø z hz h╬øz
             exact bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
               (d := d) OS lgc n ╬ø z
               ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė hz)
               ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė h╬øz)
           have hExtend_cont :
               ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n))
                 (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
             (BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
               hF_holo_BHW hF_cinv_BHW).continuousOn
           have hadj0_cont :
               ContinuousOn
                 (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                   hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
                 hChart.V0 := by
             have hpair_cont :
                 ContinuousOn
                   (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                     (x, (0 : unitInterval)))
                   hChart.V0 :=
               continuousOn_id.prod continuousOn_const
             exact hChart.adjLift_continuousOn.comp hpair_cont
               (by
                 intro x hx
                 exact Ō¤©hx, trivialŌ¤®)
           exact hExtend_cont.comp hadj0_cont
             (by
               intro x hx
               exact hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube x hx
                 (0 : unitInterval))
         ```

         This closes the ordinary continuity bookkeeping for the canonical
         lift.  It uses only the checked BHW holomorphy/continuity of
         `extendF` on `ExtendedTube`, the chart field
         `adjLift_continuousOn` ultimately supplied by
         `BHW.continuous_os45Figure24AdjacentLift`, and
         `hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube`.  It is not a substitute boundary
         theorem and it does not call any raw adjacent-Wick comparison.

      4. Jost real-environment uniqueness is applied to compact tests
         supported in the selected Jost patch `hChart.V0`.  The uniqueness
         input is that the BHW continuation from step 3 and the OS-I
         Euclidean branch from steps 1-2 have the same boundary germ on the
         real Jost environment selected by Figure 2-4.  The conclusion is the
         compact pairing equality

         ```lean
         have hcanonical_boundary :
             Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x
               =
             OS.S n ŽłZ := by
           -- OS I ┬¦4.5: equations (4.1), (4.12), (4.14), BHW continuation,
           -- and Jost real-environment uniqueness for this compact test.
           let D :=
             BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45
               (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
               hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
               Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp
           have huniq :=
             by
               have huniq0 :=
                 BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary
                   (d := d) hd n Žå hŽå_comp D.jr
               simpa [D.jr_lift_eq] using huniq0
           have hord :
               Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                   BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                     (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x
                 =
               Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                   D.jr.ordinaryBranch
                     (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x := by
             exact integral_congr_ae (by
               filter_upwards with x
               by_cases hx : x Ōłł hChart.V0
               ┬Ę rw [D.ordinary_eq_extendF_on_lift x hx]
               ┬Ę have hŽåx : Žå x = 0 := by
                   exact
                     (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                       (fun hxSupp => hx (hŽå_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
                 simp [hŽåx])
           exact hord.trans
             (huniq.trans D.adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger)
         ```

         This is the mathematical source boundary gate.  It is a compact
         distributional theorem, not a pointwise theorem about
         `permAct Žä (wick x)`, and it must not call the local scalar source
         equality, any raw adjacent-Wick theorem, final `bvt_W` locality,
         `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`, or global PET branch independence.
         If a private lemma is introduced for this line, its statement must be
         the compact equality above with the same hypotheses; a forwarding
         wrapper with weaker provenance is not allowed.

         Jost source audit for this line.  The local scan of
         `references/general-theory-of-quantized-fields.pdf`, PDF page `83`
         (printed pages `150`--`151`), shows that the theorem OS I cites here
         is the Ruelle/Jost uniqueness theorem for two analytic branches in
         difference variables.  In Lean-facing terms, it is used only in the
         following specialized form.  Let `W` be the ordinary BHW continuation
         of the OS-I branch on the ordinary extended tube, and let `WŽä` be the
         adjacent branch obtained from OS I equations (4.1), (4.12), and
         (4.14), Euclidean symmetry of the compact zero-diagonal test, and BHW
         continuation on the `Žä`-permuted tube.  Both branches are
         single-valued and `L_+(Ōäé)`-invariant on their respective extended-tube
         domains.  If their boundary distributions agree on the real Jost
         neighborhood selected by Figure 2-4, then Jost/Ruelle uniqueness
         identifies the two holomorphic branches on the connected component of
         the intersection of those domains containing that real neighborhood.
         Evaluating this branch identity on the deterministic canonical lift
         and then taking the compact boundary value gives the displayed
         equality.  The dimension hypothesis used here is exactly `2 <= d`:
         spacetime dimension is `d + 1 > 2`, so the generalized Jost theorem's
         real-intersection formulation applies.  This is a distributional
         real-environment uniqueness theorem; it does not assert pointwise
         equality of final Wightman boundary distributions and it is not
         Streater-Wightman Theorem 3-6.

         Expanded source transcript for the last line:

         ```lean
         have D :
             BHW.OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp :=
           BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45
             (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
             hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
             Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp
         -- The producer above is the source proof, not a wrapper:
         -- * use (4.1) to put the compact zero-diagonal Schwinger test in
         --   the ordered OS-I difference-variable branch;
         -- * use (4.12) to identify its Fourier-Laplace transform with
         --   the forward-tube analytic branch represented by `bvt_F`;
         -- * use (4.14) and `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`
         --   to obtain the Lorentz-invariant analytic branch needed by BHW;
         -- * apply BHW to obtain the single-valued adjacent extended-tube
         --   branch for the deterministic Figure-2-4 lift;
         -- * use the Figure-2-4 real Jost neighborhood and
         --   `OS.E3_symmetric` for the compact test `ŽłZ` to prove the
         --   common real-boundary distribution input for Jost/Ruelle
         --   uniqueness.
         -- It must fill `D.jr.ordinary_holo`, `D.jr.adjacent_holo`,
         -- `D.jr.ordinary_lorentzInvariant`,
         -- `D.jr.adjacent_lorentzInvariant`,
         -- `D.jr.jostPatch_realEmbed_mem`, `D.jr.realBoundary_eq`,
         -- `D.jr.lift_mem_of_support`, `D.jr_lift_eq`, and
         -- `D.adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.
         -- This theorem may be kept as the single honest frontier, but it may
         -- not be replaced by a public theorem that simply repackages the
         -- compact conclusion below.
         have huniq :
             Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.jr.ordinaryBranch
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x
               =
             Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.jr.adjacentBranch
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x :=
           by
             have huniq0 :=
               BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary
                 (d := d) hd n Žå hŽå_comp D.jr
             simpa [D.jr_lift_eq] using huniq0
         have hord :
             Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x
               =
             Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.jr.ordinaryBranch
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * Žå x := by
           exact integral_congr_ae (by
             filter_upwards with x
             by_cases hx : x Ōłł hChart.V0
             ┬Ę rw [D.ordinary_eq_extendF_on_lift x hx]
             ┬Ę have hŽåx : Žå x = 0 := by
                 exact
                   (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                     (fun hxSupp => hx (hŽå_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
               simp [hŽåx])
         exact hord.trans
           (huniq.trans D.adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger)
         ```

         The displayed `OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData` and
         `jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary` names are proof-doc
         labels for genuine source content.  If they are introduced in Lean,
         they must be private implementation carriers or standard
         Jost/Ruelle analytic theorems with the fields shown here; they must
         not be public wrappers around
         `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.

         The implementation-level shape of these labels is now fixed.  The
         OS-I ┬¦4.5 source proof may use the following private data carrier to
         remember the analytic branches before the compact equality is
         extracted.  This carrier is not a substitute for the canonical-lift
         theorem: its producer is exactly the source proof from equations
         (4.1), (4.12), and (4.14), Euclidean symmetry of the compact
         zero-diagonal test, Bargmann-Hall-Wightman continuation, and the
         Figure-2-4 Jost real-boundary comparison.

         ```lean
         /-- OS-free compact Jost/Ruelle uniqueness input.  This is the
         genuine generic analytic theorem surface: it mentions only the two
         analytic branches, their common real Jost boundary distribution, and
         the compact lift against which the resulting branch identity is
         integrated.  It carries no OS axioms, no scalar representative, no
         PET/EOW/locality term, and no `bvt_W`. -/
         structure BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (n : Nat)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé)) where
           ╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
           ╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®
           ╬®_connected : IsConnected ╬®
           lift : NPointDomain d n -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
           lift_mem_of_support :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
               lift x Ōłł ╬®
           jostPatch : Set (NPointDomain d n)
           jostPatch_open : IsOpen jostPatch
           jostPatch_nonempty : jostPatch.Nonempty
           jostPatch_jost :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł jostPatch -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n
           jostPatch_realEmbed_mem :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł jostPatch -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł ╬®
           ordinaryBranch :
             (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé
           adjacentBranch :
             (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé
           ordinary_holo :
             DifferentiableOn Ōäé ordinaryBranch ╬®
           adjacent_holo :
             DifferentiableOn Ōäé adjacentBranch ╬®
           ordinary_lorentzInvariant :
             ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
               (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
               z Ōłł ╬® ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł ╬® ->
                 ordinaryBranch (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
                   ordinaryBranch z
           adjacent_lorentzInvariant :
             ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
               (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
               z Ōłł ╬® ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł ╬® ->
                 adjacentBranch (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
                   adjacentBranch z
           realBoundary_eq :
             ŌłĆ Žć : SchwartzNPoint d n,
               HasCompactSupport (Žć : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
               tsupport (Žć : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå jostPatch ->
                 Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     ordinaryBranch (BHW.realEmbed x) * Žć x
                   =
                 Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     adjacentBranch (BHW.realEmbed x) * Žć x

         theorem BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (n : Nat)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (D :
               BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData hd n Žå hŽå_comp) :
             Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.ordinaryBranch (D.lift x) * Žå x
               =
             Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.adjacentBranch (D.lift x) * Žå x

         theorem BHW.jostRuelle_realPatch_eqOn_of_distributionEq
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (n : Nat)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (D :
               BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData hd n Žå hŽå_comp) :
             Set.EqOn
               (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                 D.ordinaryBranch (BHW.realEmbed x))
               (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                 D.adjacentBranch (BHW.realEmbed x))
               D.jostPatch

         theorem BHW.jostRuelle_branch_eqOn_connectedDomain
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (n : Nat)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (D :
               BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData hd n Žå hŽå_comp) :
             Set.EqOn D.ordinaryBranch D.adjacentBranch D.╬®

         structure BHW.OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0) where
           Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
           ŽåZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n
           ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n
           ŽåZ_def :
             ŽåZ =
               Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                 (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                 (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx))
                 Žå hŽå_suppŌ¤®
           ŽłZ_def :
             ŽłZ =
               permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) Žä.symm ŽåZ
           jr :
             BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData hd n Žå hŽå_comp
           jr_lift_eq :
             ŌłĆ x, jr.lift x =
               hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)
          ordinary_eq_extendF_on_lift :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł hChart.V0 ->
              jr.ordinaryBranch (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) =
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
          adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger :
            Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                jr.adjacentBranch (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) *
                  Žå x
              =
            OS.S n ŽłZ
         ```

         The OS-specific producer must itself be decomposed before Lean.  It
         is acceptable as one private frontier only if its proof term visibly
         contains the following source packets; otherwise it is just a
         renamed compact theorem.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_ordered :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
             (hV_swap_ordered :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                     (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0) :
             BHW.OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp

         /-- Domain and lift geometry for the canonical Figure-2-4 compact
         branch.  `╬®` is the connected ordinary-extended-tube BHW/Jost branch
         domain containing both the real Jost patch and every canonical lift
         point over `tsupport Žå`.  The subset field is load-bearing: it is
         what lets the ordinary branch be the restriction of
         `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)` rather than a second analytic function. -/
         structure BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0) where
           ╬® : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
           ╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®
           ╬®_connected : IsConnected ╬®
           ╬®_sub_extendedTube : ╬® ŌŖå BHW.ExtendedTube d n
           jostPatch_open : IsOpen hChart.V0 := hChart.V0_open
           jostPatch_nonempty : hChart.V0.Nonempty := Ō¤©x0, hChart.x0_memŌ¤®
           jostPatch_jost :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł hChart.V0 -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n :=
             fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)
           realEmbed_mem :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł hChart.V0 -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł ╬®
           lift_mem_of_V0 :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł hChart.V0 ->
               hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) Ōłł ╬®
           lift_mem_of_support :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
               hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) Ōłł ╬® :=
             fun x hx => lift_mem_of_V0 x (hŽå_supp hx)

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_jostRuelleDomainData_of_chart
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0) :
             BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp

         Lean-shaped proof.  This packet should not be treated as an
         additional OS-I source theorem.  The connected analytic domain can
         be chosen to be the ordinary extended tube itself:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_jostRuelleDomainData_of_chart
             ... :
             BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp := by
           refine
             { ╬® := BHW.ExtendedTube d n
               ╬®_open := BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)
               ╬®_connected := BHW.isConnected_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)
               ╬®_sub_extendedTube := by
                 intro z hz
                 exact hz
               realEmbed_mem := ?_
               lift_mem_of_V0 := ?_ }
           ┬Ę intro x hx
             exact hV_ET x (hChart.V0_sub hx)
           ┬Ę intro x hx
             exact hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube x hx (0 : unitInterval)
         ```

         The swapped real-embedding hypothesis `hV_swapET` is intentionally
         still on the surrounding canonical-boundary surface, because the
         adjacent source-patch and compact Figure-2-4 packet need it.  This
         domain constructor itself uses only `hV_ET` and
         `hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube`; it must not infer extended-tube
         membership from bare `JostSet` membership and must not introduce a
         second analytic branch domain by choice.

         /-- Ordinary branch data.  This branch is definitionally the BHW
         ordinary continuation `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)` restricted to
         `D╬®.╬®`; it is not a second analytic function. -/
         structure BHW.OS45Figure24OrdinaryBranchData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0)
             (D╬® :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp) where
           ordinaryBranch :
             (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé :=
             BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
           ordinaryBranch_def :
             ordinaryBranch = BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) := rfl
           ordinary_holo :
             DifferentiableOn Ōäé ordinaryBranch D╬®.╬®
           ordinary_lorentzInvariant :
             ŌłĆ ╬ø z, z Ōłł D╬®.╬® ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł D╬®.╬® ->
                 ordinaryBranch (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
                   ordinaryBranch z
           ordinary_eq_extendF_on_lift :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł hChart.V0 ->
               ordinaryBranch (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) =
                 BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_ordinaryBranchData_of_bvt_F
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0)
             (D╬® :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp) :
             BHW.OS45Figure24OrdinaryBranchData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp D╬®

         /-- Adjacent OS-I branch data.  This is where equations (4.1),
         (4.12), and (4.14) are consumed: (4.1) gives the compact Euclidean
         zero-diagonal boundary functional, (4.12) identifies its
         Fourier-Laplace analytic branch, and (4.14) supplies the Lorentz/BHW
         continuation used on the adjacent Figure-2-4 branch. -/
         structure BHW.OS45Figure24AdjacentBranchData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0)
             (ŽåZ ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (D╬® :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp) where
           adjacentBranch :
             (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé
           adjacent_holo :
             DifferentiableOn Ōäé adjacentBranch D╬®.╬®
           adjacent_lorentzInvariant :
             ŌłĆ ╬ø z, z Ōłł D╬®.╬® ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł D╬®.╬® ->
                 adjacentBranch (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
                   adjacentBranch z
           adjacent_realBoundary_eq_ordinary :
             ŌłĆ Žć : SchwartzNPoint d n,
               HasCompactSupport (Žć : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
               tsupport (Žć : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0 ->
                 Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.realEmbed x) * Žć x
                   =
                 Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     adjacentBranch (BHW.realEmbed x) * Žć x
           adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger :
             Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 adjacentBranch (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) *
                   Žå x
               =
             OS.S n ŽłZ

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBranchData_of_OSI45
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_ordered :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
             (hV_swap_ordered :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                     (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0)
             (ŽåZ ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (hŽåZ_def :
               ŽåZ =
                 Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                   (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                   (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx))
                   Žå hŽå_suppŌ¤®)
             (hŽłZ_def :
               ŽłZ =
                 permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n)
                   (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®).symm ŽåZ)
             (D╬® :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp) :
             BHW.OS45Figure24AdjacentBranchData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp
               ŽåZ ŽłZ D╬®

         Implementation transcript for the adjacent branch packet.  The
         theorem above is Lean-ready only after its proof is decomposed into
         the following real mathematical subclaims; a proof term that simply
         chooses an arbitrary `adjacentBranch` has not formalized OS I ┬¦4.5.

         1. **Zero-diagonal orientation.**  Define
            `Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®`,
            `ŽåZ := Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap ...Ō¤®`,
            and
            `ŽłZ := permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz Žä.symm ŽåZ`.  The checked
            compact Euclidean edge theorem is called with `Žü = 1`; normalize
            its swapped-order hypothesis by `Equiv.swap_inv` and `mul_one`
            before passing `hV_swap_ordered`.  If the proof is oriented
            through `ŽåZ`, use exactly one `OS.E3_symmetric` call to rewrite
            `OS.S n ŽåZ = OS.S n ŽłZ`.
         2. **OS-I initial adjacent tube branch.**  Equations `(4.1)` and
            `(4.12)` identify the zero-diagonal Euclidean edge distribution
            with the boundary value of the corrected OS-II Wightman analytic
            continuation on the adjacent permuted tube.  In the Lean route,
            `(4.12)` is not imported through the false OS-I Lemma 8.8; it is
            the already repaired `bvt_F`/`OSLinearGrowthCondition` object.
            The branch is therefore the OS-I adjacent permuted-tube branch
            selected by the Figure-2-4 orientation, not
            `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value` applied directly at
            `permAct Žä (wick x)`.
         3. **Lorentz/BHW continuation.**  Equation `(4.14)` supplies
            Lorentz covariance of the Wightman distribution, hence the
            complex-Lorentz invariance hypothesis for the adjacent tube
            branch.  The Bargmann-Hall-Wightman continuation then extends
            this adjacent branch single-valuedly along the connected
            Figure-2-4/Jost domain `D╬®.╬®`.  This is the field
            `adjacent_holo` plus `adjacent_lorentzInvariant`; it also
            transports the OS-I real Jost boundary equality against every
            compact test supported in `hChart.V0`, which is stored as
            `adjacent_realBoundary_eq_ordinary`.  This real-boundary field is
            not recoverable from one lift-pairing equality and cannot be
            replaced by the ordinary branch packet or by a scalar
            representative.
         4. **Canonical-lift compact pairing.**  The lift-pairing field
            `adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger` is obtained by
            continuing the compact boundary identity from the adjacent Wick
            edge along the selected Figure-2-4 branch to
            `x Ōå” hChart.adjLift x 0`.  The only compact-test input is the
            checked Euclidean edge equality for `ŽłZ`, with support restricted
            by `hŽå_supp`; no raw pointwise adjacent-Wick comparison, local
            source equality, PET independence, EOW envelope, or final
            `bvt_W` locality may appear in this proof.
         5. **Source-patch PET-overlap branch difference.**  The compact
            packet consumed by the oriented real seed is not an integral of
            raw `extendF` at the half-time common edge, and it is not obtained
            by identifying either real source branch separately with a
            Schwinger functional.  The producer must prove the direct compact
            branch-difference theorem
            `BHW.os45Figure24_sourcePatch_pairing_eq_swappedSourcePatch_of_OSI45`.
            Its proof uses the Wick compact equality from the checked
            Euclidean edge theorem, then the direct OS-I/BHW-Jost
            source-patch difference carrier to continue the zero branch
            difference to the real source trace
            `extendF (realEmbed x) = extendF (realEmbed (x Ōłś Žä))` under the
            same compact test.  It must not call the downstream
            `BHW.os45_adjacent_commonBoundaryEnvelope`.

         Paper-level OS-I transcript for these five subclaims.  The local
         scan of `references/Reconstruction theorem I.pdf` fixes the exact
         role of the three cited equations in the implementation:

         * Equation `(4.1)` is only the ordered-sector passage from the
           Euclidean Schwinger distribution to the translation-reduced
           difference-variable distribution `S_{n-1}`.  It is consumed after
           the compact test has been made zero-diagonal and supported in the
           Figure-2-4 Jost patch.  In Lean this is the checked
           `bvt_euclidean_restriction`/zero-diagonal orientation layer, not a
           pointwise adjacent Wick identity.
         * Equation `(4.12)` is the Fourier-Laplace construction of the
           Wightman momentum distribution from the reduced `S` distribution.
           On this branch it must be read through the OS-II corrected
           `bvt_F`/`OSLinearGrowthCondition` construction; it may not import
           the false OS-I Lemma 8.8 continuation route.  The theorem surface
           therefore has to identify the adjacent permuted-tube branch with
           the corrected `bvt_F` boundary branch selected by the compact test.
         * Equation `(4.14)` is the infinitesimal Lorentz covariance equation
           `X_{ij} W_n = 0`.  Its Lean consumer is a complex-Lorentz
           invariance field for the adjacent analytic branch on the connected
           Figure-2-4/Jost domain, parallel in content to
           `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` for the ordinary
           branch.  It is not a scalar-source equality and not final
           locality.
         * OS-I ┬¦4.5 then says that Euclidean symmetry plus `(4.1)`, `(4.12)`,
           and `(4.14)` gives the symmetric analytic continuation into the
           adjacent/permuted tube, and only after that applies the
           Bargmann-Hall-Wightman theorem to continue single-valuedly to the
           complex Lorentz orbit domain.  The local theorem here is exactly
           the adjacent transposition/compact-test specialization of that
           paragraph.

         A Lean implementation of
         `BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBranchData_of_OSI45` should therefore be
         split through the following private support surfaces, or inline
         proofs with exactly this content:

         ```lean
         /-- The compact OS-I `(4.1)`/`(4.12)` boundary datum for the
         adjacent transposition before BHW continuation.  This carrier is
         proof-local: it records the corrected Fourier-Laplace branch on the
         adjacent permuted forward-tube side and its compact boundary pairing
         with the zero-diagonal test `ŽłZ`. -/
         structure BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0)
             (ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n) where
           Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
           V_ordered :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
               x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1
           V_swap_ordered :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
               (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                 EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žä
           ╬®Žä : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
           ╬®Žä_sector_eq :
             ╬®Žä = BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n Žä
           ╬®Žä_open : IsOpen ╬®Žä
           permutedForward_subset_╬®Žä :
             BHW.PermutedForwardTube d n Žä ŌŖå ╬®Žä
           wick_adjacent_mem :
             ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł hChart.V0 ->
               (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Žä k))) Ōłł ╬®Žä
           branchŽä : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé
           branchŽä_holo : DifferentiableOn Ōäé branchŽä ╬®Žä
           branchŽä_eq_correctedExtendF :
             ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł ╬®Žä ->
               branchŽä z =
                 BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (fun k => z (Žä k))
           branchŽä_boundary_pairing :
             Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 branchŽä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Žä k))) * Žå x
               =
             OS.S n ŽłZ

         /-- The `(4.14)` Lorentz-covariant adjacent branch before its BHW
         extension to the Figure-2-4/Jost domain.  The carrier extends the
         preceding compact boundary branch by adding the complex-Lorentz
         invariance needed by Bargmann-Hall-Wightman. -/
         structure BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0)
             (ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp ŽłZ) where
           branchŽä_lorentzInvariant :
             ŌłĆ (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
               (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
               z Ōłł Dinit.╬®Žä ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z Ōłł Dinit.╬®Žä ->
                 Dinit.branchŽä (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
                   Dinit.branchŽä z

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentInitialBoundary_of_OSI45_4_1_4_12
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ordered :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
             (hV_swap_ordered :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                     (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0)
             (ŽåZ ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (hŽåZ_def :
               ŽåZ =
                 Ō¤©Žå, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                   (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                   (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx))
                   Žå hŽå_suppŌ¤®)
             (hŽłZ_def :
               ŽłZ =
                 permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n)
                   (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®).symm ŽåZ) :
             BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp ŽłZ

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentLorentzInvariant_of_OSI45_4_14
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0)
             (ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBHWContinuation_to_JostDomain
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hŽå_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé))
             (hŽå_supp :
               tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå hChart.V0)
             (ŽåZ ŽłZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp ŽłZ)
             (D╬® :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp)

               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp ŽłZ Dinit) :
             BHW.OS45Figure24AdjacentBranchData

         The two displayed `OS45Adjacent...` data names are proof-local
         carriers for genuine OS-I ┬¦4.5 content.  They must record boundary
         support, the fact that `╬®Žä` is exactly
         `BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n Žä`, the corrected
         Fourier-Laplace `bvt_F` branch after the coordinate relabelling
         `z Ōå” fun k => z (Žä k)`, and the Lorentz covariance needed before BHW
         continuation.  The field `branchŽä_eq_correctedExtendF` is the guard
         against arbitrary analytic data: holomorphy of `branchŽä` must be
         obtained by composing the ordinary corrected `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc
         n)` with the finite coordinate permutation, and the boundary-pairing
         field must then identify that selected branch with the OS-I
         `(4.1)`/`(4.12)` compact distribution.  The final continuation
         theorem must construct the `adjacentBranch` on `D╬®.╬®`, prove its
         holomorphy and invariance there, and transport the compact pairing
         from the adjacent permuted edge to the deterministic canonical lift.
         These carriers must not be implemented as `True`, as arbitrary
         chosen functions, or as wrappers around
         `OS45Figure24AdjacentBranchData`; if no lower-level theorem with this
         content exists, these surfaces remain the adjacent compact source
         frontier.

         Lean-shaped proof transcript for the first two carrier constructors:

         ```lean
