        Usrc_nonempty : Usrc.Nonempty
        wick_mem :
          ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 -> (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł Usrc
        wick_realSection_iff :
          ŌłĆ x : NPointDomain d n,
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł Usrc Ōåö x Ōłł V0
        double_mem :
          ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Usrc ->
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)
        wick_id_forwardTube :
          ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 ->
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n
        adjLift :
          NPointDomain d n -> unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        adjLift_def :
          ŌłĆ x t,
            adjLift x t =
              BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                (d := d) (n := n) hd
                (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®) x t
        adjLift_continuousOn :
          ContinuousOn
            (fun p : NPointDomain d n ├Ś unitInterval =>
              adjLift p.1 p.2)
            (V0 ├Ś╦ó (Set.univ : Set unitInterval))
        adjLift_mem_extendedTube :
          ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 -> ŌłĆ t, adjLift x t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        adjLift_sourceGram :
          ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 -> ŌłĆ t,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (adjLift x t) =
              BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n
                (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t))

      theorem BHW.closure_sourceChart_sub_closure_input
          [NeZero d]
          {hd : 2 <= d}
          {OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d}
          {lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS}
          {n : Nat} {i : Fin n} {hi : i.val + 1 < n}
          {V : Set (NPointDomain d n)} {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          closure hChart.V0 ŌŖå closure V :=
        closure_mono hChart.V0_sub

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              ŌłĆ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x t Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V) :
          BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0
      ```

      The proof of
      `BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage` is pure
      finite-dimensional topology.  Let
      `L := BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n` and
      `z0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`.  By
      `BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse_wickRotateRealConfig`, `L z0 = x0`.
      The set `╬® Ōł® L Ōü╗┬╣' V` is open and contains `z0`, using
      `h╬®_open`, `hV_open.preimage
      (BHW.continuous_wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n)`, and `hx0V`.
      Choose a metric ball around `z0` contained in this intersection by
      `Metric.mem_nhds_iff`; take `Usrc` to be that ball and
      `V0 := {x | wick x Ōłł Usrc}`.  Its openness is the preimage of
      `Metric.isOpen_ball` under `BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig`; its
      connectedness is the separate convexity lemma
      `BHW.isConnected_wickPreimage_ball`; it contains `x0`; and its
      inclusion in `V` follows by applying the left inverse `L` to the
      ball-subset conclusion.  The complex ball `Usrc` is connected by the
      global Mathlib theorem `isConnected_ball`.  No analytic theorem is used
      in this helper.

      With this helper the source chart is constructed as follows.  Put
      `Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®`,
      `z0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`, and
      `╬®double := {z | BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł
      BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä}`.  Openness of `╬®double`
      is the preimage of the relatively open double scalar domain: use
      `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`
      together with `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected` and
      `(BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous`.  Membership
      `z0 Ōłł ╬®double` is the Figure-2-4 source realization at the selected
      base point: the identity Wick trace lies in the ordinary forward tube by
      `hV_ordered x0 hx0V`, while the adjacent permuted source Gram is
      realized by the canonical lift
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift hd Žä x0 0`, whose extended-tube membership
      is supplied by `hV_adjLift_ET` and whose scalar Gram is identified by
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram`.  Apply
      `BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage` to
      `╬®double` and `V`; it returns both `Usrc` and the connected real patch
      `V0 := {x | (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł Usrc}`.  The fields are
      `V0_open`, `V0_connected`, `x0_mem`, `V0_sub`,
      `wick_mem` and `wick_realSection_iff` from the helper, `double_mem`
      from `Usrc ŌŖå ╬®double`, and
      `wick_id_forwardTube` from `V0_sub`, `hV_ordered`, and
      `wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector 1`.  The
      lift fields are filled by setting `adjLift :=
      BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift hd Žä`, using
      `BHW.continuous_os45Figure24AdjacentLift`, restricting
      `hV_adjLift_ET` along `V0_sub`, and applying
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at ... := by
        classical
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        let z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)
        let ╬®double : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :=
          {z | BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä}
        have h╬®_open : IsOpen ╬®double := by
          have hUrel :
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
                (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected
              (d := d) hd n).1
          have hDoubleRel :
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
                (BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä) :=
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain
              (d := d) (n := n) hUrel Žä
          simpa [╬®double] using
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.sourceMinkowskiGram_preimage_open
              (d := d) (n := n) hDoubleRel
        have hz0╬® : z0 Ōłł ╬®double := by
          have hleft :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Ōłł
                BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
            have hz0FT :
                z0 Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
              simpa [z0] using
                wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector
                  (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                  (hV_ordered x0 hx0V)
            exact Ō¤©z0, BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hz0FT, rflŌ¤®
          have hright :
              BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) Ōłł
                BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
            let ╬ö0 :=
              BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x0 (0 : unitInterval)
            exact Ō¤©╬ö0, hV_adjLift_ET x0 hx0V (0 : unitInterval), by
              have hGram :=
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x0 (0 : unitInterval)
              simpa [╬ö0, z0, BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero] using hGramŌ¤®
          exact Ō¤©hleft, hrightŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage
            (d := d) (n := n) h╬®_open hz0╬® hV_open hx0V with
          Ō¤©V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
            hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hz0Usrc,
            hUsrc_sub, hwick_realSection_iffŌ¤®
        have hdouble :
            ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Usrc ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł
                BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä := by
          intro z hz
          exact hUsrc_sub hz
        have hwick_id_FT :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 ->
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
          intro x hx
          exact wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector
            (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
            (hV_ordered x (hV0_sub hx))
        let adjLift :
            NPointDomain d n -> unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä
        have hadj_cont :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun p : NPointDomain d n ├Ś unitInterval => adjLift p.1 p.2)
              (V0 ├Ś╦ó (Set.univ : Set unitInterval)) := by
          exact
            (BHW.continuous_os45Figure24AdjacentLift
              (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä).continuousOn
        have hadj_ET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 -> ŌłĆ t,
              adjLift x t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
          intro x hx t
          exact hV_adjLift_ET x (hV0_sub hx) t
        have hadj_gram :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 -> ŌłĆ t,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (adjLift x t) =
                BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                    (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t)) := by
          intro x hx t
          simpa [adjLift] using
            BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram
              (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x t
          exact
          { V0 := V0, Usrc := Usrc,
            V0_open := hV0_open, V0_connected := hV0_conn,
            x0_mem := hx0V0, V0_sub := hV0_sub,
            Usrc_open := hUsrc_open, Usrc_connected := hUsrc_conn,
            Usrc_nonempty := Ō¤©z0, hz0UsrcŌ¤®,
            wick_mem := by
              intro x hx
              exact (hwick_realSection_iff x).2 hx,
            wick_realSection_iff := hwick_realSection_iff,
            double_mem := by simpa [Žä, ╬®double] using hdouble,
            wick_id_forwardTube := hwick_id_FT,
            adjLift := adjLift,
            adjLift_def := by intro x t; rfl,
            adjLift_continuousOn := hadj_cont,
            adjLift_mem_extendedTube := hadj_ET,
            adjLift_sourceGram := by simpa [Žä] using hadj_gram }
      ```

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24 ... := by
        classical
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        let ╬”0 := fun z => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        let ╬”Žä := fun z =>
          hRep.Phi
            (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
        have hF_holo_BHW :
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
          simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
            bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
        have hF_restricted_BHW :
            ŌłĆ (╬ø : RestrictedLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              z Ōłł BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              bvt_F OS lgc n
                (fun k ╬╝ => Ōłæ ╬Į, (╬ø.val.val ╬╝ ╬Į : Ōäé) * z k ╬Į) =
              bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
          intro ╬ø z hz
          exact bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
            (d := d) OS lgc n ╬ø z
            ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) Ō¢Ė hz)
        -- Figure-2-4 / OS ┬¦4.5 source chart around the chosen base point.
        rcases BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_open hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hV_adjLift_ET hx0V with
          Ō¤©V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
            hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
            hwick_mem, hwick_realSection_iff,
            hdouble, hwick_id_FT,
            adjLift, hadj_def, hadj_cont, hadj_ET, hadj_gramŌ¤®
        have h╬”0_diff : DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬”0 Usrc := by
          have hGramU :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Usrc ŌŖå hRep.U := by
            intro Z hZ
            rcases hZ with Ō¤©z, hz, rflŌ¤®
            rw [hRep.U_eq]
            exact (hdouble z hz).1
          exact BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram
            (d := d) (n := n) hRep.Phi_holomorphic hGramU
        have h╬”Žä_diff : DifferentiableOn Ōäé ╬”Žä Usrc := by
          have hpre :=
            BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram
              (d := d) (n := n) hRep.Phi_holomorphic Žä
          have hGramU :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Usrc ŌŖå
                {Z | BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z Ōłł hRep.U} := by
            intro Z hZ
            rcases hZ with Ō¤©z, hz, rflŌ¤®
            rw [hRep.U_eq]
            exact (hdouble z hz).2
          exact BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram
            (d := d) (n := n) hpre hGramU
        have h╬”0_wick :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 ->
              ╬”0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
          intro x hx
          have hwET : (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
            BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube (hwick_id_FT x hx)
          calc
            ╬”0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))
                = BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
                    simpa [╬”0, hRep.U_eq] using
                      hRep.branch_eq _ hwET
            _ = bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
                    exact BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube n
                      (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                      hF_holo_BHW hF_restricted_BHW
                      _ (hwick_id_FT x hx)
        have h╬”Žä_pairing :
            ŌłĆ Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n,
              HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
              tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå V0 ->
                Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    ╬”Žä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * Žå x
                  =
                Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    bvt_F OS lgc n
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Žä k))) * Žå x := by
          exact
            BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
              hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hRep
              { V0 := V0, Usrc := Usrc,
                V0_open := hV0_open, V0_connected := hV0_conn,
                x0_mem := hx0V0, V0_sub := hV0_sub,
                Usrc_open := hUsrc_open, Usrc_connected := hUsrc_conn,
                Usrc_nonempty := hUsrc_ne, wick_mem := hwick_mem,
                wick_realSection_iff := hwick_realSection_iff,
                double_mem := hdouble,
                wick_id_forwardTube := hwick_id_FT,
                adjLift := adjLift, adjLift_def := hadj_def,
                adjLift_continuousOn := hadj_cont,
                adjLift_mem_extendedTube := hadj_ET,
                adjLift_sourceGram := hadj_gram }
        exact Ō¤©V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
          hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
          hwick_mem, hwick_realSection_iff,
          hdouble, h╬”0_diff, h╬”Žä_diff,
          h╬”0_wick, by simpa [Žä] using h╬”Žä_pairingŌ¤®
      ```

      The source-neighborhood seed theorem is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              ŌłĆ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x t Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          Ōłā (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)),
              IsOpen V0 Ōł¦ IsConnected V0 Ōł¦ x0 Ōłł V0 Ōł¦ V0 ŌŖå V Ōł¦
              IsOpen Usrc Ōł¦ IsConnected Usrc Ōł¦ Usrc.Nonempty Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 ->
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł Usrc) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Usrc ->
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł
                  BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä) Ōł¦
              Set.EqOn
                (fun z =>
                  hRep.Phi
                    (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)))
                (fun z => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
                Usrc
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Apply
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24`, obtaining
         `V0`, `Usrc`, the double-domain field, differentiability of the two
         scalar pullbacks, the identity Wick-section scalarization identity,
         and the adjacent scalar-trace compact-pairing identity.
      2. Invoke the checked compact Wick equality
         `os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector` with
         `Žü = 1` and with tests supported in `V0`.  The checked theorem is in
         the Wick-rotation namespace-free layer and has production signature
         `OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost Žü hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered Žå
         hŽå_tsupport`; it does not take `hd`, `hV_open`, or a separate
         compactness hypothesis.  Since `V0 ŌŖå V`, the
         existing `hV_jost`, `hV_ordered`, and `hV_swap_ordered` hypotheses
         restrict directly, with the swapped-order target normalized by
         `Equiv.swap_inv` and `mul_one`: the theorem asks for
         `((Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®).symm * 1)` while the local
         hypothesis is stated for `Žä`.
      3. Apply
         `SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn`
         on `V0` to recover pointwise equality of the two scalarized
         source pullbacks
         `x Ōå” ╬”0 (wick x)` and `x Ōå” ╬”Žä (wick x)` from
         `h╬”0_diff`, `h╬”Žä_diff`, and
         `BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig` by Mathlib
         `ContinuousOn.comp'` with the explicit `MapsTo` proof
         `fun x hx => hwick_mem x hx`; `ContinuousOn.comp_continuous` is the
         wrong API here because the Wick map is known to land in `Usrc` only
         on `V0`, not globally.  The integral equality for these two
         scalarized pullbacks is the chain
         adjacent source-pairing theorem, checked compact Wick equality, and
         the identity scalarization `h╬”0_wick` rewritten under the integral.
         This step deliberately avoids any continuity assumption for the raw
         adjacent total function
         `x Ōå” bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Žä k)))`.
      4. Apply
         `eqOn_openConnected_of_eqOn_wickRealSection`
         to the connected complex source chart `Usrc`.  The holomorphic
         functions are the scalar pullbacks
         `z Ōå” hRep.Phi (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)` and
         `z Ōå” hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
         (sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))`; their differentiability on `Usrc`
         is supplied by the scalarization chart theorem.

      Lean-shaped pseudocode:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq ... := by
        classical
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        let ╬”0 := fun z =>
          hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        let ╬”Žä := fun z =>
          hRep.Phi
            (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
        rcases BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_open hV_jost
            hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered
            hV_adjLift_ET hx0V hRep with
          Ō¤©V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
            hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
            hwick_mem, hwick_realSection_iff,
            hdouble, h╬”0_diff, h╬”Žä_diff,
            h╬”0_wick, h╬”Žä_pairingŌ¤®
        have hV0_ne : V0.Nonempty := Ō¤©x0, hx0V0Ō¤®
        have hwick_ne :
            Ōłā x : NPointDomain d n,
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł Usrc :=
          Ō¤©x0, hwick_mem x0 hx0V0Ō¤®
        have hwick_eq :
            ŌłĆ x : NPointDomain d n,
              x Ōłł V0 ->
                ╬”Žä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
                  ╬”0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
          have hcont╬”Žä_real :
              ContinuousOn
                (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                  ╬”Žä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) V0 :=
            h╬”Žä_diff.continuousOn.comp'
              (BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig (d := d) (n := n)).continuousOn
              (by
                intro x hx
                exact hwick_mem x hx)
          have hcont╬”0_real :
              ContinuousOn
                (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                  ╬”0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) V0 :=
            h╬”0_diff.continuousOn.comp'
              (BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig (d := d) (n := n)).continuousOn
              (by
                intro x hx
                exact hwick_mem x hx)
          have hpoint :
              Set.EqOn
                (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                  ╬”Žä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)))
                (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                  ╬”0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)))
                V0 := by
            refine
              SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn
                hV0_open hcont╬”Žä_real hcont╬”0_real ?compact_eq
            ┬Ę intro Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp
              calc
                Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    ╬”Žä (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * Žå x
                    =
                  Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    bvt_F OS lgc n
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Žä k))) * Žå x :=
                    h╬”Žä_pairing Žå hŽå_comp hŽå_supp
                _ =
                  Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    bvt_F OS lgc n
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * Žå x :=
                    os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector
                      (d := d) OS lgc n i hi V0
                      (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hV0_sub hx))
                      (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                      (fun x hx => hV_ordered x (hV0_sub hx))
                      (fun x hx => by
                        simpa [Žä, Equiv.swap_inv] using
                          hV_swap_ordered x (hV0_sub hx))
                      Žå hŽå_supp
                _ =
                  Ōł½ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    ╬”0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * Žå x := by
                    exact integral_congr_ae (by
                      filter_upwards with x
                      by_cases hx : x Ōłł V0
                      ┬Ę simpa [h╬”0_wick x hx]
                      ┬Ę have hŽåx : Žå x = 0 := by
                          exact
                            (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                              (fun hxSupp => hx (hŽå_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
                        simp [hŽåx])
          intro x hx
          exact hpoint x hx
        have hEqOnUsrc : Set.EqOn ╬”Žä ╬”0 Usrc :=
          eqOn_openConnected_of_eqOn_wickRealSection
            (d := d) (n := n) Usrc hUsrc_open hUsrc_conn hwick_ne
            ╬”Žä ╬”0 h╬”Žä_diff h╬”0_diff
            (fun x hxU => by
              -- `hxU` is a Wick point in `Usrc`; shrink-chart construction
              -- supplies the corresponding `x Ōłł V0` for the real section used
              -- by the identity theorem.
              have hxV0 : x Ōłł V0 := (hwick_realSection_iff x).1 hxU
              exact hwick_eq x hxV0)
        exact Ō¤©V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
          hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne, hwick_mem,
          hdouble, hEqOnUsrcŌ¤®
      ```

      The final real-section membership bridge used in the last lambda must be
      an explicit field of the source chart: the Wick real section of `Usrc`
      used by `eqOn_openConnected_of_eqOn_wickRealSection` is exactly the
      shrunken `V0`, not an unspecified set of Euclidean configurations.  The
      source-equivalence field is consumed only to prove equality on `Usrc`;
      the exported source equality theorem may keep only the forward
      membership field `hwick_mem`.

      The scalar-image extraction theorem used by the adjacent seed is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular_allArity
          (d n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hreg : BHW.SourceComplexGramRegularAt d n z0)
          {Vsrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hVsrc_open : IsOpen Vsrc)
          (hz0Vsrc : z0 Ōłł Vsrc) :
          Ōłā U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen U Ōł¦ IsConnected U Ōł¦ z0 Ōłł U Ōł¦ U ŌŖå Vsrc Ōł¦
            Ōłā O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 Ōłł O Ōł¦
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n O Ōł¦
              IsConnected O Ōł¦ O.Nonempty Ōł¦
              O ŌŖå BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' U Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ G, G Ōłł O ->
                Ōłā z Ōłł U, BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z = G)
      ```

      Proof transcript: if `d + 1 <= n`, this is the checked theorem
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular`
      with the rank-exact field forgotten.  If `n < d + 1`, first use the
      checked all-arity local image theorem
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular`.
      Since `sourceComplexGramVariety d n =
      sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n` in this easy range by
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace_of_le`,
      pass to full symmetric coordinates with
      `BHW.sourceFullSymCoordMapCLM`,
      `BHW.isOpen_sourceFullSymCoordMap_preimage_of_relOpen_of_le`, and the
      inverse coordinate map used in
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle_easy`; choose a small
      connected coordinate ball inside the relatively open image and push it
      forward by `sourceFullSymCoordMap`.  The resulting `O` is connected,
      relatively open, nonempty, and still contained in the image supplied by
      `sourceComplexGramMap_localRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular`.

      The adjacent scalar seed theorem is then:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_regular_sourcePoint_in_open_neighborhood
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé}
          (hz0U : z0 Ōłł U) :
          Ōłā zreg,
            zreg Ōłł U Ōł¦ BHW.SourceComplexGramRegularAt d n zreg
      ```

      This helper is a one-line use of
      `(BHW.dense_sourceComplexGramRegularAt d n).exists_mem_open hU_open
      Ō¤©z0, hz0UŌ¤®`; it is listed only because both the local image theorem and
      the later path theorem need the returned membership proof.  The exact
      proof has been scratch-checked with `SourceComplexDensity.lean`
      imported:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_regular_sourcePoint_in_open_neighborhood ... := by
        rcases (BHW.dense_sourceComplexGramRegularAt d n).exists_mem_open
            hU_open Ō¤©z0, hz0UŌ¤® with
          Ō¤©zreg, hzreg_regular, hzregUŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©zreg, hzregU, hzreg_regularŌ¤®
      ```

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              ŌłĆ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x t Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          Ōłā (Wseed : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé))
            (Gseed : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed Ōł¦
            IsConnected Wseed Ōł¦
            Wseed.Nonempty Ōł¦
            Gseed Ōłł Wseed Ōł¦
            Wseed ŌŖå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn
              (fun Z =>
                hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z))
              hRep.Phi
              Wseed
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Apply
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`, obtaining
         `V0`, `Usrc`, the source-level scalar equality, and the double-domain
         membership of every scalar image point.
      2. Let `zwick0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`.  Since
         `x0 Ōłł V0`, the chart theorem gives `zwick0 Ōłł Usrc`.  Choose a complex
         source-Gram regular point `zreg Ōłł Usrc` using
         `BHW.dense_sourceComplexGramRegularAt` inside a small open ball in
         `Usrc` around `zwick0`; record `Gseed :=
         sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg`.
      3. Apply
         `BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular_allArity`
         to `zreg` inside `Usrc`.  This returns a connected open source ball
         `Ureg ŌŖå Usrc` and a connected nonempty relatively open scalar image
         `Wseed` with `Gseed Ōłł Wseed` and
         `Wseed ŌŖå sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Ureg`.
      4. Since `Ureg ŌŖå Usrc`, the source-level equality from step 1 descends
         to `Wseed`: for `G Ōłł Wseed`, choose `z Ōłł Ureg` with
         `sourceMinkowskiGram d n z = G` and rewrite.  The double-domain
         inclusion of `Wseed` follows from the double-domain field on `Usrc`.
      5. This `Wseed` is the local `S'_n` scalar overlap; it is not the final
         `S''_n` PET branch law and it does not use local EOW, real-edge
         adjacent equality, or final `bvt_W` locality.

      Lean-shaped pseudocode:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq ... := by
        classical
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        rcases BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_open hV_jost
            hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered
            hV_adjLift_ET hx0V hRep with
          Ō¤©V0, Usrc, hV0_open, _hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
            hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
            hwick_mem, hdouble, hEq_srcŌ¤®
        let zwick0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)
        have hzwick0 : zwick0 Ōłł Usrc := hwick_mem x0 hx0V0
        -- Pick a small open ball inside `Usrc` around the Wick point and use
        -- density of regular complex source configurations.
        rcases BHW.exists_regular_sourcePoint_in_open_neighborhood
            (d := d) (n := n) hUsrc_open hzwick0 with
          Ō¤©zreg, hzregUsrc, hzreg_regularŌ¤®
        let Gseed := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg
        rcases
          BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular_allArity
            (d := d) (n := n) hzreg_regular hUsrc_open hzregUsrc with
          Ō¤©Ureg, hUreg_open, hUreg_conn, hzregUreg, hUreg_sub,
            Wseed, hGseedW, hW_rel, hW_conn, hW_ne,
            hW_image, hW_realizeŌ¤®
        have hW_double :
            Wseed ŌŖå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä := by
          intro G hG
          rcases hW_realize G hG with Ō¤©z, hzUreg, rflŌ¤®
          exact hdouble z (hUreg_sub hzUreg)
        have hW_eq :
            Set.EqOn
              (fun Z => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z))
              hRep.Phi
              Wseed := by
          intro G hG
          rcases hW_realize G hG with Ō¤©z, hzUreg, hGzŌ¤®
          have hzUsrc : z Ōłł Usrc := hUreg_sub hzUreg
          have hsrc := hEq_src hzUsrc
          simpa [hGz] using hsrc
        exact Ō¤©Wseed, Gseed, hW_rel, hW_conn, hW_ne,
          hGseedW, hW_double, hW_eqŌ¤®
      ```

      The helper `BHW.exists_regular_sourcePoint_in_open_neighborhood` is only
      the standard application of `BHW.dense_sourceComplexGramRegularAt` to the
      open neighborhood `Usrc`; it may be inlined in Lean.  It must return the
      membership proof `hzregUsrc`, because that proof is used both for the
      local image theorem and later for the path from `zreg` back to the Wick
      source point.  The descent of source equality to `Wseed` has also been
      scratch-checked: `Set.EqOn`'s point argument is implicit, so the Lean
      call is `hEq_src hzUsrc`, not `hEq_src z hzUsrc`; then
      `simpa [hGz]` rewrites the realized Gram point.

      The path theorem needs the same proof in a provenance-preserving form.
      This is an implementation structure, not a second mathematical result:

      ```lean
      structure BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeedWithSourceProvenance
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (x0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) where
        V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n)
        Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)
        zreg : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé
        Wseed : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
        Gseed : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé
        V0_open : IsOpen V0
        V0_connected : IsConnected V0
        x0_mem_V0 : x0 Ōłł V0
        Usrc_open : IsOpen Usrc
        Usrc_connected : IsConnected Usrc
        wick_mem :
          ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V0 -> (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł Usrc
        zreg_mem_Usrc : zreg Ōłł Usrc
        Gseed_def : Gseed = BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg
        Wseed_relOpen :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed
        Wseed_connected : IsConnected Wseed
        Wseed_nonempty : Wseed.Nonempty
        Gseed_mem : Gseed Ōłł Wseed
        Wseed_double :
          Wseed ŌŖå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
            (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)
        Wseed_eq :
          Set.EqOn
            (fun Z =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®) Z))
            hRep.Phi Wseed
        Usrc_double :
          ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Usrc ->
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z Ōłł
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)

      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_with_sourceProvenance
          ... :
          BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeedWithSourceProvenance
            hd OS lgc n i hi V x0 hRep
      ```

      In the eventual Lean file, either return this structure directly and
      derive `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq` by
      projection, or prove the public seed theorem first and keep the same
      local variables in the path proof.  What is forbidden is discarding
      `Usrc`, `zreg`, `hwick_mem`, and `zreg_mem_Usrc` and then trying to
      recover them from the scalar-only seed.

      Since `Žä` is an adjacent transposition, Lean normalizes the inverse by
      `Equiv.swap_inv`; after `change ŽäŌü╗┬╣ = Žä` and `simpa [Žä]`, this is the
      exact equality `Žä.symm = Žä` needed by the packet whose branch label is
      `╬▓ = Žä`.  Thus the scalar function in this theorem,
      `Z Ōå” hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z)`, is exactly the
      downstream `╬▓.symm` function when the one-branch theorem is instantiated
      at `╬▓ = Žä`.

      The seed used by the corridor must carry its Figure-2-4 path
      provenance.  This provenance is not a bare scalar-domain assertion.
      First isolate the configuration-level realization path supplied by the
      Streater-Wightman Figure-2-4 geometry, then obtain the scalar path by
      applying `sourceMinkowskiGram`.

      The realization-level theorem is the genuine paper-geometry input, and
      it must be produced by the **selected** Figure-2-4 source patch.  It is
      not a theorem about an arbitrary later real-open `V` satisfying only the
      broad Jost, ordered-sector, and horizontal pulled-domain fields.  Those
      fields give the endpoint domain memberships; by themselves they do not
      give a continuous ordinary-extended-tube realization of the adjacent
      permuted Gram point along the whole Wick-to-quarter-turn deformation.

      The Lean producer is therefore a path-stable refinement of the source
      selector.  It must export two related fields.  First, on the open chart
      `V`, it exports the deterministic canonical lift membership
      `hV_adjLift_ET` used by the distributional source-pairing theorem.  This
      field is the restriction of the compact-open `Upath` theorem for
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift`; it is not the same as choosing a
      pointwise witness.  Second, for the later corridor it exports the
      following closure-level path field:

      ```lean
      hV_figPath_closure :
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
        ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł closure V ->
          let y :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x
          Ōłā (╬ō ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            Continuous ╬ō Ōł¦
            Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
            ╬ō (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōł¦
            ╬ō (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ t, ╬ō t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ t,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t)))
      ```

      This field is not a wrapper assumption.  Its proof belongs to the
      Streater-Wightman Figure-2-4 selector:

      1. Start with the explicit adjacent two-plane real witness already
         formalized in
         `ComplexLieGroups/AdjacentOverlapWitness.lean`.  In current Lean this
         is the `swapWitnessReal`/`swapWitnessRealSwapped` model, the
         `3-4-5` spatial rotation `spatialRotCLG12`, and the checked endpoint
         theorem `BHW.adjacent_overlap_real_jost_witness_exists`.
      2. Let
         `c t := ((t : ŌäØ) / 2 : Ōäé) +
           (((1 : ŌäØ) - (t : ŌäØ) / 2 : ŌäØ) : Ōäé) * Complex.I`
         on `unitInterval`.  For `x Ōłł V`, set
         `╬ō t k 0 := c t * (x k 0 : Ōäé)` and
         `╬ō t k ╬╝ := (x k ╬╝ : Ōäé)` for `╬╝ ŌēĀ 0`.  Then `╬ō 0` is the Wick
         configuration and `╬ō 1 = Q.symm (realEmbed
         (os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x))` by
         `os45QuarterTurnCLE_symm_apply`.  The identity ordered-sector field
         proves `╬ō t Ōłł ForwardTube d n`, hence `╬ō t Ōłł ExtendedTube d n`,
         because the imaginary time gaps are the positive scalar
         `(1 - t/2)` times the ordered Euclidean time gaps and the spatial
         imaginary parts are zero.
      3. The adjacent realization is not `fun k => ╬ō t (Žä k)`.  Instead use
         the Figure-2-4 two-plane Lorentz-rotation witness from the checked
         adjacent-overlap proof.  In Lean terms, promote the private rotation
         data behind `realEmbed_swapWitnessRealSwapped_eq_action_rot` to a
         public support theorem for the whole interpolation.  The support
         theorem must state the actual realization:

         ```lean
         Ōłā ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
           Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
           (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
           ŌłĆ t,
             BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
               BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                 (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t))
         ```

         It may internally represent `╬ö t` as a complex-Lorentz transform of a
         pre-rotated adjacent interpolation, but the exported conclusion is
         ordinary extended-tube membership, not bare membership of the
         relabelled path in the forward tube.
      4. The previous step is a compact-open argument, not a new analytic
         theorem.  After the bounded ordered perturbation has selected
         `xseed` inside the Figure-2-4 real environment, the explicit
         two-plane inequalities are strict for the displayed one-parameter
         interpolation at `xseed`.  The coordinate expressions are continuous
         in `(x,t)`, and `unitInterval` is compact; shrink the real source
         environment around `xseed` so the same extended-tube realization
         certificate remains valid uniformly for all `t`.  This is why the
         path field belongs to the selected source patch `V`, not to an
         arbitrary open set with similar endpoint memberships.
      5. The Gram identity is exactly Lorentz invariance plus permutation of
         labels:
         `sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction`,
         `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`, and `Equiv.swap_inv` to normalize
         `Žä.symm` to `Žä` for adjacent swaps.
         This is the point where the realization proof transports the kernel
         through the local coordinates; no function values, no local EOW
         conclusion, and no final `bvt_W` distribution appear.

      Lean-ready subpacket for `hV_figPath_closure`:

      ```lean
      def BHW.os45Figure24TimeCoeff (t : unitInterval) : Ōäé :=
        (((t : ŌäØ) / 2 : ŌäØ) : Ōäé) +
          (((1 : ŌäØ) - (t : ŌäØ) / 2 : ŌäØ) : Ōäé) * Complex.I

      def BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
          (x : NPointDomain d n)
          (t : unitInterval) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun k ╬╝ =>
          if ╬╝ = 0 then BHW.os45Figure24TimeCoeff t * (x k 0 : Ōäé)
          else (x k ╬╝ : Ōäé)

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero
          (x : NPointDomain d n) :
          BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x
              (0 : unitInterval) =
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_one
          (x : NPointDomain d n) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
          BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x
              (1 : unitInterval) =
            Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y)

      theorem BHW.continuous_os45Figure24IdentityPath
          (x : NPointDomain d n) :
          Continuous (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x)

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_of_time_zero
          (x : NPointDomain d n)
          (hx_time0 : ŌłĆ k : Fin n, x k 0 = 0)
          (t : unitInterval) :
          BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t =
            BHW.realEmbed x

      theorem BHW.continuous_os45Figure24IdentityPath_joint :
          Continuous
            (fun p : NPointDomain d n ├Ś unitInterval =>
              BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) p.1 p.2)

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_mem_forwardTube
          {x : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx_ordered :
            x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) :
          ŌłĆ t,
            BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t Ōłł
              BHW.ForwardTube d n

      -- These finite algebraic definitions and the inverse Lorentz action
      -- theorem live in
      -- `ComplexLieGroups/AdjacentOverlapWitness.lean`, not in the OS45
      -- companion file, because the public Figure-2-4 witness export needs
      -- them without creating an import cycle.
      def BHW.figure24Axis1 (hd : 2 <= d) : Fin (d + 1) := Ō¤©1, by omegaŌ¤®

      def BHW.figure24Axis2 (hd : 2 <= d) : Fin (d + 1) := Ō¤©2, by omegaŌ¤®

      def BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
        fun k ╬╝ =>
          if ╬╝ = 0 then z k ╬╝
          else if ╬╝ = BHW.figure24Axis1 hd then
            ((3 / 5 : ŌäØ) : Ōäé) * z k (BHW.figure24Axis1 hd) -
              ((4 / 5 : ŌäØ) : Ōäé) * z k (BHW.figure24Axis2 hd)
          else if ╬╝ = BHW.figure24Axis2 hd then
            ((4 / 5 : ŌäØ) : Ōäé) * z k (BHW.figure24Axis1 hd) +
              ((3 / 5 : ŌäØ) : Ōäé) * z k (BHW.figure24Axis2 hd)
          else z k ╬╝

      theorem BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse
          (hd : 2 <= d) :
          Ōłā ╬øfig : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ŌłĆ z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé,
              BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬øfig
                (BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd z) =
              z

      theorem BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction
          (╬ø : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
              (BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬ø z) =
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z

      theorem BHW.continuous_figure24RotatedIdentityPath
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
          Continuous
            (fun p : NPointDomain d n ├Ś unitInterval =>
              BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                  (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                    (d := d) (n := n) p.1 p.2)))

      theorem BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlaneRotationSupport
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
            Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          Ōłā (xfig xrot : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ŌäØ)
            (╬øfig : ComplexLorentzGroup d),
            (ŌłĆ k : Fin n, xfig k 0 = 0) Ōł¦
            xfig Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed xfig Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed xrot =
              BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
                (BHW.realEmbed (fun k => xfig (Žä k))) Ōł¦
            xrot Ōłł BHW.ForwardJostSet d n
              (Nat.succ_le_of_lt (Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le
                (Nat.zero_lt_succ _) hd)) Ōł¦
            BHW.complexLorentzAction ╬øfig (BHW.realEmbed xrot) =
              BHW.realEmbed
                (fun k => xfig (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k))

      theorem BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlanePathSupport_at_orderedSeed
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          Ōłā xseed : NPointDomain d n,
            xseed Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector
              (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) Ōł¦
            (fun k => xseed (Žä k)) Ōłł
              EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žä Ōł¦
            let ╬öseed : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
              fun t =>
                BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig (d := d) (n := n) hd
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) Žä
                    (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                      (d := d) (n := n) xseed t))
            Continuous ╬öseed Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ t, ╬öseed t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ t,
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬öseed t) =
                  BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                      (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                        (d := d) (n := n) xseed t)))

      theorem BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          Ōłā (Upath : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (xfig : NPointDomain d n),
            IsOpen Upath Ōł¦ xfig Ōłł Upath Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ k : Fin n, xfig k 0 = 0) Ōł¦
            xfig Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed xfig Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed (fun k => xfig (Žä k)) Ōłł
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Upath,
              ŌłĆ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x t Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Upath, ŌłĆ t,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                    (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x t)
                =
              BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath
                    (d := d) (n := n) x t)))
      ```

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists`: start from the
      same equal-time witness used by
      `BHW.adjacent_overlap_real_jost_witness_exists`, together with the
      concrete two-plane rotated witness from
      `BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlaneRotationSupport`.  At this equal-time
      anchor, `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath xfig t = BHW.realEmbed xfig` for
      every `t`, so the rotated adjacent path is the constant
      `BHW.realEmbed xrot` and lies in `ExtendedTube` by the checked
      `ForwardJostSet` inclusion.  The map
      `(x,t) Ōå” figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd
        (permAct Žä (os45Figure24IdentityPath x t))`
      is continuous, so compactness of `unitInterval` and
      `BHW.exists_open_nhds_forall_mem_of_compact_parameter` give one open
      neighborhood `Upath` of the equal-time anchor on which the canonical
      rotated adjacent path stays in `ExtendedTube` for all `t`.  The scalar
      Gram identity is algebraic and holds pointwise in `t` by the exported
      Lorentz inverse and `sourceMinkowskiGram_perm`; it must be exported as a
      theorem about `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift`, not hidden behind an
      existential `╬ö`.

      Crucial order: `Upath` is not an ordered-sector neighborhood; it is a
      Figure-2-4 path-stability neighborhood around the equal-time anchor.
      In `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch`, first
      intersect the Figure-2-4 real environment `Ufig` with this `Upath`, then
      choose the positive-time perturbation inside `Ufig Ōł® Upath`, and only
      after that intersect with the identity and adjacent ordered-sector
      preimages before taking the final precompact ball `V`.  This is what
      makes the closure-level `hV_figPath_closure` field share the same anchor as
      the horizontal pulled-branch-domain fields.

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlanePathSupport_at_orderedSeed`: start from
      `BHW.figure24_adjacentTwoPlaneRotationSupport`; write
      `Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®`, and choose the returned
      equal-time witness `xfig`, rotated witness `xrot`, and Lorentz element
      `╬øfig`.  The support theorem must export the formula
      `realEmbed xrot =
      figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd (realEmbed (fun k => xfig (Žä k)))`;
      this is not optional, because it is what identifies the path-stability
      base point with the checked Figure-2-4 endpoint calculation.

      The public rotation-support theorem is a wrapper around the concrete
      calculation already present in `AdjacentOverlapWitness.lean`, but the
      wrapper has mathematical content: it exposes exactly the real witness
      needed by the source-patch path.  Instantiate
      `xfig := swapWitnessReal hd i hi` and
      `xrot := swapWitnessRealSwappedRot hd i hi`, with the public axes
      `figure24Axis1 hd = e1 hd` and `figure24Axis2 hd = e2 hd`.  The
      coordinate identity
      `realEmbed xrot =
        figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd (realEmbed (fun k => xfig (Žä k)))`
      is proved by `ext k ╬╝`, then the four cases
      `╬╝ = 0`, `╬╝ = figure24Axis1 hd`, `╬╝ = figure24Axis2 hd`, and the
      remaining coordinates.  The nonzero/axis-distinct side conditions are
      exactly the existing `e1_ne_zero`, `e2_ne_zero`, and `e2_ne_e1`
      calculations, publicized under the `figure24Axis*` names.

      The forward-Jost field for `xrot` is also a concrete finite inequality
      check, not a topological assertion.  For every consecutive index `k`,
      the time difference of `xrot` is `0`.  The first spatial-axis difference
      is:

      * at `k = i`,
        `(3 / 5) * 2 + (-4 / 5) * 1 = 2 / 5 > 0`;
      * at `k = Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®`,
        `(3 / 5) * (-1) + (-4 / 5) * (-1) = 1 / 5 > 0`;
      * at every other `k`,
        `(3 / 5) * ╬öe1 + (-4 / 5) * 0 > 0`, using the already checked
        positivity of the swapped witness's first-axis difference.

      Therefore `|╬ötime| = 0 < ╬öaxis1`, which is precisely
      `ForwardJostSet d n hd1`.  Applying
      `forwardJostSet_subset_extendedTube hd1` gives the endpoint
      `realEmbed xrot Ōłł ExtendedTube d n`.  The Lorentz element exported by
      `figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse` is the inverse of the
      same `(3,4,5)` spatial rotation, so applying it to
      `realEmbed xrot` recovers `realEmbed (fun k => xfig (Žä k))`.

      The identity path theorem is where the `0 Ōēż t Ōēż 1` arithmetic enters.
      For `t : unitInterval`, use `t.property.1 : 0 Ōēż (t : ŌäØ)` and
      `t.property.2 : (t : ŌäØ) Ōēż 1` to prove
      `0 < 1 - (t : ŌäØ) / 2` by `nlinarith`.  If
      `x` is in the identity ordered positive-time sector, every consecutive
      Euclidean time gap `╬┤k` is positive.  The imaginary part of the
      corresponding consecutive difference in
      `os45Figure24IdentityPath x t` has time component
      `(1 - (t : ŌäØ) / 2) * ╬┤k` and all spatial components zero, while the
      real part is irrelevant for forward-tube membership.  Thus the imaginary
      difference lies in the open forward cone by `mul_pos`; this proves
      `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_mem_forwardTube`.  The endpoint theorems
      are coordinate extensionality: at `t = 0`, the coefficient is `I`; at
      `t = 1`, it is `1 / 2 + (1 / 2) * I`, exactly the inverse OS45
      quarter-turn coordinate of `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x`.
      The zero-time lemma
      `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_of_time_zero` is also pure coordinate
      extensionality: if `ŌłĆ k, x k 0 = 0`, then the time coordinate is
      `os45Figure24TimeCoeff t * 0 = 0`, and every spatial coordinate is
      already `(x k ╬╝ : Ōäé)`, so the whole path is `BHW.realEmbed x` for every
      `t`.

      Define the two-variable path map
      `H x t :=
        figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd
          (permAct Žä (os45Figure24IdentityPath x t))`.
      The joint-continuity helper
      `BHW.continuous_os45Figure24IdentityPath_joint` is the missing Lean
      surface here: prove it by `continuous_pi` twice, splitting on
      `╬╝ = 0`; the time branch is
      `(BHW.continuous_os45Figure24TimeCoeff.comp continuous_snd).mul`
      the continuous real coordinate
      `fun p => (p.1 k 0 : Ōäé)`, and the spatial branches are just coordinate
      projections through `Complex.continuous_ofReal`.  Then prove
      `BHW.continuous_figure24RotatedIdentityPath` by composing this joint
      path with the finite permutation map `permAct Žä` and the checked finite
      linear map `BHW.continuous_figure24RotateAdjacentConfig`.  Since `xfig` has
      zero time coordinates, `os45Figure24IdentityPath xfig t` is
      `realEmbed xfig` for every `t`; hence the exported rotation formula
      gives `H xfig t = realEmbed xrot` for every `t`.  Because
      `xrot Ōłł ForwardJostSet`, `forwardJostSet_subset_extendedTube` gives
      `realEmbed xrot Ōłł ExtendedTube d n`; `BHW.isOpen_extendedTube` and the
      compactness of `unitInterval` then give an open neighborhood `Upath` of
      `xfig` such that
      `ŌłĆ x Ōłł Upath, ŌłĆ t, H x t Ōłł ExtendedTube d n`.

      The compact-parameter step is the following reusable topology lemma,
      whose proof is the standard finite-subcover tube lemma and contains no
      BHW-specific mathematics:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_open_nhds_forall_mem_of_compact_parameter
          {X Y Z : Type*}
          [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace Y]
          [TopologicalSpace Z] [CompactSpace Y]
          {f : X ├Ś Y -> Z} (hf : Continuous f)
          {x0 : X} {U : Set Z} (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hx0 : ŌłĆ y : Y, f (x0, y) Ōłł U) :
          Ōłā V : Set X,
            IsOpen V Ōł¦ x0 Ōłł V Ōł¦
              ŌłĆ x Ōłł V, ŌłĆ y : Y, f (x, y) Ōłł U := by
        have hP : ŌłĆ y Ōłł (Set.univ : Set Y),
            ŌłĆßČĀ z : X ├Ś Y in ­ØōØ (x0, y), f z Ōłł U := by
          intro y _hy
          exact hf.continuousAt.preimage_mem_nhds
            (hU_open.mem_nhds (hx0 y))
        have hEventually :
            ŌłĆßČĀ x in ­ØōØ x0,
              ŌłĆ y Ōłł (Set.univ : Set Y), f (x, y) Ōłł U :=
          (isCompact_univ : IsCompact (Set.univ : Set Y)).
            eventually_forall_of_forall_eventually hP
        rcases mem_nhds_iff.mp hEventually with
          Ō¤©V, hV_sub, hV_open, hx0VŌ¤®
        exact Ō¤©V, hV_open, hx0V, by
          intro x hx y
          exact hV_sub hx y trivialŌ¤®
      ```

      Apply the checked bounded perturbation theorem
      `exists_ordered_small_time_perturb_in_adjacent_overlap_of_lt` to this
      `Upath`, the equal-time witness `xfig`, and the zero-time field
      `ŌłĆ k, xfig k 0 = 0`, with any fixed positive upper bound, for example
      `a = 1`.  The theorem first obtains a metric ball around `0` in the
      perturbation parameter from continuity of `adjacentTimePerturb`; it then
      chooses `╬Ą = min ╬┤ a / 2`, so `0 < ╬Ą`, `╬Ą < a`, and the perturbed point
      is still in `Upath`.  Its ordering conclusions are exactly the checked
      formula `adjacentTimePerturb_mem_identity_sector`: after perturbation,
      the time coordinate is `╬Ą * ((k : ŌäØ) + 1)`, so all times are positive
      and strictly increasing; after applying the adjacent swap, the same
      identity-order calculation is rewritten by `Equiv.Perm.mul_apply`.
      Thus the theorem returns `xseed = adjacentTimePerturb xfig ╬Ą` with
      `╬Ą > 0`, `xseed Ōłł Upath`, `xseed` in the identity ordered positive-time
      sector, and `(fun k => xseed (Žä k))` in the `Žä`-ordered sector.  With
      `╬öseed := H xseed`, continuity follows by fixing the first argument of
      the continuous two-variable map `H`, and
      `ŌłĆ t, ╬öseed t Ōłł ExtendedTube d n` is the universal field of `Upath`.

      The Gram identity for `╬öseed` is pointwise in `t`.  From
      `figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse`, choose `╬øfig` with
      `complexLorentzAction ╬øfig (╬öseed t) =
        permAct Žä (os45Figure24IdentityPath xseed t)`.  Then
      `sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction` rewrites the left side to
      the Gram matrix of the relabelled identity path, and
      `sourceMinkowskiGram_perm` rewrites that as
      `sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
        (sourceMinkowskiGram d n
          (os45Figure24IdentityPath xseed t))`.
      This proof never asserts that `permAct Žä ╬ō` is in the forward tube:
      for the adjacent swap it has the wrong ordered time orientation at the
      swapped gap.  The route uses only the rotated extended-tube certificate
      and Lorentz invariance of scalar products, exactly as OS I Figure 2-4
      requires.

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_complexLorentzAction`: extensionality in the
      two source indices reduces the statement to preservation of the complex
      bilinear Minkowski form.  Unfold `sourceMinkowskiGram` and
      `complexLorentzAction`, expand the two finite sums, commute the sums,
      factor
      `Ōłæ ╬╝, (metricSignature d ╬╝ : Ōäé) * ╬ø.val ╬╝ ╬▒ * ╬ø.val ╬╝ ╬▓`, and replace
      it by `if ╬▒ = ╬▓ then metricSignature d ╬▒ else 0` using
      `╬ø.metric_preserving`; the diagonal collapse is the same finite-sum
      calculation used by
      `MinkowskiSpace.complexQuadratic_lorentz_invariant`, without needing
      polarization or any new analytic input.

      With `hV_figPath_closure` exported by the selected source patch, the
      per-point theorem below is only the Lean projection used by the scalar
      corridor:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.swFigure24_wickToQuarterTurn_doubleETRealizationPath
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_figPath_closure :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł closure V ->
              let y :=
                BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x
              Ōłā (╬ō ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                Continuous ╬ō Ōł¦
                Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
                ╬ō (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōł¦
                ╬ō (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ t, ╬ō t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ t,
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                    BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t))))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
          Ōłā (╬ō ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
            Continuous ╬ō Ōł¦
            Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
            ╬ō (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) Ōł¦
            ╬ō (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ t, ╬ō t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ t,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t)))
      ```

      Proof transcript for the realization theorem: unfold the two `let`
      binders in `hV_figPath_closure`, prove
      `hx0_closure : x0 Ōłł closure V` by `exact subset_closure hx0V`, and apply
      `hV_figPath_closure x0 hx0_closure`.  The mathematical work is the
      producer of `hV_figPath_closure`, displayed above; this projection theorem
      has no function-value equality, no
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData`, no local EOW output, no global PET
      branch independence, and no `bvt_W` term.  Its theorem surface therefore
      carries only the explicit closure path witness and `hx0V`; the ordered,
      Jost, and horizontal-edge hypotheses belong to the producer of
      `hV_figPath_closure`, not to this projection.  If the call site can
      consume `hV_figPath_closure` directly, do not add this projection as a
      public theorem.

      The scalar path is then the mechanical corollary used by the corridor:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.swFigure24_wickToQuarterTurn_scalarPath
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_figPath_closure :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł closure V ->
              let y :=
                BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x
              Ōłā (╬ō ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                Continuous ╬ō Ōł¦
                Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
                ╬ō (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōł¦
                ╬ō (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ t, ╬ō t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ t,
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                    BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t))))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
          Ōłā ╬│fig : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé,
            Continuous ╬│fig Ōł¦
            ╬│fig (0 : unitInterval) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) Ōł¦
            ╬│fig (1 : unitInterval) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0)) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ t,
              ╬│fig t Ōłł BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä)
      ```

      Proof transcript: apply
      `BHW.swFigure24_wickToQuarterTurn_doubleETRealizationPath` with
      `hV_figPath_closure`, or consume
      `hV_figPath_closure x0 (subset_closure hx0V)` directly, and set
      `╬│fig t := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t)`.  Continuity is
      `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n` composed with `h╬ō_cont`.  The
      endpoint equations are the corresponding equations for `╬ō`.  For each
      `t`, prove
      `╬│fig t Ōłł BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä` by
      `BHW.mem_sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_iff_exists_realizations`,
      using realizations `╬ō t` and `╬ö t`, the two extended-tube membership
      fields, `rfl` for the left Gram, and the realization theorem's Gram
      identity for the right Gram.  This scalar theorem is therefore geometry
      only: it has no analytic equality field and no `hRep`.

      The non-topological BHW/Jost source package is therefore:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              ŌłĆ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd Žä x t Ōłł
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V)
          (hfig_x0 :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            let y0 :=
              BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
            Ōłā (╬ō ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
              Continuous ╬ō Ōł¦
              Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
              ╬ō (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) Ōł¦
              ╬ō (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ t, ╬ō t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ t,
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                  BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t))))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
          Ōłā (Wseed : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé))
            (╬│ : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed Ōł¦
              IsConnected Wseed Ōł¦
              Wseed.Nonempty Ōł¦
              Wseed ŌŖå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä Ōł¦
              Set.EqOn
                (fun Z =>
                  hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z))
                hRep.Phi
                Wseed Ōł¦
              Continuous ╬│ Ōł¦
              ╬│ (0 : unitInterval) Ōłł Wseed Ōł¦
              ╬│ (1 : unitInterval) =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0)) Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ t,
                ╬│ t Ōłł BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä)
      ```

      Proof transcript: first construct `Wseed` by
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq`, obtaining
      the distinguished `Gseed Ōłł Wseed` constructed as
      `sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg` with `zreg Ōłł Usrc`.  Because `Usrc` is an
	      open connected subset of the finite-dimensional source space, use the
	      checked Mathlib theorem
	      `(IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected hUsrc_open).mp hUsrc_conn` to
	      obtain `hUsrc_path : IsPathConnected Usrc`.  This theorem is in
	      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.LocPathConnected` and uses the local
	      path-connectedness instance for the finite-dimensional source space.
	      Then use
	      `hUsrc_path.joinedIn zreg hzregUsrc zwick0 hzwickUsrc` (i.e.
      `IsPathConnected.joinedIn`) to get
      `JoinedIn Usrc zreg zwick0`, where
      `zwick0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`.  Extract the actual source
      path as `hjoin.somePath`; its membership proof is
      `hjoin.somePath_mem`.  Applying `sourceMinkowskiGram d n` with
      `Path.map` and the global continuity supplied by
      `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n` gives a scalar path from `Gseed`
      to the Wick scalar point, and the scalarization chart's double-domain
      field keeps this first segment inside
      `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä`.  Concatenate it with the
      scalar path obtained from the pointwise Figure-2-4 realization
      `hfig_x0` using `Path.trans` (method form `╬│seed.trans ╬│fig`).  The
      range/membership proof for the concatenated path is
      `Path.trans_range`, or `JoinedIn.trans` if the proof is kept in
      joined-in form; do not cite an unspecified path-concatenation lemma.
      Local API audit, 2026-05-01: these exact names exist in the local
      Mathlib tree.  A scratch Lean check also shows the standalone import
      set must include `Mathlib.Analysis.Complex.Basic`; without it Lean does
      not even synthesize `TopologicalSpace (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)`.
      Then import `Mathlib.Analysis.LocallyConvex.WithSeminorms` so
      `NormedSpace.toLocallyConvexSpace` supplies
      `LocallyConvexSpace ŌäØ (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)`; that import also
      brings in `Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Module.LocallyConvex`, where
      `LocallyConvexSpace.toLocPathConnectedSpace` supplies the
      `LocPathConnectedSpace` instance used by
      `IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected`.  Existing project source
      files that already import the BHW source stack may get the complex
      topology transitively, but a new small path-support file should list
      `Mathlib.Analysis.Complex.Basic` explicitly.
      `IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected` is in
      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.LocPathConnected`; `JoinedIn.somePath`,
      `JoinedIn.somePath_mem`, and `JoinedIn.trans` are in
      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.PathConnected`; `Path.trans` and
      `Path.trans_range` are in `Mathlib.Topology.Path`.  The archived
      connected-union route must use the checked theorem
      `IsConnected.biUnion_of_reflTransGen` from
      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.Basic`, whose relation is exactly
      `(s i Ōł® s j).Nonempty Ōł¦ i Ōłł t` and whose connected version also requires
      `t.Nonempty`.
      Scratch Lean check, 2026-05-02: with imports
      `Mathlib.Analysis.Complex.Basic`,
      `Mathlib.Analysis.LocallyConvex.WithSeminorms`,
      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.LocPathConnected`,
      `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.PathConnected`, and
      `Mathlib.Topology.Path`, the exact chain
      `(IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected hUsrc_open).mp hUsrc_conn`,
      `hUsrc_path.joinedIn zreg hzregUsrc zwick0 hzwickUsrc`,
      `let ╬│src : Path zreg zwick0 := hjoin.somePath`, and
      `have h╬│src_mem : ŌłĆ t, ╬│src t Ōłł Usrc := hjoin.somePath_mem`
      elaborates.  The theorem
      `Path.trans_range ╬│seed ╬│fig :
        Set.range (╬│seed.trans ╬│fig) =
          Set.range ╬│seed Ōł¬ Set.range ╬│fig`
      also elaborates exactly.  Therefore the topology API in this path
      segment is checked support; the remaining blocker in this theorem is
      the source seed/provenance mathematics, not path concatenation.
      Its endpoint is the OS45 quarter-turn scalar point.  The
      resulting `╬│` starts in `Wseed`,
      ends at `sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (realEmbed y0))`, and stays in
      the adjacent double scalar domain.  This theorem is source geometry and
      scalarization only; it must not use local EOW, real-edge adjacent
      equality, global PET branch independence, or final `bvt_W` locality.

      Lean-shaped pseudocode:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq
          ... := by
        classical
        let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
        let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
        let y0 := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
        -- Use the strengthened seed theorem in its implementation form,
        -- retaining the source chart, `zreg`, and `Gseed` provenance.
        rcases BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_with_sourceProvenance
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_open hV_jost
            hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered
            hV_adjLift_ET hx0V hRep with
          Ō¤©V0, Usrc, zreg, Wseed, Gseed,
            hV0_open, _hV0_conn, hx0V0, hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn,
            hwick_mem, hzregUsrc, hGseed_def, hW_rel, hW_conn, hW_ne,
            hGseedW, hW_double, hW_eq, hUsrc_doubleŌ¤®
        let zwick0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé :=
          fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)
        have hzwickUsrc : zwick0 Ōłł Usrc :=
          hwick_mem x0 hx0V0
        subst Gseed
        -- Requires `Mathlib.Analysis.Complex.Basic`,
        -- `Mathlib.Analysis.LocallyConvex.WithSeminorms`, and the
        -- path-connectedness imports so the finite Pi space over `Ōäé` has
        -- both its topology and the `LocPathConnectedSpace` instance.
        have hUsrc_path : IsPathConnected Usrc :=
          (IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected hUsrc_open).mp hUsrc_conn
        have hjoin : JoinedIn Usrc zreg zwick0 :=
          hUsrc_path.joinedIn zreg hzregUsrc zwick0 hzwickUsrc
        let ╬│src : Path zreg zwick0 := hjoin.somePath
        have h╬│src_mem : ŌłĆ t, ╬│src t Ōłł Usrc := hjoin.somePath_mem
        let ╬│seed : Path
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg)
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zwick0) :=
          (╬│src.map (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous)
        have h╬│seed_double :
            ŌłĆ t, ╬│seed t Ōłł
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä := by
          intro t
          change BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬│src t) Ōłł
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä
