      ```lean
          exact hUsrc_double (╬│src t) (h╬│src_mem t)
        rcases hfig_x0 with
          Ō¤©╬ō, ╬ö, h╬ō_cont, h╬ö_cont, h╬ō0, h╬ō1, h╬ō_ET, h╬ö_ET, h╬ö_gramŌ¤®
        let ╬│fig : Path
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zwick0)
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0))) :=
          { toFun := fun t => BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t)
            source' := by simpa [zwick0] using congrArg
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n) h╬ō0
            target' := by simpa [y0] using congrArg
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n) h╬ō1
            continuous_toFun := (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous.comp h╬ō_cont }
        have h╬│fig_double :
            ŌłĆ t, ╬│fig t Ōłł
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä := by
          intro t
          exact
            (BHW.mem_sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_iff_exists_realizations
              (d := d) (n := n) Žä (╬│fig t)).2
              Ō¤©╬ō t, ╬ö t, h╬ō_ET t, h╬ö_ET t, rfl, h╬ö_gram tŌ¤®
        let ╬│path : Path
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n zreg)
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0))) :=
          ╬│seed.trans ╬│fig
        have h╬│path_double :
            ŌłĆ t, ╬│path t Ōłł
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä := by
          intro t
          have ht :
              ╬│path t Ōłł Set.range ╬│seed Ōł¬ Set.range ╬│fig := by
            have hmem :
                ╬│path t Ōłł Set.range (╬│seed.trans ╬│fig) := by
              exact Ō¤©t, rflŌ¤®
            simpa [╬│path, Path.trans_range ╬│seed ╬│fig] using hmem
          rcases ht with Ō¤©u, rflŌ¤® | Ō¤©u, rflŌ¤®
          ┬Ę exact h╬│seed_double u
          ┬Ę exact h╬│fig_double u
        exact Ō¤©Wseed, ╬│path, hW_rel, hW_conn, hW_ne,
          hW_double, hW_eq, ╬│path.continuous,
          by simpa [╬│path] using hGseedW,
          by simp [╬│path, ╬│fig, y0],
          h╬│path_doubleŌ¤®
      ```

      The provenance-strengthened helper
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_with_sourceProvenance` is not a
      separate mathematical theorem from
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq`; it is the
      implementation form of the same proof, keeping the source chart and
      regular source point instead of throwing them away before the path step.
      It must carry the Wick-membership field `hwick_mem` (or the specialized
      proof `zwick0 Ōłł Usrc`) forward to the path theorem; no later helper may
      reconstruct that membership from only the scalar seed.

      Archived pure-Gram adjacent `S'_n` implementation-readiness ledger.
      This table is retained only to document the conditional pure-Gram fork.
      On the active proper-complex/oriented route, the corresponding surfaces
      are the oriented names in the completion-plan ledger above; checked
      Figure-2-4 path geometry is not the same thing as the adjacent scalar
      seed theorem.

      | Surface or packet | Current status | First missing/required input |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentPathStableNeighborhood_exists`, `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalEnvironmentWithPathStability`, and `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch` | Checked local Lean input in `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45Figure24.lean`. | May be consumed for `hV_adjLift_ET`, closure ordered-sector fields, and `hV_figPath_closure`; does not prove any scalar-function equality. |
      | `BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt` and `BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at` | Archived pure-Gram source-chart constructor; not an active first Lean target. | On the conditional pure-Gram fork, must combine the checked source patch with `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`, `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`, Wick real-section topology including the explicit `V0_connected` convex-preimage proof, and the source Gram pullback APIs.  On the active route, use the oriented scalarization chart and oriented double-domain fields instead. |
      | `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24` | Archived pure-Gram surface, not production-Lean-ready on the active route. | On the conditional pure-Gram fork, needs `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` or an already supplied `hRep`; on the active oriented route, this surface must be restated as `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart_of_figure24` with the oriented representative before Lean.  It also needs the source chart above, checked germ pullbacks for `hRep.Phi`, and the compact OS-I Figure-2-4 pairing theorem; it may not use local EOW or final locality. |
      | `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq` | Archived pure-Gram source-equality surface; not active on the oriented route. | On the conditional pure-Gram fork, after scalarization, use the compact distributional identity theorem on the real Wick section to prove `Set.EqOn (Phi Ōłś sourcePermuteComplexGram Žä) Phi` on the connected source scalar chart; do not replace this with pointwise adjacent-Wick equality.  On the active route, this is replaced by `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`. |
      | `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq` and `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_with_sourceProvenance` | Archived pure-Gram provenance seed; not active on the oriented route. | On the conditional pure-Gram fork, preserve `V0`, `V0_connected`, `Usrc`, `zreg`, `Gseed`, `hwick_mem` or `zwick0 Ōłł Usrc`, `hUsrc_double`, and the seed equality so the later corridor path has endpoints and domain membership.  On the active route, the corresponding theorem is the oriented provenance seed. |
      | `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq` | Archived pure-Gram path theorem; not active on the oriented route. | On the conditional pure-Gram fork, consume the provenance seed, the checked `hV_figPath_closure` from the selected source patch, `IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected`, `JoinedIn.somePath`, `Path.trans`, `Path.trans_range`, and the scalar double-domain realization theorem.  On the active route, use the oriented seed path surface. |
      | Generic post-seed propagation through a connected scalar corridor | Checked support in `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean` as `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarEq_on_quarterTurnCorridor`. | Once `Wseed`, `Wscal`, `Wseed ŌŖå Wscal`, `Wscal ŌŖå sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä`, and the seed `Set.EqOn` are supplied, this theorem propagates equality by `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle`; it does not construct the OS45 seed or the Figure-2-4 path. |

      The only checked rows in this table are geometry/topology inputs from
      the Figure-2-4 source patch and lower source-variety infrastructure.
      Every row that asserts equality of scalar representatives remains
      blocked until the active scalar representative is proved
      (`sourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` on the strict
      proper-complex/oriented route, or the conditional ordinary
      `sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` only if the full-component
      Hall-Wightman fork is separately sourced) and until the OS-I compact
      adjacent branch producer is proved.

      Local implementation status correction: the downstream identity-principle
      step is already a checked generic theorem.  After the OS-specific seed
      theorem has produced

      ```lean
      hSeed :
        Set.EqOn
          (fun Z => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z))
          hRep.Phi
          Wseed
      ```

      and the Figure-2-4 path/corridor theorem has produced the connected
      relatively open `Wscal` with `Wseed ŌŖå Wscal` inside the double scalar
      domain, the propagation proof should call
      `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarEq_on_quarterTurnCorridor`.  That checked theorem
      constructs the two germ-holomorphic functions
      `Z Ōå” hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z)` and `hRep.Phi`,
      restricts them to `Wscal`, subtracts them, and applies the checked
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle` to the seed set.
      Therefore a future production Lean pass must not reprove this corridor
      identity-principle argument inside
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`; the missing
      mathematics is the OS I ┬¦4.5 compact-pairing seed and the provenance
      package that supplies `Wseed`, not the generic propagation from
      `Wseed` to `Wscal`.

      The corridor uses two finite-dimensional topology support theorems.
      They are not QFT theorems: they are local connectedness of the source
      complex Gram variety and the compact-path tube construction derived from
      it.

      The singular-rank branch of the local-basis theorem needs one additional
      rank-`<=` cone input, distinct from the already checked rank-exact cone
      theorem in `SourceComplexConeTransport.lean`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.matrixSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected
          {q : Type*} [Fintype q] [DecidableEq q]
          (r : Nat)
          {N : Set (Matrix q q Ōäé)}
          (hN_open : IsOpen N)
          (h0N : (0 : Matrix q q Ōäé) Ōłł N) :
          Ōłā C : Set (Matrix q q Ōäé),
            (0 : Matrix q q Ōäé) Ōłł C Ōł¦
            IsOpen C Ōł¦
            C ŌŖå N Ōł¦
            IsConnected
              (C Ōł® {S : Matrix q q Ōäé | SßĄĆ = S Ōł¦ S.rank Ōēż r})

      theorem BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image
          (n D : Nat) {r q : Type*} [Fintype r] [Fintype q]
          [DecidableEq r] [DecidableEq q]
          (e : Fin n Ōēā r ŌŖĢ q)
          {Aset : Set (Matrix r r Ōäé)}
          {Bset : Set (Matrix r q Ōäé)}
          {Sset : Set (Matrix q q Ōäé)}
          (hA_conn : IsConnected Aset)
          (hB_conn : IsConnected Bset)
          (hS_conn :
            IsConnected
              (Sset Ōł® {S : Matrix q q Ōäé |
                SßĄĆ = S Ōł¦ S.rank Ōēż D - Fintype.card r}))
          (hA_unit : ŌłĆ A, A Ōłł Aset -> IsUnit A.det) :
          IsConnected
            ((fun p : Matrix r r Ōäé ├Ś Matrix r q Ōäé ├Ś Matrix q q Ōäé =>
                BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph n e p.1 p.2.1 p.2.2) ''
              {p | p.1 Ōłł Aset Ōł¦ p.2.1 Ōłł Bset Ōł¦
                p.2.2 Ōłł Sset Ōł® {S : Matrix q q Ōäé |
                  SßĄĆ = S Ōł¦ S.rank Ōēż D - Fintype.card r}})

      theorem BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch
          (n D : Nat) {r q : Type*} [Fintype r] [Fintype q]
          [DecidableEq r] [DecidableEq q]
          (e : Fin n Ōēā r ŌŖĢ q)
          {N0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          {Aset : Set (Matrix r r Ōäé)}
          {Bset : Set (Matrix r q Ōäé)}
          {Sset : Set (Matrix q q Ōäé)}
          (hA_unit : ŌłĆ A, A Ōłł Aset -> IsUnit A.det)
          (hrD : Fintype.card r <= D)
          (hgraph_N0 :
            ((fun p : Matrix r r Ōäé ├Ś Matrix r q Ōäé ├Ś Matrix q q Ōäé =>
                BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph n e p.1 p.2.1 p.2.2) ''
              {p | p.1 Ōłł Aset Ōł® {A : Matrix r r Ōäé | AßĄĆ = A} Ōł¦
                p.2.1 Ōłł Bset Ōł¦
                p.2.2 Ōłł Sset Ōł® {S : Matrix q q Ōäé |
                  SßĄĆ = S Ōł¦ S.rank Ōēż D - Fintype.card r}}) ŌŖå N0) :
          ({Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé |
              Z Ōłł N0 Ōł¦
              IsUnit
                ((((Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j).reindex e e).toBlocksŌéüŌéü).det) Ōł¦
              (((Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j).reindex e e).toBlocksŌéüŌéü) Ōłł
                Aset Ōł¦
              (((Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j).reindex e e).toBlocksŌéüŌéé) Ōłł
                Bset Ōł¦
              BHW.reindexedRectSchurComplement
                (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j) e e Ōłł Sset} Ōł®
              BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) =
            ((fun p : Matrix r r Ōäé ├Ś Matrix r q Ōäé ├Ś Matrix q q Ōäé =>
                BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph n e p.1 p.2.1 p.2.2) ''
              {p | p.1 Ōłł Aset Ōł® {A : Matrix r r Ōäé | AßĄĆ = A} Ōł¦
                p.2.1 Ōłł Bset Ōł¦
                p.2.2 Ōłł Sset Ōł® {S : Matrix q q Ōäé |
                  SßĄĆ = S Ōł¦ S.rank Ōēż D - Fintype.card r}})
      ```

      Proof transcript: for
      `matrixSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected`, choose a centered ball
      `C := Metric.ball 0 ╬Ą ŌŖå N`.  The set
      `C Ōł® {S | SßĄĆ = S Ōł¦ S.rank Ōēż r}` is star-shaped at `0`: for
      `0 Ōēż t Ōēż 1`, the path `S_t := ((1 - t : ŌäØ) : Ōäé) ŌĆó S` stays in the
      centered ball by `norm_smul` and `1 - t Ōēż 1`, stays symmetric by
      transpose-linearity, and stays rank-`<= r` because nonzero scalar
      multiplication preserves rank while the endpoint has rank `0`.  Thus
      every point is joined to `0`, and connectedness follows by joining
      through `0`.  The transported arbitrary-index version is obtained from
      the `Fin (Fintype.card q)` proof by `Matrix.reindex`, exactly as in the
      checked rank-exact transport theorem.  The two Schur-graph rank-`<=`
      lemmas are the rank-`<=` analogues of the checked rank-exact image and
      coordinate-patch lemmas, using
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_mem_rankLE_iff` in place of
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_mem_rankExact_iff`.

      Lean status (2026-04-30): this rank-`<=` support is checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexCone.lean`,
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexConeTransport.lean`, and
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexSchurGraph.lean`.  The checked names are
      `BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected`,
      `BHW.matrixSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected`,
      `BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image`,
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_coordinatePatch`, and
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis
          (d n : Nat)
          {Z0 : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hZ0 : Z0 Ōłł BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          {N0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hN0_open : IsOpen N0)
          (hZ0N0 : Z0 Ōłł N0) :
          Ōłā V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            Z0 Ōłł V Ōł¦
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n V Ōł¦
            IsConnected V Ōł¦
            V ŌŖå N0 Ōł® BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n
      ```

      Proof transcript: if `n <= d + 1`, use
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace_of_le` and
      the full symmetric-coordinate equivalence to choose a small connected
      coordinate ball inside `N0`.  If `d + 1 < n`, write
      `D := d + 1` and split by the rank of `Z0`.  At rank `D`, use the
      checked complex-regular realization theorem and
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramMap_localConnectedRelOpenImage_in_open_of_complexRegular`
      to get a connected relatively open image inside `N0`.  At lower rank,
      use the checked principal Schur product chart around `Z0`: the
      determinant-unit principal block is shrunk to a connected matrix ball,
      the rectangular block to a connected ball, and the Schur-complement
      factor to a small ball intersected with the symmetric rank-`<= D-k`
      cone.  Its connectedness is exactly
      `BHW.matrixSymmetricRankLECone_small_connected`; the connected graph
      image is supplied by
      `BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image`, and its
      identification with the relatively open coordinate patch is
      `BHW.sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch`.
      The graph map is continuous, the patch is relatively open in
      `sourceComplexGramVariety d n` after rewriting
      `sourceComplexGramVariety d n` by
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le`, and the product image is
      connected and contained in `N0`.

      Lean status (2026-04-30): this local connected basis is checked in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean` as
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis`, with strict
      branch
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis_strict`,
      regular branch
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis_regular`, and
      singular branch
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis_singular`
      checked using the rank-`<=` Schur tube.  The older
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_rankExact_connected_basis` remains a
      separate rank-exact-regular-locus input for the identity-principle layer;
      it is not the lower-rank connected source-variety tube.

      The compact-path tube is implemented through connected components in the
      relatively open source variety, not through an ad hoc finite-chain
      subdivision.  First add the relative-open union and component lemmas:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.iUnion
          (d n : Nat)
          {╬╣ : Type*}
          {U : ╬╣ -> Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hU : ŌłĆ i, BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n (U i)) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n (Ōŗā i, U i)

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen
          (d n : Nat)
          {D : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hD_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n D)
          {Z0 : Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (hZ0D : Z0 Ōłł D) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
            (connectedComponentIn D Z0)
      ```

      Proof transcript for the component lemma: write
      `D = D0 Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, with `D0` open, and set
      `C := connectedComponentIn D Z0`.  For each `Y : C`, the inclusion
      `C ŌŖå D` gives `Y Ōłł D0` and
      `Y Ōłł sourceComplexGramVariety d n`.  Apply
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_local_connectedRelOpen_basis d n` to `Y`
      inside the ambient open set `D0`, obtaining a connected relatively open
      neighborhood `VY` with `Y Ōłł VY` and
      `VY ŌŖå D0 Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety d n = D`.  Since `VY` is
      preconnected, contains `Y`, and is contained in `D`,
      `IsPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn` gives
      `VY ŌŖå connectedComponentIn D Y`; because `Y Ōłł C`,
      `connectedComponentIn_eq` identifies this component with `C`.  Hence
      `VY ŌŖå C`.  Conversely every point of `C` lies in its own `VY`, so
      `C = Ōŗā Y : C, VY`.  The preceding relative-open `iUnion` lemma makes
      this union relatively open in `sourceComplexGramVariety d n`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedRelOpenTube_around_compactPath
          (d n : Nat)
          {D : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hD_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n D)
          {╬│ : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé}
          (h╬│_cont : Continuous ╬│)
          (h╬│D : ŌłĆ t, ╬│ t Ōłł D)
          {W0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)}
          (hW0_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n W0)
          (hW0_conn : IsConnected W0)
          (hW0_nonempty : W0.Nonempty)
          (hW0D : W0 ŌŖå D)
          (hstart : ╬│ (0 : unitInterval) Ōłł W0) :
          Ōłā Wtube : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wtube Ōł¦
            IsConnected Wtube Ōł¦
            W0 ŌŖå Wtube Ōł¦
            Wtube ŌŖå D Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ t, ╬│ t Ōłł Wtube)
      ```

      Proof transcript for the compact-path tube theorem: let
      `Zstart := ╬│ (0 : unitInterval)` and
      `Wtube := connectedComponentIn D Zstart`.  Relative openness is exactly
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen d n hD_rel
      (h╬│D 0)`.  Connectedness is
      `isConnected_connectedComponentIn_iff.mpr (h╬│D 0)`, and containment in
      `D` is `connectedComponentIn_subset D Zstart`.  Since `W0` is connected,
      contains `Zstart`, and is contained in `D`,
      `hW0_conn.isPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn hstart hW0D`
      proves `W0 ŌŖå Wtube`.  Finally, the image `Set.range ╬│` is preconnected
      by continuity of `╬│` and connectedness of `unitInterval`; it contains
      `Zstart` and is contained in `D` by `h╬│D`, so another application of
      `IsPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn` gives
      `ŌłĆ t, ╬│ t Ōłł Wtube`.  The theorem name retains the compact-path
      wording for downstream route stability, but the proof only needs
      connectedness of `unitInterval`.

      Lean status (2026-04-30): the relative-open union lemma
      `BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.iUnion`, the component theorem
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedComponentIn_relOpen`, and the
      compact-path tube theorem
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedRelOpenTube_around_compactPath`
      are checked in `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean`.  They use the
      checked local connected basis above and do not introduce any BHW/PET
      branch-independence, local EOW, or final-`bvt_W` shortcut.

      The BHW corridor theorem is the source form of the cited
      Bargmann-Hall-Wightman step in OS I ┬¦4.5:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentQuarterTurnScalarCorridor_of_figure24
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V -> x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          (hV_horiz_swap :
            ŌłĆ x, x Ōłł V ->
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 Ōłł V)
          (Wseed : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé))
          (hWseed_relOpen :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed)
          (hWseed_connected : IsConnected Wseed)
          (hWseed_nonempty : Wseed.Nonempty)
          (hWseed_double :
            Wseed ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          (hDouble_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)))
          (╬│ : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé)
          (h╬│_cont : Continuous ╬│)
          (h╬│_start : ╬│ (0 : unitInterval) Ōłł Wseed)
          (h╬│_end :
            ╬│ (1 : unitInterval) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                ((BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
                  (BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                      (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0))))
          (h╬│_double :
            ŌłĆ t,
              ╬│ t Ōłł
                BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                  (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let y0 :=
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0
          Ōłā (Wscal : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé))
            (Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)),
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wscal Ōł¦
              IsConnected Wscal Ōł¦
              Wseed ŌŖå Wscal Ōł¦
              IsOpen Uy Ōł¦
              BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł Uy Ōł¦
              Uy ŌŖå BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä Ōł®
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä Ōł¦
              (ŌłĆ z, z Ōłł Uy ->
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z) Ōłł Wscal) Ōł¦
              Wscal ŌŖå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Put `Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®`,
         `Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)`, and
         `y0 := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x0`.
         The adjacent ACR edge membership is obtained from the swapped ordered
         branch, not from the identity ordered hypothesis alone:

         ```lean
         have hy_acr_swapped :
           BHW.realEmbed
             (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) Žä
               (fun k => x0 (Žä k))) Ōłł
             BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä :=
           BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered
             (d := d) (n := n) Žä (hV_swap_ordered x0 hx0V)

         have hedge_eq :
           BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) Žä
             (fun k => x0 (Žä k)) = y0 := by
           simpa [Žä, y0] using
             BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
               (d := d) (n := n) i hi 1 x0
         ```

         Rewriting `hy_acr_swapped` by `hedge_eq` gives
         `BHW.realEmbed y0 Ōłł BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain ... Žä`.  The pulled BHW
         edge membership is exactly `hV_horiz_swap x0 hx0V`.  The fields
         `hV_open`, `hV_jost`, `hV_ordered`, and `hV_swap_ordered` remain on
         the theorem surface because the upstream Figure-2-4 path supplier
         consumes the same source patch; after the path `╬│` is explicitly
         supplied, this assembly theorem only uses the adjacent ACR and pulled
         BHW edge memberships.
      2. Consume the path `╬│` produced by
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq`.
         Its endpoint is the scalar point

         ```lean
         BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
           (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0))
         ```

         and its image lies in `sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä`.
         This path is the local BHW/Jost corridor input; it is not a
         consequence of `SourceScalarRepresentativeData` alone and it is not
         a theorem about an arbitrary subset of the double domain.
      3. The path in step 2 is the only OS-paper geometric input.  Thicken it
         inside the relatively open double scalar domain by
         `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_connectedRelOpenTube_around_compactPath`,
         with
         `D := sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä` and `hD_rel :=
         hDouble_rel`.  In the downstream one-branch source input,
         `hDouble_rel` is supplied by
         `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`
         from `hRep.U_relOpen` and `hRep.U_eq`.
         The output tube, enlarged to include `Wseed`, is the connected
         relatively open set `Wscal`.
      4. From the tube theorem, obtain `Wtube` with relative openness,
         connectedness, `Wseed ŌŖå Wtube`, `Wtube ŌŖå D`, and
         `ŌłĆ t, ╬│ t Ōłł Wtube`.  The endpoint identity `h╬│_end` gives
         `Zend := sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y0)) Ōłł
         Wtube`.  Write `Wtube = O Ōł® sourceComplexGramVariety d n`, with `O`
         open, and let

         ```lean
         scalarMap z := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)
         Uy :=
           scalarMap Ōü╗┬╣' O Ōł®
           BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä Ōł®
           BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä
         ```

         The map `scalarMap` is continuous by
         `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n` and `Q.symm.continuous`, so
         `Uy` is open by the checked branch-domain openness theorems.  The
         base point belongs to `Uy` by `Zend Ōłł O`, the adjacent ACR edge
         membership above, and `hV_horiz_swap x0 hx0V`.  For `z Ōłł Uy`,
         `scalarMap z Ōłł O`; also
         `scalarMap z Ōłł sourceComplexGramVariety d n` by the witness
         `Ō¤©Q.symm z, rflŌ¤®`.  Hence `scalarMap z Ōłł Wtube`.  Set
         `Wscal := Wtube`; the double-domain field is the tube containment
         `Wtube ŌŖå D`.

      This theorem is external BHW/Jost source content on the OS ┬¦4.5 route.
      It must not use `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`, the downstream
      real-edge equality theorem, global PET branch independence, or final
      `bvt_W` locality.

      Lean status (2026-04-30): the assembly theorem
      `BHW.os45AdjacentQuarterTurnScalarCorridor_of_figure24` is checked in
      `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45TraceMembership.lean`.  Its hypotheses still
      include the Figure-2-4 scalar path `╬│`; the theorem itself performs only
      the connected source-tube thickening and OS45 branch-domain pullback
      described above.

      Finally propagate the seed through the connected scalar corridor:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentScalarEq_on_quarterTurnCorridor
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          {F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé}
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData (d := d) n F)
          (Wseed Wscal : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé))
          (hWscal_relOpen :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wscal)
          (hWscal_connected : IsConnected Wscal)
          (hWseed_relOpen :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wseed)
          (hWseed_nonempty : Wseed.Nonempty)
          (hWseed_subset : Wseed ŌŖå Wscal)
          (hWscal_double :
            Wscal ŌŖå
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®))
          (hSeed :
            let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
            Set.EqOn
              (fun Z =>
                hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z))
              hRep.Phi
              Wseed) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          Set.EqOn
            (fun Z =>
              hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z))
            hRep.Phi
            Wscal
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Put `Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®`,
         `╬” Z := hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z)`, and
         `╬© := hRep.Phi`.  This theorem is deliberately generic in the
         represented holomorphic function `F`; the OS-specific object
         `bvt_F OS lgc n` enters only when constructing the
         `SourceScalarRepresentativeData` packet.  This avoids an
         OS-wrapper theorem whose axiom audit would inherit unrelated
         upstream `bvt_F` obligations.
      2. From `hWscal_double` and `hRep.U_eq`, prove
         `Wscal ŌŖå hRep.U` and
         `Wscal ŌŖå {Z | BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z Ōłł hRep.U}`.
      3. Restrict `hRep.Phi_holomorphic` to `Wscal` by
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen`, and restrict the
         permutation precomposition by
         `BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram`.
      4. Set `H Z := ╬” Z - ╬© Z` and use
         `BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.sub` to prove
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n H Wscal`.
      5. On `Wseed`, `hSeed` gives `H Z = 0` by `sub_eq_zero.mpr`; use
         `hWseed_subset`.
      6. Apply the checked germ-API theorem
         `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle` in
         `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean`, with
         `U := Wscal`, `W := Wseed`, `H := H`, `hWscal_relOpen`,
         `hWscal_connected`, `hWseed_relOpen`, `hWseed_nonempty`,
         `hWseed_subset`, `hH_holo`, and the zero result from step 5.
      7. Convert `Set.EqOn H 0 Wscal` back to the displayed equality by
         `sub_eq_zero.mp`.

      Lean status (2026-05-01): the generic theorem
      `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarEq_on_quarterTurnCorridor` in
      `BHWPermutation/SourceComplexDensity.lean` has already been ported to
      the germ API.  Its proof builds `h╬©_holo`, `h╬”_holo`, and `hH_holo`
      using `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.of_subset_relOpen`,
      `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram`, and
      `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.sub`, then calls
      `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_identity_principle`.

      **(C) Pullback assembly.**  Compose the `Set.EqOn` result from
      `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarEq_on_quarterTurnCorridor` with
      `z Ōå” sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Q.symm z)` on `Uy`.  This produces the
      adjacent `hGram` field of
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`.  No new
      mathematics occurs in this last step.

      The `hGram` production is now entirely inside the active identity and
      adjacent scalar-packet suppliers above.  The assembly theorem
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput` consumes
      `BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket`; it does not manufacture that
      packet from geometry.  The allowed source content for the packet
      producers is only the OS I ┬¦4.5 one-branch Hall-Wightman/BHW source
      theorem on the selected Figure-2-4 scalar corridor: construct the
      branch seed
      `z Ōå” bvt_F OS lgc n (permAct ╬▓.symm z)` on the appropriate `S'_n`
      ordered/permuted tube side, use OS-II `bvt_F_perm` only to identify that
      seed with the selected symmetric `S'_n` datum there, enlarge that branch
      by the BHW invariant analytic-function theorem, and propagate equality
      through the connected scalar corridor to the OS45 quarter-turn
      neighbourhood.  It must not compare the identity and adjacent pulled BHW
      branches by real-edge locality.

      In particular, a Lean proof of the packet-producing theorem may use:

      - `bvt_F_holomorphic`, `bvt_F_acrOne_package`, `bvt_F_perm`, and
        `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` for the selected
        OS-II analytic datum;
      - `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn` and the ordinary one-branch BHW
        continuation of that datum;
      - the Figure-2-4 real-environment geometry supplied by
        `BHW.swFigure24_adjacentHorizontalRealEnvironment` and the
        relabelled source patch;
      - the local Hall-Wightman/BHW scalar corridor and source-variety
        identity principle needed to identify the ACR and pulled BHW
        representatives near the quarter-turn edge.

      It may not use:

      - any `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`;
      - `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarGerm_of_OSII_Figure24`;
      - the real-edge equality theorem
        `BHW.bvt_F_adjacent_extendF_edgeDistribution_eq_of_osEOWDifferenceEnvelope`;
      - global PET branch independence or
        `hallWightman_source_permutedBranch_compatibility_of_distributionalAnchor`;
      - final `bvt_W` locality.

      Therefore the local theorem order is:

      1. prove the pure source-neighborhood geometry from `hU_ordered`,
         `hU_horiz_bhw`, `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered`,
         and openness of `os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓` and
         `os45PulledRealBranchDomain ╬▓`;
      2. prove the public scalar-packet supplier
         `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id` or
         `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`;
      3. consume that packet in
         `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput`;
      4. derive `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_sourcePatch` by adding
         the recorded holomorphy fields and restricting the `Set.EqOn`
         equality from `Uy` to the smaller domain requested by the packaging
         theorem;
      5. package `BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt` from that theorem and
         the definitional pulled-branch equality.

      Expanded source-germ proof transcript:

      1. For a fixed branch label `╬▓`, set

         ```lean
         Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
         W╬▓ z := BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
         A╬▓ z := BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z
         B╬▓ z := BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z
         ```

         The ACR-side domain is `Q.symm z Ōłł ForwardTube`; the BHW-side
         domain is `W╬▓ z Ōłł ExtendedTube`.
      2. The base point belongs to both branch domains:

         ```lean
         have hy_acr :
           BHW.realEmbed
             (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0) Ōłł
             BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ :=
           BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered
             (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ (hU_ordered x0 hx0U)

         have hy_bhw :
           BHW.realEmbed
             (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x0) Ōłł
             BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ :=
           hU_horiz_bhw x0 hx0U
         ```

         Using `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain` and
         `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`, shrink to an open `Uy`
         containing this base point and contained in the intersection of the
         two domains.  This is the pure topology part of the theorem.
      3. `A╬▓` is holomorphic on the ACR-side domain, but not because
         `W╬▓ z` is itself in the forward tube.  On
         `os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓`, the checked field is
         `Q.symm z Ōłł ForwardTube`; for nontrivial `╬▓`, the relabelled point
         `W╬▓ z` can lie outside the ordered forward tube.  The proof must
         first use the total finite-permutation symmetry field
         `bvt_F_perm OS lgc n` to rewrite
         `bvt_F OS lgc n (W╬▓ z)` as `bvt_F OS lgc n (Q.symm z)`, and only
         then use the ordinary holomorphy of `bvt_F` on `ForwardTube`.  This
         is a one-branch OS-II ACR symmetry statement, not an equality of two
         BHW real-edge branches.
      4. `B╬▓` is holomorphic on the pulled BHW domain by the checked
         `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch_holomorphicOn`.
      5. The local common representative for this branch is

         ```lean
         H╬▓ z := BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z)
         ```

         on a small complex neighborhood `Uy` of `BHW.realEmbed y0`.
         On `Uy Ōł® os45PulledRealBranchDomain ╬▓`, agreement with `B╬▓` is
         definitional after unfolding `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch`.
      6. On `Uy`, agreement with `A╬▓` is the
         one-branch OS I ┬¦4.5/BHW source input.  For `╬▓ = 1`, use
         `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id` and the algebraic
         reduction
         `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq`.  For
         `╬▓ = Žä`, use
         `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent` with the
         pointwise Figure-2-4 path `hfig_x0` coming from
         `hV_figPath_closure`.  That packet is based at the identity common
         edge `y0`, so the adjacent supplier rewrites it to the relabelled
         common edge before calling the generic one-branch source-patch
         assembly, and rewrites the packaged source-germ predicate back to
         `y0` afterward.  This is not the downstream adjacent scalar equality;
         it never compares `H1` with `HŽä`.
      7. The source theorem must output `Uy` small enough that `W╬▓ z` remains
         in the branch's extended-tube/scalar-product source domain.  The
         membership at `BHW.realEmbed y0` is supplied by `hUfig_source` for the
         chosen Figure-2-4 branch, and openness is supplied by
         `BHW.isOpen_extendedTube`, `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`,
         and continuity of `Q.symm`, `BHW.permAct ╬▓.symm`, and `BHW.realEmbed`.
      8. Package `Uy`, the inclusion `W╬▓ '' Uy ŌŖå ExtendedTube`, the
         differentiability of the branch-specific `H╬▓`, and the two agreement
         fields as `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt OS lgc n ╬▓ y0`.

      The forbidden move is to replace step 5 by a theorem that the identity
      and adjacent BHW branches are already equal, or by using a scalar
      equality produced from `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`.  The branch
      source predicate is upstream of the branch-difference EOW argument; the
      adjacent scalar equality below is downstream of it.

      Downstream scalar-germ consequence:

      After `BHW.os45_adjacent_commonBoundaryEnvelope` has been proved from
      the branchwise packets and the checked local EOW theorem, one may derive
      a local Hall-Wightman scalar equality.  This theorem is useful as a
      source-side audit/export of the real-edge equality, but it is not an
      input to `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`, `os45BranchHorizontalCommonGerm`,
      or `os45_adjacent_commonBoundaryEnvelope`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentScalarGerm_of_OSII_Figure24
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ŌäĢ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_nonempty : V.Nonempty)
          (hV_jost : ŌłĆ x Ōłł V, x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł V, BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                  BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (E :
            BHW.AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope
              (d := d) OS lgc n
              (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®) V)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)) :
          let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
          Ōłā Wscal : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> Ōäé),
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n Wscal Ōł¦
            Wscal.Nonempty Ōł¦
            Wscal ŌŖå BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n Žä Ōł¦
            Set.EqOn
              (fun Z =>
                hRep.Phi (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z))
              hRep.Phi Wscal
      ```

      Proof transcript: choose a regular `x0 Ōłł V`; construct `Oenv` by
      `sourceRealGramMap_realEnvironmentAt_of_regular`; use `E` and
      `bvt_F_adjacent_extendF_edgeDistribution_eq_of_osEOWDifferenceEnvelope`
      to obtain compact real-edge equality on `V`; convert compact equality
      to pointwise equality on `V` by the checked compact-support
      DuBois-Reymond/continuity argument used in
      `sourceAnchor_compactBranchEq_pointwise_on_realPatch`; convert that
      pointwise equality to scalar equality on `Oenv` by `hRep.branch_eq` and
      `sourceRealGramComplexify_perm`; then apply
      `sourceVariety_localChart_totallyReal_zero` to the holomorphic
      difference on the double domain.  The double-domain relative openness is
      derived inside the proof from `hRep.U_relOpen`, `hRep.U_eq`, and
      `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`.
      This theorem is the required Wick-to-real-to-source germ.  It is not a
      shortcut around local EOW, and it is not a boundary-functional demotion:
      the only real-edge equality input is the concrete
      `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope` produced by the OS45 local EOW
      construction.

      Lean proof skeleton:

      1. Put `Žä := Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®` and
         `F := bvt_F OS lgc n`.  Derive `hF_holo` from
         `bvt_F_holomorphic` and `hF_lorentz` from
         `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`.
      2. Get compact real-edge equality on `V`:

         ```lean
         hcompact :
           ŌłĆ Žå : SchwartzNPoint d n,
             HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
             tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå V ->
             Ōł½ x, BHW.extendF F (BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Žä k))) * Žå x =
             Ōł½ x, BHW.extendF F (BHW.realEmbed x) * Žå x
         ```

         by `BHW.bvt_F_adjacent_extendF_edgeDistribution_eq_of_osEOWDifferenceEnvelope`.
      3. Convert `hcompact` to

         ```lean
         hreal :
           ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
             BHW.extendF F (BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Žä k))) =
             BHW.extendF F (BHW.realEmbed x)
         ```

         using `SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn`.
         The two continuity hypotheses are exactly the `extendF`-continuity
         proof already checked inside
         `BHW.sourceAnchor_compactBranchEq_pointwise_on_realPatch`, with
         `hV_ET` and `hV_swapET` supplying the two `ExtendedTube` inclusions.
      4. Choose a regular real seed:

         ```lean
         rcases (BHW.dense_sourceGramRegularAt d n).exists_mem_open
             hV_open hV_nonempty with Ō¤©x0, hx0reg, hx0VŌ¤®
         ```

         and build `Oenv`:

         ```lean
         rcases BHW.sourceRealGramMap_realEnvironmentAt_of_regular
             (d := d) n hx0reg (hV_jost x0 hx0V) V hV_open hx0V with
           Ō¤©Oenv, hOenv_sub, hOenvŌ¤®
         ```

      5. Prove `sourceRealGramComplexify n G Ōłł DŽä` for every `G Ōłł Oenv` by
         realizing `G` as `sourceRealMinkowskiGram d n x` with `x Ōłł V`, then
         using `hV_ET`, `hV_swapET`, `BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_realEmbed`,
         `BHW.sourceRealGramComplexify_perm`, and
         `BHW.mem_sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_iff_exists_realizations`.
      6. Define `Hscal Z := hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä Z) -
         hRep.Phi Z`.  Prove
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn d n Hscal DŽä` from
         `hRep.Phi_holomorphic`, the permutation precomposition theorem, and
         subtraction.
      7. For each `G Ōłł Oenv`, choose `x Ōłł V` realizing it.  Use `hreal x hx`
         and the two `hRep.branch_eq` rewrites to prove
         `Hscal (sourceRealGramComplexify n G) = 0`.  The right-hand branch
         rewrite uses `sourceRealGramComplexify_perm`; no Euclidean value or
         `bvt_W` term appears here.
      8. Apply `BHW.sourceVariety_localChart_totallyReal_zero` with `hOenv`,
         the relative openness of `DŽä`, the membership result from step 5, and
         the zero result from step 7.  It returns the desired `Wscal`; convert
         `Set.EqOn Hscal 0 Wscal` to the displayed equality by
         `sub_eq_zero.mp`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalCommonGerm
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ŌäĢ) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (E : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hE_precompact : IsCompact (closure E))
          (hsource :
            ŌłĆ y Ōłł closure E,
              BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
                (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ y) :
          Ōłā (U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé))
            (H╬▓ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé),
            IsOpen U Ōł¦
            BHW.realEmbed '' closure E ŌŖå U Ōł¦
            DifferentiableOn Ōäé H╬▓ U Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z Ōłł U,
              z Ōłł BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ ->
                H╬▓ z =
                  BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ z Ōłł U,
              z Ōłł BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ ->
                H╬▓ z =
                  BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
      ```

      This theorem is the precise compact-gluing consumer for the one-branch
      OS I ┬¦4.5 / BHW input.  It is not an adjacent-branch equality theorem.
      The non-topological source content is exactly `hsource`; the rest of
      the proof only transports compactness of `closure E` and chooses a
      finite cover on which the same branch-specific canonical germ is valid.
      Jost/order/extended-tube fields live upstream: they are used to build
      `hsource`, `hlocal`, and the selected edge `E`, not to glue the already
      available germs.
      Precompactness of `E` is what lets the final neighborhood be chosen
      around all of `closure E`.

      Proof transcript for `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalCommonGerm`:

      1. Define the canonical branch formula

         ```lean
         Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
         W╬▓ z := BHW.permAct (d := d) ╬▓.symm (Q.symm z)
         H╬▓ z :=
           BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (W╬▓ z)
         ```

         The compact common-germ theorem must use this single formula for the
         fixed branch label `╬▓`, not glue arbitrary existential functions.  It
         also must not use the unpermuted identity-branch formula for every
         branch.
      2. At each real edge point `y Ōłł closure E`, apply `hsource y hy`.  This
         gives an open neighborhood `Uy` of `BHW.realEmbed y` on which
         `W╬▓ z Ōłł ExtendedTube`, `H╬▓` is holomorphic, and `H╬▓` agrees with the
         ACR representative on the ACR branch domain and with the pulled BHW
         representative on the pulled branch domain.
      3. The neighborhoods `Uy` cover the compact set
         `BHW.realEmbed '' closure E`; compactness follows from
         `hE_precompact` and continuity of `BHW.realEmbed`.  Choose a finite
         subcover `UyŌéü, ..., Uy_m` and set `U = Ōŗā j, Uy_j`.
      4. `U` is open and contains `BHW.realEmbed '' closure E`.  The function
         `H╬▓` is differentiable on `U` because every point of `U` lies in one
         selected `Uy_j`, and the source predicate gives differentiability of
         the same canonical formula on that open neighborhood.  The agreement
         fields on `U Ōł® os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓` and
         `U Ōł® os45PulledRealBranchDomain ╬▓` are proved by choosing a covering
         index `j` for the given point and applying the corresponding
         `hsource` fields.  No identity theorem is needed at this compact
         gluing stage.
      5. Restricting `H╬▓` to the real slice gives a smooth function on the real
         open set `Ureal := {y | BHW.realEmbed y Ōłł U}`.  Since `closure E` is
         compact and contained in `Ureal`, the cutoff lemma can choose a
         Schwartz cutoff supported in `Ureal` and equal to one on `closure E`.

      Exact branch-boundary theorem derived from the common germ:

      The only new cutoff infrastructure needed for this derivation is the
      finite-dimensional smooth Urysohn lemma in Schwartz form.  The currently
      checked theorem
      `SCV.exists_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open` is the
      support-in-open projection of this construction; the branch-BV route
      exposes the compact-support field as the following pure real-analysis
      theorem, using the same proof:

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.exists_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport
          {m : ŌäĢ} {K U : Set (Fin m -> ŌäØ)}
          (hK : IsCompact K) (hU : IsOpen U) (hKU : K ŌŖå U) :
          Ōłā Žć : SchwartzMap (Fin m -> ŌäØ) Ōäé,
            HasCompactSupport (Žć : (Fin m -> ŌäØ) -> Ōäé) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł K, Žć x = 1) Ōł¦
            tsupport (Žć : (Fin m -> ŌäØ) -> Ōäé) ŌŖå U
      ```

      This is pure real analysis: the existing proof already constructs the
      cutoff from a compactly supported smooth function before coercing it to
      a `SchwartzMap`.  It is not a theorem-2 shortcut; it only turns the
      regular one-branch real-slice value into a global Schwartz CLM after the
      source common germ has already been proved.

      The OS45 branch theorem also needs the cutoff on
      `NPointDomain d n`, so the flat cutoff theorem is transported through
      the same real-coordinate equivalence:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_nPoint_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport
          {d n : ŌäĢ} {K U : Set (NPointDomain d n)}
          (hK : IsCompact K) (hU : IsOpen U) (hKU : K ŌŖå U) :
          Ōłā Žć : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé,
            HasCompactSupport (Žć : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ x Ōłł K, Žć x = 1) Ōł¦
            tsupport (Žć : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå U
      ```

      Proof transcript: set `e := flattenCLEquivReal n (d + 1)`,
      `Kflat := e '' K`, and `Uflat := e '' U`.  The homeomorphism
      `e.toHomeomorph` sends compact sets to compact sets and open sets to
      open sets, and `hKU` gives `Kflat ŌŖå Uflat`.  Apply
      `SCV.exists_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport`
      on the flat sets, then pull the cutoff back by
      `SchwartzMap.compCLMOfContinuousLinearEquiv Ōäé e`.  Compact support and
      topological-support containment are transported by the homeomorphism,
      and the equality-on-`K` field is the pointwise identity
      `Žć x = Žćflat (e x) = 1`.

      The checked closed-ball integration CLM
      `SCV.exists_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn` is stated on flat
      coordinate spaces `Fin m -> ŌäØ`.  The OS45 branch theorem works on
      `NPointDomain d n = Fin n -> SpacetimeDim d`, so the implementation
      first proves the following flattening adapter before the branch theorem:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.exists_nPoint_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn
          {d n : ŌäĢ} {R : ŌäØ} {g : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé}
          (hg_cont :
            ContinuousOn g (Metric.closedBall (0 : NPointDomain d n) R)) :
          Ōłā T : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé,
            ŌłĆ Žł : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé,
              T Žł =
                Ōł½ x in Metric.closedBall (0 : NPointDomain d n) R,
                  g x * Žł x
      ```

      Proof transcript: set `e := flattenCLEquivReal n (d + 1)`.  The norm
      identity `flattenCLEquivReal_norm_eq` identifies
      `e '' Metric.closedBall 0 R` with the flat closed ball.  Apply
      `SCV.exists_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn` to
      `gflat u := g (e.symm u)`, with continuity transported through
      `e.symm`.  Pull Schwartz tests forward by
      `SchwartzMap.compCLMOfContinuousLinearEquiv Ōäé e.symm`, define
      `T Žł := Tflat (SchwartzMap.compCLMOfContinuousLinearEquiv Ōäé e.symm Žł)`,
      and rewrite the restricted integral by
      `integral_flattenCLEquivReal` applied to the closed-ball indicator.
      This adapter is representation bookkeeping, not a new analytic input.

      The real-analysis consumer of the common germ is isolated before the
      OS-specific branch theorem is implemented:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45RegularBoundaryValue_from_commonGerm
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ŌäĢ) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (E C U : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (H╬▓ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé) -> Ōäé)
          (hE_precompact : IsCompact (closure E))
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hU_real :
            BHW.realEmbed '' closure E ŌŖå U)
          (hH_cont :
            ContinuousOn H╬▓ U)
          (hA_eq :
            ŌłĆ z Ōłł U,
              z Ōłł BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ ->
                H╬▓ z =
                  BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
          (hB_eq :
            ŌłĆ z Ōłł U,
              z Ōłł BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ ->
                H╬▓ z =
                  BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ z)
          (hlocal :
            ŌłĆ K : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K -> K ŌŖå E ->
              ŌłĆ K╬Ę : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬Ę -> K╬Ę ŌŖå C ->
                Ōłā r : ŌäØ, 0 < r Ōł¦
                  ŌłĆ y Ōłł K, ŌłĆ ╬Ę Ōłł K╬Ę, ŌłĆ ╬Ą : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ą -> ╬Ą < r ->
                    BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬Ę ╬Ą Ōłł
                      BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Ōł¦
                    BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬Ę (-╬Ą) Ōłł
                      BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓) :
          Ōłā T : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé,
            (ŌłĆ K╬Ę : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬Ę -> K╬Ę ŌŖå C ->
              ŌłĆ Žå : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé,
                HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
                tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå E ->
                TendstoUniformlyOn
                  (fun (╬Ą : ŌäØ) ╬Ę =>
                    Ōł½ y : NPointDomain d n,
                      BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative
                        (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
                        (BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬Ę ╬Ą) *
                      Žå y)
                  (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => T Žå)
                  (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0)) K╬Ę) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ K╬Ę : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬Ę -> K╬Ę ŌŖå C ->
              ŌłĆ Žå : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé,
                HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
                tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå E ->
                TendstoUniformlyOn
                  (fun (╬Ą : ŌäØ) ╬Ę =>
                    Ōł½ y : NPointDomain d n,
                      BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative
                        (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
                        (BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬Ę (-╬Ą)) *
                      Žå y)
                  (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => T Žå)
                  (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0)) K╬Ę)
      ```

      Proof transcript:

      1. Let `Ureal = {y | BHW.realEmbed y Ōłł U}`.  It is open by continuity
         of `BHW.realEmbed`, and `closure E ŌŖå Ureal` by `hU_real`.
      2. Choose `Žć : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé` with compact support,
         `Žć = 1` on `closure E`, and `tsupport Žć ŌŖå Ureal` using
         `BHW.exists_nPoint_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport`.
      3. Choose `RŽć > 0` with
         `tsupport (Žć : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå Metric.closedBall 0 RŽć`,
         by boundedness of the compact support.  Define the continuous
         coefficient on that closed ball

         ```lean
         g y := Žć y * H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)
         ```

         and construct the CLM by the flattened real-analysis adapter
         `BHW.exists_nPoint_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn`:

         ```lean
         T Žå =
           Ōł½ y in Metric.closedBall (0 : NPointDomain d n) RŽć,
             g y * Žå y
         ```

         The continuity hypothesis for `g` is proved locally on the closed
         ball.  At points of `tsupport Žć`, `hH_cont` applies because
         `tsupport Žć ŌŖå Ureal`; at points outside `tsupport Žć`, the complement
         of `tsupport Žć` is open and `Žć` is identically zero there, so `g` is
         locally zero regardless of the arbitrary total values of `H╬▓` outside
         `U`.  This avoids reproving the `SchwartzMap.mkCLMtoNormedSpace`
         seminorm estimate inside the OS file.
      4. For all `Žå`, the closed-ball formula equals the full-space integral
         `Ōł½ y, Žć y * H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y) * Žå y`, because `Žć` vanishes off
         its topological support and that support is contained in the chosen
         closed ball.  For a test `Žå` with `tsupport Žå ŌŖå E`, `Žć y = 1` on
         `tsupport Žå`
         because `tsupport Žå ŌŖå E ŌŖå closure E`; hence
         `T Žå = Ōł½ y, H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y) * Žå y`.

         The shrink that keeps the plus and minus approach points inside the
         common-germ neighborhood `U` is a separate pure-topology lemma, not a
         reuse of the branch-domain local-wedge theorem:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.exists_uniform_realEdgeAddImag_mem_open_of_compact
             {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé)}
             (hU_open : IsOpen U)
             {K K╬Ę : Set (NPointDomain d n)}
             (hK : IsCompact K) (hK╬Ę : IsCompact K╬Ę)
             (hK_U : ŌłĆ y Ōłł K, BHW.realEmbed y Ōłł U) :
             Ōłā r : ŌäØ, 0 < r Ōł¦
               ŌłĆ y Ōłł K, ŌłĆ ╬Ę Ōłł K╬Ę, ŌłĆ ╬Ą : ŌäØ, |╬Ą| < r ->
                 BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬Ę ╬Ą Ōłł U
         ```

         Proof transcript: set
         `S = (K ├Ś╦ó K╬Ę) ├Ś╦ó ({0} : Set ŌäØ)` and
         `F ((y,╬Ę),╬Ą) = BHW.realEdgeAddImag y ╬Ę ╬Ą`.  The set `S` is compact,
         `F` is continuous by `BHW.continuous_realEdgeAddImag`, and
         `S ŌŖå F Ōü╗┬╣' U` follows from `BHW.realEdgeAddImag_zero` and `hK_U`.
         Apply `IsCompact.exists_cthickening_subset_open` to the open preimage
         `F Ōü╗┬╣' U`.  If `|╬Ą| < r`, then `((y,╬Ę),╬Ą)` lies in the thickening of
         `S` around `((y,╬Ę),0)`, so the cthickening inclusion gives the result.
         This proof shape was checked against Lean as the same compact-
         thickening argument used by the implemented local-wedge theorem.
      5. For compact `K╬Ę ŌŖå C`, let `K = tsupport Žå`.  It is compact by
         `HasCompactSupport Žå`, and `K ŌŖå E` by the support hypothesis.
         Apply `hlocal K K╬Ę` to get the branch-domain radius.  Separately
         apply `BHW.exists_uniform_realEdgeAddImag_mem_open_of_compact` to
         `U`, with `hK_U` obtained from `hU_real` and
         `K ŌŖå E ŌŖå closure E`, to get a common-germ radius.  Use the minimum
         of the two radii.  The plus point uses `|╬Ą| < r`; the minus point
         uses `|-╬Ą| = |╬Ą| < r`.
      6. On that smaller radius, rewrite the plus integrand by `hA_eq` and
         the minus integrand by `hB_eq`; both become
         `H╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬Ę (┬▒╬Ą)) * Žå y`.
      7. Uniform continuity of `H╬▓` is used only on the compact set
         `P = {BHW.realEdgeAddImag y ╬Ę t |
              y Ōłł K, ╬Ę Ōłł K╬Ę, |t| Ōēż r}`.  This set is compact by continuity of
         `realEdgeAddImag`, and it is contained in `U` by the uniform-open
         radius above.  Hence `hH_cont.mono` gives `ContinuousOn H╬▓ P`, and
         compactness gives uniform continuity on `P`.  The maps
         `(y,╬Ę,t) Ōå” realEdgeAddImag y ╬Ę (┬▒t)` converge uniformly to
         `(y,╬Ę) Ōå” BHW.realEmbed y` as `t -> 0`, either from the explicit
         affine formula or by applying
         `BHW.exists_open_nhds_forall_mem_of_compact_parameter` to the compact
         parameter space `K ├Ś K╬Ę`.  Combining these two facts gives
         `H╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬Ę (┬▒╬Ą)) ŌåÆ H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)` uniformly in
         `y Ōłł K` and `╬Ę Ōłł K╬Ę`.
         Use compact support of `Žå` only through the facts
         `Function.support Žå ŌŖå tsupport Žå = K` and
         `Integrable (fun y => ŌĆ¢Žå yŌĆ¢)`.  For all small `╬Ą`, set
         `M╬Ą` to the supremum on `K ├Ś K╬Ę` of
         `ŌĆ¢H╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬Ę (┬▒╬Ą)) - H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)ŌĆ¢`.  Then
         for every `╬Ę Ōłł K╬Ę`,
         `ŌĆ¢Ōł½ y, (H╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬Ę (┬▒╬Ą)) -
           H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)) * Žå yŌĆ¢ Ōēż M╬Ą * Ōł½ y, ŌĆ¢Žå yŌĆ¢`.
         One may also bound the last integral by
         `(sSup ((fun y => ŌĆ¢Žå yŌĆ¢) '' K)) *
          volume (Metric.closedBall (0 : NPointDomain d n) RŽå)`,
         where `RŽå` is any radius with
         `K ŌŖå Metric.closedBall 0 RŽå`.  Equivalently, avoid the finite-volume
         closed-ball bound and use
         `(Ōł½ y, ŌĆ¢Žå yŌĆ¢)` as the fixed multiplier.  The multiplier is finite
         because `Žå` is Schwartz.  Since the last sup tends to zero uniformly
         on `K╬Ę`, the required `TendstoUniformlyOn` limits follow.  The proof
         must not integrate over `K` as if arbitrary compact sets were the
         canonical measure domain; the all-space integral is localized by
         `Žå = 0` off `tsupport Žå`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalBoundaryValue
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ŌäĢ) (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (E C : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hE_open : IsOpen E)
          (hE_precompact : IsCompact (closure E))
          (hsource :
            ŌłĆ y Ōłł closure E,
              BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
                (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓ y)
          (hlocal :
            ŌłĆ K : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K -> K ŌŖå E ->
              ŌłĆ K╬Ę : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬Ę -> K╬Ę ŌŖå C ->
                Ōłā r : ŌäØ, 0 < r Ōł¦
                  ŌłĆ y Ōłł K, ŌłĆ ╬Ę Ōłł K╬Ę, ŌłĆ ╬Ą : ŌäØ, 0 < ╬Ą -> ╬Ą < r ->
                    BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬Ę ╬Ą Ōłł
                      BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ Ōł¦
                    BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬Ę (-╬Ą) Ōłł
                      BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓) :
          Ōłā T : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé ->L[Ōäé] Ōäé,
            (ŌłĆ K╬Ę : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬Ę -> K╬Ę ŌŖå C ->
              ŌłĆ Žå : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé,
                HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
                tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå E ->
                TendstoUniformlyOn
                  (fun (╬Ą : ŌäØ) ╬Ę =>
                    Ōł½ y : NPointDomain d n,
                      BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative
                        (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
                        (BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬Ę ╬Ą) *
                      Žå y)
                  (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => T Žå)
                  (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0)) K╬Ę) Ōł¦
            (ŌłĆ K╬Ę : Set (NPointDomain d n), IsCompact K╬Ę -> K╬Ę ŌŖå C ->
              ŌłĆ Žå : SchwartzMap (NPointDomain d n) Ōäé,
                HasCompactSupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ->
                tsupport (Žå : NPointDomain d n -> Ōäé) ŌŖå E ->
                TendstoUniformlyOn
                  (fun (╬Ą : ŌäØ) ╬Ę =>
                    Ōł½ y : NPointDomain d n,
                      BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative
                        (d := d) OS lgc n ╬▓
                        (BHW.realEdgeAddImag (d := d) (n := n) y ╬Ę (-╬Ą)) *
                      Žå y)
                  (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => T Žå)
                  (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0)) K╬Ę)
      ```

      Proof source and order:

      1. Call `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalCommonGerm` with `hE_precompact` and
         `hsource` to obtain the open common neighborhood `U`, the canonical
         germ `H╬▓`, and the two agreement fields.  Then call
         `BHW.os45RegularBoundaryValue_from_commonGerm` with `hlocal` to
         construct the CLM `T` and both uniform compact-direction limits.
         The remaining bullets explain the source of those two inputs.
      2. Use `bvt_F_acrOne_package` for the OS-II branch: holomorphy on
         ACR-one, total finite-permutation symmetry, and Euclidean
         restriction.  The permutation symmetry is used only to rewrite the
         ACR branch representative on `os45ACRBranchDomain ╬▓` to the ordinary
         forward-tube chart expression; it is not a boundary-locality claim.
      3. Use `bvt_F_holomorphic`, `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`,
         `BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn`, and the definition of
         `BHW.os45PulledRealBranch` for the BHW branch.  Local polynomial
         bounds for the BHW branch are obtained from the local representation
         of `extendF` near each point of the compact horizontal edge as
         `bvt_F` composed with one inverse complex-Lorentz chart; compactness
         gives finitely many such charts.
      4. Use the local OS I ┬¦4.5 / Streater-Wightman Figure 2-4 common-real
         environment to place the chosen OS45 horizontal edge inside the
         **single branch** BHW domain.  This step is a patch-level geometric
         source theorem for the selected adjacent real environment.  It is not
         the later Hall-Wightman source theorem on all of `S''_n`, and it does
         not compare the identity and adjacent branches with each other.
      5. On that source patch, invoke the local source-germ predicate
         `hsource`: the ACR representative and the BHW pulled representative
         are the two side representatives of the same one-branch analytic
         germ.  The equality input is the OS-II symmetric `S'_n` datum on the
         selected Euclidean/Jost edge together with the single-branch BHW
         enlargement of that germ.  This is exactly the local common-boundary
         statement OS I uses before the final Jost locality theorem; it must
         not be replaced by a pointwise `extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` argument
         for a permuted horizontal point.
      6. Define the branch boundary CLM from the common germ.  In the selected
         source patch the common germ is holomorphic on a complex neighborhood of
         `BHW.realEmbed '' closure E`, so the boundary distribution is regular:
         it is integration against the real-slice function
         `y Ōå” H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)` on a real neighborhood of `closure E`.
         The extra hypotheses `hE_open : IsOpen E` and
         `hE_precompact : IsCompact (closure E)` are exactly what turn this
         into the displayed global CLM: choose a smooth cutoff supported inside
         that real neighborhood and equal to one on `closure E` using
         `BHW.exists_nPoint_schwartz_cutoff_eq_one_on_compact_subset_open_compactSupport`,
         choose a closed ball containing the cutoff support, and build the CLM
         by the flattening adapter
         `BHW.exists_nPoint_closedBall_integral_clm_of_continuousOn` applied
         to the compactly supported smooth density
         `Žć y * H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)`.  Compact supports and compact direction
         sets are used for the boundary limits; no physical real-edge
         distribution is used.
      7. Prove the displayed uniform compact-direction BV limits from
         continuity of the canonical germ.  Given compact `K ŌŖå E` and compact
         `K╬Ę ŌŖå C`, `hlocal` gives a radius so the plus and minus side points
         stay in the relevant branch domains.  The separate topology lemma
         `BHW.exists_uniform_realEdgeAddImag_mem_open_of_compact` gives a
         radius so both side points also stay in the common-germ neighborhood
         `U`; take the minimum of the two radii.  On that smaller window the
         ACR and BHW representatives are literally `H╬▓`.  The compact image
         `P = {realEdgeAddImag y ╬Ę t | y Ōłł tsupport Žå, ╬Ę Ōłł K╬Ę, |t| Ōēż r}` is
         contained in `U`, so `hH_cont` gives uniform continuity of `H╬▓` on
         `P`; the explicit affine formula for `realEdgeAddImag` (or the
         compact-parameter tube lemma) gives uniform convergence of the input
         points to `realEmbed y` as `╬Ą ŌåÆ 0+`, uniformly for
         `╬Ę Ōłł K╬Ę`.  Since `Function.support Žå ŌŖå tsupport Žå ŌŖå K`, the
         all-space integral
         difference is bounded by
         `╬┤ ╬Ą * Ōł½ y, ŌĆ¢Žå yŌĆ¢`, where `╬┤ ╬Ą` is the supremum of
         `ŌĆ¢H╬▓ (realEdgeAddImag y ╬Ę (┬▒╬Ą)) - H╬▓ (BHW.realEmbed y)ŌĆ¢` over
         `y Ōłł tsupport Žå` and `╬Ę Ōłł K╬Ę`.  The factor `Ōł½ y, ŌĆ¢Žå yŌĆ¢` is finite
         because `Žå` is Schwartz.  The proof must not replace the all-space
         integral by an integral over an arbitrary compact `K`; localization
         comes from `Žå = 0` off `tsupport Žå`.  The compact-uniform continuity
         step proves `╬┤ ╬Ą -> 0`, giving the required `TendstoUniformlyOn`
         integral convergence on `K╬Ę`.
      8. Verify the compact Jost restriction of `T` by transporting a
         supported test through `BHW.os45CommonEdgePullbackCLM`, applying
         `BHW.zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap`, and then using
         `bvt_euclidean_restriction`.  For the adjacent branch, insert
         `BHW.permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz` before applying `OS.E3_symmetric`.
         This verification is downstream evidence that the branch CLM has the
         correct OS restriction; it is not the definition of `T`.

      Concrete proof order for the `hlocal` domain hypothesis:

      1. Name the branch domains

         ```lean
         Dplus ╬▓  := BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓
         Dminus ╬▓ := BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓
         ```

         `Dplus ╬▓` is open because it is the preimage of
         `BHW.ForwardTube d n` under the continuous linear equivalence
         `(BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE).symm`.  `Dminus ╬▓` is open by the checked
         theorem `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`.

         Checked status: `BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain` and
         `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain` are now implemented in
         `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45TraceMembership.lean`.
      2. Prove the pure coordinate edge-membership theorem

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered
             [NeZero d]
             (╬▓ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) {x : NPointDomain d n}
             (hx :
               x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓) :
             BHW.realEmbed
               (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓ x) Ōłł
               BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) ╬▓
         ```

         Proof transcript: unfold `os45ACRBranchDomain` and
         `os45QuarterTurnCLE_symm_apply`; the imaginary part of the time
         coordinate is exactly
         `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint ╬▓ x`, hence the forward-cone differences are
         half of the ordered Euclidean time differences.  The spatial
         imaginary parts are zero.  Conclude with
         `os45HalfTimeDirection_mem_forwardCone_of_ordered`.

         Checked status: `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered`
         is now implemented.  The proof uses the checked
         `os45QuarterTurn_perm_wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_ordered` theorem
         and the coordinate identity between the inverse quarter-turn of the
         horizontal real edge and the ordinary ordered Wick trace.
      3. Prove the BHW/Jost common-real-environment edge theorem in the
         active adjacent patch form.  The formerly tempting pointwise surface
         with only `x Ōłł JostSet`, `realEmbed x Ōłł ExtendedTube`, and ordered
         time hypotheses is too broad for implementation: the paper supplies a
         selected local real environment, not an all-Jost formula.  The
         Lean-facing theorem should therefore be a strengthened OS45 selector:
         choose, or shrink to, one patch carrying both the existing
         `BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_localEOWGeometry` fields and the new
         horizontal pulled-branch-domain facts.  It must not take an arbitrary
         previously selected `V` and assert that `V` was already inside the
         Figure-2-4 environment.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45_adjacent_identity_horizontalEdge_sourcePatch
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n) :
             Ōłā (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n)) (xseed : NPointDomain d n),
               IsOpen Ufig Ōł¦
               IsOpen V Ōł¦ IsConnected V Ōł¦ V.Nonempty Ōł¦ xseed Ōłł V Ōł¦
               IsCompact (closure V) Ōł¦
               closure V ŌŖå Ufig Ōł¦
               (let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
                  Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
                  BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Žä k)) Ōłł
                    BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł Ufig,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä)) Ōł¦
               (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V, x Ōłł BHW.JostSet d n) Ōł¦
               (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V, BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
               (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
                 BHW.realEmbed
                   (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                     BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
               (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
                 x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                   (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Ōł¦
               (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
                 (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                   EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                     (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)) Ōł¦
               (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
                 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōłł
                   adjacentOS45WickSeedDomain (d := d) (n := n) i hi
                     (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Ōł¦
               (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
                 BHW.realEmbed x Ōłł
                   adjacentOS45RealEdgeDomain (d := d) (n := n) i hi) Ōł¦
               (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
                 BHW.OS45OppositeTubeBranchGeometry (d := d) (n := n)
                   (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) Ōł¦
               (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
                 BHW.OS45OppositeTubeBranchGeometry (d := d) (n := n)
                   (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®)
                   (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k))) Ōł¦
               (let Žä : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
                  Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤®
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł V,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
                  x Ōłł EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                    (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
                  (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i Ō¤©i.val + 1, hiŌ¤® k)) Ōłł
                    EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) Žä) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) 1) Ōł¦
                (ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
                  BHW.realEmbed
                    (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x) Ōłł
                    BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) Žä) Ōł¦
                (let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
                 ŌłĆ x Ōłł closure V,
                  let y :=
                    BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) 1 x
                  Ōłā (╬ō ╬ö : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> Ōäé),
                    Continuous ╬ō Ōł¦
                    Continuous ╬ö Ōł¦
                    ╬ō (0 : unitInterval) =
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) Ōł¦
                    ╬ō (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) Ōł¦
                    (ŌłĆ t, ╬ō t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                    (ŌłĆ t, ╬ö t Ōłł BHW.ExtendedTube d n) Ōł¦
                    (ŌłĆ t,
                      BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ö t) =
                        BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n Žä
                          (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (╬ō t)))))
         ```
